meanings.englishDefinition: To border on.
meanings.example: His land abuts on the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 땅은 길에 접해 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The act of assisting another's progress over a wall or other obstacle by forming a step for one of their feet with one's hands.
meanings.example: He gave her a leg up over the fence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀가 울타리를 넘도록 발을 받쳐 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk a horse on a hard surface regularly.
meanings.example: The trainer legs up the horse daily.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조련사는 매일 말을 단단한 표면 위에서 걷게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To don fancy clothing; to dress up particularly in flattering or specially altered garments.
meanings.example: She loved to gussy up for special occasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 특별한 날을 위해 멋지게 차려입는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry up, to get moving.
meanings.example: I need to get a move on if I'm going to arrive before dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해가 지기 전에 도착하려면 서둘러야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Extinguish by crushing, as a cigar.
meanings.example: He pressed out his cigarette in the ashtray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재떨이에 담배를 눌러 껐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain access to a computer system, usually by providing a previously registered username and password.
meanings.example: You need to log in to access your account.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계정에 접속하려면 로그인해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of plants: to decay and perish through excessive moisture.
meanings.example: Sweet alyssum seedlings are particularly prone to damping off, so do not overmoisten the soil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스위트 알리섬 모종은 특히 과습으로 썩기 쉬우므로 흙을 너무 많이 적시지 마십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help out; lend assistance; contribute; to do one's part to help.
meanings.example: If we all pitch in, we can raise enough money for the renovation of the church.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두 협력하면 교회의 개조를 위한 충분한 돈을 모을 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eject, dismiss, expel, or forcefully remove (someone or something).
meanings.example: They will kick out a disruptive patron.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 난동을 부리는 손님을 쫓아낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak lacking expertise, authority, or knowledge; to invent or fabricate facts.
meanings.example: He's just talking through his hat; he doesn't know anything about it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 잘 모르면서 아는 체 말하는 거야. 그는 그것에 대해 아무것도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: An informal game of football, rugby or similar sports.
meanings.example: Let's have a kick around in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원에서 축구 한 게임 하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abuse or mistreat; to bully; to be rough with.
meanings.example: Don't let them kick you around anymore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 그들에게 함부로 다뤄지지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make the first kick in a game or part of a game.
meanings.example: The players kick off for the third quarter and the clock starts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들이 3쿼터 킥오프를 하고 시계가 시작된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kick; to collapse or break by kicking.
meanings.example: The medicine will kick in soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약이 곧 효과를 나타낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait or delay.
meanings.example: Sorry to hold you up, but I need your help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기다리게 해서 미안하지만, 네 도움이 필요해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grasp or grip firmly.
meanings.example: Hold on to the railing as you go down the stairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계단을 내려갈 때 난간을 꽉 잡으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait a moment.
meanings.example: Hang on a second, I'll be right there.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐만 기다려, 금방 갈게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold (something) out; to extend (something) forward.
meanings.example: He held out his hand, and I grabbed it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 손을 내밀었고, 나는 그것을 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: holdout.
meanings.example: The last player to sign was a holdout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계약한 마지막 선수는 협상 결렬을 주장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay (someone or something) temporarily; to keep at bay.
meanings.example: Let's try to hold off the lawyers until we are ready for them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 준비가 될 때까지 변호사들을 막아보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ponder.
meanings.example: I've been thinking about human rights since watching that documentary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 다큐멘터리를 본 이후로 인권에 대해 생각해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go into a hole, to shelter in a hole.
meanings.example: The guerrillas holed up in a small cave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게릴라들은 작은 동굴에 숨었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act with reserve; to contain one's full measure or power.
meanings.example: She held back tears when she heard the sad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 슬픈 소식을 들었을 때 그녀는 눈물을 참았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: holdover.
meanings.example: The holdover from last year's budget needs to be approved.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작년 예산에서 이월된 항목은 승인되어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (Of a resource) To support or sustain someone for a limited period.
meanings.example: These crisps should hold me over until supper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 감자칩들은 저녁 식사 때까지 나를 임시로 지탱해 줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To harp on; to remind continuously (especially of a misstep or defeat).
meanings.example: I get one parking ticket and he holds it over my head for six months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주차 위반 딱지 한 장을 받았는데, 그는 그것을 6개월 동안 계속 들먹였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear, arrive, or attend, especially suddenly or erratically.
meanings.example: Please come on time; don't just show up whenever you feel like it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제시간에 와 주세요. 마음 내킬 때마다 나타나지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exhibit the best attributes of something.
meanings.example: He likes to show off his new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 차를 자랑하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: show-off.
meanings.example: He's such a show-off, always trying to impress people.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 사람들에게 잘 보이려고 하는 자랑꾼이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse, decline, or deny.
meanings.example: She turned down the job offer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 일자리 제안을 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show up; to appear suddenly or unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Is this your t-shirt that turned up in my drawer?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것이 내 서랍에서 발견된 네 티셔츠니?

meanings.englishDefinition: turn-up.
meanings.example: She rolled up the turn-up of her trousers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 바지 단을 접어 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flip over; to rotate uppermost to bottom.
meanings.example: Turn over the box and look at the bottom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상자를 뒤집어서 바닥을 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become; to transform into something else.
meanings.example: The ice will turn into water if you leave it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖에 내버려 두면 얼음이 물로 변할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To submit something; to give.
meanings.example: You need to turn in your homework by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지 숙제를 제출해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end up; to result.
meanings.example: I had hoped our first meeting would turn out better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 첫 만남이 더 좋게 끝날 줄 알았어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish completely, especially a food (polish the plate with one's tongue) or liquor.
meanings.example: He polished off the entire pizza by himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 혼자서 피자 한 판을 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss; to fire.
meanings.example: Please turn off the lights when you leave the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방을 나갈 때 불을 꺼주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set a flow of fluid or gas running by rotating a tap or valve. from 19th c.
meanings.example: Turn on the tap/faucet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수도꼭지를 트세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tilt one's face upwards slightly, thus raising one's nose, and often to look downwards at someone, as a sign of contempt, disgust, or scorn.
meanings.example: She turned up her nose at the offer of a cheap hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 싸구려 호텔 제안에 코방귀를 뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be appalled, offended or disgusted by something, despite being deceased.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무덤에서 벌떡 일어나다 (매우 불쾌하거나 충격받았을 때)
meanings.example: My grandfather would turn in his grave if he knew what happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할아버지는 무슨 일이 있었는지 알면 무덤에서 벌떡 일어나실 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain distance.
meanings.example: You had better stand back and let me operate the chainsaw.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물러서서 내가 전기톱을 작동하게 하는 게 좋겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace; to act as a double or substitute for.
meanings.example: I asked my colleague to stand in for me so I could take the day off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가를 내기 위해 동료에게 나를 대신해 달라고 부탁했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persist in opposition or resistance (against something); to refuse to comply (with someone).
meanings.example: He decided to stand out against the unfair rules.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불공정한 규칙에 저항하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise from a lying or sitting position.
meanings.example: Stand up, then sit down again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일어섰다가 다시 앉으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To halt one's work or other activity.
meanings.example: Let's knock off early today and go to the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 일찍 일을 끝내고 해변에 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To impress greatly; amaze; stun.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 하다, 깊은 인상을 주다
meanings.example: You wouldn't expect teenagers to sing opera, but these kids will knock your socks off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 십대들이 오페라를 부를 거라고 예상하지 못하겠지만, 이 아이들은 당신을 깜짝 놀라게 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike or render unconscious
meanings.example: The boxer knocked out his opponent in the first round.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 1라운드에서 상대를 녹아웃시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease doing something, particularly annoying or unpleasant; to desist.
meanings.example: Would you two please knock it off with the shouting? I'm trying to sleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 사람, 시끄럽게 떠드는 것 좀 그만할래? 자려고 하거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bump or strike something in such a way as to tip it.
meanings.example: I knocked over a can of paint and spent the next hour cleaning up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트 캔을 쳐서 넘어뜨렸고, 다음 한 시간 동안 치우는 데 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause lasting annoyance, irritation, or hard feelings.
meanings.example: It really sticks in my craw that he never even asked me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나에게 묻지도 않았다는 사실이 정말 분하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit (someone or something) all over repeatedly; hence, to behave violently towards or mistreat (someone or something).
meanings.example: The bullies knocked him about until he gave them his money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불량배들은 돈을 줄 때까지 그를 마구 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage or coax (a person) to do something, especially something foolhardy or reckless.
meanings.example: Don't let them egg you on to do something you'll regret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 후회할 짓을 하도록 그들에게 부추김당하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase one's muscle mass.
meanings.example: He's been working out at the gym to big up his biceps.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이두근을 키우기 위해 헬스장에서 운동하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove without anyone's noticing.
meanings.example: The paintings were spirited away under cover of darkness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 그림들은 어둠을 틈타 몰래 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find time or other resources for.
meanings.example: My appointment book is pretty full, but I can just squeeze you in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 예약 장부는 꽤 꽉 찼지만, 당신을 겨우 끼워 넣을 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: Squeeze out some toothpaste and put it on your toothbrush.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치약을 좀 짜서 칫솔에 묻히세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delete or erase or remove (something) by rubbing, especially with a rubber (eraser).
meanings.example: Please rub out the mistake and write the correct answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수를 문질러 지우고 정답을 써주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something as a controlling input datum.
meanings.example: This logic keys off the user's social security number.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 논리는 사용자의 주민등록번호를 기준으로 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To forage by poking the snout here and there.
meanings.example: The pigs were rooting about in the mud for food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돼지들이 먹이를 찾아 진흙 속을 코로 뒤지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: root about.
meanings.example: She began to root around in her bag for the keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 열쇠를 찾기 위해 가방을 뒤지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To convey some subject matter in simple terms, avoiding technical or academic language, especially in a way that is considered condescending.
meanings.example: The public won't understand this concept. We need to dumb down our explanation of it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대중은 이 개념을 이해하지 못할 것이다. 우리는 그것에 대한 설명을 쉽게 해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to have feelings of love, particularly romantic love.
meanings.example: I fell in love with him the moment I first saw him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그를 처음 본 순간 사랑에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To undress or be naked.
meanings.example: The woman in this film is not shy about getting her kit off, is she?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영화의 여배우는 옷 벗는 것을 부끄러워하지 않지, 그렇지?

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate.
meanings.example: Can you work out 250 × 12 in your head for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 250 곱하기 12를 머리로 계산해 줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about (something or someone) to make it seem as good as possible or to draw positive attention to it.
meanings.example: The restaurant had been talked up way too much, that it left me somewhat disappointed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 식당은 너무 좋게 홍보되었기 때문에 다소 실망스러웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignite.
meanings.example: He fired up the engine and drove away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 엔진에 시동을 걸고 운전해 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To illuminate; to make clear.
meanings.example: The new instruments will shed some light on the history of these rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기기들이 이 암석들의 역사에 대해 일부 밝혀줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a service to (someone); to act as a servant to (someone); to serve (someone) as a waiter or waitress in a restaurant.
meanings.example: The servants waited on the guests hand and foot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하인들은 손님들을 극진히 시중들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove.
meanings.example: He took off his shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신발을 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: takeoff.
meanings.example: France never experienced a "take off" of the sort hypothesized by W. W. Rostow — a sudden spurt of output that begins sustained industrial growth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프랑스는 W. W. 로스토우가 가설을 세운 종류의 '이륙' 즉, 지속적인 산업 성장을 시작하는 갑작스러운 생산량 증대를 결코 경험하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide with equipment.
meanings.example: They were kitted out with the latest survival gear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 최신 생존 장비로 갖춰졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move in an upward direction; to ascend or climb.
meanings.example: The tired horse eventually got up and over the hurdle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지친 말은 마침내 일어나 장애물을 넘었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume (food) quickly or greedily, without regard for table manners.
meanings.example: He wolfed down his dinner in under five minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 5분도 안 되어 저녁을 게걸스럽게 먹어 치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explode.
meanings.example: The bomb went off right after the president left his office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령이 사무실을 떠난 직후 폭탄이 터졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from being on top of (something) to not being on top of it.
meanings.example: Get off your chair and help me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의자에서 내려와서 나를 도와줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, to go away. from mid-20th c.
meanings.example: He told him to piss off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go away!
meanings.example: Piss off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼지고 날 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: To take part in sexual acts with.
meanings.example: I wanna sex you up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너랑 섹스하고 싶어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something on a more detailed level.
meanings.example: The issue can be drilled down to a simple yes or no.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 문제는 간단한 예 또는 아니오로 자세히 조사될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: an internal competition used to practice marching commands, in which the last person caught improperly executing a command wins
meanings.example: We knew someone had lost when the last two people in the drill down ended up facing each other.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 두 사람이 서로 마주보게 되었을 때 누군가 졌다는 것을 알았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by blowing on it.
meanings.example: She blew the dust off the cookbook, revealing its full title.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 요리책의 먼지를 불어 날려 제목을 드러냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat voraciously or ravenously; to gorge oneself.
meanings.example: They watched the game and pigged out on chips and pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 경기를 보며 칩과 피자를 폭식했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join in with work.
meanings.example: If we all muck in, we can get this room cleaned in next to no time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두 함께 일하면 이 방을 금방 청소할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take-up.
meanings.example: In America alone, people spent $170 billion on “direct marketing”—junk mail of both the physical and electronic varieties—last year. Yet of those who received unsolicited adverts through the post, only 3% bought anything as a result. If the bumf arrived electronically, the take-up rate was 0.1%. And for online adverts the “conversion” into sales was a minuscule 0.01%.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작년에 미국에서만 사람들이 실물 및 전자 형태의 정크 메일인 "직접 마케팅"에 1,700억 달러를 썼다. 그러나 우편으로 원치 않는 광고를 받은 사람 중 3%만이 그 결과로 무언가를 샀다. 만약 광고가 전자적으로 도착했다면, 채택률은 0.1%였다. 그리고 온라인 광고의 경우 판매로의 "전환"은 미미한 0.01%였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift; to raise.
meanings.example: The books on finance take up three shelves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재무 관련 서적들이 선반 세 개를 차지한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inhale.
meanings.example: Breathe in deeply and hold it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깊게 숨을 들이쉬고 참으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure (someone or something) in a locked enclosure.
meanings.example: The dog won't escape now we've locked him in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 개를 안에 가두었으니 이제 도망가지 못할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lock-in.
meanings.example: We agreed on a price lock-in for the next five years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 향후 5년간 가격 고정에 합의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inadvertently prevent (a person, particularly oneself) from entering a place, by leaving it locked.
meanings.example: I accidentally locked myself out of my apartment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 실수로 아파트 문을 잠그고 밖에 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lockout#Noun.
meanings.example: The company announced a lockout of its workers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 노동자들에 대한 직장 폐쇄를 발표했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enthuse about a specific topic; to be engrossed in an interest, especially in discussing it with others.
meanings.example: Sorry, I didn't mean to spend fifteen minutes discussing the definition of "decimate". I guess I geeked out for a bit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송해요, "decimate"의 정의에 대해 15분 동안 이야기할 생각은 없었어요. 제가 좀 열광했나 봐요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to understand (a person).
meanings.example: We've sussed him out—he only drinks on Fridays and only in that bar in town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그를 파악했다. 그는 금요일에만 시내 그 술집에서만 술을 마신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mix or blend thoroughly and completely.
meanings.example: Don't mix up the sugar and salt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 설탕과 소금을 섞지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: A mix, blend, or variety.
meanings.example: Next time, let's ask a group with a different mix up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음번에는 다른 조합의 그룹에게 물어보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain; to preserve; to prevent from deteriorating or falling.
meanings.example: He's running so fast, I can't keep up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 빨리 달려서 나는 따라잡을 수가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to remain beside or just behind someone or something that is moving away from one.
meanings.example: It's hard to keep up with the latest fashion trends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최신 패션 트렌드를 따라가기 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate by manually stimulating one's own penis.
meanings.example: He was caught jerking off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: an obnoxious person.
meanings.example: Don't be such a jerk off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 짜증나는 사람처럼 굴지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a cannabis cigarette.
meanings.example: He sat down to skin up a joint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 앉아서 대마초를 말아 피우기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fritter aimlessly or distractedly, to procrastinate, to waste time.
meanings.example: Quit fucking around and get back to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일로 돌아가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquire or increase stock or inventory. :on
meanings.example: We need to stock up on food before the storm hits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 오기 전에 식량을 비축해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall suddenly into a very deep sleep.
meanings.example: After a full day of playing, you'd think that kid would finally zonk out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루 종일 놀고 나면, 그 아이가 마침내 떡실신할 거라고 생각할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (a crease or creases) with an iron.
meanings.example: That shirt still has a few more wrinkles to iron out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 셔츠에는 아직 다림질로 펴야 할 주름이 몇 개 더 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reprimand; to censure severely or angrily.
meanings.example: calls onto the carpet Heath and Human Services Secretary Margaret Heckler for her department's negligence in responding to the AIDS crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보건복지부 장관 마가렛 헤클러가 에이즈 위기 대응에 대한 부서의 태만을 이유로 질책받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat thoroughly; to trounce.
meanings.example: The Jayhawks completely whomped on the hapless Nimrods in today's opening-round game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제이호크스는 오늘 개막전에서 불쌍한 님로드들을 완전히 격파했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce quickly, particularly a meal.
meanings.example: She managed to whomp up a delicious meal in under an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 한 시간 안에 맛있는 식사를 뚝딱 만들어낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suck (something) into a vacuum cleaner, irrespective of brand.
meanings.example: Don't worry about the mess on the carpet - I'll hoover it up later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카펫 위의 어지러움은 걱정하지 마세요. 나중에 진공청소기로 치울게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume a sitting position from a position lying down.
meanings.example: Despite being cancer-ridden, Lorin sat up to greet the visitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암에 걸렸음에도 불구하고, 로린은 방문객들을 맞이하기 위해 앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sit-up.
meanings.example: He does fifty sit-ups every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매일 아침 윗몸 일으키기를 50개 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume a sitting position from a standing position.
meanings.example: Sit down! We have work to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 앉으세요! 할 일이 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to take place.
meanings.example: The new policy will bring about significant changes in the economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 정책은 경제에 상당한 변화를 가져올 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to pass; to develop; to occur; to take place; to happen; to exist.
meanings.example: We have to ask, how did this come about?
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 일이 어떻게 발생했는지 물어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Rest To spend time with somebody; to regularly meet with somebody.
meanings.example: I like to hang out with my friends on weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말에 친구들과 어울려 노는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kiss passionately, fondle, or have sex with.
meanings.example: He got off with my sister at the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티에서 내 여동생과 키스했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to understand; to discover or find a solution; to deduce.
meanings.example: As soon as I figure out what is wrong with my car, I will fix it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 차에 무슨 문제가 있는지 알아내자마자 고칠 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bully, harass or make fun of a victim; to bother or harass.
meanings.example: Hey! Quit picking on your brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이봐! 네 동생 좀 그만 괴롭혀.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give in. en
meanings.example: After 5 months in Madrid, I've decided it is time to pack up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마드리드에서 5개월을 보낸 후, 나는 짐을 싸야 할 때라고 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: He ate too much and threw up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 먹고 토했다.

meanings.example: We had to scrub the seats for throw up when we left the dog in the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개를 차 안에 두고 내렸을 때 좌석에서 구토물을 닦아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used as an expression of frustration or to dismiss a conversation partner.
meanings.example: His constant complaining began to throw up new obstacles for the team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 팀에 새로운 장애물들을 만들어내기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to perform a promised or planned action due to lack of courage or nerve; to run away or back down from a contest or challenge.
meanings.example: He was going to ask her out, but he bottled out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 하려 했지만, 막판에 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act as a ballast for.
meanings.example: We used some sandbags to weigh down the balloon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 풍선을 누르기 위해 모래주머니를 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To descend; to move from a higher place to a lower one.
meanings.example: go down to the grave
meanings.exampleKorean: 무덤까지 내려가다

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform oral sex on (someone).
meanings.example: He refused to go down on her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 구강 성교를 해주기를 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move backwards, especially for a vehicle to do so.
meanings.example: The driver had to back up slowly to avoid the obstacle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운전자는 장애물을 피하기 위해 천천히 후진해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flatter, especially with the intent of personal gain.
meanings.example: He's always trying to butter up the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진을 위해 항상 상사에게 아첨하려고 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take all the opponents' wickets (in whatever way) and thus end their innings.
meanings.example: They were bowled out for 181.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 181점으로 아웃되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: The mouse came out of the hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쥐가 구멍에서 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the utensils, dishes etc. used in preparing and eating a meal.
meanings.example: You wash up tonight and I'll dry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 당신이 설거지하고 내가 닦을게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put up trimmings, especially at Christmas.
meanings.example: "Have you trimmed up yet?"
meanings.exampleKorean: 아직 장식 안 했니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy (especially, a large number or complete set of people or things); to obliterate.
meanings.example: a tornado that nearly wiped out an entire town
meanings.exampleKorean: 마을 전체를 거의 휩쓸어버린 토네이도

meanings.englishDefinition: addl=; nonstandard but widespread.
meanings.example: The team suffered a complete wipe out in the finals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 결승전에서 완전한 패배를 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A command demanding someone to leave.
meanings.example: Go away! Stop annoying me!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저리 가! 귀찮게 하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart or leave a place.
meanings.example: I'm not going to buy it. Please go away and don't come back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 난 안 살 거야. 제발 가서 다시는 돌아오지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive.
meanings.example: She decided to take in a stray cat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 길 잃은 고양이를 거두기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move slowly.
meanings.example: The old horse and cart trundles along the path.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 말과 마차가 길을 따라 느릿느릿 나아간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout very loudly.
meanings.example: The teacher angrily bawled out my name.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님이 내 이름을 격렬하게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or escape.
meanings.example: In case of fire, get out by the nearest exit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화재 시 가장 가까운 출구로 나가십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: Commanding a person to leave.
meanings.example: Get out! I never want to see you in here again!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가! 다시는 네 얼굴 보고 싶지 않아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring or come down; to descend; to cause to bring or come down.
meanings.example: The kitten climbed the tree, but then couldn't get down again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새끼 고양이가 나무에 올라갔지만 다시 내려올 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of an item of merchandise, to come into a person's possession without having been paid for; to be acquired illegally; to have been stolen.
meanings.example: He said he bought it at the markets but I think it fell off a truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시장에서 샀다고 했지만 도둑맞은 물건인 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To participate, take part or get involved in something.
meanings.example: If you want to join in the game, just ask.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게임에 참가하고 싶으면 그냥 물어보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow in sequence; to come next.
meanings.example: The new film follows on from the storyline of the original 1970s release.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 영화는 1970년대 원작의 줄거리를 이어간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: follow-on.
meanings.example: The follow-on in cricket is when a team bats again immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리켓의 팔로온은 한 팀이 즉시 다시 타격하는 것을 말합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave.
meanings.example: He decided to bunk off school and go to the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학교를 몰래 빠지고 해변에 가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave school or work early without permission.
meanings.example: I knew he was gonna cop off from school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 학교를 몰래 빠질 거라는 걸 알았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A mild version of fuck off
meanings.example: He told the annoying salesman to naff off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 귀찮게 하는 판매원에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do random unplanned work or spend time idly
meanings.example: Stop mucking about and get on with your work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일이나 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To close or make watertight, referring to hatches and cargo.
meanings.example: The sailors had to batten down the hatches before the storm hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들은 폭풍이 닥치기 전에 갑판 덮개를 단단히 잠가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for trouble.
meanings.example: It's time to batten down the hatches and prepare for the coming challenge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려움에 대비하고 다가올 도전에 맞설 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sail close to the wind.
meanings.example: The ship had to bear up against the strong headwind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 강한 맞바람을 안고 항해해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: The police bore down on the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자를 압박했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach nearer to (a ship).
meanings.example: The ship began to bear in with the shore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 해안으로 접근하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand further off from (a ship)
meanings.example: The boat had to bear off from the rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 바위에서 멀어져야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quieter.
meanings.example: As the lights dimmed the audience quietened down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조명이 어두워지자 관객들은 조용해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stop functioning, v.i. To stop functioning.
meanings.example: I am afraid my computer will break down if I try to run it at too high a speed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 빠른 속도로 컴퓨터를 작동시키면 고장 날까 봐 걱정입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: breakdown.
meanings.example: The car experienced a breakdown on the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 고속도로에서 고장을 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend (time) idly but pleasantly.
meanings.example: We whiled away the hours playing cards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 카드놀이를 하며 몇 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store away, place out of the way, or stash, especially for the longer term.
meanings.example: She packed away her warm sweaters for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여름을 위해 따뜻한 스웨터를 치워두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose not to participate in something.
meanings.example: Many students opt out of the extracurricular activities.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 학생들이 방과 후 활동에 참여하지 않기로 선택한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send; to dispatch.
meanings.example: Have you sent off your application yet?
meanings.exampleKorean: 아직 지원서를 보냈나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: sendoff.
meanings.example: To administer a send off the referee must stop play and signify the action by showing the offending player a red card.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴장을 선언하기 위해 심판은 경기를 중단하고 해당 선수를 퇴장시켜야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat a topic, but omit its main points, often intentionally.
meanings.example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what you really want.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빙빙 돌려 말하지 말고 정말 원하는 게 뭔지 말해.

meanings.englishDefinition: beat around the bush.
meanings.example: There is no use beating about the bush with a man like you. I know that. You are direct, and so am I. You know my position well enough to be assured that I am empowered to treat with you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신 같은 사람에게 돌려 말할 필요는 없습니다. 저는 압니다. 당신은 직설적이고 저도 그렇습니다. 당신은 제가 당신과 거래할 권한이 있다는 것을 충분히 알고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surrender someone or something; to inform on (someone).
meanings.example: Don't give up on your dreams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꿈을 포기하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Become fully taken over by a certain quality, activity, trait, etc.
meanings.example: And now She Seems give up to Sin / Too much neglecting grace within
meanings.exampleKorean: 그리고 이제 그녀는 죄에 완전히 굴복한 것 같다 / 내면의 은총을 너무 많이 무시하면서

meanings.englishDefinition: To use lies, excuses, or deceit to satisfy (someone); to deceitfully appease someone by giving something that is spurious or inferior from what was wanted or expected.
meanings.example: When I phoned up, I was fobbed off with a string of excuses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화했을 때, 나는 변명만 늘어놓으며 얼버무려졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acknowledge, confess, or admit guilt or responsibility. :to
meanings.example: He finally owned up to breaking the window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 창문을 깬 것을 인정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a vessel: to roll so far on its side that it cannot recover; to capsize or turn turtle.
meanings.example: What a tiny little schooner! But is it not bold to spread both sails? And see, now that we have come round to the wind, how the skiff keels over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 작은 스쿠너군요! 하지만 양쪽 돛을 펼치는 것이 대담하지 않습니까? 그리고 바람을 타고 돌아오니, 작은 배가 어떻게 뒤집히는지 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch and pay attention to, in case of misbehaviour or mishap.
meanings.example: Could you keep an eye on my bag for a moment?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제 가방 좀 잠시 봐주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: bear in mind.
meanings.example: Keep in mind that he does not hear too well, so you may have to speak loudly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 청력이 좋지 않다는 것을 명심하세요, 그래서 큰 소리로 말해야 할 수도 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold (something) in the memory; to remember; also, to be mindful of or pay attention to (something); to consider; to note.
meanings.example: Please bear in mind that the deadline is tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마감일이 내일이라는 것을 명심해 주십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shy away from a daring task; to decline, refuse, or avoid something due to fear or uncertainty.
meanings.example: We almost convinced his dad to ride the roller coaster, but he chickened out when he saw how high it went.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아빠를 롤러코스터 타게 거의 설득했지만, 얼마나 높은지 보고는 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel overwhelming sorrow, jealousy, or longing; to grieve.
meanings.example: The Brazilians are eating their hearts out over their defeat by Germany in the World Cup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 브라질 사람들은 월드컵에서 독일에 패배한 것에 대해 몹시 슬퍼하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persist or continue.
meanings.example: Keep on trying, and you will succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 노력하면 성공할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate, or make extinct.
meanings.example: We killed off the Dodo by over-hunting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 과도한 사냥으로 도도새를 멸종시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become out by hitting a foul ball which is caught.
meanings.example: He fouled out to lead off the third.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 3회 선두 타자로 파울로 아웃되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An instance of fouling out.
meanings.example: A foul out was recorded on the scorecard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스코어 카드에 파울 아웃이 기록되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit or collide with
meanings.example: The strong wind knocked down the old tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강한 바람이 오래된 나무를 쓰러뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die.
meanings.example: After a long battle with cancer, the professor passed away yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암과의 오랜 투병 끝에 그 교수는 어제 돌아가셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the focal length of a zoom lens in order to obtain a magnified image, or a close-up.
meanings.example: The camera operator will zoom in on the subject for a close-up shot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카메라맨이 클로즈업을 위해 피사체에 줌인할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease the focal length of a zoom lens in order to obtain a reduced magnification of the image, or a wider-angle view.
meanings.example: The director asked to zoom out for a wider shot of the landscape.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 풍경을 더 넓게 잡기 위해 줌아웃하라고 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a conversation or situation in which one is not welcome or invited; to interject (connotes a rude or discourteous interruption).
meanings.example: Hey, this is none of your business; don’t butt in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이봐, 이건 네가 상관할 일이 아니야. 끼어들지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To put together, fabricate, or assemble, particularly if done hastily or temporarily. from 16th c.
meanings.example: He managed to knock up a simple shelter before the rain started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비가 오기 전에 간단한 임시 거처를 급히 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply oneself to study, or a task or work; to focus on or take a serious attitude towards something; to put forth the needed effort.
meanings.example: It's time to buckle down and finish this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트를 마무리하기 위해 본격적으로 착수할 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce in large volumes mechanically or as if by machine.
meanings.example: The factory can crank out thousands of cars a day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 하루에 수천 대의 자동차를 대량 생산할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop sleeping; to awake.
meanings.example: I usually wake up at 7 AM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 보통 아침 7시에 일어난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To parry, or turn aside.
meanings.example: He raised his arms to ward off the attack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공격을 막기 위해 팔을 들어 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become warmer.
meanings.example: He came inside to warm up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 안으로 들어와 몸을 녹였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To constitute, to compose.
meanings.example: Water makes up 70% of the Earth's surface.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물은 지구 표면의 70%를 구성한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: makeup.
meanings.example: She bought some new makeup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 화장품을 몇 개 샀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vent; to talk or take action (especially in a ranting or unrestrained way) so as to relieve stress.
meanings.example: After a stressful day, he went for a run to blow off steam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스트레스 받는 하루를 보낸 후, 그는 스트레스를 풀기 위해 달리기를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete; to create details from a basic outline, structure or skeleton.
meanings.example: Let's flesh out this proposal with more specific details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 제안서를 더 구체적인 세부 사항으로 구체화해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by pulling.
meanings.example: Pull off old blossoms so that the plant will keep flowering.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식물이 계속 꽃을 피우도록 오래된 꽃을 제거하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rob at gunpoint.
meanings.example: I think they intend to stick up the bank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 은행을 강탈할 의도인 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash the inside of something.
meanings.example: We must wash out the bins; they are full of maggots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쓰레기통 안을 씻어내야 해요. 구더기로 가득 차 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose energy or become weak and feeble.
meanings.example: After the illness, he began to waste away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 병을 앓은 후 그는 쇠약해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To guard and protect.
meanings.example: She stayed up all night to watch over the sick infant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아픈 아기를 지키기 위해 밤새 깨어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To outdo, to perform something slightly better than a competitor's prior effort.
meanings.example: Every year the neighbors try to one-up each other with their holiday lights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매년 이웃들은 휴일 조명으로 서로를 능가하려고 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An advantage over someone.
meanings.example: WePay wants to get one-up in the mobile payments race with its new iOS app for small businesses.
meanings.exampleKorean: WePay는 소상공인을 위한 새로운 iOS 앱으로 모바일 결제 시장에서 우위를 점하고자 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place quickly or briefly.
meanings.example: I have to dash off to the store, but I'll be back soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가게에 급히 가봐야 하지만 곧 돌아올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay, linger or loiter.
meanings.example: If you hang around after the show, you can meet the cast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공연 후에 주변에 머물면 출연진을 만날 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak or act in defence (of); to defend or protect.
meanings.example: You really need to stick up for yourself against that bully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 괴롭힘에 맞서 자신을 지지해야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: chillout.
meanings.example: He needs to chill out and stop worrying so much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진정하고 너무 걱정하는 것을 멈춰야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Rest To relax or take time out; to calm down; reduce or go easy on something.
meanings.example: Just chill out, everything will be fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 진정해, 모든 것이 잘 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: to diminish in effect
meanings.example: The effect of the injection will gradually wear off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주사의 효과는 점차 사라질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (something) to become damaged, useless, or ineffective through continued use, especially hard, heavy, or careless use.
meanings.example: If you wear out your shoes, you'll have to buy new ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신발이 닳으면 새 신발을 사야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To misbehave; to cause trouble.
meanings.example: My car is acting up again; it won't start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 차가 또 말썽이야; 시동이 걸리지 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dilute with water; to add water to.
meanings.example: You need to water down the lemonade a bit more to make it less sweet, dear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아가야, 레모네이드를 좀 더 희석해서 덜 달게 만들어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attribute or account for something; to ascribe.
meanings.example: He chalked up his success to hard work and a bit of luck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 성공을 노력과 약간의 운 덕분이라고 설명했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep outdoors, often in a tent.
meanings.example: We decided to camp out in the backyard for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 밤을 보내기 위해 뒷마당에서 야영하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To list or recite quickly.
meanings.example: When I suggested it, he promptly rattled off a dozen reasons that it wouldn't work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그것을 제안하자, 그는 즉시 그것이 작동하지 않을 것이라는 12가지 이유를 줄줄 읊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go away.
meanings.example: Why don't you just sod off and leave me alone?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 꺼져서 날 내버려 두는 게 어때?

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (unwanted elements) from a group.
meanings.example: To weed out problem users, watch new people's behavior.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제를 일으키는 사용자를 제거하기 위해 신규 사용자의 행동을 주시하라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce a large quantity of (something) rapidly and easily.
meanings.example: The factory churns out thousands of toys every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 매일 수천 개의 장난감을 찍어낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to attach with nails
meanings.example: Nail down the shelf, then paint it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선반을 못으로 고정한 다음 페인트를 칠하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attach or secure with pins.
meanings.example: It's hard to pin down the exact cause of the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제의 정확한 원인을 알아내기 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reverse (a vehicle) from a confined space.
meanings.example: He had to back out of the driveway slowly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차고에서 천천히 후진해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to bed; to go to sleep (especially at, or around midnight).
meanings.example: I'm about ready to turn into a pumpkin. You can stay up later if you want.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이제 자러 갈 준비가 되었다. 원하면 더 오래 깨어 있어도 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accompany, join, or follow; to go with.
meanings.example: If you ever visit the bamboo gardens, I'd like to tag along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대나무 정원을 방문하게 되면, 나도 따라가고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell an obvious untruth.
meanings.example: When a politician claims he can lower taxes without cutting spending, he's lying through his teeth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치인이 지출을 줄이지 않고 세금을 낮출 수 있다고 주장할 때, 그는 뻔뻔하게 거짓말을 하고 있는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: prepositional up
meanings.example: The construction crew had to blow up the old building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건설팀은 낡은 건물을 폭파해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose with a fence.
meanings.example: If we fence in that field, it will be a good pasture for a horse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밭에 울타리를 치면 말에게 좋은 목초지가 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to drink rapidly, v.t. To drink up rapidly.
meanings.example: He tossed off a glass of water and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 단숨에 마시고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone, gain someone's support, or make someone understand the truth or validity of something.
meanings.example: She managed to win over the skeptical committee members.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 회의적인 위원들을 설득하는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply mental or physical effort to (something) in order to shape, form, improve, investigate, solve it, etc.
meanings.example: John's in the studio working on the plans for the new house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존은 새 집 계획을 작업하기 위해 스튜디오에 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover or enclose (something) by folding and securing a covering entirely around it.
meanings.example: He wrapped up the parcel with brown paper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 갈색 종이로 소포를 포장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: I can't write down in the basement; it's cold there and my fingers cramp and get stiff.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지하에서는 글씨를 쓸 수 없어; 거기는 춥고 손가락이 꼬이고 뻣뻣해져.

meanings.englishDefinition: To open an office or business, especially in a profession.
meanings.example: After getting his law degree, he hung out his shingle in a small town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 법학 학위를 받은 후, 그는 작은 마을에서 개업했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To atrophy, or waste away.
meanings.example: Without sunlight and water, the plant will wither away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 햇빛과 물이 없으면 식물은 시들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach or come close to something; to border or be on the edge of something.
meanings.example: His actions verge on insanity, making everyone nervous.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 행동은 거의 광기에 가까워 모두를 불안하게 만든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat (some measure or candidate) by a majority vote.
meanings.example: His wife voted down the project the moment he suggested it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 아내는 그가 제안하자마자 그 프로젝트를 투표로 부결시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: Hurry up and get in the car!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러 차에 타!

meanings.englishDefinition: An exclamation of joy at one's success.
meanings.example: I passed the exam! Get in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격했어! 해냈다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume a large amount of carbohydrates, ostensibly for energy; generally a practice of athletes, especially runners and swimmers.
meanings.example: Let's have a spaghetti feed to carb up before the big meet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중요한 경기를 앞두고 에너지를 얻기 위해 스파게티를 잔뜩 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relinquish control or possession of something to someone.
meanings.example: I handed over the controls to the copilot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 부조종사에게 조종간을 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to brace the ship's yards on the opposite tack when going about
meanings.koreanDefinition: 항해 용어: 선회 시 돛대를 반대 방향으로 돛대받이하다
meanings.example: The sailor had to brace about the yards as the ship turned.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 방향을 틀 때 선원은 돛대를 반대 방향으로 돛대받이해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to bring the yards more square to the fore and aft line
meanings.example: The sailors braced in the yards to prepare for the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들은 폭풍에 대비하기 위해 돛대를 안으로 당겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To number off (a group of soldiers); to divide up (soldiers) in this way. from 18th c.
meanings.example: The sergeant told off the soldiers for their sloppy appearance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하사는 군인들의 단정한 외모에 대해 번호를 매겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erode gradually and progressively.
meanings.example: the sensitiveness which greeted my new position wore away, I began to rather like the freedom of my new character.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 직책에 대한 민감함이 점차 사라졌고, 나는 새로운 나의 자유를 오히려 좋아하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take further actions remaining after an event; to continue, revisit, or persist; especially, to maintain communication or verify.
meanings.example: Send a letter, then follow up promptly with a telephone call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편지를 보내고 즉시 전화로 후속 조치를 취하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease clinging to life; to die.
meanings.example: The old car finally gave up the ghost on the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 차는 마침내 고속도로에서 멈춰 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very angry with; to have a grudge against; to intend revenge against.
meanings.example: My boss must really have it in for me; that was the third lecture this week!
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장님이 나에게 정말 앙심을 품었음에 틀림없어. 이번 주에만 세 번째 꾸지람이야!

meanings.englishDefinition: off
meanings.example: He told the annoying stranger to fuck off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 귀찮게 하는 낯선 사람에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go away! Get lost!
meanings.example: If you'd tell me to fuck off, I'd have more respect for you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나에게 꺼지라고 말했다면 더 존중했을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a great effort; to take extraordinary care; to go to great lengths.
meanings.example: They bent over backwards to make sure everything was just right for the visit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 방문을 위해 모든 것이 완벽하도록 최대한 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hear rumors; to learn through hearsay.
meanings.example: I heard through the grapevine that she likes him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 그를 좋아한다는 소문을 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be missed; to escape the necessary notice or attention.
meanings.example: Some important details fell through the cracks during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 중에 몇 가지 중요한 세부 사항이 간과되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh.
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes made the audience crack up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코미디언의 농담에 관객들은 폭소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Funny; hilarious
meanings.example: That joke was crack up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 농담은 정말 웃겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To square the ship's yards to make her run before the wind, with the wind blowing straight from the stern.
meanings.example: The captain ordered the crew to square away the yards before the storm hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선장은 폭풍이 닥치기 전에 돛대를 바람에 맞추라고 선원들에게 명령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus one's aim; to zoom in and center (on something).
meanings.example: The pilot zeroed in on the bunker and launched a guided missile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조종사는 벙커에 조준하고 유도 미사일을 발사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn over (soil, hay, etc.) with a gardening fork.
meanings.example: I forked over half the allotment this morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 오늘 아침 밭의 절반을 갈아엎었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax; to laze.
meanings.example: After a long week, I just want to kick back and watch TV.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 한 주를 보낸 후, 나는 그냥 쉬면서 TV를 보고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: kickback.
meanings.example: The politician was accused of accepting kickbacks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 리베이트를 받은 혐의로 기소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Praise, congratulations.
meanings.example: Give yourself a pat on the back for a job well done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잘한 일에 대해 스스로에게 칭찬을 해줘라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise or congratulate.
meanings.example: Don't pat yourself on the back so vigorously; you might hurt yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 심하게 스스로를 칭찬하지 마, 다칠지도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: wrist-slap#Verb.
meanings.example: The judge gave the minor offender a slap on the wrist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 경미한 범죄자에게 가벼운 처벌을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: wrist-slap#Noun.
meanings.example: He got a slap on the wrist, but I don't think he'll reform.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가벼운 처벌을 받았지만, 개과천선할 것 같지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on, trust, or expect.
meanings.example: Don't count on being able to get back into the building after 5pm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후 5시 이후에는 건물에 다시 들어갈 수 있을 거라고 기대하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reform; to improve one's habits.
meanings.example: Someday I'm going to clean up my act and start researching more carefully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언젠가 나는 내 행실을 바로잡고 더 신중하게 조사하기 시작할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rip-off.
meanings.example: That price is a total rip-off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가격은 완전 바가지야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull off by ripping.
meanings.example: I can't believe how the car dealerships try to rip off their customers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차 대리점들이 고객들에게 어떻게 바가지를 씌우려고 하는지 믿을 수가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit; to give up.
meanings.example: If their restaurant can't get business even on Mother's Day, it might be time for them to throw in the towel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니날에도 장사가 안 된다면, 그들의 식당은 포기할 때일지도 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive fast; to step on the accelerator.
meanings.example: If I step on it, I can still arrive on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 속도를 내면 아직 제시간에 도착할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blow on something causing it to topple.
meanings.example: The wind blew over the pole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 기둥을 불어 넘어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To board or mount (something), especially a vehicle.
meanings.example: Please get on the bus as quickly as possible.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가능한 한 빨리 버스에 타세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Expresses surprise or disbelief.
meanings.example: Get on! You can't be serious!
meanings.exampleKorean: 말도 안 돼! 진심이 아니지!

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed with; to begin or continue, especially after an interruption.
meanings.example: Turn off the television and get on with your homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 텔레비전을 끄고 숙제를 계속하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hunt for or locate; to search for; to find.
meanings.example: I need to track down a computer so I can check my e-mails.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이메일을 확인하기 위해 컴퓨터를 찾아내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ruin (something) unintentionally; to fuck up, mess up, or screw up.
meanings.example: He cocked up the presentation by forgetting his notes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 메모를 잊어버려서 발표를 망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place by force or illicit means.
meanings.example: Someone broke in and stole his radio.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 침입해서 그의 라디오를 훔쳐갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: escape, especially by breaking barriers To escape, especially forcefully or defiantly.
meanings.example: The prisoners planned to break out of jail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄수들은 감옥에서 탈출할 계획을 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil someone's celebration.
meanings.example: I don't want to rain on your parade, but we need to discuss the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 흥을 깨고 싶지는 않지만, 예산에 대해 논의해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand; to begin to understand; to realize.
meanings.example: It took him a while to catch on to the joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그 농담을 이해하는 데는 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A blessing or expression of hope that a deceased person is at peace.
meanings.example: We held a moment of silence so his soul could rest in peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그의 영혼이 편히 쉴 수 있도록 잠시 묵념의 시간을 가졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have passed away, or to have died.
meanings.example: 1882, Nicholas Patrick Wiseman, The Dublin Review, volume 90, page 156: The late Cardinal rests in peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고 니콜라스 패트릭 와이즈먼 추기경은 고이 잠들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unleash one's anger on a person or thing other than the one that caused it. look for a dog to kick
meanings.example: Don't take it out on your husband if you had trouble with your boss at work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장에서 상사 때문에 힘든 일이 있었다고 남편에게 화풀이하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk; to join in conversation or discussion.
meanings.example: We appreciate your input, so please don't hesitate to chime in with comments and questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러분의 의견을 소중히 여기니, 질문이나 코멘트를 자유롭게 해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure the release of an arrested person by providing bail money.
meanings.example: But it passed, leaving us up to our knees in water. 'Bail out! bail out!' shouted Job, suiting the action to the word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 물이 무릎까지 차올랐다. '탈출해! 탈출해!'라고 욥이 말하며 행동으로 옮겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strengthen or reinforce; to add substance to. 1941
meanings.example: We need to beef up security around the airport.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 공항 주변의 보안을 강화할 필요가 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Common sense.
meanings.example: That eejit has no cop on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 바보에게는 상식이 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop behaving immaturely; behave, grow up.
meanings.example: You'll get in trouble with the boss if you don't cop on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정신 차리지 않으면 상사에게 혼날 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reserve nothing; to put forth all possible effort or resources.
meanings.example: They decided to go all out for the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결승전을 위해 총력을 기울이기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take (something) as one's major field of study in an institute of tertiary education (such as a college or university).
meanings.example: She decided to major in computer science at university.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대학에서 컴퓨터 과학을 전공하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To artificially maintain an outward sign of normality; to maintain an appearance of wealth, comfort or propriety.
meanings.example: Despite their financial problems, they tried to keep up appearances.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재정적인 어려움에도 불구하고 그들은 겉모습을 유지하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect or gather (something) together.
meanings.example: The city hall needs to round up all the wrongly parked bikes across the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시청은 도시 전역의 잘못 주차된 자전거들을 모두 수거해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To soil or dirty; to mess up; to clog.
meanings.example: Grease can gunk up the pipes if you're not careful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심하지 않으면 기름이 파이프를 막히게 할 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To round (a number) to the greatest integer that is not greater than it, or to some other lower value, especially a whole number of hundreds, thousands, etc.
meanings.example: Round down 3.7 to the nearest whole number, which is 3.
meanings.exampleKorean: 3.7을 가장 가까운 정수인 3으로 내림하여 반올림하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mess of; to untidy, disorder, soil, or muss.
meanings.example: The afternoon breeze messed up my hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후의 산들바람이 내 머리를 헝클어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet, to gather together, to congregate.
meanings.example: Let's get together for coffee next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 주에 커피 마시러 만나요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay (usually a bill, debt or due).
meanings.example: Everyone had to pony up their share of the expenses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두가 경비에 대한 각자의 몫을 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a rude or obscene gesture at someone.
meanings.example: Would you believe he flipped me off over that comment?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그 말 때문에 내게 손가락 욕을 했다고 믿으시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very angry or enraged; to throw a fit or go crazy.
meanings.example: My dad flew off the handle when he saw the F grade on my report card.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지께서 내 성적표의 F 학점을 보시고는 갑자기 격분하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something secret.
meanings.example: It's supposed to be a surprise, so try to keep a lid on it, will you?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이건 비밀인데, 그러니까 발설하지 않도록 노력해 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake.
meanings.example: If I go near a skateboard, I'm sure I'm just going to goof up and fall off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스케이트보드 근처에 가면 분명 실수해서 떨어질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interact or coexist well, without argument or trouble.
meanings.example: I wish the kids would get along better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 좀 더 잘 지냈으면 좋겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a controlling influence over.
meanings.example: The company managed to get its claws into the local market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 지역 시장에 대한 영향력을 행사하는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive something away with blows or military force.
meanings.example: ...which Action did not so much grieve the English, as trouble and vex the Picts and Scots, who were incessantly gauled and frequently beat off by these Danish Troops.
meanings.exampleKorean: ...이 행동은 영국인들을 그다지 슬프게 하지 않았지만, 덴마크 군대에 끊임없이 괴롭힘당하고 자주 격퇴당했던 픽트족과 스코틀랜드족을 괴롭히고 성가시게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confess to something; to admit something.
meanings.example: He finally fessed up to stealing the cookies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 쿠키를 훔친 것을 자백했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disable a section of source code by converting it into a comment.
meanings.example: If you do not want to run this line of code, just comment it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 코드 줄을 실행하고 싶지 않다면, 그냥 주석 처리하세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: She felt pent-up frustration after the long meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 회의 후에 그녀는 답답한 좌절감을 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume a horizontal position.
meanings.example: I'm going to lie down for a bit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐 누워 있을 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expand in the manner of branches.
meanings.example: The chart starts in the center and branches out from there.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차트는 중앙에서 시작하여 거기서부터 가지를 뻗어 나갑니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce in volume by boiling.
meanings.example: He boiled the soup down so it wouldn't be so weak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수프가 너무 묽지 않도록 졸였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach by moving down or reducing.
meanings.example: Wait for the temperature to come down to a reasonable level before touching the lid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뚜껑을 만지기 전에 온도가 적절한 수준으로 내려갈 때까지 기다리세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To insult (someone) by showing them the back of one's fist with the middle finger extended.
meanings.example: He flicked me off when I cut him off in traffic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내가 차선 변경으로 끼어들자 가운데 손가락을 세워 모욕했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to (someone) in a friendly, open, or casual manner, sometimes also in a charming or affected manner, usually to curry favor, and sometimes flirtatiously with the intention of establishing a romantic or sexual encounter or relationship with that person.
meanings.example: Have you been chatting up my girlfriend?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 여자친구한테 말 걸었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To bite; to bite into.
meanings.example: yanked her head over her shoulder and, taking her cue from the rat in the laboratory, she sank her teeth into Burt’s arm as hard as she could.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 머리를 어깨 너머로 젖히고 실험실의 쥐를 본떠 버트의 팔을 있는 힘껏 물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve; to correct one's bad habits or behavior.
meanings.example: He'd better shape up soon, or he'll fail the class.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 곧 태도를 바로잡지 않으면 수업에 낙제할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape notice or lack sufficient attention.
meanings.example: Many important details slipped through the cracks during the rushed project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘렀던 프로젝트 동안 많은 중요한 세부 사항들이 간과되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agree by shaking hands; to close a deal.
meanings.example: I'll bring the contract tomorrow, and if everything looks good, we'll shake on it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 계약서를 가져올게요. 모든 것이 좋으면 악수해서 합의합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adopt a position of noninvolvement.
meanings.example: The new chairman is happy to take a back seat when it comes to day-to-day operations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신임 회장은 일상 업무에 있어서는 뒤로 물러나기를 기꺼이 받아들인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abolish; to put an end to; to eliminate.
meanings.example: She tried to do away with the argument by changing the topic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 화제를 바꿔 논쟁을 없애려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (something) quickly.
meanings.example: He scarfed down the entire pizza in under five minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 5분도 안 되어 피자 한 판을 게걸스럽게 먹어 치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tighten or secure with screws.
meanings.example: Make sure you screw up the lid tightly so it doesn't leak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새지 않도록 뚜껑을 단단히 조여야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fool around.
meanings.example: Stop goofing around and get back to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일로 돌아가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To paint, or make clean or bright with a brush.
meanings.example: April 10, 1706, Alexander Pope, letter to William Wycherley
meanings.exampleKorean: 1706년 4월 10일, 알렉산더 포프, 윌리엄 와이처리에게 보낸 편지

meanings.englishDefinition: To take harsh action (against), as when enforcing a law more stringently than before.
meanings.example: The police are cracking down on speeding in this area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 이 지역에서 과속 단속을 강화하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To occur to somebody; to be realized by.
meanings.example: It slowly dawned on me that I had made a terrible mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 끔찍한 실수를 저질렀다는 것을 서서히 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel from the lungs, throat, stomach, etc. by coughing.
meanings.example: He was coughing up blood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 피를 토하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill or refill (a drink etc.) so that the container is completely full.
meanings.example: Could you top off my drink, please?
meanings.exampleKorean: 음료를 다시 채워주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To emphasize or underscore; to make something even better or worse.
meanings.example: The unexpected win topped it off, making it the best day of his life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 승리가 이를 마무리하며 그의 인생 최고의 날로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decline to participate; particularly, to decline to dance.
meanings.example: I'm going to sit out this round of the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 게임 라운드는 쉬겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sitout.
meanings.example: During seven NAACP-sponsored school “sit outs” or “holidays” in Jacksonville more than 108,000 children had been kept home by their parents to protest segregated schools and other inequities.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잭슨빌에서 NAACP가 후원한 7번의 학교 “결석” 또는 “휴일” 동안 108,000명 이상의 어린이들이 분리된 학교와 다른 불평등에 항의하기 위해 부모에 의해 집에 머물렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To monitor; to keep track of; to watch.
meanings.example: If you are careful to keep tabs on your finances, you should be able to stay within a budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재정을 주의 깊게 주시하면 예산 내에서 생활할 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a great time; to party or revel whole-heartedly or noisily.
meanings.example: They all went out to whoop it up on his 21st birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 모두 그의 21번째 생일을 맞아 신나게 놀러 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: The moon blotted out the sun and all was dark.
meanings.exampleKorean: 달이 해를 가리자 모든 것이 어두워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to begin again; to return to the beginning
meanings.example: I forgot to save my work, and I had to start over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작업 저장을 잊어서 다시 시작해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliberately exaggerate one's emotions or movements, or to overact or act hammily.
meanings.example: The comedian really hammed it up, making the audience roar with laughter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언은 정말 과장되게 행동해서 관객들을 웃음바다로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make slight corrections or adjustments to; to fill in or perfect.
meanings.example: Use a small brush to touch up the paint anywhere it is uneven.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 붓을 사용하여 고르지 않은 부분을 덧칠하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill; to replace material that is absent or has been removed.
meanings.example: After you're done laying the pipe, fill in the trench.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파이프를 설치한 후 도랑을 메우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin, especially to launch a performance of some sort.
meanings.example: Alright everyone, let's take it away with the first song!
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 여러분, 첫 곡으로 시작합시다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To collapse or fall.
meanings.example: The old bridge finally gave in under the weight of the truck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 다리가 결국 트럭의 무게를 이기지 못하고 무너졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pick up suddenly.
meanings.example: The car caught up to us quickly on the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 고속도로에서 우리를 빠르게 따라잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: catch-up.
meanings.example: We need a catch-up meeting to discuss the project's progress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트 진행 상황을 논의하기 위한 따라잡기 회의가 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erect the initial walls and roof of (a new building).
meanings.example: The construction crew began to frame up the new house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건설팀이 새집의 골조를 세우기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lecture, berate, or complain to somebody, especially to find fault or criticize.
meanings.example: His boss was always getting on his case about his standards of dress, even though he worked well and seldom left the back room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일을 잘하고 좀처럼 사무실을 떠나지 않았음에도 불구하고, 그의 상사는 항상 그의 복장 기준에 대해 계속 나무랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become more compact by folding.
meanings.example: Please fold up these towels so they will fit on the shelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수건을 선반에 맞게 접어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To investigate, explore, or consider.
meanings.example: If you are buying a new car, you might want to look into getting a hybrid or other high-efficiency vehicle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 차를 산다면 하이브리드나 다른 고효율 차량을 알아보는 것이 좋을 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get away, or escape, taking (something) with one.
meanings.example: The thieves got away with a million pounds' worth of jewellery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들은 백만 파운드 상당의 보석을 가지고 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be punished or chastised for doing something prohibited.
meanings.example: You'll get us all into trouble if you don't keep quiet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조용히 하지 않으면 우리 모두 곤경에 처할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill or end; to defeat.
meanings.example: The assassin was hired to do in the politician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암살자는 그 정치인을 제거하기 위해 고용되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten with a buckle.
meanings.example: It was eight o'clock to-morrow evening when I buckled up my travelling writing-desk in its leather case, paid my Bill, and got on my warm coats and wrappers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가죽 케이스에 여행용 책상을 버클로 채우고, 계산을 하고, 따뜻한 코트와 겉옷을 입었을 때가 다음 날 저녁 8시였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport away.
meanings.example: I need a truck to carry off all this furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 가구를 옮기려면 트럭이 필요해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay money, to disburse; especially, to pay a great deal of money.
meanings.example: We had to shell out a lot of money for the repairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수리비로 많은 돈을 지불해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A game played on a billiard table, a variation of pool.
meanings.example: He is skilled at playing shell out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 쉘 아웃 게임을 잘한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To censor or cover up by writing over with black ink.
meanings.example: The reporter had to black out the sensitive information in the document.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 문서의 민감한 정보를 까맣게 칠해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work through the details of something; especially to work through difficulties.
meanings.example: They stayed up late hashing out the details of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계약 세부 사항을 논의하느라 밤늦게까지 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) or to become less serious and more cheerful or casual; to relax.
meanings.example: You need to lighten up and stop worrying so much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좀 느긋해지고 너무 걱정하는 것을 멈출 필요가 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make straight.
meanings.example: Please straighten out this wrinkled shirt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 구겨진 셔츠를 똑바로 펴주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: absorb To absorb (fluid).
meanings.example: The dry soil sucked up water like a sponge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마른 흙이 스펀지처럼 물을 빨아들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean up (liquid) with a mop, rag, sponge, or other cleaning device.
meanings.example: Please mop up the spilled milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쏟아진 우유를 닦아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assemble something quickly.
meanings.example: We can knock together a quick meal before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님이 오기 전에 간단한 식사를 급히 만들 수 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay an exorbitant or excessive amount, either in money or in some other manner. from 17th c.
meanings.example: We had to pay through the nose for those concert tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 콘서트 티켓을 사느라 엄청난 돈을 지불해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquire, bring in, or introduce.
meanings.example: The ship took on cargo in Norfolk yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 어제 노퍽에서 짐을 실었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: around
meanings.example: She tiptoed around the sleeping baby.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자고 있는 아기 주위를 살금살금 다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more upright
meanings.example: The dog perked up when he heard the word 'walk'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개가 '산책'이라는 말을 듣고 귀를 쫑긋 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to an accounting; to examine and strike the balance of debt and credit; reckon.
meanings.example: He was very careful to cast up his accounts every quarter ; which he did, that he might spend neither more nor less than what he could spend every year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매 분기마다 장부를 정리하는 데 매우 신중했다. 그렇게 함으로써 그는 매년 쓸 수 있는 금액보다 더 많이 또는 더 적게 쓰지 않으려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retrace one's steps; to go back where one has already gone.
meanings.example: They doubled back in hopes of finding the road again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 길을 다시 찾기를 바라며 되돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confuse; especially, to lose a pursuer.
meanings.example: I never saw her without glasses before, so it threw me off when she got contact lenses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 콘택트렌즈를 낀 것을 보니 이전에는 안경 없이는 본 적이 없어서 나를 혼란스럽게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw-off.
meanings.example: Throw off points were first introduced in England, and are used very generally at coal sidings, private sidings etc.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방해점은 영국에서 처음 도입되었으며 석탄 선로, 사설 선로 등에서 매우 일반적으로 사용된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: arse around.
meanings.example: Stop arsing about and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: The wrong way round; exactly opposite to that which is desirable; contrary; conceptually inverted; wrong.
meanings.example: You've got this arse-about!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이거 거꾸로 됐잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To alert or inform (someone), especially confidentially.
meanings.example: An anonymous caller tipped off the police that the suspect would be in the area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 익명의 제보자가 용의자가 그 지역에 있을 것이라고 경찰에 밀고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Good, superior, excellent.
meanings.example: He's doing a bang up job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 훌륭한 일을 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To damage.
meanings.example: He banged up his new car last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어젯밤 새 차를 들이받아 망가뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass (the ball) to a teammate.
meanings.example: The quarterback handed off the ball to the running back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쿼터백은 러닝백에게 공을 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (food) on to a dish ready for eating.
meanings.example: She can dish out criticism but she can't take it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 비판을 잘 하지만 자신은 받아들이지 못한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slack off; to be lazy or a parasite on others.
meanings.example: Stop lousing around and find a job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일자리를 찾아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mess up; to confuse; to put into a state of disorder.
meanings.example: To be loused up by Humbolt was really a kind of privilege. It was like being the subject of a two-nosed portrait by Picasso, or an eviscerated chicken by Soutine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 훔볼트에 의해 망가지는 것은 정말 특권과도 같았다. 그것은 피카소의 두 코 그림이나 수틴의 내장 적출된 닭의 대상이 되는 것과 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure with rope, string etc.
meanings.example: Don't forget to tie up your hair before you bake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 베이킹하기 전에 머리를 묶는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait; to stop pursuing, stop fighting, or relax from a heightened readiness posture.
meanings.example: The soldiers were ordered to stand down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들에게 해산하라는 명령이 내려졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain, acquire or get and keep hold of something.
meanings.example: They latched onto the idea and gave it up only reluctantly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 아이디어를 붙잡았고 마지못해서야 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume, deplete, expend, or exhaust.
meanings.example: We used up all the milk, so we need to buy more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우유를 다 써버려서 더 사야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To announce the arrival of something; to herald.
meanings.example: The new technology will usher in an era of unprecedented growth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기술은 전례 없는 성장의 시대를 열 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end up with a mild punishment, or without serious harm.
meanings.example: My cousin got off lightly with a restraining order after he broke the boy’s leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 사촌은 그 소년의 다리를 부러뜨린 후에도 접근 금지 명령만 받고 가볍게 넘어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring to a conclusion.
meanings.example: The band usually ends up their concerts with their biggest hit song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드는 보통 가장 히트한 곡으로 콘서트를 마무리한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid or shirk, either by failing to perform, or by performing in a grossly insufficient, negligent, or superficial manner.
meanings.example: He tried to cop out of his responsibilities by pretending to be sick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아픈 척하며 자신의 책임을 회피하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually subside or diminish.
meanings.example: The crowd's cheers began to tail off as the game ended.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기가 끝나자 군중의 환호성이 점점 줄어들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually subside or diminish.
