meanings.example: The sound of the music began to tail away in the distance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 멀어지면서 점점 희미해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something and put it in a different place.
meanings.example: Mother took our plates away and came back with some fruit for us to eat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니는 우리 접시를 치우고 과일을 가져다주셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: minus
meanings.example: Two take away two equals zero.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2 빼기 2는 0이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: takeaway.
meanings.example: I'll just get a takeaway tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤은 그냥 포장해 올게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cope with something unfortunate without much effort; to accept or manage difficulties well.
meanings.example: He took it in stride when they attempted to ostracize him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그를 따돌리려 했지만 그는 태연하게 대처했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate and recover (valuable minerals) by swirling dirt or crushed rock in a pan of water, in the manner of a traditional prospector seeking gold.
meanings.example: The prospector began to pan out the riverbed for gold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 탐광자는 금을 찾기 위해 강바닥을 체로 치기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retract or withdraw (an earlier statement).
meanings.example: He had to take back his words after he realized he was wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자신이 틀렸다는 것을 깨달은 후 그는 자신의 말을 취소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume control of something, such as a business or enterprise, and sometimes by force.
meanings.example: Yard, the Lynton & Barnstaple headquarters, has been taken over by a fur trading firm, and would-be trespassers to the old engine-shed are turned back by the pungent odour of heaps of carcases.
meanings.exampleKorean: 린턴 & 반스태플 본사인 야드는 모피 무역 회사에 인수되었고, 옛 엔진 창고에 무단 침입하려는 사람들은 쌓인 시체 더미의 강한 악취 때문에 되돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss; to converse about a matter or issue.
meanings.example: Let's talk over the details of the project tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 프로젝트 세부 사항에 대해 상의합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone by spoken arguments.
meanings.example: She tried to talk around her parents into letting her go to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부모님을 설득해서 파티에 가게 하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less excited, intense, or angry.
meanings.example: Calm down before you hurt somebody.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가를 다치게 하기 전에 진정해.

meanings.englishDefinition: : To take a premature or ill-considered action.
meanings.example: Make sure none of your men go off half-cocked and ruin this operation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부하들이 성급하게 행동해서 작전을 망치지 않도록 확실히 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: Their plans to go hiking Saturday fell through because it rained.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토요일 등산 계획은 비가 와서 무산되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To associate with another in a joint enterprise.
meanings.example: The ice cream shop teamed up with the cookie and brownie place next door to create wonderful sundaes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이스크림 가게는 옆집 쿠키와 브라우니 가게와 협력하여 멋진 선데이를 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run quickly along somewhere.
meanings.example: The children were tearing along the beach, laughing with joy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 즐겁게 웃으며 해변을 쏜살같이 달리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (oneself or another person), overcoming that person's reluctance to be so removed.
meanings.example: I couldn't tear myself away from the movie after I had begun watching it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화를 보기 시작한 후에는 도저히 벗어날 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supplement.
meanings.example: The old man eked out his pension by selling vegetables from his garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 노인은 밭에서 채소를 팔아 연금을 힘겹게 보충했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record the departure or withdrawal of someone or something (such as guests, employees, books, etc.).
meanings.example: He checked his favorite mystery out for the twenty-third time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가장 좋아하는 추리 소설을 23번째로 빌려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or arrange smartly, elegantly, and neatly; to smarten.
meanings.example: Fresh paint and some new curtains would do a lot to spruce up this dark room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 페인트칠과 새 커튼은 이 어두운 방을 멋지게 만드는 데 큰 도움이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove.
meanings.example: He took out a handkerchief to wipe his brow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이마를 닦기 위해 손수건을 꺼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: takeout.
meanings.example: Let's order a pizza for takeout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피자를 포장해 주문하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To uplift, hearten, inspire or raise the spirits of.
meanings.example: Good news can buoy up people's spirits during difficult times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 소식은 어려운 시기에 사람들의 기운을 북돋아 줄 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To occur, especially suddenly or unexpectedly.
meanings.example: We'll finish tonight if no problems crop up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제가 발생하지 않으면 오늘 밤에 끝낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch a location or people, generally covertly.
meanings.example: The police decided to stake out the suspect's house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자의 집을 감시하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lash out; to strike or hit at someone or something, particularly something in arm's length of the striker and at or near the level of the striker's head.
meanings.example: The boxer struck out wildly, missing his opponent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 맹렬하게 공격했지만 상대방을 놓쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: wind-up.
meanings.example: The wind-up of the meeting was quite abrupt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의의 마무리는 상당히 갑작스러웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wind (rope, string, mainsprings, etc.) completely.
meanings.example: He wound up the clock before going to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기 전에 시계의 태엽을 감았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover, obscure, or overwhelm by being louder or more intense than.
meanings.example: The music was so loud it drowned out the conversation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 너무 커서 대화 소리를 덮어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover, obscure, or overwhelm by being louder or more intense than.
meanings.example: His booming voice drowned out the other speakers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 우렁찬 목소리가 다른 연사들을 압도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid, to sack off, to kick into the long grass.
meanings.example: The politician decided to long off the difficult question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 어려운 질문을 회피하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: a fielding position on the off side, about 70° forward of square, designed to prevent runs from drives or to catch lofted drives
meanings.example: The batsman hit the ball towards the long off fielder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자는 공을 롱 오프 수비수 쪽으로 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To double the quantity, amount or duration of something
meanings.example: I'm going to double up my enlistment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 복무 기간을 두 배로 늘릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch for or look out for.
meanings.example: I think we're getting close, so keep an eye out for the next street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거의 다 온 것 같으니 다음 거리를 잘 봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persist or continue to the end.
meanings.example: Plenty of people begin the training, but few stick it out for the year or two necessary to become proficient.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 사람들이 훈련을 시작하지만, 능숙해지기 위해 필요한 1~2년을 버티는 사람은 거의 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) lower (especially of clothes).
meanings.example: She had to pull down her skirt after sitting on the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 잔디에 앉은 후 치마를 내려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demolish (a building).
meanings.example: They plan to pull down the old factory and build apartments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 공장을 철거하고 아파트를 지을 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To earn (an amount of money).
meanings.example: He pulls down a six-figure salary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여섯 자리 수의 연봉을 번다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A pitch which was intentionally thrown outside to the catcher who stands up with the pitch for the purpose of enabling the catcher to throw out a runner.
meanings.example: The pitch out enabled the catcher to throw out the runner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피치 아웃으로 포수는 주자를 잡아낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intentionally throw the ball outside to the catcher who stands up with the pitch for the purpose of enabling the catcher to throw out a runner
meanings.example: Jones pitched out twice to no avail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존스는 두 번이나 피치 아웃을 했지만 소용이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intentionally throw pitches which are slightly out of the strike zone, hoping that the batter will swing wildly at a pitch, but assuming that you will walk him
meanings.koreanDefinition: 볼넷을 주려고 공을 일부러 바깥쪽으로 던지다
meanings.example: Jones pitched around the slugger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존스는 강타자를 상대로 볼넷을 주려고 공을 바깥쪽으로 던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: The children are playing in the sandpit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 모래 놀이터에서 놀고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To participate in a game or match.
meanings.example: Our team will play in the finals next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 팀은 다음 주 결승전에 참가할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: also 'play upon' To exploit (a potential weakness or vulnerability) by exacerbating.
meanings.example: to play on someone's fears
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가의 두려움을 이용하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to perform or compete.
meanings.example: The band decided to play on despite the technical difficulties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드는 기술적인 어려움에도 불구하고 연주를 계속하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To worry about unfortunate events which have already happened and which cannot be changed.
meanings.example: 'Tis a Folly to cry for spilt Milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 엎질러진 우유를 두고 울어봤자 소용없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel by airplane to a destination.
meanings.example: I'm going to fly out to see you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너를 보러 비행기 타고 갈 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: An instance of flying out.
meanings.example: A fly out was recorded on the scorecard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스코어 카드에 플라이 아웃이 기록되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeatedly hit a person.
meanings.example: I'm going to punch out that SOB and leave him lying on the ground bleeding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개자식을 때려눕히고 피 흘리며 쓰러지게 할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clock out of work.
meanings.example: Don't forget to punch out before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근하기 전에 퇴근 기록하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: punchout#Noun.
meanings.example: The boxer's punchout sealed his victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서의 펀치 아웃은 그의 승리를 확정지었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce a total by adding.
meanings.example: We summed up the donations and found that we had just enough to pay the bills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 기부금을 합산하여 청구서를 지불하기에 충분하다는 것을 알아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To summarize.
meanings.example: Let me sum up the main points of the discussion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논의의 주요 요점들을 요약해 드리겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To forage for, or (figuratively) to quickly prepare, something.
meanings.example: I'll see if I can rustle up a meal before the guests start arriving in 30 minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 30분 안에 도착하기 전에 식사라도 급히 마련해 볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a soft, dry sound of movement.
meanings.example: The leaves rustled up as the wind blew through the trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 나무 사이를 불자 나뭇잎들이 바스락거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collapse inward or downward.
meanings.example: The roof of the old warehouse finally caved in after the heavy snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 창고의 지붕이 폭설 끝에 마침내 무너져 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To yield to pressure or force.
meanings.example: The government finally caved in to public demands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 결국 대중의 요구에 굴복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cave-in#Noun.
meanings.example: The cave-in trapped several miners.
meanings.exampleKorean: 붕괴로 인해 여러 광부가 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A holler back; a reply.
meanings.example: He gave a quick holla back to my question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 질문에 재빨리 응답했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply.
meanings.example: “Earth to Kimi, holla back,” Yvonne taunts after I miss another easy serve.
meanings.exampleKorean: “키미, 거기 있니, 대답 좀 해봐,” 이본이 내가 또 쉬운 서브를 놓치자 놀려댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with each other.
meanings.example: The twins slept together, the straw doll between them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쌍둥이는 짚인형을 사이에 두고 함께 잤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a bed or sleeping space.
meanings.example: My brother and I used to sleep together when we were kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어렸을 때 나는 형과 함께 잤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: I need to pull the splinter out of my hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손에서 가시를 빼내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw from a competition or agreement.
meanings.example: The country decided to pull out of the treaty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 나라는 조약에서 철수하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend or move outwards.
meanings.example: The train began to pull out of the station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 역에서 출발하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get a boost or a lift.
meanings.example: I can climb over the fence if I get a leg up from you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 도와주면 울타리를 넘을 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide assistance or support.
meanings.example: He got a leg up from his mentor to start his business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업을 시작하기 위해 멘토로부터 도움을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve something.
meanings.example: Some people are very good at working the system to their advantage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 사람들은 자신들에게 유리하도록 제도를 이용하는 데 아주 능숙하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attribute or ascribe something to someone or something.
meanings.example: The success was chalked up to hard work and dedication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성공은 노력과 헌신 덕분이라고 여겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decide; to reach a conclusion.
meanings.example: She couldn't make up her mind which dress to wear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어떤 드레스를 입을지 결정을 내릴 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore or disregard; to be indifferent to.
meanings.example: He shrugged off the insults and proceeded despite them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모욕을 무시하고 계속 진행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover quickly from an illness or setback.
meanings.example: She quickly shrugged off the cold and returned to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 감기를 금방 떨쳐내고 일터로 복귀했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute; to give out, either by hand or as if so (figuratively). from 17th c.
meanings.example: Please help me hand out these flyers to the visitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방문객들에게 이 전단지들을 나누어 주는 것을 도와주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute.
meanings.example: The teacher handed out the worksheets to the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님이 학생들에게 워크시트를 나눠주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: a player is out
meanings.example: The batter hit a fly ball and was caught out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자가 플라이볼을 쳤고 잡혀서 아웃되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw a curve ball.
meanings.example: The pitcher broke one off, and the batter swung and missed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투수가 커브를 던졌고, 타자는 휘둘렀지만 빗나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break off a piece of something.
meanings.example: He broke one off a piece of chocolate and gave it to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 초콜릿 조각을 부러뜨려 나에게 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to be ejected
meanings.example: We are going to force out intolerance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 불관용을 몰아낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compel someone to leave a position or job.
meanings.example: The scandal forced him out of his position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들로 인해 그는 직위에서 물러나야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An instance of an out created by touching a base in advance of a runner who has no base to return to while in possession of a ball which has already touched the ground.
meanings.example: A force out was recorded on the scorecard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스코어 카드에 포스 아웃이 기록되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon someone who is in need or in danger, especially a colleague or one dependent.
meanings.example: You shouldn't hang your partner out to dry when things get difficult.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 어려워질 때 파트너를 곤경에 빠지게 해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave someone in a difficult or embarrassing situation.
meanings.example: He felt like his friends had hung him out to dry by not showing up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구들이 나타나지 않음으로써 자신을 곤경에 빠뜨렸다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose one's physical or emotional control.
meanings.example: {{quote-book|en| R. Weeks; Larry Hof| Marital-Relationship Therapy Casebook: Theory and Application of the Intersystem Model| Press
meanings.exampleKorean: R. Weeks; Larry Hof| 부부 관계 치료 사례집: 시스템 간 모델의 이론과 적용

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extremely angry or agitated.
meanings.example: He completely spazzed out when he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 소식을 듣고 완전히 격분했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To practise coitus interruptus, the withdrawal method of contraception.
meanings.example: looked at a woman standing further down the carriage in bridal headdress surrounded by her hen party acolytes.‘She's got the wrong aisle. At least she chose Virgin Trains. All train guards can marry folk you know. But the vows are only valid for the duration of the journey. You can always get off at Haymarket, know what I mean?’The women shrieked as the inflatable male doll her friends had stowed in the overhead luggage rack idly dangled a leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마차 안에서 신부 화관을 쓴 여성이 친구들에게 둘러싸여 서 있는 것을 보았다. '그녀는 잘못된 통로에 앉았어. 적어도 버진 트레인을 선택했잖아. 모든 기차 승무원은 결혼할 수 있어. 하지만 맹세는 여정 동안만 유효해. 언제든 헤이마켓에서 내릴 수 있지, 내 말 알겠어?' 친구들이 머리 위 짐칸에 넣어둔 풍선 남성 인형이 느긋하게 다리를 늘어뜨리자 여성들은 비명을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To advance to the post-season as a result of another team's loss, especially where one's own team has also lost.
meanings.example: The 2006 St. Louis Cardinals backed into the playoffs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2006년 세인트루이스 카디널스는 다른 팀의 패배 덕분에 플레이오프에 진출했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To align; to put in alignment; to put in correct adjustment for smooth running.
meanings.example: Please line up the chairs neatly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의자들을 깔끔하게 줄지어 놓아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: lineup.
meanings.example: The band's lineup has changed over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드의 명단은 수년에 걸쳐 바뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider (something) as a possible option among others.
meanings.example: We are ruling in the possibility of a price increase next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내년에 가격 인상 가능성을 고려하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cross an item out by drawing a straight line through it, as with a ruler.
meanings.example: As Jane Doe had an alibi, the police were able to rule her out as a suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제인 도우에게는 알리바이가 있었기 때문에 경찰은 그녀를 용의자에서 제외할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disregard (something), to dismiss or ignore (someone), as unimportant.
meanings.example: He tried to brush off the criticism, but it clearly bothered him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비판을 무시하려고 했지만, 분명히 신경 쓰여 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: brush-off.
meanings.example: She gave him a polite brush-off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그에게 정중하게 거절 의사를 표시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to enter into another zone by way of accident or overcrowding; to overflow
meanings.example: undercurrents of popular discontent spilled over into outright revolt
meanings.exampleKorean: 대중의 불만의 기류가 노골적인 반란으로 번졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the limit of one's capabilities.
meanings.example: He maxed out his credit card on the vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가 때 신용카드 한도를 모두 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become silent; to stop talking.
meanings.example: When asked about the incident, he just clammed up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사건에 대해 묻자 그는 그냥 입을 다물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a border with.
meanings.example: The town borders on the forest, so there are often deer on the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 마을은 숲과 접해 있어서 도로에 사슴이 자주 나타난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to retire, call it a day (used especially in the context of sports).
meanings.example: After a long career, the legendary footballer decided to hang up his boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 선수 생활 끝에 그 전설적인 축구선수는 은퇴하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift; to grasp and raise.
meanings.example: Please pick up the trash from the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바닥에 있는 쓰레기를 주워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: pickup.
meanings.example: I need to arrange a pickup for the package.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소포 수령을 준비해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go forward.
meanings.example: Please pass on my regards to your family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가족들에게 안부 전해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a bill and pay it, especially at a bar or restaurant.
meanings.example: When the bill came to just 33,000 forints, I did something very out of character: I picked up the tab. It blew the budget for the day, but felt great.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계산서가 겨우 33,000 포린트에 불과했을 때, 나는 평소와 달리 내가 계산했다. 그날 예산을 초과했지만 기분은 좋았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come into a room or other space through a door or passageway.
meanings.example: I can see Mr Smith now. Please ask him to come through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스미스 씨가 지금 보여요. 들어오시라고 말씀해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax; to make quieter or less obtrusive; to make milder.
meanings.example: The artist had to tone down the colors to make the painting less jarring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화가는 그림을 덜 거슬리게 하기 위해 색조를 누그러뜨려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find, obtain, or procure.
meanings.example: It's hard to lay hands on a rare book these days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요즘은 희귀한 책을 구하기 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To select a channel, station, etc., as on television or radio.
meanings.example: Don't forget to tune in to the news at 7 PM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후 7시 뉴스 채널을 맞추는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop paying attention to; to ignore; to zone out.
meanings.example: I have a hard time tuning out a television that is on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 켜져 있는 텔레비전에 주의를 기울이지 않기가 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight, especially with the fists.
meanings.example: A large crowd came to watch the boxers duke it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 관중이 복서들의 격투를 보기 위해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be alone and self-absorbed in an uncomfortable state of mind, especially while experiencing the unpleasant effects of one's own actions.
meanings.example: Instead of helping, he just let her stew in her own juices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕는 대신 그녀가 자신의 처지에 놓여 괴로워하도록 내버려 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring from a lower to a higher position.
meanings.example: When you're in the basement, can you bring up the paints?
meanings.exampleKorean: 지하실에 있을 때, 페인트 좀 위로 가져다줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To execute or complete a commitment.
meanings.example: I don't appreciate salesmen who make promises and then fail to follow through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약속하고는 이행하지 않는 영업사원들은 마음에 들지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine closely something coveted.
meanings.example: He eyed up the expensive watch in the shop window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가게 진열창에 있는 비싼 시계를 주의 깊게 살폈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break or separate into pieces.
meanings.example: Break up the cheese and put it in the salad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치즈를 부숴서 샐러드에 넣으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: breakup.
meanings.example: Their breakup was very public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 결별은 매우 공개적이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a bud (from a plant) to prevent flower and fruit from forming.
meanings.example: If you see a bad habit begin to develop, try to nip it in the bud so that it does not become ingrained.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 습관이 생기기 시작하는 것을 보면, 그것이 뿌리내리기 전에 초기에 막도록 노력하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To report (something) without taking into account the context in which it occurred.
meanings.example: The politician accused the media of taking his words out of context.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 언론이 자신의 말을 맥락 없이 인용했다고 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sign an item on a list with a tick mark, especially as a signal that the item has veen verified or completed.
meanings.example: Please tick off the items as you pack them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 짐을 쌀 때 항목에 체크 표시를 해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw up (a document etc.), to designate (a cheque). :to from 15th c.
meanings.example: Cheques may be made out to the Foo Bar Company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수표는 푸 바 컴퍼니 앞으로 작성될 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue (at a task) briskly or promptly. :with
meanings.example: We need to crack on if we want to finish this project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내려면 서둘러야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress up finely or elaborately; to deck up. often of ostentatious display from 1906
meanings.example: She's all dolled up with nowhere to go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 갈 곳도 없는데 화려하게 차려입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push or to pull, i.e. to force, (someone or something) out of somewhere.
meanings.example: The villagers drove out the invaders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마을 사람들은 침략자들을 몰아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put up to hang.
meanings.example: I hung up my wash on the line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 빨래를 줄에 널었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to keep something near one.
meanings.example: It's worth keeping a dictionary around in case you come across an unfamiliar word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모르는 단어를 만날 경우를 대비해 사전을 곁에 두는 것이 좋다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter data by keyboarding.
meanings.example: Please key in your password.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비밀번호를 입력해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in playful fun.
meanings.example: I think he was just kidding around when he said that, so don't take it personally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그렇게 말했을 때 그냥 장난친 거라고 생각하니, 개인적으로 받아들이지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kneel, especially as an act of reverence or prayer.
meanings.example: The congregation knelt down to pray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신도들은 기도하기 위해 무릎을 꿇었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a gift of (something).
meanings.example: I didn't like that book, so I gave it away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 책이 마음에 들지 않아서 다른 사람에게 주어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: That new bartender has got my name on her. Hands off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 새 바텐더가 나한테 내 이름을 적어놨어. 손대지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a specific approach to a person considered to be suitable for a professional position.
meanings.example: In earlier years, the intelligence services would often recruit agents by tapping them up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예전에는 정보기관에서 요원을 영입하기 위해 접촉하는 경우가 많았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cover up, figuratively To cover up a mistake or a crime; to hush up or whitewash.
meanings.example: The report tried to gloss over the serious financial problems of the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 보고서는 회사의 심각한 재무 문제를 얼버무리려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unfocused, as if through boredom.
meanings.example: Someone's eyes may glaze over before or during a seizure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발작 전이나 도중에 누군가의 눈이 멍해질 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume [something] rapidly.
meanings.example: The children gobbled up all the cookies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 쿠키를 전부 게걸스럽게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Be happy; cheer up.
meanings.example: Chin up: it’s your birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내, 네 생일이잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face a situation cheerfully or with confidence.
meanings.example: You'll have to chin up to get through this.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것을 헤쳐나가려면 기운을 내야 할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To generate or encourage; to campaign for.
meanings.example: The candidate gave speeches, shook hands, and kissed babies in an effort to drum up support before the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 후보는 선거 전에 지지를 얻기 위해 연설하고, 악수하고, 아기들에게 뽀뽀했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: over
meanings.example: I'm going over to my friend's house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 친구 집에 갈 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with someone, especially for the first time.
meanings.example: Remember that day? First day I was there. I knew that I couldn't get into your pants unless I said… um… what did I say? Oh yeah, “May I have my bill please, I have to leave?”
meanings.exampleKorean: 그날 기억나? 내가 거기 처음 갔던 날. 내가 말하지 않으면 네 바지에 들어갈 수 없다는 걸 알았어... 음... 뭐라고 말했지? 아, 그래, "계산서 좀 주시겠어요? 나가야 해서요?"

meanings.englishDefinition: callback.
meanings.example: I missed your call, I'll call you back later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전화를 놓쳤어요, 나중에 다시 전화할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To request someone's return; to bring back or restore; to recall.
meanings.example: Please call me back when you have a moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 있을 때 다시 전화해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain weight.
meanings.example: He is trying to bulk up by lifting weights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 웨이트 트레이닝으로 근육을 키우려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inflate by means of a pump.
meanings.example: He pumped up the tires on his bike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자전거 타이어에 공기를 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refrain from entering a place or condition.
meanings.example: After being warned, he kept out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경고를 받은 후 그는 들어가지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go ahead; to do as one pleases; to enjoy oneself; to endeavor very hard at a task.
meanings.example: He knocked himself out trying to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 몹시 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (tentative plans) more definite.
meanings.example: Can we firm up plans for the barbecue Sunday?
meanings.exampleKorean: 일요일 바비큐 계획을 확정할 수 있을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: 2007 September 28, Graham Linehan, The IT Crowd, Season 2, Episode 6:
meanings.exampleKorean: 2007년 9월 28일, 그레이엄 라이너핸, 아이티 크라우드, 시즌 2, 에피소드 6:

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (someone) into (a particular state, emotion etc.). from 19th c.
meanings.example: The therapist tried to key into the patient's feelings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치료사는 환자의 감정을 이해하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start abruptly from a standing stop, accelerating rapidly, especially so as to produce skid marks.
meanings.example: The car peeled out of the parking lot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차가 주차장에서 급출발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a form or questionnaire with requested information.
meanings.example: You need to fill out this application form to apply for the position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 직무에 지원하려면 지원서를 작성해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To selectively remove part of something.
meanings.example: The sieve will filter out large rocks and grains of sand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 체는 큰 돌과 모래알을 걸러낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be of the right size and shape to be placed in a location.
meanings.example: The round peg will fit into the circular hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 둥근 말뚝은 원형 구멍에 맞을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat or polish with sandpaper.
meanings.example: He sanded down the rough edges of the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 테이블의 거친 가장자리를 사포질했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exit in one or more single file lines.
meanings.example: The recruits filed out of the hall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신병들이 홀에서 줄지어 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter in single file.
meanings.example: The students filed into the auditorium.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들이 강당으로 일렬로 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ignored.
meanings.example: His pleas for help fell on deaf ears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 도움 요청은 묵살되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, especially a building.
meanings.example: Please go out through the back door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뒷문으로 나가 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: The keys were dropped in the fishbowl.
meanings.exampleKorean: 열쇠가 어항 안으로 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: drop-in.
meanings.example: She made a surprise drop-in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 깜짝 방문을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop wasting time in preliminaries, and concentrate on the important task.
meanings.example: You've been sitting there all week. It's time you sorted yourself out and pulled your finger out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 일주일 내내 앉아만 있었잖아. 이제 정신 차리고 서둘러야지!

meanings.englishDefinition: To start making an effort; to renew or redouble one's efforts.
meanings.example: If you want to pass the exam, you need to pull your socks up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 분발해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: For a person, to die.
meanings.example: Then fell she down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러자 그녀는 곧바로 그의 발 앞에 쓰러져 숨을 거두었다. 젊은이들이 들어와 그녀가 죽은 것을 발견하고, 그녀를 밖으로 옮겨 남편 곁에 묻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow the same path as someone; to take actions similar to another's.
meanings.example: She decided to follow in her father's footsteps and become a doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아버지의 뒤를 이어 의사가 되기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To terminate; to call the end of.
meanings.example: We need to close out the project by the end of the month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 월말까지 프로젝트를 마무리해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dine at a restaurant or such public place.
meanings.example: Jacky wanted Korean barbecue, but his mom didn't want to eat out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재키는 한국식 바비큐를 원했지만, 그의 엄마는 외식하고 싶어 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add (fat, muscle, etc.) so as to increase mass or bulk.
meanings.example: He's packed on a lot of muscle since he started lifting weights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 웨이트 트레이닝을 시작한 이후로 근육이 많이 붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed with the matter in hand.
meanings.example: Come on, get your finger out and finish the report!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서, 정신 차리고 보고서 끝내!

meanings.englishDefinition: throw-out.
meanings.example: The throw-out of old documents was necessary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 문서들의 폐기가 필요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: Please throw out the trash.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쓰레기를 버려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine something (especially written material) carefully and attentively.
meanings.example: She spent hours poring over the ancient manuscript.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 고대 원고를 몇 시간 동안 자세히 살펴보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen without participating.
meanings.example: Do you mind if I listen in on your meeting tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 회의를 제가 옆에서 좀 들어도 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue or persist, despite adversity or difficulty.
meanings.example: He soldiered on through the night, working hard and not sleeping.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 쉬지 않고 열심히 일하며 꿋꿋이 버텼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very frightened, scared, or nervous.
meanings.example: The thought of climbing that high had him quaking in his boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 높이 올라가야 한다는 생각에 그는 다리가 후들거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To figure out; to realize the truth.
meanings.example: I was planning it as a surprise, but I think he is on to me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깜짝 파티로 계획했는데, 내 생각에 그는 이미 눈치챈 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the greater part of (a project) or solve the most critical part of (a problem).
meanings.example: I've broken the back of painting the shed – I'll finish it after lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 창고 페인트칠의 가장 힘든 고비는 넘겼으니, 점심 먹고 마무리할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a visit in an impromptu manner.
meanings.example: I'll pop in to see you this afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 오후에 잠깐 들를게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a room or building with the expectation of returning soon.
meanings.example: In the menu that then pops out, select the number of degrees you want to rotate the image (90° if the image is lying on its left side, 180° if the image is upside down, or 270° if the image is lying on its right side).
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러고 나서 나타나는 메뉴에서 이미지를 회전시킬 각도를 선택하세요 (이미지가 왼쪽에 누워 있으면 90°, 거꾸로 있으면 180°, 오른쪽에 누워 있으면 270°).

meanings.englishDefinition: To hook something together.
meanings.example: Can you help me hook up this new stereo system?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새 스테레오 시스템 연결하는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quiet; to cease making sounds.
meanings.example: The government tried to hush up the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 그 스캔들을 은폐하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite somebody, especially on a date.
meanings.example: He finally asked her out after weeks of hesitation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 주간 망설인 끝에 마침내 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish something, especially a flame, especially by means of a strong current of air or another gas.
meanings.example: He blows out the match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 성냥불을 불어 끈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call up for consideration.
meanings.example: the Group was highly critical of the BTC's organisation and finances, and this prompted Marples to bring forward the Transport Act 1962, which created BR as a standalone entity with its own Board, headed by a full-time chairman. Beeching accepted this latter position, and initially became the BTC chairman in June 1961.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그룹은 BTC의 조직과 재정에 대해 매우 비판적이었고, 이에 Marples는 독립적인 기관으로 BR을 설립하는 1962년 교통법을 제안했다. Beeching은 후자의 직책을 수락했고, 1961년 6월에 BTC 회장이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: signoff#Noun.
meanings.example: The show ended with a cheerful sign off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 쇼는 즐거운 마무리 인사로 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To log off; to stop using a computer, radio, etc.; especially, to stop talking.
meanings.example: I need to sign off now, but I'll talk to you tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 로그아웃해야 하지만, 내일 다시 얘기하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create falsely, to fabricate (particularly applied to accusations, (legal) charges or evidence).
meanings.example: His electoral campaign included attempts to trump up criminal charges against opponents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 선거 캠페인에는 상대 후보들에게 형사 고발을 날조하려는 시도가 포함되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ponder or reflect on a subject.
meanings.example: When he has time to think over what he did, he will regret it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자신이 한 일을 깊이 생각할 시간이 생기면 그는 후회할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lose consciousness To faint; to become unconscious.
meanings.example: I pass out at the sight of blood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 피를 보면 기절한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to retreat from a position
meanings.example: The army was forced to draw back from the front line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 군대는 전선에서 후퇴해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retaliate against; to take revenge on.
meanings.example: She put toothpaste in his shoes to get back at him for the frog he left in her refrigerator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 냉장고에 개구리를 넣어둔 것에 대한 복수로 그의 신발에 치약을 짜 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a customary action to ward off some misfortune that is believed to be attracted by a presumptuous statement.
meanings.example: I haven't had any problems with my car yet, knock on wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 차에 아직 아무 문제도 없었어, 액운을 막기 위해 나무를 두드려야지.

meanings.englishDefinition: Hopefully; a self-directive to undertake the customary action to ward off bad luck.
meanings.example: I've never been sick, knock on wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아픈 적이 없어, 액운을 막기 위해 (나무를 두드리며).

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss something so fully as to resolve a problem or conflict; to hammer out.
meanings.example: They spent an afternoon on it, but thrashed out a solution in the end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 문제에 오후 내내 시간을 보냈지만, 결국 해결책을 찾아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain cheerful; keep smiling.
meanings.example: I know it's tough, but keep your pecker up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 거 알아, 하지만 기운 내!

meanings.englishDefinition: to cheat or treat unfairly to exploit somebody in a way which results in an advantage to oneself, at the cost of the other party gaining a considerable disadvantage
meanings.example: He really fucked me over when he sold me that car for $3,000.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나에게 그 차를 3,000달러에 팔아 나를 정말 속였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cheat someone, or ruin their chances in a game or other situation.
meanings.example: The company screwed over its employees by not paying them their wages.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 직원들에게 임금을 지급하지 않음으로써 그들을 속였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: I can't believe they passed you over for promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 당신을 승진에서 제외했다니 믿을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in frivolous behaviour; to monkey around.
meanings.example: Jake has been fooling around with a married woman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제이크는 유부녀와 바람을 피우고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To conceal or disguise.
meanings.example: The politician tried to cover up his involvement in the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 스캔들에 대한 자신의 연루 사실을 감추려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To evaluate; to estimate or anticipate the magnitude, difficulty, or strength of something.
meanings.example: It's a good idea for boxers to size up their opponents before their matches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복서들이 경기 전에 상대를 파악하는 것은 좋은 생각이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clarify by reviewing mentally.
meanings.example: It's a bit confused at the moment, I'll try to sort it out later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금은 좀 혼란스러운데, 나중에 정리해볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collapse inwards.
meanings.example: The heavy rain caused the roof to fall in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭우로 인해 지붕이 무너졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A type of transition used in visual media, in which the transition is at first black, fading to a visual image.
meanings.example: The scene began with a fade in from black.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장면은 검은 화면에서 서서히 나타나며 시작되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fade from black to a visual image in a film or other visual media.
meanings.example: The image will fade in slowly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미지가 천천히 서서히 나타날 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A type of transition used in film, usually at the end of a scene, involving a fade to black from the cut.
meanings.example: The movie ended with a fade out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화는 화면이 서서히 사라지며 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slowly disappear; to wither away.
meanings.example: The music began to fade out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악이 서서히 사라지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move one's sitting self or seat aside, so the other person has more space.
meanings.example: Scoot over, there's not enough room for everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좀 옆으로 비켜, 모두 앉을 자리가 충분하지 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was caught whacking off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위행위를 하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To merely touch.
meanings.example: If you lay a finger on my little brother, I'll have your guts for garters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 동생한테 손대면 네 내장을 다 꺼내 버릴 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write at full length or in expanded form.
meanings.example: Don't use abbreviations; write out words!
meanings.exampleKorean: 약어를 쓰지 말고, 단어를 다 써내세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was found jacking off in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공원에서 자위행위를 하다가 발견되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: a jerkoff.
meanings.example: Don't be such a jack off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 바보 같은 짓 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: We stayed up late to look up at the stars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 밤늦게까지 별을 올려다보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perceive visually through something transparent.
meanings.example: Their fabric is so thin that I can see through these curtains.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 천은 너무 얇아서 이 커튼을 통해 속이 보일 정도다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on a past success instead of trying to improve oneself further.
meanings.example: has a good basis for promoting green travel options, but we can't rest on our laurels. We need to keep on moving forwards because other modes are upping their games as well," says Brennan-Brown.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친환경 여행 옵션을 홍보할 좋은 기반이 있지만, 우리는 안주할 수 없습니다. 다른 교통수단들도 경쟁력을 높이고 있기 때문에 계속 나아가야 한다고 브레넌-브라운은 말합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a severe beating to; to assault violently with repeated blows.
meanings.example: I got beaten up by thugs on my way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 깡패들에게 맞았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Battered by time and usage; beaten up.
meanings.example: He was wearing a beat up old coat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡아빠진 오래된 코트를 입고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A person who, or thing that, has been [[#Verb|beaten up]].
meanings.example: Well, two beat ups (the bullied kids) would split that cash fifty-fifty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 글쎄, 두 명의 구타당한 아이들(괴롭힘당하는 아이들)이 그 돈을 반반 나눌 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resign, or leave, with one's credibility or reputation still intact.
meanings.example: Jane had a long spell as chairman, but bowed out after she had a child.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제인은 오랫동안 의장직을 맡았지만, 아이를 가진 후 사퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rift following a disagreement or quarrel.
meanings.example: We had a fall out, couldn't come to terms and haven't talked since.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 다퉜고, 합의에 이르지 못했으며 그 이후로 대화하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come out of something by falling.
meanings.example: I opened the cupboard and a can fell out onto my foot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 찬장을 열었는데 캔 하나가 발 위로 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Use a flag or similar kind of signal to get someone's attention.
meanings.example: He flagged down a taxi to get to the airport.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공항에 가기 위해 택시를 손짓하여 멈춰 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ruin or harm.
meanings.example: Don't drink tonight, because it'll stuff you up before your business trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 술 마시지 마, 출장 전에 망칠 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add insult to injury; to emphasize one's strengths or another's weaknesses in a manner that degrades another.
meanings.example: Not to rub it in, but I'm working from home. So I took a swim this morning and have been thoroughly enjoying the beautiful weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상처에 소금 뿌리려는 건 아니지만, 나는 재택근무 중이야. 그래서 오늘 아침 수영도 했고 멋진 날씨를 마음껏 즐기고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: off
meanings.example: The dirt rubbed off easily.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 먼지는 쉽게 지워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be acquired by or transferred to (someone) as a result of regular exposure.
meanings.example: The hippie way of life seems to have rubbed off on him, as he's a flower power aficionado.
meanings.exampleKorean: 히피 생활 방식이 그에게 스며든 것 같다. 그는 꽃의 힘 애호가이기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold while moving it out.
meanings.example: We’ll have to carry the piano out of the shop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피아노를 가게 밖으로 옮겨야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To issue a challenge.
meanings.example: We might as well dispute with Dimock on a Coronation Day, as argue with these Writers. They strut, vapour, throw down the Gauntlet, and defy us to take it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왕관 수여식 날 디목과 논쟁하는 것이 이 작가들과 논쟁하는 것과 마찬가지일 것이다. 그들은 으스대고, 허세를 부리며, 도전장을 던지고, 우리가 그것을 받아들이도록 도전한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a challenge.
meanings.example: He doesn't read Greek, but he took up the gauntlet and did his best to understand the letter, anyway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그리스어를 읽지 못하지만, 도전을 받아들이고 어쨌든 그 편지를 이해하기 위해 최선을 다했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard (trash, garbage, or the like), to toss out, to put in the trash.
meanings.example: Please throw away the trash after you finish eating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식사를 마친 후에는 쓰레기를 버려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hinder the development of something.
meanings.example: The harsh winter conditions threw back the plant's growth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 혹독한 겨울 날씨가 식물의 성장을 저해했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to idle (to run at a slow speed, or out of gear).
meanings.example: While the boss was on holiday, the deputy made sure things were ticking over in the firm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사장이 휴가 중일 때, 부사장은 회사 일이 계속 돌아가도록 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To constrain, or to confine within set limits.
meanings.example: Don't let a demanding job tie you down too much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 까다로운 직업 때문에 너무 얽매이지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dedicate a large amount of effort towards.
meanings.example: He's really getting stuck in to his new job as chief executive. He's sacked half the boardroom staff already.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 최고 경영자로서의 새 직무에 정말 몰두하고 있다. 이미 이사회 직원의 절반을 해고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit; throw up.
meanings.example: He ate too much and had to chuck up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 먹어서 토해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily.
meanings.example: It's pissing down outside, so we can't go for a walk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖에 비가 억수같이 쏟아지고 있어서 산책을 갈 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away (from).
meanings.example: Get away from the edge of the cliff!
meanings.exampleKorean: 절벽 가장자리에서 벗어나!

meanings.englishDefinition: Expressing disbelief.
meanings.example: You bought that for twenty pounds? Get away!
meanings.exampleKorean: 20파운드에 그걸 샀다고? 말도 안 돼!

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in sexual intercourse.
meanings.example: They were caught having it off in the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 사무실에서 성교하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something one is holding to drop, often forcefully.
meanings.example: He threw down his tools in frustration.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좌절감에 도구를 내던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to refill or recharge To refill (or recharge) something.
meanings.example: Could you top up my drink, please?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제 음료를 좀 더 채워주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the sum of a number of numbers or quantities.
meanings.example: As soon as Debbie can tot up the scores, we'll know who won.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데비가 점수를 합산하는 대로 누가 이겼는지 알 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be much taller or higher than something; to loom over.
meanings.example: The new skyscraper towers over the old buildings in the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 초고층 빌딩이 도시의 오래된 건물들 위로 우뚝 솟아 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Precisely accurate; exactly appropriate or fitting; spot on.
meanings.example: That's bang on the money!
meanings.exampleKorean: 정확히 맞았어!

meanings.englishDefinition: Exactly at
meanings.example: I managed to arrive bang on five o’clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정확히 다섯 시에 도착할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about something constantly and tiresomely.
meanings.example: I started recycling, just so she’d stop banging on about it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 그 문제에 대해 계속 잔소리하는 것을 멈추게 하려고 재활용을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a piece of merchandise as part of the payment or trade for something new.
meanings.example: I'm going to trade in my old car for a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구형 자동차를 새 차로 보상 판매할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take shelter; to prepare oneself for some eventuality; to focus on a task.
meanings.example: That test is worth half your grade, so you'd better hunker down and start studying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시험은 성적의 절반을 차지하므로, 자리를 잡고 공부를 시작하는 것이 좋겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get to work; to focus on a task.
meanings.example: You should knuckle down and do your homework!
meanings.exampleKorean: 숙제를 열심히 해야 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To get to work; to focus on a task.
meanings.example: It's time to knuckle down and finish this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트를 마무리하기 위해 열심히 일할 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide up: to divide, particularly into separate shares for distribution.
meanings.example: We can divvy up the last piece of cake and each have a little dessert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 케이크 조각을 나눠서 각자 디저트로 조금씩 먹을 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extinct.
meanings.example: Many species of dinosaurs have died out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 공룡 종들이 멸종했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or anger someone.
meanings.example: That joke really got my boss's back up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 농담은 정말로 상사를 화나게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scare or startle someone thoroughly.
meanings.example: He liked the water rides, but the big roller-coaster scared the pants off him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 놀이기구를 좋아했지만, 큰 롤러코스터는 그를 몹시 놀라게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a decision in an official capacity regarding some matter.
meanings.example: The judge will rule on the motion tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 내일 그 신청에 대해 판결할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move to the other side of (something, such as an obstruction) by deviating from a direct course or following a curved path.
meanings.example: The tide was too high, and we couldn't get around the rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조수가 너무 높아서 우리는 바위들을 우회할 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eventually begin or return to some task.
meanings.example: Someday I'll get around to organizing this mess.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언젠가 이 난장판을 정리할 시간을 낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, exit or escape from; to become free of.
meanings.example: All the occupants managed to get out of the burning building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 거주자들이 불타는 건물에서 빠져나올 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: here
meanings.example: If you're smart, don't sign the employment contract. Just get out of here while you still can.
meanings.exampleKorean: 똑똑하다면, 고용 계약서에 서명하지 마. 아직 할 수 있을 때 여기서 나가.

meanings.englishDefinition: here
meanings.example: Get out of here and don't come back!
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼지고 다시는 돌아오지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To test the look or fit of (a garment) by wearing it.
meanings.example: She tried on the dress and it fit perfectly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 드레스를 입어보았고 완벽하게 맞았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To test (something) to see how it works or whether it is suitable.
meanings.example: If you want to play varsity, you'll have to try out for the team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주전으로 뛰고 싶다면, 팀에 지원해서 시험을 통과해야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add a name to the list of people who are participating in something.
meanings.example: I need to sign up for the conference before the deadline.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마감일 전에 컨퍼런스에 등록해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (property or rights) to somebody else by signing a document.
meanings.example: He signed his house over to his wife in order to avoid taxes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 세금을 피하기 위해 자신의 집을 아내에게 양도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sign one's name on a list when entering a place, to indicate one's arrival.
meanings.example: In order to get into the office after hours, you'll have to sign in at the security desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근 후 사무실에 들어가려면 보안 데스크에서 서명해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something bad or illegal and escape the punishment that others would receive.
meanings.example: And what do you say to someone who might be on the left, who’d be listening to this conversation and would say: Oh look, Bannon is absolutely right. There was a financial crisis. Wall Street got away with murder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그리고 당신은 이 대화를 듣고 있을 좌파 성향의 누군가에게 뭐라고 말하겠습니까? 보십시오, 배넌은 전적으로 옳습니다. 금융 위기가 있었습니다. 월스트리트는 살인적인 범죄를 저지르고도 처벌받지 않았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reverse direction and retrace one's steps.
meanings.example: The storm forced the hikers to turn back and return to the camp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍 때문에 등산객들은 발길을 돌려 캠프로 돌아와야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone feel, by weirdness, uneasy or uncomfortable; to make one feel (that something is) weird.
meanings.example: I don't know why you're so weirded out. This is not San Quentin, it's just a nuthouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그렇게 불편해하는지 모르겠어요. 여기는 샌 퀜틴이 아니라 정신병원일 뿐이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: A negative or unpleasant experience with LSD or another psychedelic drug.
meanings.example: Don't freak out, it's just a small spider.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 놀라지 마, 그냥 작은 거미일 뿐이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To react with extreme anger or fear, to the extent that one loses one's composure or behaves irrationally.
meanings.example: My parents are going to freak out when they see my grades.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 성적표를 보시면 부모님께서 화를 내며 펄펄 뛰실 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt one's journey for a short (sometimes overnight) stay.
meanings.example: We decided to stop over in Paris for two days on our way to Rome.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로마로 가는 길에 파리에서 이틀 동안 머물기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say suddenly, without thinking
meanings.example: When in court, it is inadvisable to blurt out the first thing you think of. Instead, take time to construct coherent sentences.
meanings.exampleKorean: 법정에서는 생각나는 대로 불쑥 말하는 것이 바람직하지 않습니다. 대신, 일관성 있는 문장을 구성할 시간을 가지십시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a less aggressive position in a conflict than one previously had, or has planned to have.
meanings.example: I was about to sue them, but I had to back down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들을 고소하려 했지만, 나는 물러서야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent something from happening; to obviate or avert.
meanings.example: He drank plenty of orange juice, hoping to stave off the cold making the rounds at the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사무실에 퍼지는 감기를 막기 위해 오렌지 주스를 많이 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform oral sex on.
meanings.example: He was accused of asking his employees to suck off him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직원들에게 자신에게 구강 성교를 하도록 요구한 혐의로 기소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to distribute or circulate something
meanings.example: The charity decided to give about food and clothes to the needy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 자선 단체는 도움이 필요한 사람들에게 음식과 옷을 배포하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to give in to.
meanings.example: After a long negotiation, the company finally gave into the workers' demands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 협상 끝에 회사는 마침내 노동자들의 요구에 굴복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To utter, publish; to announce, proclaim, report.
meanings.example: best news is that we have safely foundOur king and company: the next, our ship,—Which but three glasses since we gave out split,—Is tight and yare, and bravely rigg'd as whenWe first put out to sea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 좋은 소식은 우리 왕과 일행이 무사히 발견되었다는 것입니다. 다음으로, 우리 배는—불과 세 잔 전에 우리가 부서졌다고 발표했던—처음 바다로 나갔을 때처럼 튼튼하고 민첩하며 훌륭하게 정비되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up, hand over, surrender (something).
meanings.example: Diocletian, the emperor, was so much affected with it that he gave over his sceptre and turned gardener.
meanings.exampleKorean: 황제 디오클레티아누스는 그것에 너무 큰 영향을 받아 왕위를 넘겨주고 정원사가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin moving away.
meanings.example: We will head off on our holidays tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내일 휴가를 떠날 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create in one’s mind; to invent.
meanings.example: I can think up plenty of excuses not to go, but we really should attend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가지 않을 핑계를 많이 생각해낼 수 있지만, 우리는 정말 참석해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike out (something); to draw a line through (something).
meanings.example: If you make a mistake, just cross it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수하면 그냥 지우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike out; to cross out; to draw a line through.
meanings.example: I can cross off another project from the list.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목록에서 다른 프로젝트 하나를 지울 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet expectations; to be as good as.
meanings.example: It's a passable substitute, but it will never measure up to the original.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 괜찮은 대안이지만, 원본만큼 좋지는 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter illegally or by force, especially in order to commit a crime.
meanings.example: Somebody broke into his car and stole his tools and CDs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 그의 차에 침입하여 도구와 CD를 훔쳐갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rotate so as not to face someone or something.
meanings.example: Thereat the Elfe did blush in priuitee, / And turnd his face away; but she the same / Dissembled faire, and faynd to ouersee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그때 엘프는 은밀히 얼굴을 붉혔고, 얼굴을 돌렸지만 그녀는 같은 것을 아름답게 위장하고 못 본 척했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare (food or chemical substances) by cooking or heating.
meanings.example: Let me cook up some eggs and bacon before you go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가기 전에 계란과 베이컨을 좀 요리해 줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin working time, especially by punching in (entering a workplace by punching a time card with the time clock).
meanings.example: We clocked in at 7:30, but didn't actually do any work until at least 8.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 7시 30분에 출근 기록을 했지만, 실제로 8시 이전에는 아무 일도 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end work; to officially record a time when one terminates a period of work.
meanings.example: If we clock out now we can be at the bar by happy hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 퇴근 기록을 하면 해피아워 시간에 바에 도착할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to leave work, especially by recording the leaving time on a machine
meanings.example: Most of the staff have already clocked off for the night, you should do the same.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대부분의 직원들은 이미 밤을 위해 퇴근했으니, 당신도 그렇게 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contract a facial feature so as to form wrinkles; used especially of the lips when whistling or kissing.
meanings.example: Pucker up your lips!
meanings.exampleKorean: 입술을 오므리세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To contract or get (an illness); to show symptoms of.
meanings.example: With a scratchy throat and a cough, it feels like I'm coming down with a cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목이 쉬고 기침이 나는 걸 보니 감기에 걸리는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act foolishly.
meanings.example: Stop monkeying around and get your homework done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난치지 말고 숙제나 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend against; to repel with force or effort
meanings.example: He managed to fend off the attackers with a stick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 막대기로 공격자들을 물리칠 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a pile, stack, or heap.
meanings.example: The dirty dishes began to pile up in the sink.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더러운 접시들이 싱크대에 쌓이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lead or take the lead in; to direct; to take charge of.
meanings.example: We want you to head up the effort to organize all the entries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 항목을 정리하는 노력을 당신이 이끌어주길 바랍니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be aware or conscious; to look closely or carefully; to use caution. Often used in the imperative.
meanings.example: Watch out for low doorways, so you don’t hit your head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 머리를 부딪히지 않도록 낮은 문들을 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to warn someone of an imminent danger.
meanings.example: Watch out! There's a car coming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 차가 오고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on special or fancy clothes.
meanings.example: We're going to a fancy party, so we need to dress up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 근사한 파티에 갈 거라서 차려입어야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take the sum of; to total.
meanings.example: Add up the prices and find out how much it will cost.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가격들을 더해서 얼마가 들지 알아보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become serious, organized, worthwhile, etc.
meanings.example: If you want to succeed, you need to get your act together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성공하고 싶다면 정신 차려야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reinforce or strengthen (something at risk of failure).
meanings.example: The government is trying to shore up the failing economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 실패하는 경제를 부양하려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to a stop, and turn off the road (i.e. onto the roadside or hard shoulder).
meanings.example: Can you pull over just after the post office? My house is next door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우체국 바로 다음에 차를 길가에 대줄 수 있나요? 우리 집은 옆집이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move ahead.
meanings.example: Schumacher is beginning to pull away from the rest of the racers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 슈마허가 나머지 선수들로부터 앞서 나가기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To profit from a craze; to join a trend.
meanings.example: After the incredible success of Wonka's latest low-fat chocolate bar, Fickelgruber has jumped on the bandwagon, and released a low-fat version.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웡카의 최신 저지방 초콜릿 바의 엄청난 성공 이후, 피켈그루버도 유행에 편승하여 저지방 버전을 출시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To marginally manage to progress.
meanings.example: The pass mark for the exam was 40%, and I scraped through with 43%.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 합격선은 40%였고, 나는 43%로 간신히 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize a person in a ranting way or manner.
meanings.example: The politician was trashed out by the media for his controversial remarks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 그의 논란이 되는 발언 때문에 언론에 의해 맹렬히 비난받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change one's mind, especially to begin to agree or appreciate what one was reluctant to accept at first.
meanings.example: Give her time, and she may come around and see things your way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간을 주면, 그녀는 마음을 바꿔 당신의 방식대로 생각하게 될지도 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Dead or defunct.
meanings.example: After several financial failures, the organization went belly up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러 차례의 재정 실패 후, 그 조직은 파산했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather close, or approach eagerly or assertively, as a bar for ordering alcoholic drinks.
meanings.example: He bellied up to the bar as soon as he saw a free stool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빈 의자를 보자마자 바에 다가가 술을 주문했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk more loudly or clearly.
meanings.example: Please speak up, I can't hear you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 크게 말씀해 주세요, 당신의 목소리가 들리지 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assert or promote one's opinion; to make one's thoughts known.
meanings.example: It's important to speak out against injustice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불의에 맞서 소신껏 말하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave and return in a short time.
meanings.example: I'm just popping off to the shops to pick up some bread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빵 좀 사러 가게에 잠깐 나갔다 올게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grope someone, especially someone's breasts, in a sexual manner.
meanings.example: Mostly we heard her laughing and the squeaking of the porch swing while the boyfriend felt her up. All the boys went to second base with Diana Estabrook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주로 그녀의 웃음소리와 현관 그네 삐걱거리는 소리를 들었는데, 남자친구가 그녀의 몸을 더듬고 있었다. 모든 남자아이들이 다이애나 에스타브룩과 스킨십을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose around; to tighten or shrink; to collapse.
meanings.example: Sometimes it feels like the walls are closing in on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 때로는 벽이 나를 둘러싸는 것처럼 느껴진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: Pull up that website for me; it sounds quite interesting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 웹사이트를 나를 위해 불러와 줘; 꽤 흥미롭게 들린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lecture, scold, reprimand, or rebuke, especially in an angry tone of voice.
meanings.example: The boss chewed out the employee for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 지각한 직원을 심하게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to finish school, or a given subject, program or course, due to academic shortcomings; i.e., to have too many flunking or failing grades (marks).
meanings.example: He flunked out of high school as a youth, but finished school later in life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젊었을 때 고등학교에서 퇴학당했지만, 나중에 학교를 마쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To busy oneself with.
meanings.example: marvellous witty fellow, I assure you; but I will go about with him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 재치 있는 친구라고 장담하건대; 하지만 나는 그와 함께 일을 시작할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend or lengthen excessively; to draw out.
meanings.example: I don't want to drag out this talk, so I'll stop now and answer any questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 대화를 길게 끌고 싶지 않으니, 지금 멈추고 질문에 답하겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pick up the receiver of a telephone, generally with the intention of making a phone call.
meanings.example: The phone rang for a long time before someone finally picked it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 마침내 전화를 받기 전에 전화벨이 오랫동안 울렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be nosy; to meddle or interfere in the affairs of another.
meanings.example: I was doing just fine before he came and stuck his nose in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 와서 참견하기 전까지는 잘 지내고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront a condition or situation, typically one that is unpleasant or uncomfortable.
meanings.example: We must all face up to the fact that we're not getting any younger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두는 우리가 더 이상 젊어지지 않는다는 사실을 직시해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin shooting at an enemy.
meanings.example: The soldiers were ordered to fire away at the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적에게 발포하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be physically capable of going into a space.
meanings.example: The small box should fit in the drawer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 상자가 서랍에 들어갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose an area within a fence.
meanings.example: They decided to fence off their garden to keep animals out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 동물이 들어오지 못하도록 정원에 울타리를 치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store in a file; to get rid of a document by placing it in a file or other storage.
meanings.example: Would you help me file away these reports?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서들을 서류철에 보관하는 것 좀 도와줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a contribution; help in a small way; especially, to pay for a part of something.
meanings.example: If we all chip in, we can afford to buy a pizza for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두 돈을 보태면 점심으로 피자를 살 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dawdle, lollygag; to waste time aimlessly or engage in idle activity or inactivity.
meanings.example: He sat there goofing off all day instead of working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일하는 대신 하루 종일 빈둥거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to sleep late To sleep late; to go on sleeping past one's customary or planned hour.
meanings.example: I decided to sleep in this morning since it's Saturday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토요일이라서 오늘 아침 늦잠을 자기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure without complaint or protest.
meanings.example: I don't know what they expect, but I am certainly not going to take this lying down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 무엇을 기대하는지는 모르겠지만, 나는 분명히 이것을 묵인하지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be joking, but at the same time really mean it.
meanings.example: Are you kidding me on the square, or are you serious?
meanings.exampleKorean: 진심으로 말하는 거야, 아니면 장난하는 거야?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay; to linger; to remain.
meanings.example: If you have any questions for the performers, stick around in the lobby after the show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공연자들에게 질문이 있다면, 공연 후에 로비에 머물러 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tolerate, accept, or acquiesce; to take no action.
meanings.example: I hope he doesn't think he can get away with such a thing. I'm not going to take this sitting down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그런 짓을 하고도 무사할 거라고 생각하지 않기를 바란다. 나는 이것을 그냥 보고만 있지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy by fire.
meanings.example: The forest fire caused the entire area to burn out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산불로 인해 그 지역 전체가 전소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide into portions or chunks; to ration.
meanings.example: It's a large job, but if we parcel it out among several people over several weeks, it shouldn't be too difficult.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일이 많지만, 몇 주에 걸쳐 여러 사람에게 나누어 준다면 그리 어렵지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed with; to begin.
meanings.example: I went ahead with my plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 계획을 계속 진행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persist or continue, as with an effort.
meanings.example: He kept plugging away at the work until it was done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일이 끝날 때까지 계속해서 꾸준히 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: diminish to nothing, to refer to a vein of ore.
meanings.example: The crowd's enthusiasm began to peter out as the game went on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기가 진행됨에 따라 군중의 열기가 점점 줄어들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build or acquire a basic level of understanding or control.
meanings.example: After a week of poring over the manuals, I am just beginning to get a handle on the software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매뉴얼을 며칠 동안 자세히 살펴본 후, 나는 소프트웨어를 파악하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand or grasp.
meanings.example: To work efficiently, it's important to have a handle on the procedures involved.
meanings.exampleKorean: 효율적으로 일하려면 관련된 절차를 파악하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit (a person); to pay a call to.
meanings.example: I will call on my grandmother this weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말에 할머니 댁을 방문할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be quiet; to refrain from being noisy.
meanings.example: The teacher told the noisy students to pipe down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 시끄러운 학생들에게 조용히 하라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch; to observe without participating.
meanings.example: My performance seems somehow always to get worse when there are other people looking on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 사람들이 지켜볼 때 내 공연은 항상 더 나빠지는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a telephone, to ring constantly or excessively.
meanings.example: I placed the advertisement yesterday, and the phone has been ringing off the hook ever since.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 광고를 냈는데, 그 후로 전화가 계속 울리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become sober; to cause to become sober (usually in terms of alcohol or drugs; sometimes figuratively).
meanings.example: He needs to sober up before he drives.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운전하기 전에 술이 깨야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To err, falter; to make a mistake, especially a seemingly small error.
meanings.example: I hope I don't slip up during my presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발표 중에 실수하지 않기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk with no planned destination; to meander.
meanings.example: Let's walk around the park this afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 오후에 공원을 돌아다니자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig or pull up by the roots.
meanings.example: The gardener had to root up the old tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정원사는 오래된 나무를 뿌리째 뽑아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put forth great effort to meet requirements, usually arbitrary, set by someone.
meanings.example: They really made the salesman jump through hoops before buying anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 물건을 사기 전에 영업사원에게 엄청난 노력을 하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease in temperature, activity, or temper.
meanings.example: You are getting a little too angry; you need to cool off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 화가 났으니 진정할 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover, as by asking or investigating.
meanings.example: I need to find out what time the train leaves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 몇 시에 출발하는지 알아내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry; to increase the speed of doing something.
meanings.example: Hurry up, or we'll be late!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러, 그렇지 않으면 늦을 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (someone) physical or mental fatigue.
meanings.example: The eight hour shift in the mine began to wear Tim down after a few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 광산에서의 8시간 근무는 몇 달 후 팀을 지치게 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: I was disqualified for cheating on my final exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 기말고사에서 부정행위를 하여 실격 처리되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink completely; to finish (one's drink).
meanings.example: Drink up! We have to leave soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 마셔! 곧 떠나야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support or sustain (someone), especially financially, for a limited period.
meanings.example: Could you lend me ten pounds to tide me over till payday?
meanings.exampleKorean: 월급날까지 지낼 수 있도록 10파운드만 빌려줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To specify, especially in detail.
meanings.example: They call out 304 stainless steel in the drawing, but the part was made from aluminum.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도면에는 304 스테인리스강이라고 명시되어 있지만, 부품은 알루미늄으로 제작되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: callout.
meanings.example: The technician responded to the callout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기술자는 호출에 응했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone tired; to exhaust.
meanings.example: The long hike tired out the children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹은 아이들을 몹시 지치게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block doors or windows with boards, either to prevent access or as protection from storms, etc.
meanings.example: The shop was boarded up after the hurricane.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가게는 허리케인 이후 판자로 막혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to purchase the entire stock or extent of something
meanings.example: The company was bought out by a larger competitor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 더 큰 경쟁사에 의해 전부 매수되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose strength, become weaker; to wane; to disappear or reduce slowly.
meanings.example: His memory of that day began to fade away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그날에 대한 그의 기억은 점점 희미해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A painting technique in which young women's clothes fade into the background.
meanings.example: Phillips became known for the "fade away, in which the main figures' clothing is the same color as the background, allowing the elements to partially blend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 필립스는 주인공들의 옷이 배경과 같은 색이어서 요소들이 부분적으로 섞이게 하는 "페이드 어웨이" 기법으로 유명해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply a procedure for verifying whether an arithmetic operation is probably correct or certainly incorrect using modulo characteristics of the digit 9.
meanings.example: We used the method of casting out nines to check our calculations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 계산을 확인하기 위해 9로 나누어 검산하는 방법을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin eating.
meanings.example: Let's dig in before the food gets cold.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식이 식기 전에 먹기 시작하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less virulent or strong; to subside.
meanings.example: We'll be able to sail safely across the bay once the storm dies down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 잦아들면 우리는 만을 안전하게 항해할 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: He was flat out after the marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마라톤 후에 그는 완전히 지쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: At top speed.
meanings.example: After 10 minutes of running flat out, he was out of breath.
meanings.exampleKorean: 10분간 전속력으로 달린 후 그는 숨이 찼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A total failure.
meanings.example: The project was a flat out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트는 완전한 실패였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail after a promising beginning; to disappoint expectations.
meanings.example: The business venture unfortunately flatted out due to lack of funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사업은 자금 부족으로 인해 안타깝게도 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: Where's your coat? Get it on and let's get out of here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코트 어디 있어? 입고 여기서 나가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overcome.
meanings.example: I'm trying to get over my fear of flying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 비행 공포증을 극복하려고 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time with unimportant activities; often used as an admonition to urge the other party to stop wasting time.
meanings.example: Stop fucking about and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume by lapping; to drink (something) quickly.
meanings.example: The cat's lapping up the milk in its bowl
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 그릇의 우유를 핥아 마시고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) completely full.
meanings.example: It costs a fortune these days to fill up.
