meanings.exampleKorean: 요즘은 가득 채우는 데 엄청난 돈이 든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decelerate.
meanings.example: When approaching a bend in the road, slow down, and speed up after leaving it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도로의 굽은 길에 접근할 때는 속도를 줄이고, 벗어난 후에는 속도를 높여라.

meanings.englishDefinition: prepositional down
meanings.example: to step down carefully from a train
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차에서 조심스럽게 내려오다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with the minor consequences of a previous action; to tidy up, finish, or complete.
meanings.example: Removing her name from the mailing list was her way of tying up loose ends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 이름을 우편물 목록에서 삭제하는 것은 그녀가 일을 마무리 짓는 방식이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease pestering or criticizing someone; to leave someone alone.
meanings.example: Please get off my back, I'll finish the report soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제발 좀 그만 괴롭혀요, 곧 보고서 끝낼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain idle when action is needed, perhaps because of fear, ignorance, complacency, indecisiveness, or dislike of the person(s) or situation.
meanings.example: Don't just sit on your hands; help us with this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손 놓고 있지 말고, 이 프로젝트를 도와줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: log out.
meanings.example: Please remember to log off when you are finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작업이 끝나면 로그아웃하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, to go away.
meanings.example: Bog off, you're not welcome here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져, 여기선 환영받지 못해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move in a direction opposite to that which one is facing, keeping one's attention on the thing in front being avoided.
meanings.example: WSR has remained in touch with GO-OP, despite the group deciding to back away from the project in the short term.
meanings.exampleKorean: WSR은 GO-OP와 계속 연락을 유지하고 있지만, GO-OP는 단기적으로 프로젝트에서 손을 떼기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become happier or less downcast.
meanings.example: She tried to cheer him up after his loss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 패배 후 그를 격려하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An encouragement to somebody who seems unhappy.
meanings.example: Cheer up! Things could be worse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘내! 더 나쁜 상황도 있을 수 있잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed or begin to succeed.
meanings.example: His big plans to get rich never quite seemed to get off the ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 큰 부자 계획은 결코 제대로 시작되지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie flat on one's board (face down) in order to be propelled shorewards by a broken wave.
meanings.example: I proned out in the white water, letting the wave break over me inside, riding the white water into the deep channel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 화이트 워터에서 엎드려 파도가 나를 덮치게 하고, 화이트 워터를 따라 깊은 수로로 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start (something); to set off traps; especially used for unstable situations that may magnify if disturbed.
meanings.example: Be cautious talking about religion or politics, lest you touch off an argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논쟁을 촉발하지 않도록 종교나 정치에 대해 말할 때는 조심하라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuel (a vehicle).
meanings.example: I can close down this bar / I can gas up my car / I can pack up and mail in my key
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 바 문을 닫을 수 있어 / 차에 기름을 넣을 수 있어 / 짐을 싸서 내 열쇠를 보낼 수 있어

meanings.englishDefinition: To carry out the supposed practice whereby locals (floor traders trading for their own account) buy or sell to push prices towards where they suspect stop loss orders lie, with a view to profiting from the resulting acceleration of the move when those orders hit the market.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 손절매 주문 유도 후 이익 실현
meanings.example: The traders tried to take out the stops to profit from the ensuing price surge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거래자들은 가격 급등으로 이익을 얻기 위해 손절매 주문을 유도하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate off from the main body, to move off to one side; as in troop movements on a parade ground or an organized retreat, or columns in a procession.
meanings.example: The fighter jets peeled off from the formation to attack the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전투기들이 적을 공격하기 위해 편대에서 이탈했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To serve (cooked food); to take (food) from the oven, pots, etc. and put in dishes to be served at table.
meanings.example: The writers are dishing up more of the same with this bland suburban sitcom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작가들은 이 밋밋한 교외 시트콤으로 또 같은 것을 내놓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill with rubbish or with needless articles.
meanings.example: Why do you clutter up your mind with such useless knowledge?
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그런 쓸모없는 지식으로 머리를 채우고 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To collide with.
meanings.example: He bumped into the wall. I guess that's a risk you take when you read while walking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 벽에 부딪혔다. 걸으면서 책을 읽는 위험을 감수하는 것이겠지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move backwards away from something.
meanings.example: The police told the crowd to back off and make some room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 군중에게 뒤로 물러나 공간을 비워달라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back onto.
meanings.example: The garden backs on to a small wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정원은 작은 숲으로 이어져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reverse a vehicle onto something.
meanings.example: He backed the car onto my toe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 내 발가락 위로 후진시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To communicate with a base etc, by telephone.
meanings.example: We should call in an expert to fix the plumbing issues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배관 문제를 해결하기 위해 전문가를 불러야 할 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To excavate something.
meanings.example: work involved a complete rewiring of the system, while the addition of four higher-capacity Mechan jacks and the increased length of the new train meant the entire floor (the length of four train carriages) had to be dug up, levelled and strengthened to meet tolerance requirements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작업에는 시스템의 완전한 재배선이 포함되었으며, 네 개의 고용량 Mechan 잭 추가와 새 열차의 길이 증가로 인해 전체 바닥(열차 네 칸 길이)을 파내고 수평을 맞추고 강화하여 허용 오차 요구 사항을 충족해야 했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To imprison or incarcerate (someone).
meanings.example: Make sure to lock up the office before you leave for the day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근하기 전에 반드시 사무실 문을 잠그세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recall; to cancel or call a halt to.
meanings.example: The outdoor concert was called off due to the heavy rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야외 콘서트가 폭설로 인해 취소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw close to a destination, especially using radar; to move towards a target.
meanings.example: The missile homed in on the target.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미사일이 목표물을 향해 접근했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study or cram, especially in order to refresh one's knowledge of a topic.
meanings.example: I need to bone up on my history before the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험 전에 역사 공부를 벼락치기해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift; to tug or pull upwards.
meanings.example: He hiked up his sagging trousers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 흘러내리는 바지를 위로 잡아당겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply impertinently to (someone); to talk back.
meanings.example: I'll thank you not to answer back like that! Where are your manners?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 말대꾸하지 않겠다고 말해주겠다! 예의가 어디 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into bottles.
meanings.example: Paul Brunner spoke over his shoulder, "In the last war the cruiser Königsberg hid in an African river and made her repairs." / Kurt answered, "Yes, sir, but she didn't get out again. The Royal Navy bottled her up and finally destroyed her."
meanings.exampleKorean: 폴 브루너는 어깨 너머로 말했다. "지난 전쟁 때 순양함 쾨니히스베르크는 아프리카 강에 숨어 수리를 했다." / 커트는 대답했다. "네, 각하, 하지만 다시 나오지 못했습니다. 영국 해군이 그녀를 봉쇄하고 결국 파괴했습니다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To substitute; to take somebody's place.
meanings.example: Can you sit in for him while he's gone?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 없는 동안 당신이 그의 자리를 대신할 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (a feature, subplot, item, or character) from a video game.
meanings.example: The developers decided to dummy out the unfinished quest line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개발자들은 미완성된 퀘스트 라인을 삭제하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go away.
meanings.example: The bouncer shouted “Buzz off!” and kicked me out of the establishment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원은 "꺼져!"라고 외치며 나를 그 업소에서 쫓아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave with a buzzing sound.
meanings.example: The bee was flying around my head, but then it buzzed off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벌이 내 머리 주위를 날아다니다가 윙윙거리며 떠나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquiesce to or comply with a proposal, despite lacking a desire or value for it.
meanings.example: I guess I did agree to go along with whatever her conditions were..." "We smooched on it. No backsies."
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 조건이 무엇이든 동의하기로 했던 것 같아..." "우리는 그것에 동의했어. 되돌릴 수 없어."

meanings.englishDefinition: To refrain from coming (near).
meanings.example: I'm keeping away from Joe until he calms down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조가 진정될 때까지는 그를 피하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repress.
meanings.example: The dictatorial state keeps down its dissidents very efficiently.
meanings.exampleKorean: 독재 국가는 반체제 인사들을 매우 효율적으로 억압한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay or cause to stay at a distance (from).
meanings.example: Please keep off the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잔디에 들어가지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overflow over the brim (of a saucepan etc.)
meanings.example: When he proposed, my heart brimmed over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 청혼했을 때, 내 마음은 감격으로 넘쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To automatically switch processing from a failed component in a critical system to its live spare or backup component.
meanings.example: If the Web server crashes, we can fail over to the spare in less than a second.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웹 서버가 충돌하면 1초도 안 되어 예비 서버로 자동 전환될 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to pursue and apprehend someone or something.
meanings.example: The police are trying to chase down the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자를 추적하여 잡으려고 노력 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be vigilant and aware often as an imperative to alert a person to danger.
meanings.example: Look out! There's a car coming around the corner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 모퉁이에서 차가 오고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill, especially to murder.
meanings.example: "Well, he's been wrong ever since I had to bump off Tim Harrigan. Talks about a fair break. As if I had a chance to let the old man get to a gun."
meanings.exampleKorean: "글쎄, 팀 해리건을 죽여야 했던 이후로 그는 잘못되었어. 공정한 기회에 대해 이야기해. 마치 내가 노인이 총을 잡게 할 기회가 있었던 것처럼."

meanings.englishDefinition: To garner enthusiastic or wild applause or laughter.
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes brought down the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언의 농담은 관객들을 열광시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: I approve; I agree.
meanings.example: Word up! That's a great idea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋아! 정말 좋은 생각이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pray, remain spiritually oriented and/or behave congruently with Christian doctrine.
meanings.example: You need to word up your prayers more often.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기도를 더 자주 해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inform or advise someone.
meanings.example: I'll word you up on the situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황에 대해 알려줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay awake waiting for somebody to return.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 누군가를 기다리느라 자지 않고 깨어 있다
meanings.example: Don't wait up for me, I'll be home late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 기다리지 말고 자. 늦게 들어갈 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anticipate, expect, or wait for, especially with a feeling of approval or pleasure; to be excited or eager to.
meanings.example: He was not looking forward to having to repair the downed power lines after the wind storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 강풍 피해 후 끊어진 전력선을 수리해야 하는 것을 내다보지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: a breakfast made of tomatoes, scrambled eggs, fried eggs, bacon, sausages, hash browns, baked beans, black pudding, chips, toast and mushrooms (or any combination thereof); full English breakfast
meanings.example: Though fattening, a fry up is without doubt my favourite way to start the day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 살찌기 쉽지만, 푸짐한 아침 식사는 의심할 여지 없이 내가 하루를 시작하는 가장 좋아하는 방법이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare by frying.
meanings.example: I'll just fry up a couple of eggs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계란 두 개만 프라이해 줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: go down the toilet: to fail; to degenerate rapidly.
meanings.example: The entire plan went down the tubes when they found they couldn't get strawberries in December.
meanings.exampleKorean: 12월에 딸기를 구할 수 없다는 것을 알게 되자 전체 계획이 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go down the toilet: to fail; to degenerate rapidly.
meanings.example: The project would seriously go down the pan if Mrs. Foster weren't here to keep it on the straight and narrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포스터 부인이 바로잡아주지 않는다면 그 프로젝트는 심각하게 실패할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break or spoil something, or make it inoperative, useless etc.
meanings.example: He really buggered up the engine trying to fix it himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스스로 고치려다 엔진을 정말 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Breakfast served to one in bed upon waking, commonly to show appreciation for the receiver.
meanings.example: The children made their mother breakfast in bed on her birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 어머니 생신에 침대에서 아침 식사를 만들어 드렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat one's breakfast while in bed.
meanings.example: Not that I "go in" for breakfasting in bed. It is a lazy, ignoble habit. A man ought to divide his life fairly between bed and board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 침대에서 아침을 먹는 것을 좋아하지 않는다. 그것은 게으르고 비열한 습관이다. 남자는 삶을 침대와 식사 사이에 공정하게 나누어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to fool around
meanings.example: Stop fooling about and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide (someone) with something; to furnish.
meanings.example: I need someone to fix me up with a tent and some camping gear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 텐트와 캠핑 장비를 마련해 줄 사람이 필요해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have each of a team's batting line-up positions complete an at-bat in the same half-inning.
meanings.example: Phillies thus batted around the lineup without a hit for the first time since July 17, 1992, per ESPN Stats & Info. If not for the inning-ending double play, the Phillies might have even sent 10 or 11 batters to the plate without recording a single hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 필리스는 1992년 7월 17일 이후 처음으로 한 이닝에 타순을 모두 돌렸지만 안타는 없었다. 이닝 종료 더블 플레이가 아니었다면 필리스는 안타 없이 10명 또는 11명의 타자를 타석에 세웠을 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone excessively, or far more than is wanted or appreciated.
meanings.example: He talked my ear off about his vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가에 대해 내 귀가 떨어져 나가도록 이야기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something or someone to pass from a higher to a lower place.
meanings.example: Eventually she was caught, and sent down for twelve years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결국 그녀는 체포되어 12년 형을 선고받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat hurriedly in order to do something more interesting.
meanings.example: Let's fuel up quickly before we go to the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트 가기 전에 빨리 때우자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to produce, deliver, or present (something) by inventing, creating, thinking of, or obtaining it.
meanings.example: How can you come up with such brilliant ideas?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떻게 그렇게 기발한 아이디어를 생각해낼 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a partial loss of vision having the characteristics of a brownout, to suffer a brownout.
meanings.example: After the accident, he experienced a brown out and couldn't see clearly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고 후 그는 부분적인 시력 상실을 겪었고 잘 볼 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: brownout.
meanings.example: The city experienced a brown out due to the heatwave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭염으로 인해 도시에 전력 공급 부족이 발생했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move upwards.
meanings.example: The price of gas is going up again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유가가 다시 오르고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join or close by sewing.
meanings.example: Applicant purchasing cranes with stitched up eyes—Bird's eyes found to be stitched up and bleeding while it was being conveyed by rail—Cruelty was caused by antecedent stitching up of eyes and not by manner of carriage—Obiter—It may well be that process of stitching up eyes of cranes is cruel practice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구매자가 눈이 꿰매진 크레인을 구매함—운송 중에 눈이 꿰매져 피가 나는 것이 발견됨—잔인함은 눈을 꿰매는 선행 과정에 의한 것이지 운송 방식에 의한 것이 아님—여담—크레인의 눈을 꿰매는 과정은 잔인한 관행일 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call for a time-out.
meanings.example: Time out! Let's take a break.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타임아웃! 잠시 쉬자.

meanings.englishDefinition: time-out.
meanings.example: The player was given a time out for arguing with the referee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 심판과 말다툼을 하여 타임아웃을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end (an incomplete task) after a time limit.
meanings.example: The download timed out because your Internet connection was disrupted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인터넷 연결이 끊어져 다운로드가 시간 초과로 중단되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop or disable a device by turning a switch or a lever.
meanings.example: To switch off the instrument when the clockwork is not wound up, and to remind the subscriber of doing so, there is provided an indicator, x, which is worked in the following manner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태엽이 감겨 있지 않을 때 기기를 끄고, 구독자에게 그렇게 하도록 상기시키기 위해 다음과 같이 작동하는 표시기 x가 제공된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start or enable a device by turning a switch or a lever.
meanings.example: Please switch on the light.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불 좀 켜주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To heat up:
meanings.example: The engine started to hot up after a few miles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 마일 달리자 엔진이 뜨거워지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: whale on.
meanings.example: The wrench wouldn’t budge, even though Tony was just wailing on it with a big old sledge hammer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토니가 큰 망치로 세게 내리쳤음에도 불구하고 렌치는 꿈쩍도 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike an opponent heavily and repeatedly in a fight.
meanings.example: The boxer was whaling on his opponent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 상대방을 맹렬히 공격하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know (something or someone) very thoroughly.
meanings.example: He's still new to their system, but he knows databases inside and out and will understand the rest soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아직 그들의 시스템에 익숙하지 않지만, 데이터베이스는 속속들이 알고 있으며 곧 나머지도 이해할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a job.
meanings.example: Let's wind up our bottoms and head home after this meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 회의가 끝나면 하던 일을 마무리하고 집으로 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall fast asleep; to sleep soundly.
meanings.example: He conked out on the couch after a long day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 하루 끝에 소파에서 뻗어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be fooled; to fall for.
meanings.example: I wonder how many people will get taken in by their addition of a fancy-looking front panel to a crummy device.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 조잡한 장치에 화려해 보이는 전면 패널을 추가한 것에 얼마나 많은 사람들이 속아 넘어갈지 궁금하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit or cease completely, or to promise to quit, as a bad habit.
meanings.example: One of these days, he thinks he will swear off his nightly television regimen and get some exercise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언젠가 그는 매일 밤 TV 보는 습관을 끊고 운동을 하겠다고 다짐할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce (something) quickly, especially food.
meanings.example: She can whip up a delicious meal in minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 몇 분 안에 맛있는 식사를 뚝딱 만들 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exit a user account in a computer system, so that one is not recognized until signing in again.
meanings.example: Remember to log out of your account when you're finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끝났을 때 계정에서 로그아웃하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise to the same level or status; to improve so as to make as good as others.
meanings.example: The player leveled up after completing the quest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 플레이어는 퀘스트를 완료한 후 레벨업했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex
meanings.example: It's all well and good having a relationship, but at the end of the day all I want to do is get my leg over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관계가 있다는 것은 좋지만, 결국 내가 하고 싶은 것은 성관계뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen attentively.
meanings.example: The dog pricked up its ears at the sound of the doorbell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개가 초인종 소리에 귀를 쫑긋 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extremely emotional or excitable; to lose control of one's emotions.
meanings.example: Don't make me wig out, or I'll start shouting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 흥분하게 만들지 마, 그러면 소리를 지르기 시작할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: A chaotic or crazy episode.
meanings.example: The concert turned into a wig out when the power went out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정전이 되자 콘서트는 혼란스러운 사건으로 변했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal; to escape with ill-gotten gains.
meanings.example: Thieves made away with £50,000 of jewellery in last night's heist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들이 어젯밤 강도 사건에서 5만 파운드의 보석을 훔쳐 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To renovate or to convert to a different use, particularly houses, offices, or rooms within them.
meanings.example: We're going to make over the garage into a guest suite.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차고를 손님용 스위트룸으로 개조할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To damage, especially to a significant extent; to treat harshly; to produce ill effects in.
meanings.example: The storm did a number on the coastal towns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍은 해안 마을에 큰 피해를 입혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or decorate in a showy or elaborate way.
meanings.example: She loved to gussie up for special occasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 특별한 날을 위해 화려하게 꾸미는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emerge or become known, especially unexpectedly.
meanings.example: Unless anything comes up, I'll be there every day this week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 특별한 일이 없는 한, 이번 주 매일 거기에 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An opportunity
meanings.example: I'm gonna pop some tagsOnly got 20$ in my pocketI'm, I'm, I'm huntin', lookin' for a come upThis is fucking awesome
meanings.exampleKorean: 태그 몇 개를 살 거야, 주머니엔 20달러밖에 없어, 기회를 찾고 있어. 정말 멋지다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A language exercise in which one or more words are replaced with a blank line and the reader is tasked with supplying a valid replacement.
meanings.example: The test consisted of fill-in-the-blank sentences.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험은 빈칸 채우기 문장들로 구성되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a single [[#Noun|fill in the blank]] question.
meanings.example: The Zionists intended to shape the perceptions of a global public with limited interest in or understanding of the issues, filling in the blanks with their own narrative.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시오니스트들은 문제에 대한 관심이나 이해가 제한적인 세계 대중의 인식을 자신들의 서사로 빈칸을 채워 형성하고자 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain somebody's permission to skip or cancel an event that one has previously arranged with them.
meanings.example: I wonder if I can beg off going to the meeting that day, since it will take me an extra two hours out of my way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그날 회의에 가지 않아도 된다는 허락을 받을 수 있을지 궁금하다. 그 회의는 가는 데 두 시간이 더 걸리기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transmit in succession, as from father to son, or from predecessor to successor.
meanings.example: Fables are handed down from age to age.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우화는 세대에서 세대로 전해진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or circulate in a confused or disorderly manner within a limited area.
meanings.example: The crowd milled around the entrance, waiting for the doors to open.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중은 문이 열리기를 기다리며 입구 주위를 서성였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encounter, discover; to come upon.
meanings.example: Come on, you can do it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘내, 할 수 있어!

meanings.englishDefinition: Come along with me; join me in going.
meanings.example: I'll show you where the auditorium is. Come on!
meanings.exampleKorean: 강당이 어디인지 보여줄게. 같이 가자!

meanings.englishDefinition: To expose to air; to leave open or spread out, as to allow odor or moisture to dissipate.
meanings.example: If you air out your sleeping bag after you use it, it will smell better the next time you get in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 침낭을 사용한 후 환기시키면 다음번에 사용할 때 더 좋은 냄새가 날 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere with, or intrude on something forcibly.
meanings.example: He tried to muscle in on their business deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들의 사업 거래에 강제로 끼어들려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere, or intrude forcibly.
meanings.example: Government's £500 million Restoring Your Railway initiative - awarding small grants to put back lines that should never have been closed - has turned into a muddle because backers of multi-million pound projects have also muscled in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부의 5억 파운드 규모의 철도 복원 계획은 수백만 파운드 규모의 프로젝트 지지자들이 끼어들면서 혼란에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think carefully about (something), and give it due consideration.
meanings.example: She reflected on her country's role in history.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 역사 속 자국의 역할에 대해 숙고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disconnect at the end of a phone call.
meanings.example: He thanked him and rang off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그에게 감사하다고 말하고 전화를 끊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to manhandle or beat up
meanings.example: The gangsters roughed him up a little.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갱스터들이 그를 조금 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal; to take away.
meanings.example: to carry away a child
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이를 유괴하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard or reject something.
meanings.example: However, with the dainty volume my quondam friend sprang into fame. At the same time he cast off the chrysalis of a commonplace existence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만, 섬세한 볼륨으로 나의 옛 친구는 명성을 얻었다. 동시에 그는 평범한 존재의 번데기를 벗어던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start the first row of knitting by putting stitches on a needle.
meanings.example: She showed me how to cast on stitches for a scarf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 스카프를 뜨기 위해 코를 꿰는 방법을 보여주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cast-on.
meanings.example: The cast-on row needs to be firm but not too tight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코 꿰기 단은 단단하지만 너무 꽉 조이지 않아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply impertinently or rudely; to answer in a cheeky or rude manner.
meanings.example: Jane talked back a little too often.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제인은 너무 자주 말대꾸를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or irritate someone; to bother someone.
meanings.example: His constant humming is starting to get on my nerves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 콧노래가 나를 짜증나게 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An expression of celebration.
meanings.example: The circus is coming to town? Rock on, dude!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서커스가 마을에 온다고? 신나게 즐겨, 친구!

meanings.englishDefinition: To party to music, especially rock, or to continue to perform any such music; to maintain a lifestyle in keeping with, or inspired by, youth rock culture.
meanings.example: Let's rock on all night long!
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤새도록 신나게 놀자!

meanings.englishDefinition: to make a gradual ascent or increase
meanings.example: The ivy climbed up the side of the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 담쟁이덩굴이 집 옆면을 타고 올라갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turnaround.
meanings.example: The company experienced a significant turnaround in its financial performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 재정 실적에서 상당한 전환을 경험했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically rotate (usually around a vertical axis) for a half turn (180 degrees), a whole turn (360 degrees), or an indefinite amount.
meanings.example: The car turned around in the narrow street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 차는 좁은 거리에서 회전했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coil the tail of a rope on deck so as to present a neat appearance.
meanings.example: The sailor expertly cheeses down the rope after securing the boat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원은 배를 고정한 후 능숙하게 밧줄 끝을 둥글게 감았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time, or to fool about.
meanings.example: Stop farting around and get your work done!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일을 끝내!

meanings.englishDefinition: To be a tributary of another river or waterway.
meanings.example: The River Frome feeds into the Avon in the centre of Bristol.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프롬 강은 브리스톨 중심부에서 에이번 강으로 흘러든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grope; to explore an area with one's hands, usually in search of something.
meanings.example: I felt around in my pockes for my keyring in vain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 허사로 열쇠고리를 찾기 위해 주머니 속을 더듬거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer images or animation from videotape or digital files to a traditional celluloid film print.
meanings.example: The studio decided to film out the animated movie for a theatrical release.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스튜디오는 극장 개봉을 위해 애니메이션 영화를 필름으로 옮기기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wave one's arms (or upper body) about violently, rather like a flail.
meanings.example: He flailed about in the water, trying to stay afloat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물에 뜨려고 필사적으로 물속에서 허우적거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to obstruct, hinder, or interfere
meanings.example: Don't get in the way of my work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 일에 방해되지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend money foolishly or indiscriminately; to waste money without regard of the consequences.
meanings.example: The young boy liked to throw money away at the video arcade and candy store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 어린 소년은 오락실과 가게에서 돈을 펑펑 쓰는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fulfill or honor (a promise, prediction etc).
meanings.example: He promised to pay back the loan and he made good on his word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대출금을 갚겠다고 약속했고, 그 약속을 지켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To temporarily add something to an existing part, especially to add an additional sail to take advantage of a fair wind.
meanings.example: We spring out of our berths, clap on a monkey-jacket and south-wester, and tumble up the ladder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 침대에서 뛰쳐나와, 재킷과 방수모자를 급히 걸치고 사다리를 올라갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by clawing.
meanings.example: The cat clawed off the wallpaper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 발톱으로 벽지를 긁어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To position oneself between an opposition player and the basket in anticipation of getting a rebound.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 리바운드를 위해 상대 선수와 골대 사이를 막다
meanings.example: The center boxed out his defender to secure the rebound.
meanings.exampleKorean: 센터는 리바운드를 확보하기 위해 수비수를 박스아웃했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join something, after signing.
meanings.example: I never signed on for this.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 이런 일에 서명한 적이 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex.
meanings.example: They were caught trying to get their end away in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 공원에서 성교하려다 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage easily to hit pitching by the starting and relief pitchers of the opposing team, despite the types of pitches thrown or the skill of the pitchers.
meanings.example: The strong baseball team really roughed up the bullpen in their 34-0 rout against their foes; the coach used nine relievers and they still couldn't get anybody out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강팀은 상대 투수진을 상대로 34-0 대승을 거두며 맹타를 휘둘렀다. 감독은 9명의 구원 투수를 투입했지만 아무도 막지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ease up on after inflicting great punishment.
meanings.example: Doing the opponents an obvious favor, the football coach decided to call off the dogs early after his team was up 56-0.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상대 팀에게 명백한 호의를 베풀며, 축구 감독은 팀이 56-0으로 앞선 후 일찍 공격을 멈추기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow or supervise someone too closely, causing discomfort for that person.
meanings.example: My boss never lets me get on with my work. He's always breathing down my neck and checking up on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 상사는 내가 일을 제대로 하도록 내버려 두지 않는다. 그는 항상 내 뒤를 따라다니며 나를 감시한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover from a negative situation without seemingly any damage.
meanings.example: We thought he'd die from the crash, but he bounced back to normal after 10 days in hospital.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그가 사고로 죽을 줄 알았지만, 그는 병원에서 10일간의 치료 후 정상으로 회복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retrieve from personal or computer memory.
meanings.example: The computer can call up the requested data in seconds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터는 요청된 데이터를 몇 초 만에 불러올 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Become further apart.
meanings.example: The police spread out to search a wider area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 더 넓은 지역을 수색하기 위해 퍼져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To actively search and compare options before making a selection or choice of vendor.
meanings.example: He's gone to shop around for a new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 차를 사기 위해 이 가게 저 가게 알아보고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to penetrate
meanings.example: One must be of legal age to enter into a contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계약에 참여하려면 법적 연령이어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare oneself for something demanding.
meanings.example: Man came into this world, not to sit down and muse, not to befog himself with vain subtleties, but to gird up his loins and to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인간은 이 세상에 앉아서 명상하거나, 헛된 미묘함으로 자신을 흐리게 하기 위해 온 것이 아니라, 허리띠를 졸라매고 일하기 위해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat (someone) roughly or without care, control, moderation, or respect; to act in a bullying manner toward (someone); to damage (someone or something).
meanings.example: The manager tended to ride roughshod over his employees' opinions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 직원들의 의견을 무시하고 함부로 대하는 경향이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was caught jilling off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위행위를 하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To identify among a group of similar subjects, or in a scene where the subject might not be readily seen or noticed, with a gesture of the body.
meanings.example: He pointed out the little brown bird in the tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나무에 있는 작은 갈색 새를 가리켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb or draw in a substance, especially all of that substance.
meanings.example: The tissue soaked up all of the spilled milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 휴지는 쏟아진 우유를 모두 흡수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stash or hide; to hoard, collect, save, or accumulate; to create a reserve, stash, or hoard of some supply.
meanings.example: He had bits of string, cans of nails and lots of other useful tidbits squirreled away throughout his garage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차고 곳곳에 실 조각, 못 통, 그리고 다른 유용한 잡동사니들을 몰래 저장해 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave (school, a race, etc.) prematurely and voluntarily.
meanings.example: Nothing went well in high school, so he dropped out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고등학교에서 아무것도 잘 풀리지 않아서, 그는 중퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: drift off, fall asleep To fall asleep.
meanings.example: He dropped off to sleep while reading a book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책을 읽다가 잠이 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something to a responsible person.
meanings.example: I found a wallet on the street, so I handed it in to the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 길에서 지갑을 주워서 경찰서에 제출했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A simple, short putt very close to the hole.
meanings.example: He made a simple tap-in to win the championship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 챔피언십 우승을 위해 간단한 탭인으로 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kick a simple shot, without opposition, from close range into the goal.
meanings.example: Bent was again left with the simplest of finishes when Rooney picked out Young and he unselfishly crossed for Bent to tap in from inside the six-yard area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 루니가 영을 선택했고, 그는 영리하게 벤트에게 크로스하여 벤트가 6야드 구역 안에서 쉽게 골을 넣을 수 있게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit the first shot of the hole, from the tee.
meanings.example: I always check the wind direction before I tee off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 항상 티샷하기 전에 바람 방향을 확인한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study intensely.
meanings.example: I mugged up on the meaning of road signs before taking my driving test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 운전면허 시험을 보기 전에 도로 표지판의 의미를 집중적으로 공부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To protrude.
meanings.example: His tongue was sticking out in concentration.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 집중하느라 혀를 내밀고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something mechanical, an act that often used to involve cranking.
meanings.example: Let's crank up the old motorcycle and take it for a spin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 오토바이를 시동 걸고 한번 타러 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen closely; to pay attention.
meanings.example: I'm only gonna say this once, so you really need to listen up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 번만 말할 테니 잘 들어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange for cash.
meanings.example: The gamblers cashed in their remaining chips at the end of the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도박꾼들은 밤이 끝날 무렵 남은 칩을 현금으로 바꿨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call back (hounds); to recall.
meanings.example: The music harks back to a simpler time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 음악은 더 단순했던 시대를 연상시킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hark-back.
meanings.example: The novel contains many hark-backs to the author's childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소설에는 작가의 어린 시절에 대한 많은 회상이 담겨 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agitate by shaking.
meanings.example: If you shake up a can of cola and then open it, you get an explosion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콜라 캔을 흔들고 열면 폭발이 일어난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go away.
meanings.example: Bugger off! You're joking, aren't you?
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져! 농담하는 거지, 그렇지?

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, go away, disappear.
meanings.example: We tried to catch him, but he had already buggered off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그를 잡으려 했지만, 그는 이미 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force to leave or go away.
meanings.example: The guard dog drove off the intruders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비견이 침입자들을 쫓아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: drive-off.
meanings.example: The drive-off was successful in deterring further attempts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몰아내는 데 성공하여 추가 시도를 막았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.Masturbation
meanings.example: He was caught wanking off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위행위를 하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To position (objects, people, time slots, etc.) at regular intervals with a calculated space between them.
meanings.example: She spaced out the yoga mats as much as she could while leaving room for some walkways.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 통로 공간을 남겨두면서 요가 매트를 최대한 간격을 두고 배치했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To operate as effectively as possible.
meanings.example: The team is firing on all cylinders this season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 이번 시즌 최고의 성적을 내고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep, especially unintentionally.
meanings.example: I was so tired that I nodded off during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 피곤해서 회의 중에 꾸벅꾸벅 졸았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; to get lost.
meanings.example: Just push off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 가버리고 날 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide an error or other material on a surface by covering it with correcting fluid.
meanings.example: He used white-out to correct the typo in his essay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 에세이의 오타를 수정하기 위해 수정액을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute in portions or shares; to apportion or allot.
meanings.example: The teacher portioned out the cookies to the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들에게 쿠키를 나누어 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak in a language unknown to the speaker, especially when in a state of religious ecstasy; glossolalia.
meanings.example: Acts 19:6: When Paul laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사도행전 19:6: 바울이 그들에게 안수하자 성령이 그들에게 임하시어 그들이 방언도 하고 예언도 하니라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience great luck; to be extremely fortunate or lucky.
meanings.example: I lucked out and got the last two tickets to the big show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 운이 좋아서 그 빅쇼의 마지막 두 장의 티켓을 구했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove anything by stripping, e.g. items of clothing or paint from the side of a ship.
meanings.example: He stripped off his wet clothes and put on dry ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젖은 옷을 벗고 마른 옷으로 갈아입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carry out (something planned or promised); to proceed with or continue with.
meanings.example: If you decide to go through with the surgery, remember to leave time to recover.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수술을 하기로 결정했다면, 회복할 시간을 남겨두는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stage a walkout or strike.
meanings.example: The workers decided to walk out over the unfair wages.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노동자들은 불공정한 임금 때문에 파업하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon or desert someone, especially a spouse.
meanings.example: He walked out on his family when the children were young.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아이들이 어렸을 때 가족을 버리고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reverse a vehicle into a space.
meanings.example: She carefully backed into the parking spot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 주차 공간으로 조심스럽게 후진 주차했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In the past time of.
meanings.example: back in the time of the dinosaurs
meanings.exampleKorean: 공룡 시대에

meanings.englishDefinition: wear rose-colored glasses.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 장밋빛 안경을 끼고 보다, 긍정적으로만 보다
meanings.example: You're looking through rose-tinted glasses if you think this job will be easy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 일이 쉬울 거라고 생각한다면 장밋빛 안경을 끼고 보는 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To select (someone or something) from a objects and highlight them or treat them differently.
meanings.example: The teacher singled out the student who had the best answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 가장 좋은 답을 한 학생을 따로 뽑았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease paying attention.
meanings.example: As soon as I turned my back, he started writing on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 외면하자마자 그는 벽에 글씨를 쓰기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish completely.
meanings.example: He finished off the last slice of pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 피자 조각을 마저 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bribe, especially to deter oversight.
meanings.example: I thought the guards would give us trouble, but apparently he had paid them off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원들이 우리를 귀찮게 할 줄 알았는데, 알고 보니 그가 뇌물을 준 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay (a debt or the lender) so as to provide the entire amount of money owed.
meanings.example: 2008 November 21, Graham Linehan, The IT Crowd, Season 3, Episode 1:
meanings.exampleKorean: 2008년 11월 21일, 그레이엄 라이너, 아이티 크라우드, 시즌 3, 에피소드 1:

meanings.englishDefinition: To overreact to or overstate; to treat too seriously or be overly concerned with.
meanings.example: I don’t think we need to blow it out of proportion. There’s a problem, and we should fix it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것을 과장할 필요는 없다고 생각해요. 문제가 있고, 해결해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become brighter
meanings.example: Her kindness brightened up my day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 친절함이 내 하루를 밝게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mature and become an adult.
meanings.example: What do you want to be when you grow up?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어른이 되면 무엇이 되고 싶니?

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to criticize childish behavior, especially that considered unreasonable for one's age.
meanings.example: You're giggling because I said "butt"? Grow up, seriously.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 "엉덩이"라고 해서 웃는 거야? 진지하게, 어른스러워져.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop or slow a horse by pulling the reins
meanings.example: The government has no other option than to rein in public spending.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 공공 지출을 억제하는 것 외에는 다른 선택의 여지가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider or regard; to include (as in an estimate or plan) or pay attention to; to notice; to allow for, factor in.
meanings.example: We must take into account the budget constraints before making a decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정을 내리기 전에 예산 제약을 반드시 고려해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an area or a thing clean; to pick up a mess; to tidy.
meanings.example: Clean up your room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 방을 청소해라.

meanings.englishDefinition: cleanup.
meanings.example: The cleanup after the party took hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티 후 정리에 몇 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move into (an object), such that one ends up inside it.
meanings.example: She got into the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 차에 탔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a model or demonstration; to create a preliminary version or sample.
meanings.example: The design team finished the mock up for the new app.
meanings.exampleKorean: 디자인 팀이 새 앱의 모형을 완성했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ruin unintentionally.
meanings.example: He really mucked up the project with his careless mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부주의한 실수로 프로젝트를 정말 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to fall by shooting.
meanings.example: The carnival game involved shooting down tin cans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카니발 게임은 깡통을 총으로 맞추는 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease or fail at a regimen of self-improvement or reform; to lapse back into an old habit or addiction.
meanings.example: Though he fell off the wagon several times, he eventually succeeded in quitting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여러 번 금단 증상을 겪었지만, 결국 금연에 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent from moving (from a building, in a city, etc) due to a large snowfall.
meanings.example: We were snowed in for three days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 사흘 동안 눈 때문에 갇혀 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To step on the tines of a garden rake, causing the handle of the rake to rise from the ground rapidly, striking the person walking in the face.
meanings.example: He stepped on a rake again and got hurt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 또 같은 실수를 해서 다쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Get busy with; become occupied with; become immersed in.
meanings.example: 1998, Gary McKay, In Good Company
meanings.exampleKorean: 1998년, 게리 맥케이, 인 굿 컴퍼니

meanings.englishDefinition: To add stitches to the knitting needle that were previously bound off, or that belong to the selvage, during the process of knitting or entrelac.
meanings.example: You need to pick up stitches along the edge to create a neat border.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깔끔한 테두리를 만들기 위해 가장자리를 따라 코를 주워야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Stitches that were added to the row, during knitting.
meanings.example: The pattern instructs you to pick up 20 stitches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 패턴은 20코를 줍도록 지시합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join into a team, or into teams.
meanings.example: They decided to team up for the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 프로젝트를 위해 협력하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to expect or anticipate; to be about to suffer, generally said of something unpleasant.
meanings.example: The weather forecast says we are in for three feet of snow over the next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일기 예보에 따르면 다음 주에 3피트의 눈이 내릴 것이라고 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter suddenly or unexpectedly while something is happening; to intrude or interrupt by entering.
meanings.example: He accidentally walked in on me while I was undressing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내가 옷을 벗고 있을 때 우연히 들어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sparse; to remove some of a group of newly-planted plants in order to allow the remaining ones to grow unimpeded.
meanings.example: And later on, when the crowd thinned out / I was just about to do the same / She was standing there in back of my chair / Said to me, "Don't I know your name?"
meanings.exampleKorean: 나중에 군중이 썰렁해졌을 때 / 나도 막 그러려고 할 참이었어 / 그녀는 내 의자 뒤에 서서 / 나에게 말했지, "나 당신 이름 알지 않아요?"

meanings.englishDefinition: To be more patient; to calm down.
meanings.example: Keep your shirt on! We'll get there eventually.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정해! 결국 도착할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contact a person or organisation about a particular matter.
meanings.example: You should get onto the manufacturers and complain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제조업체에 연락해서 불평해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold up one's end of a bargain; to fulfill one's promise or obligation.
meanings.example: I'm holding up my end and you had better hold up yours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 몫을 하고 있으니 당신도 당신 몫을 하는 게 좋을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put on a pedestal.
meanings.example: He placed his wife on a pedestal, believing she could do no wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내가 잘못을 저지를 수 없다고 믿으며 아내를 숭배했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To see from another's point of view; to feel what another feels.
meanings.example: How would it be, if you were standing in my shoes?
meanings.exampleKorean: 만약 당신이 내 입장이었다면 어땠을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: away
meanings.example: He blew away the dust which had collected on the book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책에 쌓인 먼지를 날려버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase something suddenly.
meanings.example: The company decided to bump up the prices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 가격을 갑자기 올리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay out, to hand over
meanings.example: The hard drive broke down, so I forked out five hundred dollars for a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하드 드라이브가 고장 나서 새것을 사느라 500달러를 지불했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cheer and support (a team or individual); to barrack; to root for.
meanings.example: The crowd cheered on their team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중은 팀을 응원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform something specific.
meanings.example: Children often act out their feelings when they can't express them verbally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 말로 표현할 수 없을 때 종종 감정을 행동으로 표현한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dissipate as the result of heat.
meanings.example: The fog burned off by mid-morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안개는 아침 중반쯤 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or spread from person to person.
meanings.example: The rumor is going around that Mr. X and Ms. Y are having an affair.   There's a cough going around.
meanings.exampleKorean: X씨와 Y씨가 불륜이라는 소문이 돌고 있다. 감기가 유행하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close (an opening) with a cork, cork-like object or other improvised obstruction.
meanings.example: He used a piece of putty to temporarily bung up the leaking gutter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새는 홈통을 임시로 막기 위해 퍼티 조각을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expend or contribute money to an expense or purchase.
meanings.example: We laid out a lot of money on the new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새 차에 많은 돈을 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: apart
meanings.example: A frayed rope will pull apart under tension.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마모된 밧줄은 장력 하에서 갈기갈기 찢어질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take steps to make something appear more attractive or compelling.
meanings.example: They tried to goose up the sales figures before the end of the quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 분기 말 전에 매출 수치를 끌어올리려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat someone very severely (especially in the head).
meanings.example: The mugger threatened to beat his brains out if he didn't hand over his wallet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강도는 지갑을 넘기지 않으면 머리를 때려 부수겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose a game of craps by rolling a seven.
meanings.example: I'm sorry to crap out on the plan at the last minute, but I'm sick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 순간에 계획에서 빠지게 되어 미안하지만, 몸이 안 좋아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To diminish or lessen gradually; to become or make smaller, slower, quieter, etc.
meanings.example: Interest in the project began to taper off after the initial excitement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초기 흥분이 가라앉은 후 프로젝트에 대한 관심이 점점 줄어들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To don a pair of ear buds in preparation for listening to a portable sound system.
meanings.example: He budded up his headphones and started listening to music.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 헤드폰을 끼고 음악을 듣기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supervise.
meanings.example: As the new editor, his job is to ride herd on the staff and check on the tone of the articles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 편집장으로서 그의 임무는 직원들을 감독하고 기사의 논조를 확인하는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon or leave; to shun; to refuse.
meanings.example: He decided to walk away from his job after expressing much dissatisfaction with his boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상사에 대한 불만을 많이 표현한 후 직장을 그만두기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill a hole or cavity, or block (an opening or passage), as with a plug.
meanings.example: Please stop up the holes in the wall before painting it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트칠을 하기 전에 벽에 난 구멍들을 막아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arouse or excite (passion or action, etc.).
meanings.example: The politician's speech stirred up a lot of controversy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인의 연설은 많은 논란을 불러일으켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect (an electrical device) to a plug socket.
meanings.example: Don't forget to plug in the lamp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 램프 플러그 꽂는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake, to go wrong.
meanings.example: You really fouled up this time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번에 정말 망쳤구나.

meanings.englishDefinition: To initiate something by providing the necessary conditions.
meanings.example: The assassination of Archduke Ferdinand sparked off World War I.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대공 황태자 암살 사건은 제1차 세계 대전을 촉발시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop paying attention and think about something else, or to think about nothing
meanings.example: I often zone out during long and boring meetings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 길고 지루한 회의 중에 종종 멍하니 정신을 놓곤 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clown around: to behave clownishly; to joke idly.
meanings.example: They were yukking it up at the party all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 밤새도록 파티에서 깔깔대며 농담했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the price of something between its wholesale and retail phase.
meanings.example: Retailers often mark up goods by 50% or more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소매업체는 종종 상품에 50% 이상 가격을 붙인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go through, to travel through, to transit or lie across a place or from one place to another.
meanings.example: We will pass through the town on our way to the coast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 해안으로 가는 길에 그 마을을 통과할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A framed, window-like aperture in the interior wall of a house, usually between a kitchen and dining room, through which items (especially food) can be passed. A serving hatch.
meanings.example: The waiter used the pass-through to deliver the food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웨이터는 음식 전달 창을 통해 음식을 전달했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease to boil when heat is no longer applied.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Farmers' Alliance History and Agricultural Digest| A. Dunning
meanings.exampleKorean: 농민 동맹 역사와 농업 소화집 | A. 더닝

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to (a difficult agreement or settlement) after much arguing and conflict.
meanings.example: Two years after the flood, my lawyer managed to hammer out a settlement with my insurance company over the damages.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수 발생 2년 후, 나의 변호사는 피해에 대해 보험사와 합의를 이끌어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live so as to subdue or refute.
meanings.example: It took him years to live down the embarrassment of his first stage performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 첫 무대 공연의 수치심을 잊고 극복하는 데는 몇 년이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce (something) little by little, either via discrete phases of increase or (by extension) by continuous gradations; to introduce in phases, or as if by phases (gradually); to begin (something) gradually.
meanings.example: The new safety regulations will be phased in over the next six months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 안전 규정들이 향후 6개월에 걸쳐 점진적으로 도입될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or relinquish the use of (something) little by little, either via discrete diminishing phases or (by extension) by continuous gradations; to remove in phases, or as if by phases (gradually).
meanings.example: The company decided to phase out the old model by the end of the year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 연말까지 구형 모델을 단계적으로 단종하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meddle; to stick one's nose into (something)
meanings.example: I don't want her coming and sticking her oar in – she doesn't know anything about our financials.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 와서 참견하는 것을 원치 않아. 그녀는 우리 재정에 대해 아무것도 모르거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink the entirety of in a short period; originally and especially, in a single gulp.
meanings.example: He drank off the entire glass of water in one gulp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 단숨에 들이켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An expression of anger and contempt directed at someone, especially after that individual has committed a very serious crime or transgression.
meanings.example: Wrex: I say every human can burn in hell now--and you'll be there to greet them!
meanings.exampleKorean: 렉스: 이제 모든 인간은 지옥에서 불탈 수 있다고 말하겠어. 그리고 너도 그들을 맞이하기 위해 거기에 있을 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, especially to leave a difficult or dangerous environment with all possible haste.
meanings.example: We need to get out of Dodge before the storm hits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 닥치기 전에 이 곳을 떠나야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold forth about something in an opinionated manner.
meanings.example: I'm sick of his sounding off about how he thinks this country should be run!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 이 나라가 어떻게 운영되어야 한다고 생각하는지에 대해 자기 의견을 강하게 말하는 것에 진저리가 나.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop what one is doing, especially in the context of a business.
meanings.example: The small bookstore decided to close up shop after fifty years in business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작은 서점은 50년간의 영업 후 문을 닫기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A send off; a farewell celebration.
meanings.example: They threw a big send out for their colleague who was moving abroad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 해외로 이사 가는 동료를 위해 성대한 환송회를 열었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To issue, dispatch or transmit.
meanings.example: Please send out the invitations by the end of the week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주 말까지 초대장을 발송해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an order for something to be delivered, especially takeaway food.
meanings.example: There's nothing in the fridge. Let's send out for pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 냉장고에 아무것도 없어. 피자나 배달 주문하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb, to soak up.
meanings.example: Use a paper towel to sop up the spilled water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쏟아진 물을 닦아내기 위해 키친타월을 사용하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute one's share of a payment; to provide funds; to pay what is due.
meanings.example: Everyone had to ante up their share for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두 파티 비용을 각자 부담해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something in portions; to apportion or dole out.
meanings.example: The judge will mete out the punishment according to the law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 법에 따라 처벌을 내릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To establish a connection with (something), especially in order to take advantage.
meanings.example: When he ran out of money, he decided to tap into his trust fund.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돈이 떨어졌을 때, 그는 신탁 기금을 활용하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: See you later.
meanings.example: I'm heading out now, peace out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나 이제 갈게, 안녕!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unconscious; to pass out.
meanings.example: He drank too much and ended up peaced-out on the couch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 마셔서 소파에서 의식을 잃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A message signaling that the game has ended, usually because the player failed (for example by losing all of their lives) but sometimes following successful completion of the game.
meanings.example: Before I could figure out what the hell was the object of the game, I hear the thing go boom-boom, and the screen lights up with a sign: game over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게임의 목적이 무엇인지 알아내기도 전에, 나는 쾅쾅 소리가 나는 것을 듣고 화면에 게임 오버라는 표시가 떴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An appearance of a game over message.
meanings.example: When you die too many times in this game, you get a game over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 게임에서 너무 많이 죽으면 게임 오버가 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive a 'game over' message.
meanings.example: If you fail this level, you'll game over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 레벨에서 실패하면 게임 오버될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To announce or record one's own arrival at a hotel, airport etc.
meanings.example: We got to the hotel last night and checked in at the front desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 어젯밤 호텔에 도착해서 프런트 데스크에서 체크인했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus on obtaining the correct details of something which are largely irrelevant or unimportant.
meanings.example: Stop counting angels on pinheads and focus on the main issue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사소한 것에 집착하지 말고 주요 문제에 집중하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: intransitive To wear smart clothes and look more stylish
meanings.example: He needs to smarten up if he wants to get that job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그 직업을 원한다면 차려입을 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Return to where one came from.
meanings.example: When I get back from holiday, I expect the house to be tidy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가에서 돌아오면 집이 깔끔하기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: make rounder by smoothing down physically To change the shape of (an object) to make it smoother and especially more circular or ovoid.
meanings.example: The sculptor carefully rounded off the edges of the statue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조각가는 조각상의 가장자리를 조심스럽게 둥글게 다듬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: The sun was beating down all day, making it too hot to go outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 햇볕이 하루 종일 강하게 내리쬐어 밖에 나가기에는 너무 더웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: beatdown.
meanings.example: He received a severe beat down from the gang.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 갱단에게 심한 구타를 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish or complete something; to add a finishing touch.
meanings.example: We went for a walk along the beach, watched the sunset, and then capped it all off with a lovely meal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 해변을 따라 산책하고, 일몰을 본 다음, 멋진 식사로 모든 것을 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seek or find despite a lack of apparent resources or availability.
meanings.example: Let me look around and see if I can scrounge up any old blankets for you to take to your picnic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주변을 둘러보고 당신의 소풍에 가져갈 낡은 담요를 어렵게 찾아낼 수 있는지 보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become completely known, felt, or understood.
meanings.example: He knows he's been naughty, but it will take a while for it to sink in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 잘못했다는 것을 알지만, 그것이 완전히 이해되기까지는 시간이 걸릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be treacherous or faithless to; to betray.
meanings.example: You can't go back on your promise now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 약속을 어길 수는 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stave from the outside, to crush inward, to cause to collapse inward.
meanings.example: "We'll get an axe and stave in all these barrels and the liquid will all run out", threatened the man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남자가 "도끼를 가져와 이 통들을 모두 안으로 찌그러뜨리면 액체가 다 흘러나올 거야"라고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck around: to sleep with numerous partners.
meanings.example: Sleeping around sleeping around / What will the neighbors say / Sleeping around all over town / What will the neighbors say
meanings.exampleKorean: 난잡하게 자고 난잡하게 자고 / 이웃들이 뭐라고 할까 / 마을 곳곳에서 난잡하게 자고 / 이웃들이 뭐라고 할까

meanings.englishDefinition: To teleport (someone or something) using a (fictional) device, especially from the surface of a planet to an orbiting starship.
meanings.example: Pookie: "They call it the "Enterprise Room", man, because it's for people who wanna be beamed up to Scotty."
meanings.exampleKorean: 푸키: "스카티에게 순간이동하고 싶은 사람들을 위한 곳이라서 그걸 '엔터프라이즈 룸'이라고 부르지."

meanings.englishDefinition: lower by cranking To lower by winding, as with a crank or windlass.
meanings.example: He used the crank to wind down the window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 손잡이를 이용해 창문을 감아서 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen to someone until that person has finished speaking; to thoroughly consider all aspects of someone's argument.
meanings.example: Stop interrupting and hear me out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 말 끊지 말고 내 말 끝까지 들어줘!

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine; to scout; to investigate.
meanings.example: Let's scope out the competition before we launch our product.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제품을 출시하기 전에 경쟁사를 살펴보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think deeply about something; to ponder, deliberate or ruminate.
meanings.example: I have been mulling over this math problem for weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 몇 주 동안 이 수학 문제를 숙고해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause an audio or video tape, digital media stream, etc. to move forward very fast, so that when the device is played, it will start at a later point.
meanings.example: I'm going to fast forward through the boring parts of the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화의 지루한 부분은 빨리감기 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The feature that allows media to be fast-forwarded.
meanings.example: The fast forward button is broken on this remote.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 리모컨의 빨리감기 버튼이 고장났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make clean, to make satisfactorily neat.
meanings.example: Can you help me tidy up the room before the guests arrive?
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님이 오기 전에 방을 정리하는 것을 도와줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: to walk out of a place stealthily
meanings.example: He thieved out of the room when no one was looking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아무도 보지 않을 때 방에서 몰래 빠져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: wear rose-colored glasses.
meanings.example: She always looks at life through rose-colored glasses, never seeing the bad side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 인생을 장밋빛 안경으로 보며, 나쁜 면을 전혀 보지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss off all senses
meanings.example: That constant noise is really peeing me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 끊임없는 소음은 정말 나를 짜증나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a competition.
meanings.example: "I hope she doesn't go in for big purple orchids," Miss Tinkham said to Mrs. Rasmussen, "there are so many pretty kinds."
meanings.exampleKorean: "그녀가 큰 보라색 난초에 출전하지 않기를 바란다"라고 팅크햄 양이 라스무센 부인에게 말했다. "아름다운 종류가 너무 많다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform cunnilingus on.
meanings.example: He was skilled at licking out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 클리토리스 애무에 능숙했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To realize; come to understand.
meanings.example: It took him a while to cotton on to what was happening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는지 알아차리는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to censor inappropriate spoken words by obscuring them with the sound of a bleep
meanings.example: The reporter had to bleep out the swear words.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 욕설을 삐 소리로 방송 금지해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become frightened or disturbed.
meanings.example: The sudden loud noise made everyone get the wind up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 큰 소리에 모두가 겁먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquire with difficulty, as by thorough scrutiny.
meanings.example: Tom managed to winkle the truth out of John eventually.
meanings.exampleKorean: 톰은 결국 존으로부터 진실을 어렵게 얻어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reconcile at the end of a shift; to compare receipts of items sold to records of credit card, check and cash placed into the drawer, verifying that correct change was given out by the clerk.
meanings.example: real benefits accrued not to the public whose streets were flooded with unregulated taxis or workers whose livelihoods evaporated, but to Uber’s early investors who were still able to cash out when the company went public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 규제되지 않은 택시로 거리가 넘쳐나거나 생계가 사라진 노동자들에게가 아니라, 회사가 상장되었을 때 현금화할 수 있었던 우버의 초기 투자자들에게 실질적인 이익이 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overwhelm; to cause to fall to the ground.
meanings.example: This tribe lived largely upon the smaller animals which they bowled over with their stone hatchets after making a wide circle about their quarry and driving it so that it had to pass close to one of their number.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 부족은 사냥감을 둘러싸고 그들이 사냥감 근처로 지나가도록 몰아 돌도끼로 쓰러뜨린 후 주로 작은 동물들을 먹고 살았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call a place of service and order food, or other goods, delivered to one's own location.
meanings.example: I'm too tired to cook, so I'll order out tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 피곤해서 요리할 수 없으니 오늘 밤은 배달 주문할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bargain or haggle with a seller in order to obtain a lower price for a good or service.
meanings.example: He tried to jew down the price of the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자동차 값을 깎으려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become quiet and calm after a period of disturbance or restlessness.
meanings.example: It took John a while to settle down after the phone call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존은 전화 통화 후에 진정하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy.
meanings.example: His constant complaining really cheeses me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 정말 나를 짜증나게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form)
meanings.example: He was teed off about the delay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지연에 대해 화가 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Annoyed, upset, angry.
meanings.example: My doctor was thoroughly teed off when she learned I have not been taking the medications she prescribed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사 선생님은 내가 처방받은 약을 복용하지 않았다는 것을 알았을 때 매우 화가 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce maple sap by boiling to make maple syrup, maple sugar or maple toffee.
meanings.example: They sugar off in the early spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 이른 봄에 단풍 시럽을 만든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck off.
meanings.example: He told him to eff off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place.
meanings.example: She decided to come back to her hometown after living abroad for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 수년간 해외에서 산 뒤 고향으로 돌아오기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Experiencing withdrawal symptoms of an addiction.
meanings.example: He looked strung out and exhausted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 금단 증상을 겪으며 지쳐 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: He was strung out on heroin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 헤로인에 중독되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To aim a gun at something.
meanings.example: The hunter took a bead on the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼이 사슴을 조준했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To aim a gun at.
meanings.example: The sniper drew a bead on the target.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저격수가 목표물을 조준했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emit; to produce and send forth; to come across in some manner.
meanings.example: The flowers give off a sweet fragrance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꽃에서 달콤한 향기가 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accumulate (an amount of time or experience).
meanings.example: To become a pilot, you must clock up at least 1,000 hours flying time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조종사가 되려면 최소 1,000시간의 비행 시간을 축적해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn out to be feasible, accurate, true, satisfactory, as expected, hoped, stated, etc.
meanings.example: The new system proved out to be very efficient.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 시스템은 매우 효율적임이 입증되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A gathering or event where participants are encouraged to add their voices in song.
meanings.example: The concert included a sing-along session.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트에는 함께 노래하는 시간이 포함되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sing some music while someone else is singing or playing the same piece of music or while it is being broadcast or performed
meanings.example: If you know the song, sing along with me!
meanings.exampleKorean: 노래를 알면 나와 함께 따라 불러!

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie down to sleep for the night, usually of livestock or machinery, or of people when sleeping somewhere worse than normal, often camping or in prison.
meanings.example: The hikers bedded down in their sleeping bags.
meanings.exampleKorean: 등산객들은 침낭에서 잠자리에 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch fire and burn until destroyed.
meanings.example: The old house burned up in the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 집이 화재로 불타 없어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: burnup.
meanings.example: The burn up of fuel was rapid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연료의 연소가 빨랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone; to call someone on the telephone.
meanings.example: Malone at once rang up Lord Roxton, and soon heard the familiar voice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말로니는 즉시 록스턴 경에게 전화를 걸었고, 곧 익숙한 목소리를 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An inspection, examination, review, or investigation.
meanings.example: The going over of the documents took several hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서류 검토에 몇 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ing-form
meanings.example: The client is going over the new proposal as we speak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고객이 지금 우리가 이야기하는 동안 새 제안서를 검토하고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do, indicate, or say something clearly and without hesitation, even if it may be unpleasant. from 16th c.
meanings.example: She made no bones about telling him exactly what she thought.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신이 생각하는 것을 정확히 말하는 데 거리낌이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave suddenly.
meanings.example: She was brought up in a very religious household, but broke away from the church in her teens.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 독실한 가정에서 자랐지만 십대에 교회를 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize or solve one's personal problems.
meanings.example: John took a week off work to sort himself out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존은 자신을 정리하기 위해 일주일간 휴가를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in horseplay.
meanings.example: Can we quit horsing around and get some work done?
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 일 좀 할 수 있을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To grow taller or larger rapidly.
meanings.example: Our operating costs have shot up due to the fuel shortage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연료 부족으로 운영 비용이 급증했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become dry (often of weather); to lose water.
meanings.example: The river has dried up in the summer heat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여름 더위에 강이 말랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow entrance into a building from a higher floor by triggering an electronic lock.
meanings.example: I'll be back in ten minutes; can you buzz me up then?
meanings.exampleKorean: 10분 후에 돌아올게요. 그때 저를 호출해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the speed of an engine, especially that of a stationary motor car.
meanings.example: He revved up the engine before speeding away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 속도를 내기 전에 엔진을 가속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a new endeavor enthusiastically and wholeheartedly.
meanings.example: Don't just get a toe wet; dive in and create your first entry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 망설이지 말고 뛰어들어 첫 항목을 만들어 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat, especially to eat vigorously.
meanings.example: We're all famished; it's time to chow down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 모두 배고파요. 이제 먹을 시간이에요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume completely.
meanings.example: He ate up all the cookies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 쿠키를 전부 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cordon; to separate from the surroundings using tape or similar material.
meanings.example: The police taped off the crime scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 범죄 현장을 테이프로 봉쇄했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cohabit as if husband and wife without being married.
meanings.example: They decided to live in sin rather than get married.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결혼 대신 죄악 속에서 살기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the volume limit of a container.
meanings.example: The truck was loaded until it cubed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 트럭은 부피 한도에 도달할 때까지 실렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take turns.
meanings.example: We took it in turns to drive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 번갈아 운전했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard.
meanings.example: She cast away her bridal dress along with other reminders of the marriage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 결혼의 다른 기억들과 함께 웨딩드레스를 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something true, equal, or correct, as for example by turning and facing a part on a lathe.
meanings.example: He trued up his faceplate before doing the next setup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다음 작업을 하기 전에 페이스플레이트의 수평을 맞췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To happen.
meanings.example: The millennium passed off without any disasters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 천년기는 재난 없이 지나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the pages of (a book) idly, reading short sections at random.
meanings.example: He leafed through the magazine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 잡지를 훑어보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be (or to place oneself) in a risky, potentially dangerous or delicate situation.
meanings.example: If I concur to that I will be skating on thin ice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것에 동의하면 나는 위태로운 상황에 처하게 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To imitate (someone or something) for the purpose of satirical humour.
meanings.example: The programme accurately sends up the British Civil Service system at Whitehall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로그램은 화이트홀의 영국 공무원 시스템을 정확하게 풍자한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss from one's presence.
meanings.example: The salesman was taking up too much time with his nonsense, so I sent him away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 판매원은 그의 헛소리로 너무 많은 시간을 차지했기 때문에 나는 그를 내보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write to a business or other organisation, requesting a thing.
meanings.example: You can send away for a brochure about this hotel, if you like.
meanings.exampleKorean: 원하시면 이 호텔에 대한 브로셔를 주문하실 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a place or state after having been there at a previous time.
meanings.example: We were getting cold so we decided to go back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추워져서 돌아가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make adjustments (to an engine or bicycle drivetrain) in order to improve its performance.
meanings.example: I need to tune up my car before the long trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장거리 여행 전에 차를 튜닝해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay in total a sum which is owed, especially when the sum has been owed for a period of time.
meanings.example: He finally paid up his gambling debts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 도박 빚을 모두 갚았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become cloudy or overcast.
meanings.example: The sky started to cloud up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하늘이 흐려지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block or cover using bricks.
meanings.example: I am going to brick up the window instead of replacing the glass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유리를 교체하는 대신 창문을 벽돌로 막을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To muck about.
meanings.example: Stop mucking around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the excrement and other rubbish from the area where an animal is kept, such as a horse stable or a dog kennel.
meanings.example: The farmer had to muck out the stables every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 매일 아침 마구간을 청소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean, especially to tidy by removing the contents.
meanings.example: We need to clean out the garage this weekend to make room for the new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 차를 넣을 공간을 만들기 위해 이번 주말에 차고를 깨끗이 비워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become deranged.
meanings.example: He started to wack out after losing his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 잃은 후 미치기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live together, especially of an unmarried couple.
meanings.example: The only thing I want to do after the war is marry some girl who's got more money than I have and shack up with lots more pretty girls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁이 끝나고 내가 하고 싶은 유일한 일은 나보다 돈이 더 많은 여자와 결혼해서 예쁜 여자들과 더 많이 동거하는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: away
