meanings.example: This car's a real bargain, and you could drive it away this afternoon!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 차는 정말 싸게 나왔어요. 오늘 오후에 바로 몰고 갈 수 있어요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten with a button or buttons.
meanings.example: Button up your shirt cuff. It has come undone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 셔츠 소매 단추를 잠그세요. 풀렸어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten (a piece of clothing, etc.); to tighten (a nut etc.)
meanings.example: I can't do up my shirt. The button is missing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 셔츠 단추를 잠글 수가 없어. 단추가 없어졌어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close with a zip fastener.
meanings.example: Always check if you've zipped up your fly before you go out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외출하기 전에 항상 바지 지퍼를 올렸는지 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To never release someone (e.g., from prison).
meanings.example: Let's get these men together in a room and I would suggest feed them bread and water and throw away the key until they come together
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 남자들을 방에 모아놓고, 함께 할 때까지 빵과 물만 주며 열쇠를 던져버리자고 제안하고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resign from a job or other position of responsibility, especially when pressured to do so.
meanings.example: The CEO decided to fall on his sword and resign following the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: CEO는 스캔들 이후 책임을 지고 사임하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay for something, often with reluctance.
meanings.example: Jim stumped up for the cinema tickets, but I paid for the coffees afterwards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 짐이 영화 티켓 값을 마지못해 냈지만, 나중에 커피 값은 내가 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into a couple; to put into, or connect together as, a group of two.
meanings.example: The teacher asked the students to couple up for the activity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들에게 활동을 위해 둘씩 짝을 지으라고 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a sudden violent attack.
meanings.example: Japanese torpedo boats launch an attack on the Suvorov. Despite burning steadily for several hours and now taking a torpedo to the stern, the ship still lashes out at its attackers with a few remaining guns. With no pressing need to continue the attack to closer range, the torpedo boats fall back, noting the position for a night attack if Suvorov survives that long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일본 어뢰정들이 수보로프에 공격을 개시했다. 몇 시간 동안 계속 불타고 선미에 어뢰를 맞았음에도 불구하고, 배는 남은 몇 문의 포로 공격자들에게 계속 공격을 퍼부었다. 더 가까운 거리에서 공격을 계속할 긴급한 필요가 없었기 때문에, 어뢰정들은 수보로프가 그 정도까지 살아남는다면 야간 공격을 위해 위치를 기록하며 후퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike a surface and be deflected
meanings.example: The ball glanced off the edge of his racquet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공이 그의 라켓 가장자리에 맞고 빗나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look at something else briefly, then look back again.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 잠시 다른 곳을 보다 다시 돌아보다
meanings.example: When I told him the truth, he glanced away, and then replied...
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 진실을 말했을 때, 그는 잠시 다른 곳을 보다가 대답했다...

meanings.englishDefinition: directed at a horse move on!, go faster!
meanings.example: Gee up, Dobbin, Gee ho, Dobbin, Gee up, Dobbin, Gee up, and gee ho - o - o!
meanings.exampleKorean: 기겁, 도빈, 기호, 도빈, 기겁, 도빈, 기겁, 그리고 기호-오-오!

meanings.englishDefinition: To encourage.
meanings.example: "US fund manager Eric Knight has a fearful reputation as a shareholder activist, geeing up underperforming managements at Royal Dutch Shell and Suez." – HSBC: activist pounces, The Week, 15 September 2007, 631, 43.
meanings.exampleKorean: "미국 펀드매니저 에릭 나이트는 로열 더치 쉘과 수에즈의 실적이 저조한 경영진을 격려하는 주주 활동가로서 두려운 평판을 가지고 있다." – HSBC: 활동가가 덮치다, 더 위크, 2007년 9월 15일, 631, 43.

meanings.englishDefinition: excited, filled with enthusiasm
meanings.example: The crowd was geed up for the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 결승전을 위해 열광했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: He felt geed up after the pep talk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 격려 연설 후에 기분이 고양되었다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave secretively.
meanings.example: ⁠I do but sing because I must,And pipe but as the linnets sing:And unto one her note is gay,⁠For now her little ones have ranged;⁠And unto one her note is changed,Because her brood is stol’n away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 단지 해야 하기 때문에 노래하고, 린넷새처럼 피리를 불 뿐이다: 그리고 한 마리에게는 그녀의 노래가 즐겁다, 이제 그녀의 어린 새끼들이 흩어졌기 때문이다; 그리고 한 마리에게는 그녀의 노래가 바뀌었다, 그녀의 새끼들이 몰래 사라졌기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily.
meanings.example: It's really chucking it down! I'm glad I brought my umbrella.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 폭우가 쏟아지고 있어! 우산을 가져오길 잘했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a rolling motion or turn.
meanings.example: The car rolled completely over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 완전히 뒤집혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make reckless or exaggerated statements.
meanings.example: He's always shooting his mouth off about things he knows nothing about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자기가 모르는 것에 대해 함부로 지껄인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stun; to surprise.
meanings.example: I was knocked back by the sheer size of the hall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 홀의 엄청난 크기에 충격을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something with somebody else, such as a conversation or relationship.
meanings.example: To make him feel welcome, she struck up a conversation with the newly arrived guest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 환영받는다고 느끼게 하기 위해, 그녀는 새로 온 손님과 대화를 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To partly obliterate text by drawing a continuous line through the centre thereof, usually to indicate the deletion of an error or obsolete information.
meanings.example: The editor decided to strike through the unnecessary paragraph.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편집자는 불필요한 단락에 취소선을 긋기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail.
meanings.example: The company's profits went down the toilet after the scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사의 이익은 스캔들 이후로 폭락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: reshape from flat to cylindrical or folded To make something into a particular shape, especially cylindrical or fold-like.
meanings.example: We should roll up the carpet before we start painting the ceiling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 천장 페인트칠을 시작하기 전에 카페트를 말아 올려야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to call the attention of potential purchasers.
meanings.example: Roll up, roll up! Pies for sale!
meanings.exampleKorean: 모여라, 모여라! 파이 팝니다!

meanings.englishDefinition: rollup.
meanings.example: The new feature includes a rollup of daily sales figures.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기능에는 일일 판매 수치 롤업이 포함됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: It's getting cold. Let's go in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추워지고 있어. 안으로 들어가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get; to obtain; to secure.
meanings.example: If you can get your hands on the right ingredients, it's an easy recipe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적절한 재료를 구할 수 있다면, 이것은 쉬운 레시피입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise, hoist, or lift a thing using a jack, or similar means.
meanings.example: He jacked the car up to change the tire
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 타이어를 교체하기 위해 차를 잭으로 들어 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing a regular activity, such as a job or studying.
meanings.example: I've had enough of working nights, so I'm going to jack in my job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야간 근무는 충분히 했으니, 직장을 그만둘 생각이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be frightened or disturbed.
meanings.example: He had the wind up when he heard the strange noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이상한 소리를 듣고 겁을 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be mobile, physically active.
meanings.example: I can't get about so much since I hurt my hip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엉덩이를 다친 후로는 많이 돌아다닐 수가 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To party to or play rock music, punk rock, grunge or heavy metal.
meanings.example: We rocked out to our favorite band all night long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 밤새도록 가장 좋아하는 밴드의 음악을 들으며 신나게 즐겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become familiar with something
meanings.example: The new employees are starting to get into their stride.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신입 사원들이 업무에 익숙해지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To subtract a quality from; make something seem not so good or interesting.
meanings.example: The scandal took away from the team's victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들은 팀의 승리에서 가치를 떨어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accompany someone to a point of departure; to ensure someone departs safely.
meanings.example: Many friends came to see me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 친구들이 나를 환송하러 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To elevate to a higher position.
meanings.example: He lifted up the heavy box with ease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무거운 상자를 쉽게 들어 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or erase with a wiping motion.
meanings.example: She wiped away her tears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 눈물을 닦아 없앴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove a piece from a whole by breaking or snapping.
meanings.example: She unwrapped the slab of chocolate and broke off a piece.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 초콜릿 판을 포장해서 한 조각을 떼어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something, especially something that is forbidden or improper.
meanings.example: Recently he's been getting up to all sorts of mischief.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최근 그는 온갖 장난을 꾸미고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hear as rumor; to learn through friends of friends, etc.
meanings.example: I heard on the office grapevine that you’re leaving us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장 동료들 사이의 소문을 통해 당신이 우리를 떠난다는 것을 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: The wool was dyed in the wool before being spun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 양모는 방적되기 전에 염색되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dyed-in-the-wool (Set in one's ways, steadfast.)
meanings.example: He's a dyed-in-the-wool conservative.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 뿌리 깊은 보수주의자이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To postpone (a decision) at least overnight, to avoid making a hasty choice.
meanings.example: I like the idea, but let me sleep on it before we go ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 아이디어는 좋지만, 진행하기 전에 하룻밤 자고 결정하게 해줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep unintentionally.
meanings.example: The lecture was so boring that I started to doze off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강의가 너무 지루해서 깜빡 졸기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A short unintentional nap, an instance of dozing off.
meanings.example: He took a short doze off after lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 점심 식사 후 잠시 졸았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confine in a restricted place or situation
meanings.example: I hate being cooped up inside all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루 종일 안에 갇혀 있는 것이 싫다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clarify, to correct a misconception.
meanings.example: All of their symptoms cleared up after the mold was removed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 곰팡이가 제거된 후 그들의 모든 증상이 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase rapidly to a new value.
meanings.example: The company decided to ramp up production to meet demand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 수요를 충족시키기 위해 생산을 확대하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An act of betrayal or treachery.
meanings.example: His business partner's betrayal was a real stab in the back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 사업 파트너의 배신은 정말 큰 배신이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray (somebody).
meanings.example: He felt like his friend had stabbed him in the back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구에게 배신당했다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: apply tack to horse To prepare a horse for riding by equipping it with tack (harness, reins, saddle etc.)
meanings.example: The groom spent an hour tacking up the horse for the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마구간지기는 경주를 위해 말에게 안장 등을 씌우는 데 한 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return, restore (a thing to its original owner or location etc.).
meanings.example: Give me back my book!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 책 돌려줘!

meanings.englishDefinition: To prove to be useful or helpful.
meanings.example: Even though he doesn't really know how to use them, he keeps the tools around, figuring they might come in handy someday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그것들을 어떻게 사용하는지 잘 모르지만, 언젠가 유용할 것이라고 생각하며 도구들을 가지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by a scraping action.
meanings.example: You can scrape the paint off using a sharp blade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 날카로운 칼날을 사용하여 페인트를 긁어낼 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make gaudy and bright.
meanings.example: She decided to jazz up her outfit with a colorful scarf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 화려한 스카프로 옷차림을 돋보이게 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disintegrate, to break into pieces.
meanings.example: My old briefcase is falling apart. I'll have to buy a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 오래된 서류 가방이 부서지고 있다. 새 것을 사야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute money; to disburse.
meanings.example: The company had to pay out a large sum in damages.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 손해 배상으로 거액을 지불해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collectively approve a nominee for an office or position as a result of voting.
meanings.example: Can only members vote in other members?
meanings.exampleKorean: 회원만이 다른 회원을 투표로 선출할 수 있습니까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel the holder of an office or other position through an act of voting.
meanings.example: The incumbent candidate was voted out in a tight race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현직 후보는 치열한 경쟁 끝에 투표로 해임되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To frighten (an animal, especially a game animal) into appearing from cover.
meanings.example: The hunters tried to scare up the rabbits from the bushes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼들은 덤불 속에서 토끼들을 겁주어 나오게 하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fully consider, so as to understand all the consequences of.
meanings.example: We need to think through all the possible outcomes before making a decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결정을 내리기 전에 가능한 모든 결과를 철저히 생각해 볼 필요가 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fulfil the expectations placed upon one.
meanings.example: She never lived up to her reputation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 명성에 부응하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine in order to find negative information for public opinion, usually with the purpose of embarrassing or discrediting a person.
meanings.example: The freelance investigative reporter made a career of digging up dirt on celebrities for tabloids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프리랜서 탐사 기자는 타블로이드 잡지를 위해 유명인들의 비리나 약점을 캐내는 것으로 경력을 쌓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell or dispose of (something) with the intent to deceive; to attempt to pass off a counterfeit or inferior product as genuine.
meanings.example: He tried to palm off a fake watch on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내게 가짜 시계를 속여 팔려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cause to fall
meanings.example: Dad has brought down a stack of old vinyl records from the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아빠가 다락방에서 오래된 바이닐 레코드 더미를 내려왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something from a wall or similar vertical surface to which it is fixed.
meanings.example: He took down the picture and replaced it with the framed photograph.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사진을 내려놓고 액자에 든 사진으로 교체했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (something attached to, on or clinging to an object) by shaking.
meanings.example: The archaeologists shook off the dust that had fallen from the roof, and promptly continued their work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고고학자들은 지붕에서 떨어진 먼지를 털어내고 즉시 작업을 계속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to keep
meanings.example: Hang onto that ticket stub; it might be valuable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 티켓 조각을 간직해, 가치가 있을지도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) last for more time than is necessary; to prolong; to extend.
meanings.example: Orion hit a rabbit once; but though sore wounded it got to the bury, and, struggling in, the arrow caught the side of the hole and was drawn out. Indeed, a nail filed sharp is not of much avail as an arrowhead; you must have it barbed, and that was a little beyond our skill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오리온이 토끼 한 마리를 쳤지만, 심하게 다쳤음에도 불구하고 굴로 들어가려다가 화살이 구멍 옆에 걸려 뽑혔다. 사실, 날카롭게 간 못은 화살촉으로는 별로 소용이 없다. 날이 있어야 하는데, 그것은 우리의 기술로는 조금 부족했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise both hands in the air in an exasperated manner.
meanings.example: When he realized he couldn't fix the car, he just threw up his hands in despair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 고칠 수 없다는 것을 깨닫고 절망하며 어쩔 수 없다는 듯이 두 손을 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hasten.
meanings.example: He was in such a hurry to leave that he almost fell over his feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 서둘러 떠나려다가 거의 발이 꼬여 넘어질 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become enforceable, or applicable.
meanings.example: The new rules will come into effect on the first of January.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 규칙은 1월 1일부터 효력을 발생할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To completely enclose or envelop.
meanings.example: the job was almost the despair of those early pioneers, for the surface was a "quaking morass," the surrounding district was undrained, and no sooner than soil was deposited than it was swallowed up, with no apparent effect, for the sponginess of the land remained unaltered.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 일은 초기 개척자들에게 거의 절망적이었다. 표면은 '흔들리는 늪'이었고, 주변 지역은 배수가 되지 않았으며, 흙이 쌓이자마자 아무런 효과도 없이 삼켜져 버렸는데, 땅의 물컹함은 변하지 않았기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to behave badly
meanings.example: Our school team is playing up at the old recreation ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 학교 팀은 옛날 레크리에이션 경기장에서 말썽을 부리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take part in a charade, deception, or practical joke.
meanings.example: James thought it was cruel for the fraternity members to make the pledge think his father had died suddenly and refused to play along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제임스는 프레터니티 회원들이 신입생에게 그의 아버지가 갑자기 돌아가셨다고 생각하게 만든 것이 잔인하다고 생각했고, 이에 순응하기를 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak up, especially in a robust, assertive manner; to say something loudly and suddenly.
meanings.example: seem to have a lot to say," remarked Silver, spitting far into the air. "Pipe up and let me hear it, or lay to."
meanings.exampleKorean: "할 말이 많은 것 같군," 실버가 멀리 공중으로 침을 뱉으며 말했다. "말해봐, 아니면 멈춰."

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or attempt to make something seem less important, likely, or obvious.
meanings.example: The government tried to play down the severity of the economic crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 경제 위기의 심각성을 축소하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ascend to the surface.
meanings.example: Bubbles began to rise up from the bottom of the lake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호수 바닥에서 거품이 솟아오르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put different items or groups together and regard or treat them as one single group (either knowingly or unknowingly, and either advisedly or unadvisedly).
meanings.example: It's unfair to lump all the employees together; they have different skills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 직원을 한데 묶는 것은 불공평하다. 그들은 각기 다른 기술을 가지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create something or cause something to come forth, by magic or by some other unexplained means.
meanings.example: The landlady magicked up a large breakfast for us and our unexpected guest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집주인이 우리와 예상치 못한 손님을 위해 푸짐한 아침 식사를 마법처럼 만들어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw; to discontinue what one is doing or saying; to back off.
meanings.example: I really should have pulled my head in once they produced that evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 그 증거를 제시했을 때 나는 정말 입을 다물었어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare for an activity.
meanings.example: The hardware store is gearing up for spring in February with garden supplies and seeds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 철물점은 2월에 정원 용품과 씨앗으로 봄맞이 준비를 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break suddenly into a gallop.
meanings.example: The horse went off at score when it heard the starting gun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말은 출발 총성을 듣자마자 갑자기 질주했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To omit, to not include, to neglect to mention.
meanings.example: The journalist decided to leave out certain details from her story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 자신의 기사에서 특정 세부 사항을 생략하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: log in#Verb.
meanings.example: He logged on to yahoo.com, skimmed the front page, and decided to sign in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 yahoo.com에 로그인하여 첫 페이지를 훑어보고 로그인하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come up with a "pop" sound.
meanings.example: The toast is in the toaster - when it pops up it will be ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토스터에 토스트가 있어. 튀어나오면 준비된 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep on someone's sofa or floor because there is no bed spare.
meanings.example: After the party, John let me doss down on the living-room floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티가 끝난 후, 존은 나를 거실 바닥에서 임시로 자게 해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to curl, make curly, shape into curls
meanings.example: Rollers are a good way to curl up your hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 롤러는 머리를 곱슬곱슬하게 말기에 좋은 방법이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover or expose as fake, insincere, or inaccurate.
meanings.example: The teacher caught the student out in a lie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생의 거짓말을 들춰냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take measures to stop (something); to put an end to (something); to exert tighter control of (something); to treat (someone or something) more harshly.
meanings.example: The police are clamping down on speeding in this area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 이 지역에서 과속을 엄하게 단속하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accumulate money, especially for a specific, planned expenditure.
meanings.example: I'm saving up my tips for a new guitar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 새 기타를 사기 위해 팁을 모으고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perceive things as different, or to perceive their difference itself; to differentiate, distinguish, discriminate.
meanings.example: They are identical twins, and if they dress the same, everybody has trouble telling them apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 일란성 쌍둥이라서, 똑같이 옷을 입으면 모두가 그들을 구별하기 어려워한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To squander or waste.
meanings.example: He frittered away his inheritance on gambling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 유산을 도박으로 탕진했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more appealing to the gay community e.g. by adding gay characters to a soap opera.
meanings.example: The network decided to gay up the show by adding a gay couple.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방송사는 동성 커플을 추가하여 쇼를 동성애자에게 어필하게 만들기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To nicen.
meanings.example: She decided to nicen up her appearance before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 면접 전에 외모를 깔끔하게 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sell all available stock, stock out To sell all of a product that is in stock.
meanings.example: The concert tickets sold out within minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트 티켓은 몇 분 만에 매진되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become detached or to drop from.
meanings.example: The paint is starting to fall off the walls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트가 벽에서 떨어지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain or otherwise express oneself in a loud, immoderate manner.
meanings.example: He's always mouthing off to his parents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 부모님에게 건방지게 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure.
meanings.example: She lives on a small pension.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 적은 연금으로 살아간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw-in.
meanings.example: The referee awarded a throw-in to the home team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심판은 홈팀에게 스로우인을 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To press on regardless of hindrances.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, she forged over towards her goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좌절에도 불구하고 그녀는 목표를 향해 계속 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal (something) and flee.
meanings.example: The thieves made off with the jewels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들은 보석을 훔쳐 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit a microphone and transmitter to (a person). Usually for television performers, or for police informers.
meanings.example: The actors were miked up before the performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배우들은 공연 전에 마이크를 달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (someone) uncomfortable or afraid; to give (someone) the creeps.
meanings.example: The old abandoned house really creeps me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 오래된 폐가는 정말 나에게 소름 끼치게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically assemble (or reassemble) from fragments or pieces.
meanings.example: The detective pieced together the clues to solve the crime.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 단서들을 맞춰 범죄를 해결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unplug; to remove (an electrical device) from its socket.
meanings.example: Don't forget to plug out the iron when you're finished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다 사용하면 다리미 플러그 뽑는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide assistance.
meanings.example: Can you help out with the dishes?
meanings.exampleKorean: 설거지 좀 도와줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop trading as a business.
meanings.example: The factory will close down next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공장은 다음 달에 폐업할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close remove a gap completely or fully.
meanings.example: Please close up the shop before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 가게 문을 완전히 닫아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seal or block the entrance to a road, an area, or a building so that people cannot enter.
meanings.example: The road was closed off due to an accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고로 인해 도로가 봉쇄되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To weep for an elongated time, or in an excessive manner.
meanings.example: She cried her eyes out after hearing the sad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 슬픈 소식을 듣고 몹시 울었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: an audio jack that produces an unamplified signal
meanings.example: The audio interface has a line out port for connecting to speakers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오디오 인터페이스에는 스피커 연결을 위한 라인 아웃 포트가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To corroborate, prove, or confirm; to demonstrate; to provide evidence for.
meanings.example: His alibi was borne out by witness testimony.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 알리바이는 증인의 증언으로 입증되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond to (a negative situation) lightheartedly, as though it were a joke.
meanings.example: He tried to laugh off his mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 실수를 웃어넘기려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intrude; to enter or interrupt suddenly and without invitation.
meanings.example: He barged in on their private conversation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들의 사적인 대화에 불쑥 끼어들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fiddle idly.
meanings.example: Stop messing around and get your homework done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 숙제를 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or decorate in an especially fancy, elaborate, or excessive manner.
meanings.example: She tricked out her car with colorful lights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 화려한 불빛으로 차를 장식했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To negotiate a lower price.
meanings.example: I managed to talk down the price of the used car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 중고차 가격을 깎을 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a quarterback or other player in the backfield, to take a number of steps back from the line of scrimmage immediately after the snap or hike of the ball, to avoid defenders.
meanings.example: The quarterback dropped back to pass under pressure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쿼터백은 압박 속에서 패스를 위해 뒤로 물러났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To land an aircraft or spacecraft.
meanings.example: The plane is scheduled to touch down at noon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기는 정오에 착륙할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh so much that one's entire body moves somewhat uncontrollably.
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes made the audience fall about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코미디언의 농담에 관객들은 배꼽을 잡고 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To snuggle.
meanings.example: They decided to cosy up by the fire with a good book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 좋은 책을 들고 벽난로 옆에서 아늑하게 지내기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sign one's name as an indication that one is leaving some location; to take some action to indicate one is leaving a secured program or web page on a computer.
meanings.example: Please sign out before you leave the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근 전에 퇴근 기록을 해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To welcome onto a stage by cheering or applauding.
meanings.example: The crowd gave it up for the band.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관중들은 밴드를 향해 환호했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act decisively on the basis of information received or deduced.
meanings.example: The policeman decided to act on the tip from his informant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 정보원의 제보에 따라 행동하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To incorporate (something extra, often an amount of money) into something else (often a bill).
meanings.example: They tried to add on extra charges to the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계산서에 추가 요금을 붙이려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: add-on.
meanings.example: The manufacturer sold many add ons for the basic camera.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제조사는 기본 카메라에 대한 많은 부가 장치를 판매했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch something with one's body.
meanings.example: She accidentally rubbed up against the painted door and got green paint on her blouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 실수로 페인트칠된 문에 몸을 스쳤고 블라우스에 초록색 페인트가 묻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defy or challenge someone
meanings.example: She decided to stand up against the unfair treatment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 불공정한 대우에 맞서기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To challenge someone.
meanings.example: The underdog team will go up against the champions in the final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약체 팀은 결승에서 챔피언 팀과 맞붙을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attend a private meeting or class without participating.
meanings.example: Parents of younger children often become upset when they are told that they cannot sit in on the psychological assessment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어린 자녀를 둔 부모들은 심리 평가에 참관할 수 없다는 말을 들으면 종종 속상해한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shove it up your ass (up yours) or back off.
meanings.example: Regarding the proposal, the boss said to sit on it and I tend to agree: it is too soon to begin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 제안에 대해 상사는 그것을 묵살하라고 말했고, 나도 동의하는 편이다. 시작하기에는 너무 이르다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by picking.
meanings.example: Before you recycle the bottle you need to pick off the label.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병을 재활용하기 전에 라벨을 떼어내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An instance of throwing out a batter leading off base.
meanings.example: The final out of the game was determined by a pick off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 경기의 마지막 아웃은 견제 아웃으로 결정되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break or demolish something by physical bodily force, especially stomping.
meanings.example: The elderly woman was rescued off the floor after a young paramedic managed to kick her door down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젊은 응급구조사가 문을 발로 차 부수고 나서야 노부인이 바닥에서 구조되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy, distress, or anger.
meanings.example: His constant complaining really torqued me off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 정말 나를 짜증나게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone.
meanings.example: Stop getting in my hair, I'm trying to concentrate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집중하려고 하니 귀찮게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: Get up.
meanings.example: It's time to get on up and start the day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일어나서 하루를 시작할 시간이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become suddenly angry; to break out into indignation or similar emotion; to flare up. from 16th c.
meanings.example: being brought home, and giving us ground to suppose he was much worse hurt than he really was, and a fever ensuing, every one flamed out; and all was laid at my door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집으로 돌아와 그가 실제보다 훨씬 더 다쳤다고 생각하게 만들었고, 열이 나자 모두들 격분했고 모든 것이 내 탓으로 돌려졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce weight; to make or become thinner.
meanings.example: She is trying to slim down before her wedding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 결혼식 전에 살을 빼려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To miss an experience or lose an opportunity, etc. that should not be missed.
meanings.example: 2006 Feb. 17, Graham Linehan, The IT Crowd, Season 1, Episode 4:
meanings.exampleKorean: 2006년 2월 17일, 그레이엄 라이너, IT 크라우드, 시즌 1, 에피소드 4:

meanings.englishDefinition: To exit or depart; to run away.
meanings.example: The thieves made off with the stolen jewels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들은 훔친 보석을 가지고 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay away from (a place or condition).
meanings.example: I am keeping out of trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 문제에 끼어들지 않고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To argue a point by repetition of the same theme, sometimes by using different words, but without making any progress.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말이 빙빙 돌다, 결론 없이 말하다
meanings.example: He kept talking in circles and never got to the point.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 계속해서 말이 빙빙 돌았고 요점에 이르지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make hair flat by putting oil, water, etc. on it.
meanings.example: His hair was slicked back, plastered down with gel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 머리는 젤을 발라 뒤로 넘겨져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push one's tongue so that it protrudes from the mouth, especially as a gesture of rudeness.
meanings.example: The child stuck his tongue out at the teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 선생님에게 혀를 내밀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dye woolen fibers before they are spun into thread.
meanings.example: The process of dyeing in the wool ensures a rich and lasting color.
meanings.exampleKorean: 원모 염색 과정은 풍부하고 지속적인 색상을 보장한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regurgitate or vomit.
meanings.example: The baby spat up her breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 아침 식사를 토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause moisture to be completely removed from; to make dry.
meanings.example: Leave the clothes out to dry out in the sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옷을 햇볕에 내놓아 말리시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit in or harmonize (with something).
meanings.example: This paragraph does not tie in with the greater themes of the story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문단은 이야기의 더 큰 주제와 조화되지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: tie-in.
meanings.example: The movie release included a tie-in with a fast-food chain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화 개봉은 패스트푸드 체인과의 연계 상품을 포함했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inform on, to rat out; to tell a person in authority that someone else has done something wrong.
meanings.example: Don't tell on your brother, keep his secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 형을 고발하지 마, 그의 비밀을 지켜줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time sitting idly, not doing anything important.
meanings.example: it was all British Rail, if they had some redundant sidings, they would dump all their old trains. There wasn't really a pressure to get rid of them. Now, with private companies owning the sites, you have to pay to store vehicles. Nobody wants old vehicles sat around - they can be graffitied, vandalised - and so the onus really is to move them on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 모두 영국 철도에 관한 것이었다. 만약 그들이 사용하지 않는 선로가 있다면, 그들은 모든 낡은 기차를 버릴 것이다. 그것들을 없애야 한다는 압박은 별로 없었다. 이제는 민간 기업이 부지를 소유하고 있기 때문에 차량을 보관하는 데 비용을 지불해야 한다. 아무도 낡은 차량이 방치되어 낙서되거나 파손되는 것을 원하지 않기 때문에, 그것들을 옮겨야 할 책임이 실제로 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start early, especially before something begins or before others begin; to get a head start.
meanings.example: If you'd like to get a jump on the holiday season, many craft stores already have holiday patterns out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연말 쇼핑 시즌을 남들보다 먼저 시작하고 싶다면, 많은 공예품 가게들이 이미 연말 상품을 내놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mock-up or prototype version of something, without some or all off its intended functionality.
meanings.example: The carpenters dummied up a set of simple props for the rehearsals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목수들은 리허설을 위해 간단한 소품 세트를 모형으로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show disrespect verbally; to smartmouth.
meanings.example: 2002, Ken Lockette, "Surviving the Soviet Legacy, in A World of Teaching: Personal Journeys Through the World's English-Speaking Classrooms, eds. John A. Hansen, Evan M. Smith, page 44
meanings.exampleKorean: 2002년, 켄 로케트, "소련 유산에서 살아남기", 존 A. 핸슨, 에반 M. 스미스 편집 "세계 영어 사용 교실을 통한 개인적인 여정" 중, 44페이지

meanings.englishDefinition: To show respect or admiration for (someone); to see (someone) as a role model.
meanings.example: I've always looked up to my older sister.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 항상 언니를 존경해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To heat, to cause to become hotter.
meanings.example: Please heat up the leftovers for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 식사를 위해 남은 음식을 데워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or upset someone, especially by repeated disagreeable actions.
meanings.example: Stop tapping your pen, you're getting on my wick!
meanings.exampleKorean: 펜 두드리는 것 좀 그만해, 짜증나게 하잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: overcome, endure To overcome; to endure.
meanings.example: Military training was hard, but I got through it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군사 훈련은 힘들었지만, 나는 그것을 견뎌냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To publicly greet or acknowledge a person, group, or organization.
meanings.example: My teacher said I shouldn't shout out all the time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 내가 계속 소리치지 말라고 하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shout-out.
meanings.example: He gave a shout-out to his fans during the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 콘서트 중에 팬들에게 환호를 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search or progress through with difficulty, as when performing a boring, repetitive research task.
meanings.example: This synopsis saves me from having to wade through a pile of textbooks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 줄거리는 내가 교과서 더미를 힘겹게 헤쳐나가는 수고를 덜어준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interrupt someone, or a situation, by doing or saying something abruptly, or forcefully, and usually without thinking about the consequences.
meanings.example: The Federal Reserve is under pressure to wade in with an emergency interest-rate cut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연준은 긴급 금리 인하를 위해 개입하라는 압력을 받고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat very quickly without chewing the food properly.
meanings.example: He gulped down his breakfast and ran out the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침을 꿀꺽 삼키고 문밖으로 달려나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cry out, e.g. when in pain.
meanings.example: The children sang out with joy when they saw the presents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 선물을 보고 기쁨에 큰 소리로 노래했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress so as to be more sexually attractive, especially with make-up and revealing clothing.
meanings.example: They tried to tart up the old house with a fresh coat of bright paint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 밝은 색 페인트를 새로 칠해 낡은 집을 화려하게 꾸미려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change one's plan or approach when an effort is already underway or at another inopportune time.
meanings.example: A change in the weather is known to be extreme / But what's the sense of changing horses in midstream?
meanings.exampleKorean: 날씨 변화는 극심한 것으로 알려져 있다 / 하지만 중도에 계획을 바꾸는 것이 무슨 소용이 있겠는가?

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay false flattery to another, particularly a superior at work, in order to get special attention.
meanings.example: He's always kissing up to the boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 상사에게 아첨한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay false compliments to; to flatter in excess.
meanings.example: She's always trying to kiss up to the teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 선생님의 환심을 사려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: The grounds settled on the bottom of the pot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 찌꺼기가 냄비 바닥에 가라앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Something which sickens, causes distress, or appalls by reason of being disgusting, gory, or repulsive.
meanings.example: That horror movie really grossed me out with all its blood and gore.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공포 영화는 피와 살점이 난무해서 정말 나를 역겹게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sicken or distress (someone) by being disgusting, gory, or lewd.
meanings.example: He was grossed out by the smell of the rotting food in the fridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 냉장고에서 썩은 음식 냄새에 역겨움을 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get comfortable or established, as in a new place.
meanings.example: I had just sat down in my favorite easy chair and settled in when the phone rang.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 좋아하는 안락의자에 앉아 편안히 쉬려고 할 때 전화가 울렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull one's socks up.
meanings.example: If you want to pass the exam, you need to pull up your socks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 분발해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To protect from intrusion by enclosing in a rope or other barrier.
meanings.example: Police cordoned off the area after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사고 후 해당 지역을 봉쇄했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight or compete against one or more opponents, in order to decide a winner.
meanings.example: The two teams will battle it out for the championship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 팀은 우승을 놓고 싸워서 결정할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: work someone's ass off.
meanings.example: He worked his arse off to get promoted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진하기 위해 엄청나게 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Less vulgar synonym of work one's ass off.
meanings.example: She worked her tail off to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 엄청나게 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overly careful in dealing with a person or situation because they get angry or offended very easily; to try very hard not to upset someone or something.
meanings.example: I feel like I'm walking on eggshells around my new boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 상사 주변에서는 매우 조심해야 하는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a show of bravery despite one's fears; to put on a brave face.
meanings.example: He was whistling in the dark, pretending he wasn't scared.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 무섭지 않은 척하며 용감한 척했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cue the cue ball in such a way as to impart backspin. On impact, the ball will follow a reverse trajectory according to the spin.
meanings.example: The player screwed back the cue ball for a difficult shot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수는 어려운 샷을 위해 큐 볼에 백스핀을 걸었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pot the cue ball accidentally after hitting the object ball.
meanings.example: He didn't mean to, but he went in off the object ball.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 의도하지 않았지만, 목적구에 맞은 후 큐 볼을 실수로 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To open.
meanings.example: Some vacancies have opened up due to retirements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴직으로 인해 몇몇 공석이 생겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cosy up.
meanings.example: Let's cozy up by the fire with a good book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 책을 옆에 끼고 벽난로 옆에서 아늑하게 지내자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone to drink liquor, usually to excess.
meanings.example: They decided to liquor up before going to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티에 가기 전에 술을 많이 마시기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move erratically by jumping.
meanings.example: The frog jumped about in the bucket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개구리가 양동이 안에서 이리저리 뛰어다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move erratically by jumping.
meanings.example: He was jumping around with excitement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신이 나서 이리저리 뛰어다니고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave an elevated position to a lower position by one jump.
meanings.example: I jumped down the small flight of stairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 계단 몇 칸을 뛰어내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter something quickly, usually a mode of transport.
meanings.example: I jumped in the car, and we sped off to the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 차에 재빨리 올라탔고, 우리는 회의 장소로 달려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from an elevated place by one jump.
meanings.example: The cat jumped off the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 식탁에서 뛰어내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To purify by melting and removing slag.
meanings.example: There is a lot of copper oxide, but I can remove that by melting everything and slagging it off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산화구리가 많지만, 모든 것을 녹여 슬래그를 제거하면 그것을 없앨 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider, to contemplate, to intend.
meanings.example: That's not what I had in mind, but I'll take it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 생각했던 것은 아니지만, 이걸로 할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exhale.
meanings.example: He took a deep breath and then slowly breathed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 심호흡을 하고 천천히 숨을 내쉬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become liked or accepted by and/or involved or associated with (especially a group of people)
meanings.example: I'm very worried about her – she's got in with the wrong crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀가 걱정된다. 그녀는 나쁜 무리와 어울리게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot someone in the head.
meanings.example: The movie depicted a character threatening to blow someone's brains out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 누군가의 머리를 쏘겠다고 위협하는 등장인물을 묘사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat, especially with gusto.
meanings.example: If you'll just let little Wackford tuck into something fat, I'll be obliged to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꼬마 웩포드가 기름진 것을 열심히 먹게 해주시면 감사하겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall from an upright or standing position to a horizontal or prone position.
meanings.example: We'll have to walk; my car has fallen over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걸어가야 해. 내 차가 넘어졌어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To board a vehicle.
meanings.example: Jump on a nº9 bus, and it will take you more or less to the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 9번 버스를 타면 거의 문 앞까지 데려다줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brave or tough enough to deal with something in a manner that is considered strong or manly.
meanings.example: Stop complaining and man up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 불평 그만하고 남자답게 행동해!

meanings.englishDefinition: countdown.
meanings.example: The final seconds of the count down were broadcast live.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카운트다운의 마지막 몇 초가 생중계되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To announce the passage of time to a precisely timed expected event.
meanings.example: The sound system counted down the seconds to meteor impact.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음향 시스템이 운석 충돌까지 남은 초를 세었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used by an umpire to invite the next batter to the batter's box, especially at the beginning of a half-inning.
meanings.example: The umpire called, "Batter up!" for the next player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심판이 다음 선수를 향해 "타석에 들어서라!"라고 외쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To batter or beat up thoroughly.
meanings.example: The thugs threatened to batter him up if he didn't pay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 깡패들은 돈을 내지 않으면 몹시 때리겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say flattering or fawning things to a person in the hope of gaining favour with them.
meanings.example: He's always trying to piss in the boss's pocket to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진을 위해 항상 상사에게 아첨하려고 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the book value of (an asset) to zero.
meanings.example: The company decided to write off the old equipment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 오래된 장비를 감가상각하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow a portion to remain unused or unconsumed.
meanings.example: The family leaves over enough food from Sunday dinner for Monday lunch and dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가족은 일요일 저녁 식사에서 월요일 점심과 저녁 식사를 위해 음식을 남겨둔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To watch secretly or covertly; to surveil.
meanings.example: The detective was hired to spy on the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 형사는 용의자를 감시하기 위해 고용되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be confident in.
meanings.example: I know I can rely on you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 당신을 믿을 수 있다는 것을 안다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect, assemble or gather small amounts (especially of money), from various sources, with some difficulty
meanings.example: They managed to scrape together enough money for a down payment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계약금으로 쓸 충분한 돈을 어렵게 마련했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase or complete by adding one or more pieces.
meanings.example: She pieced out a meal for three from what was in the cupboard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 찬장에 있는 것으로 세 사람 분의 식사를 조각내어 늘렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To count on or depend (on).
meanings.example: I reckoned on my pension being enough.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 내 연금이 충분할 것이라고 기대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect; to anticipate future actions based on.
meanings.example: I didn't plan on Truman winning the election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 트루먼이 선거에서 이길 것이라고 예상하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To object to or interfere with the actions of (someone seen as bullying, pushy, or controlling).
meanings.example: If anyone stood up to him, I bet he'd back down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가 그에게 맞서기만 한다면, 그는 물러설 것이라고 나는 장담한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak or act in support or defense of (a person, a cause, a belief, or the like).
meanings.example: She stood up for me when no one else would, and I've never forgotten it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무도 나서지 않을 때 그녀는 나를 지지해 주었고, 나는 그것을 결코 잊지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose by surrounding with walls.
meanings.example: They had walled in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정원을 벽으로 둘러쌌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate with a wall.
meanings.example: The previous owners had walled off two rooms, making an apartment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이전 집주인들은 두 개의 방을 벽으로 막아 아파트를 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seal with a wall.
meanings.example: They walled up the basement space that had been used as a coal bin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 석탄 창고로 사용되던 지하실 공간을 벽으로 막아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To descend, fall down, collapse.
meanings.example: A tree came down and hit me on the head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무가 쓰러져 내 머리를 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To squeeze (wet clothing or cloth), either by twisting with one's hands, or by passing it through a wringer, to remove the water.
meanings.example: He wrung out the wet towel before hanging it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젖은 수건을 걸기 전에 물기를 짜냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw inward using suction. en
meanings.example: This is the component of the vacuum cleaner that sucks in dust.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것이 먼지를 빨아들이는 진공청소기의 부품입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone to become slowly more and more involved in a business or situation that is often not to that person's liking.
meanings.example: I really didn't want to be on the committee, but somehow I got sucked into it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 정말 위원회에 참여하고 싶지 않았지만, 어쩌다 보니 그 일에 빠져들게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue; to proceed; to go on.
meanings.example: After spending the night resting in an abandoned church, the group decided to move on in their quest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버려진 교회에서 밤새 쉬고 난 후, 그들은 탐구를 계속하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: apart
meanings.example: And besides, I was also enjoying the way you took them apart. You know, arguing with them like you did.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그리고 게다가, 나는 당신이 그들을 조목조목 비판하는 방식도 즐기고 있었어요. 당신이 했던 것처럼 그들과 논쟁하는 것을 말이죠.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: The fuel was used up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연료가 다 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Worn out; depleted; exhausted; having nothing left; useless, due to the expenditure of all resources.
meanings.example: After a long journey, their supplies were used up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 여정 끝에 그들의 보급품은 다 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a miserable experience.
meanings.example: If you're going through hell, keep going!
meanings.exampleKorean: 끔찍한 경험을 하고 있다면, 계속 나아가세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: It's always nice to look back on fond childhood memories.
meanings.exampleKorean: 행복했던 어린 시절의 추억을 되돌아보는 것은 언제나 즐겁다.

meanings.englishDefinition: look-back.
meanings.example: The look back at his childhood was bittersweet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 어린 시절을 돌아보는 것은 달콤쌉싸름했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a screen, grate, sieve or similar means to separate large from small (objects or particles).
meanings.example: The machine screens out impurities from the water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 기계는 물에서 불순물을 걸러낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the lid of a jar or other container by unscrewing it.
meanings.example: He screwed off the cap of the bottle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 병뚜껑을 돌려 열었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An expression of surprise or disbelief.
meanings.example: "We just won a new car!" "Screw off! You're joking, right?"
meanings.exampleKorean: "우리 새 차에 당첨됐어!" "말도 안 돼! 농담하는 거지?"

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep, usually from implied exhaustion.
meanings.example: The kids sacked out in the back seat before we made it home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 집에 도착하기도 전에 뒷좌석에서 뻗어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave one's present location; to leave somewhere for another place.
meanings.example: Alright, troops, line up and move out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋아, 병사들, 줄을 서서 이동하라!

meanings.englishDefinition: To exhaust; to tire out.
meanings.example: The walk tuckered him out so much he had to take a nap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산책으로 너무 지쳐서 그는 낮잠을 자야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find (something) by using the sense of smell.
meanings.example: Because they have far more powerful noses than humans, dogs are ideal to sniff out drugs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개는 인간보다 훨씬 뛰어난 후각을 가지고 있기 때문에 마약을 찾아내는 데 이상적이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue or persist, regardless of circumstances or setbacks; to keep trying or striving.
meanings.example: Even though the project faced many challenges, they decided to keep on truckin'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 많은 어려움에 직면했음에도 불구하고, 그들은 계속 나아가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recharge, to give electrical power to something.
meanings.example: You should charge up your phone before the camping trip, as there won't be any power supplies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캠핑 여행 전에 휴대폰을 충전해야 할 것입니다. 전원 공급 장치가 없을 것이기 때문입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plump up.
meanings.example: Fluff up the pillows to get the bed ready for guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님을 위해 침대를 준비하려면 베개를 푹신하게 하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enhance the flavor of something by adding spice to it.
meanings.example: You can spice up your cooking with some fresh herbs and garlic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신선한 허브와 마늘을 넣어 요리에 풍미를 더할 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a risk, putting oneself in a vulnerable position.
meanings.example: is the cynical cafe owner who lives by his own moral code and sticks his neck out for “no one.”
meanings.exampleKorean: 자신의 도덕률에 따라 살며 누구를 위해서도 위험을 무릅쓰지 않는 냉소적인 카페 주인이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate by cooking.
meanings.example: You have to cook off the excess liquid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초과 액체를 익혀서 제거해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a silly, childish, or irresponsible way.
meanings.example: Stop playing around, and get on with your homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 숙제나 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard, or throw away.
meanings.example: This shirt has just about had it. I think I'll chuck it away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 셔츠는 거의 다 입었다. 버려야 할 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin.
meanings.example: She started off with a lullaby.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자장가로 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inform or tell.
meanings.example: It looks like somebody finally clued them in that the intercom was broken the whole time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가가 인터콤이 계속 고장났었다는 것을 마침내 그들에게 알려준 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become mentally focused after a period of being unfocused; to regain control of oneself
meanings.example: After the bad news, she needed a moment to pull herself together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 소식을 들은 후, 그녀는 마음을 다잡을 시간이 필요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave quickly
meanings.example: I have to shoot off, my interview starts in under an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빨리 가야 해요, 면접이 한 시간도 안 남았어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collide with by walking.
meanings.example: Mind you don't walk into that wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽에 부딪히지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to tell somebody to leave one alone.
meanings.example: He kept bothering me, so I told him to bug off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 계속 나를 귀찮게 했고, 그래서 나는 그에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away.
meanings.example: I lied to him to appease him so he would bug off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 달래서 그가 가버리도록 거짓말을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wander around idly to no purpose; to loaf or loiter.
meanings.example: He spent his summer bumming around the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 여름 내내 도시를 어슬렁거리며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make plumper
meanings.example: Please plump up the pillows.
meanings.exampleKorean: 베개를 푹신하게 해주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease to support a person or cause.
meanings.example: After the divorce, all his friends fell away one by one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이혼 후 그의 친구들은 하나둘씩 떨어져 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: The army was forced to fall back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대는 후퇴할 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall to the ground. To collapse.
meanings.example: Ring a-ring o' roses, / A pocketful of posies. / A-tishoo! A-tishoo! / We all fall down. — traditional nursery rhyme (British version)
meanings.exampleKorean: 장미꽃을 빙 둘러서, / 주머니 가득 꽃다발을. / 에취! 에취! / 우리 모두 넘어진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be merged or coalesce; to become identical.
meanings.example: The two companies decided to fall together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 회사는 합병하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive to the present day; to be extant in some form.
meanings.example: It is somewhat remarkable that none of bishop Ridley’s sermons have come down to us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 리들리 주교의 설교가 하나도 우리에게 전해지지 않았다는 것은 다소 놀라운 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join (a group of people).
meanings.example: He has fallen in with a bad lot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나쁜 무리와 어울리게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become detached.
meanings.example: One of the wagon wheels came off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마차 바퀴 하나가 떨어져 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away or depart, especially in haste or without notice.
meanings.example: the outlaws lit out for the border
meanings.exampleKorean: 무법자들이 국경으로 급히 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work hard at a task.
meanings.example: She won't be going out much, as she's beavering away on her thesis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 논문에 매진하고 있어서 외출을 많이 하지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy oneself, to have fun.
meanings.example: They are living it up on their vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 휴가 동안 신나게 즐기고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish (literally, especially by physically covering or rubbing); to extinguish (figuratively): to stop (a process), to kill (a process).
meanings.example: The police snuffed out the rebellion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 반란을 진압했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or abolish completely.
meanings.example: We must root out corruption from our society.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 사회에서 부패를 근절해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shirk; to avoid attending or to leave early, especially without permission.
meanings.example: He has a class on Thursday afternoons, but he skipped out this week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 목요일 오후에 수업이 있지만, 이번 주에는 몰래 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave an Indian reservation to which one was restricted.
meanings.example: The Indian may go off the reservation, he can steal from the whites and run back to the reservation with impunity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인디언은 보호구역을 벗어나 백인에게서 훔치고도 보호구역으로 무사히 돌아갈 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport someone or something to various locations by pushing a wheeled transporter such as a wheelchair, wheelbarrow or trolley.
meanings.example: pony made a moment’s pause; but, as if it occurred to him that to stop when he was required might be to establish an inconvenient and dangerous precedent, he immediately started off again, rattled at a fast trot to the street corner, wheeled round, came back, and then stopped of his own accord.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조랑말은 잠시 멈췄지만, 멈춰야 할 때 멈추는 것이 불편하고 위험한 선례를 남길 수 있다고 생각했는지 즉시 다시 출발하여 빠른 걸음으로 길모퉁이까지 달려갔다가, 돌아서서 돌아온 뒤 스스로 멈췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give something against one's will.
meanings.example: The soldiers obliged us to yield up all our money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 우리에게 모든 돈을 마지못해 내놓도록 강요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish by stamping.
meanings.example: Since 1956, Nasution had been trying to stamp out corruption in the Army.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1956년 이후, 나수티온은 군대 내 부패를 근절하기 위해 노력해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something, like a piece of cloth or bread, by tearing it with one quick strong pull.
meanings.example: He yanked off the old sticker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오래된 스티커를 세게 잡아당겨 떼어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove before publication or broadcasting (as an editorial decision).
meanings.example: The controversial scene was edited out of the film.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논란의 여지가 있는 장면은 영화에서 편집되어 삭제되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically corrode or erode (something) gradually.
meanings.example: The acid ate away at the metal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산이 금속을 서서히 부식시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard, to dispose of (something).
meanings.example: They decided to chuck out the old furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 가구를 버리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise; to excite; to stir up.
meanings.example: The speech worked up the crowd's enthusiasm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연설은 군중의 열정을 고조시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a close relationship with (someone).
meanings.example: I hear that John has taken up with Jane.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존이 제인과 사귀기 시작했다는 소식을 들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in futile or impossible activity.
meanings.example: You can't park here; I'm saving this spot for my friend.Go piss up a rope.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여기 주차할 수 없어. 친구 자리를 맡아 놨어. 헛수고하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slow; to slow down; to decelerate.
meanings.example: has been intto prison ministry work for several years and has never slowed up, but intensified her love.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간 교도소 선교 활동을 해왔으며, 결코 속도를 줄이지 않고 사랑을 더욱 intensifed했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go on strike.
meanings.example: The workers decided to throw down their tools.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노동자들은 파업하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to give a higher tone or sound; to give a higher tone to; to make more intense; to heighten; to strengthen.
meanings.example: Exercise can help tone up your muscles and improve your posture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운동은 근육의 탄력을 높이고 자세를 개선하는 데 도움이 될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass time doing unimportant things; to mess around.
meanings.example: Stop footling around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일 시작해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass time doing unimportant things; to mess around.
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon footling about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 빈둥거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste (time).
meanings.example: You've just footled away twenty minutes!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너 방금 20분이나 시간을 낭비했어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To wither, particularly during the winter.
meanings.example: same walls, when the Virginia creeper dies back in winter, show a date-mark of 1490.
meanings.exampleKorean: 같은 벽들은, 겨울에 담쟁이덩굴이 죽으면, 1490년이라는 날짜 표시를 보여준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout in a loud voice, due to pain, or fear, or unhappiness.
meanings.example: He cried out in pain when he stubbed his toe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발가락을 찧었을 때 그는 고통으로 비명을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in urgent need (of)
meanings.example: This state of affairs is crying out for an independent investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이러한 상황은 독립적인 조사를 절실히 요구하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cancel something previously arranged with someone.
meanings.example: Sorry, but I have to cry off the game on Saturday, as my mother-in-law is coming to visit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송하지만, 시어머니께서 방문하시기 때문에 토요일 경기는 취소해야 할 것 같아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To state strongly, clearly, and accurately.
meanings.example: He laid his concerns on the line during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의 중에 자신의 우려 사항을 분명하게 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: land on one's feet.
meanings.example: Despite losing his job, he fell on his feet and found a better one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장을 잃었음에도 불구하고 그는 운 좋게 더 좋은 직장을 찾았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be lucky, or successful, often in difficult situations.
meanings.example: She always seems to land on her feet, no matter what happens.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무슨 일이 있어도 그녀는 항상 운이 좋은 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inflate with air.
meanings.example: We puffed up the balloons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 풍선을 불었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inflate.
meanings.example: He puffed out his cheeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 볼을 부풀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stick one's nose into.
meanings.example: Don't poke your nose into other people's business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 사람 일에 참견하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere or try to intervene in (an issue that does not concern one).
meanings.example: He always sticks his nose into matters that don't concern him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신과 상관없는 일에 간섭한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exercise influence or authority, especially to an excessive degree or in an objectionable manner.
meanings.example: Jim was a tyrant who threw his weight around when it came to punishing his students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 짐은 학생들을 처벌할 때 권세를 부리는 폭군이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a person to be depressed or disappointed.
meanings.example: The bad news really bummed me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 나쁜 소식은 나를 정말 낙담시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block, suppress, restrain (something).
meanings.example: The chairman sat on the report until the end of the legislative session.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의장은 입법 회기 종료 시까지 보고서를 묵살했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accuse, arrest, try for a criminal act.
meanings.example: The police had him up for theft.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그를 절도 혐의로 기소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wind (a tape, cassette, or film, etc) towards the beginning; to rewind.
meanings.example: Please wind back the tape to the beginning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 테이프를 처음으로 되감아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return in time to an earlier period of history.
meanings.example: wind the clock back some 60 years, and you would find that away from the South East of England, the North West once had the most extensive and varied selection of electric routes in the country.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간을 약 60년 전으로 되돌리면, 영국 남동부에서 벗어나 북서부가 한때 전국에서 가장 광범위하고 다양한 전기 노선을 보유했음을 알 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move something indoors, or into an area.
meanings.example: Could you bring in the groceries?
meanings.exampleKorean: 식료품들을 안으로 들여다줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand erect, bristle, especially from fear.
meanings.example: The horrifying tales he told made my hair stand on end.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 들려준 끔찍한 이야기들은 내 머리카락을 곤두서게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate or destroy by fast-moving water, such as a flood or a high sea.
meanings.example: Thousands were left homeless as the flood waters washed away the eastern part of the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수물이 도시 동부를 휩쓸어 버리면서 수천 명이 집을 잃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To help to swallow (something, especially food or a pill) by drinking a liquid.
meanings.example: Now we just need a drink to wash it down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 이걸 삼키기 위해 마실 것이 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass (one) unnoticed so that one is unaffected by it.
meanings.example: The news of the actor's death just washed over me, as I had never particularly cared for him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 배우를 별로 좋아하지 않았기 때문에 그의 사망 소식이 나에게 별다른 영향을 주지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie fully extended.
meanings.example: My cat likes stretching out in front of the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 고양이는 벽난로 앞에서 몸을 쭉 펴고 눕는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter.
meanings.example: Please come in and look around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어오셔서 둘러보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To elicit, evoke, or emphasize (a particular quality).
meanings.example: Her talent for music really brings out her personality.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 음악적 재능은 그녀의 개성을 정말 잘 드러낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fetch something.
meanings.example: I brought back the groceries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 식료품들을 가져왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in doing something considered to be very difficult.
meanings.example: I don't know how, but he managed to bring off the Acme Foods deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떻게 했는지 모르겠지만, 그는 Acme Foods 거래를 성공적으로 성사시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle one's differences and forgive.
meanings.example: After their argument, they decided to kiss and make up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 싸움 후에 그들은 화해하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recline while still in a seated position, with one's back on the frame of the seat.
meanings.example: Please just sit back and watch the show.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 편히 앉아서 쇼를 보세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unwillingly stay seated until the end of an event.
meanings.example: We had to sit through a 2-hour awards ceremony before the food arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식이 도착하기 전에 우리는 2시간짜리 시상식을 끝까지 앉아 있어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain neutral on a certain topic, to not have a stance or opinion.
meanings.example: You can't sit on the fence any longer; you have to choose a side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 중립을 지킬 수 없어. 한쪽 편을 선택해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill by means of a firearm, especially deliberately and in a brutal manner.
meanings.example: The assassin gunned down the politician in broad daylight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암살자는 대낮에 정치인을 총으로 쏘아 죽였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain or continue a positive streak.
meanings.example: The new teacher was so enthusiastic; I hope she can keep it up until the end of the year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 선생님은 매우 열정적이었어요. 연말까지 그 열정을 계속 유지하길 바라요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Well done!
meanings.example: You got an A on the test? Keep it up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에서 A를 받았다고? 잘했어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more likeable to someone.
meanings.example: I didn't like this song at first, but now it's starting to grow on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음에는 이 노래가 싫었는데, 이제는 점점 좋아지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emerge from (something); to take a particular shape as a result of (something); to come to exist from (an origin).
meanings.example: I give my old clothes to charity when I've grown out of them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 옷이 작아지면 나는 헌 옷을 자선단체에 기부한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together, as a gang.
meanings.example: The bullies ganged up to intimidate the new student.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불량배들은 새 학생을 위협하기 위해 한패가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together in a group in order to overpower someone else.
meanings.example: Don't let them gang up on you; stand your ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 너에게 합세하여 괴롭히게 두지 마. 당당하게 맞서.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a map of an area.
meanings.example: You two sit down, and I'll map it out for you.” For the next fifteen minutes I told them a tale of greed, racism, treachery and murder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너희 둘은 앉아 있어. 내가 너희를 위해 계획을 세워줄게. 다음 15분 동안 나는 탐욕, 인종차별, 배신, 살인에 대한 이야기를 들려주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a liquid; to move slowly down to lower substrate levels.
meanings.example: They might say that the economy is improving, but it is taking a long time for any money to filter down to the poorer classes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경제가 나아지고 있다고 말할 수도 있지만, 그 돈이 빈곤층에게 스며드는 데는 오랜 시간이 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Cheer up; take courage; take heart.
meanings.example: Buck up! Things will get better soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기운 내! 곧 상황이 나아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become encouraged, reinvigorated, or cheerful; to summon one's courage or spirits; to pluck up courage.
meanings.example: I realized I needed to buck up and tackle the problem head-on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 기운을 차리고 문제에 정면으로 맞서야 한다는 것을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unite for a common objective.
meanings.example: “Take time,” said Lord Caterham. “And take some food. What you need is some food, to pull you together. Poached eggs now? There were some poached eggs here a minute or two ago.”
meanings.exampleKorean: “시간을 가지세요,” 캐터햄 경이 말했다. “그리고 음식을 좀 드세요. 당신에게 필요한 것은 기운을 차리게 해 줄 음식입니다. 지금 수란이요? 방금 전까지 수란이 있었는데.”

meanings.englishDefinition: To undergo a weigh-in.
meanings.example: Two days before the fight, the boxers weigh in with reporters watching.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기 이틀 전, 복서들은 기자들이 지켜보는 가운데 계체한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To influence, have an effect on.
meanings.example: That ruling bears on our application.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 판결은 우리 신청에 영향을 미친다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To severely berate someone.
meanings.example: He was just trying to help, so don't bite his head off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 단지 도우려고 했을 뿐이니, 그를 신랄하게 비난하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call a number of people by phone, usually a circle of friends, to organise something.
meanings.example: I'll ring around my friends to see who can come to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누가 파티에 올 수 있는지 알아보기 위해 친구들에게 돌려가며 전화할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return a phone call.
meanings.example: John called. He asked for you to ring him back as soon as you arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존이 전화했어요. 도착하는 대로 다시 전화해 달라고 했어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress so as to be more sexually attractive, often applying excessive makeup or wearing revealing clothing.
meanings.example: Look at her, all hussied up with her makeup and fishnet stockings! For shame!
meanings.exampleKorean: 화장과 망사 스타킹으로 화려하게 치장한 그녀를 봐! 부끄러운 줄 알아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To show annoyance, or to complain loudly about something, often when it is of little importance in reality.
meanings.example: She kicked up a fuss when her order was wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주문이 잘못되었을 때 그녀는 소란을 피웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rear back; to become more active or restless; to speed up.
meanings.example: The horse kicked up dust as it galloped away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말이 달려나가면서 먼지를 일으켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cursorily inspect To quickly check, to give a brief or cursory visual inspection.
meanings.example: He looked his eyes over the shabbily-dressed man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 남루하게 차려입은 남자를 훑어보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard, to throw away, to toss out or eliminate.
meanings.example: considering Canada's heroics in dumping out the Black Ferns, the Red Roses may need more next weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캐나다가 블랙 펀스를 탈락시킨 영웅적인 활약을 고려할 때, 레드 로즈는 다음 주말에 더 많은 것이 필요할지도 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: chew the scenery.
meanings.example: The actor was accused of chewing on the scenery in his latest role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 최근 역할에서 과장되게 연기한다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: chew the scenery.
meanings.example: The actor was accused of chewing up the scenery in his latest role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 최근 역할에서 과장되게 연기한다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to work.
meanings.example: TNo more pomp and ceremony, the new pope seemed to be saying, but sleeves rolled up and joining the fight for social and economic justice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 겉치레는 없다는 것이 새 교황의 말인 듯했다. 팔을 걷어붙이고 사회적, 경제적 정의를 위한 싸움에 동참하라는 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shootout.
meanings.example: The police were involved in a dangerous shoot out with the criminals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 범죄자들과 위험한 총격전에 휘말렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy something or remove something from its usual location by shooting.
meanings.example: You'll shoot your eye out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 총으로 네 눈을 쏘아 버릴 수도 있어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To sketch or mark with pencil, crayon, etc., on a given surface.
meanings.example: Without the proper resources, the young manager drew on his imagination to solve the crisis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적절한 자원이 없었기 때문에, 젊은 관리자는 위기를 해결하기 위해 자신의 상상력을 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy or acquire quickly, usually because the item is a bargain or in short supply or something one has been searching for.
meanings.example: I dashed into the mall; bought a gift; raced to the card store, snapped up a two-fer gift-bag special and was back in my car in 26 minutes. I could medal in power shopping.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 쇼핑몰로 달려가 선물을 샀다. 카드 가게로 가서 2개 묶음 선물 가방 특별 상품을 잽싸게 사고 26분 만에 차로 돌아왔다. 파워 쇼핑으로 메달을 딸 수도 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To terminate suddenly a mood, emotional state, or reverie.
meanings.example: I felt sad that he had left, but I had to snap out of it and get on with my life.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 떠나서 슬펐지만, 나는 슬픔에서 벗어나 내 삶을 계속 살아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refresh, enliven or restore.
meanings.example: I need some coffee to pep me up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 활기를 되찾기 위해 커피가 필요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach a person or animal without being seen or heard
meanings.example: If you sneak up from behind like that, you'll give me a heart attack!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 뒤에서 몰래 다가오면 심장마비 걸리겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter without being noticed.
meanings.example: I'm going to try to sneak in by the back door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뒷문으로 몰래 들어가 볼 생각이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave without being noticed.
meanings.example: The thief managed to slip away into the dark alley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어두운 골목으로 도둑이 몰래 빠져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat (someone) up by punching, to fist-fight.
meanings.example: He got into a punch-up outside the bar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바 밖에서 주먹 싸움을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: punch-up.
meanings.example: The police broke up the punch-up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 싸움을 말렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place, or a meeting, without being noticed
meanings.example: I'm going to try to slip off from work early, if I can.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가능하다면 일찍 퇴근하려고 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place, a meeting, etc., without being noticed; to slip away, slip off.
meanings.example: I'm going to try to slide off from work early, if I can.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가능하다면 일찍 퇴근하려고 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry up.
meanings.example: We need to get a wriggle on if we want to arrive before the show starts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쇼가 시작하기 전에 도착하려면 서둘러야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time doing pointless or silly things
meanings.example: What are you pratting about with now? Get your homework done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 또 무슨 헛짓이야? 숙제나 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hunt something and capture or kill it.
meanings.example: I managed to hunt down a surviving copy of the magazine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 잡지의 살아남은 부수 하나를 찾아낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avenge oneself.
meanings.example: He vowed to get his own back for the insult.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모욕에 대해 복수하겠다고 맹세했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To figure out; to discover or find a solution to.
meanings.example: It took him a long time to puzzle out the complex problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 복잡한 문제를 풀어내는 데 오랜 시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose to participate in something
meanings.example: You need to opt in to receive our newsletter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뉴스레터를 받으려면 참여를 선택해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Do not include me; leave me out; exclude me. from 1940s
meanings.example: If you are planning to break the rules, please include me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 규칙을 어길 계획이라면, 나는 거기서 빼 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become filled or clogged with silt.
meanings.example: the estuary has silted up, and only small pleasure craft can sail up the narrow channel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강어귀가 토사로 막혀 좁은 수로를 작은 레저 보트만 항해할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn something away; to ward off.
meanings.example: She fended away his eager hands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 열정적인 손길을 쳐냈다.
