meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to do something because of cowardice.
meanings.example: He said he was going to climb that tree, but he wussed out at the last moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 나무에 오르겠다고 했지만, 막판에 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat.
meanings.example: There's nothing I like more than snacking down on some nice crispy potato chips.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바삭바삭한 감자칩을 먹는 것보다 더 좋은 것은 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To launch into an animated diatribe, or passionate description or explanation of something.
meanings.example: I barely mentioned the band's name before he went off on one about how commercial the top forty is these days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 요즘 탑 40이 얼마나 상업적인지에 대해 열변을 토하기 시작하기도 전에 나는 겨우 밴드 이름을 언급했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attend a class, educational presentation, meeting, etc. for one time, as a visitor.
meanings.example: The professor invited me to sit in on his lecture next Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교수님은 다음 주 화요일 자신의 강의를 참관하라고 나를 초대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid or eschew.
meanings.example: He tried to keep away from danger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위험을 피하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accelerate.
meanings.example: The car began to pick up speed as it went down the hill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언덕을 내려가면서 자동차의 속도가 붙기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily.
meanings.example: It started to bucket down just as we left the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에서 나서자마자 비가 억수같이 쏟아지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about; to meditate concerning.
meanings.example: "A girl's got to think on her future."
meanings.exampleKorean: 소녀는 자신의 미래에 대해 숙고해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange a system of gear wheels or pulleys etc such that the driven part rotates or moves slower than the driving part.
meanings.example: The driver had to gear down to climb the steep hill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운전자는 가파른 언덕을 오르기 위해 기어를 낮춰야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to get ready for a fight
meanings.example: He had to square up his debts before leaving the country.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나라를 떠나기 전에 빚을 청산해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: Don't let them push you around; stand up for yourself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 너를 함부로 대하게 두지 마. 네 주장을 펼쳐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward quickly, or to make good progress with (especially a plan).
meanings.example: When stay-at-home orders kept Jeff and Janet Ralli from looking at a home in the neighborhood they had their eye on in Ocean County, New Jersey, they forged ahead online.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자택 대기 명령으로 뉴저지 오션 카운티의 이웃 동네 집을 보러 갈 수 없었을 때, 그들은 온라인으로 집을 알아보며 앞으로 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck off.
meanings.example: He told the annoying stranger to shit off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 짜증나는 낯선 사람에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to tilt to one side
meanings.example: The boat heeled over in the strong wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 강풍에 한쪽으로 기울었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat in a contest or a game by a narrow margin of victory.
meanings.example: Barcelona edged out in the penalties scoring 3-2.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바르셀로나는 승부차기에서 3-2로 간신히 이겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete vigorously, to quarrel, or to fight physically.
meanings.example: The two boxers were mixing it up in the ring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 복서가 링 위에서 치열하게 겨루고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unite or connect.
meanings.example: If you join up the dots, you will reveal a picture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점들을 연결하면 그림이 드러날 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form small teams, especially pairs, often to reduce the likelihood of any one person suffering harm without being noticed or to suit the nature of a task to be accomplished
meanings.example: The students were asked to buddy up for the group project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 조별 과제를 위해 짝을 지으라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rip away from; to pull a piece from forcibly.
meanings.example: Do not tear off the price tag if you want to return that shirt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 셔츠를 반품하려면 가격표를 떼어내지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: tear-off.
meanings.example: Please fill out the tear-off slip at the bottom of the page.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페이지 하단의 떼어내는 부분을 작성해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dust from.
meanings.example: I think it's time to dust off my old golf clubs, now that I'm retired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은퇴했으니 이제 내 오래된 골프채 먼지를 털 때라고 생각한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish by making one last addition.
meanings.example: They capped off the evening with a spectacular fireworks display.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 화려한 불꽃놀이로 저녁을 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from one position to a higher position by one jump.
meanings.example: I jumped up onto the top of the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 담 위로 뛰어올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To alight.
meanings.example: The rabbit hopped off the log and into the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토끼는 통나무에서 뛰어내려 풀숲으로 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express (an emotion, belief, or stance) overtly and make it an important part of one's public life.
meanings.example: He always wears his heart on his sleeve, so you know exactly how he feels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 감정을 숨기지 않기 때문에 당신은 그의 기분을 정확히 알 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form (a word) from letters; say the letters in a word.
meanings.example: He used Scrabble tiles to spell out “I LOVE YOU”.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스크래블 타일을 사용해 “I LOVE YOU”라고 철자를 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To question and listen attentively in order to discover a person's opinion, intent, or preference, especially by using indirect conversational remarks.
meanings.example: I tried to sound out my boss about a possible promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 승진 가능성에 대해 상사의 의향을 타진하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sign (legislation) as a mark of official approval.
meanings.example: The president signed the bill into law yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대통령은 어제 그 법안에 서명하여 법제화했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: of an achievement, emotion, idea, suggestion, etc. To belittle or dismiss; to cast doubt upon; to debunk.
meanings.example: Don't throw cold water on his enthusiasm just because you don't agree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 동의하지 않는다고 해서 그의 열정에 찬물을 끼얹지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw cold water on “to belittle or dismiss”.
meanings.example: documents from TfL poured cold water over the prospect of driverless trains. They concluded there was no financial case because because of the very high up-front infrastructure costs.
meanings.exampleKorean: TfL의 문서들은 무인 열차의 전망에 찬물을 끼얹었다. 그들은 매우 높은 초기 인프라 비용 때문에 재정적으로 타당성이 없다고 결론지었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly and sharply rebuke or insult a person, especially in response to a harmless remark.
meanings.example: I only asked him a simple question, and he snapped my head off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 단순한 질문을 했을 뿐인데, 그가 나에게 신경질적으로 대했다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To compose (a document), especially following a standard form; prepare a plan.
meanings.example: I asked my lawyer to draw up a new will.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 변호사에게 새로운 유언장을 작성해 달라고 부탁했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To neutralize the effect of.
meanings.example: We can cancel the aberration out by adding another filter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 필터를 추가하여 이상 현상을 상쇄할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be conceited.
meanings.example: Getting some feedback from others is heaps better than having tickets on yourself about your skills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자신의 기술에 대해 자만심을 갖는 것보다 다른 사람들로부터 피드백을 받는 것이 훨씬 낫다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a computer mouse pointer over (an element of the display).
meanings.example: You can see the file size when you mouse over the icon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이콘에 마우스 커서를 올리면 파일 크기를 볼 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set and cinch a saddle on a horse in preparation for riding.
meanings.example: Saddle up, men! We've got a lot of riding to do today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남자들, 안장을 얹어라! 오늘 할 일이 많다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise up, especially an animal like a horse rising up on its rear legs.
meanings.example: The horse reared up in fright when it saw the snake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말은 뱀을 보고 놀라 뒷발로 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform (a task) rapidly, often unexpectedly.
meanings.example: She fired off a quick email to her boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 상사에게 빠른 이메일을 급히 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: knock about.
meanings.example: I like to knock around the garden on Saturdays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 토요일에 정원을 어슬렁거리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To linger, loiter, or stay.
meanings.example: Stop hanging about and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 서성거리지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: wait a minute; hold on
meanings.example: Hang about! I'll be with you in a moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐만! 곧 갈게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To potter, to be gently active doing various things in an almost aimless manner.
meanings.example: I like to relax pottering about the house doing this and that at the weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말에 집 안에서 이것저것 하면서 빈둥거리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To potter, to be gently active doing various things in an almost aimless manner.
meanings.example: I like to relax pottering around the house doing this and that at the weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말에 집 안에서 이것저것 하면서 빈둥거리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go into something by falling.
meanings.example: The cat might fall into the pool if you're not careful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심하지 않으면 고양이가 수영장으로 떨어질 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exercise one's right to legal representation; to engage the services of a lawyer.
meanings.example: You'd better lawyer up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사를 선임하는 게 좋을 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To put up with something; to deal with something, such as pain or misfortune, without complaining.
meanings.example: It's a tough situation, but you just have to suck it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 상황이지만, 그냥 참아내야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (a structure) to burn to nothing.
meanings.example: The police are hoping to find the people who burned down the cottage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그 오두막을 전소시킨 사람들을 찾기를 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disapprove of (something or someone, often a behavior); to express disapproval of (something or someone).
meanings.example: Many parents frown on their children spending too much time on video games.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 부모들이 자녀들이 비디오 게임에 너무 많은 시간을 보내는 것을 못마땅해한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the electrical power on to a device as a precondition to making it operational.
meanings.example: Every morning he powered up his electronic devices, even before making coffee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매일 아침 그는 커피를 만들기 전에도 전자 기기의 전원을 켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a bossy manner toward (another person), ordering them to do things, whether or not one is actually their superior.
meanings.example: He likes to boss about his subordinates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부하 직원들에게 윗사람처럼 지시하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a bossy manner toward (another person), ordering them to do things, whether or not one is actually their superior.
meanings.example: She always bosses about the new interns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 신입 인턴들에게 윗사람처럼 지시한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a bossy manner with another person, ordering them to do things, whether or not one is actually their superior.
meanings.example: He likes to boss around his subordinates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부하 직원들에게 윗사람처럼 지시하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a bossy manner with another person, ordering them to do things, whether or not one is actually their superior.
meanings.example: She always bosses around the new interns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 신입 인턴들에게 윗사람처럼 지시한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gobble; to consume.
meanings.example: Everyone at the table now had their eyes fixed on Lantier, who, quietly seated beside Coupeau, was gobbing up the last piece of Savoy cake, which he sopped in his glass of wine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 테이블에 있던 모두가 랑티에에게 시선을 고정했다. 그는 쿠포 옆에 조용히 앉아 와인 잔에 적셔 마지막 남은 사보이 케이크 조각을 게걸스럽게 먹고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: exclude or discount To exclude; to dismiss from participation or eligibility.
meanings.example: I may not be as young as I used to be, but I can still handle this job. Don't count me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예전만큼 젊지는 않지만, 이 일을 처리할 수 있어요. 저를 무시하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve a prototype, or first draft.
meanings.example: The estimated figures are not bad, but somebody will have to work them over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추정치는 나쁘지 않지만, 누군가가 그것들을 수정해야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store; to put by.
meanings.example: They decided to lay up extra supplies for the winter month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 겨울 동안 쓸 추가 보급품을 비축하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike blows in all directions.
meanings.example: He lay about him with his fists.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주먹으로 사방을 마구 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat up or launch an attack against; to strike forcefully.
meanings.example: U.S. ships did the nautical version of someone stuck in a pub when the chairs and glass starts flying, who just looked around, shrugged their shoulders, and immediately lays into the nearest combatant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미국 선박들은 술집에서 의자와 유리잔이 날아다니는 상황에 처한 사람처럼 허둥지둥하며, 주변을 둘러보고 어깨를 으쓱한 뒤 즉시 가장 가까운 전투원에게 맹렬히 공격하는 항해 버전의 행동을 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: soaked, very wet (such that water gets through the clothes to reach the skin)
meanings.example: If you stay out in the rain, you will get wet through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비 속에 계속 있으면 흠뻑 젖을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To soak or drench thoroughly
meanings.example: The rain wet us through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 우리를 흠뻑 적셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become rounder or plumper; to complete or supplement something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 둥글게 하다, 통통하게 하다, (과정 등을) 마무리하다[보완하다]
meanings.example: She decided to round out her education with a master's degree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 석사 학위로 교육 과정을 보완하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brought to bed in childbirth.
meanings.example: She is due to lie in next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 다음 달에 출산 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed to do something as soon as possible, especially so as to have something unpleasant behind oneself.
meanings.example: I hate getting shots, but it's best just to get it over with.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주사를 맞는 것은 싫지만, 어서 끝내는 것이 가장 좋다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be at a disadvantage.
meanings.example: People who do not speak a second language find that they lose out when looking for a job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제2외국어를 못하는 사람들은 일자리를 구할 때 불리한 입장에 놓인다는 것을 알게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To practice aiming, adjusting and firing a rifle with no ammunition
meanings.example: The soldiers were told to snap in before the live fire exercise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 실탄 사격 훈련 전에 공포탄으로 사격 연습을 하라는 지시를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To queue form a line while waiting.
meanings.example: People had to queue up for hours to get tickets for the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들은 콘서트 티켓을 구하기 위해 몇 시간 동안 줄을 서야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a line while waiting.
meanings.example: We had to queue up for tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 표를 사기 위해 줄을 서야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: She stumbled on a solution to the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 문제에 대한 해결책을 우연히 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attempt to ascertain a person's point of view, or the nature of a situation, by cautious and subtle means.
meanings.example: I think we should feel out your mom's thoughts about this before we decide anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무엇을 결정하기 전에 어머니의 생각을 떠보는 것이 좋다고 생각한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unassisted and without comfort in a situation likely to result in distress or failure.
meanings.example: He was left twisting in the wind after his business partner disappeared with all the money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업 파트너가 모든 돈을 가지고 사라진 후 그는 곤경에 처해 도움 없이 기다려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something hollow.
meanings.example: we have crossed the Glenderamackin stream, which drains the northern slopes of Saddleback, and the latter has united with the St. John's Beck to form the Greta, however, we see ahead the miniature canyon the Greta has hollowed out for itself, and into the depth of which the train now descends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새들백의 북쪽 경사면을 배수하는 글렌데라마킨 개울을 건넜고, 후자는 세인트 존스 벡과 합류하여 그레타를 형성했다. 그러나 우리는 앞에서 그레타가 스스로 파낸 작은 협곡을 보았고, 기차가 이제 그 깊은 곳으로 내려간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To knock an object, usually a ball, away from oneself.
meanings.example: The ball can still be batted away with one or both hands. It can be batted from a player's hands or batted away during a shot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공은 한 손 또는 양손으로 쳐낼 수 있다. 선수의 손에서 쳐내거나 슛 중에 쳐낼 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grasp firmly; to become attached to.
meanings.example: The baby latched on to his mother's breast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 엄마 젖을 단단히 빨았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Describing an item that becomes bound to the player that loots it. The player cannot transfer it to another player; they may only sell it to an in-game vendor or discard it, destroying the item. Used mainly on quest MacGuffins and rare drops. Compare to bind on equip.
meanings.example: This legendary weapon is bind on pickup, so you can't trade it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 전설적인 무기는 획득 시 귀속이므로 거래할 수 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become permanently associated with a player character upon being picked up.
meanings.example: This sword binds on pickup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 검은 획득 시 귀속됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Describing a piece of equipment (armor, weapons, accessories) that becomes bound to a player character when equipped by them. The player may sell/trade/give it to another character so long as they have not used it. After use, the player may only sell it to an in-game vendor or discard it, destroying the item. Used mainly on quest rewards and moderate to rare drops. Compare to bind on pickup.
meanings.example: This armor is bind on equip, so you can wear it on multiple characters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 갑옷은 장착 시 귀속이므로 여러 캐릭터에 착용할 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become permanently associated with a player character upon being equipped.
meanings.example: This armor binds on equip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 갑옷은 장착 시 귀속됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy; to take pleasure in.
meanings.example: She delights in teasing her brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 남동생을 놀리는 것을 즐거워한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy.
meanings.example: He takes delight in his children's success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아이들의 성공을 기뻐한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw a pitch at a much slower than expected speed.
meanings.example: He changed up on Wilson after three fastballs and struck him out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 세 번의 빠른 공 이후에 윌슨에게 속구를 늦추어 삼진을 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deploy or release (a new film or software, etc.); to launch (a product or service), especially in a gradual fashion across multiple regions.
meanings.example: The software company is ready to roll out the new version next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어 회사는 다음 주에 새 버전을 출시할 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deploy or release a new product or service.
meanings.example: The company plans to roll out the new smartphone next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 다음 달에 새 스마트폰을 출시할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a remark or provide information when it is inappropriate or indiscreet to do so, or when one does not have permission or the authority to do so.
meanings.example: He talked out of turn and revealed the secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주제넘게 말해서 비밀을 누설했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk out of turn.
meanings.example: She spoke out of turn and offended the guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 주제넘게 말해서 손님들을 불쾌하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's act together.
meanings.example: You need to get it together if you want to pass the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 정신 차려야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by digging.
meanings.example: The archaeologist dug out a Saxon dagger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고고학자는 색슨족의 단검을 발굴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tie or fasten behind.
meanings.example: Marie tied back her long hair to keep it out of her way when she worked.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마리는 일할 때 긴 머리가 방해되지 않도록 뒤로 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: prepare for confrontation To get ready for a confrontation (debate, argument, fight).
meanings.example: The two boxers squared off before the match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 복서가 경기 전에 서로 마주 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn to as a substitute; to rely on as a backup.
meanings.example: If the business fails, I'll have to fall back on my savings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업이 실패하면 저축에 의지해야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall back on.
meanings.example: He had no one to fall back upon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 의지할 사람이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scroll down to the next page of content.
meanings.example: Press the Page Down key to scroll to the bottom of the document.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문서 맨 아래로 스크롤하려면 Page Down 키를 누르세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scroll up to the previous page of content.
meanings.example: When I paged up or down, the text would sometimes get mangled and duplicated.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페이지를 위아래로 넘길 때 텍스트가 손상되거나 중복되는 경우가 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be independent of others
meanings.example: This new theory can stand on its own without needing further evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 이론은 추가 증거 없이도 독자적으로 설 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a computer using its bootstrap procedure.
meanings.example: It takes a few minutes for the computer to boot up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터가 부팅되는 데는 몇 분이 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bail out.
meanings.example: He decided to bale out of the project when he realized it was too risky.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 위험하다는 것을 깨닫고 프로젝트에서 손을 떼기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think deeply about; to ponder or mull over.
meanings.example: I need some time to chew over the proposal before I give you my answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 답변하기 전에 제안에 대해 곰곰이 생각할 시간이 필요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An expression of disbelief.
meanings.example: Come off it, mate! You can't be serious.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말도 안 돼, 친구! 진심일 리가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop doing something or stop talking about something.
meanings.example: Oh, come off it with the endless questioning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아, 끝없는 질문은 그만해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accompany.
meanings.example: Would you like to come along with us to the movies?
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리와 함께 영화 보러 가지 않겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: come-along.
meanings.example: The mechanic used a come-along to pull the car out of the ditch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정비사는 견인기를 사용해 차를 도랑에서 끌어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Expression of encouragement or mild reproach.
meanings.example: Come along, dear, there's no point crying over it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서 와, 얘야, 울어봤자 소용없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeatedly do the same thing without making any progress.
meanings.example: We've been discussing this for hours, but we're just going round in circles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 몇 시간 동안 이것을 논의했지만, 제자리걸음만 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a workplace by punching a timecard.
meanings.example: I'm going to punch in a bit early today to get more work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 좀 더 일찍 출근 기록을 해서 일을 더 많이 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a precarious situation, unsure of the future.
meanings.example: His life was left hanging in the balance after he was shot in the side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옆구리에 총을 맞은 후 그의 생명은 위태로운 상태에 놓였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unsettle, to catch by surprise.
meanings.example: The unexpected question threw her off balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 질문이 그녀를 당황하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attain balance through the contrasting or reciprocating effect of different elements.
meanings.example: If you consider all the negative and all the positive factors, it should balance out to something acceptable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 부정적인 요인과 긍정적인 요인을 고려하면, 수용 가능한 수준으로 균형을 이룰 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be hiding, waiting to ambush or attack someone, or simply to accost them unexpectedly.
meanings.example: The hunters lay in wait for the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼들은 사슴을 잡기 위해 매복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend wastefully.
meanings.example: Don't piss away your youth on trivial matters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사소한 일에 젊음을 낭비하지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scold.
meanings.example: One night – it was 2.30am – he dressed down a graduate, screaming in her face: “Are you stupid? Are you a fucking stupid cunt?” I had never seen anything like it in a workplace, or on TV, and I haven’t since.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어느 날 밤 2시 30분에 그는 한 졸업생에게 “멍청하니? 빌어먹을 멍청한 년이야?”라고 소리치며 야단쳤다. 나는 직장이나 TV에서 그런 것을 본 적이 없었고, 이후로도 본 적이 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear informal clothes.
meanings.example: It's casual Friday, so we can dress down today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일은 캐주얼 데이라서 오늘 편하게 입어도 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something, especially spend money, more extravagantly than usual, particularly for a celebration.
meanings.example: They really pushed the boat out for their anniversary party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 기념일 파티를 위해 정말 돈을 아낌없이 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel a child from a family by placing them for adoption; to put a child up for adoption privately, without going through an adoption agency.
meanings.example: The Chinese girl was adopted out to the United States.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 중국 소녀는 미국으로 입양되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A substitute; a replacement.
meanings.example: Her stand in muffed a lot of lines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 대역이 대사를 많이 틀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To substitute for; to replace; to serve as an understudy.
meanings.example: That coat stands me in four dollars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코트는 나에게 4달러였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be completely attentive to what another person is saying.
meanings.example: The students hung on every word of the professor's lecture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 교수의 강의를 한마디 한마디 열심히 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate from a larger group.
meanings.example: The company decided to hive off its subsidiary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 자회사를 분리하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch fire and burn.
meanings.example: The entire building went up in smoke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건물 전체가 불타 없어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To switch off (something electrical).
meanings.example: Google Glass batteries power down after 45 minutes of continuous recording, making it unlikely anyone could capture an entire film.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구글 글래스 배터리는 45분간 연속 녹화 후 전원이 꺼지므로 누군가가 전체 영화를 녹화하기는 어려울 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of weather: to clear.
meanings.example: They said it was going to fair off later on, but it didn't.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나중에 날씨가 갤 거라고 했지만 그렇지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clear
meanings.example: They said it was going to fair up later on, but it didn't.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나중에 날씨가 갤 거라고 했지만 그렇지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brave; to show some courage, especially in a situation where one has so far failed to do so.
meanings.example: He finally strapped on a pair and stood up to the bully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 용기를 내어 불량배에게 맞섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle a way of doing something; do decide to do something in a particular way.
meanings.example: We haven't decided if we want to move yet, but if we go down that road, there'll clearly be no way back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 아직 이사할지 결정하지 않았지만, 만약 그렇게 하기로 결정한다면 되돌아갈 방법은 없을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To employ a way of doing something; to do something in a particular way.
meanings.example: Watts, Politics - the Good, the Bad and the Ugly
meanings.exampleKorean: 왓츠, 정치 - 좋음, 나쁨, 추함

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise suddenly.
meanings.example: He started up when he heard the scream.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비명 소리를 듣고 갑자기 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience; to suffer; to fall upon.
meanings.example: With the rise of the Internet, some media fell on hard times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인터넷의 등장으로 일부 언론 매체는 어려움을 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to produce by writing To produce by writing.
meanings.example: The reporter spent the evening writing up the interview notes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 저녁 시간에 인터뷰 노트를 정리하여 기사를 작성했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the price of.
meanings.example: The writing is good, but I had to mark you down for failing to cite references.
meanings.exampleKorean: 글은 좋지만, 참고 문헌을 인용하지 않아 점수를 깎아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seal by sewing.
meanings.example: the Narrinyeri in South Australia men of the Snake clan sometimes catch snakes, pull out their teeth or sew up their mouths, and keep them as pets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호주 남부의 나리네리족 뱀 부족 남성들은 때때로 뱀을 잡아 이빨을 뽑거나 입을 꿰매어 애완동물로 키운다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set upon or attack.
meanings.example: The angry crowd started to light into the politician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화난 군중이 정치인을 맹렬히 공격하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch all or nearly all the fish in (a given body of water), so that few or none remain.
meanings.example: Settlers came and soon fished out the lake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정착민들이 와서 곧 호수의 물고기를 모두 잡아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enquire of different people about something.
meanings.example: I don't know the answer to that, but maybe someone else does – I'll ask around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것에 대한 답은 모르지만, 다른 사람이 알지도 몰라 – 여러 사람에게 물어볼게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily; piss down.
meanings.example: Take an umbrella with you; it's starting to piss it down outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우산 챙겨가. 밖엔 비가 억수같이 쏟아지기 시작했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay off, convince to refrain etc. by corrupt payment or other service.
meanings.example: The company tried to buy off the journalist to prevent the story from being published.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 기사가 보도되는 것을 막기 위해 기자에게 뇌물을 주려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hallucinate or otherwise alter one's consciousness as a result of drugs.
meanings.example: They started to trip out after five minutes of eating the mushrooms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 버섯을 먹은 지 5분 후에 환각을 경험하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a new idea or piece of advice, and act accordingly.
meanings.example: Schools and colleges will have to take on board the views of young people and parents in their area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학교와 대학은 해당 지역의 젊은이들과 학부모들의 의견을 받아들여야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Lacking money; impecunious; in financial difficulties.
meanings.example: He was so hard up that he couldn't even afford to buy a new pair of shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 돈이 없어서 새 신발 한 켤레 살 여유조차 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: harden up (to become or make hard)
meanings.example: The dough needs to harden up in the fridge before baking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반죽은 굽기 전에 냉장고에서 단단해져야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten one's seat belt.
meanings.example: Please belt up before the plane takes off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이륙 전에 안전벨트를 매주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover with; to smear or spread on to.
meanings.example: Our house got done over last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 집은 어젯밤에 도둑맞았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (memory contents) into a swap file.
meanings.example: The system will swap out inactive pages to free up memory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시스템은 메모리를 확보하기 위해 비활성 페이지를 스왑 파일로 옮길 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Anything that is swapped out for another; an exchange.
meanings.example: Luckily, on this show we have swap outs, so we don't have to wait.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다행히 이 쇼에는 교체품이 있어서 기다릴 필요가 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange (someone or something) for another of the same kind.
meanings.example: Jerry was slowing down, so I swapped in Tom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제리가 느려지고 있어서, 톰으로 교체했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (memory contents) to auxiliary storage.
meanings.example: N. Packer, and Tuning Databases on the Solaris Platform
meanings.exampleKorean: N. 패커, 그리고 솔라리스 플랫폼에서의 데이터베이스 튜닝

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (memory contents) from auxiliary storage.
meanings.example: The system will page in the required data when it's needed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시스템은 필요할 때 필요한 데이터를 보조 저장 장치에서 가져올 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a prior state.
meanings.example: The system administrator had to roll back the changes after the update caused errors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 업데이트로 오류가 발생한 후 시스템 관리자는 변경 사항을 이전 상태로 되돌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show (someone) in: to introduce (someone) to the inside of a place.
meanings.example: L. Worley, Question of Respect: The First of Three Novels of the War of 1812, Publishing
meanings.exampleKorean: L. 워리, 존중의 문제: 1812년 전쟁 소설 3부작 중 첫 번째, 출판

meanings.englishDefinition: To don a suit, such as a spacesuit, a formal suit, a hazmat suit or (archetypally or figuratively) a suit of armour.
meanings.example: Each day for three weeks they suited up and went out to the module and fed it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 3주 동안 매일 그들은 정장을 차려입고 모듈에 나가 먹이를 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss (a tenant, etc.) while replacing manual labour with tractors. 1930s
meanings.example: The landlord decided to tractor out the small farmers and rent the land to a large agricultural company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집주인은 소작농들을 내보내고 그 땅을 대규모 농업 회사에 임대하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit.
meanings.example: The baby puked up all over my shirt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 내 셔츠에 온통 토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assess a person or a situation.
meanings.example: She looked at him carefully, trying to weigh him up. Was he really telling the truth?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그를 주의 깊게 보며 평가하려고 했다. 그가 정말 진실을 말하고 있는 걸까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay calm; to be patient; to calm down.
meanings.example: All right, all right, just keep your hair on, mate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 알았어, 알았어, 진정해, 친구.

meanings.englishDefinition: to say (something) unexpectly To say (something) unexpectedly.
meanings.example: He came out with a very dubious excuse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 의심스러운 변명을 불쑥 내놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erect; to construct.
meanings.example: They are building up a new city in the desert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 사막에 새로운 도시를 건설하고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: buildup.
meanings.example: The buildup of plaque can lead to gum disease.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치태 축적은 잇몸 질환으로 이어질 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share; especially, to share evenly.
meanings.example: Let's share out the cake equally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 케이크를 똑같이 나눠 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove all of one's clothing.
meanings.example: As soon as he got home from exercising, Ryan stripped down and took a shower.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운동을 마치고 집에 오자마자 라이언은 옷을 모두 벗고 샤워를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To browbeat or reprimand (someone) harshly, usually in front of other people.
meanings.example: She slapped down her subordinate at the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 회의에서 부하 직원을 호되게 질책했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: slapdown.
meanings.example: The politician faced a public slapdown for his controversial remarks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 논란이 된 발언으로 대중의 질책을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand some distance apart from something or someone.
meanings.example: He stood off from the fire, for fear of getting scorched.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불에 타는 것을 두려워하여 불에서 거리를 두고 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come together with (someone), often at a prearranged time and/or location.
meanings.example: The next time you come to my town, we should meet up somewhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음에 우리 동네에 올 때 어디선가 만나자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove, whether bit by bit or suddenly.
meanings.example: When we stripped away the old wallpaper, we discovered an interesting mural painted on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 벽지를 벗겨냈을 때 벽에 그려진 흥미로운 벽화를 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To advance or increase with stealth, unnoticed (literally or figuratively).
meanings.example: The lioness has to creep up behind her prey so as not to be noticed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암사자는 눈에 띄지 않기 위해 먹잇감 뒤로 살금살금 다가가야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh uproariously.
meanings.example: We all laughed our heads off at his jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그의 농담에 모두 배꼽 빠지게 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive in a storm (about a ship) and keep afloat.
meanings.example: The ship managed to ride out the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 폭풍우를 헤쳐나갈 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very angry or upset.
meanings.example: When I failed the test, I flipped out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에 떨어졌을 때 나는 격분했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wish intensely that someone succeeds in what they are doing. Often implies a silent, or almost inaudible wish.
meanings.example: The pleasure of identifying with Rambo as he kicks and punches his persecutors is the willing him on to succeed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 람보가 박해자들을 발로 차고 때리는 것에 동질감을 느끼는 즐거움은 그가 성공하기를 바라는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an imaginative, unusual or foolish idea, to invent something unreal.
meanings.example: He dreamed up a fantastic story to explain his absence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결석을 설명하기 위해 환상적인 이야기를 꾸며냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very rich.
meanings.example: After the company went public, the founders began to roll in wealth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사가 상장된 후, 창립자들은 부유해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappear gradually into.
meanings.example: The Roman Empire has melted into history.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로마 제국은 역사 속으로 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perceive the truth about, especially of future events.
meanings.example: It's hard to see into the future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미래를 간파하기는 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: I poured out three beers for my guests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들을 위해 맥주 세 잔을 따랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put off by one's facial expression
meanings.example: She can look a man on or look him off, either way. I wouldn't have thought any woman could look him off, I'd think she'd need a hatpin or a red-hot poker
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 표정으로 사람을 끌어들이거나 불쾌하게 만들 수 있다. 어떤 여자도 그렇게 할 수 있다고 생각하지 않았을 텐데, 모자핀이나 달궈진 쇠지렛대가 필요할 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To upset (a person) by being raunchy (tacky or vulgar).
meanings.example: His crude jokes really raunched out the audience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 저속한 농담은 청중을 정말 화나게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be alive, existent, or present.
meanings.example: I'll be around for another hour or so.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 시간 정도 더 있을게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be experienced in worldly matters; to be seasoned, not naive.
meanings.example: "Oh, I've been around a little before," said Tom coolly."Yes, you look like a lad who has seen something of the world."
meanings.exampleKorean: "오, 저는 좀 경험이 있어요," 톰이 쌀쌀맞게 말했다. "네, 세상 물정에 밝아 보이는 소년이군요."

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; to sod off.
meanings.example: Just rack off and leave me alone!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 꺼져서 날 내버려 둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: To obliterate by painting over.
meanings.example: Joseph's face was painted out. In place of Joseph's monarchic face the word Constitution was painted in. But the reign of Joseph was not over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요셉의 얼굴은 페인트로 덮여 지워졌다. 요셉의 군주다운 얼굴 대신 '헌법'이라는 단어가 칠해졌다. 하지만 요셉의 통치는 끝나지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or decorate in the style of goth subculture.
meanings.example: Toward the evening, we return to our bed and breakfast in order to get 'gothed up', which entails making our appearance as impressive as possible...
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁이 되면 우리는 '고스족 스타일로 꾸미기' 위해 침대와 아침 식사 장소로 돌아간다. 이는 우리의 외모를 가능한 한 인상적으로 만드는 것을 포함한다...

meanings.englishDefinition: To submit to the rules of a higher authority; obey; conform.
meanings.example: The new employees quickly fell in line with the company's policies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신입 직원들은 회사의 정책에 빠르게 순응했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: illuminated.
meanings.example: the other hand, to arrive after dusk, when the multitude of garish little public-houses are lit up, giving glimpses of crowded jostling bars and taprooms, is an introduction to a fine city well calculated to affect even the most nonchalant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 한편으로는 해가 진 후, 요란한 작은 술집들에 불이 켜져 붐비고 밀치는 바와 탭룸의 모습이 보이는 시간에 도착하는 것은 가장 무심한 사람에게도 영향을 미치도록 잘 계산된 멋진 도시에 대한 소개이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: The Christmas tree was beautifully lit up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리스마스 트리가 아름답게 불이 켜졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve appearance or appeal by increasing stylishness or functionality, or by adding other attractive features.
meanings.example: They decided to snazz up the old car with a new paint job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새 페인트칠로 오래된 차를 멋지게 꾸미기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: You can lean on me if you need help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도움이 필요하면 나에게 의지해도 돼.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell (someone) off.
meanings.example: The coach rarked up the players for their poor performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 선수들의 저조한 성적에 대해 야단쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surround and enclose in a way that restricts movement.
meanings.example: of the loveliest rivers in Wales, the Teifi, loops and swirls down to Llanybyther through a lush valley hemmed in by range upon range of wooded hills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 웨일스에서 가장 사랑스러운 강 중 하나인 테이피 강은 울창한 계곡을 따라 굽이쳐 흘러 숲이 우거진 언덕들이 연이어 둘러싸고 있는 라니비더까지 이어진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be excited or aroused by; to derive pleasure from.
meanings.example: I don't get off on champagne.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 샴페인에서 즐거움을 얻지 못해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To react with extreme agitation.
meanings.example: My friend's job at a call center is so stressful he could tear his hair out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 친구는 콜센터 직업이 너무 스트레스 받아서 머리를 쥐어뜯을 지경이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: yuk it up.
meanings.example: They were yucking it up at the comedian's jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그 코미디언의 농담에 배꼽을 잡고 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bat so that a runner on base effects (a run).
meanings.example: He batted in the winning run in the bottom of the ninth inning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 9회 말에 결승점을 타격하여 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop functioning; to come to a halt.
meanings.example: The engine seized up after only a few miles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엔진이 몇 마일도 가지 않아 고장 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have the thing mentioned as the only or most important part.
meanings.example: Tolerance consists in respecting other people’s opinions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관용은 다른 사람의 의견을 존중하는 데 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attach splints to a broken limb in order to allow it to grow together.
meanings.example: The veterinarian splintered up the horse's broken leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수의사는 말의 부러진 다리에 부목을 댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exit a place on foot, often for a short time.
meanings.example: She opened the car door and stepped out of the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 차 문을 열고 차에서 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure (a hardship or challenge) by being tough: relying on one's toughness.
meanings.example: We have to tough out this difficult period.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 어려운 시기를 힘겹게 견뎌내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax idly and mindlessly; to veg out.
meanings.example: The Nanny is one of Pippa's favourite shows, but tonight the pleasure of mindlessly blobbing out in her usual Sunday-laid-back-luxury style...
meanings.exampleKorean: 내니는 피파가 가장 좋아하는 쇼 중 하나지만, 오늘 밤은 평소 일요일의 여유로운 럭셔리 스타일로 멍하니 시간을 보내는 즐거움...

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stuck (as if in a bog) and unable to progress.
meanings.example: The negotiations have bogged down over the issue of trade tariffs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상은 관세 문제로 교착 상태에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To back out of something because of cowardice.
meanings.example: He promised to go bungee jumping, but he wimped out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 번지 점프를 하겠다고 약속했지만, 막판에 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persevere through; to complete in spite of pain, etc.
meanings.example: She had to guts out the marathon despite her injury.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부상에도 불구하고 마라톤을 용기를 내어 끝까지 완주해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defend oneself by fighting.
meanings.example: The bully hadn't been expecting his victim to fight back, and now the bully has quadriplegia.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불량배는 피해자가 반격할 것이라고 예상하지 못했고, 이제 그 불량배는 사지마비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit oneself to a task or activity that is employment-related or that requires serious effort or concentration.
meanings.example: Alright everyone, let's get down to business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 여러분, 이제 본론으로 들어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: off
meanings.example: He went to sleep, lying there under a wing of his plane, and presently Bland himself drifted off into dreams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비행기 날개 밑에 누워 잠이 들었고, 곧 블랜드 자신도 꿈속으로 빠져들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take up.
meanings.example: She decided to take up painting as a hobby.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 취미로 그림 그리기를 시작하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overcome reluctance to say something particular or to speak in general.
meanings.example: I know you have something to tell me, so spit it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 나에게 할 말이 있다는 걸 알아, 그러니 어서 말해봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: His arrogance needed taking down a peg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 오만함은 기선을 꺾을 필요가 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a particular effect in combination.
meanings.example: The costs of all the parts added up to five times the cost of the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 부품의 비용을 합하니 자동차 비용의 다섯 배에 달했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crush or form into a ball shape.
meanings.example: She balled up her paper in frustration.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 좌절감에 종이를 구겨 공으로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To support with, or as if with, a prop.
meanings.example: The government is trying to prop up the failing economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 실패하는 경제를 부양하려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To alter course so as to get farther away from an object.
meanings.example: The ship hauled off to avoid the iceberg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배는 빙산을 피하기 위해 방향을 틀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel from one end of something to the other.
meanings.example: The train went through the tunnel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 터널을 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete a hole by sinking the ball.
meanings.example: He holed out on the 18th green for a birdie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 18번 홀 그린에서 버디를 잡으며 홀인했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss to reach an agreed conclusion, or decision.
meanings.example: They argued out their differences and reached a compromise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 서로의 차이점을 논쟁하여 타협에 이르렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To succeed in defeating a challenge, or an attack.
meanings.example: The platoon fought off the guerrilla attack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소대는 게릴라 공격을 싸워 물리쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve; to gain by successful effort.
meanings.example: John has just notched up another "salesman of the month" prize.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존은 방금 또 "이달의 판매왕" 상을 달성했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flip over; to rotate top to bottom.
meanings.example: The police turned the house upside down looking for the stolen goods.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 도난품을 찾기 위해 집안을 샅샅이 뒤졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite someone to enter one's house.
meanings.example: We were getting cold talking on the doorstep, so I asked him in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문 앞에서 이야기하니 추워져서 그를 안으로 들어오라고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become cloudy or overcast.
meanings.example: The sky is clouding over. I hope you have an umbrella.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하늘이 흐려지고 있어요. 우산이 있으면 좋겠네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Return to (an activity)
meanings.example: I'll get back to work as soon as I've finished this game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 게임을 끝내자마자 다시 일하러 갈게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell something at auction.
meanings.example: I took your advice and auctioned off that old painting. You'll never believe I got over 2,000 pounds for it!
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 조언을 듣고 그 오래된 그림을 경매로 팔았습니다. 2,000파운드가 넘는 돈을 받았다는 걸 믿으실 수 없을 거예요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon, or stop supporting someone or something.
meanings.example: He promised to help, but he bailed out on me at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕겠다고 약속했지만, 막판에 나를 저버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a lot of percussive noise while moving around.
meanings.example: Stop banging about in the attic, you're making too much noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다락방에서 쿵쾅거리며 돌아다니지 마, 너무 시끄러워.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a lot of percussive noise while moving around.
meanings.example: She banged around with those tools all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 밤새도록 도구들을 가지고 쿵쾅거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The first batter in the batting order.
meanings.example: Jones has been the team's lead off hitter for three years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존스는 3년 동안 팀의 1번 타자였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: off
meanings.example: The kitchen and the lounge lead off from the hallway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부엌과 거실이 복도에서 이어진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rely on someone or something; to count on something; to depend on something.
meanings.example: I'm banking on you being there. I don't have your expertise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신이 거기 있을 거라고 믿고 있어요. 당신의 전문 지식이 없어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically damage something or assault someone.
meanings.example: He bashed about the furniture in a fit of rage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 분노에 휩싸여 가구를 마구 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break or dent badly by hitting violently.
meanings.example: We got home to find that someone had bashed in the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 돌아와 보니 누군가 문을 쳐서 부숴 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write or type something very quickly, without much thought.
meanings.example: I've got about an hour in which to bash something out for the morning edition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아침판을 위해 한 시간 안에 뭔가를 급히 써내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assault someone with the intention of causing physical injury; to beat up.
meanings.example: He shouted at us, so we bashed him up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 우리에게 소리쳤기 때문에 우리는 그를 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: face-off.
meanings.example: The two rivals had a tense face-off before the match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 라이벌은 경기 전에 긴장된 대결을 펼쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront (each other).
meanings.example: The two teams will face off in the championship game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 팀은 챔피언십 경기에서 맞붙을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break wind.
meanings.example: Whoa! Did somebody step on a duck?
meanings.exampleKorean: 워! 누가 방귀 뀌었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave abruptly.
meanings.example: The rain finally cleared off, and the sun came out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마침내 비가 그치고 해가 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy.
meanings.example: Don't honk me off with your silly jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 바보 같은 농담으로 나를 짜증나게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study particularly hard to learn (a subject) quickly.
meanings.example: You should swot up on your French before travelling to Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파리로 여행 가기 전에 프랑스어를 열심히 공부해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive.
meanings.example: I expect John will be along soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존이 곧 올 거라고 예상한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach someone in a very determined way.
meanings.example: I could see the headmaster bearing down on me, and looked hopelessly round for a means of escape.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교장 선생님이 나에게 맹렬히 다가오는 것이 보였고, 나는 절망적으로 탈출구를 찾았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become fat or fatter, especially as a result of excessive eating.
meanings.example: He really blimped out after he quit his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 그만둔 후 정말 살이 쪘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease the aperture of a photographic lens, moving from an f/stop represented by a lower number to an f/stop represented by a higher number.
meanings.example: To get a greater depth of field, you need to stop down the lens.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 깊은 피사계 심도를 얻으려면 렌즈의 조리개를 조여야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sound a rhythm on a percussion instrument such as a drum.
meanings.example: The drummer beat out a steady slow march.
meanings.exampleKorean: 드러머는 꾸준하고 느린 행진곡 리듬을 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease in intensity of anger, agitation, or excitement.
meanings.example: Just simmer down, everything will be okay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 진정해, 모든 것이 괜찮아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow diligently.
meanings.example: The reporter dogged around the celebrity for weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 기자는 몇 주 동안 그 연예인을 끈질기게 따라다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To outsmart.
meanings.example: He tried to psych out his opponent before the match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경기 전에 상대방을 심리적으로 제압하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The act of unnerving somebody.
meanings.example: His stare was a form of psych out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 노려는 일종의 심리적 압박이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss or minimize (a problem, etc.) by explanation.
meanings.example: He couldn't just explain away all of his problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 모든 문제들을 단순히 해명하여 없었던 일로 할 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a fight
meanings.example: Watch out or Brody'll lay the smack down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해, 안 그러면 브로디가 싸움을 걸 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk out of one's ass.
meanings.example: He's just talking out of his ass, don't believe him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그냥 허풍 떠는 거야, 믿지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more clichéd, or more cheesy.
meanings.example: The actor really hammed up the scene with his overacting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 과장된 연기로 장면을 정말 느끼하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform gentle exercise at the end of a training session before cooling off.
meanings.example: It's important to warm down after a strenuous workout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 격렬한 운동 후에는 정리 운동을 하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make more specific.
meanings.example: All the food on the menu looked delicious, so I tried to narrow down my choices to only healthy foods.
meanings.exampleKorean: 메뉴의 모든 음식이 맛있어 보였기 때문에 건강한 음식으로만 선택의 폭을 좁히려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: trash
meanings.example: Please take out the trash before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 쓰레기를 버려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To base on.
meanings.example: The movie is based off of a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To base on.
meanings.example: The movie is based off of a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce by paring or a similar gradual process.
meanings.example: As companies keep paring down their delivery times – from two days to a day to two hours to an hour – longer stretches of the last mile will have to run through urban territory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기업들이 배송 시간을 계속 줄여나가면서 – 이틀에서 하루로, 두 시간에서 한 시간으로 – 라스트 마일의 더 긴 구간은 도시 지역을 통과해야 할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in the game of craps, to roll a seven after having established the "point".
meanings.example: He sevened out and lost the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 7을 굴려 게임에서 졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quieter.
meanings.example: As the lights dimmed the general noise quieted down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조명이 어두워지자 전반적인 소음이 잦아들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat up physically.
meanings.example: The thugs threatened to smack up anyone who didn't pay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 깡패들은 돈을 내지 않는 사람은 누구든 때리겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To irritate.
meanings.example: But his didacticism is beginning to wear on me, and I wonder if I am on a fool's errand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 그의 교육적인 태도가 나를 괴롭히기 시작했고, 내가 헛수고를 하고 있는지 궁금하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To point one's firearm at another person, thereby gaining dominance in a situation.
meanings.example: He got the drop on them and proceeded to shoot and kill them before they could fire a shot or respond.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들에게 총을 겨누고, 그들이 총을 쏘거나 대응하기 전에 그들을 쏘아 죽였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: separate To separate by or as with a boundary line.
meanings.example: The surveyor marked off the boundaries of the property.
meanings.exampleKorean: 측량사는 재산의 경계를 표시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck off.
meanings.example: He told the annoying stranger to F off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 짜증나는 낯선 사람에게 꺼지라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck off.
meanings.example: F off, I don't want to talk to you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져, 너랑 말하고 싶지 않아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To boil to such an extent as to overflow its container.
meanings.example: I forgot about the rice and it boiled over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밥을 잊어버렸고 끓어 넘쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: These trousers are too big around the waist and won't stay up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 바지는 허리가 너무 커서 자꾸 내려간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a romantic relationship with (someone).
meanings.example: I came home and dumped on him about everything that was going on at work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 와서 직장에서 일어나는 모든 일에 대해 그에게 불평했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive around.
meanings.example: They spent the afternoon bombing around the city in their new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오후 내내 새 차를 타고 도시를 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave (a place) hastily.
meanings.example: It's time I bugged out of this town: it ain't safe no more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 마을을 떠날 시간이야: 더 이상 안전하지 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: A rapid retreat, a rout.
meanings.example: The army's bug out was chaotic and disorganized.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대의 급퇴각은 혼란스럽고 무질서했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attach oneself to.
meanings.example: He glommed onto the idea of starting his own business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업을 시작하자는 아이디어에 달라붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attach oneself to something.
meanings.example: She glommed on to his arm as they walked.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 걸을 때 그녀는 그의 팔에 달라붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered in ice, usually of a body of water.
meanings.example: The lake will ice over in the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호수는 겨울에 얼음으로 뒤덮일 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered or clogged with ice.
meanings.example: The airplane stalled and crashed after its wings iced up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비행기는 날개가 얼어붙은 후 실속하여 추락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sustain a living by consuming produce that one harvests or hunts from the countryside.
meanings.example: They decided to live off the land after retiring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 은퇴 후 자급자족하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the last details of a task.
meanings.example: They were finishing up the dishes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 설거지를 마무리하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: betray To betray, especially in a manner which causes serious difficulty for the one betrayed.
meanings.example: He felt like his business partner had sold him down the river.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업 파트너에게 배신당했다고 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flub; to botch or mess up.
meanings.example: He flubbed up the presentation and forgot his lines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 발표 중에 실수를 했고 대사를 잊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take a pew.
meanings.example: Come on, pull up a pew and join us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서 와서 자리에 앉아 우리와 함께하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply by rubbing.
meanings.example: Flattening chicken breast halves serves two purposes: It provides more surface area to rub in the Greek-influenced seasoning, and it ensures that the chicken cooks quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 닭가슴살을 납작하게 만드는 것은 두 가지 목적을 제공한다: 그리스 풍의 양념을 문질러 바를 더 넓은 표면적을 제공하고, 닭고기가 빨리 익도록 보장한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emphasize or make something unpleasant more obvious.
meanings.example: I know I made a mistake, you don't have to rub it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수했다는 건 알아요, 굳이 상기시킬 필요 없잖아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transact, trade, or negotiate badly, by paying, providing, or conceding too much to the other party.
meanings.example: He was so eager to make the sale that he gave away the store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 판매를 성사시키고 싶어서 너무 많이 양보했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal a secret or disclose information carelessly.
meanings.example: Don't give away the store before the product is launched.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제품 출시 전에 비밀을 누설하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: give away the store.
meanings.example: She accidentally gave the store away when she mentioned the surprise party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 깜짝 파티를 언급하다가 실수로 비밀을 누설했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To claim as deserving support for patriotic reasons.
meanings.example: Both parties wrap themselves in the flag every election.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 정당 모두 선거 때마다 애국심에 호소한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use patriotism to justify or defend something.
meanings.example: The politician tried to wrap the controversial policy in the flag.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 논란이 많은 정책을 애국심으로 정당화하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hand over, pass through or transfer to a lower level, next generation, etc.
meanings.example: In poor families, solid clothes are passed down from elder children to kid siblings, who must wear them out or if they outgrow them pass them down to still younger relatives
meanings.exampleKorean: 가난한 가정에서는 튼튼한 옷이 큰 아이들로부터 어린 형제들에게 물려지고, 아이들은 그것을 다 낡을 때까지 입거나 더 어린 친척들에게 물려준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transmit (a gene or characteristic) genetically.
meanings.example: This trait is passed down through generations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 특성은 세대를 거쳐 유전된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy someone; to get on someone's nerves.
meanings.example: His constant whistling really gets up my nose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 끊임없는 휘파람 소리는 정말 내 신경을 거슬리게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit an error, make a mistake.
meanings.example: Be careful not to trip up on the details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세부 사항에서 실수하지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose, lock up inside something.
meanings.example: The police had not yet caught the thieves, but were closing in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 아직 도둑을 잡지 못했지만, 포위망을 좁혀가고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ejaculate while wearing one's trousers; to experience an orgasm while clothed.
meanings.example: He was so excited he almost creamed his jeans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 흥분해서 거의 옷 입은 채로 사정할 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To list or recite quickly.
meanings.example: She rhymed off all the names on the list.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 목록에 있는 모든 이름을 줄줄 외웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restrain; to check.
meanings.example: He struggled to hold down his anger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 분노를 억누르기 위해 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time drinking alcohol at the bar in a pub.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 술집 바에서 술 마시며 시간을 보내다
meanings.example: He spent most evenings propping up the bar at the local pub.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대부분의 저녁을 동네 술집 바에서 술을 마시며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attempt to contact, reach out.
meanings.example: Please get in touch if you have any questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문이 있으시면 연락해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cite an instance; to adduce an example.
meanings.example: Of all the opinions, this one instanced in by you is in your judgment the truest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 의견 중에서 당신이 예로 든 이 의견이 당신의 판단에 가장 진실됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To promulgate law.
meanings.example: Six or eight thousand years ago / They laid down the law / In Mesopotamia
meanings.exampleKorean: 6천 또는 8천 년 전 메소포타미아에서 그들은 법을 제정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail, especially in a dramatic or particularly decisive manner.
meanings.example: The project fell on its face due to lack of funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트는 자금 부족으로 인해 처참하게 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell assets for the purpose of disposing of them quickly rather than for maximizing profit.
meanings.example: The company decided to sell off its subsidiary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 자회사를 헐값에 팔기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (something such as dirt) by wiping.
meanings.example: Please wipe off the table after you finish eating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식사 후에는 테이블을 닦아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart, especially for a sea voyage or military assignment.
meanings.example: He shipped out to Korea for his military service.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 군 복무를 위해 한국으로 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn upside-down; to invert.
meanings.example: The crisis turned the formulas that had seemed to work on their head.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 위기는 효과가 있는 것처럼 보였던 공식들을 완전히 뒤바꿔 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience the suffering or discipline necessary to bring one to a certain degree of knowledge or skill, or to a certain mental state.
meanings.example: She really went through the mill before she became successful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 성공하기 전에 정말 많은 고생을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dip one's toe in.
meanings.example: I'm just dipping my toe into the world of online dating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 온라인 데이트의 세계를 살짝 시도해보고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a clownish, irresponsible or inefficient manner.
meanings.example: Quit arsing around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To call to task or to reprimand severely.
meanings.example: She raked him over the coals for lying to her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그가 그녀에게 거짓말한 것에 대해 심하게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time or fool around; to engage in activity which produces little or no accomplishment.
meanings.example: Stop fannying about and do something useful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 유용한 일을 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: fanny about.
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon fannying around the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 집에서 빈둥거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To burn brightly again.
meanings.example: The fire flared up after we added more wood to it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 더 많은 나무를 넣자 불이 다시 타올랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: flare-up.
meanings.example: He had a flare-up of his old illness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 예전 질병이 갑자기 악화되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To configure or set up (particularly a complex machine with many configuration options [especially knobs and dials]).
meanings.example: You need to dial in the correct settings for the printer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프린터에 올바른 설정을 조정해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know something, even if only a little, about.
meanings.example: He doesn't know beans about computers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 컴퓨터에 대해 아는 것이 전혀 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overly active relative to the enclosed space in which one is.
meanings.example: The kids were bouncing off the walls after eating all that candy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 사탕을 잔뜩 먹고 나서 매우 활동적이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: He looked down from the top of the building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 건물 꼭대기에서 아래를 내려다보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regard or treat as inferior.
meanings.example: She tends to look down on people she thinks are less intelligent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신보다 덜 똑똑하다고 생각하는 사람들을 얕보는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: We need to scale up production to meet demand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수요를 충족시키기 위해 생산을 확대해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the size of something whilst maintaining proportion.
meanings.example: The company had to scale down its operations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 운영 규모를 축소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: top it off
meanings.example: The beautiful sunset was the perfect way to top it all off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아름다운 일몰은 모든 것을 마무리하는 완벽한 방법이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To administer an oath to, as an oath of office or of citizenship.
meanings.example: The Chief Justice swore him in as President on Inauguration Day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대법원장은 취임식 날 그를 대통령으로 취임시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To construct in a shoddy, makeshift manner.
meanings.example: They had to lash up a shelter from branches and leaves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 나뭇가지와 나뭇잎으로 임시 거처를 만들어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resort to the help (of); to call on the support (of), especially to help prove an argument.
meanings.example: He had to pray in aid of his colleagues to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 동료들의 도움을 구해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To modify the engine of (a motor vehicle) to give a higher performance than the specifications.
meanings.example: Imagine souping up a Mini with a Cosworth engine: the car would fly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코스워스 엔진으로 미니의 성능을 높이는 것을 상상해보라: 그 차는 날아갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a romantic or sexual advance to.
meanings.example: He kept coming on to her at the party, but she wasn't interested.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티에서 계속 그녀에게 추근댔지만, 그녀는 관심이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To absorb (liquid).
meanings.example: The dry land drank in the rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 메마른 땅이 비를 빨아들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: through
meanings.example: He looked through the binoculars at the bird.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 쌍안경으로 새를 살펴보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become more relaxed or less demanding.
meanings.example: I suggest that you ease up a bit at work. You're getting stressed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장에서 좀 쉬라고 제안합니다. 스트레스를 너무 많이 받고 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: A write-off.
meanings.example: The company declared a charge-off for the bad debt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 부실 채권에 대한 손실 처리를 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write off (an expense).
meanings.example: The accountant charged off the uncollectible amount.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회계사는 회수 불가능한 금액을 손실 처리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To request benefits, especially monetary ones and especially if not entitled to them.
meanings.example: Whenever there was an appropriations bill, he always had his hand out for his guys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예산안이 나올 때마다 그는 항상 자기 사람들을 위해 돈을 요구했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to criticise someone
meanings.example: I don't mean to bag you out, but that top is really not flattering on you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신을 비난하려는 건 아니지만, 그 상의는 당신에게 정말 안 어울려요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow up, so as to ensure completion.
meanings.example: Could you chase up last month's invoices?
meanings.exampleKorean: 지난달 송장들을 확인해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: Chiefly of a heterosexual woman, to engage in lesbian activity.
meanings.example: She decided to lez out with her friend one night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어느 날 밤 친구와 레즈비언 행위를 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spray thoroughly with a hose.
meanings.example: He had to hose down the dirty patio.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더러운 파티오에 물을 뿌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To completely empty.
meanings.example: We need to clear out the garage before winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울이 오기 전에 차고를 완전히 치워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To misbehave, fool around.
meanings.example: Stop jacking around and get back to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난치지 말고 일로 돌아가!

meanings.englishDefinition: to become frosty
meanings.example: The windows often frost up in winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울에는 창문에 종종 성에가 낀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To purchase (something already sold, misplaced, destroyed or given away).
meanings.example: Investors have already started buying back shares they had sold last year in an effort to increase their profits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투자자들은 이익을 늘리기 위해 작년에 팔았던 주식을 되사기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cross out with a series of the letter X, or with scribbles or lines.
meanings.example: On a close to final copy the title The Way We Die Now has Now Xed out and replaced with Today. Then Today is Xed out and Now is returned to its place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거의 완성된 복사본에서 'The Way We Die Now'라는 제목의 'Now'는 X 표시로 지워지고 'Today'로 대체되었다. 그런 다음 'Today'는 X 표시로 지워지고 'Now'가 원래 위치로 돌아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a lot of money.
meanings.example: The new restaurant is really raking it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새 식당은 정말 돈을 많이 벌고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To step sideways to make a space for someone else.
meanings.example: Stand aside, please, so the doctor can get through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사가 지나갈 수 있도록 옆으로 비켜주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To last too long.
meanings.example: Debate on the Kellogg Mar renunciation treaty dragged on in the senate today with no immediate prospect of final action.
meanings.exampleKorean: 켈로그 마르 조약 비준 조약에 대한 토론은 최종 조치의 즉각적인 전망 없이 오늘 상원에서 질질 끌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on financially.
meanings.example: He lived off his parents until he was 30.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 30세까지 부모에게 의존해 살았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unusually enthusiastic.
meanings.example: The fans were falling over themselves to get autographs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팬들은 사인 받기 위해 매우 열심이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: knock someone's socks off.
meanings.example: The performance was so good it blew everyone's socks off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 공연은 너무 훌륭해서 모두를 깜짝 놀라게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean or wash by scrubbing.
meanings.example: She had to scrub up the kitchen floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 부엌 바닥을 문질러 닦아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: look down on.
meanings.example: The mechanical toys were very superior, and looked down upon every one else; they were full of modern ideas, and pretended they were real.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기계 장난감들은 매우 우수했고 다른 모든 것을 경멸했다. 그들은 현대적인 아이디어로 가득 차 있었고 진짜인 척했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart, leave; to make off.
meanings.example: The thieves made away with the stolen jewels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도둑들은 훔친 보석을 가지고 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go from a standing position to a sitting one.
meanings.example: Come in and take a load off your feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어와서 발 좀 쉬세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up, to quit.
meanings.example: He decided to chuck in his job and travel the world.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장을 그만두고 세계를 여행하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up, to quit.
meanings.example: I rode 700 miles one day before packing it in for the night, yet after 15 minutes of rest in my hotel room, I realized that I could have gone farther.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤을 위해 그만두기 전에 하루에 700마일을 탔지만, 호텔 방에서 15분 쉬고 나니 더 갈 수도 있었다는 것을 깨달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become friends.
meanings.example: happened to pal up with a Free French bloke who'd been in the orthopaedic wards, and when this fellow went home with a couple of bone grafts Rushleigh got an invitation to stay at his place down at Nice, buckshee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정형외과 병동에 있던 자유 프랑스인과 우연히 친구가 되었는데, 이 친구가 뼈 이식 몇 개를 하고 집에 갔을 때 러쉬리는 니스에 있는 그의 집에서 공짜로 머물라는 초대를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refuse not accept; forgo.
meanings.example: He passed up my invitation for dinner, saying he was too busy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 바쁘다고 말하며 저녁 식사 초대를 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair by adding a patch.
meanings.example: I needed to patch up my trousers after ripping them on the brambles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 덤불에 찢어진 바지를 꿰맬 필요가 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark (a territory or area) with pegs. from 19th c.
meanings.example: I was soon the proud possessor of a miner's right, and my claim was pegged out for me away on the reefs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 곧 광산업자의 권리를 자랑스럽게 소유하게 되었고, 내 광맥은 암초 위에 표시되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten something to the ground using pegs.
meanings.example: The tent needs to be pegged down securely to withstand the strong winds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강풍을 견디기 위해 텐트를 단단히 말뚝으로 고정해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill in or write in using pencil.
meanings.example: I'll pencil you in for tomorrow at noon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 정오로 일단 잡아 놓을게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by picking.
meanings.example: Please pick out the bad apples from the basket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바구니에서 상한 사과들을 골라내 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To joke or play.
meanings.example: Stop pissing about and get back to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 일하러 돌아가!

meanings.englishDefinition: piss about.
meanings.example: Don't just piss around all day; find something productive to do.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루 종일 빈둥거리지 말고 생산적인 일을 찾아봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue with a menial, tedious or time-consuming task.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 계속 밀고 나가다, 끈기 있게 하다
meanings.example: If you want to get good grades in the exam, you have to plow on with the book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험에서 좋은 성적을 받으려면 책을 계속해서 공부해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a person: to gain weight quickly.
meanings.example: line is single track as far as Halesworth, but many of the stations retain their original buildings - including refurbished Beccles, which boasts a station cafe and community rooms. The same is true of Wickham Market and Woodbridge stations. You could easily pile on the pounds with a 'cafe crawl' along this line!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 노선은 헬스워스까지 단선이지만, 많은 역들이 개조된 베클스를 포함하여 원래의 건물을 유지하고 있으며, 역 카페와 커뮤니티 룸을 자랑합니다. 위컴 마켓과 우드브리지 역도 마찬가지입니다. 이 노선을 따라 '카페 순례'를 하면 쉽게 살이 찔 수 있습니다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To polish something to make it shiny.
meanings.example: I need to polish up my shoes before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 면접 전에 구두를 닦아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To phone; to contact by telephone.
meanings.example: ‘I don’t believe it!’ cried Maxine, who had only phoned up in order to relieve her own boredom and have a good moan about Serena.
meanings.exampleKorean: 맥신은 지루함을 달래고 세레나에 대해 불평하려고만 전화했었는데, '말도 안 돼!'라고 외쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (the first object) to become (the second object); to change or transform.
meanings.example: Basic training will make you into a man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기초 훈련은 당신을 남자로 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persist, to continue to advance.
meanings.example: Railways are, however, pressing on with the introduction of standard equipment, and in this, the fourth year of unified working, standard locomotives and rolling stock will be coming into service in considerable numbers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러나 철도는 표준 장비 도입을 계속 추진하고 있으며, 통합 운영 4년차인 올해에는 표준 기관차와 차량이 상당수 서비스에 투입될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make pretty.
meanings.example: She spent hours trying to pretty up the room for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 방을 예쁘게 꾸미느라 몇 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: printout.
meanings.example: Please give me a print out of the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보고서 인쇄물을 주세요.
