meanings.englishDefinition: to print (a computer document)
meanings.example: I need to print out this document.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문서를 인쇄해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vanish or fall into obscurity; to become forgotten or ignored.
meanings.example: After the scandal, the politician dropped off the radar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들 이후, 그 정치인은 잊혀졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something, so that it comes inside.
meanings.example: After falling out of the boat, the crew pulled him in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보트에서 떨어진 후, 승무원들이 그를 안으로 끌어당겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To jump the queue, especially in a forceful manner.
meanings.example: It's rude to push in line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 줄에서 새치기하는 것은 무례하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A zoom in which the camera physically moves towards the subject
meanings.example: The director decided to use a push in shot to emphasize the character's emotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 등장인물의 감정을 강조하기 위해 줌인 샷을 사용하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move into a winning position (e.g. in a race or competition).
meanings.example: After being neck-and-neck the whole race, Gibbs managed to pull ahead in the final lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경주 내내 엎치락뒤치락했지만, 깁스는 마지막 랩에서 앞서 나갈 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrange in a rack.
meanings.example: At the start of the game, the referee racks up the red balls.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게임 시작 시, 심판이 빨간 공들을 선반에 배열한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring (a fish etc.) out of the water by winding the line on which it is caught onto a reel (e.g. of a fishing rod).
meanings.example: He carefully reeled in the fish, trying not to break the line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낚싯줄이 끊어지지 않도록 조심스럽게 물고기를 감아 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recite or perform quickly and easily.
meanings.example: She could reel off the names of all the presidents in order.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 모든 대통령의 이름을 순서대로 술술 외울 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy by ripping.
meanings.example: The frustrated writer ripped up her manuscript.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좌절한 작가는 자신의 원고를 찢어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: }} To regard as inferior or distasteful; to hold in contempt.
meanings.example: He looks down his nose at anyone who doesn't have a college degree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대학 학위가 없는 사람은 누구든 깔본다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue with a task despite it being menial, difficult, or boring
meanings.koreanDefinition: 계속 밀고 나가다, 끈기 있게 하다
meanings.example: We could stop for a coffee, or plough on to the hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피를 마시러 멈출 수도 있고, 호텔까지 계속 갈 수도 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plow back.
meanings.example: The company decided to plough back its profits into research and development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 이익을 연구 개발에 재투자하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reinvest profits into a business.
meanings.example: The dividends were small last year because we plowed back most of the money to buy new machinery
meanings.exampleKorean: 작년 배당금은 적었는데, 왜냐하면 우리는 새 기계를 사기 위해 수익의 대부분을 재투자했기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crash into something.
meanings.example: The lorry ploughed into the line of stationary cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 트럭은 정차해 있던 차들에 돌진했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crash into something.
meanings.example: The lorry plowed into the line of stationary cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 트럭은 정차해 있던 차들에 돌진했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persevere with an activity of consuming something, both literally and figuratively.
meanings.example: I ploughed through two helpings, but then I didn't have room for any more.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 두 그릇을 열심히 먹었지만, 더 이상 먹을 공간이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plough through.
meanings.example: He had to plow through a mountain of paperwork.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 산더미 같은 서류 작업을 해내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To train the muscles for increased size and strength.
meanings.example: We spent the summer trying to muscle up for football next term.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 다음 학기 미식축구를 위해 근육을 키우려고 여름을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work, or cause to work, excessively or to the point of exhaustion; see work + someone's ass off.
meanings.example: He worked his ass off to get that promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진하기 위해 엄청나게 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to quickly inspect something (newspaper, notes) with the eyes
meanings.example: are other people coming besides the Princess Ziska, are there not, Mr. Murray?" inquired Sir Chetwynd Lyle, with an obtrusively bantering air. Denzil Murray glanced him over disdainfully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 머레이 씨, 지스카 공주 외에 다른 사람들도 오는 건가요? 하고 체트윈드 라일 경이 눈에 띄게 장난스러운 태도로 물었다. 덴질 머레이는 경멸적으로 그를 훑어보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A dribbling move, or feint, in football (soccer), used to fool a defensive player into thinking the offensive player, in possession of the ball, in which the foot is moved over the ball without making contact with it.
meanings.example: The player used a step over to get past the defender.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 수비수를 제치기 위해 스텝 오버를 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relocate oneself to a position of a few steps away; step aside.
meanings.example: Please step over to the side so I can get through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지나갈 수 있도록 옆으로 비켜 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move out of the way of somebody or something.
meanings.example: I stepped aside to let her pass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀가 지나가도록 옆으로 비켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Indicates a time or age in the very near future.
meanings.example: It's going on three o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거의 세 시가 되어간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ing-form
meanings.example: What is going on here?
meanings.exampleKorean: 여기서 무슨 일이 일어나고 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To have fun in an irresponsible manner.
meanings.example: Stop clowning around and get back to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 일로 돌아가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit to something with; to partner with.
meanings.example: They decided to throw in with the other company to compete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 경쟁하기 위해 다른 회사와 제휴하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit to something with; to partner with.
meanings.example: He's going in with his brother to start a new business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 형과 함께 새 사업을 시작할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To administer quinine.
meanings.example: The physician dined that day at Mr Allworthy's; and having after dinner visited his patient, he returned to the company, and told them, that he had now the satisfaction to say, with assurance, that his patient was out of all danger: that he had brought his fever to a perfect intermission, and doubted not by throwing in the bark to prevent its return.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 그날 올워디 씨 댁에서 저녁을 먹었고, 저녁 식사 후 환자를 방문한 뒤 일행에게 돌아와 환자가 더 이상 위험하지 않다고 확신 있게 말할 수 있어 만족스럽다고 말했다. 열을 완전히 멈추게 했고, 퀴닌을 투여하면 열이 다시 돌아오는 것을 막을 수 있을 것이라고 의심하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: work someone's ass off.
meanings.example: Her son, Dominic, had been ranked third at the end of his junior year, but had “worked his butt off this year,” she said.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아들 도미니크가 주니어 해 마지막에 3위였지만, 올해는 “엄청나게 열심히 일했다”고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Less vulgar synonym of work someone's ass off.
meanings.example: She worked her tail off to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 엄청나게 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive by chance; to occur by happenstance.
meanings.example: "Now, if a rattling good earthquake were to happen along, you might awake in the morning to find yourself on an island, or even under water."
meanings.exampleKorean: “만약 엄청난 지진이 우연히 발생한다면, 아침에 일어나 보니 섬에 있거나 물속에 있을 수도 있다.”

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose by drawing a box around.
meanings.example: The defense successfully boxed in the opposing team's strikers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수비진이 상대 팀 공격수들을 성공적으로 가두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To revolve.
meanings.example: tourned him about, and behelde her saynge: Doughter be off goode comforte, thy fayth hath made the safe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 돌려 세우고 그녀가 말하는 것을 보았다: 딸아, 안심하거라, 네 믿음이 너를 안전하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn and turn about.
meanings.example: They took turns reading the story, turn about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 차례차례 이야기를 읽었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turnabout.
meanings.example: There was a sudden turn about in public opinion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여론에 갑작스러운 전환이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be attractive or socially successful or have an aura of success.
meanings.example: She really has it going on tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오늘 밤 정말 매력적이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience good luck; to be fortunate or lucky.
meanings.example: I hope I luck in and win the draw.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추첨에 운 좋게 당첨되기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: waste time To waste time on an unproductive activity.
meanings.example: Stop dicking around and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart quietly, without being noticed.
meanings.example: He decided to slope off before the meeting ended.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의가 끝나기 전에 몰래 떠나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come through pain and trouble through perseverance.
meanings.example: There was once a poor, very poor widow, who had an only son. She pulled through with the boy till he was confirmed; but then she told him that she could not feed him any longer; he would have to go out and earn his own bread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛날에 외아들을 둔 아주 가난한 과부가 있었다. 그녀는 아들이 견진성사를 받을 때까지 그와 함께 힘겹게 살아갔지만, 더 이상 그를 먹여 살릴 수 없다고 말하며 스스로 빵을 벌어야 한다고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull-through.
meanings.example: The patient made a remarkable pull-through after the surgery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환자는 수술 후 놀라운 회복을 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh uproariously.
meanings.example: The comedian's jokes had the audience rolling in the aisles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언의 농담에 관객들은 배꼽을 잡고 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: roll in the aisles.
meanings.example: The audience was rolling in the aisle at his performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들은 그의 공연에 폭소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of (something or someone unwanted) by transferring it to another.
meanings.example: He tried to pawn off his old car on his brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 형에게 낡은 차를 떠넘기려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain in a particular place or status; to survive, maintain, persist, persevere; to resist.
meanings.example: Hang in there; things will definitely get better soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조금만 더 버텨봐. 곧 상황이 분명히 나아질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To short circuit.
meanings.example: The faulty wiring caused the lights to short out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잘못된 배선 때문에 불이 합선되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a computer file to disk.
meanings.example: Don't forget to save down your work before you close the program.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로그램을 닫기 전에 작업 내용을 저장하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to unite, to come together
meanings.example: The villagers banded together to protect their homes from the invaders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마을 사람들은 침략자들로부터 집을 지키기 위해 단결했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to strike or hit repeatedly
meanings.example: bang away on the drums
meanings.exampleKorean: 드럼을 계속 두드리다

meanings.englishDefinition: apart
meanings.example: She tore her ex-boyfriend’s clothes apart in fury.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 분노하여 전 남자친구의 옷을 갈기갈기 찢었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate or divide something that was whole.
meanings.example: The wind chopped up the lake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 호수를 거칠게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: apart
meanings.example: The quarterback picked apart the secondary defense in the first half.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쿼터백은 전반전에 상대 수비진을 완전히 무너뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to expel, using bats, paddles or similar things.
meanings.example: The angry crowd batted out the intruders from the town hall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화난 군중이 시청에서 침입자들을 방망이로 쫓아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sing (a song) loudly; to emit (music) loudly.
meanings.example: Lively show tunes being belted out by the pianist on the grand piano.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피아니스트가 그랜드 피아노로 신나는 쇼 음악을 목청껏 연주하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend one's upper body forward and down while standing or kneeling.
meanings.example: He had to bend over to pick up the coins from the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바닥에서 동전을 줍기 위해 몸을 굽혀야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go (to a place or event).
meanings.example: Let's boogie on down to the club tonight!
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 클럽에 가서 신나게 놀자!

meanings.englishDefinition: to write down hurriedly; to make a note of
meanings.example: Tell me his number, and I'll jot it down on this scrap of paper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 전화번호를 말해주면, 이 종잇조각에 적어둘게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: over
meanings.example: He walked all over the Lakes region that Summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 여름에 레이크스 지역을 마음대로 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to remove liquid using a pump
meanings.example: They had to pump out the flooded basement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 침수된 지하실의 물을 퍼내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide, particularly into separate shares for distribution.
meanings.example: divide up the cake into 5 equal parts
meanings.exampleKorean: 케이크를 5등분하다

meanings.englishDefinition: to search, to look for
meanings.example: We need to scout out a good location for our picnic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 피크닉 장소로 좋은 곳을 찾아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow or help to settle in; to make (someone) feel at home.
meanings.example: It took some time to be bedded in, but soon he knew his way around his new workplace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자리를 잡는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸지만, 곧 그는 새 직장에서 길을 알게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape (from); break out.
meanings.example: to bust out of prison
meanings.exampleKorean: 감옥에서 탈출하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To chew so as to make something pulpy or tangled.
meanings.example: She had to chew up the food for him because his teeth are too small.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이가 너무 작아서 그녀가 음식을 씹어서 으깨줘야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause.
meanings.example: Excessive drinking can bring on depression.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과도한 음주는 우울증을 유발할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss-up.
meanings.example: They decided to have a piss-up to celebrate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 축하하기 위해 술잔치를 열기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to screw up, make a mess of
meanings.example: He really pissed up the whole project with his careless mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부주의한 실수로 전체 프로젝트를 완전히 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to begin ascent under rocket power
meanings.example: The rocket is scheduled to blast off at dawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로켓은 새벽에 발사될 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become blank.
meanings.example: He removed the paper from the heat, and the paper blanked out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 종이를 열에서 떼어냈고, 종이는 하얗게 변했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience bliss; to be blissful.
meanings.example: She was so blissed out that she could barely stand, but a moment of true happiness will do that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 너무나 행복해서 거의 서 있을 수도 없었지만, 진정한 행복의 순간은 그런 법이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread out into the shape of a fan.
meanings.example: of the summit the cutting fans out at the entrance to the new yard on the north side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정상에서 절벽은 북쪽 새 마당 입구에서 부채꼴로 펼쳐진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: soup up.
meanings.example: He spent all his savings to supe up his old car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오래된 차의 성능을 높이기 위해 모든 저축을 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with.
meanings.example: For Northton's young males, to " get over " on a woman – that is, to lure her into sex with vague promises of love and marriage; to get over on a woman and, particularly, to impregnate one (or three or five), and to " get away without being held legally accountable for out-of-wedlock children " is to prove strength, virility, and status. To a number of underclass youths, love is only another hustle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 노스턴의 젊은 남성들에게 여성을 "넘어뜨리는 것" 즉, 사랑과 결혼에 대한 모호한 약속으로 그녀를 유혹하여 성관계를 갖는 것; 특히 여성을 임신시키고 (세 명 또는 다섯 명), "혼외 자녀에 대해 법적 책임을 지지 않고 벗어나는 것"은 힘, 정력, 지위를 증명하는 것이다. 많은 하층 계급 청소년들에게 사랑은 그저 또 다른 속임수일 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find and put together in the same place.
meanings.example: Please collect up all the toys and put them in the box.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 장난감을 모아서 상자에 넣어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be too frightening or upsetting to even contemplate.
meanings.example: If the train had come just five seconds earlier... well, it just doesn't bear thinking about!
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 단 5초만 더 일찍 왔다면... 글쎄, 생각하기도 싫은 일이야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer a young plant from an inside location such as a greenhouse to an outdoors flower bed.
meanings.example: The gardener will bed out the seedlings in the spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정원사는 봄에 모종을 밭에 옮겨 심을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider the future, to anticipate future events.
meanings.example: You should forget the past and look ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과거는 잊고 미래를 내다봐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate or set apart from others; split out; segregate.
meanings.example: The judge ordered the jury to sunder out fact from fiction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 배심원단에게 허구에서 사실을 가려내라고 명령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start doing something quickly; to stop procrastinating; to hurry up.
meanings.example: If I don't get my skates on now I'm going to be late!
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 서두르지 않으면 늦겠어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To frustrate, infuriate, irritate or disturb someone.
meanings.example: Please stop reading the name of every sign we come across; it's doing my head in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 지나가는 모든 간판 이름을 읽는 것을 멈춰줘. 정말 짜증나!

meanings.englishDefinition: To relieve oneself psychologically or emotionally by speaking to someone.
meanings.example: I finally told him what was bothering me, it really got it off my chest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마침내 그에게 나를 괴롭히는 것을 말했고, 마음속 응어리를 풀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make dry.
meanings.example: After the shower, I need to dry off with a towel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샤워 후에는 수건으로 물기를 닦아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress up; to dress in smart or special clothes.
meanings.example: No real hand would ever dude himself up in Wild West clothes, like chaps with silver conchas and fifty-dollar boots so skintight the man's feet were starting to curl like a ram's horns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정한 남자는 서부 시대 옷차림처럼 자신을 멋지게 차려입지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat up, to give a beating to.
meanings.example: I heard Nick got duffed up behind the shopping centre at the weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 닉이 주말에 쇼핑센터 뒤에서 심하게 맞았다는 소식을 들었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover with dust.
meanings.example: The old books were left on the shelf to dust up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 책들은 먼지가 쌓이도록 선반에 놓여 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To link or join together.
meanings.example: Let's link up after the meeting to discuss the details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 후에 만나서 세부 사항을 논의합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve or stimulate a penile erection.
meanings.example: I love my boyfriend, but am left unsatisfied because he can't get it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남자친구를 사랑하지만, 그가 발기하지 못해서 만족스럽지 못하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become more even.
meanings.example: The company is trying to even out the workload among the employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 직원들 간의 업무량을 균등하게 분배하려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depend on; to be contingent upon.
meanings.example: The success of this plan hinges upon his approval.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 계획의 성공은 그의 승인에 달려 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stretch one's muscles to make them more limber, usually as a preparation for physical exercise.
meanings.example: The swimmer limbered up for a few minutes before the final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 수영 선수는 결승전 전에 몇 분간 몸을 풀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make softer.
meanings.example: That was what Agnes thought at two o'clock in the afternoon, but by evening she had softened up some.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 애그니스가 오후 두 시에 생각했던 것이지만, 저녁이 되자 그녀는 다소 부드러워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move along very slowly.
meanings.example: The horse-drawn carriage just poked along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마차가 느릿느릿 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something in a mechanical, unreflective manner, especially as a matter of routine and without commitment or enthusiasm; to perform a task perfunctorily.
meanings.example: Aleck was a Christian from the cradle, and duty and the force of habit required her to go through the motions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 알렉은 태어날 때부터 기독교인이었고, 의무와 습관의 힘으로 그녀는 형식만 갖추어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do someone a favour.
meanings.example: If you scratch my back, I'll scratch yours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 내 등을 긁어주면, 나도 네 등을 긁어줄게. (상호 호혜)

meanings.englishDefinition: to censor (an image) by blurring.
meanings.example: The network had to fuzz out the sensitive parts of the video.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방송사는 영상의 민감한 부분을 흐리게 처리해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill with smoke.
meanings.example: The room began to smoke up from the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 방이 불 때문에 연기로 가득 차기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To completely block or obstruct.
meanings.example: The drain is blocked up with leaves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배수구가 나뭇잎으로 막혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to prevent from entering or penetrating.
meanings.example: The sunglasses block out the harsh sunlight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선글라스는 강한 햇빛을 차단해 준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to remove by boiling
meanings.example: Cooking it will boil off the vodka.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리하면 보드카가 끓어서 날아갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive out (something or somebody) using smoke.
meanings.example: We smoked the critters out of their hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 연기를 피워 그 구멍에서 동물들을 몰아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill up with an unpleasant odour.
meanings.example: Don't smoke in here, you'll smell up the whole house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여기서 담배 피우지 마, 집 전체에 악취가 밸 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy, or be destroyed by smashing.
meanings.example: The vandals smashed up the car overnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 반달리스트들이 밤새 차를 부쉈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cause to become mushy; to crush into a paste
meanings.example: My baby's food needs to be mushed up before she can eat it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기 음식을 먹이기 전에 으깨야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cause to become rumpled
meanings.example: He rumpled up the paper after reading it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 읽은 후 종이를 구겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rub from top to bottom, e.g. for cleaning, stripping paint or massage.
meanings.example: were taken to a light airy stable, and placed in boxes adjoining each other, where we were rubbed down and fed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가볍고 통풍이 잘 되는 마구간으로 옮겨져 서로 이웃한 칸에 배치되었고, 그곳에서 우리는 문질러 닦이고 먹이를 공급받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to make something rough or rougher
meanings.example: The sandpaper will roughen up the surface.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사포가 표면을 거칠게 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create the rough shape of (something, such as a machined part or a sculpture); to do the first-fix setup of (something, such as plumbing or wiring).
meanings.example: The architect roughed out a plan for the new building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건축가는 새 건물에 대한 계획을 개략적으로 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tie up using a rope.
meanings.example: They had to rope up the horses before leaving them in the stable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 말을 마구간에 두기 전에 밧줄로 묶어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to ripen; become ripe
meanings.example: The fruit will ripen up in a few days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과일은 며칠 안에 익을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to provide with equipment or gear
meanings.example: They were all rigged out in their finest clothes for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 모두 파티를 위해 가장 좋은 옷을 차려입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress someone in particular clothes.
meanings.example: She rigged him out in a clown costume.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그에게 광대 의상을 입혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a thing, a group, a person or oneself with requisites; to kit out.
meanings.example: They spent the afternoon fitting out the new office with furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오후 내내 새 사무실에 가구를 갖추는 데 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (a horse) to fall when riding.
meanings.example: fully restrained passenger rode down the impact forces with a much lower level of injuries than could be expected in a collision of this severity. His major injury was an AIS 2 contusion which resulted from the restraint shoulder strap. Had he been unrestrained, the expected injury from violent impacts with the A pillar and other frontal interior components could have been fatal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 완전히 제약을 받은 승객은 이 정도 심각도의 충돌에서 예상할 수 있는 것보다 훨씬 낮은 수준의 부상으로 충격력을 완화시켰다. 그의 주요 부상은 안전벨트에서 발생한 AIS 2 등급의 타박상이었다. 만약 그가 제약을 받지 않았다면, A 필러 및 기타 전면 내부 부품과의 격렬한 충돌로 인한 예상 부상은 치명적이었을 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clear out, to empty.
meanings.example: We need to rid out the old furniture from the attic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다락방에서 오래된 가구를 치워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To empty, to clear out.
meanings.example: Please rid up your desk before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 떠나기 전에 책상을 정리해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to lease, let; to allow possession of (a property etc.) in exchange for rent
meanings.example: They decided to rent out their spare room to students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 남는 방을 학생들에게 임대하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to prepare for use, to get ready
meanings.example: Let's ready up the equipment for the camping trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캠핑 여행을 위해 장비를 준비하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring up or uncover (something), as embarrassing information, past misdeeds, etc.
meanings.example: As for those that will (by faults which charity hath raked up, or common honesty concealed) make themselves a name with the multitude, or, to draw their rude and beastly claps, care not whose living faces they intrench with their petulant styles, may they do it without a rival, for me! I choose rather to live graved in obscurity, than share with them in so preposterous a fame. Nor can I blame the wishes of those severe and wise patriots, who providing the hurts these licentious spirits may do in a state, desire rather to see fools and devils, and those antique relics of barbarism retrieved, with all other ridiculous and exploded follies, than behold the wounds of private men, of princes and nations:
meanings.exampleKorean: 자선이 덮어주거나 정직함이 숨겨온 잘못으로 인해 대중에게 이름을 알리려는 자들, 혹은 무례하고 야만적인 박수를 받기 위해 거친 문체로 누구의 살아있는 얼굴이든 침범하는 것을 개의치 않는 자들은 나에게 있어서 경쟁자 없이 그렇게 하도록 내버려 두라! 나는 그런 터무니없는 명성을 그들과 나누느니 차라리 무명 속에 묻혀 살기를 택하겠다. 또한 국가에 이러한 방탕한 정신들이 끼칠 해악을 예방하며, 사적인 사람들의 상처, 왕과 국가들의 상처를 보는 것보다 어리석은 자들과 악마들, 그리고 야만성의 고대 유물들과 다른 모든 우스꽝스럽고 폐기된 어리석음들을 회수하는 것을 더 보고 싶어하는 엄격하고 현명한 애국자들의 소망을 탓할 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cancel (an event) due to excessive rain
meanings.example: The cricket match was rained off after just a few overs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리켓 경기는 시작한 지 얼마 안 되어 비 때문에 중단되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall from the sky as, or like, rain.
meanings.example: I'm soaking wet because it rained down on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 쏟아져서 흠뻑 젖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to ante up
meanings.example: Everyone had to ante in before the game started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두 게임 시작 전에 돈을 걸어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to supply, or be supplied with, arms (weapons)
meanings.example: The country decided to arm up its forces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 나라는 군대에 무기를 공급하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill (a vehicle, vessel, room, etc.) with a load of cargo or similar.
meanings.example: We loaded up the wagon with supplies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 마차에 보급품을 가득 실었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate an average or rough figure.
meanings.example: Let's average up the scores from the last three games.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지난 세 경기의 점수를 평균 내보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat as a baby.
meanings.example: I've been babied up for more than a decade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 10년 넘게 아기처럼 대우받아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare by baking.
meanings.example: She will bake up some cookies for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티를 위해 쿠키를 구울 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pack or collect into a bale or bundle.
meanings.example: The farmer began to bale up the hay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 건초를 곤포로 만들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to make, or become, coiled
meanings.example: The snake coiled up in the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 뱀이 풀밭에 몸을 감았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit (e.g. a window or door) with bars; to otherwise block or dam up.
meanings.example: They had to bar up the windows for security.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 보안을 위해 창문을 막대기로 막아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To barricade with bars.
meanings.example: The police had to bar off the street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 거리에 바리케이드를 쳐야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: verb To appear small or unimportant in relation to something else.
meanings.example: Before Minecraft released The Nether Update in 2020, diamonds were the strongest material in the game. Leather, iron, and gold paled in comparison to the strength, durability, and protection diamond gear provided.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마인크래프트가 2020년에 네더 업데이트를 출시하기 전까지는 다이아몬드가 게임에서 가장 강력한 재료였다. 가죽, 철, 금 장비는 다이아몬드 장비가 제공하는 강도, 내구성, 보호력과 비교하면 초라했다.

meanings.example: This is pale in comparison to that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것은 저것과 비교하면 초라하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To open out into a bell shape.
meanings.example: Her dress belled out at the bottom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 드레스는 아래쪽이 종 모양으로 퍼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend one's legs while upright to get to a lower position.
meanings.example: 1975, Bob Dylan, Tangled Up in Blue
meanings.exampleKorean: 1975, 밥 딜런, Tangled Up in Blue

meanings.englishDefinition: To wrap, as with bandages.
meanings.example: The nurse had to bind up the wound carefully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 간호사는 상처를 조심스럽게 붕대로 감아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy by blasting with explosives.
meanings.example: They were blasting away at each other for 10 minutes before the shooting died down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총격이 잦아들기 전 10분 동안 그들은 서로에게 총격을 가했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To burn more brightly
meanings.example: The fire blazed up suddenly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불길이 갑자기 거세졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become angry suddenly.
meanings.example: He blazed up when he heard the accusation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비난을 듣자마자 갑자기 화를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become glazed
meanings.example: The chemicals in the factory can cause your eyes to glaze up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공장의 화학 물질은 당신의 눈을 침침하고 멍하게 만들 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make bloody; to cover in blood
meanings.example: The fight left him bloody up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 싸움으로 그는 피투성이가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To knock over with an air current, most often wind.
meanings.example: I'll huff and I'll puff and I'll blow your house down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 훅 불고 퍼프 불어서 네 집을 날려버릴 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the bottom; to reach the nadir or lowest point.
meanings.example: Going over that speed bump, my car bottomed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과속방지턱을 넘다가 차 바닥이 긁혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pack into boxes.
meanings.example: to be boxed up in narrow quarters
meanings.exampleKorean: 좁은 공간에 상자 속에 갇히다

meanings.englishDefinition: To come or gather together.
meanings.example: The students began to bunch up around the teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들이 선생님 주위로 모여들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect, assemble or gather small amounts (especially of money), from various sources, with some difficulty; to scrape together.
meanings.example: We managed to scratch together enough money for a down payment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 계약금으로 쓸 돈을 겨우 모을 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean up, especially a room.
meanings.example: I need to redd up my room before my parents get home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님이 집에 오시기 전에 방을 치워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to pick up or clear up by scooping
meanings.example: Use this to scoop up the ice cream.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것으로 아이스크림을 퍼내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to draw attention to the speaker's characterization of someone or something.
meanings.example: Talk about a loose cannon! Did you hear his TV speech?
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 통제 불능이네! 그의 TV 연설 들었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To charge, to charge up.
meanings.example: I need to juice up my phone because the battery's dead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배터리가 다 돼서 휴대폰을 충전해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink alcohol excessively, to the point of being drunk
meanings.example: He tied one on at the party last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어젯밤 파티에서 술을 진탕 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand; fathom; solve.
meanings.example: It took me a while to get my head around the new concept.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 개념을 이해하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To list or recite quickly.
meanings.example: He trotted out the same old excuses again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 또 똑같은 변명들을 줄줄 늘어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: crossover.
meanings.example: The crossover appeal of the movie attracted a wide audience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화의 폭넓은 매력은 다양한 관객을 끌어들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass from one side or area, physical or abstract, to another.
meanings.example: To reach the other bank of the river, we tried crossing over on a log.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강 건너편에 닿기 위해 우리는 통나무 위로 건너가려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to waste, squander
meanings.example: Don't fool away your youth on trivial matters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 젊음을 사소한 일에 낭비하지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (space or time) available.
meanings.example: Selling those old books freed up most of the spare room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 책들을 팔아 남는 방의 대부분을 비울 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash or groom oneself in order to be more attractive.
meanings.example: Women spend longer than men at freshening up before going out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여성들은 외출 전에 몸단장을 하는 데 남성들보다 더 많은 시간을 보낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To successfully arrange the marriage of someone, especially a relative.
meanings.example: Her parents were eager to marry her off to a wealthy man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 부모는 그녀를 부유한 남자와 결혼시키기를 간절히 원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep someone uninformed and waiting for a response.
meanings.example: The company is keeping the negotiations on ice until they get more information.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 더 많은 정보를 얻을 때까지 협상을 보류하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To alter (a cartridge) by forcing it into a smaller calibre and size.
meanings.example: The gunsmith necked down the rifle barrel to accept a smaller caliber.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총기 제작자는 더 작은 구경을 받도록 총열의 목 부분을 가늘게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a pigeon, to enter the loft after returning from a race or training flight.
meanings.example: The bird was trapped in the netting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 새는 그물에 갇혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a person feel emotionally moved in a pleasant way.
meanings.example: The view from the top, over the sea to the islands, simply takes your breath away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바다와 섬들을 내려다보는 정상에서의 경치는 정말 숨 막힐 듯 아름답다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become clumpy; to form a clump.
meanings.example: The flour began to clump up in the wet mixture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밀가루가 축축한 혼합물 속에서 덩어리 지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex.
meanings.example: They decided to get their freak on after the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티 후에 성관계를 갖기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move off, come loose; to go off suddenly away (from) a given position.
meanings.example: The old paint began to fetch away from the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 페인트가 벽에서 떨어져 나가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move upward in bubbles or in a way suggesting bubbles.
meanings.example: The soda began to bubble up when it was poured into the glass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 탄산음료를 잔에 붓자 거품이 솟아오르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bubble-up.
meanings.example: Economists debate whether the bubble-up effect is better than trickle-down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경제학자들은 낙수 효과보다 상향 성장 효과가 더 나은지 토론한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defecate.
meanings.example: He had to pinch one off quickly before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의 전에 빨리 똥을 싸야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get dressed up in showy clothing or jewellery.
meanings.example: He pimped up his car with flashy rims and spoilers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 번쩍이는 휠과 스포일러로 차를 화려하게 치장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mock; to ridicule.
meanings.example: Stop ripping on him just because he made a mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수했다고 그를 놀리지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become chubby; to grow fat.
meanings.example: The baby started to chub up after he began eating solid food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 이유식을 먹기 시작한 후 통통해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become accustomed to the playing conditions, and thus bring one's hand-eye coordination to a reasonable level.
meanings.example: It took him a few overs to get his eye in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 감각을 익히는 데 몇 오버가 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To falsify, or modify something, so that it appears to be better than it is.
meanings.example: A good way to doctor up a basic cake mix is by adding lemon zest, or coffee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기본 케이크 믹스를 좋게 보이게 하는 한 가지 방법은 레몬 제스트나 커피를 추가하는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep in practice.
meanings.example: He needs to keep his eye in by playing regularly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정기적으로 경기를 하면서 연습을 계속해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express one's innermost thoughts or feelings effusively.
meanings.example: She poured her heart out to her best friend after the breakup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 헤어진 후 가장 친한 친구에게 속마음을 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hollow by carving.
meanings.example: Fortunes were carved out of the property of the crown.
meanings.exampleKorean: 왕가의 재산으로 부를 쌓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emerge, come up; be about to happen; to approach or arrive on the scene; to present (itself or oneself).
meanings.example: "They're the finest football leaders that ever came down the pike."
meanings.exampleKorean: "그들은 지금까지 등장한 최고의 축구 리더들이다."

meanings.englishDefinition: cheat or mistreat (someone) To cheat or treat unfairly.
meanings.example: Stop jerking me around and give me a straight answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 속이지 말고 똑바로 대답해.

meanings.englishDefinition: around
meanings.example: Try looking in the office: there should be a few pens floating around there.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사무실을 한번 찾아봐. 펜 몇 개가 거기 있을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regularly copulate with people that one is not in a relationship with.
meanings.example: Richard likes to whore around. His wife has no idea about the women on his "business trips".
meanings.exampleKorean: 리처드는 난잡하게 놀기를 좋아한다. 그의 아내는 그의 "출장"에 대해 여성들이 있다는 것을 전혀 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lubricate with oil, cover with oil To lubricate with oil; to cover or smear with oil.
meanings.example: You need to oil up the gears before using the machine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기계를 사용하기 전에 기어에 기름칠을 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add something extra to something to make it appear more substantial.
meanings.example: Some students pad out their essays by adding a whole lot of quotes from random sources.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 학생들은 무작위 출처의 인용구를 많이 추가하여 에세이를 늘린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid out of fear or caution.
meanings.example: He tends to shy away from confrontation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대립을 피하는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to draw back in fear, cringe, back down
meanings.example: The horse shied away from the loud noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말은 큰 소리에 움츠러들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lock up; to lock in a safe place, out of view.
meanings.example: She decided to lock away her valuable jewelry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 귀중품을 잠가두기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin tracking a target via an automated system.
meanings.example: The missile locked on to the enemy aircraft.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미사일이 적 항공기를 추적하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become loose; to loosen; to relax (a muscle, etc.).
meanings.example: He needs to loosen up and stop being so tense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴장을 풀고 너무 뻣뻣하게 굴지 않아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a meal at home.
meanings.example: We decided to eat in tonight instead of going out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오늘 밤 외식 대신 집에서 식사하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill (someone or something); to cause to die suddenly.
meanings.example: God will strike you down!
meanings.exampleKorean: 신이 너를 벌할 것이다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover with cream.
meanings.example: She creamed up the cake with frosting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 케이크에 프로스팅을 발랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Before the flop, to call as opposed to raising.
meanings.example: He limped in after injuring his leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다리를 다친 후 절뚝거리며 들어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: Avebury from any direction is like stepping back into the past. Before the centre of the village is reached, the road passes through an immense circle of brooding stones which stand like petrified ghosts of an age about which little is known.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어느 방향에서든 에이브버리는 과거로 돌아가는 듯한 느낌을 준다. 마을 중심부에 도달하기 전에, 도로는 잘 알려지지 않은 시대의 석화된 유령처럼 서 있는 거대한 돌 원형을 지난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A setback, downgrade, or deterioration.
meanings.example: The company experienced a step back in its stock price.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 주가 하락이라는 좌절을 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a formal, ceremonious, or overly polite manner.
meanings.example: Please make yourself at home – there's no need to stand on ceremony.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편하게 지내세요. 격식을 차릴 필요는 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw from a problematic situation.
meanings.example: Company lawyers told him to walk away from the deal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사 변호사들은 그에게 그 거래를 관두라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain (a warrant or the like) by swearing to facts.
meanings.example: The police swore out a warrant for his arrest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 그의 체포 영장을 발급받기 위해 사실을 진술했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create (an object) by calling its constructor.
meanings.example: In Java, you use the 'new' keyword to new up an object.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자바에서는 'new' 키워드를 사용하여 객체를 생성합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To align the sights of a firearm.
meanings.example: He spent the morning sighting in his new rifle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새 소총의 조준기를 조정하며 아침을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce (a message, rhythm, or other thing) by tapping.
meanings.example: He tapped out a rhythm on the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 테이블을 두드려 리듬을 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win a set, or by extension a match, by holding serve (winning a game as the server).
meanings.example: She served out the match to win the championship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 챔피언십에서 우승하기 위해 서브로 경기를 마무리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set a boat in motion by pushing with an oar or boathook.
meanings.example: Well, it looks like it's time to go. Let's shove off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자, 이제 갈 시간인 것 같군. 출발하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat (especially in an argument), get the better of, achieve a success over, gain an advantage or win points over, make a point to the detriment or at the expense of, make appear foolish.
meanings.example: The old hag never missed out on any opportunity to score off on her daughter-in-law.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 늙은 마녀는 며느리에게 우위를 점할 기회를 절대 놓치지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate (something).
meanings.example: They decked out the hall for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티를 위해 홀을 화려하게 꾸몄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something without seeing evidence supporting it, by trust or confidence.
meanings.example: You have to take his word on faith; he's never lied to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 말을 믿음으로 받아들여야 해. 그는 너에게 거짓말을 한 적이 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: to frisk
meanings.example: The police shook over the suspect for any hidden weapons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 숨겨진 무기가 있는지 용의자를 몸수색했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard.
meanings.example: He has been slaving away at his desk all day to finish the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 보고서를 끝내기 위해 하루 종일 책상에서 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kick a ball over the touchline in a game of rugby, often to avoid pressure from the opponent team in a difficult situation, to end injury time or to gain territory.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 공을 터치라인 밖으로 차내다, (어려운 상황을) 회피하다
meanings.example: It seemed like a good idea at the time, but I'm going to kick it into touch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그때는 좋은 생각 같았지만, 나는 그 문제를 회피할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: button one's lip.
meanings.example: He was told to button up his lip and not reveal the secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 입을 다물고 비밀을 누설하지 말라고 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: zip one's lip.
meanings.example: Just zip up your lip and let me handle this.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 입 다물고 내가 이 일을 처리하게 둬.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappoint or discourage someone by ruining or criticizing their plans or aspirations.
meanings.example: Don't piss on my bonfire; I'm really excited about this opportunity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 의욕을 꺾지 마, 나는 이 기회에 정말 신나 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach an agreement on a plan.
meanings.example: He is mad to think all men can't piss in a quill, nor see with his spectacles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 남자가 계획에 합의할 수 없다고 생각하는 것은 미친 짓이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in an unusual manner; to behave differently.
meanings.example: He's been sailing on another board since the accident, very quiet and withdrawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사고 이후로 매우 조용하고 내성적으로 변해 평소와 다르게 행동하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to move one's (usually temporary) habitation, especially rapidly.
meanings.example: They just pulled up stakes and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그냥 이사해서 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spur or urge (someone) to do something.
meanings.example: The coach spurred on his team to victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 팀에게 승리를 향해 박차를 가하도록 격려했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To collect something such as leaves into a pile, often with a rake.
meanings.example: The new business is raking in a lot of money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 사업은 많은 돈을 벌어들이고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray by informing on.
meanings.example: He was caught trying to grass up his accomplice to the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공범을 경찰에 밀고하려다 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray by informing on.
meanings.example: What I was pissed off about, though, was him telling everyone that he'd only grassed on me because I'd grassed on him first, which was a total lie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 내가 화가 났던 것은 그가 나를 먼저 밀고했기 때문에 나를 밀고했다고 모두에게 말한 것이었는데, 그것은 완전한 거짓말이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquire a specified average value as more instances occur.
meanings.example: Between 1854 (and the addition of in-patient facilities) and 1870 the mortality rates in the Wakefield Infirmary averaged out to 9.94 per cent for in-patients and 3.18 per cent for out-patients.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1854년(입원 시설 추가 포함)부터 1870년까지 웨이크필드 병원의 사망률은 입원 환자 9.94%, 외래 환자 3.18%로 평균되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: idiomatic go down the toilet: to fail.
meanings.example: All our efforts to save the company have gone down the drain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사를 살리려는 우리의 모든 노력이 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disregard, dismiss or ignore something, as unimportant.
meanings.example: He brushed aside all my objections and went ahead with the project regardless.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나의 모든 반대를 무시하고 프로젝트를 강행했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down To remove something with a brush, or ones hands using a downward brushing motion.
meanings.example: After tripping over, he picked himself up, brushed himself down, and carried on walking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 넘어진 후, 그는 몸을 일으켜 먼지를 털고 계속 걸어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be very enthusiastic, or highly excited.
meanings.example: She was bubbling over with laughter as she ran into the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 방으로 뛰어 들어오며 웃음으로 넘칠 듯했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a relationship.
meanings.example: 1977-1980, Lou Sullivan, personal diary, quoted in 2019, Ellis Martin, Zach Ozma (editors), We Both Laughed In Pleasure
meanings.exampleKorean: 1977-1980, Lou Sullivan, 개인 일기, 2019년 Ellis Martin, Zach Ozma (편집자), We Both Laughed In Pleasure에서 인용

meanings.englishDefinition: To rev (up) a car's engine; to accelerate.
meanings.example: The race car driver quickly tached up the engine before the start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 레이싱카 운전자는 출발 전에 빠르게 엔진 회전수를 높였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Let it be divided into equal parts.
meanings.example: The inheritance was d. in p. aeq. among the siblings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상속 재산은 형제들 사이에 균등하게 나누어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather, assemble or collect, usually into a group or mass.
meanings.example: She gathered up the scattered papers from the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 바닥에 흩어진 서류들을 모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confuse or fluster.
meanings.example: He bollixed up the presentation with his nervousness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴장 때문에 발표를 망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be of very poor quality, even repulsive.
meanings.example: His latest movie stinks on ice; I wouldn't recommend it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 최신 영화는 매우 형편없다; 추천하고 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To verbally attack or criticise.
meanings.example: The teacher ripped into Johnny when he found out he had plagiarised.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 조니가 표절한 것을 알았을 때 그를 심하게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut into pieces.
meanings.example: The British carved up the Ottoman Empire after World War I.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영국은 제1차 세계 대전 이후 오스만 제국을 분할했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring back old problems; to dig up old trouble.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 옛날 일을 들추다, 과거의 문제를 다시 꺼내다
meanings.example: Let's not rake over old coals; we need to focus on the future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛날 일을 들추지 말자; 우리는 미래에 집중해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pushback.
meanings.example: There was significant pushback from the community regarding the new development.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 개발에 대해 지역 사회로부터 상당한 반발이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To refute or argue against a position or an accusation; to express one's own view on a situation.
meanings.example: The scandal is growing legs. We need to start pushing back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스캔들이 확산되고 있다. 우리는 반박하기 시작해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accompany a guest to the door when he or she leaves also used figuratively.
meanings.example: Please see our guest out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님을 배웅해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit.
meanings.example: I had like pains all over and felt I could sick up and at the same time not sick up, and I began to feel like in distress, O my brothers, being fixed rigid too on this chair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 온몸이 아프고 토할 것 같으면서도 토하지 못하는 느낌이었고, 형제들이여, 이 의자에 꼼짝 못하고 고정된 채 괴로움을 느끼기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid addressing or tackling.
meanings.example: He tried to skate over the issue of funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자금 문제에 대해 언급하기를 회피하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of (a pain, illness, condition) by sleeping.
meanings.example: I'm going to sleep off this headache.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 두통은 잠을 자서 없애야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend the night as a guest in someone's home.
meanings.example: Can I sleep over at your house tonight?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 당신 집에서 자고 가도 될까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: become steamy To become steamy.
meanings.example: The windows steamed up from the heat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 창문이 열기로 인해 김이 서렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coat, or become coated, with condensation. To steam up.
meanings.example: My glasses fogged up when he opened the shower door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 샤워실 문을 열었을 때 내 안경에 김이 서렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become straight, or straighter.
meanings.example: He straightened up and walked out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 똑바로 서서 걸어 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten with a strap
meanings.example: He had to strap up his injured ankle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다친 발목을 끈으로 묶어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a short visit somewhere, on the way to another place, either same-day or overnight.
meanings.example: We stopped off in Paris on our way to Rome.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 로마로 가는 길에 파리에 들렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act as a replacement or substitute.
meanings.example: The manager had to step in when the players argued.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들이 다투자 감독이 개입해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stressed.
meanings.example: Will you stop worrying? You're stressing me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걱정 좀 그만해. 나를 초조하게 만들잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish (a cigar, cigarette) by crushing the tip.
meanings.example: He stubbed out his cigarette in the ashtray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재떨이에 담배를 비벼 껐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose (weight) by sweating (from heavy exercise, sauna etc.).
meanings.example: He hopes to sweat off the extra pounds he gained over the holidays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴일 동안 늘어난 체중을 땀으로 빼기를 바란다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean by sweeping.
meanings.example: I'll wash the floor after sweeping up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쓸고 나서 바닥을 닦을게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill up (for example, a tank in a vehicle with gas, petrol, etc.).
meanings.example: We Americans have grumbled loudly about rising gasoline prices while tanking up as if almost nothing has happened. Over the Fourth of July holiday, a record 40.7 million Americans took trips of more than 50 miles, up 1.2 percent from a year earlier.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 미국인들은 거의 아무 일도 없었던 것처럼 기름을 가득 채우면서도 치솟는 휘발유 가격에 대해 큰 소리로 불평해 왔다. 독립기념일 연휴 동안, 4천만 7백만 명의 미국인들이 50마일 이상 여행을 떠났는데, 이는 작년보다 1.2% 증가한 수치이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair using tape.
meanings.example: He taped up the broken box.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부서진 상자를 테이프로 붙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become tense.
meanings.example: She tensed up when she heard the loud noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 큰 소리를 듣고 긴장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be victorious. Usually of emotions and human qualities.
meanings.example: Love always wins out over hate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사랑은 항상 증오를 이긴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To persuade someone who disagrees to agree with one's own point of view.
meanings.example: My parents didn't want me to see him any more, but I finally won them around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님은 내가 더 이상 그를 만나지 않기를 원했지만, 나는 마침내 그들을 설득했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: have someone's back.
meanings.example: Don't worry, I've got your back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걱정 마, 내가 네 뒤를 봐줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be prepared and willing to support or defend someone.
meanings.example: If you ever need help, just ask. You know I have your back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도움이 필요하면 언제든지 말해. 내가 네 뒤를 봐줄 거라는 걸 알잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw or backpedal on a statement or promise.
meanings.example: He had to walk back his earlier statement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이전 발언을 철회해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To double one's wager, particularly, the name of a specific doubling bet allowed in blackjack.
meanings.example: He'd bet three thousand and double down to six thousand, all of it hanging on the turn of the next card.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 3천을 걸고 6천으로 배액했는데, 그 모든 것이 다음 카드의 결과에 달려 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disturb the smoothness of.
meanings.example: The wind ruffled up his hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 그의 머리카락을 헝클어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause bruises to appear, usually by beating or battering.
meanings.example: Instead, we must find an unworthy substitute for the defeat of a foe in that small grin we quickly suppress when we learn that a beheadphoned texter got hit by a car—no, not seriously injured, just bruised up enough to give him a wake-up call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대신, 우리는 헤드폰을 끼고 문자를 보내던 사람이 차에 치였다는 것을 알게 되었을 때 우리가 재빨리 억누르는 작은 미소에서 적의 패배에 대한 가치 없는 대용품을 찾아야 한다. 심각하게 다친 것은 아니지만, 그에게 경각심을 일깨워줄 만큼 멍이 들었을 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To initiate an encounter or interaction by behaving in an unrestrainedly aggressive, confrontational, or accusatory manner.
meanings.example: He came out swinging in his defense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 방어에서 맹렬하게 공격적으로 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout louder than (someone) in order to force through one's argument or point of view.
meanings.example: The protesters shouted down the speaker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시위자들은 연설자의 말을 고함쳐서 듣지 못하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliver a message by telephone when etiquette demands the effort and respect conveyed by communication in person.
meanings.example: He decided to phone in sick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아프다고 전화로 알리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To topple, collapse.
meanings.example: The old wall is starting to tumble down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 벽이 무너지기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe (something, especially something implausible); to accept (a craze, fad, or notion) as valid.
meanings.example: Many people buy into the idea that money brings happiness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 사람들이 돈이 행복을 가져다준다는 생각을 믿는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy the whole of; to purchase the entire stock of something.
meanings.example: The marshland was bought up by a housing company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 습지는 주택 건설 회사에 의해 전부 사들여졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do hard physical exercise or work, and sweat as a result.
meanings.example: I had to sweat it out in the gym for hours to prepare for the competition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 대회 준비를 위해 몇 시간 동안 헬스장에서 땀 흘리며 노력해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To agitate a piece of cloth or other flexible material in order to remove dust, or to try to make it smooth and flat.
meanings.example: We need to run more tests to shake out any remaining bugs in the software.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소프트웨어의 남은 결함들을 해결하기 위해 더 많은 테스트를 거쳐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start with fright.
meanings.example: The sudden loud noise made me jump in my skin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑작스러운 큰 소리에 나는 겁먹고 움찔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain weight, especially as a result of vigorous or excessive eating.
meanings.example: After the holidays, many people pack on the pounds due to overeating.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴일이 지나면 많은 사람들이 과식으로 인해 살이 찐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pack on the pounds.
meanings.example: He started to pack on pounds after he quit exercising.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운동을 그만둔 후 살이 찌기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lather; to cover with suds.
meanings.example: Please suds up the dishes before rinsing them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 설거지를 헹구기 전에 거품을 내주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lather; to cover with suds
meanings.example: He lathered up his face with shaving cream.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 쉐이빙 크림으로 얼굴에 거품을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or scoot over in order to make room for someone, especially when sitting.
meanings.example: Budge up, there's plenty of room on the bench.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좀 비켜요, 벤치에 자리가 많아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ruin, mess up.
meanings.example: the school books say about the united efforts of the two great Rossums is all a fairy tale. They used to have dreadful rows. The old atheist hadn't the slightest conception of industrial matters, and the end of it was that young Rossum shut him up in some laboratory or other and let him fritter the time away with his monstrosities, while he himself started on the business from an engineer's point of view. Old Rossum cursed him and before he died he managed to botch up two physiological horrors. Then one day they found him dead in the laboratory. And that's his whole story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학교 교과서에 나오는 두 위대한 로섬의 합작품에 대한 이야기는 모두 꾸며낸 이야기다. 그들은 끔찍한 싸움을 벌이곤 했다. 늙은 무신론자는 산업 문제에 대해 전혀 이해하지 못했고, 결국 젊은 로섬은 그를 어떤 실험실에 가두고 괴물들과 시간을 보내게 했으며, 자신은 엔지니어의 관점에서 사업을 시작했다. 늙은 로섬은 그를 저주했고, 죽기 전에 두 개의 생리학적 공포를 망쳐 놓았다. 그러던 어느 날 그들은 실험실에서 그가 죽은 것을 발견했다. 그것이 그의 전말이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thicker, fatter or more bulky.
meanings.example: The pet food was made from meat bulked out with rusk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 애완동물 사료는 러스크로 부피를 늘린 고기로 만들어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reserve or book all of something, for example by purchasing all the tickets.
meanings.example: Flights are beginning to book up as people flee the region.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 지역을 떠나면서 항공편이 모두 예약되기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend the night somewhere.
meanings.example: Since you stay in the main lodge, it's easy to pad down at all hours to a cozy stay before the fireplace, or farther down the hall to the small Gothic-style chapel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 본관에 머물기 때문에, 언제든지 벽난로 앞의 아늑한 곳이나 복도 저편의 작은 고딕 양식 예배당에서 하룻밤 묵기 쉽습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure with ropes; to tie down
meanings.example: We need to lash down the cargo before driving in the rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비 오는 날 운전하기 전에 짐을 밧줄로 단단히 묶어야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily.
meanings.example: The waterfall continued to pour down even during the drought.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가뭄 중에도 폭포수는 계속해서 쏟아져 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily; to sleet, snow or hail heavily.
meanings.example: It started to pelt down just as we left the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집을 나서자마자 비가 억수같이 쏟아지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain heavily.
meanings.example: The rain began to whip down, making it impossible to see the road.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 억수같이 쏟아지기 시작해서 도로가 보이지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by hacking; to cut off
meanings.example: The surgeon had to hack off the diseased limb.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과의사는 병든 팔다리를 베어서 잘라내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become quiet; to cease making sounds.
meanings.example: The crowd began to hush down as the speaker approached the podium.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연설자가 연단으로 다가오자 군중은 조용해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To completely empty.
meanings.example: Please empty out the trash can before putting in a new bag.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 봉투를 넣기 전에 쓰레기통을 완전히 비워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To locate by following a scent.
meanings.example: To be plain, I much question whether the politician, who hath generally a good nose, hath not scented out somewhat of the utility of this practice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 솔직히 말해서, 나는 일반적으로 좋은 후각을 가진 정치인이 이 관행의 유용성을 어느 정도 냄새로 찾아내지 못했는지 의심스럽다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To affect in a sudden, unprecedented or surprising manner; to overwhelm a person's ordinarily contrary impulse.
meanings.example: A sudden feeling of sadness began to come over her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑자기 슬픈 감정이 그녀를 엄습하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fade or wane
meanings.example: 1874, Marcus Clarke, For the Term of His Natural Life Chapter VI
meanings.exampleKorean: 1874년, 마커스 클라크, 그의 자연 수명 동안의 기간 제6장

meanings.englishDefinition: To take off (clothing).
meanings.example: He did off his coat before entering the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 집에 들어가기 전에 코트를 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get back on one's feet. 19th c.
meanings.example: After a long illness, she finally began to win up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랜 투병 끝에 그녀는 마침내 회복하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart quickly or exit abruptly, especially in a manner which does not attract notice and before a meeting, event, etc. has concluded.
meanings.example: He had to duck out of the meeting early to catch his flight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비행기를 타기 위해 회의에서 일찍 몰래 빠져나가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring a ridden animal to a halt by pulling on the reins.
meanings.example: 1856: Gustave Flaubert, Madame Bovary, Part III Chapter X, translated by Eleanor Marx-Aveling
meanings.exampleKorean: 1856년: 귀스타브 플로베르, 마담 보바리, 제3부 제10장, 엘레노어 마르크스-에이블링 번역

meanings.englishDefinition: To come together, to form a crowd.
meanings.example: They all crowded up and leaned over the rails, nearly in my face, and kept still, watching with all their might. I could see them first-rate, but they couldn't see me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 모두 모여들어 난간 위로 몸을 기울였고, 거의 내 얼굴 가까이 다가와서 꼼짝 않고 온 힘을 다해 지켜보았다. 나는 그들을 아주 잘 볼 수 있었지만, 그들은 나를 볼 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away.
meanings.example: She looked at me and then turned and glanced at my arm about her, and then she seemed quite suddenly to realize the scantiness of her apparel and drew away, covering her face with her palms and blushing furiously.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 나를 보더니 몸을 돌려 내 팔을 흘끗 보았다. 그러더니 갑자기 자신의 옷차림이 얼마나 야한지 깨달은 듯 얼굴을 손바닥으로 가리고 얼굴을 붉히며 멀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form; to start to exist.
meanings.example: The universe came into being with the Big Bang.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우주는 빅뱅과 함께 존재하게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show anger or indignation at something.
meanings.example: He bridled up when he heard the accusation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비난을 듣고 화내며 뻣뻣해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add additional elements or components to (a framework, a concept, a device, etc.) in order to extend it.
meanings.example: We will build on the success of last year's event.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작년 행사의 성공을 발판 삼아 나아갈 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to add components to a larger entity without interfering with the purpose or functionality of the larger entity
meanings.example: The new software has many features built into it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 소프트웨어에는 많은 기능이 내장되어 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to add components to a larger entity without interfering with the purpose or functionality of the larger entity
meanings.example: Safety features are built into the car's design.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안전 기능이 차량 설계에 통합되어 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a decision based on chance, for example by flipping a coin or rolling a dice.
meanings.example: We'll toss up to see who goes first.
meanings.exampleKorean: 누가 먼저 갈지 동전 던지기로 결정합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue with one's pursuit
meanings.example: Just jog on, you're almost there.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 계속 가세요, 거의 다 왔어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: go away
meanings.example: Jog on, I don't want to talk to you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져, 너랑 얘기하고 싶지 않아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run around in celebration
meanings.example: The players wheeled away after scoring the winning goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들은 결승골을 넣은 후 환호하며 달려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer for suggestion.
meanings.example: Let's toss around some ideas for the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트에 대한 아이디어를 몇 가지 제안해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover with snow.
meanings.example: The heavy snowfall snowed out the entire town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설로 마을 전체가 눈에 덮였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to fall from the sky, as snow
meanings.example: It started to snow down heavily in the mountains.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산에는 눈이 많이 내리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: Her nightgown was thin, and she felt chilly as she stepped across the hall, pulling back the curtain that shielded Gabriel's room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 잠옷은 얇았고, 가브리엘의 방을 가리고 있던 커튼을 젖히며 복도를 건너자 쌀쌀함을 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out of
meanings.example: The worm wriggled out of the man's pocket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벌레가 남자의 주머니에서 꿈틀거리며 빠져나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unzip.
meanings.example: He zipped down his jacket to cool off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더위를 식히기 위해 재킷의 지퍼를 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to locate; to search for.
meanings.example: The head of the fiord, and along its sides, can still be seen the hulks of German destroyers and submarines. These sought shelter in this secluded spot, but the British Navy sought them out and sank them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피오르드의 머리 부분과 그 측면을 따라 독일 구축함과 잠수함의 잔해가 여전히 보입니다. 이들은 이 외딴 곳에 피신처를 찾았지만, 영국 해군은 그들을 찾아내 침몰시켰습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inspect something fully.
meanings.example: The inspector will see over the building tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 검사관이 내일 건물을 전반적으로 살필 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to resell.
meanings.example: He bought the car cheap and sold it on for a profit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 싸게 사서 이익을 붙여 재판매했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (someone or something) more glamorous.
meanings.example: He would become absorbed in what he was doing and forget that they were going out to a reception at the City Hall or somewhere. Stella would appear at the study door all glammed up in her best coat and he would look up from his reading like a startled animal caught drinking at a watering hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 하던 일에 몰두하여 시청이나 다른 곳에서 열리는 리셉션에 외출한다는 것을 잊곤 했다. 스텔라는 최고의 코트를 입고 화려하게 꾸민 채 서재 문 앞에 나타났고, 그는 마치 물웅덩이에서 물을 마시다 잡힌 놀란 동물처럼 독서에서 고개를 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into pairs; to put into a group of two.

meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 활동을 위해 짝을 지어야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go somewhere.
meanings.example: Let's shimmy on down to the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해변으로 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in meaningless activity.
meanings.example: I can't wait until I'm old enough to "putter around".

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept without flinching or complaining.

meanings.englishDefinition: An action where the goalkeeper deflects the ball over the crossbar.

meanings.englishDefinition: (to cause) to topple over and fall

meanings.englishDefinition: To place a golf ball on a tee.

meanings.englishDefinition: To measure out (a piece of land) so as to be able to accurately fire upon it.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive out; to expel.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take up an issue with another person's boss or other superior rather than beginning or continuing to deal with the original person.
meanings.example: She went over his head and took her complaint directly to the president of the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 직속 상사를 건너뛰고 회사 사장에게 직접 불만을 제기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A reckless individual, often with nothing to lose; a person prone to crashing out.
meanings.example: Niggas really be crash outs wit no motion biggest life asset be a pistol that shit crazy
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정말 아무것도 가진 것 없는 막가파들이야, 인생의 가장 큰 자산은 권총이지, 정말 미친 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold on tightly; to grip hard or adhere.
meanings.example: The child clung on to his mother's leg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 엄마 다리에 매달렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A reckless individual, often with nothing to lose; a person prone to crashing out.
meanings.example: Niggas really be crash outs wit no motion biggest life asset be a pistol that shit crazy
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정말 아무것도 가진 것 없는 막가파들이야, 인생의 가장 큰 자산은 권총이지, 정말 미친 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explain (something) to (someone), step by step.
meanings.example: On my first day at work, the secretary walked me through the process of using the photocopier.
meanings.exampleKorean: 입사 첫날, 비서가 복사기 사용법을 단계별로 설명해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A reckless individual, often with nothing to lose; a person prone to crashing out.
meanings.example: Niggas really be crash outs wit no motion biggest life asset be a pistol that shit crazy
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정말 아무것도 가진 것 없는 막가파들이야, 인생의 가장 큰 자산은 권총이지, 정말 미친 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To want to enter something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 안으로 들어가고 싶어 하다, 참여하고 싶어 하다
meanings.example: Everyone wants in on the latest business venture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 사람들이 최신 사업 투자에 참여하고 싶어 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A reckless individual, often with nothing to lose; a person prone to crashing out.
meanings.example: Niggas really be crash outs wit no motion biggest life asset be a pistol that shit crazy
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정말 아무것도 가진 것 없는 막가파들이야, 인생의 가장 큰 자산은 권총이지, 정말 미친 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To want to leave something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 밖으로 나가고 싶어 하다, 그만두고 싶어 하다
meanings.example: After six months in a tedious desk job, Anna wants out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지루한 책상 업무를 6개월간 한 후, 안나는 그만두고 싶어 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (a moving vehicle) to stop by using an arm gesture.
meanings.example: He waved down a taxi to get to the airport.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공항에 가기 위해 택시를 손짓으로 불러 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A reckless individual, often with nothing to lose; a person prone to crashing out.
meanings.example: Niggas really be crash outs wit no motion biggest life asset be a pistol that shit crazy
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정말 아무것도 가진 것 없는 막가파들이야, 인생의 가장 큰 자산은 권총이지, 정말 미친 짓이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a wave of one's hand, or of a flag or wand, so as to signal (someone) to desist or leave.
meanings.example: He waved off the taxi, signaling it to stop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 택시를 향해 손을 흔들어 멈추라고 신호했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather (especially people or dogs) together, calling them by whistling.
meanings.example: He chanced to miss his dog; we stood still till he had whistled him up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 우연히 자신의 개를 놓쳤다; 그가 휘파람으로 개를 부를 때까지 우리는 가만히 서 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become informed; to inform oneself of something, or come to a realization.
meanings.example: You might have thought that I was dumb / But I'm all wised up / Game's up, move on, why don't you get out of my hair
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 내가 멍청하다고 생각했을지도 모르지만 / 나는 모든 것을 알게 되었어 / 게임은 끝났어, 이제 그만 내 신경에서 벗어나

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose (weight) by doing physical work; to burn off the calories gained from eating something.
meanings.example: We need to get moving to work off these donuts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 도넛을 먹고 난 칼로리를 소모하기 위해 움직여야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a letter to, e.g. a publication.
meanings.example: If you have any questions, you can write in to the following address.
meanings.exampleKorean: 질문이 있으시면 다음 주소로 편지를 보내주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair; mend.
meanings.example: The old car was vamped up for the sale.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 차는 판매를 위해 수리되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Rest To relax in a slack attitude, not moving much.
meanings.example: After an exhausting day at work, I would veg out in front of the TV.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 하루 일과 후, 나는 TV 앞에서 빈둥거릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To misguide.
meanings.example: The detective tried to throw the suspect off the trail with false information.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 거짓 정보로 용의자를 혼란스럽게 하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coach; to instruct.
meanings.example: "Someone said it was Mrs. Debbs' first visit." "Yes but they could easily coach her up.
meanings.exampleKorean: "누군가 데브스 부인이 처음 방문했다고 말했다." "네, 하지만 그들은 쉽게 그녀를 지도할 수 있었어요."

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive by luring with a false promise or misleading implication.
meanings.example: He was hooked in by promises of a quick profit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빠른 수익을 약속받고 속았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To portray (something) (as unimportant); to dismiss or make light of (a factor in a situation, or one's embarrassment about it); to pretend not to be embarrassed, upset, impressed or otherwise affected by (it).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 중요하지 않게 보이게 하다, 대수롭지 않게 여기다
meanings.example: She tried to play off her nervousness during the presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 발표 중에 긴장한 것을 대수롭지 않게 보이려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fraudulently or disingenuously pass (goods, or ideas) on to.
meanings.example: The salesman tried to foist off a faulty product on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 판매원은 나에게 결함 있는 제품을 속여서 넘기려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write down, especially hurriedly; to make a note of.
meanings.example: Please note down the important points from the lecture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강의의 중요한 점들을 적어두세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by scratching.
meanings.example: He had to scratch out a living as a street musician.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 거리 음악가로서 어렵게 살아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the inside of (something) by using a sponge.
meanings.example: She sponged out the inside of the vase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 꽃병 안쪽을 스펀지로 닦아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: along
meanings.example: I refuse to go along with another of his dangerous schemes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그의 또 다른 위험한 계획에 동의하기를 거부한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cheer loudly for.
meanings.example: The crowd roared on their team to victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중은 팀의 승리를 위해 열렬히 응원했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce a sense of nostalgia.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 과거를 회상하게 하다, 옛날로 돌아간 듯한 느낌을 주다
meanings.example: The old photographs rolled back the years for her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 오래된 사진들은 그녀에게 과거를 회상하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To count down to zero (i.e. signaling the end (or start) of something).
meanings.example: The minutes ticked away as they waited for the results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결과를 기다리는 동안 몇 분이 흘러갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover the stem or leaves of plants with soil, as to encourage root growth or protect from cold.
meanings.example: Earth up a potato crop several times during the season to encourage more tubers to grow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 많은 덩이줄기가 자라도록 계절 동안 감자 작물에 여러 번 흙을 덮어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce in a laborious and formulaic manner.
meanings.example: The author had to grind out a new novel every year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 작가는 매년 새로운 소설을 힘들게 써내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something hard smaller and smoother by gradually rubbing off tiny bits; to wear down by grinding action.
meanings.example: The old dog's teeth had been ground down by constant use.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 늙은 개의 이빨은 계속 사용되어 마모되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grow in size, so as to be able to fit (something, especially an item of clothing).
meanings.example: His new jumper was a bit big for him, but he'll grow into it in a couple of years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 새 스웨터는 그에게 조금 컸지만, 몇 년 안에 자라서 맞게 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become extinguished.
meanings.example: The candle guttered out in the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 촛불이 바람에 꺼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something into a slot.
meanings.example: Can you slot in this new file into the folder?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새 파일을 폴더에 끼워 넣을 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a previous state.
meanings.example: We had a terrible year so we agreed to turn back the clock and go on as if it hadn't happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 끔찍한 한 해를 보냈기 때문에 시간을 되돌리고 마치 아무 일도 없었던 것처럼 계속하기로 합의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demonstrate affection (on somebody).
meanings.example: Two or three times a day, I bundle up, put on my boots, go out the front door, sit in Greigg's glider and love up on Cork a bit — he's never far from the door.
