meanings.exampleKorean: 하루에 두세 번, 나는 옷을 껴입고 부츠를 신고 현관문을 나서서 그레그의 글라이더에 앉아 코크에게 조금 애정을 표현한다 - 그는 문에서 멀리 떨어지지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull down forcefully.
meanings.example: Mr. Douglas got up from the couch, shucked down his pants, and then knelt down beside my mother. He reached out and grabbed her big round bubbies, and began to squeeze and play with them, while he teased her by prodding his cock against her red behind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더글라스 씨는 소파에서 일어나 바지를 힘껏 끌어내린 다음 어머니 옆에 무릎을 꿇었다. 그는 손을 뻗어 그녀의 크고 둥근 가슴을 움켜쥐고 만지기 시작했고, 그녀의 붉은 엉덩이에 그의 성기를 찔러 넣으며 그녀를 놀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add clay to (something).
meanings.example: The potter will clay up the sides of the pot to strengthen it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도공은 항아리를 튼튼하게 하기 위해 옆면에 점토를 바를 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something by, or as if by, sweeping.
meanings.example: The floodwaters swept away the bridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수물이 다리를 휩쓸어버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be easily imbibed; to be easy to drink.
meanings.example: I don't take vodka straight, but it slips down with a bit of lemonade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보드카를 스트레이트로 마시지는 않지만, 레모네이드와 섞으면 술술 넘어간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include (e.g. a certain word or phrase) into a sentence discreetly
meanings.example: She managed to slip in a compliment about his new haircut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 새로운 헤어스타일에 대한 칭찬을 몰래 끼워 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on some clothes rapidly.
meanings.example: He slipped into his coat and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재빨리 코트를 입고 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (something from something) in a sweeping motion.
meanings.example: He used his arm to rake the books off the shelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 팔을 이용해 책을 선반에서 휩쓸어 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash something onto the shore.
meanings.example: When the Columns N S E W, are compleated, cast up the Figures in the Columns N and S, and also those in the Columns E and W.
meanings.exampleKorean: N, S, E, W 열이 완성되면, N열과 S열의 숫자들, 그리고 E열과 W열의 숫자들도 합산하시오.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strengthen with bracing material.
meanings.example: They had to brace up the old bridge before it could be used again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 다리를 다시 사용하기 전에 버팀목으로 보강해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stash or store something in a secret location.
meanings.example: ‘It's rather like a beautiful Inverness cloak one has inherited. Much too good to hide away, so one wears it instead of an overcoat and pretends it's an amusing new fashion.’
meanings.exampleKorean: ‘이건 마치 물려받은 멋진 인버네스 망토 같아. 숨겨두기엔 너무 좋아서, 코트 대신 입고 재미있는 새 유행이라고 둘러댄다.’

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide somewhere.
meanings.example: The criminals decided to hide out in a remote cabin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 범죄자들은 외딴 오두막에 몸을 숨기기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something (especially an item of clothing) with a swift movement.
meanings.example: If you but aſſiſt me, I'll engage to whip her off to France, and you ſhall never hear more of her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 도와준다면, 그녀를 프랑스로 휙 데려가서 다시는 그녀에 대해 듣지 못하게 할 것을 약속하지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent hunting hounds from straying from the pack by whipping them.
meanings.example: He whipped in a dangerous cross, and Faulkner headed into the net.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 위험한 크로스를 올렸고, 포크너가 헤딩으로 골을 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something extremely quickly and perfunctorily.
meanings.example: She managed to whip through her homework before dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 저녁 식사 전에 숙제를 재빨리 해치울 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten the laces of something.
meanings.example: He took a moment to lace up his boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 잠시 신발 끈을 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to fall down and break by hitting it hard.
meanings.example: The angry mob decided to smash down the gates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화난 군중은 문을 부수어 넘어뜨리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: Go and play out in the yard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가서 마당에서 놀아라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replay a recording.
meanings.example: Can you play back the last part of the conversation?
meanings.exampleKorean: 대화의 마지막 부분을 다시 재생해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or acquire by plucking from, for example, the ground; to pick up.
meanings.example: He plucked up the courage to ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To introduce (a substitute) into the game.
meanings.example: The coach decided to send on a new player in the second half.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 후반전에 새로운 선수를 교체 투입하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass or progress quickly and easily.
meanings.example: She sailed through her exams without any difficulty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아무 어려움 없이 시험을 순조롭게 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass from high-ranked people to lower-ranked people
meanings.example: The CEO's benefits are unlikely to trickle down to the factory workers.
meanings.exampleKorean: CEO의 혜택이 공장 노동자들에게까지 낙수 효과를 미칠 가능성은 낮다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose value on the stock exchange.
meanings.example: the New England Patriots ever pass on trading down?
meanings.exampleKorean: 뉴잉글랜드 패트리어츠가 드래프트 순위를 낮추는 것을 포기할 수 있을까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain value on the stock exchange.
meanings.example: of the few surprises saw the New York Giants take offensive tackle Andrew Thomas at number four while the Buccaneers traded up to 13 for offensive tackle Tristian Wirfs to give some protection to their new quarterback Brady.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 안 되는 놀라운 일 중 하나는 뉴욕 자이언츠가 4번으로 공격 태클 앤드류 토마스를 지명한 반면, 부캐니어스는 새로운 쿼터백 브래디를 보호하기 위해 13번으로 트레이드업하여 공격 태클 트리스탄 워프스를 지명한 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demolish.
meanings.example: They're going to tear down the old shack when they redevelop the land.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 땅을 재개발할 때 낡은 오두막을 철거할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To promise that something is (not) true.
meanings.example: I swear down I didn't take your keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 열쇠를 가져가지 않았다고 진심으로 맹세해.

meanings.englishDefinition: Northern England I promise; no lie.
meanings.example: I'll be there on time, swear down!
meanings.exampleKorean: 정시에 갈게, 정말이야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to stick to a surface.
meanings.example: I can't stick this picture down. I need better glue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 사진을 붙일 수가 없어. 더 좋은 접착제가 필요해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean with a sponge
meanings.example: He sponged down the dirty table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더러운 테이블을 스펀지로 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A quick clean of something (namely the body) with a sponge.
meanings.example: A quick sponge down is all I have time for.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빠른 스펀지 세안이 내가 할 수 있는 전부다.

meanings.englishDefinition: splashdown.
meanings.example: The splashdown was successful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 착수는 성공적이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To land in water.
meanings.example: The spacecraft is expected to splash down in the Pacific Ocean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우주선은 태평양에 착수할 것으로 예상된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to fall down by shaking it, or something it is attached to.
meanings.example: shake down apples from an apple tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사과나무에서 사과를 흔들어 떨어뜨리다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down heavily in a seat.
meanings.example: After a long day, he plumped down onto the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하루 끝에, 그는 소파에 털썩 주저앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: Please move down inside the carriage to allow other passengers to board the train.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 승객들이 기차에 탈 수 있도록 객차 안쪽으로 이동해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To furnish with equipment.
meanings.example: The factory is being tooled up for the new production line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 생산 라인을 위해 공장에 장비가 갖춰지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire new staff; to fill vacancies in a workforce.
meanings.example: The company decided to staff up for the holiday season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 연말연시를 맞아 직원을 충원하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into a stack.
meanings.example: Stack up the boxes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상자들을 쌓아라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move closer together, in order to make more space for someone else.
meanings.example: Everyone had to squeeze up to make room for the new guest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 손님을 위해 모두 바짝 붙어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to vomit
meanings.example: He ate too much and spewed up later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 먹어서 나중에 토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rekindle.
meanings.example: to go back to the history of British Railways, there were moves to introduce electrification more widely when the West Coast Main Line was sparked up in the 1960s, but this was rejected by dyed-in-the-wool old regional railway managers who did not like the hassle of putting up the wires.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영국 철도의 역사를 되돌아보면, 1960년대 웨스트 코스트 본선에 전철화가 더 널리 도입되려는 움직임이 있었지만, 전선 설치의 번거로움을 싫어했던 완고한 옛 지역 철도 관리자들에 의해 거부되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to entangle
meanings.example: The traffic was snarled up for miles due to the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고로 인해 교통이 몇 마일 동안 얽혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to confuse (one or more objects with another object or other objects)
meanings.example: Whenever I meet the twins, I always muddle up their names. I call John Jim, and Jim John.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쌍둥이를 만날 때마다 나는 항상 이름을 혼동한다. 존을 짐이라고 부르고, 짐을 존이라고 부른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To devour greedily.
meanings.example: We snaffled up all the chips before Jeff came to the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제프가 테이블에 오기 전에 우리는 칩을 모두 게걸스럽게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to sing louder.
meanings.example: Please sing up so everyone can hear you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두가 당신을 들을 수 있도록 더 크게 노래해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to pay what one owes (a debt, a bill etc.)
meanings.example: I need to settle up with you for the money I borrowed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빌린 돈에 대해 당신에게 빚을 갚아야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrape completely; cover all over with scrapes.
meanings.example: He should not have used that old dirty rag. Look how it scraped up his paint job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 낡고 더러운 걸레를 사용하지 말았어야 했다. 페인트 작업이 어떻게 긁혔는지 보라.

meanings.englishDefinition: to approach or come near to while riding.
meanings.example: My jeans always seem to ride up on me in public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공공장소에서 내 청바지는 항상 말려 올라가는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To motivate, encourage or mentally (emotionally) prepare.
meanings.example: The coach tried to psych up his team before the big game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 중요한 경기 전에 팀원들의 사기를 북돋우려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase.
meanings.example: The company is trying to ratchet up its profits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 이익을 점진적으로 늘리려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (something) higher or further.
meanings.example: Please move up the chair so I can reach the shelf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선반에 닿을 수 있도록 의자를 위로 옮겨 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to increase over a period of time; to accumulate
meanings.example: The debt continued to mount up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빚은 계속해서 증가했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assemble; to congregate; to combine.
meanings.example: After the attack, we all came together and acted as one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공격 후, 우리는 모두 모여 하나처럼 행동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoo by wafting with the hands.
meanings.example: She wafted the flies off the food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파리를 손짓으로 음식에서 쫓아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restore one's life (or a given situation etc.) to a normal state, after a calamity, shock etc.
meanings.example: Picking up the pieces after the Suez disaster, the British found themselves getting used to the idea that they are not as big a power as they thought they were.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수에즈 재난 이후 수습하면서, 영국인들은 자신들이 생각했던 만큼 강대국이 아니라는 사실에 익숙해지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase over a period of time; to accumulate.
meanings.example: But in reality it was only partially accomplished, because Vukub's two sons, Zipacna and Cabrakan, still remained to be dealt with. Zipacna was daily employed in heaping up mountains, while Cabrakan, his brother, shook them in earthquake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 실제로는 부분적으로만 달성되었는데, 부쿠브의 두 아들인 지프낙나와 카브라칸이 여전히 처리되어야 했기 때문이다. 지프낙나는 매일 산을 쌓는 데 사용되었고, 그의 형제인 카브라칸은 지진으로 그 산들을 흔들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume something by first licking it from a surface.
meanings.example: There was likewise a homeless dog, with his tail between his legs, licking up the crumbs and gnawing the fragments of the feast
meanings.exampleKorean: 마찬가지로 꼬리를 다리 사이에 끼운 채 음식을 핥아 먹고 잔치의 조각들을 씹는 떠돌이 개가 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into a bundle.
meanings.example: Please bundle up the clothes you want to donate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기부하고 싶은 옷들을 꾸러미로 싸주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep on (something); to consider (something) by dreaming about it.
meanings.example: I'll dream on it and let you know my decision tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것에 대해 꿈을 꾸고 내일 결정을 알려드리겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make even; to make level.
meanings.example: Let's even up the score in the next game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 경기에서 점수를 동점으로 만들자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to cease or desist by laughter.
meanings.example: to laugh down a speaker
meanings.exampleKorean: 연설자를 웃음으로 단념시키다

meanings.englishDefinition: To progress in a smooth fashion.
meanings.example: The renovations for our house are rocking along nicely.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 집 리모델링은 순조롭게 진행되고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Followed by at:
meanings.example: He turned up his nose at the cheap hotel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 싸구려 호텔을 보고 콧방귀를 뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: off
meanings.example: I'd sooner chew off my hand than go out with you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너와 데이트하느니 차라리 내 손을 씹어 떼겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill cracks in something, for example a wall.
meanings.example: We need to chink up the holes in the wall before winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울이 오기 전에 벽의 구멍을 메워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce or weaken bit by bit; often used with at.
meanings.example: Matthew Towers, senior industry analyst at Frost & Sullivan, thinks the PowerPC will gradually build market share over the next several years. “They'll ‘chip’ away at Intel, but slowly,” he says.
meanings.exampleKorean: Frost & Sullivan의 수석 산업 분석가인 Matthew Towers는 PowerPC가 향후 몇 년 동안 점진적으로 시장 점유율을 높일 것이라고 생각한다. 그는 “그들은 인텔의 점유율을 조금씩 깎아내겠지만, 느리게 진행될 것”이라고 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause something to have chips, cracks or dents.
meanings.example: It were when my father were employed as mason under 'brick and mortar Benson,' as they called him, for repairs of a wall, and they were short of stones, and they chipped up the figure I be telling you of.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽 수리를 위해 석공으로 일하던 아버지가 돌이 부족해서 내가 말하는 그 조각상을 흠집 냈을 때였어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make more cheerful; to cause to perk up.
meanings.example: The good news helped to chirk her up after a long day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 좋은 소식은 긴 하루를 보낸 후 그녀를 기운 나게 하는 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of; to cause to come to an end.
meanings.example: to choke off a speaker by uproar
meanings.exampleKorean: 소란으로 연설자를 중단시키다

meanings.englishDefinition: To (temporarily) lose one's power of speech, because of strong emotion.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 감정 때문에 목이 메어 말을 못 하다
meanings.example: Hearing that song always makes me choke up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 노래를 들으면 항상 목이 메어 말을 할 수 없게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form (sides or teams), usually for a game, by having two individual players choose their teammates.
meanings.example: Let's choose up teams for the soccer match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축구 시합을 위해 팀을 짜자.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: They had to chop down the old oak tree because it was diseased.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 오래된 오크 나무는 병이 들어서 베어낼 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come in handy for someone in the aftertime.
meanings.example: His knowledge of history will stand him in good stead when he applies for the job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 역사 지식은 직업을 지원할 때 그에게 큰 도움이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sweet to the taste.
meanings.example: I sweetened up the barbecue sauce by adding some sugar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 설탕을 좀 넣어 바비큐 소스를 달콤하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume a clear and complete form when separate elements come together; to be fully realized at last.
meanings.example: The puzzle pieces finally fell into place, and the picture was complete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퍼즐 조각들이 마침내 제자리를 찾았고 그림이 완성되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered in haze.
meanings.example: In the city at night, the sky hazes over so you can't see the stars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤에는 도시의 하늘이 흐릿해져 별을 볼 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read or inspect, looking for errors or problems.
meanings.example: Can you check this over before I turn it in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제출하기 전에 이것 좀 검토해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: A reading or inspection, looking for errors or problems.
meanings.example: A quick check over of the engine revealed no problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엔진을 빠르게 점검했지만 문제는 발견되지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recover or retake, with great effort, something that was lost, often by a government or official agency.
meanings.example: I was initially refused a refund, but eventually managed to claw back part of the money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 처음에는 환불을 거부당했지만, 결국 일부 금액을 되찾는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To click on a link in order to move to another page.
meanings.example: Click through the link to see more details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 많은 세부 정보를 보려면 링크를 클릭하여 이동하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut down, especially in large numbers.
meanings.example: The machine can mow down a field of wheat in just a few hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 기계는 몇 시간 만에 밀밭 전체를 베어낼 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become valid or active, to inure.
meanings.example: The new law will come into force at the start of next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법은 내년 초부터 효력을 발휘할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find by diligently searching, especially from unsavory sources.
meanings.example: Where did you dredge up the idea that they didn't know what hospital he was born in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 그가 어느 병원에서 태어났는지 몰랐다는 생각을 어디서 어렵게 찾아냈어?

meanings.englishDefinition: To celebrate a person's death or downfall triumphantly.
meanings.example: is all aside from my personal disgust at the idea that we should spare any thought for those who would kill us. No one is suggesting dancing on their graves, but neither should we shed even a fraction of a tear for them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리를 죽이려는 사람들을 동정해야 한다는 생각에 대한 나의 개인적인 역겨움과는 별개로, 아무도 그들의 무덤 위에서 춤추자고 제안하는 사람은 없지만, 우리도 그들을 위해 눈물 한 방울 흘릴 필요는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unravel; to determine; solve.
meanings.example: It took a lot of effort to tease out the truth from his confusing story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 혼란스러운 이야기에서 진실을 알아내기 위해 많은 노력이 필요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit or hack up.
meanings.example: My cat is always cacking up hairballs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 고양이는 항상 헤어볼을 토한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shore up.
meanings.example: means of 'reasoned' argument) not only serves to sure up the sense of certainty which people attach to their moral judgments and to the world views from which their judgements ultimately derive, it serves to sure up their sense of
meanings.exampleKorean: '이성적인' 주장을 통해 사람들의 도덕적 판단과 궁극적으로 그 판단이 파생되는 세계관에 부여하는 확실성의 감각을 지지할 뿐만 아니라, 그들의 감각을 지지한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be wearing.
meanings.example: She has on a nice red shirt and skinny jeans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 멋진 빨간 셔츠와 스키니진을 입고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: I don't like to stay in motels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 모텔에 머무르는 것을 좋아하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss as silly something presented with genuine conviction or treated seriously.
meanings.example: The claim that his dog wrote the poems was laughed out of court by publishers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 개가 시를 썼다는 주장은 출판사들에 의해 터무니없다고 일축되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: How does your hat manage to stay on in such strong wind?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 강한 바람에도 모자가 어떻게 계속 머리에 있을 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay back; to return someone a previous good deed.
meanings.example: After John gave her a lovely wooly hat for her birthday, she wanted to make it up to him so she took him out for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존이 그녀의 생일에 사랑스러운 울 모자를 선물한 후, 그녀는 그에게 저녁 식사를 대접하며 보상하고 싶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat one's opponent easily and completely during a fight, contest, or debate.
meanings.example: The invading army made mincemeat out of our troops.
meanings.exampleKorean: 침략군은 우리 군대를 완전히 격파했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive positively or react to with enthusiasm, especially to an excessive degree.
meanings.example: He always goes gaga over his favourite band's newest album.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신이 가장 좋아하는 밴드의 새 앨범에 열광한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return (something) to its origin.
meanings.example: The package was damaged, so I had to send it back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소포가 손상되어 반송해야 했다.

meanings.example: The wound was sewn up by the doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상처는 의사에 의해 봉합되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Sealed by sewing.
meanings.example: The bag was sewn up tightly to prevent anything from escaping.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무엇이든 새어 나오지 않도록 가방은 단단히 바느질로 봉해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To annoy or irritate.
meanings.example: Stop talking so much, you're really getting on my tits!
meanings.exampleKorean: 말 좀 그만해, 정말 짜증나게 하잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To play an all in pot; to commit all of one's chips to a pot.
meanings.example: He decided to stack off his entire chip count on the final hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 핸드에서 가진 칩을 모두 걸기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in an offensive, burdensome, or tiresome manner, with the result that one's continued presence is unwanted within a residence, commercial establishment, or social group.
meanings.example: Don't wear out your welcome by staying too long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 오래 머물러서 환영받지 못하게 되지 않도록 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail at an early stage or never come to fruition, typically due to neglect, infeasibility, or lack of resources.
meanings.example: His ambitious plan to build a flying car died on the vine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 야심찬 비행 자동차 건설 계획은 초기에 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To salt (something) for preservation and then store it away for winter.
meanings.example: "You have more than two thousand salted away, I know, Moses, between prize-money, wages, adventures, and other matters."
meanings.exampleKorean: "모세스, 상금, 임금, 모험 등으로 이천 달러 넘게 저축해 둔 거 알아요."

meanings.englishDefinition: To peel off
meanings.example: Perhaps the surface of the red brick, long unpainted, had scaled off a little more here and there.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아마도 오랫동안 칠하지 않은 붉은 벽돌 표면이 이곳저곳 조금씩 벗겨졌을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ring loudly.
meanings.example: and lending their clear and joyous voices to the wild chorus of the vintage-song that their elders were pealing out
meanings.exampleKorean: 그리고 어른들이 울려 퍼뜨리는 빈티지 노래의 거친 합창에 맑고 즐거운 목소리를 더했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blossom, to show beauty.
meanings.example: "It must be Rena," he murmured. "Who could have dreamed that she would blossom out like that? It must surely be Rena!"
meanings.exampleKorean: "르나일 거야," 그가 중얼거렸다. "누가 그녀가 그렇게 활짝 피어날 거라고 꿈이나 꿨겠어? 분명 르나일 거야!"

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep (someone) happy or compliant, as by encouragement or flattery.
meanings.example: When there was a shipping delay, the salesman jollied the purchasing agent along to keep him from canceling the order.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배송 지연이 발생했을 때, 영업사원은 구매 담당자를 달래서 주문 취소를 막았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steal, especially by surreptitiously removing an unguarded item.
meanings.example: Someone walked off with my umbrella while I was in the shop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 가게에 있는 동안 누군가 내 우산을 훔쳐 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: }} To shirk, avoid, or fail to fulfill (a task, responsibility, etc.)
meanings.example: He tried to weasel out of his commitment to help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕겠다는 약속을 회피하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry up.
meanings.example: “If yeh're goin' to see yer fren', yeh better get a wiggle on. He won't last long.”
meanings.exampleKorean: “친구를 보러 갈 거면 빨리 움직이는 게 좋을 거야. 오래 못 버틸 거야.”

meanings.englishDefinition: To become very angry
meanings.example: He flew into a rage when he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 소식을 듣고 격노했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To notice, observe, learn, or understand, especially something otherwise overlooked.
meanings.example: She quickly picked up on his nervousness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 초조함을 빨리 알아차렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dance.
meanings.example: After finishing their exams, the students went out to kick up their heels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시험을 마친 후, 학생들은 나가서 신나게 놀았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose one's physical or emotional control.
meanings.example: Oh no, my pen's spazzed out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이런, 내 펜이 오작동했어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause someone offense, especially by trying to control something that is under their authority, or interfering in their private affairs.
meanings.example: I'm trying to be careful not to step on anyone's toes with this new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새로운 프로젝트로 누구의 기분도 상하게 하지 않도록 조심하고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rebel against authority; to defy orders or instructions.
meanings.example: He was always kicking over the traces when he was younger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젊었을 때 항상 권위에 반항하곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: for a pitcher to throw a pitch at or near the batter, typically to frighten the batter or to have him stand farther away from home plate.
meanings.example: That pitcher doesn't like the hitters too close to the plate so every once in a while he'll dust off a batter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 투수는 타자가 너무 가까이 오는 것을 싫어해서 가끔 타자를 향해 공을 던진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of information, knowledge or practice; to move slowly up to other levels of an organisation, or population.
meanings.example: There are some brilliant new approaches being created at grass-roots level, but it is taking a long time for them to filter up to the larger institutions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 풀뿌리 수준에서 훌륭한 새로운 접근 방식들이 만들어지고 있지만, 그것이 더 큰 기관으로 서서히 퍼지는 데는 오랜 시간이 걸리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be friendly toward (with or to) someone, especially in an ingratiating way; to form a friendship (with).
meanings.example: I chummed up with a few of my new work colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 새로 온 직장 동료 몇 명과 친하게 지냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to come apart, from the process of rusting.
meanings.example: The old car's muffler had rusted off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 차의 머플러가 녹슬어 떨어져 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait; to falter; to avoid proceeding through reluctance.
meanings.example: From this one poor hunt, then, the best lance out of all Nantucket, surely he will not hang back, when every foremast-hand has clutched a whetstone?
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇다면 이 한 번의 불운한 사냥에서, 난터켓 최고의 창잡이가, 모든 앞잡이들이 숫돌을 움켜쥐었을 때, 분명 망설이지는 않을 것이다?

meanings.englishDefinition: To be threatening, to be imminent.
meanings.example: A shadow of doubt hung over my academic future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 학업적 미래에 대한 의심의 그림자가 드리워져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be connected.
meanings.example: The story does not hang together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 이야기는 일관성이 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically combine or join together by adhesion.
meanings.example: The wet pages of the book began to stick together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책의 젖은 페이지들이 서로 달라붙기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To diverge into two or more separate paths.
meanings.example: The road branches off into two smaller paths.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 도로는 두 개의 더 작은 길로 갈라진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flee rapidly; to run away.
meanings.example: The startled bird flew off into the trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 놀란 새가 나무 속으로 급히 날아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove using a file (the tool).
meanings.example: He had to file off the rough edges of the wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나무의 거친 가장자리를 줄로 갈아내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to stow or secure aloof in a safe place
meanings.example: those the things from the station yesterday? They must've stowed away!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 역에서 가져온 물건들 말이야? 분명 몰래 숨겨둔 게 틀림없어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To attract.
meanings.example: Their concerts draw in big crowds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 콘서트는 많은 관중을 끌어들인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by using a siphon.
meanings.example: The butler drew off wine from a barrel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집사는 통에서 와인을 빼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot out.
meanings.example: The gun fired out a round of bullets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총에서 총알이 발사되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: discard.
meanings.example: She threw aside the old letters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오래된 편지들을 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to remove something by shoveling or digging.
meanings.example: They had to shovel out the snow from the driveway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 차도에서 눈을 삽으로 퍼내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to remove by sweeping or brushing.
meanings.example: Please sweep out the dust from under the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소파 밑의 먼지를 쓸어서 내보내 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to rapidly remove.
meanings.example: He was whisked off to the hospital.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 병원으로 급히 이송되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove something or someone suddenly and quickly.
meanings.example: The garbage truck whisked away the trash bin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쓰레기 트럭이 쓰레기통을 휙 치워갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hatch from an egg.
meanings.example: The chicks will hatch out soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병아리들이 곧 알에서 깨어날 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To heal.
meanings.example: That wound should heal up in a day or two.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상처는 하루 이틀이면 나을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have something transported in by truck.
meanings.example: They trucked in supplies from the nearest town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 가장 가까운 마을에서 트럭으로 보급품을 운반해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send something away by truck.
meanings.example: We will truck out the furniture tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내일 가구를 트럭으로 내보낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build up a supply of (something), usually for use at a particular time in the future, when the time is right.
meanings.example: Animals store up food during the autumn to survive the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 동물들은 겨울을 나기 위해 가을 동안 먹이를 비축한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To press a mound of something against something else.
meanings.example: The farmer banked up the soil around the plants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 식물 주위에 흙을 돋우었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie or sit in a comfortable, relaxed position, especially holding someone else.
meanings.example: The children cuddled up on the sofa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 소파에 바싹 붙어 껴안았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie or sit in a comfortable, relaxed position, holding someone or something else.
meanings.example: I was snuggling up in bed with my blanket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 담요를 껴안고 침대에서 아늑하게 파고들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discontinue an activity, accepting whatever gains or losses one has incurred; to give up.
meanings.example: "Looks like these grandees'll have to cash in their chips and quit, but it's a darned shame."
meanings.exampleKorean: "이 거물들은 포기하고 그만둬야 할 것 같지만, 정말 안타까운 일이다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To become enthusiastic about something that is not yet guaranteed to happen.
meanings.example: I try to set low expectations and not to get my hopes up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 기대를 낮추고 희망을 갖지 않으려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blush (to go red).
meanings.example: She colored up when he complimented her.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그녀를 칭찬하자 그녀는 얼굴을 붉혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: color up.
meanings.example: He coloured up at the unexpected praise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 예상치 못한 칭찬에 얼굴을 붉혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the size of something by filing (using the abrasive effects of a file).
meanings.example: He filed down the rough edges of the wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나무의 거친 가장자리를 줄로 갈아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise (put at a higher level)
meanings.example: They raised up the flag.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 깃발을 들어 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall over to fall from an upright or standing position to a horizontal or prone position.
meanings.example: The vase toppled over and broke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꽃병이 넘어지면서 깨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To request and receive money or goods of value from members of a group, especially for a charitable purpose.
meanings.example: The charity organization decided to take up a collection for the refugees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자선 단체는 난민들을 위해 성금을 모으기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overcome or make uncomfortable by staring intently.
meanings.example: She stared down her opponent in the debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 토론에서 상대를 노려보아 제압했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An abandonment, withdrawal from, or softening of a previously expressed opinion, policy, argument, etc.
meanings.example: The politician had to climb down from his previous statements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 이전 발언에서 물러나야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: It took 2 hours to climb up, but 4 to climb down as our energy grew less and less.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올라가는 데는 2시간이 걸렸지만, 기력이 점점 줄어들어 내려오는 데는 4시간이 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add (an ingredient) by mixing or blending.
meanings.example: You have to blend in the eggs very slowly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 달걀을 아주 천천히 섞어 넣어야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smear or otherwise lubricate with oil or grease; especially, to grease (something, such as a machine or a baking pan) thoroughly.
meanings.example: He greased up the pan before cooking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 요리하기 전에 팬에 기름칠을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: See come and into
meanings.example: Please come into the living room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 거실로 들어오세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bite so hard as to remove something from its source.
meanings.example: She bit off a piece of the bread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 빵 조각을 깨물어 떼어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike a person in the head, causing them to fall to the ground, especially in an unconscious condition; to beat up a person.
meanings.example: He was so angry he threatened to knock his block off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 화가 나서 상대방의 머리를 날려버리겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: knock someone's block off.
meanings.example: If you don't stop talking, I'm going to knock your block off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속 떠들면 네 머리를 날려버릴 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: to scare, to frighten
meanings.example: The strange man with the large head spooked me out so much until I realized it was only a picture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 이상한 남자가 큰 머리를 하고 있어서 사진인 줄 알기 전까지는 나를 너무 겁먹게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to leave, depart
meanings.example: He told the kids to trot off to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아이들에게 잠자리에 들라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to ruin, mess up.
meanings.example: He really queered up my plans for the weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정말로 내 주말 계획을 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to write (something) quickly
meanings.example: She dashed down the important points in her notebook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 중요한 요점들을 공책에 빨리 써 내려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to calculate the costs of something.
meanings.example: We need to cost up the project before we can submit a bid.
meanings.exampleKorean: 입찰을 제출하기 전에 프로젝트 비용을 계산해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to encourage; motivate; to offer encouragement to.
meanings.example: The crowd urged on their team to victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 팀의 승리를 위해 격려했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seek or pursue, especially to determinedly pursue something to one's own benefit.
meanings.example: He is out for all he can get.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 얻을 수 있는 모든 것을 노리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave as if one is in control of; to make a display of having an advantage over or superiority to.
meanings.example: They were in a manner absolute despots in their little domains, lording it, if so disposed, over both law and gospel, and accountable to none but the mother-country.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 작은 영역에서 절대적인 폭군이었으며, 원한다면 법과 복음 모두에 대해 거들먹거렸고, 모국 외에는 누구에게도 책임을 지지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: over; to act as a lord or boss in relation to (someone or something).
meanings.example: But if you think that you can lord over him, then he is going to lord over you — he'll be your lord and master with force.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 당신이 그를 지배할 수 있다고 생각한다면, 그는 당신을 지배할 것입니다. 그는 힘으로 당신의 주인과 상사가 될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to plan, organize, make a plan for something
meanings.example: We need to plan out our vacation itinerary carefully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가 일정을 신중하게 계획해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To jump on top of someone or something quickly.
meanings.example: As football linebackers pile on a quarterback in a blitz, the newspaper editorialist heaped sarcasm onto the president.
meanings.exampleKorean: 축구 라인배커들이 블리츠에서 쿼터백에게 달려들듯이, 신문 논설위원은 대통령에게 비꼬는 말을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dance, especially in a hip hop style.
meanings.example: The DJ played some great music, and everyone started to break it down on the dance floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: DJ가 신나는 음악을 틀었고, 모두 춤추기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw up the sponge.
meanings.example: After weeks of struggling, he finally threw in the sponge and quit the competition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 주간의 어려움 끝에, 그는 마침내 포기하고 대회에서 기권했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: add insult to injury.
meanings.example: Not only did he lose his job, but to heap insult on injury, his car was also stolen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직업을 잃었을 뿐만 아니라, 설상가상으로 차까지 도난당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel sorry for oneself; to feel sadness or regret, especially in combination with self-pity.
meanings.example: Stop crying in your beer and do something about your situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자기 연민에 빠져서 네 상황에 대해 무언가 해봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: cry in one's beer.
meanings.example: He was crying into his beer after failing the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험에 떨어진 후 자기 연민에 빠져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cry in one's beer.
meanings.example: She was weeping in her beer over the breakup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 헤어진 것에 대해 자기 연민에 빠져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cry in one's beer.
meanings.example: He sat alone, weeping into his beer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 혼자 앉아 자기 연민에 빠져 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To release (a product) quickly.
meanings.example: News agencies reported Lavrov had been sent to hospital for a checkup, only for the Russian foreign ministry to rush out a picture of Lavrov in shorts, sporting an Apple watch and Jean-Michel Basquiat-inspired T-shirt, thumbing his notes for his first address at the summit on Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 러시아 외무부는 라브로프가 정상회담에서의 첫 연설을 위해 화요일에 반바지와 애플 워치, 장 미셸 바스키아 스타일의 티셔츠를 입고 메모를 만지작거리는 사진을 서둘러 공개했을 뿐이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inform authorities (about someone); to tell on (somebody).
meanings.example: Don't you dare rat me out or I'll kill you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 밀고할 생각은 꿈도 꾸지 마, 안 그러면 죽여버릴 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To rummage; to pick over.
meanings.example: The police had to rat through the suspect's belongings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 용의자의 소지품을 샅샅이 뒤져야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend one's hand(s) forward; to reach for something.
meanings.example: He reached out to catch the falling snowflake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 떨어지는 눈송이를 잡기 위해 손을 뻗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unreel
meanings.example: The fisherman expertly reeled out the fishing line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낚시꾼은 능숙하게 낚싯줄을 풀어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put onto a reel; to completely reel.
meanings.example: He reeled up the anchor from the seabed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 해저에서 닻을 감아 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive casually at a place.
meanings.example: The guests started to roll in around 7 PM.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 오후 7시경부터 도착하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass; to go on; to elapse.
meanings.example: Time rolls on, and we must adapt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간은 흘러가고, 우리는 적응해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Used to express anticipation
meanings.example: Roll on summer!
meanings.exampleKorean: 여름이 어서 오기를!

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a mother and her new baby; to stay together in the same room.
meanings.example: The hospital allowed the mother to room in with her newborn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병원에서는 산모가 신생아와 함께 머무는 것을 허용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (someone) to become involved in something they are reluctant to do; to draw into something.
meanings.example: India| 68 Years Ago, ‘Criminal’ Tribes Still Fight Stigma, Poverty| March last year, the Supreme Court had set aside its own 2009 judgment and set six convicts free, who were earlier sent to death row. The Supreme Court said the six men were falsely implicated and roped in by the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인도 | 68년 전, '범죄' 부족은 여전히 낙인과 빈곤과 싸우고 있다 | 작년 3월, 대법원은 자체적인 2009년 판결을 기각하고 사형수였던 6명의 죄수를 석방했다. 대법원은 6명의 남성들이 경찰에 의해 거짓으로 연루되었다고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate by ropes; to cordon off.
meanings.example: The police roped off the crime scene to keep people out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사람들의 접근을 막기 위해 범죄 현장에 줄을 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obstruct.
meanings.example: The lorry is blocking off the road, so traffic has come to a standstill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 트럭이 길을 막고 있어서 교통이 완전히 멈췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To skive; to fail to attend school when required to do so.
meanings.example: "I was in the third year at school and I was sagging off with a few of my mates. We were on a building site and two of us got arrested and given a caution. "
meanings.exampleKorean: "나는 학교 3학년이었고 친구 몇 명과 무단결석 중이었다. 우리는 건축 현장에 있었고 우리 중 두 명이 체포되어 경고를 받았다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (something) to flee by frightening it.
meanings.example: A quick growl scared off the mouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재빨리 으르렁거리자 쥐가 겁을 먹고 달아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail; to produce no or very poor results; to drop out of or be eliminated from a competition.
meanings.example: The team bombed out in the first round of the tournament.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 토너먼트 첫 라운드에서 탈락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act in a determined manner by firmly maintaining one's beliefs, demands, situation, etc. in the face of opposition.
meanings.example: She dug in her heels and refused to compromise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 완강히 버티며 타협을 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly turn away from someone or something in order to depart rapidly, especially as expressive of haughtiness, disapproval, or evasiveness.
meanings.example: There he stood, answering shortly and gruffly to all questions proposed to him,... and as soon as the ancient priestess had handed him his glass of the salutiferous water, turned on his heel with a brief good-morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 거기에 서서 제기된 모든 질문에 짧고 무뚝뚝하게 대답했고, 고대의 여사제가 그에게 건강에 좋은 물 한 잔을 건네자마자 짧은 아침 인사와 함께 홱 돌아섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In patent law, to describe the solution to a problem in a way that excludes a particular alternative to solving that problem addressed by a later invention.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 특허법: 후발 발명에서 다룬 문제 해결의 특정 대안을 배제하도록 해결책을 기술하다
meanings.example: The patent claims were written to teach away from using the new material.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 특허 청구항은 새로운 재료의 사용을 피하도록 작성되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticise with excessive and unexpected harshness.
meanings.example: Try to remember next time, but don’t fret — I won’t jump down your throat if you forget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음에는 꼭 기억하도록 노력해, 하지만 걱정하지 마 – 네가 잊더라도 내가 심하게 비난하지는 않을 테니.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry utensils, dishes etc. that have been washed.
meanings.example: Please wipe up the counter after you finish cooking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리가 끝나면 카운터를 닦아서 말려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vigorously attack, either physically or verbally.
meanings.example: The coach laced into the team after their poor performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 팀의 저조한 성적에 대해 맹렬히 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: of a man to marry a woman generally an existing girlfriend; to make a woman one’s wife
meanings.example: He finally decided to wife up his long-time girlfriend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 오랜 여자친구를 아내로 맞이하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add colors to a black-and-white drawing, using colored drawing equipment.
meanings.example: The children love to color in the pictures in their new books.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 새 책에 있는 그림들에 색칠하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To colourise; to add colours to a black-and-white drawing, using coloured drawing equipment.
meanings.example: She spent the afternoon colouring in a picture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오후 내내 그림에 색칠을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach.
meanings.example: We're coming up to the end of the night, so please finish your glasses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 밤이 끝나갈 무렵이니 잔을 비워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: apart
meanings.example: The cheap toy began to come apart after just a few hours of play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 저렴한 장난감은 가지고 논 지 불과 몇 시간 만에 망가지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offer help or information, especially about a crime.
meanings.example: The police are asking witnesses to come forward.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 목격자들이 나서 줄 것을 요청하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become separated from something.
meanings.example: A large piece of bark had come away from the old tree.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 나무에서 커다란 나무껍질 한 조각이 떨어져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish or criticize (someone).
meanings.example: The boss really came down on him for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 그가 늦은 것에 대해 정말 엄하게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to be afflicted by (a visible disruption of the body)
meanings.example: He's come out in spots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 반점이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be subjected to.
meanings.example: If you go into the forest, you'll come in for a nasty surprise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 숲에 들어가면 끔찍한 놀라움을 겪게 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assemble rapidly, without precision.
meanings.example: We threw together a quick meal before the guests arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 도착하기 전에 우리는 급히 식사를 준비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hastily put on (clothes).
meanings.example: He threw on a T-shirt and raced outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 급히 티셔츠를 걸치고 밖으로 달려나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard or give up (a plan or project, etc.). from 19th c.
meanings.example: They decided to throw over the old strategy and try a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오래된 전략을 버리고 새로운 것을 시도하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suffer a series of deaths; to die one by one; to progressively become extinct. (If a group of plants, animals, or people dies off, the entire group dies over a period of time.)
meanings.example: Many species of dinosaurs died off millions of years ago.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 공룡 종들이 수백만 년 전에 멸종했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have dinner away from one's house, usually at a restaurant.
meanings.example: We like to dine out on our anniversary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 기념일에 외식하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place without informing anyone.
meanings.example: He dipped out of the party early without saying goodbye.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 작별 인사도 없이 파티에서 일찍 몰래 빠져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To betray (someone) by informing on them.
meanings.example: I can't believe you dobbed her in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네가 그녀를 밀고했다니 믿을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute in apportioned amounts.
meanings.example: The teacher doled out the worksheets to the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들에게 워크시트를 나누어 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be idle, to shirk one's work or duty.
meanings.example: Stop dossing about and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be idle, to shirk one's work or duty.
meanings.example: He spent his youth dossing around the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 젊은 시절 도시를 빈둥거리며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bend over deeply at the waist.
meanings.example: She was doubled over with laughter after hearing the joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 농담을 듣고 너무 웃겨서 몸을 가누지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower (curtains, a portcullis, etc.).
meanings.example: He drew down the blinds to block out the sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 햇빛을 가리기 위해 블라인드를 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose contact, to lose one's friendship or closeness.
meanings.example: We used to be good friends but we drifted apart over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 좋은 친구였지만 수년에 걸쳐 멀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to talk in a boring manner for a long time.
meanings.example: He droned on about his vacation for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 시간 동안 휴가에 대해 지루하게 늘어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less or fewer.
meanings.example: Eustace gaped at him in amazement. When his urbanity dropped away from him, as now, he had an innocence of expression which was almost infantile. It was as if the world had never touched him at all.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유스터스는 놀라서 그를 멍하니 바라보았다. 지금처럼 그의 세련됨이 사라졌을 때, 그는 거의 어린아이 같은 순수한 표정을 지녔다. 마치 세상이 그를 전혀 건드리지 않은 것 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to reduce pressure; to become less serious
meanings.example: You should ease off him a while, or he might get upset.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 잠시 내버려 둬, 그렇지 않으면 그가 화를 낼지도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach or move toward a target little by little, or furtively.
meanings.example: The charity fund edged up toward its goal as more and more people donated.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 많은 사람들이 기부하면서 자선 기금은 목표를 향해 조금씩 다가갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh uncontrollably.
meanings.example: The audience fell about the place at his jokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들은 그의 농담에 배꼽을 잡고 웃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time, or to fool about.
meanings.example: Stop farting about and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to gain weight by means of feeding.
meanings.example: My cat looks underweight, so I'll fatten him up with higher-calorie cat food.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 고양이가 너무 말라 보여서, 고칼로리 사료로 살을 찌울 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To praise excessively in order to get a favor.
meanings.example: She always fawns over the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진을 위해 항상 상사에게 아첨한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume as part of a diet.
meanings.example: It seems like her boss feeds off her unhappiness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 상사는 그녀의 불행을 먹고 사는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discover something after searching.
meanings.example: The detective ferreted out the truth from the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 용의자로부터 진실을 캐냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: faff about.
meanings.example: Stop faffing around and get to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일하러 가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time; to spend time idly.
meanings.example: He spent the whole afternoon faffing about in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 정원에서 빈둥거리며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fiddle around.
meanings.example: Stop fiddling about with that remote control.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 리모컨을 만지작거리지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To toy or play (with something) in an idle, fidgety, aimless or unstructured way; to mess around (with); to muck around (with).
meanings.example: Stop fiddling around with those matches before you set the place on fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불을 지르기 전에 성냥을 만지작거리지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play the fiddle idly.
meanings.example: There's nothing I like more than fiddling away Saturday night playing dominoes with myself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토요일 밤에 혼자 도미노를 하며 빈둥거리는 것보다 더 좋은 것은 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep from exhaustion or intoxication; to lie down.
meanings.example: John said he could drive me to the airport, but then he flaked out on me, and I had to call a cab at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존은 나를 공항까지 태워다 줄 수 있다고 했지만, 그가 약속을 어기는 바람에 막판에 택시를 불러야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flare; to become inflamed.
meanings.example: The campfire flamed up when he threw more wood on it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 더 많은 장작을 던지자 캠프파이어에 불꽃이 일었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change channels on a television.
meanings.example: The numbers on the odometer flicked over from 999 to 1000.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주행 거리계의 숫자가 999에서 1000으로 바뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To browse rapidly.
meanings.example: I was flicking through the newspaper when I glimpsed a funny headline.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재미있는 헤드라인을 본 순간 신문을 빨리 훑어보고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plump up
meanings.example: She fluffed out her hair to make it look fuller.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 머리를 더 풍성해 보이게 하기 위해 부풀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Stay overnight in a place away from one's home, sleep over.
meanings.example: Can I stay over at your place tonight?
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 너희 집에서 자고 가도 될까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To set the standard.
meanings.example: The company laid down the marker for future projects with this innovative design.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 회사는 이 혁신적인 디자인으로 미래 프로젝트의 기준을 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was caught pawing off in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위행위를 하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become gooey or gummy.
meanings.example: Some types of lubricating grease gum up as they age.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 종류의 윤활 그리스는 오래되면 끈적거리게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: carve out, or cut out a form, or to make something hollow by using heavy cutting tools such as an axe.
meanings.example: The sculptor hewed out a statue from the block of marble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조각가는 대리석 덩어리에서 조각상을 깎아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave quietly and unnoticed.
meanings.example: He slipped out of the room during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의 중에 방에서 몰래 빠져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emerge suddenly; to jump out. from 14th c.
meanings.example: 1790, James Boswell, in Danziger & Brady (eds.), Boswell: The Great Biographer (Journals 1789–1795), Yale 1989, p. 102:
meanings.exampleKorean: 1790년, 제임스 보스웰, 단치거 & 브래디 (편집), 보스웰: 위대한 전기 작가 (일지 1789-1795), 예일 1989, p. 102:

meanings.englishDefinition: To consent to.
meanings.example: You've got to understand this is what Stockman is proposing. The president hasn't bought off on it yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스톡만이 제안하는 것이 이것이라는 걸 이해해야 해요. 대통령은 아직 이에 동의하지 않았어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry, to proceed quickly.
meanings.example: She snapped up the last remaining ticket to the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 콘서트의 마지막 남은 티켓을 재빨리 샀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine scrupulously.
meanings.example: The police combed through the crime scene for any evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 증거를 찾기 위해 범죄 현장을 샅샅이 수색했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine scrupulously.
meanings.example: He combed over the documents to ensure accuracy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정확성을 확인하기 위해 서류를 꼼꼼히 검토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: through
meanings.example: We are in a drought of famous influencers, and it may be here to stay. People just aren’t breaking through online the way they used to, becoming household names and garnering real, mainstream fame.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유명 인플루언서 가뭄이 왔고, 이는 계속될 수도 있다. 사람들은 더 이상 예전처럼 온라인에서 성공하여 유명인이 되고 진정한 주류의 명성을 얻지 못하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make smooth.
meanings.example: She smoothed out the wrinkles in her dress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 드레스의 주름을 폈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To achieve something by a small margin.
meanings.example: The team managed to squeak out a victory in the final seconds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀은 마지막 몇 초 만에 아슬아슬하게 승리를 거두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: The dentist had to suck out the infection from the tooth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치과의사는 치아에서 감염을 빨아내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An instance of winning a hand by [[#Verb|sucking out]].
meanings.example: Poker is not, nor should it be, a passionless, colorless, characterless game; there's too much at stake: money and reputation, perhaps foremost. Miraculous suck-out victories and tragic suck-out beats will always engender a response, good and bad, classy and tacky.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포커는 열정 없고, 색깔 없고, 개성 없는 게임이 아니며, 그래서도 안 된다. 돈과 명성이라는 너무 많은 것이 걸려 있기 때문이다. 기적적인 역전승과 비극적인 역전패는 항상 좋은 쪽이든 나쁜 쪽이든, 품위 있든 없든 반응을 불러일으킬 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find using one's sense of smell.
meanings.example: The dog nosed out the hidden treat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개가 숨겨진 간식을 냄새로 찾아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to exit (outside) in large quantities
meanings.example: People were spilling out of the stadium after the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들이 경기 후 경기장에서 쏟아져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send away, with belongings, for a long time.
meanings.example: I was packed off to boarding school for three months and only saw my parents during the holidays.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 3개월 동안 기숙 학교로 보내졌고 방학 때만 부모님을 볼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill with spectators, passengers etc.
meanings.example: The stadium was packed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기장은 관중으로 가득 찼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate into groups of two.
meanings.example: The students were asked to pair off for the activity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 활동을 위해 둘씩 짝지으라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To superficially hide or disguise a problem or a defect without addressing it; to provide a veneer of normality or success.
meanings.example: They tried to paper over their disagreements with a superficial agreement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 피상적인 합의로 그들의 불화를 덮으려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To frisk; to check someone's body for concealed items, especially weapons.
meanings.example: The security guard had to pat down the suspicious individual.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원은 의심스러운 사람의 몸수색을 해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep working (at something).
meanings.example: She's been pegging away at her novel for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 몇 년 동안 꾸준히 소설을 써왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look through a group or list in order to select what one desires.
meanings.example: He picked through the items at the flea market, looking for a bargain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 싼 물건을 찾으며 벼룩시장의 물건들을 골라보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (e.g. a sheet of paper) on display, using a pin.
meanings.example: She pinned up the notice on the bulletin board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 게시판에 공고문을 핀으로 고정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the pits and go out onto the race track
meanings.example: It was so hot, I was starting to pit out by mid-morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 더워서, 나는 아침 중반쯤에는 피트에서 나와 트랙으로 나가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (a plant) into the ground outside, from a greenhouse or a plant pot.
meanings.example: It's time to plant out the seedlings in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 정원에 모종을 심을 때이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be sexually unfaithful out of one's home
meanings.example: He was accused of playing away while his wife was out of town.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내가 집을 비운 동안 외도했다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a plug or bung into something; to interrupt the flow of.
meanings.example: The drain was plugged up with leaves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배수구가 나뭇잎으로 막혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to do something because of cowardice.
meanings.example: Finally.I thought maybe you were pussying out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 드디어. 네가 겁먹고 포기하는 줄 알았어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mention briefly; to cursorily discuss.
meanings.example: We touched on the subject of wages, but we didn't reach a conclusion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 임금에 대해 간략히 언급했지만 결론에 이르지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To count (a number of things), typically aloud.
meanings.example: The soldiers counted off to ensure everyone was present.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 모두 참석했는지 확인하기 위해 차례대로 셌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit due to tiredness.
meanings.example: Do you poop out at parties?
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에서 지쳐서 그만두니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk, ride, drive etc. at a leisurely pace
meanings.example: We pootled along the coast road, enjoying the scenery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 해안 도로를 따라 경치를 즐기며 느긋하게 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue towards an objective.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, they decided to press ahead with their plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차질에도 불구하고, 그들은 계획을 계속 추진하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (as prey).
meanings.example: Owls prey on small mammals, such as mice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올빼미는 쥐와 같은 작은 포유류를 먹이로 삼는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the price of.
meanings.example: Simply that when marketers think of pricing up, they need to think of themselves as Icarus. Their objectives may be noble, like improving profitability, being able to give their consumers a better product or experience, or being able to spend more on media, but when they get carried away in pricing up, like Icarus, they get burned.
meanings.exampleKorean: 단지 마케터들이 가격 인상을 생각할 때 이카로스처럼 자신을 생각해야 한다는 것이다. 그들의 목표는 수익성 개선, 소비자에게 더 나은 제품이나 경험 제공, 미디어에 더 많은 지출 등 고귀할 수 있지만, 이카로스처럼 가격 인상에 열중하면 타버릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on (clothes) by tugging.
meanings.example: I pulled on my winter coat before stepping out into the snow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 눈 속으로 나가기 전에 겨울 코트를 잡아당겨 입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to jabber; to gabble; to ramble
meanings.example: He could jaw away for hours about his favorite topic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좋아하는 주제에 대해 몇 시간이고 수다를 떨 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flirt with; to approach and speak to (someone), seeking romance, love, sex, etc.
meanings.example: He tried to crack onto her at the bar, but she wasn't interested.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바에서 그녀에게 추근대려 했지만, 그녀는 관심이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: over
meanings.example: We carried her over the bridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그녀를 다리 위로 옮겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport away.
meanings.example: The police carted off the stolen goods.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 도난당한 물품들을 차로 옮겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to count the money taken by a business at the end of the day.
meanings.example: The shopkeeper cashed up at closing time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가게 주인은 문 닫을 시간에 매출을 정산했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: discard.
meanings.example: He cast aside his old habits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 옛 습관들을 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sexually penetrate (or be penetrated) in a vigorous but pleasurable manner.
meanings.example: He wanted to fuck her brains out all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새도록 그녀와 격렬하게 성교하고 싶어 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win (something) that one has previously lost.
meanings.example: He lost $1000 in one hand, and spent most of the night trying to win it back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 한 번에 1000달러를 잃었고, 밤새 그것을 되찾으려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do nothing in particular; to be idle.
meanings.example: He spent his vacation just lazing around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가를 빈둥거리며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do nothing in particular; to be idle.
meanings.example: No, I don’t like work. I had rather laze about and think of all the fine things that can be done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아니, 나는 일을 좋아하지 않아. 차라리 빈둥거리며 할 수 있는 모든 멋진 일들을 생각하는 게 낫겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do nothing in particular; to be idle.
meanings.example: He likes to loaf about town on weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말에 마을을 빈둥거리며 돌아다니는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to do nothing in particular, be idle.
meanings.example: Thanks for coming on such short notice to clean my house. I'm just going to loaf around while you work, but I'll let you know if you're in my way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이렇게 급하게 집 청소하러 와줘서 고마워. 당신이 일하는 동안 나는 그냥 빈둥거릴 건데, 방해가 되면 말해줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mislead, to try to make someone believe a lie.
meanings.example: "But when I fell into the mistake I have so long remained in, at least you led me on?" said I. / "Yes," she returned, again nodding steadily, "I let you go on."
meanings.exampleKorean: "하지만 내가 오랫동안 빠져 있었던 그 실수에 빠졌을 때, 적어도 당신이 나를 잘못 이끈 것 아닌가요?" 내가 말했다. / "네," 그녀는 다시 꾸준히 고개를 끄덕이며 대답했다. "당신이 계속하도록 내버려 뒀어요."

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide (food or drinks) for free.
meanings.example: At the conference, they laid on a wonderful buffet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컨퍼런스에서 그들은 훌륭한 뷔페를 무료로 제공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bow, to bend oneself as a gesture of deference or respect.
meanings.example: 8 Thou shalt not make thee any grauen image, or any likenesse of any thing that is in heauen aboue, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the waters beneath the earth.9 Thou shalt not bow downe thy selfe vnto them, nor serue them: for I the Lord thy God am a ielous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers vpon the children, vnto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me,10 And shewing mercy vnto thousands, of them that loue me, and keepe my commandements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 8 너는 너를 위하여 새긴 우상을 만들지 말고 위로 하늘에 있는 것이나 아래로 땅에 있는 것이나 땅 아래 물 속에 있는 것의 어떤 형상도 만들지 말라. 9 너는 그것들에게 절하지 말며 그것들을 섬기지 말라. 나 너희 하나님 여호와는 질투하는 하나님인즉 나를 미워하는 자의 죄를 갚되 아버지로부터 아들에게로 삼사 대까지 이르게 하거니와 10 나를 사랑하고 내 계명을 지키는 자에게는 천 대까지 은혜를 베푸느니라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spill or pour out, because of a leak.
meanings.example: The secret plan eventually leaked out to the press.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 비밀 계획은 결국 언론에 누설되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To omit.
meanings.example: Please leave off the last chapter of the book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책의 마지막 장은 빼놓아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: I poured some flour into the scoop and levelled it off with a knife.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 국자에 밀가루를 붓고 칼로 수평을 맞췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To render smooth, to remove roughness from
meanings.example: She smoothed her nails down with a file.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 손톱 파일을 이용해 손톱을 다듬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do nothing in particular; to be idle.
meanings.example: I spent the day lying around reading a detective novel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 탐정 소설을 읽으며 하루를 빈둥거리며 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie down by moving in a posterior direction, ending up supine.
meanings.example: He lay back on the sofa and closed his eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소파에 뒤로 기대 눈을 감았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain at a safe distance from (another vessel or hazard)
meanings.example: Try to lie off the cardinal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 항로 표지에서 안전거리를 유지하려고 노력하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Publicly to forbid or suppress (something).
meanings.example: The critics cried down the new play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 새 연극을 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw or write down (something) using chalk.
meanings.example: The coach chalked out the game plan on the board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 칠판에 경기 계획을 분필로 써넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to convert to, to make a transition from one system to another
meanings.example: We need to change over to the new software by next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 다음 주까지 새 소프트웨어로 전환해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose electrical power
meanings.example: The battery will charge down if you leave the lights on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전등을 켜두면 배터리가 방전될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive someone or something away.
meanings.example: The bears were interested in the campers' food, but they chased them off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 곰들이 캠핑족의 음식에 관심을 보였지만, 그들은 곰들을 쫓아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark items on a list (with a checkmark or by crossing them out) that have been chosen for keeping or removal or that have been dealt with (for example, completed or verified as correct or satisfactory).
meanings.example: Please check off the items you have completed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 완료한 항목들을 체크해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: Once you chew on it that way, you start thinking, jeez, maybe QAnon was almost inevitable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 식으로 그것을 곰곰이 생각하기 시작하면, '이런, Q아논은 거의 불가피했는지도 몰라'라고 생각하게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To block something completely and physically.
meanings.example: Too many fallen leaves can easily clog up the drainage pipes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낙엽이 너무 많으면 배수관이 쉽게 막힐 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have fun in an irresponsible manner.
meanings.example: The children were clowning about in the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 공원에서 장난치며 돌아다니고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include (someone) in an activity, etc.
meanings.example: Are you going to the party? Can I count you in?
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티에 갈 거야? 나도 같이 가도 될까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To count (things or people) so as to add up and get a sum total.
meanings.example: Please count up all the money in the register.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계산기의 모든 돈을 세어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extract the cream or creamy part from something.
meanings.example: The company managed to cream off the most profitable part of the business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 사업의 가장 수익성 높은 부분을 빼내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to clean by removing crumbs from
meanings.example: Crumb down the table.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식탁의 부스러기를 털어내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become mentally tougher.
meanings.example: You need to toughen up if you want to survive in this city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 도시에서 살아남으려면 정신적으로 강해져야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: drive or jaunt about in a leisurely way To drive or jaunt about, going from place to place without any specific direction or goal.
meanings.example: We spent the afternoon tooling around the countryside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 내내 시골을 어슬렁거리며 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smoke cannabis
meanings.example: They were sitting in the park, toking up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 공원에 앉아 대마초를 피우고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: occupied; busy
meanings.example: I'm sorry, I can't talk right now, I'm tied up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송해요, 지금 말할 수 없어요. 제가 좀 바빠서요.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: The package was tied up with string.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소포는 끈으로 묶여 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work very hard (at); to toil
meanings.example: He had to slug away at the project for months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 달 동안 그 프로젝트를 위해 열심히 일해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give (thanks, praise) to God.
meanings.example: They gathered to offer up prayers for peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 평화를 위한 기도를 바치기 위해 모였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit a foul by knocking the ball forward.
meanings.example: The player was penalized for knocking on the ball.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 공을 앞으로 치는 반칙으로 페널티를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grow or increase sharply.
meanings.example: Stock prices are expected to rocket up next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주가는 내년에 급증할 것으로 예상된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop interfering; mind one's own business.
meanings.example: The government should butt out of people's private lives.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 사람들의 사생활에 간섭하지 말아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve.
meanings.example: I'll need to buff up on my Italian before my trip to Genoa.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제노아 여행 전에 이탈리아어 실력을 갈고 닦아야 할 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to be shiny or more shiny.
meanings.example: He spent the morning shining up his shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침 내내 구두를 닦으며 광을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose with bricks.
meanings.example: They had to brick in the old doorway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오래된 출입구를 벽돌로 막아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To test (ideas) on someone.
meanings.example: I have a plan to bounce off you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신에게 시험해 볼 계획이 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store up or stock up.
meanings.example: We need to bottle away some food for the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 겨울을 위해 식량을 비축해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To check in (to a hotel).
meanings.example: We can book you in after three o'clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세 시 이후에 예약해 드릴 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A traditionally Māori soup/stew made with potatoes, pork and other ingredients.
meanings.example: The boil up was a hearty meal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보일업은 푸짐한 식사였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook or prepare by boiling.
meanings.example: Can you boil up the vegetables while I baste the meat?
meanings.exampleKorean: 고기 양념하는 동안 채소를 삶아줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To start eating with gusto.
meanings.example: He closed the door and motioned them to a tray. Bog in, it's all on the house.'
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문을 닫고 그들에게 접시를 가리키며 말했다. '맘껏 드세요, 전부 서비스입니다.'

meanings.englishDefinition: To send (children or pets) to stay with other people (or to boarding school, in the case of children).
meanings.example: We decided to board out our dog while we were on vacation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 휴가 동안 개를 위탁하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fire (firearms) repeatedly.
meanings.example: The soldiers were ordered to blaze away at the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적에게 연달아 발사하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to be produced loudly
meanings.example: The music was blaring out from the speakers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악이 스피커에서 요란하게 울려 퍼지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to ruin; spoil; mess up.
meanings.example: He completely bitched up the project with his careless mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부주의한 실수로 프로젝트를 완전히 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move about on the ground while rotating and turning one's body.
meanings.example: Pigs like to roll around in the mud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돼지는 진흙 속에서 뒹구는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disappoint or irritate someone.
meanings.example: Don't piss in my cornflakes by canceling the trip at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 막판에 여행을 취소해서 나를 실망시키지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prostitute, take advantage of, exploit, show off; to hire out or provide to others like a whore.
meanings.example: My father started to whore out my little brother as muscle for his construction company when he was six years old.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 아버지는 여섯 살 때부터 내 남동생을 건설 회사 인력으로 이용하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prostitute, take advantage of, exploit, use, to hire out or provide to others like a whore.
meanings.example: He's known to pimp out his high-end cars for film productions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 영화 제작을 위해 자신의 고급 차량들을 대여해 주는 것으로 알려져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Retract (an envelope of skin or flesh, or an analogous protective sheath).
meanings.example: Carefully skin back the protective film from the new screen.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 화면에서 보호 필름을 조심스럽게 뒤로 젖히세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an adventurous or perilous lifestyle; to behave in a manner which creates risks for oneself.
meanings.example: Skydiving and rock climbing show that he loves to live on the edge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스카이다이빙과 암벽 등반은 그가 위험한 삶을 즐긴다는 것을 보여준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond to an attack by attacking one's opponent or opponents.
meanings.example: The soldiers were prepared to strike back at the enemy camp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적진에 반격할 준비를 갖추고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: mire
meanings.example: He seems to wallow in the mire of his own making.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스스로 만든 수렁에서 헤어나오지 못하는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (or be removed) by washing; to clean off (dirt or the thing that it is besmirching) by washing.
meanings.example: Wash the mud off your boots.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장화에 묻은 진흙을 씻어내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull something out with a sudden jerk.
meanings.example: He whipped out his phone to take a picture of the rare bird.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 희귀한 새의 사진을 찍기 위해 재빨리 휴대폰을 꺼냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's cornflakes.
meanings.example: “Dayum, young blood, who done pissed in your Cheerios?” a voice called out from a big-body Mercedes-Benz S500 that pulled up as Jordan almost reached the corner of his block.
meanings.exampleKorean: “이런, 애송이, 누가 네 기분 망쳤냐?” 조던이 블록 모퉁이에 거의 다다랐을 때 멈춰 선 큰 벤츠 S500에서 목소리가 들려왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: through
meanings.example: A mostly novelized story shot through with some factual names and dates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대부분 소설화되었지만 사실적인 이름과 날짜가 섞인 이야기.

meanings.englishDefinition: to bid goodbye to (someone) with a kiss.
meanings.example: My girlfriend came to kiss me off at the airport.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여자친구가 공항에 와서 키스로 작별 인사를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: keep on truckin'.
meanings.example: unlike those weapons, which could sustain high speeds in the 40s of knots in short bursts, the Type 93, whilst still being somewhat faster than them, could keep on trucking right out to 22,000 yards. At a more mundane 36 knots, it would run twice as far with its near-eleven-hundred-pound warhead. For the first time in the dreadnought era, a nation had a torpedo that could legitimately be fired, not only beyond the practical range of enemy guns, but also beyond the absolute range limits of practically any battleship gun then in service, at least in the slower speed setting, whilst also being near-invisible, due to the almost complete absence of exhaust gasses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 단거리에서 시속 40노트 이상의 고속을 유지할 수 있었던 다른 무기들과 달리, 93식은 그들보다 다소 빠르면서도 22,000야드까지 계속 나아갈 수 있었다. 더 평범한 36노트에서는 거의 1,100파운드에 달하는 탄두로 두 배의 거리를 갈 수 있었다. 전함 시대에 처음으로, 한 국가는 적의 총기 사거리뿐만 아니라 당시 복무 중이던 거의 모든 전함 총기의 절대 사거리 한계를 넘어 발사될 수 있는 어뢰를 보유하게 되었는데, 이는 적어도 느린 속도 설정에서 그랬으며, 배기 가스가 거의 없어 거의 보이지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To excite (people).
meanings.example: Don't let the cat hop up onto the kitchen counter!
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 부엌 조리대로 뛰어오르지 못하게 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw back in fear; cringe, back down, shy away.
meanings.example: The child shrank away from the stranger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 낯선 사람을 보고 겁먹고 움츠러들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prolong; to make longer (in time).
meanings.example: Ashby summit it was difficult to spin out the more than ample schedule allowance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 애쉬비 정상회담에서는 충분한 일정 여유를 늘리는 것이 어려웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: look out; watch out; be vigilant
meanings.example: Mind out! There's a car coming!
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심해! 차가 오고 있어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront; to abash by stern looks.
meanings.example: That old philosopher grew cross, / Who could not tell what motion was; / Because he walked against his will, / He faced men down, that he stood still.
meanings.exampleKorean: 움직임이 무엇인지 말할 수 없었던 그 늙은 철학자는 화가 났다. / 그는 자신의 의지에 반하여 걸었기 때문에, 가만히 서서 사람들을 압도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace something or take someone's place
