meanings.example: As the Medford native was replacing McKennie, Shaq Moore ran off the bench to sub in for Dest. And England manager Gareth Southgate made another move while he could, Marcus Rashford for Bukayo Saka.
meanings.exampleKorean: 메드퍼드 출신이 맥케니를 대신하면서, 샤킬 무어가 벤치에서 나와 데스트를 대신해 투입되었다. 그리고 잉글랜드 감독 가레스 사우스게이트는 할 수 있는 동안 또 다른 선수 교체를 단행했는데, 부카요 사카 대신 마커스 래시포드를 투입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: substitute, verb To replace with a substitute: to substitute (something or someone).
meanings.example: The coach decided to sub out the tired player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 지친 선수를 교체하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: copiously decorated with jewelry such as precious stones and metals
meanings.example: The rapper appeared on stage with an iced out watch and necklace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 래퍼는 보석으로 화려하게 장식된 시계와 목걸이를 차고 무대에 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: The entire lake was iced out overnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤새 호수 전체가 얼음으로 뒤덮였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to take one or more steps forward, especially in response to a request for identification
meanings.example: Private Johnson, step forward!
meanings.exampleKorean: 존슨 이병, 앞으로 나오시오!

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate (the best part of something from the remainder).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 가장 좋은 것을 빼내다, 이득을 챙기다
meanings.example: was also the influx of a third of a million road lorries, sold at giveaway prices after their war roles ceased and used by competing one-man businesses to skim off sundry agricultural freight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁 역할을 마친 후 헐값에 팔려 경쟁적인 1인 사업체에서 잡다한 농산물 운송 이익을 챙기는 데 사용된 30만 대의 트럭이 유입된 것도 마찬가지였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss (batsmen) quickly.
meanings.example: His team was skittled out for 34.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 팀은 34점으로 모두 퇴장당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to show, to be visible.
meanings.example: A small mouse peeped out from behind the curtain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 쥐 한 마리가 커튼 뒤에서 빼꼼 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn off, to cut the power to.
meanings.example: Please power off your mobile phones before the movie starts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화가 시작되기 전에 휴대폰 전원을 꺼주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn on (an electrical device).
meanings.example: You need to power on the computer before you can use it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 컴퓨터를 사용하려면 먼저 전원을 켜야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: When one is in the middle of a process, activity, or conversation, to adjust rapidly, effectively, and intelligently to new developments or changing circumstances.
meanings.example: "There will be wine," said Medora. "Drink it. There may be toasts. Be ready to respond."Abner could think on his feet—speech would not fail.
meanings.exampleKorean: "와인이 있을 거예요. 마셔요. 축사가 있을지도 몰라요. 응답할 준비를 하세요." 메도라가 말했다. 애브너는 임기응변에 능했고, 말실수는 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform godlike or superhuman feats.
meanings.example: He may be rich and influential, but he can't walk on water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부유하고 영향력이 있을지 모르지만, 초인적인 능력을 발휘할 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually increase speed or output.
meanings.example: The car moved through the gears smoothly as it accelerated.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가속하면서 차는 부드럽게 기어를 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep (someone or something) from entering.
meanings.example: The security guard barred out unauthorized personnel from the building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원은 허가받지 않은 인원의 건물 출입을 막았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To acquire knowledge or develop evidence that reveals the truth about someone's character or behavior, especially criminal behavior.
meanings.example: "Bob, they've got the goods on you. There's a warrant out."
meanings.exampleKorean: "밥, 네 약점을 잡았어. 영장이 나왔어."

meanings.englishDefinition: get the goods on.
meanings.example: The detective finally got the goods on the suspect after weeks of surveillance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 몇 주간의 감시 끝에 마침내 용의자의 약점을 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in sex with various partners; to sleep around; to cheat on a sexual partner.
meanings.example: He was accused of catting around while his wife was away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아내가 집을 비운 동안 바람을 피웠다는 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) black or darker.
meanings.example: Cantor starts blacking up, right then and there, painting his face with a burnt piece of cork.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캔터는 바로 거기서 얼굴에 탄 코르크 조각을 칠하며 검게 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To affix a notice, announcement etc, to a post, board, wall or the like.
meanings.example: If the bailiff does not find the witness at his home, he will post up a court warrant there instead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집행관이 증인을 그의 집에서 찾지 못하면, 대신 법원 영장을 거기에 게시할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to reduce the intensity of (a fire)
meanings.example: years after England’s World Cup victory, Stiles was at Wembley again to help Manchester United become the first English team to win the European Cup final. Again Eusébio was one of his opponents, playing for Benfica, and again Stiles was detailed to keep him quiet. Although he was not as comprehensively successful in damping down his man the second time around, he still did a perfectly satisfactory job – and United won 4-1.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잉글랜드의 월드컵 우승 후 몇 년이 지나 스토일스는 맨체스터 유나이티드가 유러피언 컵 결승에서 우승하는 첫 잉글랜드 팀이 되도록 돕기 위해 웸블리에 다시 갔다. 에우제비오는 다시 그의 상대 중 한 명이었고, 벤피카에서 뛰고 있었으며, 스토일스는 다시 그를 조용히 시키라는 임무를 받았다. 비록 두 번째에는 그의 상대를 완전히 제압하는 데는 성공하지 못했지만, 그는 여전히 완벽하게 만족스러운 일을 해냈고, 유나이티드는 4-1로 승리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act defensively.
meanings.example: After the criticism, she went into her shell and refused to speak to anyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비판을 받은 후, 그녀는 움츠러들었고 아무에게도 말하려 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend a lot of money on something desired but not necessary.
meanings.example: I decided to splash out on a trip around Europe after getting a nice Christmas bonus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 크리스마스 보너스를 받은 후 유럽 여행에 돈을 흥청망청 쓰기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: I have my eye on you, so you'd better not cause any trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네게 눈독 들이고 있으니, 문제 일으키지 않는 게 좋을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: come back to bite.
meanings.example: His decision to invest all his savings in that risky stock is really biting him in the arse now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 모든 저축을 그 위험한 주식에 투자하기로 한 결정이 지금 그에게 좋지 않은 결과를 가져오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to increase the size of a cartridge's chamber and calibre.
meanings.example: The gunsmith decided to neck up the rifle's chamber for a larger caliber bullet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총기 제작자는 더 큰 구경의 총알을 위해 소총의 약실을 늘리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To upgrade to a higher technology level.
meanings.example: The company plans to tech up its manufacturing process next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 내년에 제조 공정을 기술적으로 향상시킬 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tidy up, to remove mess or obstacles from a place to make it neat.
meanings.example: Further along the gallery was the huge skeleton barrel of a brontosaurus. My museum hypothesis was confirmed. Going toward the side of the gallery I found what appeared to be sloping shelves, and clearing away the thick dust, I found the old familiar glass cases of our own time. But these must have been air-tight to judge from the fair preservation of some of their contents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갤러리를 더 따라가자 거대한 브론토사우루스의 뼈대가 있었다. 나의 박물관 가설이 확인되었다. 갤러리 옆쪽으로 가자 경사진 선반처럼 보이는 것을 발견했고, 두꺼운 먼지를 치우자 우리 시대의 익숙한 유리 진열장을 발견했다. 하지만 내용물의 보존 상태로 보아 이것들은 밀폐되었음에 틀림없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal cards to someone entering a game; to enter someone into a game by dealing cards to that person.
meanings.example: We can deal you in on the robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 너를 강도 사건에 끌어들일 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: not to deal cards to someone who is leaving a game.
meanings.example: Deal me out of this round: I have to make a phone call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 판은 나 빼고 해줘: 전화할 게 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shout or cheer.
meanings.example: The crowd howled out their support for the home team.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중들은 홈팀을 향해 환호성을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep at a distance
meanings.example: The security guards were ordered to pin back the fans who were trying to get closer to the celebrities.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원들은 연예인들에게 더 가까이 다가가려는 팬들을 멀리 떼어놓으라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter into a condition or situation which is favorable; to develop in a pleasing or advantageous manner.
meanings.example: Despite the initial setbacks, the project eventually came up roses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초기 좌절에도 불구하고, 그 프로젝트는 결국 성공적으로 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become lost; to lose one's way.
meanings.example: I didn’t do well in cities and remembered why as I crawled up roads at snail’s pace and got turned around by one-way streets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 도시에서 길을 잘 찾지 못했고, 달팽이처럼 느린 속도로 도로를 기어가다가 일방통행 도로 때문에 방향을 잘못 잡았던 이유를 기억했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach one's highest point.
meanings.example: The stock market is expected to top out by the end of the year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주식 시장은 연말까지 최고점에 도달할 것으로 예상된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in the temporary possession or control (of).
meanings.example: All job applications pass through the hands of my secretary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 지원서는 내 비서를 거쳐간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To menstruate; to be on one's period.
meanings.example: She couldn't go swimming because she had the painters in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 생리 중이라 수영을 갈 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust the spacing of in-between frames so as to smoothly decelerate movement of an object as it comes to rest
meanings.example: The animator used slow in to make the character's landing appear more natural.
meanings.exampleKorean: 애니메이터는 캐릭터의 착지가 더 자연스럽게 보이도록 감속 구간을 조정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adjust the spacing of intermediate frames so as to smoothly accelerate movement of an object as it leaves a resting position.
meanings.example: The animation used slow out to show the character smoothly getting up from the chair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 애니메이션은 캐릭터가 의자에서 부드럽게 일어나는 것을 보여주기 위해 가속 구간을 조정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to find; look around; search.
meanings.example: The hounds cast about for the fox's trail.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥개들이 여우의 흔적을 찾아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hover an RFID device such as a smartcard over a receiver, often with a graphical user interface, in order to make a payment or gain entry.
meanings.example: Just tag on your card as you enter the subway station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지하철역에 들어갈 때 카드를 단말기에 대기만 하면 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hover an RFID device such as a smartcard over a receiver, often with a graphical user interface, in order to confirm the end of use or one's exit from the vehicle.
meanings.example: Remember to tag off when you get off the bus.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버스에서 내릴 때 태그를 끄는 것을 잊지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go for a ride with another or others; especially, to join police officers on their patrol in a patrol car.
meanings.example: My son asked if he could ride along with us to the park.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아들이 공원까지 우리와 함께 타도 되냐고 물었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ridealong.
meanings.example: The journalist was granted a ridealong with the police on their night shift.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기자는 경찰의 야간 근무에 동승 취재를 허가받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Something unexpectedly said or done which causes shock or offense; an insult, rebuke or rebuff.
meanings.example: Losing the competition was a real slap in the face for the ambitious athlete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대회에서 패배한 것은 야심 찬 선수에게 정말 큰 충격이자 모욕이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To insult or offend unnecessarily.
meanings.example: the election of George Bush this country has apparently chosen an answer to the AIDS crisis consisting of the most reactionary social principles. Adding insult to injury, it is a response which makes a show of slapping all marginalized groups in the face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조지 부시의 당선으로 이 나라는 에이즈 위기에 대해 가장 반동적인 사회 원칙으로 이루어진 해답을 선택한 것으로 보인다. 설상가상으로, 이는 모든 소외된 집단을 모욕하는 듯한 대응이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To indulge (someone); to bolster, encourage (a person, feeling etc.).
meanings.example: His parents always cockered him up, which made him spoiled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 부모님은 항상 그의 응석을 받아주었고, 그것이 그를 버릇없게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To corrode metallic plates by means of an acid, as part of an etching process.
meanings.example: The acid will bite in the metal surface to create the desired pattern.
meanings.exampleKorean: 산이 원하는 패턴을 만들기 위해 금속 표면을 부식시킬 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remind people of (something, usually unpleasant, from the past).
meanings.example: I don't know why John had to drag up the incident of the car accident. It was really embarrassing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존이 왜 그 교통사고 사건을 들추어내야 했는지 모르겠다. 정말 창피했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To belittle or intimidate.
meanings.example: He tried to do down his opponent by spreading rumors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소문을 퍼뜨려 상대를 깎아내리려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to decorate, tidy or clean
meanings.example: We need to do out the house before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들이 오기 전에 집을 청소해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To figure out, to find out, find, decipher
meanings.example: The detective finally managed to dope out the killer's motive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 마침내 살인자의 동기를 알아낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (other people or another person) to suffer the same fate, or to feel the same negative mood.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 다른 사람을 같은 운명에 처하게 하거나 부정적인 기분을 느끼게 하다
meanings.example: I feel bad for him, but at the same time I cannot let him drag me down into his depths of negativity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 안쓰럽지만, 동시에 그의 부정적인 심연으로 나를 끌어내리게 할 수는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: Don't drag me into your argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 싸움에 나를 끌어들이지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be last in a moving line of people, to walk or go behind others in a line.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 행렬의 맨 뒤에 서다, 줄에서 다른 사람들 뒤에 걷거나 가다
meanings.example: The slowest runners brought up the rear of the marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 느린 주자들이 마라톤 대열의 맨 뒤를 이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cast about.
meanings.example: She cast around for a solution to her problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 문제에 대한 해결책을 찾아보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to visit someone's home or other location.
meanings.example: Come on over to my place for dinner tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 저녁 우리 집에 와서 저녁 식사하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become listless, vacant, or unresponsive.
meanings.example: After staring at the screen for hours, I started to zombie out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 시간 동안 화면을 쳐다본 후, 나는 멍해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seize, convulse or twitch, especially when sudden.
meanings.example: The patient began to seize out uncontrollably.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환자가 통제할 수 없이 발작하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: forward
meanings.example: with the Olympics approaching, the Greek government has grown increasingly heavy-handed in its efforts to move the project forward.
meanings.exampleKorean: 올림픽이 다가옴에 따라 그리스 정부는 프로젝트를 추진하기 위한 노력에 점점 더 강경해지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To advance toward success.
meanings.example: She is very ambitious and determined to get ahead in her career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 매우 야심차고 경력에서 성공하기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or circulate in a confused or disorderly manner within a limited area.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 한정된 지역 내에서 혼란스럽거나 무질서하게 움직이거나 돌아다니다
meanings.example: The crowd began to mill about nervously as they waited for news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군중은 소식을 기다리며 초조하게 서성거리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to listen to, endure, or accept something, nodding in agreement without making comment.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말없이 고개를 끄덕이며 듣거나, 참거나, 받아들이다
meanings.example: He just nodded through the entire presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 발표 내내 말없이 고개만 끄덕였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart in an angry mood.
meanings.example: After the argument, she stomped off without saying goodbye.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논쟁 후, 그녀는 작별 인사도 없이 씩씩거리며 나가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to severely defeat.
meanings.example: The team stomped on their opponents in the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 결승전에서 상대 팀을 압도적으로 이겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slacken or lessen; to become less intense.
meanings.example: The rain began to slacken off in the afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비는 오후에 잦아들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something more interesting, fun, lively, etc.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무언가를 더 흥미롭고 재미있고 활기차게 만들다
meanings.example: The new manager tried to ginger up the team's morale.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 감독은 팀의 사기를 북돋우려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate off from the main body; to move off to one side (as in troop movements on a parade ground or in an organized retreat, or columns in a procession).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 주요 부대에서 분리되다; 옆으로 이동하다 (연병장에서의 부대 이동이나 조직적인 후퇴, 행렬의 열 등에서)
meanings.example: minutes later, Mertesacker peeled away from Gallas to meet Walcott's right-wing cross with a superb header.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 분 후, 메르테사커는 월콧의 오른쪽 크로스를 받아 멋진 헤더를 하기 위해 갈라스에게서 떨어져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To train or educate.
meanings.example: am impressed, young Frank,” began the master criminal. “Breaking into my house in the dead of night. You are a boy after my own heart. You need to come and live here with me and your mother. I could be the father you never had. I could train you up. Teach you everything I know. You could become a master criminal like me. One day all this could be yours.”
meanings.exampleKorean: “인상 깊군, 젊은 프랭크,” 거물 범죄자가 말했다. “한밤중에 내 집에 침입하다니. 자네는 내 마음에 드는 소년이야. 나와 자네 어머니와 함께 여기 와서 살아야 해. 내가 자네가 가져보지 못한 아버지가 되어줄 수 있어. 내가 자네를 훈련시킬 수 있지. 내가 아는 모든 것을 가르쳐 줄 수 있어. 자네는 나와 같은 거물 범죄자가 될 수 있어. 언젠가 이 모든 것이 자네 것이 될 수 있지.”

meanings.englishDefinition: To include (the costs of a possible future event, especially a negative one) in an estimation of the total value of something.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 어떤 것의 총 가치 추정치에 (특히 부정적인 미래 사건의 비용을) 포함하다
meanings.example: The recent interest rate change was anticipated and has been priced in to the value of the stock by the market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최근의 금리 변화는 예상되었으며 시장에서 주식 가치에 반영되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time on a pointless or fruitless task; do something that is ineffective or counterproductive.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무익하거나 결실 없는 일에 시간을 낭비하다; 비효율적이거나 역효과를 낳는 일을 하다
meanings.example: You can make a complaint if you like, but you'll just be pissing in the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 원하면 불평할 수 있지만, 그것은 시간 낭비일 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extract or remove by threshing.
meanings.example: We need to thresh out all the details of the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 계획의 모든 세부 사항을 철저히 논의해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To participate in an activity, especially a successful or attractive one.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활동에 참여하다, 특히 성공적이거나 매력적인 활동에 참여하다
meanings.example: The scramble for this business is already on; everybody wants to get into the act. More than 370 American concerns have applied for franchises to give service over foreign and domestic airways.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 사업을 차지하려는 경쟁이 이미 시작되었고, 모두가 참여하고 싶어 합니다. 370개 이상의 미국 기업이 국내외 항공편 서비스를 제공하기 위한 프랜차이즈를 신청했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain participation in an activity, especially an attractive one.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 활동에 참여하다, 특히 매력적인 활동에 참여하다
meanings.example: Mr. Stetzle sighed. "Gee, I wish I could get in on something like that. Running a chopmill is a mighty slow way to get rich."
meanings.exampleKorean: 스테츨레 씨는 한숨을 쉬었다. "이런 일에 참여할 수 있다면 좋을 텐데. 제재소 운영은 부자가 되는 아주 느린 방법이야."

meanings.englishDefinition: To don a strap-on.
meanings.example: she strapped on and was the aggressor when she was with a woman, she was definitely the woman when she had sex with men.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 스트랩온을 착용했고 여성과 있을 때는 공격적이었으며, 남성과 성관계를 가질 때는 확실히 여성이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something larger fit in a smaller or tight place with brute force
meanings.koreanDefinition: 무력으로 더 큰 것을 더 작거나 좁은 곳에 맞게 하다
meanings.example: He had to force in the last suitcase into the trunk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마지막 여행 가방을 트렁크에 억지로 밀어 넣어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go into (a cramped space); to barely fit into.
meanings.example: We had to squeeze into the small elevator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 작은 엘리베이터에 비집고 들어가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take up the slack.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 느슨해진 것을 메우다, 부족한 것을 보충하다
meanings.example: When she got sick, her colleagues had to pick up the slack.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 아팠을 때 동료들이 부족한 부분을 메워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To communicate something beyond the level of comprehension of the target.
meanings.example: The professor's lecture was talking over the students' heads.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교수님의 강의는 학생들의 이해 수준을 넘어서고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To research a particular subject.
meanings.example: She is going to dig into Egyptian basket-weaving this semester.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 이번 학기에 이집트 바구니 짜기 연구에 깊이 파고들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose courage or cause to lose courage.
meanings.example: Ministers and a good many of their supporters worked in relays, and had so manned down the feeble and numerically small Opposition till we had not strength nor mental energy enough to resist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장관들과 그들의 많은 지지자들은 교대로 일했고, 약하고 수적으로 적은 야당의 사기를 저하시켜 우리가 저항할 힘이나 정신적 에너지가 충분하지 않을 때까지 그렇게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or depart.
meanings.example: The toddler toddled off to his room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기가 자기 방으로 아장아장 걸어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To present (food or drink) to those who will consume it; to serve.
meanings.example: She serves up pizza at nights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 밤에 피자를 차려낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scream out to one's full capacity.
meanings.example: The children screamed their heads off when they saw the roller coaster.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 롤러코스터를 보고 목청껏 비명을 질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act foolishly.
meanings.example: Stop fooling up and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 바보짓 그만하고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: to attain one's goal in the end, despite obstacles along the way
meanings.example: With hard work and determination, they will win through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 열심히 노력하고 결단력이 있다면, 그들은 성공할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tilt something upward in order to discharge its contents.
meanings.example: He tipped up the bucket to empty it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 양동이를 기울여 비웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a grading system based on the scale of performance of a group to normalize a right-skewed grade distribution (with more lower scores) into a bell curve, so that more can receive higher grades, regardless of their actual knowledge of the subject; to curve a grade.
meanings.example: He asked the teacher to grade (the test) on a curve.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 선생님에게 시험 점수를 상대평가해달라고 요청했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To circumvent, to bypass, to go around in order to avoid.
meanings.example: Yet the reality in Japan was far more gruesome than the artful depiction in the film, which skirts around the human suffering caused by the bomb.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 영화의 교묘한 묘사와 달리 일본의 현실은 폭탄으로 인한 인간의 고통을 외면하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surrender or resign, especially in a military context.
meanings.example: Admiral Middlebrooks laid down his flag as a result of the humiliating defeat at the hands of France.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미들브룩스 제독은 프랑스에게 굴욕적인 패배를 당한 결과 항복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put something in an unstable position by failing to give it a secure foundation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 모래 위에 집을 짓다, 기초가 부실하다
meanings.example: Loveless marriages often end in divorce because they are built on sand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사랑 없는 결혼은 기초가 부실하기 때문에 종종 이혼으로 끝난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study intensely, swot
meanings.example: There appears to be something essential about that. I search my memory for every reference to decapitation found in a lifetime of nerding out at the library
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것에 대해 필수적인 무언가가 있는 것 같다. 나는 도서관에서 평생을 박식함을 뽐내며 찾은 참수와 관련된 모든 참조를 기억에서 더듬어 본다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate or otherwise make more attractive.
meanings.example: He was all spiffed up, this chap, decked out in a spotless white shirt and a bow tie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 얼룩 하나 없는 흰 셔츠와 나비 넥타이를 맨 채 멋지게 차려입고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten (a criminal etc.) to a wheel and break his limbs or beat him to death.
meanings.example: The criminal was sentenced to break on the wheel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 범죄자는 바퀴에 묶여 고문형을 선고받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go by airplane to a destination.
meanings.example: then the summer surge was already dwindling, as travellers switched to the roads or jetted off to beach resorts abroad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러자 여름철의 붐이 이미 줄어들고 있었고, 여행객들은 도로로 향하거나 해외 해변 휴양지로 비행기를 타고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To berate.
meanings.example: Pat's Irish but don't hold it against him— he's likely to take your head off.'
meanings.exampleKorean: 팻은 아일랜드인이지만 그를 탓하지 마라. 그는 당신을 심하게 꾸짖을 가능성이 높다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a liquid, especially tea, coffee, or beer) by brewing.
meanings.example: Gavin's tank brewed up on the first hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개빈의 탱크는 첫 번째 타격으로 작동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: procrastinate, fool around, behave immaturely bowdlerization of fuck around
meanings.example: Stop joking around and focus on your work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난 그만치고 일에 집중해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To snoop.
meanings.example: She's been snooping around asking questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 주변을 살금살금 돌아다니며 질문하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contract or shrink completely; shrivel up.
meanings.example: The cold made his skin shrink up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 추위 때문에 그의 피부가 오그라들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rub one's body against another person as a form of sexual stimulation or flirting.
meanings.example: Had to let it know that I still had baller status. So when I was on the dance floor, busting my moves, I didn't mind the shorty with the tight, tight blue mini-skirt who was rubbing up on me and leading me on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 여전히 멋진 사람이라는 것을 알게 해야 했다. 그래서 춤을 추고 있을 때, 나는 꽉 끼는 파란색 미니스커트를 입은 여자가 내 몸에 비비며 나를 유혹하는 것을 신경 쓰지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove paint or light scratches from the surface by friction, wearing it out by polishing or blasting with sand or by employing some similar method.
meanings.example: You can buff out minor scratches on the car with a special polish.
meanings.exampleKorean: 특수 광택제로 자동차의 작은 흠집을 없앨 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: conk out.
meanings.example: The old car finally konked out on the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 차는 결국 고속도로에서 멈춰버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: laugh in one's sleeve.
meanings.example: He tried to hide his amusement, but I could see him laughing up his sleeve.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 웃음을 참으려 했지만, 나는 그가 속으로 웃는 것을 볼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to laugh in secret or to oneself, usually gloatingly
meanings.example: She was laughing in her sleeve at his misfortune.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 불행을 보고 속으로 웃고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sink one's teeth into.
meanings.example: This is a project I can really get my teeth into.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것은 내가 정말 몰두할 수 있는 프로젝트다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or search erratically, especially as if hurried or confused.
meanings.example: My mother warned me not to squirrel around in my dad's workshop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니는 아버지의 작업실에서 이리저리 돌아다니지 말라고 경고하셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To verify through brief investigation or revisit for assurance that there are no problems.
meanings.example: I dropped by to check up on my friend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 친구를 확인하러 들렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or eject from a place.
meanings.example: I'm feeling a bit pipped this morning—got turfed out of bed at seven o'clock and all that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 아침 좀 짜증 나—아침 7시에 침대에서 쫓겨났거든.

meanings.englishDefinition: To focus excessively on one's plans or on prospective future events without paying adequate attention to the present.
meanings.example: He's already planning his victory speech, but he needs to get ahead of himself and focus on the upcoming match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이미 승리 연설을 계획하고 있지만, 그러기 전에 다가올 경기에 집중해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put one over.
meanings.example: He tried to get one over on his boss by faking an illness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아픈 척하며 상사를 속이려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean by removing material with a rake.
meanings.example: Please rake out the stables tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 마구간을 갈퀴로 긁어내 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To know an area.
meanings.example: London black cab drivers are obliged to undergo a nearly 3-year-long examination known as the Knowledge to prove that they know their way around the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 런던의 택시 기사들은 도시 지리에 익숙하다는 것을 증명하기 위해 '더 지식'이라는 거의 3년간의 시험을 거쳐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dent, bend, or injure.
meanings.example: Be careful not to ding up the car when you park it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주차할 때 차에 흠집을 내지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed or move forward with more urgency, especially when facing adversity.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, the team decided to press forward with their project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좌절에도 불구하고, 팀은 프로젝트를 계속 추진하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make more attractive in a superficial way, typically by adding flashy ornaments or jewelry.
meanings.example: She decided to bling out her new phone with stickers and charms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 휴대폰을 스티커와 장식품으로 화려하게 꾸미기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a visual element) gray or lighter in colour to indicate that it is disabled or unavailable.
meanings.example: The option to save will gray out if you haven't made any changes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변경 사항이 없으면 저장 옵션이 회색으로 비활성화됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: gray out.
meanings.example: The save button will grey out if no changes are made.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변경 사항이 없으면 저장 버튼이 회색으로 비활성화됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fritter away.
meanings.example: He frit away his inheritance on gambling and fast cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 유산을 도박과 빠른 차에 낭비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unnoticeable, by staying visible in a setting that masks presence.
meanings.example: I have satisfied myself that she is alive, and apparently well, and hiding in plain sight. Prudence prevents me from saying where.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 살아있고, 보아하니 괜찮으며, 뻔히 보이는데 숨어있다는 것을 스스로 확인했다. 신중함 때문에 나는 어디인지 말할 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed into oral expression in a manner which is fluent, appealing, or glib.
meanings.example: Her name is difficult to pronounce, but her song lyrics really roll off the tongue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 이름은 발음하기 어렵지만, 그녀의 노래 가사는 정말 입에 착 붙는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: roll off the tongue.
meanings.example: The phrase 'I love you' should roll off one's tongue easily.
meanings.exampleKorean: '사랑해'라는 말은 쉽게 입에서 나와야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: roll off the tongue.
meanings.example: His name is hard to remember, but the phrase 'the brilliant scientist' trips off the tongue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이름은 기억하기 어렵지만, '그 천재 과학자'라는 말은 입에 착 붙는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly and decisively defeat someone, either in a physical fight or, figuratively, in a competition.
meanings.example: Our team is so much better; we're going to beat the pants off them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 팀이 훨씬 더 잘해; 그들을 완전히 이길 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly and decisively defeat someone, either in a physical fight or, figuratively, in a competition.
meanings.example: The underdog team managed to lick the pants off the champions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약체 팀이 챔피언을 완전히 이기는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: beat the pants off.
meanings.example: He's so skilled, he can whip the pants off any opponent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매우 능숙해서 어떤 상대든 압도할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: tell tales out of school.
meanings.example: Don't talk out of school about the surprise party; it's supposed to be a secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깜짝 파티에 대해 비밀을 누설하지 마; 비밀로 하기로 했잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a stage of development or maturity where one has achieved strength and confidence, economic security, or respect and social acceptance.
meanings.example: After years of practice, she finally came into her own as a pianist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간의 연습 끝에 그녀는 마침내 피아니스트로서 자신의 진가를 발휘하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to find by chance
meanings.example: Yesterday I happened on the solution while we were seeing a movie on the couch; such things just happen along, by sheer happenstance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 소파에서 영화를 보다가 우연히 해결책을 발견했어; 그런 일은 정말 우연히 일어나는 법이지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become less impassioned, aggressive, or argumentative; to exercise restraint; to yield or capitulate.
meanings.example: He's very opinionated, but he needs to learn to pull in his horns sometimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기주장이 강하지만, 때로는 자제할 줄 알아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull in one's horns.
meanings.example: You need to pull your horns in and stop arguing so aggressively.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자제하고 공격적으로 논쟁하는 것을 멈춰야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull in one's horns.
meanings.example: The company decided to draw in its horns and focus on core business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 자제하고 핵심 사업에 집중하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull in one's horns.
meanings.example: He realized he was being too pushy and decided to draw his horns in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 너무 강압적이라는 것을 깨닫고 자제하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bring down the house.
meanings.example: He sits back in anticipation of bringing the house down, but there’s merely silence. Until pretty much everyone chimes in with: ‘So, Horner’s syndrome then?’
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 대성공을 거둘 것을 기대하며 뒤로 물러섰지만, 침묵만이 흘렀다. 거의 모든 사람이 '호너 증후군인가요?'라고 말할 때까지.

meanings.englishDefinition: pour one's heart out.
meanings.example: She needed a friend to pour out her heart to after the breakup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 헤어진 후 마음을 털어놓을 친구가 필요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut the face of (a hedge) on one side so as to lay bare the principal upright stems of the plants.
meanings.example: If the fence be old and ragged at the bottom, all the strong stems are sawn off near the roots; and when there are any young slender shoots, they are reserved for the purpose of plashing or laying, and are fastened down with short hook pegs, the cop having been previously "breasted up," as it is termed, with the contents of the ditch; if there should not be young wood enough to form a sufficient fence to the field, a small beard of thorns is put along the top of the cop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 울타리가 아래쪽이 낡고 너덜너덜하다면, 모든 튼튼한 줄기는 뿌리 근처에서 잘라내고, 젊고 가는 새순이 있다면 그것들을 엮거나 눕히는 데 사용하기 위해 남겨두고 짧은 갈고리 말뚝으로 고정한다. 도랑의 내용물로 덮개가 미리 '다듬어진' 상태라고 불린다. 밭에 충분한 울타리를 형성할 만큼 젊은 나무가 충분하지 않다면, 가시나무 수염을 덮개 상단에 놓는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually reduce the size or scale of.
meanings.example: The government plans to phase down subsidies for fossil fuels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 화석 연료 보조금을 점진적으로 축소할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be adversely affected by ice, and not function.
meanings.example: these precautions lost some of their merit when the L.T. trains themselves became frozen up at depots overnight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이러한 예방 조치들은 L.T. 열차 자체가 밤새 차고에서 얼어붙었을 때 그 가치를 일부 잃었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To freeze on the surface.
meanings.example: The lake in the park freezes over every winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원의 호수는 매년 겨울 얼어붙는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat very badly.
meanings.example: The politician was shat on by the media for his controversial remarks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 논란이 된 발언 때문에 언론의 심한 비난을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To furnish with a corbel of courses, each projecting beyond the one below it.
meanings.example: The architect decided to corbel out the balcony to give it a unique look.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건축가는 독특한 외관을 위해 발코니를 코벨식으로 돌출시키기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop lazing around.
meanings.example: If you want to succeed, you need to get off your ass and start working.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성공하고 싶다면, 빈둥거리는 것을 멈추고 일하기 시작해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume carelessly or wastefully; to waste.
meanings.example: He tends to slattern away his money on unnecessary gadgets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불필요한 장치에 돈을 낭비하는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly wash one's hands and forearms in preparation for performing a surgery.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수술 준비로 손과 팔뚝을 철저히 씻다
meanings.example: The surgeon began to scrub in before entering the operating room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과 의사는 수술실에 들어가기 전에 손을 씻기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To uncover (a secret or scandal).
meanings.example: The investigative journalist's report blew the lid off the corruption scandal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 탐사보도 기자의 보고서는 부패 스캔들을 폭로했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear at a distance, to emerge in the field of vision.
meanings.example: After a long journey, the ship finally hove into sight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 항해 끝에 배가 마침내 시야에 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark for attention; to flag.
meanings.example: Please flag up any errors in the document.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문서의 오류를 표시해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endeavor to accomplish a task.
meanings.example: He went about to prove his innocence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 무죄를 증명하기 위해 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait through (something): wait (through) till the end; patiently endure.
meanings.example: We decided to wait out the storm in the cabin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오두막에서 폭풍이 지나가기를 기다리기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticise or mock
meanings.example: He's always slinging off at his colleagues behind their backs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 동료들 뒤에서 그들을 비난한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw out the waste from the chamberpot in a prison cell or a similar waste receptacle.
meanings.example: In the old days, prisoners had to slop out their cells daily.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옛날에는 죄수들이 매일 감방의 오물을 버려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive a difficult period or event
meanings.example: My grandfather lived through the Great Depression.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 할아버지는 대공황을 겪고 살아남으셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a rough surface) smooth
meanings.example: The decorator used sandpaper to smooth over the rough patches on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장식가는 벽의 거친 부분을 매끄럽게 하기 위해 사포를 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove one variable (by giving it a fixed value) in order to identify any correlation between others
meanings.koreanDefinition: 다른 변수 간의 상관관계를 파악하기 위해 변수 하나를 고정하다
meanings.example: To analyze the data, we need to partial out the effect of age.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데이터를 분석하기 위해 우리는 나이의 영향을 분리해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider as a factor.
meanings.example: We need to factor in the cost of materials when calculating the budget.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예산을 계산할 때 재료비를 고려해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feed (a person or animal) until they are at a healthy weight.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사람이나 동물을 건강한 체중이 될 때까지 먹이다
meanings.example: The rescue team fed up the malnourished animals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 구조팀은 영양실조에 걸린 동물들을 살찌웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid or shirk, either by failing to perform, or by performing in a grossly insufficient, negligent, or superficial manner.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 수행 실패 또는 매우 불충분, 태만, 피상적으로 수행하여 회피하다
meanings.example: He promised to help, but he finked out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돕겠다고 약속했지만, 막판에 발을 뺐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a conversation at the tail end, without knowing much about its content.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 대화 내용 잘 모르고 끝부분에 끼어들다
meanings.example: I arrived late and just picked up stompies of their argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 늦게 도착해서 그들의 논쟁 끝부분만 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish (the painting of a wall etc.) by rolling a rag over a coat of paint or glaze.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 페인트나 유약 코팅 위에 천을 굴려 벽 등을 마감하다
meanings.example: The artist used a rag to rag off the excess paint, creating a textured effect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예술가는 천을 이용해 여분의 페인트를 닦아내 질감 효과를 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone perform a task or duty, especially one they are not prepared or willing to do; to make something serve a function, especially one it was not designed or intended for.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 준비되지 않았거나 꺼리는 일을 하도록 만들다
meanings.example: Anyone with basic first-aid training was pressed into service as a triage nurse after the earthquake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기본적인 응급처치 교육을 받은 사람은 누구나 지진 후 분류 간호사로 투입되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To diminish the effects of (something unpleasant or severe); to dull.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 불쾌하거나 심한 것의 효과를 줄이다, 무디게 하다
meanings.example: A little bit of sugar can take the edge off the bitterness of the medicine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 설탕을 조금 넣으면 약의 쓴맛을 완화할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scale up (a system) by adding more servers or nodes.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더 많은 서버나 노드를 추가하여 시스템을 확장하다
meanings.example: To handle the increased traffic, we need to scale out our servers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 증가하는 트래픽을 처리하기 위해 서버를 확장해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear at the surface of the ground; to outcrop.
meanings.example: Geologists studied the rock formations that crop out on the mountainside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지질학자들은 산비탈에 노두를 이룬 암석 지형을 연구했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set up in a perfect or complete way.
meanings.example: The mechanic dialed in the bicycle before the customer picked it up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정비사는 고객이 자전거를 가져가기 전에 완벽하게 조정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep a supply of something, in a place or container.
meanings.example: It's wise to store away some extra food in case of emergencies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비상사태에 대비해 여분의 식량을 비축해 두는 것이 현명하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put a rug on a horse
meanings.example: The groom decided to rug up the horse to keep it warm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마구간지기는 말을 따뜻하게 하기 위해 담요를 덮어주기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tire; to become tired
meanings.example: The long hike began to wear up the hikers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹은 등산객들을 지치게 하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn in one's grave.
meanings.example: If my grandmother saw this mess, she would roll in her grave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할머니께서 이 엉망인 것을 보시면 무덤 속에서 통곡하실 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shortchange a task To deliver a performance without commitment or effort, with lackluster results.
meanings.example: He just mailed it in during the presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 발표 중에 성의 없이 임했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a surgical operating theater where one has previously scrubbed in.
meanings.example: 17 May 2012, New Scientist, New device lets surgeons check medical images during an operation without having to scrub out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2012년 5월 17일, 뉴 사이언티스트, 새로운 장치로 외과 의사들이 수술실을 나오지 않고도 수술 중 의료 영상을 확인할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive, mislead, or trick (someone).
meanings.example: The magician faked out the audience with his disappearing act.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마술사는 사라지는 묘기로 관객을 속였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shoo-in.
meanings.example: The election was a shoo-in for the incumbent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선거는 현직자의 압승이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (a competitor) to win a fixed race.
meanings.example: They tried to shoo in their favored candidate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 자신들이 선호하는 후보의 승리를 조작하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lay hands on.
meanings.example: are the engines that develop ill-health and begin to lose time, or the wagons that develop hot boxes and have to be removed, initiating delays that steadily pile up—or at worst, the weather lays its hand on the whole District.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건강에 문제가 생겨 시간이 지체되기 시작하는 엔진이나, 뜨거운 박스가 생겨 제거해야 하는 왜건들로 인해 지연이 꾸준히 쌓이거나, 최악의 경우 날씨가 해당 지역 전체에 영향을 미친다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To downmix.
meanings.example: Please fold down the back seats of the car to make more space.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공간을 더 확보하기 위해 차 뒷좌석을 접어 내려주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay more than one should have; to pay more than fair market value.
meanings.example: He really gave away the farm to buy that old car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 낡은 차를 사기 위해 바가지를 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change or revise a previous opinion or decision.
meanings.example: Indeed, part of the problem was that Armstrong was rowing back on so much previous behaviour and years of aggressive lambasting of reporters, officials and team-mates who had claimed he was doping. "I don't forgive Lance Armstrong, who lied to me in two interviews. And I suspect most of America won't, either," Kurtz wrote.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실제로 문제의 일부는 암스트롱이 과거의 많은 행동과 자신을 도핑했다고 주장한 기자, 관계자, 팀 동료들에 대한 공격적인 비난을 철회하고 있었기 때문이었다. 커츠는 "나는 두 번의 인터뷰에서 나에게 거짓말을 한 랜스 암스트롱을 용서하지 않는다. 그리고 미국 대부분도 그러지 않을 것이라고 생각한다"라고 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: row-back.
meanings.example: The government announced a row-back on its controversial tax policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 논란이 된 세금 정책에 대한 입장 철회를 발표했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To polish or scrub; to cover (something with a substance) by rubbing.
meanings.example: I rubbed up the brass buttons on my jacket to make them shine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 재킷의 황동 단추를 문질러 윤을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rub-up.
meanings.example: The rub-up process made the metal surface smooth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문지르는 과정으로 금속 표면이 매끄러워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold back; to refuse to give or share.
meanings.example: He kept back some of the money for himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 돈의 일부를 자신을 위해 숨겨두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark the boundaries of an area—used of land, wood, metal, etc.—before working upon it.
meanings.example: I don't want to be marked out as a failure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 실패자로 낙인찍히고 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resolve (a problem) by talking about it.
meanings.example: Let's talk out our differences and find a solution.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 차이점을 대화로 해결하고 해결책을 찾자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move from place to place.
meanings.example: The nomads move about the desert in search of water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유목민들은 물을 찾아 사막을 돌아다닌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become swollen
meanings.example: My ankle started to swell up after the injury.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부상 후 발목이 붓기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start living or working in a new place; to transport one's belongings to a new home or workplace; to make one's home or workplace into a suitable environment.
meanings.example: They are planning to move in next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 다음 달에 이사할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To misbehave
meanings.example: Kids! Stop messing about and do your work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 얘들아! 장난치지 말고 숙제해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To melt fully, especially metal or glass so that it can be remade into something else.
meanings.example: Great Exhibition of 1851 is also very much in King's Cross's DNA. The clock in its short, Italianate central tower came from the Exhibition and in its early years it struck the hours, but the bells were first silenced in the First World War and then melted down during the Second World War.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1851년 대전시도 킹스 크로스의 DNA에 많이 담겨 있다. 짧은 이탈리아식 중앙 타워의 시계는 전시회에서 왔고 초기에는 시간을 쳤지만, 종은 제1차 세계 대전 때 처음으로 침묵했고 제2차 세계 대전 때 녹아내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get involved in; to investigate or explore.
meanings.example: I don't want to go into the details now.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금은 세부 사항에 들어가지 않고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk for an exceedingly long time, annoying the audience.
meanings.example: He rabbit on for hours about his holiday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가에 대해 몇 시간 동안 장황하게 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel or act spitefully towards.
meanings.example: Don't hate on him just because he's successful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 성공했다고 해서 그를 미워하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: Emphatic to remove, to eject, to expel; to dismiss (from a position, job, etc.).
meanings.example: The company had to boot out the underperforming employee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 성과가 저조한 직원을 해고해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have an intense argument or fight until one side prevails.
meanings.example: For once I'd met a man which was willing and able to stand up and slug it out with me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 번은 나에게 맞서 싸울 의지와 능력이 있는 남자를 만났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tolerate or put up with bravely.
meanings.example: She had to brave out the difficult situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어려운 상황을 용감하게 견뎌내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face up to or confront (one's own misconduct or deceit) without shame.
meanings.example: He tried to brazen out the accusations, but it didn't work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비난에 대해 뻔뻔하게 대처하려 했지만 통하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bring round.
meanings.example: The smell of coffee helped to bring him around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피 향이 그가 의식을 되찾는 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to stink.
meanings.example: The broccoli really stank up the refrigerator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 브로콜리가 냉장고에 정말 악취를 풍겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to stink; to fill with stench.
meanings.example: The broccoli really stank out the refrigerator.
meanings.exampleKorean: 브로콜리가 냉장고를 악취로 가득 채웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ground (an opinion, a conclusion, etc) on.
meanings.example: The movie is based on a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become involved, start to play a role.
meanings.example: When the police arrived, new factors came into play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 도착하자 새로운 요인들이 작용하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with, to be focused on.
meanings.example: That's what it's all about.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것이 전부이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a reduction in the amount, extent, etc. of something.
meanings.example: The government had to scale back its ambitious building plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 야심 찬 건설 계획을 축소해야만 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extol, praise.
meanings.example: He's always crying up his own achievements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신의 업적을 과찬한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, especially in a hurry.
meanings.example: I have to nick off now, I'm late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 서둘러 가야 해요, 늦었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go away; get lost.
meanings.example: Nick off, you're bothering me!
meanings.exampleKorean: 꺼져, 귀찮게 하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a baserunner, when a fly ball is hit, to put one's foot on the base one is currently at until the ball is caught. When the ball is caught, the baserunner may attempt to advance to the next base, at the risk of being tagged out.
meanings.example: The runner tagged up at second base and advanced to third after the fly ball was caught.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주자는 플라이 볼이 잡힌 후 2루에서 태그업하여 3루로 진루했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run away, especially fearfully.
meanings.example: The mouse scurried away into its hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쥐는 구멍 속으로 재빨리 도망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport or convey to a place.
meanings.example: I'll bring over some sandwiches to the school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학교로 샌드위치를 좀 가져갈게.

meanings.englishDefinition: Look within oneself to summon a particular positive quality, such as strength, energy or courage.
meanings.example: I mustered up all my remaining willpower.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 남은 의지력을 모두 끌어모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look within oneself to find and put into action a particular positive quality, such as strength, energy or courage.
meanings.example: I summoned up all my remaining willpower.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 남은 의지력을 모두 짜냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with something hurriedly; to treat in a perfunctory manner.
meanings.example: He slurped over the details of the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사고의 세부 사항을 얼버무렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rush headlong.
meanings.example: The car ripped along the highway.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 고속도로를 쏜살같이 달렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compel someone to do, or abstain from, a particular act.
meanings.example: The judge bound him over to keep the peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 그에게 평화를 유지하라고 맹세하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to go through something hastily
meanings.example: I only had time to skim through the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보고서를 훑어볼 시간밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Tide over.
meanings.example: Can you lend me some money to tie me over until payday?
meanings.exampleKorean: 월급날까지 쓸 돈을 좀 빌려줄 수 있니?

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute money jointly with others.
meanings.example: A sailing vacation does not need to be expensive, if you club together with friends and share a week's rent of a 10-berth yacht.
meanings.exampleKorean: 친구들과 돈을 모아 10인승 요트를 일주일간 빌려 함께 사용한다면, 요트 휴가가 비쌀 필요는 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To include in communications about something; to keep informed about something.
meanings.example: Please loop me in on all future discussions about this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트에 대한 향후 모든 논의에 나를 포함시켜 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create (something) as a by-product or a secondary derived work.
meanings.example: a line of merchandise spun off from a TV series
meanings.exampleKorean: TV 시리즈에서 파생된 상품 라인

meanings.englishDefinition: Of the price of a security, to increase to an unexpected degree.
meanings.example: The stock price is melting up today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 주가가 급등하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take advantage of, to exploit (usually a tragic or unfortunate situation).
meanings.example: I would never make mileage out of the recent tragic deaths in their family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그들의 가족에게 최근 일어난 비극적인 죽음을 절대 이용하지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow in; grant permission or admittance to; invite in or over; admit.
meanings.example: They decided to have us in for a cup of tea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 우리를 차 한잔 하러 들이기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put one over.
meanings.example: Most audiences are too savvy to pull one over on. I assumed most people would be so far ahead of us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대부분의 관객들은 속이기에는 너무 눈치가 빠르다. 나는 대부분의 사람들이 우리보다 훨씬 앞서 나갈 것이라고 가정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In a patience or solitaire game, to return a card from a foundation to the tableau.
meanings.example: He had to worry back a card to make his next move.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다음 수를 두기 위해 카드를 되돌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach a sufficient spinning speed for reads and writes to take place.
meanings.example: The hard drive spun up and the system booted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하드 드라이브가 회전 속도를 높이고 시스템이 부팅되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send someone away quickly and without fussing.
meanings.example: They bundled the children off to school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 아이들을 서둘러 학교로 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically damage or ruin; to be ruined; to go bankrupt; to end (something).
meanings.example: Their business finally went bust up after years of losses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 사업은 수년간의 손실 끝에 마침내 파산했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supply a specimen of breath for alcohol analysis.
meanings.example: The driver was asked to blow in the bag at the roadside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운전자는 길가에서 음주 측정기에 숨을 불어넣으라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To formally announce one's arrival at a location or event (usually a hotel) to a proprietor or employee of that location or event in order to secure admission, accommodations, or other services.
meanings.example: We left Tan Son Nhut and checked into the Globe. Unfortunately, the hotel was all but full for the first time, and since rooms were short, our crew checked into just two suites rather than three rooms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 탄손누트를 떠나 글로브 호텔에 체크인했다. 안타깝게도 호텔은 처음으로 거의 만실이었고, 방이 부족했기 때문에 우리 팀은 방 세 개 대신 스위트룸 두 개만 예약했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain indoors or in a particular place.
meanings.example: It's raining, so we have to keep in today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 오고 있어서 오늘은 안에 있어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to have a good relationship with (someone)
meanings.example: He tried to keep in with his boss by doing extra work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 추가 근무를 해서 상사와 좋은 관계를 유지하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid drawing attention to oneself; to stay out of trouble.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 몸을 사리다, 눈에 띄지 않게 행동하다
meanings.example: I had to keep my head down so my ex wouldn't recognize me as I walked by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지나가다가 전 애인에게 들키지 않으려면 몸을 사려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compel or force into a certain state or condition.
meanings.example: The rain drove in through the open window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 열린 창문으로 몰아쳐 들어왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive; to turn up.
meanings.example: He pitched up at the party uninvited.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 초대받지 않았는데도 파티에 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discharge (especially from a police force or military) for medical reasons; to discharge (a person) because they are not fit for duty. Chiefly used in the past tense.
meanings.example: He was medically out from the army due to an injury.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부상으로 인해 군에서 의병 제대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive or endure.
meanings.example: I'm afraid your grandfather isn't going to last out for very long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 할아버지께서 오래 버티지 못하실 것 같습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To examine or inspect something in order to determine its condition; to check out.
meanings.example: I will check up on the car tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 차 상태를 점검할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force to leave by crowding; to push out or away through strength of numbers.
meanings.example: The new company's aggressive marketing strategy began to crowd out smaller competitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 회사의 공격적인 마케팅 전략이 소규모 경쟁사들을 밀어내기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive somewhere, especially unexpectedly.
meanings.example: After wandering for days, they finally fetched up at a small village.
meanings.exampleKorean: 며칠을 헤맨 끝에 그들은 마침내 작은 마을에 도착했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act a part in an exaggerated or affected manner.
meanings.example: The actor really camped it up in his role, much to the audience's delight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 역할에 정말 과장되게 연기했고, 관객들은 매우 즐거워했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cheat, swindle, dupe (someone).
meanings.example: He tried to mug off the tourists by selling them fake watches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가짜 시계를 팔아 관광객들을 속이려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: jump down someone's throat.
meanings.example: When I told him the bad news, he leaped down my throat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그에게 나쁜 소식을 전하자, 그는 나를 격렬하게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: jump down someone's throat.
meanings.example: She threatened to climb down my throat if I didn't finish the report on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 내가 제시간에 보고서를 끝내지 않으면 나를 격렬하게 비난하겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stamp out.
meanings.example: It's time to stomp out crime in America.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미국에서 범죄를 근절할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be deliberately unproductive in one's work or study.
meanings.example: Emily is slacking off since this is her last semester; she completely stopped trying to make good grades.
meanings.exampleKorean: 에밀리는 마지막 학기이기 때문에 게으름을 피우고 있다. 좋은 성적을 받으려는 노력을 완전히 멈췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end up being successful when doing so was uncertain or unlikely.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 기대 이상으로 성공하다, 예상외의 활약을 하다
meanings.example: Despite the initial doubts, the underdog team turned up trumps in the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초기의 의심에도 불구하고, 약체 팀은 결승전에서 예상외의 활약을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a temporary disruption of the operations of, usually an airport.
meanings.example: Fog socked in O'Hare almost until noon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안개 때문에 오헤어 공항은 정오 가까이까지 운항이 방해받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set off an area or shape with chalk.
meanings.example: The teacher chalked off the important sections of the text on the board.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 칠판에 교재의 중요한 부분들을 분필로 표시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face or turn toward the audience more than would be natural, for instance in a staged conversation.
meanings.example: The actor was criticized for cheating out during the scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 장면 중에 관객을 향해 부자연스럽게 돌아섰다는 비판을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disrespect someone; to disdain or denigrate one's worth or dignity; to betray.
meanings.example: His betrayal was like spitting in her face after all she had done for him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 배신은 그녀가 그를 위해 했던 모든 것을 무시하는 것과 같았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit down on a chair: to go from a non-seated position to sitting on a chair.
meanings.example: Pull up a chair! The meeting's about to begin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의자 끌어다 앉아요! 회의가 곧 시작될 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Sit down on a floor.
meanings.example: Let's pull up a floor and have a chat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바닥에 앉아서 이야기 좀 하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: know inside and out.
meanings.example: She knows the city inside out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 도시를 속속들이 알고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To publicly support (someone).
meanings.example: He decided to stand up with his colleague against the unfair policy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불공정한 정책에 맞서 동료를 공개적으로 지지하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consume gradually, especially by erosion.
meanings.example: Rust ate into the old swords and armour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 녹이 오래된 검과 갑옷을 좀먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill holes or undulations in an uneven wall with plaster, in order to make it flatter.
meanings.example: The plasterer will dub out the damaged areas of the wall before painting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미장공이 칠하기 전에 벽의 손상된 부분을 메울 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dispose of in a bargain, usually with a sense of loss or disadvantage.
meanings.example: He bargained away his valuable antique for a small sum of money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 얼마 안 되는 돈을 받고 귀중한 골동품을 깎아서 팔아버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To declare war or go to war.
meanings.example: The two tribes decided to take up the hatchet after years of peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 부족은 수년간의 평화 이후 전쟁을 시작하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To haul up; usually, to haul the slack of (a rope) through its leading block, or to haul up (a tackle which hangs loose) by its fall.
meanings.example: Moncrieff rounded in the slack of the rope and landed the captain most beautifully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몬크리프는 밧줄의 느슨한 부분을 감아 올려 선장을 아주 멋지게 착륙시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay; to defer; to put off; to lay aside.
meanings.example: He tried to shift off the responsibility onto his subordinate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책임을 부하에게 떠넘기려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To petition for and take out, or to apply for and obtain.
meanings.example: to sue out a writ in chancery; to sue out a pardon for a criminal
meanings.exampleKorean: 법원에 소장을 제출하다; 범죄자에 대한 사면을 신청하여 받다

meanings.englishDefinition: To press out with the feet; to press out, as wine or wheat.
meanings.example: to tread out grain with cattle or horses
meanings.exampleKorean: 가축이나 말로 곡식을 밟아 탈곡하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw out, throw away.
meanings.example: I had rather she had flung out at me, as many a woman would do, than taken it as she did.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 그렇게 받아들인 것보다 차라리 많은 여자들처럼 나에게 화를 내며 내던졌으면 좋았을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw oneself up suddenly, as in anger or defiance; to bristle up.
meanings.example: He sprunt up in anger when he heard the accusation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비난을 듣자 분노하여 갑자기 꼿꼿이 섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be hanged.
meanings.example: The traitor was sentenced to dance on nothing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반역자는 교수형에 처해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be hanged.
meanings.example: The criminals were forced to dance on a rope.
meanings.exampleKorean: 범죄자들은 교수형에 처해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become untwisted or frayed, as the end of a rope, or the edge of canvas.
meanings.example: The rope began to fag out after prolonged use.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밧줄은 오래 사용한 후 올이 풀리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten up.
meanings.example: She had to hitch up her long skirt to walk comfortably on the grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 잔디 위를 편하게 걷기 위해 긴 치마를 끌어 올려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blow increasingly cooler.
meanings.example: The wind began to breeze up in the afternoon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후에 바람이 점점 시원해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To send away through the postal service; to mail.
meanings.example: I need to post off this package before the post office closes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우체국 문 닫기 전에 이 소포를 부쳐야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To survive or get by with some difficulty.
meanings.example: Dinsmore has experienced 'love in a cottage,' and probably has no very idyllic recollections; she knows what it is to rub along with one servant, or even none, and keep up appearances and worry about the next month's rent
meanings.exampleKorean: 딘스모어는 '오두막에서의 사랑'을 경험했고, 아마도 목가적인 추억은 별로 없을 것이다. 그녀는 하인 한 명 또는 아예 없이 어렵게 지내면서 겉모습을 유지하고 다음 달 월세를 걱정하는 것이 어떤 것인지 안다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall asleep.
meanings.example: He corked off after a long day of work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 근무 후 잠들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To measure by steps or paces; hence, to divide (a space), or to form a series of marks, by successive measurements, as with dividers.
meanings.example: He measured the garden by stepping it off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정원을 걸음으로 재어 측정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of something desirable, to be received by someone with little or no effort or by chance.
meanings.example: The perfect job opportunity fell into her lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 완벽한 직업 기회가 그녀에게 저절로 찾아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave in a hurry.
meanings.example: I rushed off a letter, warning them that I would be arriving the next day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 다음 날 도착할 것이라고 경고하는 편지를 서둘러 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress up.
meanings.example: She loved to rag out for special occasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 특별한 날에 화려하게 차려입는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Said of a morphism: to be equal to a composition of two morphisms, one of which is the given morphism (that is being said to be "factored through").
meanings.example: 16.3 An arrow f : A \rightarrow B is epic iff 1_B is the smallest sub-object of B that f factors through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 16.3 화살표 f : A \rightarrow B는 f가 통과하는 B의 가장 작은 부분 대상이 1_B일 때만 에픽이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grow fat.
meanings.example: He started to pork up after he quit exercising.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 운동을 그만둔 후 살이 찌기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end one's career.
meanings.example: film even picks up with his colleagues at the university throwing him a retirement party, but it never explores how Indy feels about finally hanging up his hat as a professor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화는 심지어 대학 동료들이 그의 은퇴 파티를 열어주는 장면까지 보여주지만, 인디가 마침내 교직에서 은퇴하는 것에 대해 어떻게 느끼는지는 전혀 탐구하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to execute; to perform successfully, all the way to the end.
meanings.example: Despite the difficulties, she managed to carry through her project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려움에도 불구하고 그녀는 프로젝트를 끝까지 해냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer to a new page, column, ledger, or similar entity.
meanings.example: The remaining balance will be carried forward to the next accounting period.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남은 잔액은 다음 회계 기간으로 이월될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a wild and carefree manner, so as to use up excess energy or exuberance.
meanings.example: The teenagers were determined to get their ya-yas out before college started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 십대들은 대학이 시작되기 전에 마음껏 즐기기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Be silent; shut up.
meanings.example: did keep time, sir, in our catches. Sneck up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 노래에서 박자를 맞췄어요. 입 다물어요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To dwell on a single subject with disagreeable or wearisome persistence.
meanings.example: He always harps on one string about the economy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 경제에 대해 한 가지 주제만 계속 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fight until there is decisively and conclusively a winner
meanings.example: The two teams had to fight it out in overtime to determine the winner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 팀은 승자를 가리기 위해 연장전에서 끝까지 승부해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To temporarily disable (a portion of source code) by converting it into a comment.
meanings.example: You should rem out the old code before implementing the new feature.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기능을 구현하기 전에 이전 코드를 주석 처리해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten by one of the ends into a wall or some other support.
meanings.example: to tail in a timber
meanings.exampleKorean: 목재의 끝을 벽에 박아 고정하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compete with others eagerly or fiercely in pursuit of the same goal(s).
meanings.example: The salespeople were crawling over each other to get the biggest client.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영업사원들은 가장 큰 고객을 확보하기 위해 서로 앞다투었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up or calculate.
meanings.example: Please figure up the total cost of the trip, including all meals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 식비를 포함하여 여행의 총 비용을 계산해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit.
meanings.example: At the present time, October 15th, the patient gulps up, with little or no apparent effort, what resembles almost pure dark defibrinated blood
meanings.exampleKorean: 현재 10월 15일, 환자는 거의 노력 없이 거의 순수한 검은색 탈섬유화된 혈액과 유사한 것을 토해낸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To man a rope for hauling, the men standing in a line or tail
meanings.example: “Now then — to the main topsail now! Tally on to that gantline. Don't stand about there!”
meanings.exampleKorean: “자, 이제 메인 돛대에! 그 밧줄을 당겨. 거기 서 있지 마!”

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn the head of a vessel either way by bracing the headyards aback.
meanings.example: The helmsman had to box off the ship to avoid the iceberg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 키잡이는 빙산을 피하기 위해 배머리를 돌려야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go off half-cocked.
meanings.example: He tends to go off halfcocked without thinking through the consequences.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결과를 깊이 생각하지 않고 성급하게 행동하는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack; to assault; to abuse.
meanings.example: The critics pitched into the new play, calling it a disaster.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비평가들은 새 연극을 재앙이라고 부르며 공격했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand a complex matter.
meanings.example: "Why, mother, I should have thought you could see into a millstone as far as most people, and yet you can't see to the bottom of a foolish girl's heart."
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니, 대부분의 사람들만큼이나 물레방아 돌을 꿰뚫어 볼 수 있다고 생각했는데, 어리석은 소녀의 마음은 전혀 이해하지 못하시는군요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expire due to having only females surviving the death of the last male in a of a surname or of heritable property in a patrilineal naming or inheritance system
meanings.koreanDefinition: 성이나 재산이 여성만 남아 대가 끊기다
meanings.example: The family name will daughter out if the current generation has no male heirs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현재 세대에 남자 후계자가 없으면 가문은 대가 끊길 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To berate (someone or something) using profane language; to swear at (someone or something), especially at length.
meanings.example: He was so angry that he cussed out the delivery driver.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 화가 나서 배달 기사에게 욕설을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cuss out.
meanings.example: She cursed out the person who cut her off in traffic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 끼어든 사람에게 욕설을 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise (the whole ring of an arch) off its centering, by driving in the keystone forcibly.
meanings.example: The students were all keyed up before their final graduation performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 마지막 졸업 공연을 앞두고 모두 한껏 긴장하고 흥분되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To back out in a cowardly way; to chicken out.
meanings.example: He promised to come to the party but funked out at the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티에 오겠다고 약속했지만 막판에 겁먹고 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: roll in wealth.
meanings.example: After selling his company, he was rolling in dough.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사를 판 후 그는 돈을 많이 벌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put an end to; to defeat or frustrate (a scheme or project).
meanings.example: My young brothers and sisters looked very awestruck at this first glimpse of barbaric life, and I fear many of the theories they had formed about going into the wilds alone, and experiencing some Robinson Crusoe-like adventures, were suddenly "knocked on the head," to use a forcible colonial expression, one, however, which rather pointed to their probable fate if they had attempted anything of the kind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 어린 남동생들과 여동생들은 야만적인 삶의 첫 모습에 매우 경외감을 느꼈고, 나는 그들이 홀로 야생으로 가서 로빈슨 크루소와 같은 모험을 경험할 것이라는 이론들이 갑자기 "좌절되었다"고 생각할까 봐 두렵다. 이는 그들이 그런 일을 시도했다면 겪었을 법한 운명을 가리키는 표현이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The down on cotton, etc.
meanings.example: The cotton seeds were covered in a fine down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목화씨는 고운 솜털로 덮여 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To meet with hospitality; to be welcome.
meanings.example: When we arrived at their house, we found the latchstring out and were warmly welcomed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 집에 도착했을 때, 우리는 환대를 받았고 따뜻하게 환영받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To convey or extend in an upward course or direction; to build.
meanings.example: to carry up a wall
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽을 쌓아 올리다

meanings.englishDefinition: To change direction; to change from one tack to another.
meanings.example: Don't throw your clothes about on the floor like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 옷을 바닥에 그렇게 아무렇게나 던져두지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shift to any point of the compass.
meanings.example: The wind began to haul around to the north.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 북쪽으로 방향을 바꾸기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed or ruined; to go up in smoke.
meanings.example: All their plans ended in smoke when the project was cancelled.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 취소되면서 그들의 모든 계획은 수포로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept a challenge or adopt a quarrel.
meanings.example: He took up the glove and agreed to the debate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 도전을 받아들이고 토론에 동의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expose oneself for the protection of something; to make defence against any assailing danger; to take the place of a fallen defender or supporter.
meanings.example: She stood in the gap for her colleague who was ill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아픈 동료를 대신해 위험을 무릅썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crash catastrophically into the ground.
meanings.example: The pilot lost pitch control and the jet augered in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조종사가 피치 제어력을 잃고 제트기가 땅으로 추락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rail, revile, or scold (someone or something).
meanings.example: The angry customer clawed away at the poor waiter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화난 고객이 불쌍한 웨이터를 심하게 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be concerned or involved in.
meanings.example: He seems to have a finger in every pie in the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 도시의 모든 일에 관여하는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie (tell a falsehood) flatly or outright.
meanings.example: I know not where he lodges, and for mee to deuise a lodging, and say he lies heere, or he lies there, were to lye in mine owne throat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 어디에 묵는지 모르고, 내가 숙소를 꾸며서 그가 여기 묵는다거나 저기 묵는다거나 하는 것은 완전히 거짓말하는 것이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have the toes of each foot, in standing or walking, incline from the other foot.
meanings.example: When he stands, he tends to toe out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 설 때 발가락을 바깥쪽으로 벌리는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand or carry the feet in such a way that the toes of each foot incline toward the other.
meanings.example: She walks with her toes in, which is a sign of pigeon-toed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 발가락을 안쪽으로 모으고 걸어서 팔자걸음의 징후이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gobble ravenously, devour voraciously
meanings.example: natures do pursue, / Like rats that ravin down their proper bane
meanings.exampleKorean: 자연은 쫓는다, / 자신의 파멸을 게걸스럽게 먹어 치우는 쥐들처럼

meanings.englishDefinition: To retain one's skill through regular practice.
meanings.example: He still plays chess occasionally to keep his hand in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 실력을 유지하기 위해 여전히 가끔 체스를 둔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To occupy a place or position of advantage, power, or defence.
meanings.example: The new legislation helps small businesses stand in the gate against large corporations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 법안은 소기업들이 대기업에 맞서 유리한 위치를 차지하도록 돕는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain; to carry off, as a prize.
meanings.example: to bear off a blow; to bear off a boat
meanings.exampleKorean: 타격을 피하다; 보트를 육지로 끌어내다

meanings.englishDefinition: To get married.
meanings.example: They are planning to walk down the aisle next spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 내년 봄에 결혼할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inscribe the name of (a student) on a board or tablet in a college.
meanings.example: 1834, author not stated, possibly Alfred, Lord Tennyson???, Preface to Remains in Verse and Prose of Arthur Henry Hallam
meanings.exampleKorean: 1834년, 저자 미상, 아마도 알프레드 테니슨 경, 아서 헨리 할람의 시와 산문 유고 서문

meanings.englishDefinition: To teach or instil by harsh repetition.
meanings.example: The coach beat the importance of teamwork into the players.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 선수들에게 팀워크의 중요성을 반복해서 주입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up a contest; to acknowledge defeat; to throw in the towel.
meanings.example: But he was too brave a man to throw up the sponge to fate, and had work to do yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 그는 운명에게 항복하기에는 너무 용감한 사람이었고, 아직 해야 할 일이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get started; to begin working; to become active.
meanings.example: Come on, get on the stick, we're already late!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어서 시작해, 이미 늦었어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep something to oneself; to prevent from escaping.
meanings.example: to hold in laughter, or one's emotions
meanings.exampleKorean: 웃음이나 감정을 참다

meanings.englishDefinition: go round in circles.
meanings.example: We've been discussing this for hours and just going around in circles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 몇 시간 동안 이 문제를 논의했지만 제자리걸음만 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go round in circles.
meanings.example: The project has been going in circles for months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 프로젝트는 몇 달 동안 진전이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encroach on.
meanings.example: The ocean gains on the land.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바다가 육지를 잠식해 간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat contemptuously and unfairly; to bully
meanings.example: Stop pushing me about and treat me with respect!
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 함부로 대하지 말고 존중해 줘!
