meanings.englishDefinition: To force (legislation) to be passed.
meanings.example: The corridor was so packed with people that I had to push through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복도는 사람들로 너무 붐벼서 밀고 나아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force (someone) to leave a group.
meanings.example: key imponderable is the attitude of the companies that will no longer have a role in the business. It is worth noting here that several have already thrown the towel in or been gently pushed out, such as Stagecoach and National Express.
meanings.exampleKorean: 핵심적인 고려 사항은 더 이상 사업에서 역할을 하지 않을 회사들의 태도이다. 여기서 주목할 만한 점은 Stagecoach와 National Express와 같이 이미 포기했거나 부드럽게 밀려난 회사들이 몇몇 있다는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: In nine ball pool, an optional shot after a legal break shot where a player is allowed to hit any ball first, or not any, as well as not hit a rail, and the opponent can choose who shoots the next shot.
meanings.example: He decided to push out on the break shot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 브레이크 샷에서 푸시 아웃을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: forward
meanings.example: We must push forward with our plans despite the challenges.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려움에도 불구하고 우리는 계획을 추진해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act, or to manage matters, to someone's advantage or benefit.
meanings.example: By refusing to negotiate, he is playing right into their hands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상을 거부함으로써 그는 그들에게 유리하게 작용하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store (clothing, etc.) with sprigs of lavender, i.e. to keep fresh.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 라벤더와 함께 보관하다 (옷 등을 신선하게 유지하기 위해)
meanings.example: She carefully laid in lavender the linens she had just washed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 방금 세탁한 리넨을 조심스럽게 라벤더와 함께 보관했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come down the pike.
meanings.example: A new opportunity is coming down the line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 기회가 곧 다가오고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come down the pike.
meanings.example: We'll see what comes down the road for this company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 회사가 앞으로 어떻게 될지 지켜볼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To physically isolate an area or building for security reasons preventing passage of humans or vehicles.
meanings.example: Police sealed off the building following an anonymous bomb warning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 익명의 폭탄 협박 후 건물을 봉쇄했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To open a remote-controlled door to allow (a person) to enter after sounding the doorbell or buzzer.
meanings.example: I'm going to buy some more coffee. Could you buzz me in when I get back?
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피 좀 더 사러 갈게. 돌아오면 초인종 눌러서 문 열어줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: change horses in midstream.
meanings.example: It's too risky to swap horses in midstream when the project is almost complete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 거의 완료된 시점에서 중도에 계획을 바꾸는 것은 너무 위험하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sow, as ploughed land, with grass seed.
meanings.example: The farmer decided to stock down the field in the spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 봄에 밭에 잔디 씨를 뿌리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die gradually or slowly.
meanings.example: The sound of the music slowly died away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악 소리가 천천히 점점 희미해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be superior in something.
meanings.example: In the competition, she bore away the bell with her outstanding performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 대회에서 뛰어난 공연으로 으뜸을 차지했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a beginning in a certain work; to become accustomed to a particular business.
meanings.example: He's still getting his hand in as a new manager.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 신임 관리자로서 아직 일을 익히는 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To concern or affect someone.
meanings.example: This decision doesn't stand me in hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 결정은 나와 관련이 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To permit someone to continue onward, e.g. through a security checkpoint, after verifying their identity, tickets, etc.
meanings.example: The guard checked through the passengers before they boarded the train.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원은 승객들이 기차에 탑승하기 전에 통과시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to be questioned; to introduce doubt regarding.
meanings.example: His integrity has been called into question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 진실성에 의문이 제기되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: or with}} to barely be able to survive
meanings.example: They had to scrape along on a very small income.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 아주 적은 수입으로 근근이 살아나가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean the exterior surface of an object, from top to bottom, typically by hand using a damp dishcloth or other cleaning cloth.
meanings.example: Please wipe down the kitchen counter after use.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사용 후 주방 조리대를 닦아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike out (a batter) without the batter swinging at the called strike.
meanings.example: He struck out looking in a crucial moment of the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경기 중 결정적인 순간에 헛스윙 삼진을 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of an investment, to make sense financially or to be expected to generate the desired returns.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 재정적으로 타당하다, 수익이 날 것으로 예상되다
meanings.example: The business plan doesn't quite pencil out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업 계획이 재정적으로 타당하지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brought back to reality; to emerge from a daydream.
meanings.example: After the vacation, it was hard to come down to earth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴가 후 현실로 돌아오기가 힘들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain or criticise
meanings.example: Stop bitching off about your boss and do your work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사에 대해 불평하는 것을 그만두고 네 일을 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn upside down.
meanings.example: He flipped the pancake over to cook the other side.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 팬케이크를 뒤집어 다른 면을 익혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rock someone's socks.
meanings.example: The band really rocked our socks off last night!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어젯밤 밴드가 정말 우리를 열광시켰어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve a person's mood by making them more energetic.
meanings.example: The music really livened up the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악이 파티를 정말 활기차게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a plant, to open its buds.
meanings.example: The trees are starting to leaf out in spring.
meanings.exampleKorean: 봄이 되자 나무들이 잎을 내기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak rudely, harshly or belligerently.
meanings.example: Don't you lip off to me, young man!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 녀석, 나한테 말대꾸하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: to fall from the sky, as hail
meanings.example: Criticism hailed down on them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비난이 그들에게 쏟아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end up; to arrive or result, especially if it is not planned.
meanings.example: After traveling for weeks, they finally landed up in Paris.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 주간 여행한 끝에 그들은 결국 파리에 도착했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To see; look at; glimpse.
meanings.example: Had he seen her once? He debated that a moment. No. The habits of long service years had made him note and remember people. He knew that if ever he had laid eyes on her lovely face, he would never have forgotten.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 본 적이 있었나? 그는 잠시 생각했다. 아니었다. 오랜 복무 경험으로 그는 사람들을 주목하고 기억했다. 만약 그의 눈에 그녀의 아름다운 얼굴이 들어왔다면 절대 잊지 않았을 것이라는 것을 그는 알았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compute the total price of something.
meanings.example: Can you price out these items for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 물건들의 가격을 계산해 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: go a long way.
meanings.example: Your help will go a long way in finishing this project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 도움은 이 프로젝트를 완료하는 데 큰 도움이 될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cohabit without being married.
meanings.example: They decided to live over the brush before getting married.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결혼 전에 동거하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demonstrate (a rule, law, or policy) by breaking or breaching it.
meanings.example: The fact that he didn't show up showed honor in the breach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나타나지 않았다는 사실은 규칙 위반으로 증명되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: nick off.
meanings.example: He was caught trying to knick off some items from the store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가게에서 물건 몇 개를 훔치려다 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take into account.
meanings.example: Taking everything into consideration, both students ought to get another chance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 것을 고려해 볼 때, 두 학생 모두에게 또 다른 기회가 주어져야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To violently cause to fall down.
meanings.example: The farmer scythed down the tall grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 긴 풀을 낫으로 베어 쓰러뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To skive; to play truant; to slack off.
meanings.example: He often skived off school to go to the arcade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오락실에 가기 위해 자주 학교를 빼먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to behave responsibly, courageously, or both.
meanings.example: It's time for you to woman up and face your problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 네 문제를 직면하고 책임감 있게 행동할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell one's business or other major asset (such as a home).
meanings.example: They decided to sell up and move to the countryside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 사업체를 팔고 시골로 이사 가기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten or attach
meanings.example: you want to turn around, then turn around, but I shan't fasten you up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 돌고 싶다면 돌되, 나는 당신을 묶어두지 않을 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk in circles.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 빙빙 돌려 말하다, 요점을 말하지 않다
meanings.example: Stop speaking in circles and tell me what you really want.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빙빙 돌려 말하지 말고 진짜 원하는 게 뭔지 말해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tarnish or spoil enough so that something or someone is no longer respectable.
meanings.example: The scandal dragged the politician's reputation through the mud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들은 정치인의 명예를 실추시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to
meanings.example: That pipe is leaking again. I'll get on to the plumber in the morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파이프가 또 새고 있어. 아침에 배관공에게 연락할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider something to be important.
meanings.example: We place importance on customer satisfaction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 고객 만족을 중요하게 여깁니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay calm.
meanings.example: All right, okay! Keep your pants on, I'm coming!
meanings.exampleKorean: 알았어, 알았어! 진정해, 가고 있잖아!

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to terms with a fact, a state of affairs, etc. that one was previously unable or refusing to accept.
meanings.example: I can't get it through my head why he would do such a thing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 왜 그런 짓을 했는지 이해가 안 간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a person) discouraged or dejected.
meanings.example: Disclose it not to the others, for none can strive with Fate and gain the victory, and it would but cast down their hearts. But it is fitting we be ready against evil hap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 이들에게 밝히지 마라, 운명과 싸워 이길 자는 아무도 없으며, 그것은 그들의 마음을 낙담시킬 뿐일 것이다. 하지만 우리는 불행에 대비하는 것이 마땅하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve one's performance, or the quality of one's work.
meanings.example: If you want to win, you need to step up your game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이기고 싶다면 실력을 향상시켜야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: ing-form
meanings.example: She is going away on vacation next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 다음 주에 휴가차 떠난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Ahead of the competitors and still increasing the lead.
meanings.example: The horse is going away at the finish line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 말은 결승선에서 앞서 나가고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give one's daughter's hand in marriage.
meanings.example: The father was emotional as he gave his daughter away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지는 딸을 시집보내며 감상에 젖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a great romantic relationship with each other.
meanings.example: The new couple seems to be making beautiful music together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 커플은 서로 잘 지내는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain positive and cheerful.
meanings.example: Even when things get tough, it's important to keep your chin up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 어려워져도 긍정적인 태도를 유지하는 것이 중요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose oneself in music.
meanings.example: He loves to just vibe out to his favorite playlist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 좋아하는 플레이리스트에 맞춰 그냥 심취하는 것을 좋아합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live or work in an unorganized and unplanned way.
meanings.example: They just muddle along without any clear goals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 뚜렷한 목표 없이 되는대로 살아갑니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flow or flood into something or somewhere.
meanings.example: The rain poured in and it filled it with water while we were inside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 쏟아져 들어와 우리가 안에 있는 동안 집을 물로 채웠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To triumph over (someone).
meanings.example: Don't crow over your victory too much; be humble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승리에 대해 너무 의기양양해하지 말고 겸손해지세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak with pride on behalf of (someone else); to boast of the accomplishments of (someone else).
meanings.example: I get very tired of parents who constantly brag on their children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자녀들을 끊임없이 자랑하는 부모님들 때문에 매우 지칩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a new endeavor enthusiastically and wholeheartedly, though possibly without experience.
meanings.example: She decided to plunge in and start her own business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 사업을 시작하기로 결심하고 뛰어들었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reprimand or reform someone vigorously.
meanings.example: Someone needs to knock some sense into him before he makes a big mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 큰 실수를 저지르기 전에 누군가 정신이 번쩍 들게 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to be extremely fond of
meanings.example: He goes a bundle on collecting vintage cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빈티지 자동차 수집에 아주 열중합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide sufficient people to make an event worthwhile.
meanings.example: We need eleven players, but we are only nine. Can you help us make up the numbers?
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수 11명이 필요한데, 우리는 9명뿐입니다. 인원수를 채우는 데 도움을 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time out of one's house.
meanings.example: He stayed out all night in the bar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 술집에서 밖에 머물렀습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: for
meanings.example: the keeper went up for a caught behind
meanings.exampleKorean: 골키퍼가 캐치된 공을 잡기 위해 올라갔습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide a simple and understandable explanation.
meanings.example: I'm sorry but I can't spell it out for you, because the whole issue is not that simple.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죄송하지만 그 문제를 명확히 설명해 드릴 수 없습니다. 전체 사안이 그렇게 간단하지 않기 때문입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide simple and understandable details.
meanings.example: Here is an article that spells things out, so that it is easier to grasp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이해하기 쉽도록 자세히 설명하는 기사가 여기 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: catch fire.
meanings.example: The dry leaves on the ground can easily catch fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 땅에 있는 마른 나뭇잎에 쉽게 불이 붙을 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clap to the rhythm of (a song).
meanings.example: The children clapped out the rhythm of the song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 노래의 리듬에 맞춰 박수를 쳤습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together, to connect.
meanings.example: Please connect up the printer to the computer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프린터를 컴퓨터에 연결해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate or dress in a fancy way in order to make attractive.
meanings.example: All over that big ole doodied up house she walks and talks and I follow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 크고 화려하게 꾸며진 집을 가로질러 그녀는 걷고 말하며 나는 따라갑니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be deliciously soft and creamy; to create a melting sensation when eaten; to need little chewing.
meanings.example: This chocolate cake just melts in your mouth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 초콜릿 케이크는 입에서 살살 녹습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Deliciously soft and creamy, creating a melting sensation when eaten, and therefore needing little chewing.
meanings.example: The dessert was so melt-in-the-mouth that I didn't need to chew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 디저트는 너무 입에서 살살 녹아서 씹을 필요가 없었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retrace one's steps; to retreat.
meanings.example: Seeing the danger, they decided to take the back track.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위험을 감지하고 그들은 왔던 길을 되돌아가기로 결정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rein in.
meanings.example: The government needs to reign in spending.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부는 지출을 억제할 필요가 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To embrace someone affectionately or thankfully.
meanings.example: When she saw her long-lost son, she fell on his neck.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랫동안 잃어버렸던 아들을 보자 그녀는 그를 껴안았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to eat greedily.
meanings.example: We really porked out at the buffet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 뷔페에서 정말 게걸스럽게 먹었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Connected or relevant.
meanings.example: Social mobility is often closely bound up with socioeconomic status.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사회적 이동성은 종종 사회경제적 지위와 밀접하게 관련됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: His arm was bound up after the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사고 후 그의 팔은 붕대로 감겨 있었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To weird out or disgust; to make (someone) feel uneasy and squeamish.
meanings.example: The strange noise really squigged me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 이상한 소리는 나를 정말 불쾌하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter usage; to begin being used.
meanings.example: This new technology will soon come in use for many industries.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 신기술은 곧 많은 산업에서 사용되기 시작할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be overly attentive to someone or something.
meanings.example: Stop fussing over me, I'm perfectly fine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 너무 신경 쓰지 마세요, 저는 괜찮습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hit the gas.
meanings.example: We need to step on the gas if we want to get there on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제시간에 도착하려면 가속 페달을 밟아야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: Don't rush in without thinking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 생각 없이 서둘러 들어가지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be dead.
meanings.example: If he doesn't get help soon, he'll be pushing up daisies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 곧 도움을 받지 못하면 죽어서 땅에 묻힐 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover (a building, especially a porch) with window screens.
meanings.example: We decided to screen in the porch to keep the bugs out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벌레를 막기 위해 현관을 방충망으로 덮기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break wind.
meanings.example: He accidentally stepped on a frog during the hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하이킹 중에 실수로 방귀를 뀌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be confident, spirited.
meanings.example: This is incredible stuff from Paraguay now. They have their tails up and are imposing wave after wave of attacks on the Argentina goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 파라과이의 놀라운 플레이가 펼쳐지고 있다. 그들은 자신감이 넘치고 아르헨티나 골문에 연이은 공격을 퍼붓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sweat; to secrete sweat because of heat, nerves etc.
meanings.example: I need to sweat out this cold with a good workout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운동으로 이 감기를 땀 흘려 떨쳐내야겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To convince (someone) by talking and suggesting.
meanings.example: She finally talked me into going to the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 마침내 나를 설득해서 파티에 가게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak to another person in a demeaning or patronizing way.
meanings.example: Stop talking down to me; I'm just as capable as you are.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 깔보지 마. 나도 당신만큼 능력이 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become full of fruit, seeds, or spores.
meanings.example: The grass and weeds come up to my waist and the plum trees are already fruiting up, though most of the fruit'll go to the wasps and the worms, Vinny says, 'cause he can't be arsed to pick it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 풀과 잡초가 허리까지 자랐고 자두나무에는 이미 열매가 맺히고 있지만, 비니 말로는 대부분의 열매는 말벌과 벌레에게 갈 것이라고 한다. 귀찮아서 따지 않을 것이기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail to do correctly; to make a mess of.
meanings.example: The webpage didn't display properly because I bollocksed up the formatting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서식 설정을 망쳐서 웹페이지가 제대로 표시되지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk to someone in order to dissuade them from doing something.
meanings.example: I tried to talk him out of quitting his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 직장을 그만두지 않도록 설득하려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach (someone) in a sexual manner.
meanings.example: "The first obstacle you need to overcome is your attitude about black people being dogged out by the entire white race."
meanings.exampleKorean: "당신이 극복해야 할 첫 번째 장애물은 백인 전체에 의해 흑인들이 성적으로 이용당한다는 당신의 태도입니다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To put away by folding; to collapse something into a space where it can be stored while not in use.
meanings.example: Please fold away the chairs after the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 후에 의자를 접어서 치워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: assume the mantle.
meanings.example: Educators took up this mantle enthusiastically.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교육자들은 이 역할을 열정적으로 이어받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront an unpleasant situation
meanings.example: then I'll fade away and not have to face the facts / It's not easy facing up when your whole world is black
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러면 나는 사라져서 사실을 직면하지 않아도 될 것이다 / 세상 전부가 칠흑 같을 때 직면하는 것은 쉽지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: give out strongly; scold or chide severely
meanings.example: The boss gave out a real stink when he saw the mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 실수를 보고는 정말 심하게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become angry or annoyed; to lose one's temper.
meanings.example: He really got his shirt out when they criticized his work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그의 일을 비판했을 때 그는 정말로 격분했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blather.
meanings.example: “Can you tell me why it is,” said Ian in an aggressive way, “every time we go into a fucking pub you go ballsing on about bleeding Lance Platt. Can you tell me that?”
meanings.exampleKorean: “왜 그런지 말해줄 수 있나?” 이안이 공격적으로 말했다. “우리가 빌어먹을 술집에 갈 때마다 당신은 빌어먹을 랜스 플랫에 대해 장황하게 떠들어대잖아. 그걸 말해줄 수 있나?”

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a lot of activity with many unwanted or unnecessary details.
meanings.example: This is just comfortable. I don't want no ballsing about with rows and noise in flats.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것은 그냥 편안하다. 나는 아파트에서 싸움과 소음으로 부산 떨고 싶지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: balls about.
meanings.example: We really want to discover if there really is any authentic student radicalism — how much is just imitation, ballsing around, a matter of fashion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 진정한 학생 급진주의가 정말로 존재하는지 알고 싶다. 얼마나 많은 것이 그저 모방이고, 헛된 시간 보내기이며, 유행의 문제인지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To nasalize all of one’s phonemes during speech.
meanings.example: He talks through his nose, which can be annoying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 콧소리로 말하는데, 그것은 짜증날 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk out of one's ass.
meanings.example: He doesn't know what he's talking about; he's just talking out of his ass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 무슨 말을 하는지 모른다. 그는 그냥 터무니없는 소리를 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crease or become creased.
meanings.example: He fell flat on his face and everyone just creased up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 얼굴을 바닥에 박고 넘어졌고 모두들 웃음을 터뜨렸다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience a difficult period, especially financially.
meanings.example: After the company closed, many employees fell on hard times.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사가 문을 닫은 후, 많은 직원들이 어려운 시기를 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To telephone, to call on the telephone.
meanings.example: These comments are guaranteed to dial up the animosity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이러한 발언들은 적대감을 불러일으킬 것이 확실하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dial in to.
meanings.example: I need to dial into the conference call from my mobile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴대폰으로 컨퍼런스 콜에 전화 접속해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mildly mock, tease, make fun of; usually implying good-natured fun more than biting sarcasm.
meanings.example: The students were goofing on the food safety training, pretending to eat off the floor, to shortchange their handwashing, and so on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 음식 안전 훈련을 가지고 장난치며, 바닥에서 먹는 척하고 손 씻는 것을 제대로 하지 않는 등이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move slightly (sideways), in order to make room for someone; for example to move in a bed to make room for someone else to lie on, or to move sideways on a seat so as to allow someone room to sit and share that one seat.
meanings.example: Could you hutch up a bit so I can sit down?
meanings.exampleKorean: 앉을 수 있도록 조금만 자리를 내줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with hardship with toughness and without complaining; man up.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 힘든 일을 꿋꿋이 견디다, 남자답게 행동하다
meanings.example: COWBOY UP!! Be a man, son. Walk it off, says the coach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘내! 남자답게 행동해, 아들아. 그냥 털어버려, 코치가 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to ruin
meanings.example: Don't scuzz up the new carpet with your muddy shoes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진흙 묻은 신발로 새 카펫을 더럽히지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: to reduce the intensity of (a fire)
meanings.example: The firefighters worked to dampen down the flames.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소방관들은 불길을 잡기 위해 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become damp, especially too damp for an intended agricultural purpose.
meanings.example: The seedlings began to dampen off due to excessive moisture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과도한 습기 때문에 묘목들이 축축해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: The police used dogs to flush out the suspect from the woods.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 숲속에 숨은 용의자를 찾아내기 위해 경찰견을 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Suggests that the performer of the action is not sincere in their grief.
meanings.example: He tried to console her, but his words felt hollow, as if he were just taking out an onion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 위로하려 했지만, 마치 양파를 꺼내듯 그의 말은 공허하게 느껴졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce by shitting (i.e. defecating).
meanings.example: Oh look, Zumbetel shat out another smartphone. Expect about two hours of battery life, at best.
meanings.exampleKorean: 봐, 줌베텔이 또 스마트폰을 똥 싸서 만들었네. 기껏해야 배터리 수명은 두 시간 정도 예상해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about something with others, but without knowing the exact facts or truth of the matter.
meanings.example: There are a lot of different figures being bandied around, but the exact cost will not be known for some time yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러 가지 다른 수치들이 떠돌고 있지만, 정확한 비용은 시간이 좀 지나야 알 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about something frequently, but without knowing the exact facts or truth of the matter.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 확인 안 된 소문을 자주 말하다
meanings.example: There are a lot of different figures being bandied about, but the exact cost will not be known for some time yet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러 가지 다른 수치들이 떠돌고 있지만, 정확한 비용은 시간이 좀 지나야 알 수 있을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to leave somewhere angrily
meanings.example: He was so angry about the decision that he stormed off the stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결정에 너무 화가 나서 무대를 박차고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dick around.
meanings.example: Stop dickin' about and get back to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 다시 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To become enraged in an imposing or intimidating manner.
meanings.example: When he saw his car damaged, he hulked out and started yelling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차가 망가진 것을 보고는 위압적으로 분노하며 소리를 지르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or walk away, especially in an angry or enraged manner, and said especially of large and physically imposing persons or objects.
meanings.example: The angry customer hulked off without paying the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화난 고객은 계산서를 내지 않고 덩치를 위압적으로 움직이며 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become mellow or to soften in intensity (such as a smell, emotion or sound), or to change from uptight to relaxed.
meanings.example: After a long day at work, I just want to mellow out on the couch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 하루 일과 후, 나는 그저 소파에서 편안해지고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look through the scope of a rifle.
meanings.example: The sniper scoped in on his target.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저격수는 그의 목표물에 조준경을 맞췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was so bored that he decided to knock one out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 지루해서 자위행위를 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover or coat in lubricant: to lubricate thoroughly.
meanings.example: You need to lube up the gears before you put them together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기어를 조립하기 전에 윤활유를 발라야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sneak up to (someone or something) with the intention of causing harm.
meanings.example: The gang rolled up on the rival group's territory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 갱단은 경쟁 그룹의 영역으로 해를 끼치려 몰래 다가갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to accept, inaccurately, that a creature or object is one's parent and consequently to act as if it is.
meanings.example: The ducklings imprinted on the first moving object they saw.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새끼 오리들은 처음 본 움직이는 물체에 각인되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rust completely; to become covered in rust; by extension, to break down over time and cease working.
meanings.example: The old car was left in the yard to rust out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 차는 마당에 버려져 완전히 녹슬게 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (something) in a sack.
meanings.example: Please sack up the potatoes before storing them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감자를 보관하기 전에 자루에 넣어 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: sack up (only in the "become more manly" meaning)
meanings.example: He needs to sac up and face his fears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 남자다워져서 자신의 두려움에 맞서야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce or diminish in effect or intensity.
meanings.example: Let's dial down the anger in this conversation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 대화에서 분노의 강도를 낮춥시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce (one's energy or intensity); to restrain (a feeling or action).
meanings.example: The politician had to dial back his aggressive rhetoric.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 공격적인 수사를 자제해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink a beverage, especially alcoholic and in large amounts.
meanings.example: He tipped back the bottle and finished the whiskey.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 병을 기울여 위스키를 단숨에 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something by sorting through materials or (in the case of information) by speaking to contacts and following leads.
meanings.example: She spent hours ferreting around in the attic for old photos.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오래된 사진을 찾기 위해 다락방을 몇 시간 동안 샅샅이 뒤졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To back away or retreat; to abandon an effort.
meanings.example: He shrank back from the angry dog.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화난 개를 보고 움츠러들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make progress on a difficult problem; to continue trying.
meanings.example: to hack away at the tough roots of a tree
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무의 질긴 뿌리를 꾸준히 제거하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To become completely shriveled; to completely dry up and become wrinkled.
meanings.example: "Look! - look! - look! she's shrivelling up! she's turning into a monkey!" and down he fell upon the ground, foaming and gnashing in a fit.
meanings.exampleKorean: "봐! - 봐! - 봐! 그녀가 쪼그라들고 있어! 원숭이로 변하고 있어!" 그러더니 그는 거품을 물고 이를 갈며 발작을 일으키며 땅에 쓰러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dance, typically in a funky manner.
meanings.example: Let's boogie down to some good music!
meanings.exampleKorean: 신나는 음악에 맞춰 춤추자!

meanings.englishDefinition: In solitaire card games, to place a card over another card of higher value (e.g. 4♦ over 5♣).
meanings.koreanDefinition: 카드 게임에서 낮은 숫자를 높은 숫자 위에 놓다
meanings.example: In this game, you can build down on red cards with black cards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 게임에서는 검은색 카드로 빨간색 카드를 내려놓을 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept or recognize as reliable, tangible, or factual; to depend on.
meanings.example: You can hang your hat on his promise; he always keeps his word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 약속은 믿어도 돼. 그는 항상 약속을 지키니까.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bite (something) very hard and usually for a purpose (like to hold it in one's mouth).
meanings.example: Biting down on my wallet, I had two hands free to open the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지갑을 꽉 물고 있어서 문을 열 두 손이 자유로웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give someone a serious beating using one's fists; to punch someone until he or she is unconscious.
meanings.example: If you insult my family again, I'll punch your lights out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 가족을 다시 모욕하면, 주먹으로 네놈을 때려 눕혀주마!

meanings.englishDefinition: punch someone's lights out.
meanings.example: He was so angry that he threatened to knock someone's lights out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 화가 나서 누군가를 때려 눕히겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or depart angrily.
meanings.example: She was so upset that she stormed out of the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 너무 화가 나서 방을 뛰쳐나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck up.
meanings.example: I really effed up this time and I don't know how to fix it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번에 정말 망쳤고 어떻게 고쳐야 할지 모르겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To type (a document) in its final, presentable form.
meanings.example: Typing up these notes into a finished essay will take some time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 메모들을 타이핑해서 완성된 에세이로 만드는 데 시간이 좀 걸릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove, especially unfairly, from an organization or position of responsibility.
meanings.example: I was drummed out of the job by slanderous lies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 비방하는 거짓말 때문에 직장에서 불명예스럽게 해고당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make sufficiently tight.
meanings.example: As noted in the last chapter, you should tighten up your argument and prose when you revise the first draft for substantive change.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 장에서 언급했듯이, 실질적인 변화를 위해 초고를 수정할 때 논증과 문체를 더 다듬어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow (something, often hair) to grow unimpeded.
meanings.example: I'm going to grow my hair out and donate it to charity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 머리를 기르고 자선 단체에 기부할 생각이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become rigid, tense or stiff.
meanings.example: My muscles started to stiffen up after the long hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹 후에 근육이 뻣뻣해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To compact a substance (usually soil) until it is flat.
meanings.example: Mr. Trump’s aides also have tried to tamp down questions about the former president’s frustrations, so as not to elevate Mr. DeSantis.
meanings.exampleKorean: 트럼프 전 대통령의 보좌관들은 데산티스 씨를 부각시키지 않기 위해 전 대통령의 불만에 대한 질문을 잠재우려 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce costs on a project or product to an unreasonable degree; to cut corners.
meanings.example: That's not the kind of product you want to cheap out on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 제품은 비용을 아끼려고 해서는 안 되는 종류다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cheap out.
meanings.example: Don't cheapen out on the materials for this project; quality is important.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로젝트의 재료비를 너무 아끼지 마세요. 품질이 중요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: mike up.
meanings.example: The actors were mic'd up before going on stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배우들은 무대에 오르기 전에 마이크를 달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To care for (someone), usually excessively or in an overly emotional manner.
meanings.example: She tends to dote over her grandchildren, spoiling them with gifts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 손주들을 지나치게 아끼는 경향이 있어 선물로 응석을 받아준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come closer to, as if to catch or hunt.
meanings.example: The predator began to move in on its unsuspecting prey.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포식자가 방심한 먹잇감에게 천천히 다가가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up or cravenly abandon something difficult.
meanings.example: When the going got tough, he punked out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상황이 어려워지자 그는 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a secret with; to talk of sensitive and personal issues with (someone).
meanings.example: I can always confide in my best friend when I have problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제가 있을 때 나는 항상 가장 친한 친구에게 털어놓을 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dance.
meanings.example: I wanna go out and get my groove on while the night is young!
meanings.exampleKorean: 젊은 밤이 있는 동안 나가서 신나게 춤추고 싶어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To recall or remember something; to experience a flashback.
meanings.example: Seeing the old photograph made him flash back to his childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 사진을 보고 그는 어린 시절을 갑자기 떠올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell (someone) step by step how to do something.
meanings.example: Let me talk you through the process.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 과정을 단계별로 설명해 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward in time.
meanings.example: His dream flashed forward to an incident in grade school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 꿈은 초등학교 시절의 사건으로 미래를 보여주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move (oneself, or someone/something else) so as to free up space.
meanings.example: Could you move over a little so I can sit down?
meanings.exampleKorean: 앉을 수 있도록 조금 옆으로 비켜주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue without being distracted; to keep going.
meanings.example: The soldiers were unmoved but the policeman immediately placed himself between Jimmy and the entrance. 'Good morning, madam. No loitering please. Now move along.'
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 움직이지 않았지만 경찰관은 즉시 지미와 입문 사이에 섰다. '안녕하세요, 부인. 서성거리지 마세요. 이제 계속 진행하세요.'

meanings.englishDefinition: To write out on a computer keyboard.
meanings.example: I need to type out this report before the deadline.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마감 시간 전에 이 보고서를 타이핑해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a phenomenon, voice, etc.: to slowly diminish in frequency, intensity, or volume; also, to diminish more quickly over time.
meanings.example: His voice began to trail off as he got more tired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더 피곤해지면서 목소리가 점점 약해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To empty (something) completely of liquids; to drain completely.
meanings.example: has not been found necessary, even in the most severe conditions, since the water is drained out if a locomotive spends any appreciable time out of service and in normal working conditions the coolant is always warm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가장 심각한 조건에서도 필요하지 않다는 것이 밝혀졌는데, 기관차가 상당한 시간 동안 운행 중지 상태에 있으면 물이 완전히 빠지고 정상적인 작업 조건에서는 냉각수가 항상 따뜻하기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To interfere or involve oneself with (something); to become deeply involved with (something); to intrude into or be nosy about (something).
meanings.example: He always gets up in other people's business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 다른 사람들의 일에 참견한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell all of an item or group of items.
meanings.example: They had to sell away all their possessions to pay off their debts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 빚을 갚기 위해 모든 재산을 팔아치워야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: buy-in.
meanings.example: We need to get buy-in from all stakeholders before proceeding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진행하기 전에 모든 이해관계자로부터 동의를 얻어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept an idea as valid; to join in on a concept.
meanings.example: It's important for the team to buy in to the new strategy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀이 새로운 전략에 동의하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To summon; to cause to depart.
meanings.example: I was called away from the meeting to deal with a medical emergency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 응급 의료 상황을 처리하기 위해 회의에서 불려갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pray for To pray for; to request from God.
meanings.example: I shall call down God's wrath upon you!
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 위에 신의 분노를 내리게 할 것이다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose all of the desirable items from (an array of options); to look for the most desirable items among.
meanings.example: She picked over the vegetables at the market, looking for the freshest ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가장 신선한 것을 찾기 위해 시장에서 채소를 골랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continually or inexorably harm someone; to continue to do harm; to needle over time; to weaken someone over time.
meanings.example: The constant criticism began to pick away at his confidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끊임없는 비판은 그의 자신감을 서서히 약화시키기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take something away in order to dispose of it; to remove a large volume of materials.
meanings.example: The construction crew carted away the debris from the site.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건설 인부는 현장에서 잔해를 차로 싣고 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become comfortable with a place or routine.

If so, passengers on the West of England line are in for a rough ride, as the reliability of these tired old British Rail trains inevitably declines, and maintenance costs rise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이러한 의사 결정 마비가 영국 철도청이 설립되고 새로운 리듬에 익숙해질 때까지 계속될 것인가? 그렇다면, 웨스트 오브 잉글랜드 노선의 승객들은 힘든 여정을 겪게 될 것이다. 낡은 영국 철도 열차의 신뢰성은 필연적으로 떨어지고 유지보수 비용은 상승할 것이기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurriedly and forcibly take, appropriate or seize control of; to arrest (someone).

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to a conclusion about someone or something; to make a note of one's conclusion about someone or something.

meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그를 처음부터 믿을 수 있는 직원으로 여겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shift one's weight forward; to lean forward or towards something.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trick (someone) into giving something up; to unfairly deprive someone of (something).

meanings.englishDefinition: To successfully engage the company of someone for a period of time.

meanings.englishDefinition: to discard, throw away To throw away; to get rid of; to dispose of (something, especially food that has gone bad).

meanings.exampleKorean: 1980년대부터 런던 시민이자 단골 이용자로서, 그곳이 다양한 건축 양식이 뒤섞여 급조된 끔찍한 장소였고 승객에게 전혀 친절하지 않았던 것을 기억합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain one's emotional composure; to keep it together.
meanings.example: Despite the bad news, she managed to hold herself together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 소식에도 불구하고 그녀는 감정을 추스를 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause to stay together.
meanings.example: The glue was strong enough to hold the broken pieces together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 접착제는 부서진 조각들을 붙잡아 둘 만큼 충분히 강했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather together in an organized manner.
meanings.example: The general marshaled together his troops for the final assault.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장군은 마지막 공격을 위해 병력을 조직적으로 집결시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carelessly and casually arrange or organize.
meanings.example: She tossed together a quick salad for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 점심으로 재빨리 샐러드를 되는대로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be coworkers with someone; to share a workspace with someone.
meanings.example: The two departments will work together on the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 부서는 새로운 프로젝트를 위해 협력할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a partnership; to decide to work together in a duo.
meanings.example: Students were asked to partner up for the group activity.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 그룹 활동을 위해 파트너를 맺도록 요청받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To combine permanently, as with cement.
meanings.example: The bricks were cemented together to form a strong wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽돌은 단단한 벽을 형성하기 위해 시멘트로 붙여졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair (something), especially in an improvised manner or with insufficient equipment.
meanings.example: He had to patch together a makeshift shelter from branches and leaves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나뭇가지와 잎사귀로 임시변통의 피난처를 기워 붙여야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain allied through difficulties.
meanings.example: 1984, Paul McCartney and the Frog Chorus, We All Stand Together (song)
meanings.exampleKorean: 1984년, 폴 매카트니와 프로그 코러스, 우리는 모두 함께 서 있다 (노래)

meanings.englishDefinition: To dispute by arguing; to hash out or battle out (an issue).
meanings.example: They decided to quarrel out their disagreement instead of letting it fester.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 불화가 악화되도록 내버려두는 대신 말다툼으로 의견 불일치를 해결하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: through
meanings.example: The students dutifully worked through two pages of algebra problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 충실하게 대수 문제 두 페이지를 풀어나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower in pitch, level, or intensity; to tone down.
meanings.example: The composer decided to key down the melody to make it more melancholic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작곡가는 멜로디를 더 우울하게 만들기 위해 음높이를 낮추기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep somewhere other than home, forced to do so by circumstances.
meanings.example: He had to kip down in his car after missing the last train.
meanings.exampleKorean: 막차를 놓친 후 그는 차에서 임시로 자야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk at length, in an annoying, boring and long-winded way.
meanings.example: He tends to yack on about his vacation for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 휴가에 대해 몇 시간 동안 장황하게 지껄이는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk continuously, especially with no-one paying attention.
meanings.example: She yammered on about her problems without realizing no one was listening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무도 듣고 있지 않다는 것을 깨닫지 못한 채 그녀는 자신의 문제에 대해 계속 투덜거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: I argued him down to twenty dollars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그와 흥정하여 가격을 20달러로 낮췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become preoccupied by (some topic or activity).
meanings.example: She absorbed herself in her studies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 공부에 몰두했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be barely admitted into something, or to be admitted only at the last minute.
meanings.example: He barely aced into the university.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 간신히 그 대학에 입학했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to pass (a test, interviews etc.) perfectly.
meanings.example: She aced out the final exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 마지막 시험을 완벽하게 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become too old for an activity, program or institution; to become too mature for a behavior.
meanings.example: When foster kids age out, it is important that the state provides them with support.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위탁 아동이 나이가 들어 더 이상 지원받을 수 없게 될 때, 국가가 그들에게 지원을 제공하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack or target a group that is weaker or of lower status than oneself.
meanings.example: Punching down is never acceptable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약자를 공격하는 것은 절대 용납될 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bury
meanings.example: Maybe we were a little too quick to throw dirt on this game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아마도 우리는 이 게임을 너무 빨리 끝내버린 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: have someone's back.
meanings.example: Don't worry, I've got your back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 걱정 마, 내가 네 편이야.

meanings.englishDefinition: have someone's back.
meanings.example: I'll cover your back if anything goes wrong.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무슨 일이라도 생기면 내가 네 뒤를 봐줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a venue covertly, without permission.
meanings.example: They bunked in without buying tickets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 표를 사지 않고 몰래 들어갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid doing something due to cowardice.
meanings.example: He was going to ask her out, but he pussed out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 하려 했지만, 겁먹고 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rile; to aggravate.
meanings.example: Don't rile him up, he's already angry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 더 화나게 하지 마, 이미 화났어.

meanings.englishDefinition: monkey around.
meanings.example: Stop monkeying about and get to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난치지 말고 일 시작해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurt someone by hitting or kicking them many times.
meanings.example: She beat up on me pretty bad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 나를 심하게 때렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (something); to bite (something).
meanings.example: He chomped down on the sandwich.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 샌드위치를 우적우적 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: I'll play the first couple of bars by myself to lead you in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 처음 몇 마디를 연주해서 당신을 이끌게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: lead-in.
meanings.example: The lead-in to the main story was quite long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 이야기에 대한 도입부가 꽤 길었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cop on, stop behaving immaturely
meanings.example: He needs to cop himself on and start acting like an adult.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정신 차리고 어른처럼 행동하기 시작해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in homoerotic activity with another woman.
meanings.example: They were rumored to dyke out together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 함께 동성애적 행위를 했다는 소문이 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about a time or experience; to recall.
meanings.example: When I think back on those times we shared together, I can't help but feel a little sad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 함께 보냈던 그 시절을 회상하면, 슬픔을 느끼지 않을 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail at or leave incomplete.
meanings.example: He would rather put a dent in his bankroll than fall down on anything he started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시작한 일을 중단하느니 차라리 돈을 잃는 편을 택할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become exhausted; to run out of gas or steam.
meanings.example: The runner gassed out in the last mile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 마지막 마일에서 기진맥진했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To observe. from c. 2000
meanings.example: That's something CIA hasn't managed to do in five years.” “We've had eyes on him,” Carmichael countered.
meanings.exampleKorean: CIA가 5년 동안 해내지 못한 것이군요.” “우리는 그를 감시해 왔습니다,”라고 카마이클이 반박했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Expressing eager anticipation of an event or undertaking, especially a competition of some sort.
meanings.example: The match is about to start, game on!
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기가 곧 시작될 거야, 시작하자!

meanings.englishDefinition: Ready for the beginning or escalation of a project or competition.
meanings.example: Everyone is here, so the game is on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두 와 있으니, 시작할 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take advantage of (someone) through manipulation and deceit.
meanings.example: Be careful of him, he'll game on you in a heartbeat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 조심해, 그는 순식간에 당신을 속일 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn away, refusing to listen.
meanings.example: He pulled away his shoulder when I tried to talk to him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그에게 말을 걸려고 했을 때 그는 외면했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He was caught rubbing one out in the bathroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화장실에서 자위행위를 하다가 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study in detail; to familiarize oneself with all available information.
meanings.example: I find the doctors are very surprised when they realise I'm genned up about my condition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사들이 내 상태에 대해 내가 철저히 공부했다는 것을 알게 되면 매우 놀란다는 것을 알게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: divvy up.
meanings.example: Let's divy up the profits equally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수익을 공평하게 나누자.

meanings.englishDefinition: divvies up.
meanings.example: He divies up the tasks among the team members.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 팀원들 사이에 업무를 분담한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of words, an idea, etc.: to go unheeded.
meanings.example: My advice fell on stony ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 조언은 소용없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit intensely, prolongedly, or both.
meanings.example: He drank too much and ended up puking his guts out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 많이 마셔서 결국 심하게 토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail miserably and with long-term consequences.
meanings.example: The company's new product launch went down in flames due to poor marketing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사의 신제품 출시가 형편없는 마케팅 때문에 끔찍하게 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch fire.
meanings.example: The old wooden shed went up in flames within minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 나무 창고가 몇 분 안에 불타버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut off as the top or extreme part of anything, especially to prune a small limb off a shrub or tree, or sometimes to behead someone.
meanings.example: Manchester Airport-Newcastle has both ends lopped off to become Manchester Victoria-York.
meanings.exampleKorean: 맨체스터 공항-뉴캐슬은 맨체스터 빅토리아-요크가 되기 위해 양 끝이 잘려나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a self-confident and ostentatious manner; show off.
meanings.example: He likes to swag it out with his expensive car and designer clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비싼 차와 명품 옷으로 과시하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: swag it out.
meanings.example: Arian Foster do a lot of talking, man. He swag it up with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아리안 포스터는 말을 많이 해, 이봐. 그는 그것으로 과시해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emphasise or stress; to draw attention to. from 20th c.
meanings.example: Alive and Well claims to be a "well-informed, loving guide to lesbian health, both mental and physical," it falls dismally short of the mark and only serves to point up how much lesbians truly need such a volume.
meanings.exampleKorean: Alive and Well은 "정신적, 육체적 레즈비언 건강에 대한 정보가 풍부하고 사랑스러운 안내서"라고 주장하지만, 목표에 턱없이 부족하며 레즈비언들이 진정으로 그러한 책을 얼마나 필요로 하는지를 지적할 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join together.
meanings.example: But as Meghan and Marin know, whether you married up or tried to have some downtime, if you’re a woman trying to live her life, the world will find a way to make you pay.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 메건과 마린이 알듯이, 당신이 더 나은 사람과 결혼했든 아니면 휴식을 취하려고 했든, 삶을 살려는 여성이라면 세상은 당신에게 대가를 치르게 할 방법을 찾을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink a lot of beer; especially, to get drunk on beer.
meanings.example: After a long week of work, they decided to beer up at the local pub.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 한 주간의 일을 마치고, 그들은 동네 술집에서 맥주를 진탕 마시기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use unfair means to deprive (someone) of (something).
meanings.example: That swindler did me out of £100.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 사기꾼은 나에게서 100파운드를 부당하게 빼앗았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress up in order to impress others.
meanings.example: She always poons herself up before going to parties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티에 가기 전에 항상 멋지게 치장한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (a plant) into a pot
meanings.example: It's time to pot up the seedlings into larger containers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모종을 더 큰 화분으로 옮겨 심을 때입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform (a song or piece of music) very loudly.
meanings.example: He would pound out jazz tunes on the old piano every night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매일 밤 낡은 피아노로 재즈 곡을 요란하게 연주하곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break wind.
meanings.example: He tried to discreetly squeeze one out during the quiet meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 조용한 회의 중에 몰래 방귀를 뀌려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: of an elected official, lease, etc. To finish the term.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Environmental Law Reporter, Volume 2006
meanings.exampleKorean: 환경법 보고서, 2006년

meanings.englishDefinition: idiomatic The transfer of debt within a company's balance sheet without acquiring new debt often through the capitalization of short-term to long-term debt.
meanings.example: The company decided to term out its short-term debt to improve its financial stability.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 재정 안정을 개선하기 위해 단기 부채를 장기 부채로 전환하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go down the tubes: to fail; to degenerate rapidly.
meanings.example: After the scandal, his political career went down the tube.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들 이후 그의 정치 경력은 망해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: slang go down the toilet: to fail; to degenerate rapidly.
meanings.example: His business venture went down the khazi after only a few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 사업은 몇 달 만에 망해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swing and miss at a pitch.
meanings.example: The batter swung through at the pitch, but missed the ball.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자는 공을 휘둘렀지만 공을 놓쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be sold in large quantities, very rapidly.
meanings.example: The new video game is flying off the shelves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 비디오 게임이 불티나게 팔리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: keep in mind.
meanings.example: Please keep in mind that the deadline is tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마감일이 내일이라는 것을 명심해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make some information more widely known.
meanings.example: We need to get the word out about the upcoming event.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다가오는 행사에 대해 소식을 알려야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To record all three outs in an inning with a strikeout.
meanings.example: The pitcher managed to strike out the side in the top of the ninth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투수는 9회 초에 삼진으로 이닝을 끝낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pour one's heart out.
meanings.example: I wrote this song for you, I poured out my soul
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 노래는 당신을 위해 썼어요, 제 영혼을 쏟아부었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: pour one's heart out.
meanings.example: She sat with her friend for hours, pouring her soul out about her troubles.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 친구와 몇 시간 동안 앉아 자신의 어려움에 대해 속마음을 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease to be vain and self-obsessed.
meanings.example: When he told me I was to serve him as if he were a Russian czar, I told him to get over himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나에게 러시아 차르처럼 그를 섬겨야 한다고 말했을 때, 나는 그에게 자만심을 버리라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say or do something deliberately and unnecessarily malicious.
meanings.example: After he lost the competition, his rival stuck the knife in by making fun of his performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁에서 진 후, 그의 라이벌은 그의 공연을 조롱하며 악의적으로 상처를 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize oneself in order to be able to deal with or achieve something; to mature.
meanings.example: He needs to get his shit together if he wants to pass the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 정신을 차려야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wheelsuck.
meanings.example: {{quote-newsgroup|en| Brooke| SPOILER (stage 8) - days when you wish the stage was 5k longer....
meanings.exampleKorean: 브룩 | 스포일러 (8단계) - 스테이지가 5km 더 길었으면 좋겠다고 바라는 날들....

meanings.englishDefinition: To wheelsuck.
meanings.example: The cyclist sat in the wheels of the leader, conserving energy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사이클 선수는 에너지를 절약하기 위해 선두 주자의 바람막이를 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steer one's bicycle to the side in order to allow other riders to overtake.
meanings.example: He swung off to let the faster riders pass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더 빠른 라이더들이 지나가도록 옆으로 비켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce power to (an engine), reduce speed
meanings.example: The pilot had to throttle down the engines due to the bad weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조종사는 악천후 때문에 엔진 출력을 줄여야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: slang To shake hands with.
meanings.example: Name's Jack. Lay some skin on me, man.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 이름은 잭이야. 악수하자, 친구.

meanings.englishDefinition: To derive enjoyment from something.
meanings.example: Stylax: And where do you get your rocks off, Aurelius? / Aurelius: Anywhere, mate. Bars, galleries, the temple. / Marcus: The temple? / Aurelius: Yeah, the temple is full of quality women.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스타일락스: 아우렐리우스, 당신은 어디서 즐거움을 얻나요? / 아우렐리우스: 어디든요, 친구. 술집, 갤러리, 신전이요. / 마커스: 신전이요? / 아우렐리우스: 네, 신전에는 괜찮은 여성들이 많아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall from the sky, especially in vast quantities
meanings.example: 2-6-2 locomotive No. 4771 Green Arrow, Blunt suddenly noticed that the tunnel mouth was silhouetted in a dazzling white glare and that incendiary bombs were showering down in their hundreds, he slammed on all his brakes and brought his train to a stop just inside the tunnel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2-6-2 기관차 4771호 그린 애로우, 블런트는 갑자기 터널 입구가 눈부신 백색 섬광으로 실루엣을 이루고 수백 개의 소이탄이 쏟아지는 것을 알아차렸고, 그는 모든 브레이크를 밟아 터널 안으로 기차를 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To store up; to build up a store of an emotion or passion; to recall or make a note of a certain trait or quality.
meanings.example: He tried to garner up the courage to ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 할 용기를 내려고 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intrude or butt in :on.
meanings.example: He always horns in on our conversations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 우리 대화에 끼어든다.

meanings.englishDefinition: informal Performed well; excellent; exact; correct.
meanings.example: That was a cock on shot!
meanings.exampleKorean: 정말 훌륭한 샷이었어!

meanings.englishDefinition: informal Good work; well done; excellent.
meanings.example: Cock on! You finished the project ahead of schedule.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잘했어! 예정보다 일찍 프로젝트를 마쳤네.

meanings.englishDefinition: slang To exaggerate; to overcharge.
meanings.example: The mechanic really cocked on me with that repair bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정비사는 수리비로 나에게 바가지를 씌웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: home in.
meanings.example: The detective began to hone in on the suspect.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 용의자에게 집중하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce to zero.
meanings.example: The bill was over $400, but the server zeroed it out as a gesture of gratitude.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계산서가 400달러가 넘었지만, 웨이터가 감사 표시로 0으로 만들어 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relinquish, to yield.
meanings.example: The team traded away their best player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 최고의 선수를 트레이드했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place a weight on, attach a weight to, load with a weight; to encumber with a weight.
meanings.example: The heavy snow weighed down the branches of the trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설이 나무 가지를 무겁게 눌렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat very harshly.
meanings.example: The judge brought down the hammer on the defendant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 피고에게 엄하게 처벌했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wander away from somewhere.
meanings.example: He got bored and wandered off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지루해져서 어슬렁거리며 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by tearing.
meanings.example: We tore out the old carpeting to reveal the wood floor underneath.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 아래의 나무 바닥을 드러내기 위해 오래된 카펫을 찢어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose (animals) in a pen.
meanings.example: The farmer penned in the sheep for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 밤을 위해 양을 우리 안에 가두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose (animals) in a pen.
meanings.example: Pent up in Utica he vainly formsA poor Epitome of Roman Greatneſs,And, cover’d with Numidian Guards, directsA feeble Army, and an empty Senate,Remnants of mighty Battels fought in vain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유티카에 갇힌 그는 로마의 위대함의 빈약한 축도만을 헛되이 형성하고, 누미디아 근위병에게 덮여, 약한 군대와 텅 빈 원로원을 지휘하며, 헛되이 싸운 거대한 전투의 잔재들을 이끈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become misty.
meanings.example: The bathroom mirror misted up when he opened the shower door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 샤워 문을 열자 욕실 거울에 김이 서렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inspect a building or area.
meanings.example: We're interested in buying this house. Can we look around tomorrow?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 집을 사는 데 관심이 있습니다. 내일 둘러볼 수 있을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To slouch, stoop, curl, or lean.
meanings.example: He hunched over his desk, working late into the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 책상에 구부정하게 앉아 밤늦게까지 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop buds.
meanings.example: For three years we put out pear trees; they have budded out and that is all they have done since.
meanings.exampleKorean: 3년 동안 우리는 배나무를 내놓았는데, 싹이 튼 것 외에는 그 이후로 아무것도 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find time or space for, amid other things.
meanings.example: It'll be a tight schedule, but we can work you in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일정이 빠듯하겠지만, 당신을 위한 시간을 낼 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To convert into a gross figure; to total (up).
meanings.example: We need to gross up these figures to include taxes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세금을 포함하기 위해 이 수치들을 총액으로 계산해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set in motion; to act as a causal or preparatory event or sequence of events.
meanings.example: If it is a new constitution, the historico-legal method is probably the most appropriate, since the events leading up to the introduction of the constitution are still so recent and relevant that they must be used for guidance when the constitution is interpreted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 헌법이라면, 헌법 도입으로 이어지는 사건들이 여전히 매우 최근이고 관련성이 높기 때문에 헌법 해석 시 지침으로 사용되어야 하므로 역사-법적 방법이 아마도 가장 적절할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To paste (things) into position, especially in layout and page makeup.
meanings.example: About all you need to do is paste up the finished art for the printer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인쇄를 위해 완성된 아트워크를 배치하기만 하면 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk about history or the past.
meanings.example: Let's go back in time and see what caused World War I.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과거로 돌아가 제1차 세계 대전의 원인이 무엇이었는지 알아보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie; to deceive; to take in.
meanings.example: Don't try to swack me up, I know what you did.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 속이려 하지 마, 네가 뭘 했는지 알아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come forth with a prompt answer.
meanings.example: "Answer up, Tom," but Tom didn't answer up. So he said it again, and Tom didn't answer up again; and come to look, there warn't anything there but the mule—Tom was gone.
meanings.exampleKorean: "대답해, 톰," 하지만 톰은 대답하지 않았다. 그래서 그는 다시 말했고, 톰은 또 대답하지 않았다. 그리고 보니, 거기에는 노새 말고는 아무것도 없었다—톰은 가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: humorous piss in someone's cornflakes.
meanings.example: That woman shit in her husband's Cheerios when she divorced him on their anniversary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 여자는 남편과 기념일에 이혼함으로써 남편의 기분을 망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anger or annoy someone.
meanings.example: Don't get his Irish up by mentioning that topic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 주제를 언급해서 그를 화나게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate, isolate or block by means of a curtain or other barrier.
meanings.example: They decided to curtain off the stage during the renovation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 보수 공사 중 무대를 커튼으로 가리기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn towards the wind so that the sails flap
meanings.example: The sailor had to luff up to avoid the oncoming wave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원은 다가오는 파도를 피하기 위해 방향을 틀어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: He finally spit out the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 진실을 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay for the privilege to do business with people at special events.
meanings.example: My first sales at this event will be used for getting off the nut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 행사에서의 첫 판매 수익은 이 특권 비용을 지불하는 데 사용될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cotton on to.
meanings.example: She quickly cottoned onto the fact that he was lying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그가 거짓말을 하고 있다는 사실을 금방 알아차렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage to keep something.
meanings.example: Desperate to hold onto power, Pervez Musharraf has discarded Pakistan's constitutional framework and declared a state of emergency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 권력을 유지하려는 필사적인 노력으로, 페르베즈 무샤라프는 파키스탄의 헌법 체계를 폐기하고 비상사태를 선포했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat (someone) in an unfair manner; to cheat or betray.
meanings.example: He did the dirty on his business partner and stole all the profits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업 파트너를 배신하고 모든 이익을 가로챘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be drunk.
meanings.example: He's had a jag on all night and is starting to get annoying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 술에 취해 있어서 짜증나기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get drunk.
meanings.example: 1899, Roswell Martin Field and Eugene Field (translators), "The Preference Declared" from Horace, Ode I.38, in Echoes from the Sabine Farm
meanings.exampleKorean: 1899년, 로스웰 마틴 필드와 유진 필드(번역가), "사빈 농장의 메아리" 중 호라티우스의 송가 I.38 "선언된 선호"

meanings.englishDefinition: To run into (someone); to have a chance encounter with (someone).
meanings.example: I happened to lam into my old teacher at the supermarket yesterday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 나는 슈퍼마켓에서 우연히 옛 선생님을 마주쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Leave, depart; to run away from someone or something.
meanings.example: After the argument, he decided to lam out of the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논쟁 후, 그는 집을 떠나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lining up.
meanings.example: The students were lining up for the assembly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 집회를 위해 줄을 서고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To damage or harm someone's eye to the point that it can no longer see.
meanings.example: Hey, watch where you're throwing that thing! You could've poked Jeff's eye out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이봐, 던지는 것 좀 봐! 제프 눈을 멀게 할 뻔했잖아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a submissively appeasing, accommodating, overly flattering, or flamboyant way, especially when excited.
meanings.example: After the concert, I totally queened out when I met Cher in person backstage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트 후, 나는 백스테이지에서 체어를 직접 만났을 때 완전히 흥분해서 과장되게 행동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: break the back of overburden, when applied to oneself.
meanings.example: He broke his back trying to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 허리가 부러지도록 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become frothy; to rise with a frothy surface or covered with something resembling froth.
meanings.example: The boiling liquid began to froth up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 끓는 액체가 거품이 일기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become foamy, create a foam; to rise with a foamy surface or covered with something resembling foam
meanings.example: The soap foamed up when I added water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 물을 붓자 비누가 거품이 일었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace (source code or other software elements) with a stub that exhibits the desired behaviour, typically for use in automated testing.
meanings.example: 2011, Scott Millett, Jerrel Blankenship, Matthew Bussa, Pro Agile.NET Development with SCRUM (page B-18)
meanings.exampleKorean: 2011년, 스콧 밀렛, 제럴 블랭켄십, 매튜 부사, "Pro Agile.NET Development with SCRUM" (B-18페이지)

meanings.englishDefinition: play Old Gooseberry.
meanings.example: Stop playing up Old Gooseberry and do your homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난치지 말고 숙제를 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drop; leave off; omit.
meanings.example: Please don't miss off any important details from the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보고서에서 중요한 세부 사항을 빠뜨리지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: up
meanings.example: where's my Labour membership card, so I can consider whether to tear it up? Blast, it's plastic...
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 노동당 회원 카드는 어디 있지? 찢어버릴지 말지 고려해봐야겠어. 젠장, 플라스틱이잖아...

meanings.englishDefinition: To be subjected to a punishment most prevalent in the United States in the 18th and 19th centuries in which an offender was made to straddle a fence rail held on the shoulders of two or more bearers. The victim was then paraded around town or taken to the city limits and dumped by the roadside.
meanings.example: ...they'd be along pretty soon and give me 'bout half an hour's start, and then run me down if they could; and if they got me they'd tar and feather me and ride me on a rail, sure. I didn't wait for no breakfast--I warn't hungry.
meanings.exampleKorean: ...곧 와서 나에게 30분 정도의 출발 시간을 주고, 잡을 수 있으면 나를 뒤쫓을 것이다. 만약 잡히면 타르와 깃털을 뒤집어씌우고 기둥에 태워 처벌할 것이다. 나는 아침도 기다리지 않았다. 배고프지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: transitive To waste prior work by subsequent bad decisions.
meanings.example: All our efforts to save the company were poured down the drain when the main investor pulled out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 투자자가 철수하면서 회사를 살리려는 우리의 모든 노력이 헛수고가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: into
meanings.example: They could be quickly strapped into the jump-seats with seatbelts, rather than roping them into the bed of the truck in a downpour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 폭우 속에서 트럭 침대로 끌려가는 대신 안전벨트로 점프 시트에 빨리 고정될 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront someone with (facts or evidence) that they find unpleasant in order to make them suffer.
meanings.example: I want him to rub his nose in the issue. I want a local judgement about a local or specific event, supposed to have happened in a particular region of time and space.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그가 그 문제에 대해 그의 코를 비비게 하고 싶다. 나는 특정 시간과 공간의 특정 지역에서 일어났다고 가정되는 지역적 또는 구체적인 사건에 대한 지역적 판단을 원한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rub someone's nose in.
meanings.example: This weekend you're here to rub his face in one failure, his not securing a Beaumaris to marry one of his stepdaughters, while I rub his nose in the ultimate failure for any Beaumaris, true or not, by informing him he needs to pack his bags and get...the fuck...out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말 당신은 그의 실패 중 하나, 즉 그의 의붓딸 중 한 명과 결혼하기 위해 보마리스를 확보하지 못한 것을 그의 얼굴에 문지르기 위해 여기에 왔고, 나는 그가 짐을 싸서 나가야 한다고 말함으로써 어떤 보마리스에게든 궁극적인 실패를 그의 코에 문지를 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) red in color.
meanings.example: When they had red up the house, the Auld Licht lassies sat in the gloaming at their doors on three-legged stools, patiently knitting stockings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집을 붉게 물들였을 때, 올드 리히트 소녀들은 세 발 달린 의자에 앉아 황혼 속에서 양말을 뜨개질하며 인내심 있게 기다렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive widespread acceptance among members of the general American public.
meanings.example: This new product is designed to play in Peoria and appeal to a broad audience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 신제품은 대중적으로 널리 받아들여지고 폭넓은 대중에게 어필하도록 설계되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To render stylish, to give a fashionable turn to.
meanings.example: She styled up her old jacket with some new patches.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 패치를 붙여 오래된 재킷을 멋지게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually become estranged.
meanings.example: They were best friends in college, but they've grown apart over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 대학 시절 절친이었지만, 수년 동안 점점 멀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go crazy on something or someone.
meanings.example: The crowd dumbed out when the band played their biggest hit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드가 히트곡을 연주하자 군중은 열광했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become a pirate; to join a pirating or filibustering expedition.
meanings.example: After his ship was captured, he decided to go on the account and seek revenge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 배가 나포된 후, 그는 해적이 되어 복수하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win a game having lost a service game, or during a tiebreak, to win a point against the serve having lost a point while serving.
meanings.example: The player managed to break back in the crucial final game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 결정적인 마지막 게임에서 상대방의 서브를 따내는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon, to avoid, to get rid of, to cut relations with.
meanings.example: He decided to sack off his old friends after they got into trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 옛 친구들이 곤경에 처하자 그들과의 관계를 끊기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become even.
meanings.example: Will the seemingly insatiable demand for the product ever level out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 끊임없이 계속될 것 같은 그 제품에 대한 수요가 언젠가는 안정될까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue persevering; to carry on carrying on; to carry on with one's current activity or endeavor; an expression of encouragement.
meanings.example: It's a tough situation, but you just have to keep on keeping on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 상황이지만, 그냥 계속 노력해야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quickly focus on a particular subject or object.
meanings.example: The camera zoomed in on the actor's face to capture his emotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카메라는 배우의 감정을 포착하기 위해 그의 얼굴을 확대했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To isolate or identify one particular thing from a collection that includes less relevant things.
meanings.example: The software helps to sift out irrelevant information from search results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소프트웨어는 검색 결과에서 관련 없는 정보를 골라내는 데 도움이 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To argue to a conclusion or resolution.
meanings.example: We had out our differences and in the end shook hands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 우리의 차이점에 대해 논쟁했고 결국 악수를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To argue in order to air or resolve a conflict.
meanings.example: I've been unhappy about her behaviour for some time, and last night we finally had it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀의 행동에 대해 얼마간 불만이었고, 어젯밤 우리는 마침내 그것을 따졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To host (a visiting guest) at one's house.
meanings.example: I had Tom over yesterday, and we chatted about this and that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 어제 톰을 초대했고, 이것저것 잡담을 나눴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find a way to avoid or circumvent a problem or obstacle.
meanings.example: We'll have to work around the new regulations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 새로운 규정을 우회해야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become covered with mist.
meanings.example: The windows began to mist over as the temperature dropped.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기온이 떨어지면서 창문이 흐려지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate to orgasm.
meanings.example: After a long day, he just wanted to go home and crack one off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하루를 보낸 후, 그는 집에 가서 자위하고 싶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter somewhere, and then quickly leave.
meanings.example: He was in and out of the office all morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침 내내 사무실에 왔다 갔다 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dress or act in a sexually promiscuous manner.
meanings.example: She decided to slut it up at the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파티에서 난잡하게 행동하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cast anchor or become fastened.
meanings.example: We have to moor up for the night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 밤을 위해 정박해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up; to calculate.
meanings.example: Can you foot up these figures for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 수치들을 합계해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To employ or bring out (something predictable or perennial).
meanings.example: The politician wheeled out the same old arguments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 똑같은 옛날 주장들을 꺼내 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To (cause to) come off in flakes.
meanings.example: The apartment felt damp, and the paint on the walls was beginning to flake off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아파트는 축축했고 벽의 페인트가 벗겨지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To modernise
meanings.example: They plan to modern up the old building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오래된 건물을 현대화할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have somebody in one's power, or in a compromising or helpless position.
meanings.example: Every crime commentator on cable news will have him on toast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 케이블 뉴스의 모든 범죄 해설가들이 그를 곤경에 빠뜨릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: I had a stressful day at work, so when I got home I fapped off and immediately went to bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직장에서 스트레스 받는 하루를 보냈기 때문에 집에 와서 자위하고 바로 잠자리에 들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rake in.
meanings.example: The company managed to rack in millions of dollars last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 작년에 수백만 달러를 벌어들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lead or direct someone to an enclosed space, usually a room.
meanings.example: I'll be in the library. When my guests arrive, show them in, Charlie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 도서관에 있을 거야. 손님들이 도착하면, 찰리, 그들을 안으로 안내해줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relocate to a different place.
meanings.example: I lost contact with my friend after she had moved away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 이사 간 후 친구와 연락이 끊겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep to one's own kind; to avoid intermixing or associating with other types of people.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 자기 일에 집중하다, 주제를 벗어나지 않다
meanings.example: He needs to learn to stay in his lane and stop commenting on things he doesn't understand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자기 일에 집중하고 이해하지 못하는 것에 대해 논평하는 것을 멈춰야 한다는 것을 배워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide the fact that one is gay.
meanings.example: He's been hiding in the closet for years, afraid of what his family would say.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 가족들이 뭐라고 할까 두려워 수년간 커밍아웃하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bulge or billow outward.
meanings.example: His shirt was too tight and his belly was starting to belly out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 셔츠는 너무 타이트했고 배가 나오기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to make a telephone call to someone, especially from the same building.
meanings.example: Could you ring through to reception and ask for a taxi?
