meanings.exampleKorean: 접수처로 전화해서 택시를 불러줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To slowly fill with water, as due to a leak or being washed by high waves; to begin to sink.
meanings.example: The boat began to take on water after hitting the rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 바위에 부딪힌 후 물이 새어 들어오기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take on water.
meanings.example: The old ship was taking in water and slowly sinking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 배는 물이 스며들어 천천히 가라앉고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make the ground uneven, generally unintentionally.
meanings.example: Just the footfall will churn up the wet turf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발걸음만으로도 젖은 잔디가 파헤쳐질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change direction quickly, turn, pivot, whirl about.
meanings.example: Thus hath the course of justice wheel’d about,And left thee but a very prey to time;Having no more but thought of what thou wert,To torture thee the more, being what thou art.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정의의 흐름은 그렇게 바뀌었고, 너를 시간의 먹잇감으로 남겨두었네; 네가 무엇이었는지에 대한 생각만이, 네가 지금 어떤 존재인지를 더욱 고문할 뿐.

meanings.englishDefinition: shoot one's mouth off.
meanings.example: He's always gobbin' off about his achievements, but I don't think he's done anything remarkable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신의 업적에 대해 떠벌리지만, 나는 그가 대단한 일을 했다고 생각하지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (a part from a machine).
meanings.example: But there was so much ground to make up and so much corruption to strip out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 만회해야 할 점이 너무 많았고, 제거해야 할 부패도 너무 많았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close (part of a road) by putting traffic cones around.
meanings.example: Police had to cone off the area due to the accident.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 사고 때문에 그 지역을 교통 콘으로 막아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop allowing people to join or enter; to follow a policy of isolationism.
meanings.example: After the scandal, the company decided to pull up the drawbridge and stop all external collaborations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스캔들 이후, 회사는 외부와의 교류를 차단하고 모든 외부 협력을 중단하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: suddenly decline dramatically in performance
meanings.example: The company's stock price started to fall off the cliff after the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사의 주가는 나쁜 소식 이후 급격히 떨어지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pump someone's tires.
meanings.example: I need to pump up my tires before the long drive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장거리 운전을 하기 전에 타이어 공기를 넣어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: soften the ground.
meanings.example: The rain helped to soften up the ground for planting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 땅을 부드럽게 하여 심기에 좋게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reside on the premises of one's employer (used especially for domestic staff such as nannies, cooks, maids, etc.)
meanings.example: The housekeeper has to live in from Monday to Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가정부는 월요일부터 금요일까지 고용주 집에서 거주해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not reside on the premises of one's employer (used especially for domestic staff such as nannies, cooks, maids, etc.)
meanings.example: Our nanny used to live out, but now she lives with us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 보모는 원래 출퇴근했지만, 지금은 우리와 함께 산다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hover or loiter aimlessly around a place.
meanings.example: He was just waspimg around the neighborhood, looking for trouble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문제를 찾으려 동네를 어슬렁거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pray the gay away.
meanings.example: He tried to pray the gay away, but it didn't work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기도로 동성애를 없애려 했지만 효과가 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attempt to alter one's homosexuality through prayer.
meanings.example: was my secret problem, my sin, and I earnestly hoped and prayed nearly every day of my life that it would be taken away from me. But of course, because it was an intrinsic part of me (albeit a part that I hadn't yet come to embrace), I was never able to pray the gay away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 비밀스러운 문제이자 죄였고, 나는 거의 매일 그것이 내게서 사라지기를 간절히 바라고 기도했다. 하지만 물론 그것이 내 본질적인 부분이었기 때문에 (비록 내가 아직 받아들이지 못한 부분이었지만), 나는 결코 동성애를 기도로 없앨 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the shoots of a plant:
meanings.example: You need to pinch out the side shoots to encourage the main stem to grow stronger.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주 줄기가 더 강하게 자라도록 곁순을 따내야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: open the kimono.
meanings.example: The CEO finally decided to open up the kimono and explain the company's financial situation.
meanings.exampleKorean: CEO는 마침내 속마음을 털어놓고 회사의 재정 상황을 설명하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wet a surface and be absorbed completely of a liquid
meanings.example: The new paint will soak in quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 페인트는 빨리 스며들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take up little space in a house or other building that one occupies; to live somewhere that is needlessly spacious.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 넓은 곳에 텅 빈 듯이 살다
meanings.example: After the children moved out, they began to rattle around in the big house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 이사한 후, 그들은 큰 집에서 텅 빈 듯이 살기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To allow others to use temporarily in exchange for money.
meanings.example: The company hires out construction equipment to other businesses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 다른 사업체에 건설 장비를 대여해준다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To track down; to find by searching.
meanings.example: Job, time's up, Job; but I never did expect to have to come and hunt you out in this 'ere place, Job'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잡, 시간이 다 됐소, 잡; 하지만 이런 곳에서 당신을 찾아내야 할 줄은 꿈에도 몰랐소, 잡.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emerge suddenly.
meanings.example: He would hide in corners and other dark places, and jump out, scaring one half to death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 구석이나 다른 어두운 곳에 숨어서 갑자기 나타나 사람들을 겁먹게 하곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take off leave the ground and begin flight.
meanings.example: then had to endure a four-hour-long change of bogies at Brest - from the Soviet 1520mm-wide gauge to the 1435mm Polish and West European - when our carriage, with passengers inside, was lifted off the track by giant railway jacks, giving us a brief and not entirely unpleasant feeling of levitation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 브레스트에서 네 시간 동안이나 궤도를 바꿔야 했다. 소련의 1520mm 궤간에서 폴란드와 서유럽의 1435mm 궤간으로. 승객들이 탄 우리 객차는 거대한 철도 잭에 의해 궤도에서 들어 올려졌고, 우리에게 짧지만 아주 불쾌하지 않은 부양감을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To intrude; butt or muscle in on another's territory, property, romantic partner, etc.; to be nosy.
meanings.example: He tried to geg in on our conversation even though he wasn't invited.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 초대받지 않았는데도 우리 대화에 끼어들려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel (someone) from an area because he or she lacks official permission to be there.
meanings.example: And when he came back to get the permit renewed, Du Toit said no, and endorsed him out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그리고 그가 허가 갱신을 받으러 돌아왔을 때, 뒤 투아는 거절하고 그를 쫓아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make hair flat by putting oil, water, etc. on it.
meanings.example: He slicked down his hair with gel for the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파티를 위해 머리에 젤을 발라 윤기 나게 빗어 넘겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a bad mood; be grumpy.
meanings.example: Don't talk to him right now, he's got a real cob on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금 그에게 말 걸지 마, 정말 기분이 안 좋아 보여.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle through conflict.
meanings.example: The debate was fought out in Parliament over the next few months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 논쟁은 다음 몇 달 동안 의회에서 싸워서 해결되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rise and become more alert.
meanings.example: The dog's ears pricked up at the sound of the doorbell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개가 초인종 소리에 귀를 쫑긋 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plow up.
meanings.example: The farmer ploughed up the field in preparation for planting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 파종을 준비하기 위해 밭을 갈아엎었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something, especially in an unorganized way
meanings.example: Pitne For: Also...merely out of curiosity: When you were poking about, did you perhaps run across a shipping manifest that belongs to me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 핏니 포: 또한...단지 호기심으로: 당신이 이것저것 찾아볼 때, 혹시 제 선적 목록을 발견했나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something, especially in an unorganized way
meanings.example: He was poking around in the old attic, looking for forgotten treasures.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 잊혀진 보물을 찾기 위해 오래된 다락방을 뒤지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lead or represent.
meanings.example: She was chosen to front up the new marketing campaign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새로운 마케팅 캠페인을 대표하도록 선발되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking or arguing; shut up.
meanings.example: Just wind your neck in and let me finish my story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 입 다물고 내가 이야기 끝내게 해줘.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exert considerable effort to change something or someone into a desired state.
meanings.example: The coach whipped the team into shape before the championship game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 챔피언십 경기를 앞두고 팀을 좋은 상태로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exert considerable effort to change (someone or something) into a desired
meanings.example: The manager had to lick the new employees into shape.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 신입 사원들을 제대로 교육시켜야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: whip into shape.
meanings.example: The drill sergeant beat the recruits into shape.
meanings.exampleKorean: 훈련 교관은 신병들을 혹독하게 훈련시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To smooth out undesired concepts and teachings in a text, often one of fundamental importance to a religion or ideology, by interpreting that text contrary to its spirit or original meaning.
meanings.example: While there is much room for reassessing the specifics, the basic fact that the Bible teaches some form of patriarchy cannot be interpreted away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세부 사항을 재평가할 여지가 많지만, 성경이 가부장제의 어떤 형태를 가르친다는 기본적인 사실은 다르게 해석될 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: around
meanings.example: The man tries to embrace the statue, but the statue disappears before he can get his arms around it and it reappears magically on the opposite side of the room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 남자는 조각상을 껴안으려 했지만, 조각상은 그가 팔을 두르기 전에 사라졌고 방 반대편에서 마법처럼 다시 나타났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's arms around.
meanings.example: The cost of capital is a "big" calculation. Many students have trouble wrapping their arms around it. It's therefore important to develop the whole idea before getting lost in the calculations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자본 비용은 '큰' 계산이다. 많은 학생들이 그것을 이해하는 데 어려움을 겪는다. 따라서 계산에 빠지기 전에 전체 아이디어를 개발하는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall over continually, especially through drunkenness, clumsiness, or incompetence.
meanings.example: He had been drinking all night and was falling around the place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 밤새 술을 마셨고 계속해서 비틀거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give (one's name), present (one's card) when making a social call.
meanings.example: Please send in your name card when you arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도착하시면 명함 좀 내밀어 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be serious, organized, worthwhile, etc.
meanings.example: She seems to have her act together; she's always prepared and organized.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 준비가 철저해 보인다. 항상 준비되어 있고 체계적이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To read someone's instant message(s) without giving a response, particularly on a platform that lets one see whether a message has been viewed.
meanings.example: I can't believe he left me on read after I sent him that important message.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그 중요한 메시지를 보냈는데도 읽씹하다니 믿을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: leave someone on read.
meanings.example: She always leaves me on seen when I ask her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 내가 데이트 신청을 할 때마다 항상 읽씹한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try, in public, to attract people into a business establishment.
meanings.example: The doorman waved customers in from the street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문지기는 거리에서 손짓으로 손님들을 안으로 끌어들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set (someone) free, allowing them to go their own way and do what they choose.
meanings.example: After years of strict supervision, they finally whistled him down the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수년간의 엄격한 감독 끝에, 그들은 마침내 그를 자유롭게 내버려두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unsuccessful due to neglect or inaction.
meanings.example: Without proper funding, the project will likely wither on the vine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 적절한 자금 지원 없이는 프로젝트가 방치되어 실패할 가능성이 높다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause the air to become filled with dust from a surface.
meanings.example: The construction work kicked up dust all over the neighborhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 건설 작업으로 동네 전체에 먼지가 날렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pay forward.
meanings.example: She decided to pay it forward by volunteering at the local shelter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 지역 보호소에서 자원봉사를 하며 받은 호의를 다른 사람에게 베풀기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Having been the recipient of help or a good deed, to propagate goodwill by helping another person in turn.
meanings.example: He always tries to pay forward the kindness he has received.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 받은 친절을 다른 사람에게 베풀려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To contribute money for a joint purchase.
meanings.example: As they'd expected, Nino had turned them down when they'd asked their future brother-in-law to go in on the purchase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상대로, 니노는 그들이 미래의 제부에게 공동 구매를 위해 돈을 내달라고 했을 때 거절했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To marry someone who is from another family; To avoid incest.
meanings.example: E. B. Tylor's idea that humans have only one choice, namely either to marry out or to die out, has become the most commonplace functional explanation. The necessity to marry out is always a corollary to the prohibition to marry sisters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인간에게는 다른 가문과 결혼하거나 멸망하거나 둘 중 하나뿐이라는 E. B. 타일러의 생각은 가장 흔한 기능적 설명이 되었다. 다른 가문과 결혼해야 할 필요성은 항상 자매와의 결혼 금지에 대한 필연적인 결과이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ground or base on.
meanings.example: Unversed in financial projects and calculations and budgets, his approbation of them was bottomed on his confidence in the man. But Hamilton was not only a monarchist, but for a monarchy bottomed on corruption.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재정 프로젝트, 계산, 예산에 익숙하지 않았기 때문에, 그는 그들에 대한 승인이 그 사람에 대한 그의 신뢰에 근거했다. 하지만 해밀턴은 군주주의자였을 뿐만 아니라 부패에 근거한 군주제를 지지했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a place angrily.
meanings.example: He stormed in and started shouting at everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화가 나서 들어와 모두에게 소리치기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to frolic in water splashing some of it
meanings.example: The children loved to splash about in the swimming pool.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 수영장에서 물장구 치는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep outside
meanings.example: We decided to sleep out under the stars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 별빛 아래에서 밖에서 자기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ferret around.
meanings.example: She was ferreting about in her bag for her keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 열쇠를 찾기 위해 가방 안을 뒤지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Stop it; an admonition to a person who is misbehaving.
meanings.example: Stop teasing him, leave it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 놀리지 마, 그만해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease doing something.
meanings.example: Please leave it out; your comments are not helpful.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그만하세요. 당신의 의견은 도움이 되지 않습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the sludge from.
meanings.example: They had to sludge out the bottom of the pond.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 연못 바닥의 침전물을 제거해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To outdo.
meanings.example: He always tries to get one up on his colleagues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 동료들보다 우위에 서려고 노력한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a transplanted organ: to acquire a pinkish hue as a result of blood entering it from the circulatory system.
meanings.example: The transplanted kidney began to pink up after the surgery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이식된 신장은 수술 후 분홍색을 띠기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sum up.
meanings.example: In summary, the project was a success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요약하자면, 그 프로젝트는 성공적이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die.
meanings.example: He turned up his toes last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어젯밤에 죽었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk up; to promote extravagantly.
meanings.example: The media hyped up the new movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 언론은 새 영화를 과장 광고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put in chains
meanings.example: drugged me, beaten and chained me up to the solitary confinement cell bars with metal restraints.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나에게 약물을 투여하고, 때리고, 금속 구속 장치로 감금실 창살에 쇠사슬로 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accidentally encounter (a person or situation).
meanings.example: I tumbled on an old friend at the market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시장에서 옛 친구를 우연히 만났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in the wind.
meanings.example: Trying to change his mind is like spitting in the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 마음을 바꾸려 하는 것은 헛수고나 마찬가지다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a crew
meanings.example: He decided to crew up on a cargo ship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 화물선에 승무원으로 합류하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: while away.
meanings.example: We whiled away the afternoon reading books.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오후 내내 책을 읽으며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn the boat.
meanings.example: We need to turn the boat around before it's too late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 늦기 전에 상황을 반전시켜야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try to take advantage of a confused or troubled situation.
meanings.example: He's always trying to fish in troubled waters for his own gain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신의 이익을 위해 어려운 상황을 이용하려고 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start something.
meanings.example: The director rang up the curtain on the new play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 새 연극의 막을 올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply, respond.
meanings.example: Please revert back to me by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일까지 회신해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end something.
meanings.example: Ring down the curtain on To-day And give the Past the right of way, Till fields of battle red with rust, Shine through the ashes and the dust Across the Age, and burn as plain As glowing Mars through window-pane.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘에 막을 내리고 과거에게 길을 열어주라, 녹으로 붉어진 전장터가 재와 먼지를 뚫고 시대를 가로질러 빛나며, 창문을 통해 빛나는 화성처럼 분명하게 타오를 때까지.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do a large amount of cooking at once; to prepare a great deal of cooked food.
meanings.example: When I started to lose weight, she started cooking up a storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 체중 감량을 시작하자, 그녀는 음식을 많이 만들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of.
meanings.example: I went to a party to chase away the blues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 우울함을 떨쳐버리기 위해 파티에 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To report to the authorities; to snitch on or betray to an enemy.
meanings.example: He decided to dime out his accomplice to the police.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공범을 경찰에 밀고하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cause to become spiky
meanings.example: Prices tend to spike up during the peak holiday season.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주요 휴가철에는 가격이 급등하는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to change to a lower gear in a vehicle
meanings.example: You need to change down when going downhill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내리막길에서는 기어를 낮춰야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To devolve as matter of right without reference to immediate right of possession.
meanings.example: The property will vest in interest to the heir.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 재산은 상속인에게 권리가 양도될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die.
meanings.example: The old king finally fell off his perch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늙은 왕은 마침내 죽었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remain (at work, school, organisation, country, etc.) after normal hours
meanings.example: The students had to stay back after school for detention.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 벌칙 때문에 방과 후에 학교에 남아있어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ed-form
meanings.example: He felt burnt out after working for 12 hours straight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 12시간 연속으로 일한 후 탈진한 기분이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Completely damaged by fire or heat.
meanings.example: The burnt out building was a sad sight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불에 타버린 건물은 슬픈 광경이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Have great interest in something; be enthusiastic about something.
meanings.example: She's big on jazz music.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 재즈 음악을 매우 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: be on to.
meanings.example: I think the police are on to us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 우리를 눈치챈 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come on to.
meanings.example: He came onto the stage with confidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신감 있게 무대 위로 올라왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue for too long, especially of a debate or discussion that has little likelihood of reaching an acceptable conclusion.
meanings.example: The argument rumbled on for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 논쟁은 몇 시간 동안 계속되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To uncover or unearth (something) through plowing.
meanings.example: The farmer plowed up the field to plant new crops.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 새 작물을 심기 위해 밭을 경작하여 드러냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To measure an amount or quantity or size that is needed or wanted, to mark the dimensions of something.
meanings.example: Please measure out the ingredients for the recipe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 레시피에 필요한 재료들을 측정해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anger someone with a disposition resistant to anger.
meanings.example: Her behavior would be enough to piss the Pope off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 행동은 교황조차 매우 화나게 할 정도였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of food that one has eaten: to cause one to burp.
meanings.example: That soup is repeating on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 수프 때문에 트림이 나온다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be about to become important or central.
meanings.example: With the current leader's health failing, a new successor is waiting in the wings.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현 지도자의 건강이 악화되면서, 새로운 후계자가 등장할 준비를 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a previous habit
meanings.example: The 20-year-old started life as a central defender at Charlton Athletic and even though he has been used on the right side of defence by Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp he slipped back into his old routine with real aplomb, showing his awareness with a series of crucial interceptions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 20세의 그는 찰턴 애슬레틱에서 중앙 수비수로 경력을 시작했으며, 리버풀 감독 위르겐 클롭에 의해 수비 오른쪽 측면에서 뛰었음에도 불구하고, 그는 일련의 중요한 가로채기로 그의 인식을 보여주며, 진정한 침착함으로 그의 옛 습관으로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join when not wanted; to force one's way into a situation where one is unwelcome.
meanings.example: Seemingly here was an intruder who was violating custom. Moreover, the partners had come to look upon this exceedingly rich district as their exclusive property. And so their indignation was extreme. "The low-down, ornery cuss!" said Dobbs. "The nerve of him, crowdin' in on us, just as if there wasn't lots of other places for him to go!"
meanings.exampleKorean: 겉보기에는 관습을 위반하는 침입자가 있었다. 게다가, 파트너들은 이 매우 부유한 지역을 자신들의 전유물로 여기게 되었다. 그래서 그들의 분노는 극에 달했다. "저 비열하고 야비한 놈!" 도브스가 말했다. "다른 곳도 많은데 우리한테 끼어들다니, 배짱 한번 두둑하군!"

meanings.englishDefinition: To deceive (someone) in order to convince them to give up.
meanings.example: He really thought, however, he had heard Col. Macy speak in high terms of young Mr. Ashley as a favored suitor; and Miss Adelaide always seemed pleased with his attentions; he wouldn't be bluffed out of that, any way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 그는 메이시 대령이 애슐리 씨를 총애받는 구혼자로 높이 평가하는 것을 들었다고 생각했고, 애들레이드 양은 항상 그의 관심을 기뻐하는 것처럼 보였기 때문에, 그는 어떤 식으로든 그것으로 속아 넘어가지 않을 것이라고 생각했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find or locate by smell.
meanings.example: I can smell out dishonesty from a mile away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 부정직함을 한참 전부터 맡을 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak English.
meanings.example: He's from France, but he speaks in dollars fluently.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프랑스 출신이지만 영어를 유창하게 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat in; to eat a purchased meal on the premises where one bought it, rather than taking it away.
meanings.example: We decided to dine in at the new Italian restaurant tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 오늘 밤 새 이탈리안 레스토랑에서 매장 식사를 하기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go off on; to raise hell at.
meanings.example: My parents would go slap off on me if I didn't do my homework.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 숙제를 하지 않으면 부모님은 나에게 격렬하게 화를 내실 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place one's bets in such a way as not to lose in any event.
meanings.example: With his careful strategy, he managed to stand on velvet in the poker game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 신중한 전략 덕분에 포커 게임에서 어떤 경우에도 잃지 않도록 베팅할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put (a player) out by knocking down the stump or wicket.
meanings.example: Phil was stumped out in the chase of 162.
meanings.exampleKorean: 필은 162점 추격전에서 스텀프 아웃되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: eke out.
meanings.example: She had to eek out a living on a very small salary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아주 적은 월급으로 간신히 살아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bend over backwards.
meanings.example: leaning over backwards to give gay people a place to go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 동성애자들이 갈 곳을 제공하기 위해 최대한 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw in the towel.
meanings.example: Comforting news. If that's the extent of the left's imagination, we may as well throw in the cards and admit defeat and go en masse to sign up at our nearest Anita Bryant Treatment Center!
meanings.exampleKorean: 안심되는 소식이다. 만약 그것이 좌파의 상상력의 전부라면, 우리는 항복하고 패배를 인정하고 가장 가까운 아니타 브라이언트 치료 센터에 일제히 등록하러 가는 것이 낫다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To count or sum; to add up.
meanings.example: When we tallied up the scores at the end of the game, we were surprised to find that Jack was the overall winner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 게임이 끝났을 때 점수를 합산해보니 잭이 종합 우승자라는 사실에 놀랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain the support of (a person) through bribery.
meanings.example: The company tried to buy over the union leader.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 노조 지도자를 뇌물로 매수하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adhere to a plan, protocol, or train of thought without any deviation or distraction; to stick to the straight and narrow.
meanings.example: "As I have mentioned some of the modern miracles of science and industry," he went on, "you have asked me how men ever managed to achieve them. This anecdote is my answer.... They walked in straight lines.... Each man had his 'pine tree' in the distance — his goal."
meanings.exampleKorean: "내가 과학과 산업의 현대적 기적 중 일부를 언급했듯이," 그가 말을 이었다. "사람들이 어떻게 그것을 달성했는지 물었다. 이 일화가 나의 대답이다.... 그들은 직선으로 걸었다.... 각 사람은 멀리서 자신의 '소나무'를 보았다 - 그의 목표였다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To end badly.
meanings.example: Their argument ended in tears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 논쟁은 결국 슬프게 끝났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adulterate (a drug) for the purpose of increasing its bulk, and therefore the profit when sold; to dilute, cut.
meanings.example: seems implausible. If ecstasy is going to be stepped on, you'd dilute it with something cheaper than heroin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 믿기 어렵다. 엑스터시가 희석된다면, 헤로인보다 싼 것으로 희석할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill (spaces in masonry) with mortar.
meanings.example: The bricklayers will slush up the gaps between the stones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 벽돌공들이 돌 사이의 틈을 모르타르로 채울 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: The sailors hauled up the anchor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들이 닻을 끌어올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause distress to or impose a burden on; to trouble.
meanings.example: The responsibility of the project began to weigh on him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트에 대한 책임감이 그를 짓누르기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure (something).
meanings.example: The military was ordered to lock down the entire city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대는 도시 전체를 봉쇄하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear confident.
meanings.example: Even though he was nervous, he tried to style it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴장했지만, 태연한 척하려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hit with a boxing glove.
meanings.example: “You were so good he couldn't lay a glove on you. You were terrific. He tired to hit you with his knockout punch but you skillfully avoided it - do you remember how his glove whistled harmlessly past your head?
meanings.exampleKorean: “당신은 너무 잘해서 그가 당신을 글러브로 칠 수 없었어요. 당신은 훌륭했어요. 그는 당신을 녹아웃 펀치로 치려고 했지만 당신은 능숙하게 피했어요 - 그의 글러브가 당신 머리를 스쳐 지나가는 것을 기억하나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit; to give up; to admit defeat.
meanings.example: The fighting spirit of the German army is supplied by the officer and N. C. O. It has been proved that when a German soldier sees his bayonet his fighting value is finished — he "chucks up the sponge."
meanings.exampleKorean: 독일군의 투지는 장교와 부사관에 의해 공급된다. 독일 병사가 총검을 보면 그의 전투 가치는 끝난다는 것이 증명되었다 - 그는 "스펀지를 던진다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall many times during a performance.
meanings.example: On the other hand, you may see a skater hit all his triples in practice, then he mops up the ice during the competition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반면에, 연습에서는 모든 트리플 점프를 성공하는 스케이터를 볼 수도 있지만, 경기 중에는 얼음 위에서 여러 번 넘어지기도 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come away without having received anything.
meanings.example: A former gold medallist, he nevertheless came away empty from this year's Olympic Games.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전 금메달리스트였지만, 그는 그럼에도 불구하고 올해 올림픽 게임에서는 빈손으로 돌아왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Do something (especially talking) circuitously.
meanings.example: Stop going around the houses and tell me what you really want.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빙빙 돌려 말하지 말고 진짜 원하는 게 뭔지 말해봐.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anger or annoy someone.
meanings.example: Don't get his dander up by criticizing his work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 일을 비판해서 그를 화나게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a joke concerning; to ridicule or make fun of.
meanings.example: Life: Rutgers Anthropologist Examines Sex Life On the Raritan
meanings.exampleKorean: 삶: 럿거스 인류학자가 라리탄 강의 성생활을 조사하다

meanings.englishDefinition: push up daisies.
meanings.example: This parrot is no more. It has ceased to be. It's expired and gone to see its maker. This is a late parrot. It's a stiff. Bereft of life, it rests in peace. If you hadn't nailed it to the perch it would be pushing up the daisies. It's run down the curtain and joined the choir invisible. This is an ex-parrot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 앵무새는 더 이상 살아있지 않다. 죽어서 제작자를 보러 갔다. 죽은 앵무새다. 평화롭게 잠들어 있다. 횃대에 못 박지 않았더라면 죽었을 것이다. 막을 내리고 보이지 않는 합창단에 합류했다. 이것은 죽은 앵무새다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disassemble (a system such as a computer or a car) in order to reuse the component parts.
meanings.example: He decided to part out the old car for scrap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡은 차를 분해해서 고철로 팔기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put someone in a difficult situation.
meanings.example: The poor bastard's in the shit with the syndicate in London. They want thirty grand from him. He's right on the floor at the moment. Seeing that we dropped him in it, yeah and don't forget he doesn't know it was us who had him over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불쌍한 놈은 런던의 범죄 조직 때문에 곤경에 처했어. 그들에게서 3만 파운드를 원하거든. 지금 그는 완전히 바닥이야. 우리가 그를 곤경에 빠뜨렸다는 걸 생각하면, 그래 그리고 그게 우리 짓인 줄 모른다는 것도 잊지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: Especially at restaurants, called when someone's food order is ready to be retrieved or served.
meanings.example: Order up! Table five, your pasta is ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주문하신 음식 나왔습니다! 5번 테이블, 파스타 준비되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To summon for military duty.
meanings.example: His unit was ordered up last Tuesday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 부대는 지난 화요일에 소집 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attain a decided advantage over (someone).
meanings.example: With their new technology, the company got the bulge on its competitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신기술 덕분에 그 회사는 경쟁사들보다 우위를 점했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to move unpredictably and often violently, usually unaware
meanings.example: The drowning man thrashed about in the water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 익사하는 남자가 물속에서 허우적거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease to be in love (with someone).
meanings.example: {{quote-book|en| Scott Peck| Road Less Traveled: A New Psychology of Love, Traditional Values and Spiritual Growth| and Schuster
meanings.exampleKorean: 사랑에 빠지지 않게 되는 것은 흔한 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on clothing, make-up, etc. with a camouflage pattern, to wear camouflage.
meanings.example: The hunters camouflaged up before entering the forest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼들은 숲에 들어가기 전에 위장복을 입었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be invited to dinner because of one's conversation about (a particular subject or incident).
meanings.example: She always dined out on the story of her near-death experience.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 죽을 뻔했던 경험에 대한 이야기로 저녁 식사를 대접받곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To measure something (such as wood, fabric etc), prior to marking or cutting it
meanings.example: Please measure off two yards of fabric for me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저를 위해 천 2야드를 재주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have at the ending; to have as its termination.
meanings.example: The movie ends in disaster.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 재앙으로 끝난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close all commercial businesses for the day.
meanings.example: 'Japanese nightlife can be frantic,' Joanna said, 'but they start to roll up the sidewalks early by Western standards.'
meanings.exampleKorean: '일본의 밤 문화는 열광적일 수 있지만, 서구 기준으로는 일찍 영업을 종료하기 시작한다'고 조안나가 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull the blankets or duvet up over (someone in bed); to put (someone) to bed.
meanings.example: The mother tucked up her child into bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 엄마는 아이를 침대에 눕히고 이불을 덮어주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put into a bag.
meanings.example: Shall I bag up your groceries for you, sir?
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님, 식료품을 봉투에 담아드릴까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear a smaller size of clothing.
meanings.example: If your shirt is baggy, you can size down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 셔츠가 헐렁하다면, 한 사이즈 작게 입으시면 됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in high spirits; to feel frisky.
meanings.example: After a good night's sleep, he was on his beanwater all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밤에 푹 자고 나서, 그는 하루 종일 기분이 매우 좋았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove from a list or register.
meanings.example: She struck off his name from the list.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 명단에서 그의 이름을 삭제했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To isolate a common factor from an expression.
meanings.example: We should factor out the variables that we cannot control.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 통제할 수 없는 변수들은 고려 대상에서 제외해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy utterly by burning.
meanings.example: The invaders laid the city in ashes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 침략자들은 도시를 완전히 불태워 없앴다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw money away.
meanings.example: He likes to throw money around on expensive cars.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비싼 차에 돈을 함부로 쓰는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely happy.
meanings.example: Ever since she got the promotion, she's been walking on sunshine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승진한 이후로 그녀는 매우 행복해하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean or tidy, especially a place that is very messy.
meanings.example: The room hadn't been swamped out in memory of man, and it smelled strongly of stale cigarette smoke and vomit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 방은 사람 기억으로는 청소된 적이 없었고, 오래된 담배 연기와 구토 냄새가 강하게 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find by chance, to chance upon.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Barnes| I Learned in Life from a Liquid Manure Pit
meanings.exampleKorean: 바네스는 액체 거름통에서 삶의 교훈을 얻었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become furry
meanings.example: The kitten's fur started to fur up as it grew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새끼 고양이의 털은 자라면서 보송보송해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ruin, botch, bungle; to make a mess of.
meanings.example: This whole thing's his big, bright idea because they don't like having me around crapping up their big, romantic weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 것은 그의 기발한 아이디어 때문인데, 그들은 내가 그들의 로맨틱한 주말을 망치는 것을 좋아하지 않기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrest (someone or something's) forward motion; to halt before a goal is reached.
meanings.example: The soldiers spurred their horses forward but were brought up short by Lieutenant Montoya.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 말을 앞으로 몰았지만 몬토야 중위에 의해 갑자기 멈춰야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have one's eye on, to set one's sights on, to seek, to pursue.
meanings.example: He has designs on the CEO position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 CEO 자리를 노리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get into someone's pants.
meanings.example: Things were finally comfortable between us; I didn't need my sister messing that up... or trying to get in his pants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리 사이에 드디어 편안해졌는데, 여동생이 그 상황을 망치거나 그와 성관계를 가지려고 할 필요는 없었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: enter the equation.
meanings.example: With the new evidence, the suspect's alibi no longer enters into the equation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 증거로 인해, 용의자의 알리바이는 더 이상 고려 대상이 되지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a place or abandon a situation or activity, usually quickly, because of feelings of fear or disgust.
meanings.example: noped outta there real quick
meanings.exampleKorean: 겁이 나서 재빨리 그곳을 빠져나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a witty or sarcastic retort.
meanings.example: On Monday night, President Trump tweeted a clip of Ms. Thunberg's speech, along with a message many believed to be sarcastic. "She seems like a very happy young girl looking forward to a bright and wonderful future," he wrote. "So nice to see!" Ms. Thunberg, who has nearly two million Twitter followers of her own, clapped back in her bio, which had been changed on Tuesday: "A very happy young girl looking forward to a bright and wonderful future."
meanings.exampleKorean: 월요일 밤, 트럼프 대통령은 툰베르그의 연설 영상을 올리며 많은 이들이 비꼬는 것으로 받아들인 메시지를 덧붙였다. "그녀는 밝고 멋진 미래를 기대하는 아주 행복한 어린 소녀처럼 보이는군요."라고 그는 썼다. "정말 보기 좋습니다!" 트위터 팔로워가 거의 2백만 명에 달하는 툰베르그는 화요일에 변경된 자신의 프로필 소개글에 이렇게 응수했다: "밝고 멋진 미래를 기대하는 아주 행복한 어린 소녀."

meanings.englishDefinition: To not do something out of fear; chicken out.
meanings.example: talk shit like I'll escape then bitch out at the last second.
meanings.exampleKorean: 탈출할 것처럼 허세를 부리다가 막판에 겁먹고 포기하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take an item down from a high shelf or the like.
meanings.example: Just reach me down that volume, would you.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 책 좀 내려줄래.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a false version of something.
meanings.example: He decided to phony up an excuse for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 늦은 것에 대해 변명거리를 꾸며내기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: phony up.
meanings.example: If you take time to edit a person's answers or phoney up the story in any way, the public viewers, millions of them throughout the country, are getting a distorted view of what is actually happening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람의 답변을 편집하거나 어떤 식으로든 이야기를 꾸며내는 데 시간을 쓴다면, 전국에 있는 수백만 명의 대중 시청자들은 실제로 일어나고 있는 일에 대해 왜곡된 시각을 갖게 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stare in an absent-minded or unfocused way.
meanings.example: I don't think he heard a word I said. He just sat there, staring into space.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 말을 전혀 듣지 못한 것 같다. 그는 그냥 거기 앉아서 멍하니 허공을 응시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show courage, strength, toughness, or responsibility; a gender-neutral alternative to "man up".
meanings.example: It's time for you to person up and take responsibility for your actions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 네 행동에 책임을 지고 어른스럽게 행동해야 할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To push something such that it falls.
meanings.example: Be careful not to push over the vase.
meanings.exampleKorean: 꽃병을 밀어서 넘어뜨리지 않도록 조심해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swallow (something) with a strong effort.
meanings.example: He had to worry down the large pill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 큰 알약을 삼키기 위해 애써야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To devise or solve by a process of thought.
meanings.example: We need to think out a new strategy to increase sales.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판매 증대를 위한 새로운 전략을 숙고해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To creep out; to give a creepy or sketchy feeling to someone.
meanings.example: The awkward and agitated customer began to sketch them out a bit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어색하고 동요된 고객은 그들에게 약간의 불안감을 주기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's cornflakes.
meanings.example: He tried to pee in my cornflakes by spreading rumors about me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나에 대한 소문을 퍼뜨려 내 기분을 망치려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear suddenly on a screen.
meanings.example: The word "ERROR" flashed up on the monitor, accompanied by a beeping sound.
meanings.exampleKorean: "ERROR"라는 단어가 경고음과 함께 모니터에 깜빡였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurt someone's feelings.
meanings.example: I didn't mean to tread on your corns with my comment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 말로 당신의 감정을 상하게 할 의도는 없었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss (a person) promptly.
meanings.example: The boss sent the lazy employee about his business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 게으른 직원을 해고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (something).
meanings.example: Let's nosh off some pizza before the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영화 보기 전에 피자 좀 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add sauce to; to serve sauce onto.
meanings.example: Guess I was sauced up and accidentally sold a few bottles to Julian.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 취해서 줄리안에게 실수로 몇 병 팔았던 것 같네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a contest.
meanings.example: She decided to take up the cudgels for the rights of the underprivileged.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 불우한 사람들의 권리를 위해 싸우기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: After a fly ball is caught, to tag the base (a runner) was on at the time of the pitch, thereby causing them to be out for failure to tag up.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 주자가 태그업 실패로 아웃되도록 투구 시점에 있던 베이스를 태그하다
meanings.example: The fielder doubled the runner off for the second out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야수가 주자를 더블 플레이로 아웃시켜 두 번째 아웃을 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To affix a label to (potentially dangerous machinery) to indicate why it has been shut down. To avoid accidents, only the person who affixed the label is authorized to remove it and restart the machinery.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 위험 기계에 가동 중단 이유를 알리는 라벨을 붙이다
meanings.example: The mechanic tagged out the faulty equipment before repairing it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정비사는 고장난 장비를 수리하기 전에 태그를 붙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mess up, to foul up.
meanings.example: He really boogered up the presentation with his technical difficulties.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기술적인 문제로 발표를 정말 망쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put oneself in a difficult situation.
meanings.example: He tied himself in knots trying to explain the complex theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 복잡한 이론을 설명하려다 스스로 곤경에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drown out (a person attempting to speak) by coughing deliberately.
meanings.example: The audience coughed down the speaker who was rambling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청중들은 장황하게 말하는 연설자의 목소리를 기침 소리로 덮었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cease rowing without shipping the oars.
meanings.example: The rowers decided to lie on the oars and rest for a moment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조정 선수들은 노를 젓기를 멈추고 잠시 쉬기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To settle old grudges by taking revenge.
meanings.example: He vowed to pay off old scores against those who had wronged him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신을 잘못 대했던 사람들에게 복수하겠다고 맹세했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To squeeze a large number of people in a small space.
meanings.example: If we're all going to fit in this elevator, we're going to have to crowd in!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 엘리베이터에 다 타려면, 꽉 껴서 타야 할 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape punishment, such as by being acquitted in court.
meanings.example: I don't know how she skated on those charges, but the jury said not guilty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 어떻게 그 혐의를 벗어났는지는 모르겠지만, 배심원단은 무죄라고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To react overly enthusiastically or aggressively, especially if sudden.
meanings.example: Your baby-mama will not wild out if you make sure she's straight and she's comfortable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 괜찮고 편안한지 확인하면, 당신의 아이 엄마는 난동을 부리지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide (something) into portions by weight.
meanings.example: Let's weigh out the flour into half-ounce portions to make the individual pies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개별 파이를 만들기 위해 밀가루를 0.5온스씩 무게를 달아 나누자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ride defensively so as to deflect an opposing player.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 상대 선수를 막기 위해 방어적으로 타다
meanings.example: The cyclist rode off the opponent to maintain his lead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사이클 선수는 선두를 유지하기 위해 상대 선수를 막아섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To piss away: to waste or squander time, money, etc.
meanings.example: He piddled away three hours at the bus station waiting for Gabe to show up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 게이브를 기다리며 버스 정류장에서 세 시간을 허비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry (oneself or another person or thing) completely, using a towel.
meanings.example: After his shower, he quickly toweled off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샤워 후 그는 재빨리 수건으로 몸을 말렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly defeat an opponent in a fight or competition.
meanings.example: The boxer was so dominant, he towelled up his opponent in the first round.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 너무 압도적이어서 첫 라운드에서 상대를 완전히 제압했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply a force to (an entity) such that it moves to a more distant position.
meanings.example: He pushed away the chair to make more room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 공간을 더 만들기 위해 의자를 밀어냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save or hoard (something).
meanings.example: I've been socking away cash for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 수년간 현금을 몰래 저축해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mourn or grieve so intensely as to slowly decline in health, vitality or energy.
meanings.example: She pined away after her husband's death.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 남편 사후 수척해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win (a competition or contest) handily or by a large margin.
meanings.example: The team walked away with the championship trophy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 챔피언십 트로피를 쉽게 따냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To save or hoard (something); to hide (something) in a safe spot; to squirrel away.
meanings.example: She's been tucking away cash for a rainy day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 비상금을 위해 돈을 저축해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: continue discussing or trying to solve To continue discussing or trying to solve. :at
meanings.example: He hammered away at the problem until he found a solution.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 해결책을 찾을 때까지 그 문제에 계속 매달렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reserve (something) for the future.
meanings.example: We laid away some extra food for the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 겨울을 위해 여분의 음식을 비축해 두었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To check that (someone or something) is okay or that a process is continuing appropriately; to check up on; to monitor (someone) through brief, often surreptitious checks.
meanings.example: Could you look in on the kids while I'm out?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 외출하는 동안 아이들 좀 봐줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To personally verify that (someone or something) is safe, copacetic, appropriate or satisfactory.
meanings.example: I sent Tom to check on the northside's branch's progress.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 톰을 보내 북부 지점의 진행 상황을 확인하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To account for or rely on a particular fact or notion; to calculate (something) using a set of facts.
meanings.example: I'll figure on nine people for lunch tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내일 점심은 9명으로 예상할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To express contempt and disrespect for someone or something, or to act accordingly.
meanings.example: Don't piss on my efforts!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 노력을 무시하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To attempt something that is futile; to say something that is not heeded.
meanings.example: Trying to convince him is like whistling in the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 설득하려는 것은 헛수고다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cookout.
meanings.example: We're having a cookout this Saturday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이번 주 토요일에 야외 만찬을 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook a group meal outside as a family, workplace or other social unit.
meanings.example: Let's cook out in the backyard this weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말에 뒷마당에서 야외 요리를 하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take preemptive action before a potential development becomes reality.
meanings.example: We need to get ahead of the competition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 경쟁에서 앞서나가야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resume something previously cancelled or delayed; to continue something despite known concerns.
meanings.example: They decided to go ahead with the project despite the risks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 위험에도 불구하고 프로젝트를 계속 진행하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move forward very quickly; to proceed despite obstacles.
meanings.example: The train steamed ahead towards its destination.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차는 목적지를 향해 맹렬히 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plan for the future; to look forward to something; to be excited for something happening in the future.
meanings.example: She looked ahead to her retirement with great anticipation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 은퇴를 매우 기대하며 앞날을 내다보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue doing something difficult; to continue without regard for resistance.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, they plowed ahead with their plans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차질에도 불구하고 그들은 계획을 계속 밀고 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do enough of a task in order to remain ahead of schedule; to stay ahead.
meanings.example: It's important to keep ahead of your workload.
meanings.exampleKorean: 업무량에서 앞서 나가는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: transitive To organize behind a leader or a principle.
meanings.example: The citizens lined up behind their new leader.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시민들은 새로운 지도자를 지지하며 따랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprisingly succeed despite long odds; to stealthily or unobtrusively rise up from a low-status position.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 깜짝 놀라게 성공하다, 뒤에서 치고 올라오다
meanings.example: The underdog team came up from behind to win the championship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약체 팀이 예상외로 뒤에서 치고 올라와 챔피언십에서 우승했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To miss a basket by bouncing the ball off of around the rim.
meanings.example: The shot rimmed out at the buzzer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버저 비트에 슛이 림에서 빗나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay one's tab and leave a bar.
meanings.example: I need to tab out before I go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가기 전에 외상값을 계산하고 나가야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To print something on a printer; to make a printing of something.
meanings.example: Could you print up these documents for me?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문서들을 나를 위해 출력해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To relocate to new homes repeatedly; to not live in any one place for long.
meanings.example: My dad was in the military, so we used to move around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아버지가 군인이셔서 우리는 이사를 자주 다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand on two rear legs, as an aggressive or fearful gesture.
meanings.example: The horse reared back in fright.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말은 겁에 질려 뒷발로 일어섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have begun at a particular time; to have records of something extending to a time in history.
meanings.example: This tradition dates back 1000 years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 전통은 천 년 전으로 거슬러 올라간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss (something) as unimportant.
meanings.example: He just laughed away my concerns about the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내 프로젝트에 대한 걱정을 그저 대수롭지 않게 여겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire an outside service for a project instead of handling it internally; to sign a contract for provision of a good or service.
meanings.example: The company decided to contract out the manufacturing process to a specialized firm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 제조 공정을 전문 업체에 외주를 주기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sneakily arrive near someone in a position to startle them; to startle someone by creeping up behind them.
meanings.example: The cat crept up on the unsuspecting mouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 방심한 쥐에게 몰래 다가갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart on or in a vehicle, usually dramatically or loudly with engine noises.
meanings.example: The race car roared off the starting line with a deafening sound.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경주용 자동차가 귀청을 터뜨릴 듯한 소리를 내며 출발선에서 굉음을 내며 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To loudly proclaim; to declare or shout (something).
meanings.example: He roared out his defiance to the crowd.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 군중에게 그의 저항을 소리쳐 외쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To loudly or angrily depart.
meanings.example: After the argument, he roared away in his car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 논쟁 후, 그는 차를 타고 쾅쾅거리며 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To park a vehicle in such a way as to prevent (a different vehicle) from leaving.
meanings.example: I can't leave: I've been parked in by some inconsiderate jerk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나갈 수가 없어: 어떤 무례한 놈이 차를 대놓아 막고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anticipate something eagerly.
meanings.example: The lawyer rubbed his hands together, anticipating a big win.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변호사는 큰 승리를 기대하며 손뼉을 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sweep up (a mess) thoroughly with a broom.
meanings.example: The janitor broomed up the shards of glass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 청소부가 유리 조각들을 빗자루로 쓸어 담았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: skate over.
meanings.example: She'd skated around the social circle for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 수년간 사교계 주변을 스케이트 타듯 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove dirt or mud from an area.
meanings.example: Before we start renovating, we'll need to mud out the whole building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 리모델링을 시작하기 전에 건물 전체의 진흙을 퍼내야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss or brainstorm (ideas) on a topic.
meanings.example: We bounced around some suggestions in-house and then workshopped the best ones afterward.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 내부적으로 몇 가지 제안을 자유롭게 논의한 후 가장 좋은 것들을 워크숍으로 다듬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forced into doing something that one is uncomfortable with.
meanings.example: I didn't want to, but the salesman bounced me into it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 원하지 않았지만, 영업사원이 나를 마지못해 그것을 하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull (something) up by the roots; to uproot.
meanings.example: These are gothic notions of beauty—the ivy is beautiful, but, when it insidiously destroys the trunk from which it receives support, who would not grub it up?
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것들은 고딕적인 미의 개념이다. 담쟁이덩굴은 아름답지만, 그것이 지지받는 줄기를 은밀하게 파괴할 때, 누가 그것을 뿌리째 뽑아내지 않겠는가?

meanings.englishDefinition: grub up.
meanings.example: They had to grub out the old tree stump from the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 정원에서 오래된 나무 그루터기를 뿌리째 뽑아내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (something) to someone else, especially as a replacement for oneself (such as an official office or duty); to transfer something that was originally given to oneself from someone else.
meanings.example: She handed on her responsibilities to her successor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 후임자에게 자신의 책임을 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become completely healed; to become covered in fresh, new, healthy skin.
meanings.example: The wound will heal over in a few weeks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상처는 몇 주 안에 완전히 나을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become hot throughout.
meanings.example: Make sure the food heats through completely before serving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음식을 내놓기 전에 속까지 완전히 데워졌는지 확인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose or surround (something) with a hedge, trees or other plant life.
meanings.example: I've been working on hedging in the yard with trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 나무로 마당을 둘러싸는 작업을 해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or produce by smacking.
meanings.example: He smacked out clouds of dust by striking the rug.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 양탄자를 쳐서 먼지 구름을 날려 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eject (someone) from an organization.
meanings.example: Joe was bowler hatted out of the R.A.F. for misconduct.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조는 비행 행동으로 공군에서 해고되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be cancelled due to rain.
meanings.example: The baseball game was rained out in the third inning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야구 경기는 3회에 비 때문에 취소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To properly shoe a horse.
meanings.example: The farrier came to shoe up the horses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마구장이 말이 편자를 박으러 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get drunk on wine.
meanings.example: He had too much to drink and started to wine up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술을 너무 많이 마시고 와인에 취하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on or be dressed in a sweater; to be bundled up in clothes including a sweater.
meanings.example: It's cold outside, you should sweater up before you go.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밖에 추우니 나가기 전에 스웨터를 껴입어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be expelled from the U.S. Army.
meanings.example: He was chaptered out of the army for disciplinary reasons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 징계 사유로 군에서 제명되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cope: to remain calm in adversity, to successfully manage one's difficulties.
meanings.example: Despite the bad news, she managed to hold it together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 소식에도 불구하고, 그녀는 감정을 추스를 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rappel down a mountain.
meanings.example: The climbers will rap down the steep cliff face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 등반가들은 가파른 절벽 면을 로프를 타고 내려갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abseil down a mountain.
meanings.example: They had to ab off the side of the building after the fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 화재 후 그들은 건물 측면을 로프를 타고 내려가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to enter or leave a place vigorously (as a clap of thunder); to proceed vigorously. 17th–18th c.
meanings.example: He clapped in and out of the room, barely stopping to speak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 거의 멈춰서 말하지도 않고 방에 힘차게 들어갔다가 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To qualify for a bonus or other amount of money that has been promised.
meanings.example: It will take several years for the author to earn out the initial advance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작가가 초기 선인세를 다 갚으려면 몇 년이 걸릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recuperate or recover, such as after an illness or injury.
meanings.example: After a long illness, she finally picked up her crumbs and started to feel better.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랜 투병 끝에 그녀는 마침내 기운을 차리고 나아지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away; to fuck off.
meanings.example: I said leave me alone, so why don't you just frig off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 혼자 내버려 두라고 했잖아, 그러니까 그냥 꺼져!

meanings.englishDefinition: To mess up or ruin; to fuck up.
meanings.example: Don't bother coming over, I already frigged everything up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 올 필요 없어, 내가 이미 다 망쳐놨어!

meanings.englishDefinition: whale on.
meanings.example: "One of my coke babies had retinal damage and seizures and to me that is just as severe as somebody waling on a kid," she says.
meanings.exampleKorean: “내 코카인 중독자 중 한 명은 망막 손상과 발작을 일으켰는데, 내게는 그것이 누군가가 아이를 때리는 것만큼이나 심각한 일이다”라고 그녀는 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: yack on.
meanings.example: He can yak on for hours about his favorite topic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 좋아하는 주제에 대해 몇 시간이고 계속 지껄일 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue following the same course.
meanings.example: You can stand on your rights and refuse to answer the question.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 자신의 권리를 내세워 질문에 대한 답변을 거부할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change one's clothes to prepare for outdoors activities, such as physical education; to be wearing clothes appropriate for such activities.
meanings.example: The players are in the locker room, getting ready to dress out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들이 라커룸에서 경기복으로 갈아입을 준비를 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To call and place an order for food to be delivered.
meanings.example: We were too tired to cook, so we decided to order in some pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 요리하기에 너무 지쳐서 피자를 배달시키기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surreptitiously and/or inadvertently set up two people to hook up as partners in romance, business, crime, etc.
meanings.example: The more you try to break Romeo and Juliet apart, the more you'll push them together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로미오와 줄리엣을 떼어놓으려고 할수록, 당신은 그들을 더 가깝게 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To figure out; determine by calculation, computation, or logic; make out
meanings.example: But suppose, now, that we could reckon it out, that the inmost secret of it all lay as clear and plain to us as a rule-of-three sum, should we be any the happier?
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 이제 우리가 그것을 계산하여 알아낼 수 있고, 그것의 가장 깊은 비밀이 우리에게 삼단 논법처럼 명확하고 분명하게 놓여 있다면, 우리가 더 행복해질 수 있을까?

meanings.englishDefinition: To add up; count up; compute the amount of; tally; come to an accounting of.
meanings.example: How many things are there which I ought to reckon up, that I have neglected; and how many others are there, which, if I did reckon them up, would overwhelm me with confusion. The grocer has given me a subject that I shall do well to pursue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 무시해야 할 것들은 얼마나 많으며, 만약 내가 그것들을 총계를 낸다면 나를 혼란에 빠뜨릴 것들은 또 얼마나 많은가. 식료품 가게 주인이 내가 잘 추구해야 할 주제를 나에게 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come without an appointment to a place that would normally require one.
meanings.example: If you walk in, you're going to have to wait at least an hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예약 없이 가면 최소 한 시간은 기다려야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be careful and alert to the possibility of danger.
meanings.example: In this competitive business, you must always keep your guard up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 경쟁적인 비즈니스 환경에서는 항상 경계심을 늦추지 말아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get rid of.
meanings.example: I need to get rid of this old furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 낡은 가구를 처분해야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw from a cask and put into bottles.
meanings.example: The winery bottles off the wine after it has aged.
meanings.exampleKorean: 와이너리는 와인이 숙성된 후 병에 담습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rotate about (an axis); to orbit.
meanings.example: The Earth revolves around the Sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지구는 태양 주위를 돈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's end away.
meanings.example: They were caught getting their end in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 성교하는 중에 붙잡혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fob off.
meanings.example: Don't try to fub me off with a cheap excuse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 값싼 핑계로 나를 속여 넘기려 하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die by violence, especially by hanging.
meanings.example: He was afraid he would die in his shoes for his crimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 범죄로 인해 교수형을 당할까 봐 두려워했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn up trumps.
meanings.example: She really came up trumps when we needed help.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 도움이 필요했을 때 그녀는 정말 기대 이상으로 활약했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To omit (some portion of program logic) through optimization, when it is found to be unused or unnecessary.
meanings.example: The loop that counts from 1 to 1,000,000 is optimized out by the compiler, because nothing happens inside the loop and the value is not used afterwards.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1부터 1,000,000까지 세는 루프는 루프 내에서 아무 일도 일어나지 않고 나중에 값이 사용되지 않기 때문에 컴파일러에 의해 최적화되어 제거됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Be sufficiently noteworthy as to be remembered by future generations.
meanings.example: This heroic victory will go down in history forever.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영웅적인 승리는 역사에 영원히 기록될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stall completely; to come to an end or quit through stalling
meanings.example: My car was running fine and then, for no reason, it stalled out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 차는 잘 달리고 있었는데, 이유 없이 시동이 꺼졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To emit a series of irregular, erratic popping or spitting sounds before shutting off.
meanings.example: better give me something so I don't die / Novocaine for the soul / Before I sputter out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죽지 않게 뭔가 줘 / 영혼을 위한 노보케인 / 내가 꺼지기 전에.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disgust or repulse.
meanings.example: hate going in my parents' room. It skeeves me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모님 방에 가는 것이 싫다. 역겹다.

meanings.englishDefinition: with
meanings.example: I didn't go down there on my own, I went down with Sam and Betty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 혼자 그곳에 간 것이 아니라 샘과 베티와 함께 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To test someone to see how much bad behaviour they will tolerate; to try to deceive someone to see how gullible they are.
meanings.example: He's always trying it on with his boss to see what he can get away with.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 상사에게 얼마나 봐줄 수 있는지 시험해 본다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter into an irregular form of common-law marriage, in which both parties jumped over a broomstick.
meanings.example: In some historical contexts, couples would marry over the broomstick as a symbolic union.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일부 역사적 맥락에서, 커플들은 상징적인 결합으로서 빗자루 위를 뛰어넘어 결혼했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use or abuse narcotics.
meanings.example: He was found to have been doping up for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년간 마약을 해온 것이 밝혀졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move around quickly, perhaps frequently changing directions.
meanings.example: The children darted about the playground, full of energy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 에너지가 넘쳐 놀이터를 뛰어다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat up or handle roughly.
meanings.example: He was tired of his boss smacking him around at work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 직장에서 상사에게 구타당하는 것에 지쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ecstatic.
meanings.example: She's been walking on air ever since she got the promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진한 이후로 붕 뜬 기분이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain (something) by taking advantage of the charity of (someone or something).
meanings.example: He's been mooching off his parents for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 년 동안 부모에게 빌붙어 살고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change, usually in regards to how one feels, thinks, or behaves.
meanings.example: People will switch up on you in the middle of the stream and just as soon as you turn your back they will stab you in it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사람들은 흐름 중간에 태도를 바꾸고, 등을 돌리자마자 당신을 배신할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of an animal: to cost more to feed than it is worth.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 먹이는 데 드는 비용이 가치보다 더 들다
meanings.example: That dog eats its head off; I should have gotten a smaller breed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 개는 먹이는 데 돈이 너무 많이 들어. 더 작은 품종을 살 걸 그랬어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escort someone out of an event or place.
meanings.example: It's getting late; I'll show myself out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦었네요. 제가 나가겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hunt for; to seek out.
meanings.example: She had agreed with Malone's conclusions. She had even hunted up a good deal of evidence of her own.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 말론의 결론에 동의했다. 그녀는 스스로 상당한 증거를 찾아내기도 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To validate or check for accuracy; verify the balance of numbers or figures; audit
meanings.example: The prior year's numbers can generally be tied out to previously published numbers, and applicable accounting systems are a good source to garner both the prior and current year numbers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전년도 수치는 일반적으로 이전에 발표된 수치와 대조될 수 있으며, 해당 회계 시스템은 전년도 및 당해 연도 수치를 모두 얻는 데 좋은 출처입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stash or lay away for later use
meanings.example: He decided to hive up some extra cash for a rainy day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비상금을 위해 여윳돈을 좀 숨겨두기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To seem insignificant by comparison to something much more important.
meanings.example: All other problems pale into insignificance compared to the health of your family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가족의 건강에 비하면 모든 다른 문제들은 아무것도 아닌 것처럼 보인다.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk someone's ear off.
meanings.example: He can talk your arm off about his favorite hobby.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신이 좋아하는 취미에 대해 실컷 이야기할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk someone's ear off.
meanings.example: She was so excited that she talked my head off all the way home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 너무 신이 나서 집에 오는 내내 나에게 실컷 이야기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carefully go through a set of objects, or a collection of information, in order to find something.
meanings.example: The detective had to sift through a lot of evidence to find the killer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 살인자를 찾기 위해 많은 증거를 샅샅이 조사해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swallow with difficulty.
meanings.example: I was already stuffed, but I managed to choke down some more apple pie anyway. It was so good that I couldn't stop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이미 배가 불렀지만, 어쨌든 사과 파이를 더 억지로 삼켰다. 너무 맛있어서 멈출 수가 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass in a tedious manner.
meanings.example: to linger out a miserable life
meanings.exampleKorean: 비참한 삶을 지루하게 보내다

meanings.englishDefinition: play into someone's hands
meanings.example: By refusing to negotiate, the company is playing right into their competitor's hands.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상을 거부함으로써, 그 회사는 경쟁사의 계획에 유리하게 작용하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start crying, especially in a way that the speaker finds irritating.
meanings.example: She started to turn on the waterworks when she heard the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 나쁜 소식을 듣자마자 갑자기 울음을 터뜨리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expand, to widen, to unwrap, unfold or uncover, to spread out from a compact state.
meanings.example: though the station the deep-throated exhaust of G.C.R. engines, and particularly the "Directors", could be heard in ascending crescendo as the drivers opened out to take the gradient.
meanings.exampleKorean: 역에서는 G.C.R. 엔진의 깊은 배기음, 특히 "디렉터스"의 소리가 운전사들이 경사를 타기 위해 속도를 높이면서 점점 커지는 크레센도로 들릴 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure stoically.
meanings.example: Sometimes you just have to gut out the difficult times and keep moving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 때로는 그저 힘든 시간을 꿋꿋이 견뎌내며 계속 나아가야만 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a black person, to behave aggressively or violently.
meanings.example: He started to chimp out when he lost the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 게임에서 지자 폭력적으로 행동하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: chimpout.
meanings.example: The chimp out at the bar led to a police investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 술집에서의 폭력적 행동은 경찰 조사를 초래했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be angry or defensive.
meanings.example: He's had his back up all morning. There's no point in trying to talk to him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침 내내 화가 나 있었다. 그와 이야기하려는 것은 소용없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discard one's weapons and cease fighting.
meanings.example: The rebels finally agreed to lay down their arms and negotiate for peace.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 마침내 무기를 내려놓고 평화 협상을 하는 데 동의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give one's life, to die. :for
meanings.example: Gold was there, and get at it they would, and at any cost. Many of our fellow-passengers suffered a rude awakening, and more than one man laid down his life in the quest for gold that had taken so fierce a hold upon him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 황금이 있었고, 그들은 어떤 대가를 치르더라도 그것을 얻으려 했다. 우리 동승자 중 많은 이들이 혹독한 현실을 맞닥뜨렸고, 금에 대한 강렬한 욕망에 사로잡힌 한 남자 이상이 금을 찾아 목숨을 바쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave the wickets without having been put out.
meanings.example: The batsman carried out his bat after scoring a century.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 타자는 센추리를 기록한 후 아웃되지 않고 경기를 마쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: patch up.
meanings.example: He tried to piece up the broken vase with glue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 접착제로 깨진 꽃병을 수선하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steer away from the wind
meanings.example: The sailboat bore away from the shore to catch the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 범선은 바람을 잡기 위해 해안에서 멀어져 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To score an own goal.
meanings.example: The soccer team just scored on itself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 축구팀은 방금 자책골을 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a man: to urinate.
meanings.example: He had to wring out his sock after a long hike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 긴 하이킹 후에 소변을 봐야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A contracting iris shot used at the end of a scene.
meanings.example: The scene irised out to reveal a wider view of the desert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장면이 동그랗게 사라지면서 사막의 더 넓은 경치가 드러났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a contracting iris shot at the end of a scene.
meanings.example: The editor used an iris out to transition between the two scenes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편집자는 두 장면 사이의 전환을 위해 원형 사라짐 효과를 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: An expanding iris shot used at the beginning of a scene.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 장면 시작 시 쓰이는 확대되는 아이리스 샷
meanings.example: The film opened with a slow iris in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 느린 아이리스 인으로 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use an expanding iris shot at the beginning of a scene.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 장면 시작 시 확대 아이리스 샷 사용
meanings.example: The director decided to iris in on the main character's face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 주인공의 얼굴에 아이리스 인을 사용하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stubborn; to refuse to change one's mind or stance.
meanings.example: He stubbornly up when asked to apologize.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사과하라는 요청에 고집을 부렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retaliate; to give a sharp retort.
meanings.example: He bit back his anger and managed a courteous reply.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 분노를 억누르고 정중한 답변을 할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To retrace events so as to determine where an operation went wrong and who was responsible.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 사건을 되짚어 잘못된 점과 책임자 파악
meanings.example: We need to walk back the cat to find out why the project failed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 왜 실패했는지 알아내기 위해 사건을 되짚어봐야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit in with difficulty; squeeze in.
meanings.example: He managed to edge in a few words during the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의 중에 몇 마디 말을 겨우 끼워 넣을 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop using bad language, or atone for doing so.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 욕설 사용 중단, 잘못된 언행에 대해 속죄
meanings.example: Wash out your mouth, your lies are gettin' rusty
meanings.exampleKorean: 입을 씻어내, 네 거짓말은 녹슬어가고 있어

meanings.englishDefinition: To send away; to shoo.
meanings.example: The birds were shooed off the crops.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새들이 농작물에서 쫓겨났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be plain for all to see, obvious, easy to see, very noticeable.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 매우 분명한, 명백한, 눈에 잘 띄는
meanings.example: His lie stuck out a mile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 거짓말은 명백했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stick out a mile.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 매우 분명한, 명백한, 눈에 잘 띄는
meanings.example: Her talent for music stood out a mile from a young age.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 음악적 재능은 어린 나이에도 불구하고 매우 두드러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a crush on, be infatuated with (especially when unannounced)
meanings.example: I had a huge crush on my teacher in high school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 고등학교 때 선생님에게 엄청 반했었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inform one's workplace or school that one temporarily cannot attend work or school due to illness.
meanings.example: He had to call in sick because he had a terrible fever.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 심한 열 때문에 병가를 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To appear in succession, as if from a film spool.
meanings.example: The names of the winners began to spool out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우승자들의 이름이 연이어 나오기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold back or suppress (an emotion, expression of emotion, or utterance), with difficulty.
meanings.example: When she slapped me, I tried hard to choke back my tears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 나를 때렸을 때, 나는 눈물을 억지로 참으려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say (something) with difficulty, while or as if choking.
meanings.example: He choked out the words, 'I love you'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 목이 메어 '사랑해'라는 말을 간신히 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in the wind.
meanings.example: Trying to convince him is like pissing into the wind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 설득하려는 것은 헛수고다.

meanings.englishDefinition: blow out of proportion.
meanings.example: Don't blow this small issue out of proportions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 작은 문제를 과장하지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get out of bed.
meanings.example: He tumbled up early to start his day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루를 시작하기 위해 일찍 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause or force a price, quantity, rate, etc. to decrease.
meanings.example: The new competition is driving down prices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 경쟁이 가격을 낮추고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use an acid-resistant liquid (stopout) to cover the parts of a surface that should not be bitten.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 산에 부식되지 않는 액체로 보호할 부분 덮기
meanings.example: The artist used stop out to protect the areas not to be etched.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예술가는 식각되지 않을 부분을 보호하기 위해 스톱아웃을 사용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop criticizing someone.
meanings.example: Just get off my case! I know what I'm doing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 간섭 좀 그만해! 내가 뭘 하는지 알아.

meanings.englishDefinition: swear upon.
meanings.example: I swear on my mother's grave that I didn't do it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어머니 묘를 걸고 맹세하건대, 나는 그것을 하지 않았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To jump around from place to place as a small bird.
meanings.example: The little bird hopped about in the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 새가 정원에서 이리저리 뛰어다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become drowsy and fall asleep; nod off.
meanings.example: He was so tired that he started to nod out during the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 너무 피곤해서 영화를 보는 동안 꾸벅꾸벅 졸기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss or reject; fob off; put off by evasion.
meanings.example: She tried to fluff off his concerns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 걱정을 무시하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stay calm; to not get flustered or angry.
meanings.example: Keep your knickers on, mate. It was only a joke.
meanings.exampleKorean: 진정해, 친구. 그냥 농담이었어.

meanings.englishDefinition: walk on eggshells.
meanings.example: I felt like I was treading on eggshells around my new boss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 상사 주변에서는 마치 달걀 껍데기를 밟는 것처럼 조심스러웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get along well; to make friends.
meanings.example: The two new colleagues shook together quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 두 명의 새 동료는 금방 친해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vacillate.
meanings.example: He kept shifting about his decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결정을 계속 망설였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To offend someone.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 누군가를 불쾌하게 하다, 기분을 상하게 하다
meanings.example: Be careful not to tread on anyone's toes with your comments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 발언으로 누구의 기분도 상하게 하지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trim; to set in order.
meanings.example: He snod up his hair before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 면접 전에 머리를 다듬었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: thumbs down.
meanings.example: Exploiting a member's indecision are lobbyists who station themselves outside the House and Senate chambers and signal members with a thumb up or a thumb down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집권층의 결정을 이용하는 것은 하원과 상원 회의장 밖에 자리 잡고 엄지손가락을 위로 또는 아래로 움직여 의원들에게 신호를 보내는 로비스트들이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get (a passing vehicle) to stop and give one a ride, signalling to the driver by holding out one's thumb (as a hitchhiker).
meanings.example: I was stranded in the middle of nowhere, but luckily I managed to thumb down a passing car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아무것도 없는 곳에 갇혔지만, 운 좋게 지나가는 차를 히치하이킹으로 세울 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: level a surface by applying furring to it.
