meanings.example: The carpenter had to fur out the wall before he could hang the cabinets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목수는 캐비닛을 걸기 전에 벽에 덧대어 수평을 맞춰야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: heave in sight.
meanings.example: The ship began to heave in view on the horizon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 수평선 너머로 보이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: heave in sight.
meanings.example: The ship began to heave in view on the horizon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 수평선 너머로 보이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: heave in sight.
meanings.example: The ship began to heave into sight on the horizon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 수평선 너머로 보이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: heave in view.
meanings.example: The ship began to heave into view on the horizon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 수평선 너머로 보이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To park one's motor vehicle.
meanings.example: He decided to park up by the side of the road and take a break.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 길가에 차를 세우고 잠시 쉬기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be under an obligation to pay out on a debt; to owe
meanings.example: In all, we owed out about $600,000 as opposed to $300,000 in receivables.
meanings.exampleKorean: 총 30만 달러의 수취 채권에 비해 60만 달러를 갚아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work out or fix a problem, usually in a makeshift fashion.
meanings.example: Backdoors and hidden control codes were found and deleted, alternate routes were explored, and power systems were kludged out of anything available—one major router in Boston was being powered by teams of bicycle riders taking shifts turning portable generators.
meanings.exampleKorean: 백도어와 숨겨진 제어 코드가 발견되어 삭제되었고, 대체 경로가 탐색되었으며, 사용 가능한 모든 것으로 전력 시스템이 임시방편으로 해결되었습니다. 보스턴의 주요 라우터 중 하나는 휴대용 발전기를 돌리는 교대 근무를 하는 자전거 타는 팀에 의해 전력이 공급되고 있었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attend to one's duties; to do what one would normally be doing if not detained, distracted, etc.
meanings.example: Calpurnia appeared in the front door and yelled, “Lemonade time! You all get in outa that hot sun ‘fore you fry alive!” Lemonade in the middle of the morning was a summertime ritual. Calpurnia set a pitcher and three glasses on the porch, then went about her business. Being out of Jem’s good graces did not worry me especially. Lemonade would restore his good humor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캘퍼니아가 현관에 나타나 소리쳤다. “레모네이드 시간이야! 타 죽기 전에 다들 저 뜨거운 햇볕에서 들어와!” 아침 한가운데 레모네이드는 여름 의식이었다. 캘퍼니아는 현관에 투수와 잔 세 개를 놓고는 자기 일에 집중했다. 쳄의 호감을 잃는 것은 특별히 나를 걱정시키지 않았다. 레모네이드가 그의 좋은 기분을 회복시켜 줄 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accuse someone of something, or reproach them for it.
meanings.example: throw something in someone's teeth
meanings.exampleKorean: 누군가의 약점을 비난하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To accuse someone of something, or reproach them for it.
meanings.example: He cast her past mistakes in her teeth, trying to undermine her confidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀의 자신감을 떨어뜨리려 그녀의 과거 잘못을 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To link to an external website.
meanings.example: A brief aside about hoarding PageRank: many people have taken this to an extreme and built sites where they refused to link out to other quality websites, because they feared losing visitors and link juice.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페이지 랭크를 쌓아두는 것에 대한 짧은 여담: 많은 사람들이 이를 극단으로 몰고 가서 다른 양질의 웹사이트로 연결하는 것을 거부하는 사이트를 만들었는데, 이는 방문자와 링크 주스를 잃을까 두려워했기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To amplify; to increase the power, interest, intensity, volume, etc. of (something).
meanings.example: The band decided to amp up the energy for their final performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드는 마지막 공연을 위해 에너지를 증폭시키기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to increase the power; interest; intensity; volume; level etc. of
meanings.example: The director tried to torque up the suspense in the final scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 마지막 장면의 긴장감을 높이려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to fire (a weapon, or ammunition); to shoot (using a firearm)
meanings.example: They loosed off a couple of rounds at the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 적에게 몇 발을 발사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to sunbathe; to soak up the sun
meanings.example: We spent the morning sunning up on the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 해변에서 오전 내내 햇볕을 쬐었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be engaged in meaningless activity.
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon puttering about the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 집 안에서 빈둥거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suppress (a feeling or emotion).
meanings.example: He fought down the urge to laugh.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 웃고 싶은 충동을 억눌렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fix to the ground, with or as if with tacks.
meanings.example: We need to tack down the posters to the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포스터를 벽에 압정으로 고정해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak plainly.
meanings.example: Let me speak in terms: I don't think this plan will work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 솔직하게 말하자면, 이 계획은 성공하지 못할 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be bushy; to protrude in a thick tuft.
meanings.example: The rose bushes have begun to bush out after the spring rain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 봄비 후에 장미 덤불이 무성해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To score one's first points
meanings.example: now Leclerc has won two races while Vettel is still to get off the mark this year. Looking at the remaining races - the tracks they are on, and the pattern of Ferrari's form - it is far from certain he will get another chance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 르클레르는 두 번의 우승을 차지했지만 베텔은 올해 아직 첫 득점도 올리지 못했다. 남은 경주들, 그들이 달리는 트랙, 그리고 페라리의 폼 패턴을 보면 그가 또 다른 기회를 얻을지는 전혀 확실하지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch up with and get a chance at passing or overtaking.
meanings.example: lap 23, Hamilton got a run on Leclerc into the second chicane after the two had overtaken Nico Hulkenberg's out-of-stop-sequence Renault down the main straight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 23랩, 두 사람이 메인 스트레이트에서 니코 훌켄베르크의 르노를 추월한 후 해밀턴이 두 번째 시케인에서 르클레르를 추월할 기회를 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain something, especially information, about (something) from a source (especially electronic sources).
meanings.example: I need to search up the definition of this word.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 단어의 정의를 찾아봐야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To organize one's affairs so that one is ready to act efficiently.
meanings.example: "Senator Allen was one reason he made up his mind to go to Washington. He'd like to get some of the windbags out of our way and line up our ducks."
meanings.exampleKorean: 앨런 상원의원이 워싱턴에 가기로 마음먹은 이유 중 하나였다. 그는 우리 길을 막는 수다쟁이들을 치우고 일을 효율적으로 처리할 준비를 하고 싶어했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tighten something that is slack so that it is taut.
meanings.example: You need to take up the slack in your cables so shifters and brakes function properly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 변속기와 브레이크가 제대로 작동하도록 케이블의 느슨한 부분을 조여야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to control a high ball with the chest (for example, in order to shoot, dribble, or pass it)
meanings.example: The striker chest down the ball and scored a goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공격수는 가슴으로 공을 받아내 골을 넣었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shun or snub; to give the cold shoulder.
meanings.example: Instead, she chose to ice him out without a glance as they passed on the stairs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대신 그녀는 계단에서 마주쳤을 때 힐끗 보지도 않고 그를 냉대하기로 선택했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To don a pair of (e.g. surgical) gloves.
meanings.example: The surgeon gloved up and began the operation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 외과 의사는 장갑을 끼고 수술을 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get drunk.
meanings.example: He got his load on at the party last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 어젯밤 파티에서 술에 취했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dust off.
meanings.example: preparations are being dusted down and refreshed and will continue to stand their owners in good stead in November.
meanings.exampleKorean: 준비가 먼지를 털고 새롭게 되어 11월에도 계속해서 소유주들에게 좋은 결과를 가져다줄 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: BOLO.
meanings.example: Hoadley issued a "be on the lookout" ("BOLO"), describing the person as a tall, thin, black male, wearing light colored clothing and traveling on foot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 호들리는 키가 크고 마른 흑인 남성이 밝은 색 옷을 입고 걸어가고 있다고 묘사하며 "수배"("BOLO")를 발령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on the lookout
meanings.example: The wanted announcement and the be on the lookout for Salerno Bondi, (BOLO) was aired on radio and television, and all points bulletins were put out for police on patrol to be on the lookout for Salerno.
meanings.exampleKorean: 살레르노 본디에 대한 수배 발표와 수색 요청(BOLO)이 라디오와 텔레비전에 방송되었고, 순찰 중인 경찰에게 살레르노를 찾도록 모든 지점에 전단이 배포되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike out or cross out; to draw a line through one or more written words.
meanings.example: Please score out the incorrect answers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 틀린 답에 줄을 쳐서 지워주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To strike out or cross out; to draw a line through one or more written words.
meanings.example: The editor scored through the unnecessary sentences.
meanings.exampleKorean: 편집자는 불필요한 문장들에 줄을 쳐서 지웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close, seal, or end something by tying a knot.
meanings.example: Tie off the end of the rope securely.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밧줄 끝을 단단히 매듭지어 끝내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flatter or suck up to someone
meanings.example: He's always brushing up to the boss to get a promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 승진하기 위해 항상 상사에게 환심을 사려 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pull upwards, e.g. to pull plants out of the ground.
meanings.example: He champed up the weeds from the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 정원에서 잡초를 위로 뽑아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do for; to finish or use up.
meanings.example: The intense competition has completely chawed up his energy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 치열한 경쟁이 그의 에너지를 완전히 소진시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scout; to go on a scouting mission.
meanings.example: Foster wanted Hateher to go on another scout and he told the Lieutenant that if he would pay back his money that he had paid out for the State, so he could get some blankets—as the weather was getting bad—that he would go on the scout.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포스터는 해처가 또 정찰하러 가기를 원했고, 주를 위해 지불한 돈을 돌려주면 담요를 살 수 있을 것이라고 장교에게 말했고, 날씨가 나빠지고 있었기 때문에 정찰하러 갈 것이라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trick or deceive deliberately; to play a prank on.
meanings.example: Are you kidding me on?
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 놀리는 거야?

meanings.englishDefinition: bat around certain senses
meanings.example: Let's bat about some ideas for the new project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 프로젝트에 대한 아이디어를 이리저리 논의해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get off at Haymarket.
meanings.example: When we asked if girls got pregnant, he said that occasionally that happened. How did most couples avoid it? He replied: The bloke would have to get off at Redfern instead of going on to Central.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여자들은 임신을 하느냐고 물었을 때, 가끔 그런 일이 생긴다고 말했다. 대부분의 커플은 어떻게 피했는가? 그는 레드펀에서 센트럴까지 계속 가는 대신 레드펀에서 내려야 한다고 대답했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ridicule, to make fun of (someone).
meanings.example: They were ragging on him because he wore a funny hat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그가 이상한 모자를 썼기 때문에 그를 놀렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To evict someone, e.g. from a form of transport, perhaps for unruly behaviour, or because the final destination has been reached.
meanings.example: The passengers were chucked off the train for fighting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 승객들은 싸움 때문에 기차에서 쫓겨났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be rare, not abundant, not easily acquired.
meanings.example: Don't expect a raise every year; money doesn't grow on trees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매년 임금 인상을 기대하지 마세요. 돈은 나무에서 자라지 않아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: work one's butt off.
meanings.example: I worked my little buns off making my special souffle for you!" She said
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신을 위해 특별한 수플레를 만드느라 정말 열심히 일했어요!" 그녀가 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To score a try
meanings.example: The player dotted down the ball in the corner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수는 코너에서 공을 트라이했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit, to stop engaging in a line of work or type of activity.
meanings.example: Figured I'd find me some shack or lean-to and hang up my spurs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 헛간이나 간이 차고를 찾아서 내 경력을 마무리할 생각이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to think very hard about.
meanings.example: I've been beating my brains out trying to solve this problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제를 풀기 위해 머리를 싸매고 고민해왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: change horses in midstream.
meanings.example: “It is not good to change horses in mid-stream”, is what the famous American President Abraham Lincoln said.
meanings.exampleKorean: 에이브러햄 링컨 전 미국 대통령은 "중도에서 방침을 바꾸는 것은 좋지 않다"고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide informational feedback to.
meanings.example: His employees fed back a lot more than he wanted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 직원들은 그가 원했던 것보다 훨씬 더 많은 피드백을 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erode.
meanings.example: The river is eating away at the river bank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강이 강둑을 침식하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a mistake, a bad decision or act senselessly and get into an extremely difficult situation.
meanings.example: If you are not careful, you can fall into a trap of focusing too much on getting more loans instead of paying off other bills.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조심하지 않으면, 다른 청구서를 갚는 대신 더 많은 대출을 받는 데 너무 집중하는 함정에 빠질 수 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop worrying about or trying to do something about; to ignore.
meanings.example: Roth's continued productivity ensures that we can't yet close the book on his career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로스의 지속적인 생산력 덕분에 우리는 아직 그의 경력에 대해 더 이상 신경 쓰지 않을 수 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to work very hard.
meanings.example: He was sweating his guts out to finish the project on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내기 위해 뼈 빠지게 일하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sweat one's guts out.
meanings.example: The team fought their guts out in the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 마지막 경기에서 사력을 다해 싸웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sweat one's guts out.
meanings.example: The athlete played her guts out, even though she was injured.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수는 부상에도 불구하고 전력을 다해 플레이했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sweat one's guts out.
meanings.example: He slogged his guts out for years to build his business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사업을 구축하기 위해 수년간 힘들게 고생하며 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sweat one's guts out.
meanings.example: She worked her guts out to get the promotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 승진하기 위해 열심히 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sacrifice oneself for the sake of others.
meanings.example: He fell on a grenade to save his comrades.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 동료들을 구하기 위해 수류탄 위에 몸을 던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen attentively.
meanings.example: The students pinned their ears back as the professor explained the complex theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 교수님이 복잡한 이론을 설명하자 학생들이 귀를 기울였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To benefit in the long run from what seems initially to be a setback.
meanings.example: In fact, many of their winning mantras essentially describe the notion of falling up: "I've failed over and over again in my life," Jordan once said, "and that is why I succeed."
meanings.exampleKorean: 사실, 그들의 많은 성공 좌우명은 본질적으로 '결과적으로 이득을 보는 것'이라는 개념을 묘사합니다. 조던은 한때 "내 인생에서 실패를 거듭했지만, 그것이 내가 성공한 이유다"라고 말했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift or carry anything along the deck; to help in hauling along.
meanings.example: We had to light along the heavy cargo to the ship.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 무거운 짐을 배로 끌어당겨야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a man: to have sexual intercourse with (a woman).
meanings.example: And Judah said unto Onan, Go in unto thy brother's wife, and marry her, and raise up seed to thy brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유다는 오난에게 이르되 네 형수에게 들어가서 네 형수로서의 의무를 행하여 네 형을 위하여 씨를 세우라 하였으나

meanings.englishDefinition: knock together assemble hastily.
meanings.example: I managed to slap together a quick meal before the guests arrived.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님이 도착하기 전에 재빨리 간단한 식사를 준비할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fondle or grope the breasts of.
meanings.example: She felt uncomfortable when he started to tit her up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 그녀의 가슴을 만지기 시작했을 때 그녀는 불편함을 느꼈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show off. from 18th c.
meanings.example: He likes to show away his expensive watch whenever he can.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기회가 있을 때마다 비싼 시계를 과시하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cut down to size.
meanings.example: It's part of his control-freak sickness, his let's-play-get-the-girl. Let's tell her she's great, beautiful, sexy, fun... then let's bring her down to size. All the way down until she's somewhere way beneath him, leaving Marcus on top and in a very superior position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것은 그의 통제광적인 질병의 일부이며, '소녀를 얻는 놀이'입니다. 그녀가 대단하고, 아름답고, 섹시하고, 재미있다고 말한 다음 그녀의 거만함을 꺾읍시다. 그보다 훨씬 아래에 있을 때까지 완전히 꺾어서, 마커스가 위에 있고 매우 우월한 위치에 있도록 말입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cut down to size.
meanings.example: “Watch it with the delirious power trips, pal,” she warned him. “Keep it up, and I'll have to knock you back down to size."
meanings.exampleKorean: “정신 나간 권력 남용은 그만해, 친구,” 그녀는 그에게 경고했다. “계속하면 널 거만함을 꺾어버릴 거야.”

meanings.englishDefinition: cut down to size.
meanings.example: If parents try to trim a 7-year-old egoist down to size with sarcastic quips or disparaging comments, they only add to his sense of inferiority and thereby agitate his continued construction of a defensive front.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부모가 비꼬는 농담이나 경멸적인 말로 7살짜리 이기주의자의 거만함을 꺾으려 하면, 오히려 열등감을 더해주고 방어적인 태세를 계속 구축하도록 부추길 뿐이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: cut down to size.
meanings.example: If he starts playing the big, tough hombre on the southside, those boys are just liable to whittle him down to size."
meanings.exampleKorean: 만약 그가 남부에서 거칠고 강한 척하기 시작하면, 그 동네 녀석들은 그의 거만함을 꺾어버릴 가능성이 높다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To give off smoke or steam; to be smoking.
meanings.example: The old factory was smoking around the clock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 낡은 공장은 시도 때도 없이 연기가 나고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be born.
meanings.example: It was early spring when my little baby boy came into the world.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 사랑스러운 아기가 세상에 태어난 것은 이른 봄이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive (an insult, an affront, etc.) without open resentment, or without seeking redress.
meanings.example: Well, ruffian, I must pocket up these wrongs
meanings.exampleKorean: 그래, 깡패야, 나는 이 억울함을 분노를 참아야만 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain or snow; to precipitate.
meanings.example: It's making down heavily today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 비가 많이 내리고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn back the clock.
meanings.example: I wish I could turn the clock back to my childhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어린 시절로 시간을 되돌릴 수 있다면 좋겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to, visit.
meanings.example: Do you mind if I pull up on you in about half an hour?
meanings.exampleKorean: 한 30분 뒤에 잠깐 들러도 괜찮을까요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To do a task that one is not ordinarily expected to do because there is no one else to do it.
meanings.example: When the lead actor fell ill, the understudy had to step into the breach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주연 배우가 아팠을 때, 대역 배우가 어려운 상황에 나서야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop or create in a scientific way
meanings.example: That is the calibre of person who, in the age of express delivery, is working on sciencing-up the plain cardboard box.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이는 특송 시대에 평범한 골판지 상자를 과학적으로 개발하기 위해 노력하는 사람의 역량입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an ostentatious show of; to show to everybody in a boastful manner.
meanings.example: He was flashing around his new sports car all weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말 내내 새 스포츠카를 과시하며 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: keep one's guard up.
meanings.example: After being robbed, she had her guard up whenever she went out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강도를 당한 후, 그녀는 외출할 때마다 경계 태세를 갖췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's claws into.
meanings.example: The charismatic leader managed to get his hooks into the entire organization.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 카리스마 넘치는 지도자는 조직 전체를 사로잡는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To synchronize.
meanings.example: Many people believe in menstrual synchrony, the idea that women's menstrual periods sometimes sync up when they work closely together, but the scientific methodology for confirming its existence remains weaker than many assume.
meanings.exampleKorean: 많은 사람들은 여성의 생리 기간이 함께 가까이 일할 때 때때로 동기화된다는 월경 동기화라는 생각을 믿지만, 그것의 존재를 확인하기 위한 과학적 방법론은 많은 사람들이 가정하는 것보다 약하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tell jokes too rapidly when performing comedy, failing to milk the maximum laughter from the audience.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 너무 빨리 농담하여 웃음 유발 실패
meanings.example: The comedian stepped on the laughs by rushing through his punchlines.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 코미디언은 펀치라인을 서둘러 말하며 웃음을 유발하는 데 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To charge (someone) with an offence.
meanings.example: They tried to stick him on a charge he didn't commit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그가 저지르지 않은 죄를 그에게 뒤집어씌우려 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause public discontent or controversy.
meanings.example: His controversial remarks were intended to stir up the animals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 논란이 되는 발언은 대중의 불만을 일으키려는 의도였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare to fight; to arm oneself. :against/:for
meanings.example: The citizens decided to take up arms against the invaders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시민들은 침략자들에 맞서 무장하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lay down one's arms.
meanings.example: After a long siege, the defenders were forced to lay down arms.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 포위 공격 끝에 수비대는 무기를 버릴 수밖에 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put one's feet up.
meanings.example: After a long day at work, he just wanted to kick his feet up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 힘든 하루 일을 마치고 그는 그저 발을 올리고 쉬고 싶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To obtain information about someone or something.
meanings.example: Can you get a line on when the next train leaves?
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 기차가 언제 출발하는지 알아낼 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: lay down one's arms.
meanings.example: The rebels were ordered to lay down their weapons.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반군들은 무기를 버리라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add cheese to; make cheesy or cheesier.
meanings.example: Let's cheese up these fries with some extra cheddar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 감자튀김에 체다 치즈를 더 넣어 치즈 맛을 더하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fire (an employee).
meanings.example: The company had to select out several employees due to budget cuts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 예산 삭감으로 인해 여러 직원을 해고해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent (an event) from occurring due to snow.
meanings.example: The concert was snowed up due to the heavy blizzard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭설 때문에 콘서트가 취소되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deplete an area of all individuals of a certain animal, bird, etc., by trapping.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 덫으로 특정 동물을 모두 잡아 없애다
meanings.example: They decided to trap out the invasive species from the lake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 호수에서 침입종을 덫으로 모두 없애기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To convert steer to beef; to slaughter cows, etc.
meanings.example: The rancher will beef out the old cows next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목축업자는 다음 달에 늙은 소들을 도축할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abort (a fetus) by injecting saline solution into the uterus.
meanings.example: The procedure to salt out the fetus is no longer common.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식염수를 이용한 태아 낙태 시술은 더 이상 흔하지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend an especially lengthy period of time inactively, while expecting the arrival of someone or while anticipating some other event, often impatiently and often without a satisfactory outcome.
meanings.example: I'm tired of waiting around for him to call.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 전화해주기를 하염없이 기다리는 것에 지쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform two or more tasks within the same project or organization.
meanings.example: She had to double in brass as both a writer and editor on the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 프로젝트에서 작가와 편집자를 겸해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pay a debt or hand over money.
meanings.example: He finally had to brass up to pay off his gambling debts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결국 도박 빚을 갚기 위해 돈을 내놓아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply blackface to (an actor).
meanings.example: The actor was asked to paint down for the role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 역할 때문에 블랙페이스 분장을 하라는 요청을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To divide (a drug) into capsules.
meanings.example: The lab technicians will cap up the medication.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실험실 기술자들이 약을 캡슐로 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend as if hanging like a drape.
meanings.example: The long curtains drape off the window frame beautifully.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 커튼이 창틀에서 아름답게 늘어뜨려져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate; to skip over or ignore.
meanings.example: They decided to blip out the irrelevant data from the report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 보고서에서 관련 없는 데이터를 제거하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The act of hiding and waiting for someone with the intent to kill or inflict serious bodily harm to them.
meanings.example: The police were alerted to a potential lying in wait.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 잠재적인 매복 가능성에 대해 경고를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: ing-form
meanings.example: The hunter was lying in wait for the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼은 사슴을 숨어서 기다리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plow ahead.
meanings.example: Despite the difficulties, they decided to plough ahead with the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려움에도 불구하고 그들은 프로젝트를 계속 추진하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring into a state of conformity; to make consistent with others.
meanings.example: The new regulations aim to bring all companies into line.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 규정은 모든 회사를 일치시키는 것을 목표로 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hold the fort.
meanings.example: Can you hold down the fort while I go get some coffee?
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 커피 좀 가지러 가는 동안 자리를 지켜줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow someone's example; to behave as someone did.
meanings.example: He decided to tread in his father's steps and become a doctor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아버지의 전례를 따라 의사가 되기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: tread in someone's steps.
meanings.example: She hopes to tread in her mother's footsteps as a teacher.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 교사로서 어머니의 전례를 따르기를 희망한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink alcohol excessively, to the point of being drunk.
meanings.example: He went out and hung one on with his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구들과 나가서 술을 진탕 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to use something too much so that a hole develops in it
meanings.example: My favorite sweater is starting to wear through at the elbows.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 가장 좋아하는 스웨터가 팔꿈치 부분이 낡아서 구멍이 나기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn and attack someone or something.
meanings.example: When confronted, he began to round on his accusers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 직면하자 그는 고발자들을 향해 돌아서서 공격하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extend from a central position, so as to create drag or instability
meanings.example: When performing this exercise, do not allow your arms to flare out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 운동을 할 때 팔을 바깥으로 벌리지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: hunker down.
meanings.example: The family decided to bunker down during the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가족은 폭풍우 동안 몸을 숨기기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To maintain operating expenses for a business.
meanings.example: It's hard to keep the lights on during a recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불황기에 사업을 유지하기는 어렵다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stick in someone's craw.
meanings.example: What began to stick in the gullet was the growing belief during our years in opposition (1974– 79) that the only thing that really mattered was the control of the money supply.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 야당에 있던 시절(1974-79)에 정말 중요했던 것은 통화 공급의 통제뿐이라는 믿음이 커지면서 그것이 목에 걸리기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stick in someone's craw.
meanings.example: 'Tis this confounded London fog that sticks in my throat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 망할 런던 안개가 내 목에 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach with a lot of energy.
meanings.example: He came on strong in the negotiation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 협상에서 강하게 밀어붙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To affect negatively, often in an unexpected manner; to derail.
meanings.example: The environment secretary’s campaign was blown off course at the weekend after revelations about cocaine use. But on the day the Tory leadership contest launched in earnest, Gove insisted he was still “in it to win it”.
meanings.exampleKorean: 환경부 장관의 캠페인은 주말에 코카인 사용에 대한 폭로 이후 항로를 벗어났습니다. 그러나 보수당 대표 경선이 본격적으로 시작된 날, 고브는 여전히 "이기는 데 전념하고 있다"고 주장했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to refuse to change one's plans or ideas, especially when counseled otherwise
meanings.example: She dug her heels in and refused to apologize.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 완강히 버티며 사과를 거부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place the blame for (something) on (someone), especially unjustly.
meanings.example: The police pinned the theft on my brother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 도둑질 혐의를 내 동생에게 뒤집어씌웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: walk back the cat.
meanings.example: He had to walk the cat back after realizing his mistake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 실수를 깨닫고 말을 취소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To check someone or give them a curt answer.
meanings.example: His unexpected question took her up short.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 예상치 못한 질문에 그녀는 말문이 막혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To explore or begin; to enter or get involved in tentatively and for the first time.
meanings.example: Given how many other politicians, from Ed Miliband to Bill Clinton, have dipped their toes into the world of podcasting, it’s perhaps inevitable that Barack and Michelle Obama are entering the field too.
meanings.exampleKorean: 에드 밀리밴드부터 빌 클린턴까지 많은 다른 정치인들이 팟캐스팅의 세계에 발을 담갔다는 점을 감안할 때, 버락과 미셸 오바마도 이 분야에 진출하는 것은 어쩌면 당연한 일이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase voter turnout, typically in a highly partisan way.
meanings.example: The campaign's main goal was to get out the vote.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 캠페인의 주요 목표는 투표 참여를 독려하는 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak with a simper.
meanings.example: He simpered out an apology.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 억지 미소를 지으며 사과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To restrain.
meanings.example: She managed to hold her anger in hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 분노를 억제할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To recook a drug (especially crack cocaine) in an aqueous solution (water) in order to isolate and concentrate the active ingredients, thereby increasing its potency.
meanings.example: This product is really low. You might need to cook it back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 제품은 품질이 정말 낮아요. 다시 가공해야 할 수도 있어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: prick up one's ears.
meanings.example: The dog perked up its ears at the sound of the doorbell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 개가 초인종 소리에 귀를 쫑긋 세웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To date a person of a different race from oneself.
meanings.example: He doesn't mind dating out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다른 인종과 데이트하는 것을 꺼리지 않는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act with vigour.
meanings.example: She wired away at her work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 일에 힘차게 몰두했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to act with vigour.
meanings.example: He wired in to the task.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 일에 힘차게 몰두했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or be excluded because of an injury or infirmity
meanings.example: The player had to invalid out due to an ankle injury.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 선수는 발목 부상으로 인해 경기에서 제외되어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To consider thoroughly; to ponder for a while.
meanings.example: Let me marinate on that proposal for a few days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 제안에 대해 며칠간 숙고해 보겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To threaten (someone); to act as a constant preoccupation for (someone).
meanings.example: Justice Roskill delivered the decision of the three justices, upholding the fines against the paper and editor Lemon. The justices also granted court costs to anti-gay crusader Mary Whitehouse, the person who brought the suit against the paper. A prison sentence hanging over Lemon's head was dismissed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 로스킬 판사는 세 명의 판사들의 결정을 내렸고, 신문과 레몬 편집자에 대한 벌금을 유지했습니다. 판사들은 또한 신문에 소송을 제기한 반동성애 운동가 메리 화이트하우스에게 소송 비용을 승인했습니다. 레몬의 머리 위에 드리워진 징역형은 기각되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: His arrogant attitude was knocked down a peg when he failed the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 거만한 태도는 시험에 떨어졌을 때 기가 꺾였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reheat food that was previously cooked.
meanings.example: Hale saved half the steak to be warmed over for breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 헤일은 아침 식사용으로 스테이크 절반을 남겨 데워 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To avoid discussing or calling attention to (something embarrassing or unpleasant).
meanings.example: A steady 69 m.p.h. was maintained along the sea wall before Birchington, but beyond there it is best to draw a veil over the difficult starts from stops at all stations to Ramsgate, the priming and increasing loss of time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버친턴 이전에는 방파제를 따라 시속 69마일의 일정한 속도가 유지되었지만, 그 너머에서는 모든 역에서 램스게이트까지 정차 후 출발이 어려웠던 점, 프라이밍 및 시간 손실 증가는 언급하지 않는 것이 가장 좋습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: seize upon.
meanings.example: The opposition party seized on the government's mistakes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 야당은 정부의 실수를 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come up with snake eyes.
meanings.example: His plan to start a business came up snake eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사업을 시작하려는 그의 계획은 완전히 실패했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To combine the magnitude of two uncorrelated signals, usually noise or errors, by taking the square root of the sum of the squares.
meanings.example: The derived expression for the g‐r noise consists of a thermal component and a background component which add in quadrature.
meanings.exampleKorean: g-r 잡음에 대해 유도된 표현은 열 성분과 직교적으로 더해지는 배경 성분으로 구성됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to produce loud music or noise
meanings.example: The speakers are blasting out their announcements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스피커에서 안내 방송이 요란하게 울려 퍼지고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange or swap one (person, thing, etc.) for another.
meanings.example: We had to switch out the old tires for new ones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 낡은 타이어를 새 타이어로 교체해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take the weight off one's feet.
meanings.example: After a long day of work, she sat down to take the weight off her feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하루 일과 후, 그녀는 발의 무게를 덜기 위해 앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring something to an end.
meanings.example: The final performance brought down the curtain on the successful play.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 공연은 성공적인 연극의 막을 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bring down the curtain.
meanings.example: scenes of celebration at the final whistle at the City Ground were in the sharpest contrast to their last visit to Nottingham Forest, when defeat brought the curtain down on a season-long Premier League title quest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시티 그라운드에서의 마지막 휘슬에 환호하는 장면은, 시즌 내내 이어졌던 프리미어 리그 우승 경쟁에 패배로 막을 내렸던 노팅엄 포레스트 방문과는 극명한 대조를 이루었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show courage and hide one's apprehension.
meanings.example: He decided to gut up and face his fears.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 용기를 내어 두려움에 맞서기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make an impression; to show off. 18th–19th c.
meanings.example: He tried to figure away with his expensive suit, but no one was impressed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 값비싼 양복으로 뽐내려 했지만 아무도 감명받지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a bud, to split from its parent and form a new plant, etc.
meanings.example: A young Martian, there can now be no dispute, was really born upon earth during the war, and it was found attached to its parent, partially budded off, just as young lily bulbs bud off, or the young animals in the fresh-water polyp.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이제 논란의 여지 없이, 어린 화성인은 전쟁 중에 지구에서 태어났으며, 어린 백합 구근이 떨어져 나가거나 담수 말미잘의 어린 동물이 분리되는 것처럼, 부분적으로 떨어져 나가 부모에게 붙어 있는 채로 발견되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: honor in the breach.
meanings.example: the beginning of February British Railways were admitting in public that in some parts of the system running to time had been honoured more in the breach than the observance during the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2월 초 영국 철도는 겨울 동안 시스템의 일부에서 정시 운행이 규정 준수보다는 말뿐인 존중으로 여겨졌음을 공개적으로 인정하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: For an actor to position themselves relative to another person or object in a scene.
meanings.example: The actor had to dress off to avoid blocking the other character's light.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배우는 상대방 캐릭터의 빛을 가리지 않기 위해 위치를 조정해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's kit off.
meanings.example: He had to take his kit off before going swimming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수영하러 가기 전에 옷을 벗어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark a response on a form, particularly on a multiple-choice exam, by filling in a circular area (‘bubble’).

meanings.englishDefinition: To protect or look out for oneself.
meanings.example: Make sure you cover your back before making such a risky decision.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그런 위험한 결정을 내리기 전에 당신 자신을 보호해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a family or group through marriage.
meanings.example: Marriage patterns were likely exogamous to the band, with women marrying in as wives, and with daughters and sisters marrying out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결혼 양상은 부족에 외혼이었을 가능성이 높으며, 여성들은 아내로 결혼하여 들어왔고, 딸과 자매들은 결혼하여 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To draw a line underneath, for example to mark the end of a topic or of an accounting period.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Regester Humphrey; George Anton Nahstoll| Records for Country Creameries
meanings.exampleKorean: {{quote-text|en| 레제스터 험프리; 조지 앤턴 나스톨| 컨트리 크리머리스 기록

meanings.englishDefinition: to imprison
meanings.example: The authorities decided to clap up the suspects.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당국은 용의자들을 투옥하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to imprison
meanings.example: He was afraid they would mew him up in a dungeon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그들이 자신을 지하 감옥에 가둘까 봐 두려웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enclose in a bottle sealed with a cork.
meanings.example: Here's a bottle to cork that up in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 병에 코르크로 막아 보관하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to completely seal, enclose with a seal
meanings.example: You need to seal up the leftovers in airtight containers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남은 음식은 밀폐 용기에 담아 잘 밀봉해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get older; to advance in age
meanings.example: He's starting to age up and his hair is getting gray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나이가 들기 시작했고 머리카락이 희끗희끗해지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accustom (someone) to a physical place, especially a building, site for an event, etc.
meanings.example: Let me show you around the new office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 사무실을 둘러보게 해 드릴게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave foolishly.
meanings.example: Stop pratting around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 어리석게 굴지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To utter what one is thinking.
meanings.example: I'm just thinking out loud here, but maybe we should reconsider the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그냥 혼잣말하는 건데, 계획을 재고해야 할지도 몰라요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show signs or symptoms of excessive crack cocaine use.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 크랙 코카인 과다 사용 증상을 보이다
meanings.example: Look over there. That kid is cracking out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저기 봐. 저 아이가 크랙 코카인 과다 사용 증상을 보이고 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To analyze one's thoughts and emotions with a view to self-improvement.
meanings.example: I need to check in with myself to see if I'm happy with my career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 경력에 만족하는지 스스로를 성찰할 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dish out or place food on a plate prior to serving.
meanings.example: The chef began to plate up the main course for the banquet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 셰프가 연회를 위한 메인 요리를 접시에 담기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause a person's or group's self-esteem or importance to be decreased.
meanings.example: Even discount superstore Wal-Mart, once considered a recession-proof business, was taken down a notch after it reported lackluster earnings last month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 한때 불황에도 끄떡없는 사업으로 여겨졌던 할인점 월마트조차도 지난달 실적 부진을 보고한 후 한 단계 낮아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: The coach decided to bring down the star player's ego a notch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 스타 플레이어의 자존심을 한 단계 낮추기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: You need to dial down your enthusiasm a notch; it's a bit overwhelming.
meanings.exampleKorean: 열정을 한 단계 낮출 필요가 있어. 좀 부담스러워.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: His arrogant attitude was knocked down a notch after the defeat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 거만한 태도는 패배 후 한 단계 낮아졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: The company's stock price moved down a notch after the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나쁜 소식 이후 회사의 주가가 한 단계 하락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: His boss had to pull his ambition down a notch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 상사는 그의 야망을 한 단계 낮춰야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: She thought she was the best, but her boss brought her down a peg.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신이 최고라고 생각했지만, 상사가 그녀의 기를 꺾었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take down a notch.
meanings.example: After the mistake, he had to move down a peg in the company hierarchy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실수 후 그는 회사 계급에서 한 단계 내려가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spontaneously roll suddenly and rapidly to one side or the other upon entering a stall (due to one wing stalling slightly before the other does, combined with the reduced effectiveness of ailerons for roll control at high angles of attack).
meanings.example: In addition to the large decrease in lift caused by the stall itself, further performance degradation can be expected as the airplane rolls off, as the spoilers on the high wing deploy in an attempt to fight the plane's sudden rolling motion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 실속 자체로 인한 양력의 큰 감소 외에도, 높은 날개의 스포일러가 비행기의 갑작스러운 롤링 동작에 맞서기 위해 전개되면서 비행기가 굴러 떨어질 때 추가적인 성능 저하가 예상될 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rub or polish with wax to create a slick, shiny and/or waterproof coating; to wax.
meanings.example: We're waxin' down our surfboards / We can't wait for June
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 서핑보드에 왁스를 바르고 있어 / 6월이 너무 기다려져

meanings.englishDefinition: feedforward.
meanings.example: The system uses a feed forward mechanism to predict future states.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 시스템은 미래 상태를 예측하기 위해 피드포워드 메커니즘을 사용한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: trail off.
meanings.example: His eyes in and out of the cafés and scanning the park benches but no sign of her as the afternoon trailed away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 눈은 카페 안팎을 훑고 공원 벤치를 살폈지만, 오후가 저물어가는 동안 그녀의 흔적은 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: Please move back from the platform edge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 플랫폼 가장자리에서 뒤로 물러서 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull oneself together.
meanings.example: "My place," he said and he fought to get himself together.
meanings.exampleKorean: "내 집이야," 그가 말하며 마음을 다잡으려 애썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wind up onto a spool.
meanings.example: The large rotational inertia of the heavy compressor and turbine rotors makes the engine slow to spool up from idle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무거운 압축기와 터빈 로터의 큰 회전 관성 때문에 엔진은 공회전 상태에서 회전 속도를 높이는 데 시간이 오래 걸린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decrease in rotational speed, producing a decrease in thrust.
meanings.example: All ground personnel MUST be made aware that big jet engines take a considerable time to spool down. We don't want any more mechanics getting sucked in or catapulted across the apron.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 지상 요원은 대형 제트 엔진이 회전 속도를 줄이는 데 상당한 시간이 걸린다는 사실을 인지해야 합니다. 더 이상 정비사가 빨려 들어가거나 에이프런을 가로질러 날아가는 사고를 원하지 않습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shoulder
meanings.example: I visited the Kings Cross office on an extremely foggy day and about 3.45 I was looking over the shoulder of the Section Controller for the Kings Cross-Finsbury Park stretch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 매우 안개가 낀 날 킹스 크로스 사무실을 방문했고, 3시 45분경 킹스 크로스-핀스베리 파크 구간의 섹션 컨트롤러의 어깨 너머로 보고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become expressionless; to put on a poker face.
meanings.example: When his mama had ventured in the most amiable way to remark on her occasional free and easy ways, he had pokered up at once and declared that it was her very lack of pretension which he found most engaging.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 어머니가 가장 친절한 방식으로 그녀의 가끔씩 자유롭고 편안한 방식에 대해 언급했을 때, 그는 즉시 표정 없이 만들고 그녀의 허세 없는 태도가 가장 매력적이라고 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take care of; to attend to; to check up on.
meanings.example: I'll see about that when the time comes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 때가 되면 그것을 살펴볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit or lie down heavily and inelegantly.
meanings.example: They plopped themselves down on the bed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 침대에 털썩 주저앉았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grind (on someone)
meanings.example: The couple started to booty down on the dance floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 커플은 댄스 플로어에서 몸을 흔들며 추기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To study as or qualify in a subsidiary subject at college or university.
meanings.example: She decided to minor in computer science while majoring in mathematics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 수학을 전공하면서 컴퓨터 과학을 부전공하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pass the pikes.
meanings.example: We had to pass through the pikes several times on our road trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 자동차 여행 중에 여러 번 통행료를 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: down
meanings.example: He looked her up and down. Sucked on the cigar.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 위아래로 훑어보았다. 시가를 빨았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shed light on.
meanings.example: prosecutorial energy toward transformative justice may also further abolitionist ends by casting light on the criminal legal system's "inability to solve the crises it creates."
meanings.exampleKorean: 변혁적 사법을 향한 검찰의 노력은 또한 형사 사법 시스템이 "자신이 만들어낸 위기를 해결할 수 없다는 점"을 밝힘으로써 폐지론자들의 목적을 더욱 증진시킬 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: (typically with of) To impede the progress or achievement (of).
meanings.example: They won't let their parents stand in the way of their love.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 부모님이 그들의 사랑을 방해하도록 내버려두지 않을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat roughly.
meanings.example: The playful dog loved to muss up his owner's hair.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장난기 많은 개는 주인의 머리카락을 헝클어뜨리는 것을 좋아했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be similar or the same.
meanings.example: Unfortunately, none of the numbers on my lottery ticket matched up with the ones drawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 안타깝게도 내 복권 번호는 추첨 번호와 일치하는 것이 하나도 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to begin doing something or become active suddenly and very quickly.
meanings.example: When the alarm sounded, the firefighters leaped into action.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자 소방관들은 즉시 행동에 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mistakenly reveal a secret.
meanings.example: You don't want to mention the money involved; that would give the game away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관련된 돈에 대해 언급하고 싶지 않을 것이다. 그러면 비밀이 누설될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be aware of one's worth and insist that others respect it, for example by not underpricing one's services.
meanings.example: As an artist, it's important to stand in your value and not let clients dictate your prices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예술가로서 자신의 가치를 주장하고 고객이 가격을 결정하도록 내버려두지 않는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: wild out.
meanings.example: The crowd started to wile out when the band played their hit song.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드가 히트곡을 연주하자 군중은 미쳐 날뛰기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue or keep on.
meanings.example: This process has marched on no matter how we try to stop it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 아무리 막으려 해도 이 과정은 계속 나아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have gained an insight that could lead to future success.
meanings.example: The detective feels like he is onto something significant in this case.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 이 사건에서 중요한 무언가를 알아내고 있다고 느낀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rankle in one's heart.
meanings.example: The unfair criticism really stuck in his gizzard for days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부당한 비판은 며칠 동안 그의 마음에 정말 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to skip school; to play truant
meanings.example: He decided to go on the mitch and play video games all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 학교를 빼먹고 하루 종일 비디오 게임을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place all one's trust or hope in someone or something.
meanings.example: She pinned all her hopes on passing the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 시험 합격에 모든 희망을 걸었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take turns leading an expedition.
meanings.example: The experienced guides will lead through the treacherous mountain pass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경험 많은 가이드들이 험준한 산길을 차례로 이끌 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To muster the bravery and will to do something.
meanings.example: He had to pluck up his courage to ask her out on a date.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 데이트를 신청하기 위해 용기를 내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: glow-up.
meanings.example: His recent glow up has made him unrecognizable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 최근 외모 개선은 그를 알아볼 수 없게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sleep very well and untroubled.
meanings.example: sleep the sleep of the just
meanings.exampleKorean: 의로운 사람의 잠을 자다

meanings.englishDefinition: of a vehicle To start moving.
meanings.example: Cerr spent so long complaining to us about the quality of the coal that we had barely time to get into coach No. 51, the oldest type of third-class six-wheeler, as we moved off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세르는 석탄의 품질에 대해 우리에게 너무 오랫동안 불평했기 때문에 우리가 출발할 때쯤에는 3등석 6륜차 중 가장 오래된 51호 객차에 겨우 탈 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop attempting to do something; to give up.
meanings.example: outcome was that the contractor threw in his hand, and the work was completed by the company's own engineer for the area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 결과는 계약자가 포기했고, 그 지역의 회사 자체 엔지니어에 의해 작업이 완료되었다는 것이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pluck up one's courage.
meanings.example: He screwed up his courage and asked her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on batting pads preparatory to taking the field to bat.
meanings.example: Normally only one batsman is padded up and Dean Jones was listed to bat next.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보통 타자 한 명만 패드를 착용하고 있었으며 딘 존스가 다음 타자로 예정되어 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To look through a collection of documents, facts, etc. to make sense of them or put them in order.
meanings.example: You have to sort through the facts and come to a conclusion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사실들을 자세히 조사해서 결론을 내려야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: throw a spanner in the works.
meanings.example: His sudden resignation really gummed up the works on the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 갑작스러운 사임은 프로젝트를 정말 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be listening on citizens' band radio.
meanings.example: Are you having your ears on? I'm trying to reach you.
meanings.exampleKorean: CB 라디오 듣고 있어? 너한테 연락하려고 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: count on the fingers of one hand.
meanings.example: The number of people who truly understand this is something you can count on one hand.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것을 진정으로 이해하는 사람의 수는 손가락으로 셀 수 있을 정도로 적다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To connect by inserting or installing a cable.
meanings.example: We need to cable up the new server to the network.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 서버를 네트워크에 케이블로 연결해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: especially of a train To arrive at.
meanings.example: The train was drawing into the station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 역으로 들어오고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: brush aside.
meanings.example: the Great Northern Railway, U,S.A., which has a considerable mileage of line bordering the Pacific seaboard, has put in service mobile equipment for applying anti-corrosion treatment to rails. An old motor-car has been adapted to carry two oxy-acetylene flame-cleaning heads, which simultaneously clean both sides of both rails of all mill-scale and rust as the car is hand-propelled forward; men follow to brush away the loosened material; the final operation is that of spraying both rails, by means of paint guns carried on a trolley, with a corrosion-resisting compound.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태평양 연안을 따라 상당한 거리의 선로를 보유하고 있는 미국 북부 철도는 레일에 부식 방지 처리를 하기 위한 이동식 장비를 운용하기 시작했습니다. 오래된 자동차 한 대가 두 개의 산소-아세틸렌 화염 세척 헤드를 운반하도록 개조되었는데, 이 헤드는 자동차가 앞으로 수동으로 추진될 때 모든 밀 스케일과 녹을 제거하여 선로 양쪽의 양면을 동시에 세척합니다. 작업자들이 따라가 느슨해진 물질을 털어내고, 마지막 작업은 트롤리에 실린 페인트 건을 사용하여 부식 방지 화합물을 양쪽 선로에 분사하는 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To say something, especially something controversial, while on the record; to take a position publicly.
meanings.example: The senator decided to go on record with his concerns about the bill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상원의원은 법안에 대한 우려를 공식적으로 표명하기로 결정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regard with scorn.
meanings.example: He held the critics in derision, believing their opinions were worthless.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비평가들의 의견이 가치 없다고 믿으며 그들을 경멸했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To judge someone to have been in contempt of court.
meanings.example: The judge warned the witness that she would hold him in contempt if he refused to answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판사는 증인이 대답을 거부하면 법정 모독죄로 간주할 것이라고 경고했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be able to do something; to have the capacity to achieve something.
meanings.example: Do you have it in you to show any respect, politeness, patience, or, God forbid, an ounce of forbearance?
meanings.exampleKorean: 존중, 예의, 인내심, 아니면 신이 금하는 참을성이라도 보여줄 능력이 당신에게 있습니까?

meanings.englishDefinition: get down to brass tacks.
meanings.example: Let's stop discussing the theory and get down to cases.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이론 논의는 그만하고 본론으로 들어갑시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get down to brass tacks.
meanings.example: We need to get down to bedrock to understand the root cause of the problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 문제의 근본 원인을 이해하기 위해 근본으로 돌아가야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone angry.
meanings.example: Don't get my shirt out by telling me that again!
meanings.exampleKorean: 또 그 말 해서 나 화나게 하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To eliminate (a legal representative) due to a conflict of interest.
meanings.example: The lawyer was conflicted out of the case due to his prior relationship with the defendant.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 변호사는 피고와의 이전 관계로 인해 사건에서 배제되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a measurement by counting the number of equally spaced steps.
meanings.example: He paced out the length of the room to estimate its size.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 방의 길이를 걸음으로 측정하여 크기를 추정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept something without seeing evidence supporting it, by trust or confidence.
meanings.example: I'll take your word on trust that the package has been sent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소포가 발송되었다는 당신의 말을 믿고 받아들이겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lay hold of.
meanings.example: He managed to lay hold on the rope just in time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 간신히 로프를 붙잡는 데 성공했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To soak into the ground.
meanings.example: Gaps between paving slabs allow rainwater to soak away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 포장석 사이의 틈은 빗물이 스며들게 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pour a libation or a liquid from a container, particularly from a forty of malt liquor, as an act of remembrance for someone deceased.
meanings.example: They poured one out for their fallen comrade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 전사한 동지를 위해 술을 따랐습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To visit a place or person briefly and casually.
meanings.example: I'll look in on my grandmother on my way home to see if she needs anything.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집에 가는 길에 할머니가 필요한 건 없으신지 잠깐 들러서 살펴볼게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Any involvement or opportunity.
meanings.example: When it came to the tournament, our team didn't get a look in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토너먼트에서 우리 팀은 참여 기회를 얻지 못했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win a race as a long shot.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 이변을 일으키다, 예상 밖의 승리를 거두다
meanings.example: Giacomo blew up the tote by winning the 2005 Kentucky Derby at odds of 50-1.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지아코모는 50대 1의 확률로 2005년 켄터키 더비를 우승하며 이변을 일으켰습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A partial
meanings.example: Before asking the reader to plunge into the subject of linear models I shall, in accordance with a sensible custom, attempt in the few pages which follow to give some idea of what this subject is.
meanings.exampleKorean: 독자들에게 선형 모델이라는 주제에 몰두하도록 요청하기 전에, 현명한 관습에 따라 다음 몇 페이지에서 이 주제가 무엇인지에 대한 아이디어를 제공하려고 노력할 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to get (someone) off (an addiction), to rid (someone) of a habit
meanings.koreanDefinition: 습관을 끊게 하다, 금단 증상을 겪게 하다
meanings.example: It's difficult to wean a child off a pacifier.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이에게 공갈 젖꼭지 습관을 끊게 하는 것은 어렵습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have been in a (friendly, romantic, etc.) relationship that dates back far into the past.
meanings.example: He and I go way back. We met when we were six years old.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그와 나는 아주 오래전부터 알고 지냈다. 우리는 여섯 살 때 만났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confuse (someone) with a sudden occurrence.
meanings.example: The unexpected question blitzed him out during the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 질문에 그는 면접 중에 당황했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To follow a shelter-in-place strategy.
meanings.example: During the tornado warning, residents were advised to shelter in place.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토네이도 경보 중에 주민들은 실내 대피하라는 권고를 받았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come back to bite.
meanings.example: His past mistakes have come back to haunt him in his career.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 과거 실수는 경력에서 그를 괴롭히는 결과로 돌아왔습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confront with courage, without fear.
meanings.example: She decided to look the problem in the face and find a solution.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 문제에 정면으로 맞서 해결책을 찾기로 결정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Come/go visit (our/a business or event).
meanings.example: Feel free to hop on down to the office if you have any questions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 궁금한 점이 있으면 언제든지 사무실에 들러주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: Come/go visit (our/a business or event).
meanings.example: Hop on down to our store for the grand opening sale!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그랜드 오픈 세일을 위해 저희 가게로 놀러 오세요!

meanings.englishDefinition: To devour; to gobble; to eat (something) voraciously.
meanings.example: two boxes of Mr Kipling's Almond Slices they wolfed back between the four of them, talk about gannets!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들 네 명이 사이좋게 미스터 킵핑의 아몬드 슬라이스 두 상자를 게걸스럽게 먹어 치웠다. 정말 탐욕스럽군!

meanings.englishDefinition: to lean over the edge of a boat, in order to counterbalance its weight when it is tilted, to avoid heeling.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 배의 균형을 잡기 위해 기울어진 쪽으로 몸을 기울이다
meanings.example: The sailors had to hike out to keep the boat from capsizing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들은 배가 전복되는 것을 막기 위해 몸을 기울여야 했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resume an activity after an interruption; to pick up.
meanings.example: After the break, she picked up the threads of her research.
meanings.exampleKorean: 휴식 후 그녀는 연구를 다시 시작했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To insist that others treat one with respect.
meanings.example: Even though he was offered a lower position, he stood on his dignity and refused.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 낮은 직책을 제안받았음에도 불구하고, 그는 체면을 지키며 거절했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To try or experiment with (something or someone) in order to see if it works, is true, or is successful.
meanings.example: You will also have to test out any HTML code or Java applets that you include, and you will have to test the whole site once it is published to a server.
meanings.exampleKorean: 또한 포함하는 모든 HTML 코드나 Java 애플릿을 테스트해야 하며, 서버에 게시된 후 전체 사이트를 테스트해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A trial; an experiment to see how or whether something works; the act of testing something out.
meanings.example: I suppose the master electrician who performs that work on board ship, or the leading man, his representative on board, sees that done, and the final test outs show whether it is properly done or not.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선박에서 그 작업을 수행하는 수석 전기 기술자 또는 현장 책임자가 그것을 제대로 수행했는지 최종 테스트를 통해 알 수 있다고 생각합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lay in lavender.
meanings.example: She decided to lay up the antique dress in lavender for special occasions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 특별한 날을 위해 앤티크 드레스를 아껴두기로 결정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: blow up the tote.
meanings.example: The bomb squad had to light up the tote to disarm it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭탄 제거반은 그것을 무력화시키기 위해 토트를 폭파시켜야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry to complete a task or arrive at a destination by a specified time, only for nothing to happen, due to other requirements that have yet to be satisfied.
meanings.example: We were told to hurry up and wait for the meeting to start.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 회의 시작을 기다리기 위해 서둘러야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enliven or invigorate.
meanings.example: Dan brisked up a bit at the sight of two men before the window displaying the white wolf, evidently engaged in a wrangle over the merits of that historic beast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 댄은 역사적인 짐승의 장점에 대해 논쟁하고 있는 두 남자가 창문 앞에 있는 것을 보고 기운을 차렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's shit together.
meanings.example: You need to get your crap together if you want to pass this exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 시험에 합격하고 싶다면 정신 차려야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: get one's shit together.
meanings.example: He finally got his poop together and started studying seriously.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 정신을 차리고 진지하게 공부하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase someone's sense of their importance or skill; to make confident.
meanings.example: Don't let their praise geek your head up too much; stay humble.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 칭찬에 너무 우쭐대지 말고 겸손함을 유지해.

meanings.englishDefinition: keep someone posted.
meanings.example: I'll keep you posted up on any developments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 발전이든 계속 소식을 알려줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fail with an error.
meanings.example: The program will error out if you enter invalid data.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잘못된 데이터를 입력하면 프로그램이 오류로 실패할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work day shifts.
meanings.example: I'm on days this week, so I'll be home early.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주는 주간 근무라 일찍 집에 갈 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work night shifts.
meanings.example: He's on nights this month, so he's always tired.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 이번 달에 야간 근무라서 항상 피곤해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To withdraw temporarily from a situation in order to evaluate it; to cease one's course of action and take a moment to reflect on it; to try and gain some perspective.
meanings.example: establishing GBR, the Department for Transport intends to "take a step back" from the day-to-day running of the railways, which will be music to the ears of many.
meanings.exampleKorean: GBR을 설립하면서 교통부는 철도의 일상 운영에서 잠시 물러나 상황을 평가할 의도이며, 이는 많은 사람들에게 반가운 소식이 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make drastic changes to one's way of life as a form of self-improvement.
meanings.example: After hitting rock bottom, he decided to turn his life around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바닥을 친 후, 그는 인생을 바꾸기로 결심했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spoil; to make yucky.
meanings.example: The market, she notes, has been over-saturated with dresses "yucked up with a lot of trim.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 시장에 장식이 많이 달려 있어 망가진 드레스들이 과잉 공급되었다고 지적한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disgust or nauseate.
meanings.example: Dave is quite yucked out from the altitude.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데이브는 고산병 때문에 상당히 구역질이 난다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform a slam dunk over (a defender).
meanings.example: He got dunked on by the opposing player.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상대 선수에게 덩크슛을 당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on; to continue ticking.
meanings.example: He shuts off the engine, which ticks on mechanically, cooling down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 엔진을 끄자 기계적으로 째깍거리며 식기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form untidy creases or folds in an article of clothing.
meanings.example: My shirt will ruck up if I sit like this for too long.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이렇게 오래 앉아 있으면 셔츠가 구겨질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: Go to hell! Go to the devil! Expression of anger and contemptuous dismissal directed at a person or idea.
meanings.example: You think I'll agree to that? Die in a fire!
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그것에 동의할 거라고 생각해? 지옥에나 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a twinkling light, to cease to twinkle.
meanings.example: The stars began to twinkle out as the dawn approached.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새벽이 다가오자 별들이 반짝임을 멈추기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die, particularly in one's sleep.
meanings.example: He was afraid he would wake up dead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자다가 죽을까 봐 두려웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To confidently and coolly send a direct message to someone by means of social media, especially with romantic or sexual intentions.
meanings.example: is okay, but reckless thirst have me slide into your DMs and give you a glass of water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 괜찮지만, 무모한 갈증 때문에 네 DM으로 연락해서 물 한 잔을 줄 수도 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go on the fritz, to become broken or malfunctioning.
meanings.example: My old computer finally fritz up and died.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 오래된 컴퓨터가 마침내 고장 나서 죽었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch closely, to scrape (against); to press firmly (against).
meanings.example: {{quote-journal|en| 25, 2021| Empire| Murphy review – a triumphant dancefloor workout| Guardian
meanings.exampleKorean: 2021년 11월 25일, 가디언지 '엠파이어'의 머피 리뷰 - 승리적인 댄스플로어 운동

meanings.englishDefinition: sell out (of an item).
meanings.example: The popular item stocked out within hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인기 상품은 몇 시간 만에 매진되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: stockout.
meanings.example: The store experienced a stock out of milk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 가게는 우유 재고 부족을 겪었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make it through, but only barely; to pass or succeed by a very narrow margin.
meanings.example: She managed to squeak through the exam with a C grade.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 C 학점으로 간신히 시험을 통과했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: run into the ground.
meanings.example: He drove his car into the ground by never getting it serviced.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 전혀 점검받지 않아서 망가뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dickride.
meanings.example: Stop being on my nuts, I'll do it when I'm ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 뒤에 끈질기게 굴지 마, 준비되면 할게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or exit a location or a vehicle in large numbers.
meanings.example: The students piled out of the bus after the field trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 현장 학습 후 버스에서 떼지어 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter or move into a location or a vehicle in large numbers.
meanings.example: The fans began to pile in as soon as the stadium gates opened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경기장 문이 열리자마자 팬들이 떼지어 들어오기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain.
meanings.example: He's always beaking off about something.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 뭔가에 대해 불평한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a dap handshake.
meanings.example: He dapped up his teammate after scoring the winning goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결승골을 넣은 후 팀 동료와 악수했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: wash one's mouth out.
meanings.example: The doctor told him to wash out his mouth with salt water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 의사는 그에게 소금물로 입을 헹구라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do something well or thoroughly.
meanings.example: She decided to do the project up brown, leaving no stone unturned.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어떤 것도 빠뜨리지 않고 프로젝트를 완벽하게 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a person, to pass away before retirement.
meanings.example: He was a dedicated worker who died in harness at the age of 65.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 65세에 퇴직 전에 사망한 헌신적인 노동자였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: past
meanings.example: The army fell back under heavy enemy fire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군은 격렬한 적의 공격 아래 뒤로 물러섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: on
meanings.example: He rested his head on her shoulder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀의 어깨에 머리를 기댔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continually be optimistic.
meanings.example: Despite the hardships, they continued to live in hope.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어려움에도 불구하고 그들은 희망을 품고 계속 살았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dwell on and ruminate about past events.
meanings.example: Stop living in the past and focus on the future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과거에 사는 것을 멈추고 미래에 집중하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: They decided to live together before getting married.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 결혼하기 전에 함께 살기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have severe diarrhea.
meanings.example: I ran to the bathroom and pissed out my ass for an hour, thanks to that old hamburger I ate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 오래된 햄버거 때문에 한 시간 동안 화장실에 가서 설사를 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make changes for space for new fresh ideas
meanings.example: Let's go for a walk to blow the cobwebs away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 머리를 식힐 겸 산책하러 가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be brutal towards someone, especially physically.
meanings.example: The bully used to slap the smaller kids around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불량배는 어린 아이들을 때리곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sexual intercourse.
meanings.example: I went into his office and found him having it away with his secretary.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그의 사무실에 들어갔고 그가 비서와 관계를 맺고 있는 것을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crouch.
meanings.example: He crotched down in the blocks to check for comfort before rising up out of them at half speed and trotting a few meters up the track.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 블록에서 편안함을 확인하기 위해 쪼그려 앉은 다음, 절반의 속도로 일어나 트랙을 몇 미터 달려 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: chop up
meanings.example: Please chop up the vegetables for the salad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샐러드를 위해 채소를 잘게 썰어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To arch the back and thrust the hips backwards.
meanings.example: The dancers threw it back to the rhythm of the music.
meanings.exampleKorean: 댄서들은 음악의 리듬에 맞춰 허리를 젖혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To replace (a jockey) with another.
meanings.example: The trainer decided to jock off the current jockey and put in a new one.
meanings.exampleKorean: 트레이너는 현재 기수를 교체하고 새로운 기수를 투입하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put someone in prison, or into their individual cell.
meanings.example: The police decided to dub up the suspect after finding evidence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 증거를 발견한 후 용의자를 감옥에 가두기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to perform the role of a judge; to take on the power of judgement
meanings.example: It's not our place to sit in judgement of their choices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들의 선택을 판단하는 것은 우리의 몫이 아니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To euthanize.
meanings.example: Max could no longer move his hind legs and was defecating all over the floor, so Jeff thought it was time to help him along.
meanings.exampleKorean: 맥스는 더 이상 뒷다리를 움직일 수 없었고 바닥에 배변을 했기 때문에, 제프는 그를 안락사시킬 때라고 생각했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build on the previous discoveries or creativity of someone.
meanings.example: Then can we say of the relevance of Classical languages and literature, Greek and Latin; what reason can we offer for the study of long dead languages. The answer is much in every way. For better or worse, we stand on the shoulders of the ancients, if not at their feet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇다면 우리는 고전 언어와 문학, 그리스어와 라틴어의 관련성에 대해 말할 수 있을까요? 오래전에 사라진 언어를 공부해야 할 이유는 무엇일까요? 답은 여러 면에서 많습니다. 좋든 나쁘든, 우리는 고대인들의 발치에 있지 않다면 그들의 어깨 위에 서 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter someone's personal space in an aggressive or hostile manner.
meanings.example: my flag, can't step on my heelCrime means cops that'll get on my grillIf I don't bust in the ends, who, whatBlud, if I don't bust in the ends, who will?
meanings.exampleKorean: 내 깃발, 내 발뒤꿈치를 밟지 마. 범죄는 나를 괴롭힐 경찰을 의미해. 내가 끝내지 않으면 누가, 무엇을? 친구야, 내가 끝내지 않으면 누가 할 건데?

meanings.englishDefinition: To attend to one's duties.
meanings.example: Despite the news, she decided to go about her day as usual.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소식에도 불구하고 그녀는 평소처럼 일상적인 일을 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hurry up; get a move on.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Knickerbocker; Or, New-York Monthly Magazine
meanings.exampleKorean: 서둘러, 어서 움직여.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surpass or defeat utterly.
meanings.example: The underdog team beat their opponents into fits in the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 약체 팀은 결승전에서 상대 팀을 완전히 격파했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk in a boastful or overweening manner.
meanings.example: He's always blowing out his bags about his achievements.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 자신의 성과에 대해 허풍을 떤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a baby: to be left alone to cry until falling asleep (recommended in some approaches to parenting).
meanings.example: The baby cried it out for a few minutes before finally falling asleep.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아기는 마침내 잠들기 전에 몇 분 동안 울음을 그쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's cornflakes.
meanings.example: Don't piss on my chips by telling him the secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그에게 비밀을 말해서 내 계획을 망치지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (an incarcerated juvenile offender) to an adult prison earlier than the usual age of 18 or 21.
meanings.example: The court decided to star up the teenager due to the severity of his crimes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 법원은 그의 범죄의 심각성 때문에 그 십대 청소년을 성인 교도소로 이송하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliver a spoken or written account or report of something one has been asked or required to do or investigate.
meanings.example: authorities and public bodies such as the ORR and Network Rail are required to report back to RAIB on how any recommendations addressed to them have been considered and what actions have or are planned to be taken.
meanings.exampleKorean: ORR 및 Network Rail과 같은 당국 및 공공 기관은 권고 사항이 어떻게 고려되었는지, 그리고 어떤 조치가 취해졌거나 취해질 예정인지에 대해 RAIB에 보고해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter an introspective or meditative state.
meanings.example: He fell into himself, his half-closed eyes looking into an edgeless nowhere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 눈을 반쯤 감은 채 끝없는 허공을 응시하며 내면으로 빠져들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give (a set or a prop) an impression of wear and tear by spraying it unevenly with dye.

meanings.exampleKorean: 무대 감독들은 소품을 더 낡아 보이게 하기 위해 염료로 더럽혀야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To punish; to pay back or take revenge on.
meanings.example: He swore he would tar out anyone who betrayed him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신을 배신한 누구에게든 복수하겠다고 맹세했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hang up one's boots.
meanings.example: After thirty years of playing professionally, the musician decided to hang up his fiddle.
meanings.exampleKorean: 30년간 프로로 연주한 후, 그 음악가는 은퇴하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scold or castigate someone severely.
meanings.example: The boss lit his assistant's ass up for being late again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 비서가 또 늦어서 심하게 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To commit the criminal offence of making off without payment.

meanings.englishDefinition: The period of a live action roleplaying event when players are in character.

meanings.englishDefinition: To snoop around; to discreetly or covertly search or examine a place or situation.
meanings.example: The detective was nose around in the suspect's apartment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 용의자의 아파트를 뒤지고 있었다.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 낡은 판자 몇 개로 임시 거처를 대충 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lower by cranking by cranking.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover by plowing

meanings.englishDefinition: to rain heavily.

meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일 좀 해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To rain very heavily.
meanings.example: We had to cancel the picnic because it was tipping it down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 억수같이 쏟아져서 소풍을 취소해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: in
meanings.example: c. 1792–3, Jane Austen, ‘Catharine, or The Bower’, Juvenilia:
meanings.exampleKorean: 1792-3년경, 제인 오스틴, '캐서린 또는 나무 그늘', 유치한 글 모음:

meanings.englishDefinition: large it.
meanings.example: Let's go out tonight and large it up!
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 나가서 신나게 놀자!

meanings.englishDefinition: get off one's chest.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 마음속 응어리를 풀다, 속 시원히 말하다

meanings.englishDefinition: To partially repay (a loan or, metonymically, the thing financed by it).

meanings.englishDefinition: To undergo an overnight change, often an unplanned one used with and.
meanings.example: The feud between the Hatfields and the McCoys must have been fueled by some event, as no reasonable person would wake up one day and decide they hated everyone in the other family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 햇필드와 맥코이 가문의 불화는 어떤 사건에 의해 촉발되었음에 틀림없다. 합리적인 사람이라면 어느 날 갑자기 일어나 상대 가족 모두를 증오하기로 결정하지는 않을 것이기 때문이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat all the food on one's plate.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.

meanings.englishDefinition: To regain or return to consciousness or attentiveness after having blacked out.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accept repayment of a debt in the goods or services in which the debtor deals, rather than in money.
meanings.example: {{quote-text|en| Fink| Country, Iowa: Rural Women, Tradition, and Change
meanings.exampleKorean: 핀크 | 아이오와 시골: 시골 여성, 전통, 그리고 변화

meanings.englishDefinition: To fulfil one's task; to do what is required.
meanings.example: We just need one more deal to get over the line for this quarter.

meanings.englishDefinition: to have a meltdown; to ramble excessively.
meanings.example: He started to sperg out when he realized he had lost his keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 열쇠를 잃어버렸다는 것을 깨닫고 격분하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform a painful or unpleasant, but necessary, action quickly so as to minimize the pain or fear associated with it.
meanings.example: folks. I don't want to write this recap any more than you want to read it. But let's rip off the Band-Aid and get through this thing together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여러분. 여러분이 이 요약을 읽고 싶어 하지 않는 것만큼 나도 쓰고 싶지 않다. 하지만 반창고를 떼어내듯 이 일을 빨리 끝내고 함께 헤쳐나가자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shove something down someone's throat.
meanings.example: Don't try to force your opinions down my throat.

meanings.englishDefinition: to treat very badly; to thoroughly insult or demean.
meanings.example: He's always crapping on his employees.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be stingy or miserly.
meanings.example: He always stinge out on gifts for his family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 가족에게 줄 선물에 인색하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin an activity that has been suspended for a long time; to return to something after a long time, especially when there is a need to regain former familiarity or facility.
meanings.example: It's time to dust off the cobwebs and get back to work.

meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 공제 후, 당신이 받을 금액은 500달러가 될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: back
meanings.example: He lashed back at his critics with a series of angry tweets.

meanings.englishDefinition: To botch or ruin; to make a mess of.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something less cold; to warm up slightly
meanings.example: Let the soup sit for a few minutes to take the chill off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수프의 냉기를 없애기 위해 몇 분간 그대로 두세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeat incessantly.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회의에서 같은 요점을 계속 반복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: have it in for.

meanings.exampleKorean: 어제 늦어서 사장님이 나에게 앙심을 품고 있는 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put on several layers of clothing.
