meanings.englishDefinition: To develop a purely sexual attraction towards someone.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve or modify the appearance or performance of something by installing new components or ornaments.

meanings.englishDefinition: wrap around one's little finger.

meanings.englishDefinition: To catch (someone) doing something objectionable with exceptionally clear evidence, such as a high-resolution image or a public social media post.
meanings.example: The security camera caught the thief in 4K as he broke into the store.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보안 카메라가 그가 가게에 침입하는 것을 명백한 증거로 현장 포착했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rot so as to create holes in the substance that's rotting.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of the ceremony of having a deceased official, such as a head of state, be placed in a state building to allow the public to pay their respects, often followed by a state funeral.

meanings.englishDefinition: To temporarily suspend an activity in order to do something else.
meanings.example: “Everyone recognised him but he was very humble and was happy to take time out for people to take photographs with him,” the spokesperson said. “It was a real pleasure to have him compete at our event.”
meanings.exampleKorean: “모든 사람이 그를 알아보았지만 그는 매우 겸손했고 사람들이 그와 사진을 찍을 수 있도록 기꺼이 시간을 내주었습니다. 우리 대회에 참가해 준 것은 정말 즐거운 일이었습니다.”라고 대변인이 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be much more important or impressive than.
meanings.example: You won “Last Comic Standing” in 2006. What impact did that have on your career?Well, it just blew the doors off everything I was doing up to that point. I’ve been doing 200 shows a year ever since then. It definitely launched me into the abyss.
meanings.exampleKorean: 2006년에 “Last Comic Standing”에서 우승하셨군요. 당신의 경력에 어떤 영향을 미쳤나요? 글쎄요, 그건 제가 그때까지 하고 있던 모든 것을 압도해 버렸습니다. 그 이후로 매년 200번의 쇼를 하고 있습니다. 확실히 저를 심연으로 이끌었죠.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand someone's thoughts or feelings.
meanings.example: Yet unlike many of Jackson’s supporters, he would not label his fellow senators racist. “I’m not going to try to get into the head of my colleagues to understand what’s motivating them. It’s a fool’s errand,” he told me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 잭슨의 많은 지지자들과 달리 그는 동료 상원의원들을 인종차별주의자로 규정하지 않을 것이라고 말했다. 그는 나에게 “동료들의 동기가 무엇인지 이해하기 위해 그들의 머릿속으로 들어가려고 하지 않을 것이다. 그것은 헛수고다.”라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to jump the queue.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move (a rail vehicle) so that its buffers are touching the buffers of another rail vehicle.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thaw completely.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop or expand (a system, structure, project etc.).

meanings.englishDefinition: to feast

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover or smear with grime or mud.

meanings.englishDefinition: jack up.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defecate.

meanings.englishDefinition: gather steam.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve, optimize, or maximize the attributes of (something).

meanings.englishDefinition: To seduce a love interest; to attract someone using rizz.

meanings.englishDefinition: whip into shape.

meanings.englishDefinition: To induce a gay person to undergo gender transition so that they become heterosexual.
meanings.example: The idea of trying to 'trans the gay away' is a harmful and outdated concept.
meanings.exampleKorean: '동성애를 성전환으로 없애려는' 생각은 해롭고 시대에 뒤떨어진 개념이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put one's heart in.
meanings.example: She really has her heart in her work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 일에 진심으로 열정을 쏟는다.

meanings.englishDefinition: }} To try to remember a particular time or event; to think about something in the past.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be neutralized and deposited onto a solid surface.
meanings.example: In this galvanic cell, Ag+ ions are reduced into metallic silver and plate out onto an electrode.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 갈바닉 셀에서 Ag+ 이온은 금속 은으로 환원되어 전극에 석출된다.

meanings.example: The fleet executed a turn in succession to avoid the incoming storm.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change course simultaneously, without waiting to reach the turning point of the leading ship.

meanings.englishDefinition: greenout.
meanings.example: He had a bad green out after trying edibles for the first time.

meanings.englishDefinition: To feel unwell or get sick as a result of using an excessive amount of cannabis.

meanings.example: The volcano began to spout out lava.

meanings.englishDefinition: fall short.
meanings.example: They kept coming up short every time they closed out the register.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계산대를 마감할 때마다 계속해서 돈이 부족했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to weather the storm
meanings.example: The company managed to ride out the storm of the economic recession.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 경제 불황의 폭풍을 이겨낼 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish up, to complete, something previously started, particularly a time frame.
meanings.example: former Spitfire pilot in Royal Air Force who had been shot down over Germany and finished out the war in a POW camp.

meanings.englishDefinition: make plans; sketch.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy the integrity or cohesion of.
meanings.example: That one small confession to a man she barely knew blew a hole through her emotional dam and everything she'd held back for the last week flooded out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 거의 알지 못하는 남자에게 한 작은 고백 하나가 그녀의 감정의 댐에 구멍을 냈고, 지난주 동안 억눌렀던 모든 것이 쏟아져 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To roster someone to work a particular shift, time, or day
meanings.example: I will roster on an extra staff member for Friday night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 금요일 밤 근무에 직원을 한 명 더 배치할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To date someone who is more desirable or of a higher social status than oneself.
meanings.example: She's trying to date up by going to exclusive clubs.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 고급 클럽에 가서 더 나은 상대를 만나려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: quake in one's boots.
meanings.example: I'd be shaking in my shoes if I had to give a speech in front of a thousand people.
meanings.exampleKorean: 천 명 앞에서 연설해야 한다면 정말 몹시 두려울 것 같아요.

meanings.englishDefinition: quake in one's boots.
meanings.example: “I was quaking in my shoes working with that guy, because I’m such a fan. He’s incredible at this sound we were making — the best in the business,” he said in a statement.
meanings.exampleKorean: “나는 우리가 만들고 있는 이 사운드에 있어서 그가 믿을 수 없을 정도로 뛰어나고 업계 최고이기 때문에, 그와 함께 일하는 것이 너무 떨렸다”라고 그는 성명에서 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: put the hammer down.
meanings.example: The boss had to lay the hammer down on the new policy to ensure everyone followed it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 사람이 새 정책을 따르도록 하기 위해 사장은 단호하게 결정해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start to perform effectively or to progress
meanings.example: The new system finally clicked into gear after a few initial problems.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 시스템은 몇 가지 초기 문제를 겪은 후 마침내 효과적으로 작동하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have a particular view or opinion about (someone or something); to judge or describe (someone or something) as having a particular character or feature.
meanings.example: I had him down as a reliable person, but he let me down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그를 믿을 만한 사람이라고 생각했지만, 그는 나를 실망시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: heat
meanings.example: The discovery of a third batch of unsecured documents in a third location is turning up the heat on the White House.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세 번째 장소에서 세 번째로 보안되지 않은 문서가 발견되면서 백악관에 대한 압박이 높아지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spread caps (lies) or false rumors (about someone); to slander (someone); to insult (someone's) family.
meanings.example: That guy is always capping on me—don't believe a word he says.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 남자는 항상 나를 모욕해. 그가 하는 말은 하나도 믿지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do everything possible to achieve something, even if it involves great risk or discomfort.
meanings.example: She'd walk on broken glass for her children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자녀들을 위해서라면 무슨 일이 있어도 해낼 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience enjoyment to the full, usually through music, relaxation, or spending time with others; to play music, especially in informal bands and featuring improvisation.
meanings.example: I spent the night just jamming out to good music.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 밤새 좋은 음악을 들으며 신나게 즐겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go off the reservation.
meanings.example: He's going off the res by suggesting such a radical change.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그런 급진적인 변화를 제안함으로써 예정된 틀을 벗어나고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat delicately, to baby, to coddle.
meanings.example: word to the wise—don't wrap him in cotton wool, because he'll resent it later.
meanings.exampleKorean: 현명한 사람에게 하는 말인데, 그를 과잉보호하지 마. 나중에 그가 분개할 테니까.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drift off; to fall asleep
meanings.example: But lately, he'd been tossing about all day, too exhausted to get proper sleep, and only snoozed off toward the last hour.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 최근 그는 하루 종일 뒤척이며 제대로 잠을 잘 기운도 없을 정도로 지쳐서, 마지막 한 시간쯤에야 졸다가 잠들곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be alright, okay, or in agreement with; approve of; stand for; to accept as good, right, or preferred.
meanings.example: I am not down with you children bullying and disrespecting one another, so cut it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너희 아이들이 서로를 괴롭히고 무시하는 것에 나는 동의하지 않아. 그러니까 그만둬!

meanings.englishDefinition: screw around.
meanings.example: Stop screwing about and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 네 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be in a bad mood.
meanings.example: Why do you have such a face on? Did something happen?
meanings.exampleKorean: 왜 그렇게 기분이 안 좋아 보여? 무슨 일 있었어?

meanings.englishDefinition: to hoist up using a rope
meanings.example: They used a rope to trice up the heavy equipment to the top of the building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 밧줄을 이용해 무거운 장비를 건물 꼭대기까지 끌어올렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to clothe; to wrap up in clothing
meanings.example: You need to muffle up before going out in this freezing weather.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 추운 날씨에 나가기 전에 옷을 두껍게 입어야 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: to separate
meanings.example: They decided to portion off the land into smaller plots for sale.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 판매를 위해 땅을 더 작은 구획으로 나누기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To misrepresent something as.
meanings.example: He tried to pass off the imitation Rolex as genuine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 모조 롤렉스를 진짜인 것처럼 행세하려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pretend to be.
meanings.example: The con artist passed himself off as a wealthy businessman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사기꾼은 자신이 부유한 사업가인 체했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a phone call or video call.
meanings.example: Can you hop on the conference call in five minutes?
meanings.exampleKorean: 5분 후에 컨퍼런스 콜에 참여할 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To lecture, scold, reprimand, or rebuke, especially in an angry tone of voice.
meanings.example: The coach really reamed out the players for their poor performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 저조한 경기를 보여준 선수들을 아주 호되게 단단히 꾸짖었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave like a slob; to be lazy and careless.
meanings.example: He just slobbed out on the couch all weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말 내내 소파에서 게으르게 굴었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fit well (with something, in colour, mood, etc.).
meanings.example: The new curtains tone in nicely with the wallpaper.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 커튼이 벽지와 잘 어울린다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean by rinsing.
meanings.example: I rinsed off my shirt under the tap, to get rid of the worst of the mud.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 셔츠의 진흙을 가장 심하게 제거하기 위해 수도꼭지 아래에서 헹궈냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To man up; to act in a tough or assertive way.
meanings.example: He needs to stop complaining and just macho up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 불평하는 것을 멈추고 남자답게 행동해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To disgust (somebody).
meanings.example: I saw her picking her nose and it totally barfed me out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀가 코를 파는 것을 보았고 그것은 정말 나를 구역질 나게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act disrespectfully toward (someone).
meanings.example: Don't you dare cheek up to your elders like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어른에게 감히 그런 식으로 건방지게 굴지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act disrespectfully toward (someone).
meanings.example: He tried to cheek up to the boss and almost lost his job.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사장에게 건방지게 대들었다가 일자리를 잃을 뻔했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give a great performance that causes the audience to go wild with enthusiasm.
meanings.example: I gotta tell you, you blew the roof off. You see that girl over there? She was just raving about your performance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 말해야겠는데, 당신은 관중을 열광시켰어요. 저기 저 여자 보여요? 그녀는 당신의 공연에 대해 극찬하고 있었어요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become more characteristic of an alpha male.
meanings.example: That dude just alpha-ed up on this police officer by shoulder checking him. Insane!
meanings.exampleKorean: 저 남자는 경찰관을 어깨로 밀치며 우두머리처럼 행동했어. 미쳤어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce gradually by removing pieces.
meanings.example: To Ukraine, it has become an important line to hold, both to whittle down Russia’s forces and to deprive them of a victory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우크라이나에게 이곳은 러시아 군대를 점진적으로 줄이고 승리를 빼앗기 위한 중요한 방어선이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give oral sex to someone.
meanings.example: He decided to whip some skull on his girlfriend after dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 저녁 식사 후 여자친구에게 구강 성교를 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to cheat or treat unfairly.
meanings.example: Stop yanking me around and just tell me the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나를 속이지 말고 그냥 진실을 말해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop talking to (someone); to cut (someone) out; to ghost (someone).
meanings.example: Tina locked me off when she found out what happened.
meanings.exampleKorean: 티나는 무슨 일이 있었는지 알게 되자 나에게 연락을 끊었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To screw up greatly; to make a mistake, usually inviting rude/worried comments from others.
meanings.example: Man, you really fucked it up this time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이런, 이번엔 정말 제대로 망쳤네.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wear until becoming comfortable or fitting properly; to cause (something) to wear in this way (by using it).
meanings.example: These new shoes need to be worn in before the marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 새 신발은 마라톤 전에 길들여야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sort out one's thoughts.
meanings.example: After the breakup, she needed time to get her head together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 이별 후 마음을 다잡을 시간이 필요했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: The act of discarding a subset of random variables from a probability distribution to find the marginal distribution.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 주변 분포를 찾기 위해 확률 분포에서 변수 일부를 제거하다
meanings.example: way to eﬃciently deﬁne such a large set of functions is to learn a probability distribution over all the relevant variables, then solve the classiﬁcation task by marginalizing out the missing variables.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 많은 함수 집합을 효율적으로 정의하는 방법은 모든 관련 변수에 대한 확률 분포를 학습한 다음, 누락된 변수를 주변화하여 분류 작업을 해결하는 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To worry, panic; to suffer an anxiety attack.
meanings.example: right, look, don't prang out. They had this paint-party-brunch thing. But I only stayed for 45 minutes, painted a tiny bit of a door, ate half an almond croissant and bounced.
meanings.exampleKorean: 알았어, 봐, 당황하지 마. 페인트 파티 브런치가 있었어. 하지만 난 45분만 머물렀고, 문짝을 아주 조금 칠하고, 아몬드 크루아상 절반을 먹고 나왔어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To generate pressure on (someone or something); to challenge.
meanings.example: delivering services, Bobby brought his revolutionary nationalist theory to the job and used the opportunity to push up against the ideological bias in the government program.
meanings.exampleKorean: 서비스를 제공하면서 바비는 그의 혁명적인 민족주의 이론을 업무에 가져왔고, 정부 프로그램의 이념적 편견에 도전할 기회를 이용했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return (something).
meanings.example: Please complete this form and return back to me by next Monday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 서식을 작성하여 다음 주 월요일까지 저에게 반납해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To discuss (something).
meanings.example: We need to discuss about the project details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 프로젝트 세부 사항에 대해 논의할 필요가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shout down.
meanings.example: The audience howled down the speaker when he started to spout nonsense.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연사가 말도 안 되는 소리를 하기 시작하자 청중들은 고함을 질러 그를 잠재웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To solve or determine by reasoning.
meanings.example: We need to reason out a solution to this problem.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 문제에 대한 해결책을 이성적으로 찾아야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sit around.
meanings.example: He spent the whole day sitting about doing nothing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 빈둥거리며 아무것도 하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go mad; to behave irrationally.
meanings.example: He schizzed out when he heard the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나쁜 소식을 듣고 미쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be defensive or hypervigilant; to adopt a defensive posture; to avoid conflict
meanings.example: When criticized, he tends to turtle up and refuse to engage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비판받으면 그는 움츠러들고 대화를 거부하는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To roll up a marijuana cigarette.
meanings.example: He sat in his room, billing up a joint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 방에 앉아 대마초를 말았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sign in (to access a webpage or computer)
meanings.example: You need to sign into your account to access this feature.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 기능을 사용하려면 계정에 로그인해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cry, shed tears, weep loudly.
meanings.example: She bawled her eyes out after watching the sad movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 슬픈 영화를 보고 몹시 울었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To roughly assemble; to put together.
meanings.example: We had to bash together a presentation in a hurry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 서둘러 발표 자료를 대충 만들어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To listen attentively for something expected.
meanings.example: Listen out for the doorbell, it might ring any minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 초인종 소리를 주의 깊게 들어, 곧 울릴지도 몰라.

meanings.englishDefinition: bear in.
meanings.example: These problems having been bearing in on my mind for years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제들이 수년간 내 마음을 압박해 왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move quickly and sharply in an inward direction during a race; to veer in.
meanings.example: That horse always bears in on the turns.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 말은 항상 코너에서 안쪽으로 방향을 튼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bear in.
meanings.example: Can you lug in the extra chairs from the other room?
meanings.exampleKorean: 다른 방에서 의자를 더 가져와 줄래?

meanings.englishDefinition: bear out (for a horse to veer out)
meanings.example: The horse lugged out at the final stretch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 말은 마지막 직선 주로에서 바깥쪽으로 방향을 틀었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fire away.
meanings.example: He just worked away at his desk all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 책상에 앉아 계속 일했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to work overtime; to make up time off by working overtime; to work beyond a specific shift.
meanings.example: I have to work back tonight to finish this report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 보고서를 끝내기 위해 오늘 밤 초과 근무를 해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to old habits; to return to an unhealthy or unhappy situation.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 예전 습관으로 돌아가다, 좋지 않은 상황으로 되돌아가다
meanings.example: He fell back into his old habits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 예전 습관으로 돌아갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: A notification that someone who previously passed a fingerprinting or background check has since been arrested or otherwise done something that may have or would have changed the results of the earlier check.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 재확인 통지 (이전 검사 결과에 영향을 줄 만한 사건 발생 시)

meanings.englishDefinition: To return by rappelling.
meanings.example: The climbers will rap back down the cliff face.
meanings.exampleKorean: 등반가들은 밧줄을 이용해 절벽을 내려올 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write (something) in a scrawl; to write quickly, usually in illegible handwriting.
meanings.example: He scrawled out a quick note before leaving.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 떠나기 전에 서둘러 메모를 휘갈겨 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write a draft of a document, plan, legislation or budget, usually a first draft.
meanings.example: I drafted up the original complaint, so I'll take a look at your notes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제가 원래 고소장을 초안했으니, 당신의 메모를 살펴보겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lasso someone or something; to catch someone with a leash or rope.
meanings.example: The cowboy lassoed up the stray calf.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카우보이가 길 잃은 송아지를 올가미로 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become knowledgeable on a subject.
meanings.example: We've been cluing up all week, so we'll be ready.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 일주일 내내 정보를 얻었으니 준비가 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue telling stories for a long time; to yammer without interruption.
meanings.example: Nothing would stop him from yarning on.
meanings.exampleKorean: 무엇도 그가 이야기를 계속하는 것을 막을 수 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wrap up in a parcel.
meanings.example: She carefully parceled up the fragile items.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 깨지기 쉬운 물건들을 조심스럽게 소포로 포장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To find by searching.
meanings.example: The scientists are trying to search out new sources of clean energy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과학자들은 깨끗한 에너지의 새로운 원천을 찾아내려 애쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time frivolously or busy oneself with minor tasks; to experiment or tinker.
meanings.example: Stop futzing around and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일 시작해!

meanings.englishDefinition: To suddenly become active, powerful or functional after a period of decline.
meanings.example: The economy began to roar back after the government implemented new policies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정부가 새로운 정책을 시행한 후 경제가 다시 활발하게 살아나기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scarf down.
meanings.example: He scoffed down his lunch in minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 분 만에 점심을 게걸스럽게 먹어 치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To inflate (something, such as a tire or air mattress).
meanings.example: I need to air up the tires on my bike.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자전거 타이어에 공기를 넣어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat (a child) as an orphan, such as by sending them into foster care or adopting them out.
meanings.example: The war orphaned off many children.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전쟁으로 많은 아이들이 고아가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add alcohol to a drink that doesn't normally have it.
meanings.example: He Irish up his coffee every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매일 아침 커피에 술을 타서 마신다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten a seatbelt
meanings.example: Everyone, please strap in before we take off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모두, 이륙 전에 안전벨트를 매주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull short.
meanings.example: The car pulled short up just before hitting the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 벽에 부딪히기 직전에 갑자기 멈췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bring up short.
meanings.example: Kerdeman asks us to prepare for the occasions on which we will be “pulled up short.”
meanings.exampleKorean: 커더먼은 우리가 '갑자기 멈추게 될' 상황에 대비하라고 요청한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cover (something) in soap lather.
meanings.example: I soap up as soon as I get in the shower.
meanings.exampleKorean: 샤워를 하자마자 비누칠을 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to salt food for seasoning.
meanings.example: Please salt up the soup to your taste.
meanings.exampleKorean: 취향에 맞게 수프에 소금을 넣어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To salt (something) to slow decomposition, such as to prepare food, treat wood or preserve a corpse.
meanings.example: County highway crews manned their trucks to salt down the roads, but the snowfall was predited to continue until tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 카운티 고속도로 작업반은 도로에 염장 작업을 하기 위해 트럭을 동원했지만, 눈은 오늘 밤까지 계속 내릴 것으로 예상되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a beverage; to empty a cup by drinking the contents.
meanings.example: Lift your glass and drink it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 잔을 들어 다 마셔버려!

meanings.englishDefinition: To plan out a plot for a story or to create a scheme or plan.
meanings.example: I've plotted out a whole trilogy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 전체 삼부작을 구상했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pull in (To park frontways in a parking spot)
meanings.example: The train will pull into the station soon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 곧 역으로 들어올 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eject violently; to kick out.
meanings.example: The manager had to cuff out the unruly customer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매니저는 난동을 부리는 고객을 쫓아내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To show contempt for someone or something.
meanings.example: You just completely spat on my job performance here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신은 여기서 내 업무 성과를 완전히 모욕했어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To depart from a dwelling unaccompanied.
meanings.example: The butler was nowhere to be found, so I saw myself out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집사가 어디에도 보이지 않아, 나는 스스로 나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To separate, or become separated, by removal from a larger group or mass.
meanings.example: We need to separate out the recyclable materials.
meanings.exampleKorean: 재활용 가능한 물질들을 분리해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To yell; to say or sing in a loud voice
meanings.example: The drill sergeant bellowed out his orders.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조교가 그의 명령을 고함쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make dirty; to sully
meanings.example: Don't dirty up the clean floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 깨끗한 바닥을 더럽히지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sexually penetrate someone in a vigorous, intense manner, as if causing them to throw out their back.
meanings.example: The phrase is often used in a vulgar context.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 구절은 종종 저속한 맥락에서 사용됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: blow off steam.
meanings.example: He went for a run to blow off some steam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스트레스를 풀기 위해 달리기를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something snotty (covered in snot), as a handkerchief or tissue, or to similarly cover with other bodily fluids, like a dog breathing on glass.
meanings.example: He sneezed and snot up the tissue.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재채기를 하며 휴지를 코물로 더럽혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sit down
meanings.example: Please be sitten down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 앉아 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wander or linger aimlessly with a sad or gloomy demeanor, displaying a lack of energy or motivation.
meanings.example: Since her pet passed away, she has been moping around the house, feeling lonely and sad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반려동물을 잃은 후, 그녀는 외롭고 슬픈 기분으로 집 안을 시무룩하게 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wander or linger aimlessly with a sad or gloomy demeanor, displaying a lack of energy or motivation.
meanings.example: Since her pet passed away, she has been moping about the house, feeling lonely and sad.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반려동물을 잃은 후, 그녀는 외롭고 슬픈 기분으로 집 안을 시무룩하게 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rest on one's laurels.
meanings.example: He won the championship last year, but he shouldn't sit on his laurels.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작년에 우승했지만, 그는 현재에 안주해서는 안 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: get a word in edgewise.
meanings.example: Don't know. Even if I wanted to know, couldn't get a word in with Lashawn long enough to ask. Woman talks a blue streak.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모르겠어요. 알고 싶었더라도, 라숀에게 물어볼 만큼 말을 끼어들 수가 없었어요. 그 여자는 말을 너무 빨리 해요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be forced to leave a school or university because of unsatisfactory grades.
meanings.example: He failed out of college after his first year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 첫해 후에 성적 불량으로 대학에서 제적당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare something.
meanings.example: I need to prep up for the surgery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수술을 위해 준비해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go to great lengths.
meanings.example: She went through great lengths to ensure her children had the best education.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자녀들이 최고의 교육을 받도록 하기 위해 매우 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To behave in a raucous, wild, passionate manner free from restraint; to let loose.
meanings.example: Then the unthinkable might be actual, the unprecedented possible. You could safely kick out the jams, dissolve the old hesitations, break with adults, be done with compromises, get on with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러면 상상도 못 했던 일이 현실이 되고, 전례 없던 일이 가능해질 수 있다. 마음껏 신나게 놀고, 오래된 망설임을 떨쳐버리고, 어른들과의 관계를 끊고, 타협은 끝내고, 앞으로 나아가라.

meanings.englishDefinition: call into question.
meanings.example: His recent actions call into doubt his commitment to the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 최근 행동은 프로젝트에 대한 그의 헌신에 의문을 제기한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bite in the arse.
meanings.example: That unexpected bill really bit me in the ass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 예상치 못한 청구서는 정말 나를 곤경에 빠뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To undergo gender transition.
meanings.example: He decided to troon out and live as a woman.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 성전환을 하고 여성으로 살기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lie along
meanings.example: The cat was lain along the sunny windowsill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 햇볕 드는 창턱에 따라 누워 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lie around
meanings.example: He spent the whole weekend lain around the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말 내내 집에서 빈둥거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make lush
meanings.example: rain and a south-west wind, lushing up the young grass in his meadows, and breathing gently upon the pear and the plum blossom!
meanings.exampleKorean: 비와 남서풍이 그의 목초지의 어린 풀을 무성하게 하고, 배꽃과 자두꽃에 부드럽게 숨결을 불어넣었다!

meanings.englishDefinition: show around
meanings.example: The new student was shown around the campus by a senior.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신입생은 선배에게 캠퍼스를 안내받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bear away
meanings.example: The flood waters bore away the bridge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 홍수 물이 다리를 휩쓸어 갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: show away
meanings.example: He was shown away from the secret meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비밀 회의에서 배제되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shrink away
meanings.example: The child shrunken away from the loud noise.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이는 큰 소리에 움츠러들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stem from; to originate in.
meanings.example: The awqaf system in India goes back to the early days of the Islamic presence in the subcontinent.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인도의 와크프 제도는 이슬람이 인도 대륙에 존재했던 초기 시대로 거슬러 올라간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lie back
meanings.example: She was lain back on the sofa, reading a book.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 소파에 편안히 누워 책을 읽고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shrink back
meanings.example: He shrunken back in fear when he saw the snake.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 뱀을 보고 두려움에 뒷걸음질 쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: strike back
meanings.example: The boxer, though knocked down, managed to strike back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복서는 다운되었지만, 반격할 수 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend time with someone as a friend.
meanings.example: He likes to pal around with his college buddies on weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말에 대학 친구들과 어울리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: strike off
meanings.example: His name was stricken off the list of attendees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 이름은 참석자 명단에서 삭제되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unhook or remove from a hook.
meanings.example: At about 4.5 p.m. it come back, tender in front, with 15 or 16 waggons, and stopped with the last of the waggons clear of No. 7; about five were hooked off and let down into the siding by gravity, and as soon as I had restored No. 7 point for the main line, the driver had put the engine into foraward motion and was just upon starting when the collision took place at 4.8 p.m. three minutes before the passenger train was due in the station.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오후 4시 30분경, 기관차가 15~16량의 객차를 끌고 돌아와 7번 선로 밖에서 멈췄다. 약 다섯 량은 걸이에서 풀려 중력에 의해 측선으로 내려갔고, 내가 7번 선로 전환기를 본선으로 복구하자마자 기관사는 전진 기어를 넣고 출발하려던 참이었는데, 승객 열차가 역에 도착하기 3분 전인 오후 4시 8분에 충돌이 발생했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come into somebody's possession without that person making any effort to attain it, and possibly without them expecting it.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 뜻하지 않게 얻게 되다, 우연히 손에 들어오다
meanings.example: After his uncle passed away, a large inheritance landed in his lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 삼촌이 돌아가신 후, 그는 막대한 유산을 뜻하지 않게 얻게 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in lesbian activity.
meanings.example: This time the girls came back on together and started to lez up. They had more toys but these ones were not manufactured with the single woman in mind, if you know what I mean.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번에 여자들이 다시 함께 와서 레즈비언 활동을 시작했다. 그들은 더 많은 장난감을 가지고 있었지만, 당신이 무슨 뜻인지 안다면, 이것들은 독신 여성을 염두에 두고 만들어진 것이 아니었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To build a dyke along (a body of water or a piece of land).
meanings.example: Plumstead Marshes and Barking Level made one morass, four miles wide at least, or nearer five, drowned twice a day into a great level sheet of water, until some civilisation came to dyke up the tidal stream and confine it to the central bed, which it had scoured in its windings through such a desolation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 플럼스테드 습지와 바킹 레벨은 최소 4마일, 아니면 5마일에 가까운 하나의 습지를 이루었고, 하루에 두 번 거대한 수면으로 잠겼다. 조수 흐름에 둑을 쌓아 황량한 지대를 구불구불 흐르며 침식시킨 중앙 수로에 가두기 전까지는 말이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, depart
meanings.example: He breezed off without saying goodbye.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 작별 인사 없이 휙 가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bang the gavel to indicate the conclusion of a court session.
meanings.example: a marathon session that included more than 14 hours of mostly negative public comment, the council agreed 11 to 4 to fund the center, and then gaveled out just before 6 a.m.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대부분 부정적인 공개 발언이 14시간 이상 포함된 마라톤 회의 끝에, 의회는 찬성 11, 반대 4로 센터 지원을 승인했고, 오전 6시 직전에 개회를 선언했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become associated with (a person or group of people); to connect with.
meanings.example: The first thing I did, and this is even before we had a writer, I went to India by myself. Actually at the time, I tied in with 60 Minutes, who were going over. They took the adopted mother to meet the birth mother.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작가도 없었던 시절, 가장 먼저 한 일은 혼자 인도로 간 것이었다. 사실 그때 나는 마침 가고 있던 60 Minutes와 제휴했다. 그들은 입양된 어머니를 친어머니에게 만나게 해주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make ugly.
meanings.example: The artist tried to ugly up the portrait with harsh brushstrokes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 화가는 거친 붓질로 초상화를 못생기게 만들려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To close up or draw together by knitting.
meanings.example: But daily he knit up the fabric that their bad loving had ripped the night before.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하지만 그는 매일 밤 그들의 잘못된 사랑으로 찢어진 천을 뜨개질로 기웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dyke up.
meanings.example: They had to dike up the river to prevent flooding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 홍수를 막기 위해 강에 둑을 쌓아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To trip or stumble and almost fall, as a result of bumping into someone or something with one's feet.
meanings.example: Be careful not to trip over the rug.
meanings.exampleKorean: 러그에 발이 걸려 넘어지지 않도록 조심하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: spirit away.
meanings.example: The magician spirited the rabbit off the stage.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마술사는 토끼를 무대에서 몰래 사라지게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stand without doing anything; to do nothing productive; to waste time.
meanings.example: Why are some people working and some are standing around?
meanings.exampleKorean: 어떤 사람들은 일하는데 왜 다른 사람들은 빈둥거리고 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: outstare.
meanings.example: He stared out the window, lost in thought.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 생각에 잠겨 창밖을 응시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin living in a place.
meanings.example: After moving to London, I took up residence in a two-bedroom flat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 런던으로 이사한 후, 나는 방 두 개짜리 아파트에 거주하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond to some event with energetic activity.
meanings.example: When the alarm sounded, the firefighters jumped into action.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경보가 울리자 소방관들은 즉시 행동에 나섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To load heavily, with a significant burden.
meanings.example: The big and little inmates of the Edgewater Orphanage will receive a visit from St. Claus tonight, when members of the Townsmen Society of Norfolk, loaded down with toys and useful presents, will visit the institution to put on their annual Christmas party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 밤 에지워터 고아원의 크고 작은 수감자들은 산타클로스의 방문을 받을 것이다. 노퍽의 타운스멘 소사이어티 회원들이 장난감과 유용한 선물로 가득 실은 채, 연례 크리스마스 파티를 열기 위해 시설을 방문할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move away at a slow, steady pace, especially if making chugging noises.
meanings.example: Locked in such musings as her train chugged away from New York City, the fourteen-year-old suddenly felt overwhelmed with longing for her own family.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기차가 뉴욕시를 떠나 천천히 나아가는 동안 그런 생각에 잠겨 있던 열네 살 소녀는 갑자기 가족에 대한 그리움에 압도당했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by gouging.
meanings.example: The angry crowd threatened to gouge out the criminal's eyes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 분노한 군중은 범인의 눈을 파내겠다고 위협했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To end a contract and give a pension to.
meanings.example: to pension off a worker for political reasons
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치적인 이유로 직원을 연금 지급 후 퇴직시키다

meanings.englishDefinition: To build a dam on, thus restricting the flow.
meanings.example: to dam up a lake
meanings.exampleKorean: 호수에 둑을 쌓아 막다

meanings.englishDefinition: To create; to bring to life; to give birth to
meanings.example: The artist's imagination brought into being a fantastical world.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 예술가의 상상력은 환상적인 세계를 존재하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: freshen up.
meanings.example: I need to fresh up before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 상쾌하게 할 필요가 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To elaborate; to give a better, false impression of something
meanings.example: He tried to hoke up a story to explain his absence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 결석을 설명하기 위해 이야기를 꾸며내려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: }} To discuss a particular topic at length.
meanings.example: She sends the documentation Carrie’s way by text while — of all times — Steinem is waxing on about the new frontier of feminism.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캐리가 문자를 보내는 동안, 하필이면 스타인엠은 페미니즘의 새로운 지평에 대해 길게 이야기하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To adorn, dress up, spruce up.
meanings.example: The way they dicky up the corpse in the fancy funeral parlours today, with cosmetics and other tricks of the trade, and dressed in the best clothes, one would think they were trying to tell you what Our Lord said about the little daughter of Jairus, 'She is not dead but sleeping.'
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘날 호화로운 장례식장에서 시신을 화장품과 기타 기술로 치장하고 최고의 옷을 입히는 방식은, 마치 주님께서 야이로의 어린 딸에 대해 '죽은 것이 아니라 자고 있다'고 말씀하신 것을 말하려는 것처럼 생각될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make several phone calls, usually for the purpose of locating an item, comparing prices or inquiring about a topic.
meanings.example: I'll have to call around to find a good mechanic.
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋은 정비사를 찾기 위해 여러 곳에 전화해 봐야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hurry away.
meanings.example: The Fourth Stooge assed out like a motherfucker.
meanings.exampleKorean: 네 번째 어릿광대는 망할 놈처럼 서둘러 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exhaust through heavy labour.
meanings.example: The boss worked his employees into the ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상사는 직원들을 지칠 때까지 일하게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase the skills of (typically a worker); to train.
meanings.example: The company is investing in programs to upskill its employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 직원들의 기술 향상을 위한 프로그램에 투자하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en To tick off on a list.

meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 위장병이 나를 덮쳤다. 나는 몸을 가누기 위해 집 가장자리에 한 손을 짚었다. 말할 필요성에 입술이 갑자기 마르는 것 같아 축였다. 나는 한순간도 그에게서 눈을 떼지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience the ending or completion of something.
meanings.example: am going to let the evidence (T-3745) stand for whatever it is worth and you may go ahead and put the other one in too and then we will have that over with, and you can go ahead and put the other one in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 증거(T-3745)가 가치가 있든 없든 그대로 두겠으니, 다른 것도 넣고 그것을 끝내도록 합시다. 그러면 다른 것도 넣을 수 있을 겁니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pour out; to talk volubly.
meanings.example: Chloe took a deep breath and it all flooded out. What started off as a stream soon became a rushing river of emotion.
meanings.exampleKorean: 클로이는 심호흡을 했고 모든 것이 터져 나왔다. 처음에는 작은 시냇물 같았던 것이 곧 감정의 거센 강이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: spill one's guts.
meanings.example: After a few drinks, he spilled his guts out to his new friend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몇 잔 마신 후, 그는 새 친구에게 모든 것을 털어놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prep up; to prepare or coach.
meanings.example: The coach primed up the team before the big game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코치는 중요한 경기 전에 팀을 준비시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave in a huff
meanings.example: “Let me see!” Chloe pulled the leaflet out of Annabelle’s hand. There were some advantages to being the older sister; sometimes you could use brute force. Annabelle huffed off with the rest of the leaflets.
meanings.exampleKorean: “내가 볼래!” 클로이는 애너벨의 손에서 전단지를 낚아챘다. 언니라는 장점도 있었다. 때로는 완력을 쓸 수도 있었다. 애너벨은 나머지 전단지를 가지고 퉁명스럽게 가버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be extremely obvious to (someone), to be impossible for (someone) to miss and ignore.
meanings.example: These new recruits are terrible soldiers. They wouldn't know what to do with an enemy if one came up and bit them.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 신병들은 끔찍한 병사들이다. 적이 나타나서 물더라도 어떻게 해야 할지 모를 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To impose judgment on (oneself or others) by telling them what they should do.
meanings.example: Stop shouldering your opinions on me; I can make my own decisions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내게 네 의견을 강요하지 마. 내 결정은 내가 할 수 있어.

meanings.englishDefinition: worry or fret.
meanings.example: He was chewing on his nails while waiting for the test results.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험 결과를 기다리며 손톱을 물어뜯고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go to the trouble.
meanings.example: She went through the trouble of baking a cake for his birthday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 생일을 위해 케이크를 굽는 수고를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pay a brief informal visit.
meanings.example: I'll swing over to your place after work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 퇴근 후에 네 집에 잠깐 들를게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To invite an interviewee to a further interview.
meanings.example: The hiring manager liked her resume and decided to ask her back for a second interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 채용 담당자는 그녀의 이력서를 마음에 들어 해서 두 번째 면접을 위해 다시 부르기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make smooth or blunt by sandpapering.
meanings.example: He sanded off the rough edges of the wood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 나무의 거친 가장자리를 사포질하여 매끄럽게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, depart; to go on ahead.
meanings.example: Alright, I'm going to head out now. See you tomorrow!
meanings.exampleKorean: 좋아, 이제 출발할게. 내일 봐!

meanings.englishDefinition: To sit or lie in a casual or lazy manner
meanings.example: flop down on the sofa
meanings.exampleKorean: 소파에 편하게 드러눕다

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie or fall down in a tired manner
meanings.example: flump down on my bed
meanings.exampleKorean: 침대에 지쳐 쓰러지듯 눕다

meanings.englishDefinition: To lie or fall down in a tired or lazy manner
meanings.example: slump down on my bed
meanings.exampleKorean: 침대에 힘없이 쓰러지듯 앉다

meanings.englishDefinition: To track down
meanings.example: The detective traced down the suspect to an abandoned warehouse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 형사는 용의자를 버려진 창고까지 추적했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fire (a shot or shots) by pulling the trigger of a firearm.
meanings.example: The hunter squeezed off a single shot at the deer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사냥꾼은 사슴을 향해 총을 한 발 발사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel or arrive in a wheeled vehicle.
meanings.example: When we bowled up, it was at the tail end of a weekend party. There was a lot of smoking going on, there was a little kid, about 12 years old, rapping for everyone; he was great.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리가 차를 타고 도착했을 때, 주말 파티가 거의 끝나갈 무렵이었다. 담배 연기가 자욱했고, 12살쯤 된 어린아이가 모두를 위해 랩을 하고 있었는데, 그는 정말 대단했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use up in a hasty or destructive manner.
meanings.example: He burned through his inheritance in less than a year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 1년도 안 되어 유산을 다 써버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bring to the surface.
meanings.example: When I arrived within sight or hearing of the army, it again harrowed up my melancholy feelings that had subsided on my journey.
meanings.exampleKorean: 군대가 시야나 들릴 거리에 다다랐을 때, 그것은 다시 여정 중에 가라앉았던 나의 우울한 감정을 괴롭혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be grounded on or derived from.
meanings.example: The movie is based on a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: slip through the cracks.
meanings.example: Several important details slipped through the net during the investigation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조사 과정에서 몇 가지 중요한 세부 사항이 간과되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mope over past defeats or failures.
meanings.example: Stop dining on ashes and look to the future.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과거의 실패를 곱씹는 것을 그만두고 미래를 보라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin vehemently supporting a cause.
meanings.example: She took up cudgels for the rights of the underprivileged.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 불우한 사람들의 권리를 위해 열렬히 지지하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To vomit violently.
meanings.example: He drank too much and ended up puking his brains out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술을 너무 많이 마시고 심하게 토했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To post in a thread containing ongoing drama due to reading about it on another subreddit.
meanings.example: Don't piss in the popcorn!
meanings.exampleKorean: 드라마에 끼어들지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure something so that it will not be moved away.
meanings.example: Ed Dennis couldn't specify when the Fitzgerald 's crew had begun replacing and fastening down the hatch covers, but other witnesses maintained that it wasn't at all uncommon for crews to fasten down only half of the hatch cover clamps, especially during the summer months, when the weather was good and there was no danger of encountering heavy seas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 에드 데니스는 피츠제럴드호 선원들이 해치 덮개를 교체하고 단단히 고정하기 시작한 시점을 특정할 수 없었지만, 다른 증언자들은 특히 날씨가 좋고 거친 바다를 만날 위험이 없는 여름철에는 선원들이 해치 덮개 클램프의 절반만 단단히 고정하는 것이 드문 일이 아니었다고 주장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reply quickly and sharply.
meanings.example: She shot back a witty retort.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 재치 있는 말로 쏘아붙였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pluck up one's courage.
meanings.example: He gathered up his courage and asked her out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 용기를 내어 그녀에게 데이트 신청을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To creep out.
meanings.example: This man deliberately brushed my bare leg on the train: it really skeezed me out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 남자가 기차에서 고의로 내 맨다리를 스쳤다: 정말 소름 끼쳤다!

meanings.englishDefinition: end up.
meanings.example: The street ended out in a rough neighborhood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 거리는 결국 험악한 동네로 이어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bleep out.
meanings.example: The swear words were beeped out of the broadcast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 욕설은 방송에서 삐 소리로 삭제되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a difficult situation even worse.
meanings.example: A task force will determine which research projects get funding – piling Pelion on Ossa – there are even plans to restrict admissions which would mean that universities will be able to recruit only five per cent more students than last year in order to prevent them poaching from lesser rivals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 태스크포스는 어떤 연구 프로젝트에 자금을 지원할지 결정할 것인데, 이는 상황을 더욱 악화시키는 것이며, 대학들이 경쟁 대학에서 인력을 빼앗기는 것을 막기 위해 작년보다 5%만 더 많은 학생을 모집할 수 있도록 하려는 계획까지 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exchange, change over positions etc.
meanings.example: a change of driver halfway at Crianlarich. Glasgow crews bring the 35-year-old Class 156 north, then wait to take over the next train back south. Crews from Mallaig, Oban and Fort William take their trains from the coast to Crianlarich and swap over. There's a tiny rest room on the platform, with a microwave and a sink, while they wait. Some drivers are signed all the way to the city. Most are not.
meanings.exampleKorean: 크리아날라릭에서 중간에 운전기사가 교대한다. 글래스고 팀은 35년 된 클래스 156을 북쪽으로 가져온 다음, 다음 남행 열차를 맡기 위해 기다린다. 말레이그, 오반, 포트 윌리엄 팀은 해안에서 크리아날라릭까지 기차를 몰고 와서 교대한다. 기다리는 동안 플랫폼에는 전자레인지와 싱크대가 있는 작은 휴게실이 있다. 어떤 운전기사들은 도시까지 모두 운전할 수 있지만, 대부분은 그렇지 않다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To endure; to tough it out.
meanings.example: can work without the perks, just you and meThug it out ’til we get it right
meanings.exampleKorean: 특전 없이 일할 수 있어, 너와 나만 제대로 될 때까지 힘내자.

meanings.englishDefinition: to rain heavily
meanings.example: It was teeming down when we left the concert.
meanings.exampleKorean: 콘서트를 떠날 때 비가 억수같이 쏟아지고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To indicate to a person or vehicle that they can proceed, by giving a wave hand gesture.
meanings.example: The guard waved the car through the checkpoint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경비원은 차에 손짓으로 검문소를 통과하라고 허락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce strong emotions of sadness or pity in someone.
meanings.example: The charity's advertisement played on people's heartstrings to encourage donations.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 자선단체의 광고는 기부를 장려하기 위해 사람들의 감정에 호소했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To choose (something) in exchange for something else, where having both at once is not an available option.
meanings.example: In the entry-level product category, the engineers had to trade off maximal durability for affordable upfront cost.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보급형 제품 범주에서 엔지니어들은 저렴한 초기 비용을 위해 최대 내구성을 희생해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To load or load up in preparation for, or as an aspect of, heading out.
meanings.example: They loaded out the trucks before dawn.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 동이 트기 전에 트럭에 짐을 실었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of an actor, to put oneself into the mindset of the character they are playing.
meanings.example: The actor spent weeks getting into character for his role.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 배우는 배역을 위해 몇 주 동안 몰입했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To rinse (something) completely from top to bottom.
meanings.example: Rinse down the car after washing it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세차 후 차를 위에서 아래로 헹궈라.

meanings.englishDefinition: the skeleton of (a building).
meanings.example: The frame out of the house is complete.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집의 골조 공사가 완료되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To travel through a lock, or a flight of locks, on a waterway.
meanings.example: The boat will lock through the canal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배가 운하의 수문을 통과할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain momentum; to get up to speed.
meanings.example: The team is starting to go through the gears in the second half.
meanings.exampleKorean: 팀이 후반전에 속도를 내기 시작하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To result victorious after a long period of struggle, competition, etc.
meanings.example: He ultimately came out on top after some initial loses in the first few electoral contests.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 번의 선거 초반 패배 끝에 결국 승리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make anxious.
meanings.example: The constant delays began to nerve up the passengers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계속된 지연은 승객들을 불안하게 만들기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To home in; zoom in
meanings.example: The photographer had to zone in on the subject.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사진작가는 피사체에 초점을 맞춰야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bungle or mess up something.
meanings.example: He really monkeyed up the presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 발표를 정말 망쳐버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: jump on the bandwagon.
meanings.example: I didn’t jump on the Taylor Swift boat for a long, long time. When she first released Fearless—an album I didn’t think warranted such an unapologetic, self-assured title—I didn’t really understand what was making her stand out as pop-country singer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 아주 오랫동안 테일러 스위프트 열풍에 편승하지 않았다. 그녀가 처음 'Fearless'를 발매했을 때 — 나는 그 앨범이 그렇게 당당하고 자신감 넘치는 제목을 받을 만하다고 생각하지 않았다 — 나는 그녀가 팝 컨트리 가수로 두각을 나타내는 이유를 정말 이해하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: render to have fat drip off meat from cooking
meanings.example: Render off the excess fat from the bacon before frying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 베이컨을 튀기기 전에 과도한 지방을 빼내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: blimp out.
meanings.example: He started to blimp up after he quit exercising.
meanings.exampleKorean: 운동을 그만둔 후 그는 살이 찌기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be on the brink of death; to be almost dead.
meanings.example: You can sit on death's doorstep, And rest your weary bones.
meanings.exampleKorean: 죽음의 문턱에 앉아 지친 뼈를 쉬게 할 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make someone angry, annoyed or irritated.
meanings.example: Don't get his monkey up, he's in a bad mood.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 화나게 하지 마, 기분이 안 좋아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mince
meanings.example: them all there, squashed in together in a big tower of rats, made her think of the block she and Dad and Sheila lived in. Still, thought Zoe. The rats had it worse. What with the getting minced up into burgers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들이 모두 쥐 탑 안에 빽빽하게 갇혀 있는 것을 보고 그녀는 자신과 아빠, 쉴라가 살았던 블록을 떠올렸다. 그래도 조이는 생각했다. 쥐들은 더 심했어. 햄버거로 다져지는 것과는 달리.

meanings.englishDefinition: To take charge, especially for purposes of discipline or improvement.
meanings.example: But thinke not thou that takest in hand to striue against God, that thou shalt escape vnpunished.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그러나 신에게 도전하려 손을 댄다고 해서 처벌을 피할 수 있다고 생각하지 마라.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave hurriedly
meanings.example: The children race off to school every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 매일 아침 서둘러 학교로 간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: call on the carpet.
meanings.example: The boss called him onto the carpet for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 그가 늦어서 질책했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase one's level of effort to the maximum.
meanings.example: was the sight of numerous Arsenal players ­whipping up the crowd, including after the breakthrough goal, which had been advertised. From a Raya throw, ­Martinelli switched on the afterburners to glide away from Konaté before crossing for Bukayo Saka, who had timed his run. Saka’s headed connection was not true.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수많은 아스날 선수들이 골 돌파 후 군중을 열광시키는 모습이었다. 라이어의 던지기에서 마르티넬리는 코나테를 제치고 부카요 사카에게 크로스를 올렸는데, 사카는 타이밍을 잘 맞췄다. 사카의 헤더 연결은 정확하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expel through the throat; to cough up, regurgitate, or vomit.
meanings.example: am worried about how much nutrition she is missing by hoarfing up her last meal every time she's out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 외출할 때마다 마지막 식사를 토해내서 얼마나 많은 영양분을 놓치고 있는지 걱정된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gobble, scarf down.
meanings.example: He horfed down the entire pizza in minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 분 만에 피자 한 판을 게걸스럽게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn in, to enter.
meanings.example: The athletes will bow in at the opening ceremony.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수들은 개막식에서 입장할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig rows for planting or to plant in rows
meanings.example: We need to row up the garden before planting the seeds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 씨앗을 심기 전에 밭에 줄을 만들어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait, to delay.
meanings.example: They are trying to time it out until the last minute.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 마지막 순간까지 시간을 끌려고 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extinguish (a flame or lit cigarette) by dunking it into a liquid.
meanings.example: He dunked out his cigarette in the ashtray.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재떨이에 담배를 비벼 껐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stifle; to cause to dwindle or be suppressed.
meanings.example: She tried to bottle down her emotions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 감정을 억누르려고 노력했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make thoroughly smooth; to smooth out.
meanings.example: We'll smooth up the ground when we're done with the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트가 끝나면 땅을 매끄럽게 다듬을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove or conceal by smoothing; to remove through gradual action.
meanings.example: His rough edges have been smoothed away by a period of personal growth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 거친 면모는 개인적인 성장을 통해 다듬어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drop a facade and reveal one's true nature.
meanings.example: It's time to take the mask off and show who you really are.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가면을 벗고 당신이 진짜 누구인지 보여줄 때다.
