meanings.exampleKorean: 나한테 집적거리지 마, 난 관심 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismiss something without paying much attention to it; to decide something is too unimportant or unnecessary to respond to
meanings.example: She waved aside his concerns as trivial.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 우려를 사소한 것으로 여기며 무시했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fail upwards.
meanings.example: He's a classic example of someone who seems to fail up the corporate ladder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 회사에서 실패해도 계속 승진하는 사람의 전형적인 예이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: attack, criticize.
meanings.example: The boss jumped all over me for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 상사는 내가 늦었다고 나를 맹렬히 비난했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go off half-cocked.
meanings.example: Don't go off at halfcock; make sure you have all the facts first.
meanings.exampleKorean: 성급하게 행동하지 마. 먼저 모든 사실을 확인해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply lotion or sunscreen to one's skin.
meanings.example: After swimming, I need to lotion up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수영 후에는 로션을 발라야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scratch the surface of (something) thoroughly.
meanings.example: The cat scratched up the furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 가구를 긁어서 자국을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause (something) to become blocked or clogged; to clog up.
meanings.example: The traffic jammed up on the boulevard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대로에 교통이 막혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: out
meanings.example: I was twelve years old, just playin footballNo I am swinging out yutes with the largest tool
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 열두 살이었고, 그냥 축구를 하고 있었다. 이제 나는 가장 큰 도구로 젊은이들을 나가게 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make fun of; ridicule severely or insultingly.
meanings.example: They roasted up the new guy for his terrible haircut.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 그의 끔찍한 머리 모양 때문에 신입사원을 심하게 조롱했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To signal support or approval of something by voting for it; to achieve enough support to pass.
meanings.example: He votes up all my posts on that site.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 사이트에서 내 모든 게시물에 찬성 투표를 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dry something or someone with a towel.
meanings.example: The nurse was toweling down the baby.
meanings.exampleKorean: 간호사가 아기를 수건으로 닦고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enthusiastically accept an offer.
meanings.example: When I offered him the job, he bit my hand off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 그에게 일자리를 제안했을 때, 그는 덥석 받아들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shower oneself thoroughly or to clean another in a shower.
meanings.example: After practice, we showered up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 연습 후에 우리는 샤워를 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean with a sponge
meanings.example: I sponged up the juice spill.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 스펀지로 흘린 주스를 닦아냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly study a subject or become current in knowledge on a topic
meanings.example: Last night I was studying up on organic chemistry for the test.
meanings.exampleKorean: 어젯밤 나는 시험을 위해 유기화학을 열심히 공부했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire one or more lobbyists; to become active in lobbying to ensure a particular outcome.
meanings.example: The company lobbied up after losing the lawsuit.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 소송에서 패소한 후 로비 활동을 강화했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move or place in a heap by shoveling.
meanings.example: The snow was shoveled up too close to the sidewalk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 눈이 보도에 너무 가깝게 퍼 올려졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook a food through simmering.
meanings.example: First, simmer up a portion of peas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 먼저 완두콩 한 부분을 뭉근히 끓이세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To paint a surface, place or picture.
meanings.example: I spent the weekend painting up my shed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 주말 동안 내 창고를 칠하는 데 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To land atop of; to fall on the surface of.
meanings.example: In 1969, Apollo 11 was the first manned spacecraft to land on the moon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 1969년, 아폴로 11호는 달에 착륙한 최초의 유인 우주선이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean through a scrubbing action; to thoroughly clean up by scrubbing.
meanings.example: The maid scrubbed down the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하녀가 바닥을 문질러 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be unrestrained, especially in acting in a violent or punitive manner.
meanings.example: The negotiations became intense, and both sides finally took off their gloves.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상이 치열해지자 양측은 마침내 본색을 드러내며 거칠게 맞붙었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move along a seat or bench to make room.
meanings.example: She sat over toward the front of the train car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 기차 칸 앞쪽으로 자리를 옮겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To give less than full effort; to let off on energy or pressure.
meanings.example: I thought we sat off them in the second half of the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 우리가 경기 후반에 그들에게 힘을 뺐다고 생각했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join (often as an observer, supervisor or outsider) a group working together on a task.
meanings.example: I'll have to sit in with the interviews for the deputy manager position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부매니저 직책 면접에 참여해야 할 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To unite and remain with one's mother.
meanings.example: We have to open the pen so the calves can mother up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 송아지들이 어미 곁으로 돌아갈 수 있도록 우리를 열어줘야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean up an area or an item or person, usually in preparation for an inspection; to police an area for problems.
meanings.example: We have to police up the grounds before the end of the day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하루가 끝나기 전에 장소를 정돈해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To die under the care or watch of (someone).
meanings.example: I can't handle any more patients dying on me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 이상 환자가 내 앞에서 죽는 것을 감당할 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shift to a position to ready to bunt, i.e. by moving to face the pitcher and hold the bat laterally above the home plate.
meanings.example: The batter squared around, ready to bunt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 타자는 번트를 댈 준비를 하며 자세를 잡았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swing at a pitch, as opposed to bunting; to take a full swing.
meanings.example: He swung away at the first pitch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 첫 투구에 힘껏 방망이를 휘둘렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lift weights in decreasing increments.
meanings.example: After reaching the top set, he started to pyramid down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 최고 중량 세트에 도달한 후, 그는 중량을 줄여가며 운동하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To surprise (somebody) by attacking them, especially in a drive-by shooting.
meanings.example: Those guys rode up on me and started shooting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 남자들이 차를 타고 와서 나에게 총을 쏘기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave or depart.
meanings.example: We need to dust out of here before it gets too late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 늦기 전에 여기서 떠나야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To flee or run away.
meanings.example: He decided to lam off after the robbery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 강도 사건 후 도망치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be promoted from within a command or unit (e.g. for an officer on a ship to be promoted to commanding officer of the ship).
meanings.example: The captain will fleet up to admiral next year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 함장은 내년에 제독으로 내부 승진할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliberately disrespect a commanding officer; to be insubordinate; to disregard a lawful command.
meanings.example: He was disciplined for gaffing off his superior officer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 상관에게 불복종했다는 이유로 징계를 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To act alone.
meanings.example: She decided to single up and finish the project herself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 단독으로 행동하여 프로젝트를 스스로 끝내기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wash (a person or thing) thoroughly.
meanings.example: He had to douche down the entire car after the muddy trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 진흙탕 여행 후 차 전체를 철저히 씻어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To light the fire on a gas boiler to turn it on.
meanings.example: Please light off the boiler before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님이 도착하기 전에 보일러를 켜주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To overthink a problem; to apply an overcomplicated solution to a problem.
meanings.example: Don't nuke it out; just find a simple solution.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지나치게 고민하지 말고 간단한 해결책을 찾아.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook by toasting.
meanings.example: Todd toasted up a slice of wheat bread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 토드는 통밀빵 한 조각을 토스트했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare or treat a fabric by basting (to sew with long or loose stitches).
meanings.example: She decided to baste up the seams before sewing them permanently.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 솔기를 영구적으로 꿰매기 전에 임시로 꿰매기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook or prepare by grilling.
meanings.example: Let's grill up some burgers for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁으로 버거를 구워 먹자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook or prepare by barbecuing.
meanings.example: We're going to barbecue up some ribs this weekend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 주말에 갈비 바비큐를 할 예정이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop a rash.
meanings.example: I felt awful, and I rashed up all over.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몸이 너무 안 좋았고, 온몸에 발진이 생겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sauté up.
meanings.example: He sauteed up some vegetables for the stir-fry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 볶음 요리를 위해 채소를 볶았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To harm or destroy by scorching.
meanings.example: The intense heat scorched up the dry grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강렬한 열이 마른 잔디를 태워 손상시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pickle (something); to produce a pickled item.
meanings.example: She decided to pickle up some cucumbers for the summer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여름을 위해 오이를 좀 절이기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce by stewing.
meanings.example: He stewed up some beef for the stew.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스튜를 위해 소고기를 졸여 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook (something) by microwaving.
meanings.example: I'll just microwave up some leftovers for lunch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 점심으로 남은 음식을 전자레인지로 데워 먹어야겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be let into a particular pasture; to be let out for pasture.
meanings.example: Daisy is pasturing out in the west field.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데이지가 서쪽 들판에서 방목 중이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave in a vehicle quickly (impliedly gunning the engine).
meanings.example: He gunned off in his car, leaving us behind.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 급히 몰고 우리를 뒤로 남겨둔 채 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gun the engine of a vehicle.
meanings.example: He gunned up the engine before the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경주 전에 엔진을 과격하게 작동시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add flavor to (something); to make (something) more flavorful.
meanings.example: Add some spices to flavor up the dish.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리에 맛을 더하기 위해 향신료를 좀 넣으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by lobbying.
meanings.example: He was lobbied out from the Cabinet.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 로비로 인해 내각에서 축출되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To manage (something) or maintain (something) to fruition.
meanings.example: The investors husbanded up my inheritance into a small fortune.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투자자들은 내 유산을 알뜰히 관리하여 작은 부를 이루게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce (something) by blending (often in a blender).
meanings.example: I'm gonna blend up some smoothies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스무디를 좀 갈아 만들겠습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blanch (become pale).
meanings.example: When he saw the ghost, he blanched up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 유령을 보고 겁먹어 얼굴이 창백해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prepare (something).
meanings.example: I'll prepare us up a big salad for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 식사로 먹을 샐러드를 푸짐하게 준비할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: make it up to.
meanings.example: I'll make it up to you for being late.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦은 것에 대해 당신에게 보상할게요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliver an indictment (e.g. to a judge).
meanings.example: The grand jury will hand up an indictment next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 대배심은 다음 주에 기소장을 제출할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook (eggs) into scrambled eggs.
meanings.example: I scrambled up three eggs for breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아침으로 계란 스크램블 3개를 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut up (something) with a dicing action.
meanings.example: First, dice up a large cucumber.
meanings.exampleKorean: 먼저, 큰 오이를 깍둑썰기하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cut up (something) with a slicing action.
meanings.example: Next, slice up two onions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음으로, 양파 두 개를 썰어주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook (something) by broiling.
meanings.example: Let's broil up some chicken for dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁으로 닭고기를 좀 구워 만들자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To cook (something) by poaching.
meanings.example: She poached up some eggs for breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 아침으로 수란을 몇 개 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather large quantities of (something); to stockpile (something); to gorge on (something); to hoard (something).
meanings.example: We need to load up on snacks before the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 파티 전에 간식을 잔뜩 사야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce (something) by delivery as expected under a contract.
meanings.example: The company will deliver up the goods by Friday.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 금요일까지 상품을 인도할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hand over or surrender (something or someone) to.
meanings.example: The hostages were delivered up to the waiting agents.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인질들은 기다리고 있던 요원들에게 넘겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: deliver over to.
meanings.example: The prisoner was delivered over into the custody of the authorities.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 죄수는 당국의 구금 하에 넘겨졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer ownership to (someone or something).
meanings.example: He delivered over to her all his rights to the property.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀에게 그 부동산에 대한 모든 권리를 넘겨주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ponder; to consider or think about.
meanings.example: The projections cast doubt over the future of the business.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 예측들은 사업의 미래에 대해 의문을 제기한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delight in or obsess over homosexual relationships between men; to act like a fujoshi.
meanings.example: She fujoshi'd out over the new manga.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 새 만화에 BL물을 즐겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: That political party is treading on minority rights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정당은 소수자의 권리를 짓밟고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hold up (to wait or delay)
meanings.example: Hol' up, I need to grab my keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠깐만, 열쇠 좀 챙겨야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ingest a large amount of sugar, e.g. in candy.
meanings.example: The kids sugared up good last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 어젯밤에 사탕을 많이 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: dam up.
meanings.example: They decided to damn up the river to create a reservoir.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 저수지를 만들기 위해 강을 막기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lower the price of (something, such as a financial security or a contract) by bidding lower and lower prices.
meanings.example: The competitors tried to bid down the price of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경쟁자들은 계약 가격을 낮추려고 입찰가를 낮췄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To receive property immediately after winning an auction for the property.
meanings.example: He bid off the antique chair at the auction.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경매에서 그 골동품 의자를 낙찰받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coat with a substance by painting it on.
meanings.example: He painted over the graffiti on the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 벽의 낙서를 페인트로 덮어칠했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To create a window to access (a file, website, etc); to put (a file, website, etc.) in a window.
meanings.example: She quickly windowed up the document she needed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 필요한 문서를 재빨리 창으로 열었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the number of candidates for something by judging their quality and rejecting some.
meanings.example: The judges had to winnow down the contestants to just three.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심사위원들은 참가자를 세 명으로 줄여야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: winnow down.
meanings.example: The committee will winnow down the applications to a shortlist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 위원회는 지원서를 추려내어 최종 후보 명단을 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To superimpose or overlay (something) atop (something else); to move a (figurative) window over a target.
meanings.example: He windowed GPS data over the map.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지도 위에 GPS 데이터를 겹쳐 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be placed in a box to the side of the main text in a document.
meanings.example: This info should be windowed out in a chart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 정보는 차트의 옆 상자에 배치되어야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be removed or disqualified through a time window.
meanings.example: He was time windowed out of the competition due to late entry.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 늦게 참가하여 시간 제한으로 대회에서 제외되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fill to overflowing.
meanings.example: He stuffed his pockets out with stolen money.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 훔친 돈으로 주머니를 가득 채웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To winnow (something).
meanings.example: He winnowed up all of those oats.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 모든 귀리를 까불었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fulfill low or bad expectations.
meanings.example: She lived down to her reputation as an awful person.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 형편없는 사람이라는 평판에 걸맞게 행동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply shingles to (a roof).
meanings.example: The roofers will shingle up the house next week.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지붕 공사 업체가 다음 주에 집 지붕에 슁글을 깔 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ballast (stabilize or load with a weight) (something, e.g. a ship).
meanings.example: The ship was ballasted up with sand and rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 모래와 돌로 평형수를 채워 실었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: light upon.
meanings.example: While walking through the forest, I happened to light on a hidden stream.
meanings.exampleKorean: 숲을 걷다가 나는 우연히 숨겨진 시냇물을 발견했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come to be annoying, boring or grating to (someone).
meanings.example: He enjoyed the game at first, but it had palled on him over the years.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 처음에는 게임을 즐겼지만, 수년이 지나면서 지겨워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To entice, trick or manipulate (someone) into joining up with a group or activity.
meanings.example: We'll scoop him in to the plan for sure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 분명 그를 계획에 꾀어들일 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To talk excessively, ignorantly and/or wrongly.
meanings.example: Don't keep flapping off about someone you don't know.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모르는 사람에 대해 계속 헛소리하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: scoot over.
meanings.example: Could you scooch over a bit to make more room?
meanings.exampleKorean: 좀 더 공간을 만들기 위해 조금 옆으로 옮겨 주시겠어요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave quickly or run away.
meanings.example: Seeing the police, he decided to shin out of the alley.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰을 보고 그는 골목에서 재빨리 도망치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove one's clothes.
meanings.example: He shucked off around the campfire until he was nearly naked.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 거의 벗은 채로 캠프파이어 주위에서 옷을 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean up or organize (something).
meanings.example: Let's slick up the room before the guests arrive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님이 도착하기 전에 방을 정리하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move outwards like a snake.
meanings.example: His hand was snaking out from the window.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 손이 창문 밖으로 뱀처럼 뻗어 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To zhoosh (something); to make (something) more pretty and lively.
meanings.example: She sparkled up the kitchen to impress the visitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 방문객들에게 깊은 인상을 주기 위해 부엌을 더 예쁘고 활기차게 꾸몄다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop liking (something).
meanings.example: He soured on the trumpet, so she never heard it again.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 트럼펫에 흥미를 잃었기 때문에 다시는 그것을 연주하지 않았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tease or ridicule (someone); to reject (someone) verbally.
meanings.example: Don't throw off on him just because he's different.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 다르다는 이유만으로 그를 놀리지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: strike something repeatedly To whale on something in an ongoing manner (i.e., with progressive aspect).
meanings.example: The kids had decided to whale away on some pumpkins with baseball bats; it held their interest for a little while.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들은 야구 방망이로 호박을 계속 세게 치기로 결정했고, 그것은 잠시 동안 그들의 흥미를 끌었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop to rest in a shady area.
meanings.example: We decided to shade up under the large tree during the midday heat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 한낮의 더위를 피해 큰 나무 그늘에서 쉬기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell (something, most often a home) despite current habitation or reliance from others and without telling the current users of the property until it is too late for them to react.
meanings.example: I've gotta move in a hurry because my landlord sold the house out from under me.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집주인이 내 몰래 집을 팔아버려서 서둘러 이사 가야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To escape from a debt.
meanings.example: With all this debt, I don't know how we'll ever get out from under.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 빚 때문에 어떻게든 벗어날 수 있을지 모르겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To approach (someone or a place) aggressively in a vehicle, such as for a drive-by; to drive up to (someone) to confront them.
meanings.example: The gang members rode down on the rival territory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갱단원들이 경쟁 구역으로 차를 몰고 위협적으로 접근했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To target (somebody) in a drive-by shooting.
meanings.example: The car slowed down, and they prepared to ride on the pedestrian.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차가 속도를 늦췄고, 그들은 보행자를 향해 차에서 총을 쏘려고 준비했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set up a hammock.
meanings.example: That looks like a good spot to sling up your hammock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해먹을 설치하기에 좋은 장소처럼 보이네요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To walk past (someone, something) without stopping or acknowledging them.
meanings.example: I said hello, but she walked on by.
meanings.exampleKorean: 인사를 건넸지만 그녀는 그냥 지나쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To notice (something) and comment on it; to recognize that (something) is important or novel enough to comment on it.
meanings.example: He remarked on how good you are at the game.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 당신이 게임을 얼마나 잘하는지 언급했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue with normal business; to go about one's day; to ignore potential distractions and continue with the task at hand.
meanings.example: Let the small problems go on by and focus on the main goal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 문제들은 무시하고 주요 목표에 집중하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) cheesy (overdramatic, trite); to make (something) less cool, especially by making it more mainstream.
meanings.example: That ending really cheesed out the whole movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 결말 때문에 영화 전체가 진부해졌어.

meanings.englishDefinition: zip up.
meanings.example: Zip up your jacket, it's cold outside.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자켓 지퍼 올려, 밖에 추워.

meanings.englishDefinition: zip down.
meanings.example: He unzipped his pants.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 바지 지퍼를 내렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anchor (a kite-balloon or blimp) down with weighted bags.
meanings.example: They had to bag down the blimp due to strong winds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강풍 때문에 비행선을 봉투로 고정해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tan thoroughly (skin to change to a darker color from sun exposure).
meanings.example: She spent the whole summer tanning up on the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 여름 내내 해변에서 햇볕에 몸을 태웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move close enough to (someone or something) to guard them, stop them from scoring or passing, etc.
meanings.example: You need to get up into him or he's gonna keep sinking baskets.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그를 막지 않으면 계속 득점할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continually advance (on an opponent) and punch (the opponent) in order to control the fight and dominate the opponent, while shrugging off the opponent's punches.
meanings.example: He's relentlessly walking him down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 끊임없이 상대를 압박하며 나아가고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deliberately annoy or irk someone; to deliberately start a conflict with someone.
meanings.example: Stop bugging me up, I'm trying to concentrate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집중하려고 하니 일부러 짜증나게 하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: bog down.
meanings.example: The negotiations have bogged down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 협상이 교착 상태에 빠졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have lunch either outdoors or in a restaurant; to dine out for lunch.
meanings.example: Let's lunch out today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 점심은 외식하자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat lunch inside and at home; to dine in on lunch.
meanings.example: I'm too tired to go out, so I'll lunch in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 피곤해서 나가기 싫으니 집에서 점심을 먹을 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To understand, perceive or realize (something) that isn't obvious.
meanings.example: I think he's wigged on to our plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 우리 계획을 간파한 것 같아.

meanings.englishDefinition: wig on to.
meanings.example: She quickly wigged onto his intentions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그의 의도를 빨리 알아챘다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To wait for a period of time before proceeding.
meanings.example: You should hold back on starting to cook.
meanings.exampleKorean: 요리 시작하는 것을 좀 늦춰야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize.
meanings.example: Don't start on her about that again!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀를 그 문제로 다시 비난하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: start on (To criticize).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내가 늦었다고 나를 비난하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tighten up a sailboat's sheets in order to prevent luffing.
meanings.example: Harden up the mainsheet, the wind is picking up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 바람이 거세지니 메인 시트를 팽팽하게 당겨.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin again at criticizing (someone or something).
meanings.example: Don't start up on me about that again!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것 때문에 또 나한테 시비 걸지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To dab (to press lightly in a repetitive motion with a soft object without rubbing) using paint to produce a desired effect.
meanings.example: She dabbed out the highlights in the painting with a sponge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 스펀지로 그림의 하이라이트를 두드려 표현했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean (usually a liquid spill) by dabbing; to soak up a fluid by dabbing (To press lightly in a repetitive motion with a soft object without rubbing).
meanings.example: He dabbed up the spilled coffee with a paper towel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 페이퍼 타월로 쏟은 커피를 두드려 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin a return trip to a starting point.
meanings.example: After a brief stop, they started back towards home.
meanings.exampleKorean: 잠시 멈춘 후, 그들은 집으로 돌아가기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resume insulting, confronting and/or shouting at someone.
meanings.example: He started back in on his opponent after the foul.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 파울 후 상대방에게 다시 욕설을 퍼붓기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To murder (someone).
meanings.example: The gangsters were ordered to murk out the witness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 갱단은 증인을 살해하라는 명령을 받았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shave (someone, something).
meanings.example: He needs to shave up before the interview.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 면접 전에 면도해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: about
meanings.example: I don't know about him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그에 대해 모른다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To resume talking about a topic, most often in an argument, criticism or interrogation; to resume nagging or complaining about a topic.
meanings.example: Oh, don't start back on that!
meanings.exampleKorean: 아, 그거 또 시작하지 마!

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue to excel.
meanings.example: Keep shining on, champion!
meanings.exampleKorean: 챔피언, 계속 잘해나가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To project benevolent or unfortunate qualities on (someone).
meanings.example: Let's hope luck is shining down on me today!

meanings.exampleKorean: 태양이 평화로운 마을을 비추고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be dismissive towards (someone or something, e.g. questions, concerns).
meanings.example: They keep asking, but she keeps shining them off.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use, sit on or sleep on a couch.

meanings.exampleKorean: 수잔과의 싸움 이후로 나는 남동생 집 소파에서 지내고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: log on.

meanings.exampleKorean: 계정에 접속하려면 웹사이트에 로그인하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To couch (a thread) onto a fabric (use small stitches to attach it in order to produce a textured effect).

meanings.exampleKorean: 장인이 비단 위에 금실을 수놓을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mark off using lines.

meanings.exampleKorean: 예술가는 그림의 구역들을 선으로 표시할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To finish a sketch or outline by using a permanent marker, pencil, etc. to trace over lines made with a softer implement.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 가는 펜으로 스케치에 선을 그릴 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: She slung over everything to be here today.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오늘 이곳에 오기 위해 모든 것을 둘러메었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak (some words, or a message); to write out and spread (something).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그것이 당신이 보통 내뱉는 종류의 생각인가요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To arrive (somewhere).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 내일 아침 공항에 도착할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sling along.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아무 걱정 없이 마을을 어슬렁거리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hang around or loiter.
meanings.example: Stop slinging along and get to work!
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일하러 가!

meanings.englishDefinition: To give up on a job; to quit or resign; to abandon an effort.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일을 그만두고 자신의 사업을 시작하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To turn (something) into jerky (dried meat).
meanings.example: We will jerky up the venison for the camping trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 캠핑 여행을 위해 사슴고기를 육포로 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spice (a food) with jerk (Jamaican spice blend).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 닭고기를 굽기 전에 저크 양념을 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase in energy; to excite (someone).

meanings.exampleKorean: 커피 한 잔은 아침에 활력을 줄 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come up with (something); to brainstorm (an idea).

meanings.exampleKorean: 이 문제에 대한 창의적인 해결책을 몇 가지 생각해내자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coat (something) in breadcrumbs.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 치킨 커틀릿을 튀기기 전에 빵가루를 입힐 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: crumble up.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 새들을 위해 오래된 빵을 부술 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) into crumbs.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 파이 크러스트를 만들기 위해 쿠키를 부술 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be ranked at (something), based on polling.
meanings.example: The Tories are on twenty-five percent in that constituency.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 지역구에서 보수당은 25%를 득표하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complain to (someone).

meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 몇 달 동안 뒷방 청소에 대해 우리에게 잔소리하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a non-rhyming song, poem, etc., into a rhyming one.

meanings.exampleKorean: 노래는 좋지만, 아직 운율을 맞춰야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform, utter or orate a rhyme.

meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 청중을 위해 시를 읊을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be spread like butter over a surface or, figuratively, over a concept, group of people, etc.
meanings.example: The goo buttered out over the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 점액이 바닥으로 버터처럼 퍼져나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To butter (something) thoroughly; to add a substantial amount of butter to (something).

meanings.englishDefinition: To nick or notch (someone or something) many times.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire mercenaries in a game.
