meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 라운드에서 이기려면 용병을 고용해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To freak out; to become very nervous.
meanings.example: He reallyNERVED OUT when he saw the police car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경찰차를 보고 정말 당황했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply rubber without oil as part of the French polishing process.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 프렌치 폴리싱 시 오일 없이 고무를 바르다
meanings.example: The craftsman stiffed up the surface before applying the final coat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장인은 마지막 코팅을 적용하기 전에 표면에 고무를 발랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex with one or more members of a gang as an initiation.
meanings.example: The initiation ritual required him to sex in with several senior members.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가입 의식에서 그는 여러 선임 회원들과 성관계를 가져야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join a criminal gang by committing an act of violence (often murder) as an initiation.
meanings.example: He had to blood in by killing a rival gang member.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 경쟁 조직원을 살해함으로써 조직에 가입해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a criminal gang by committing a murder for the organization in order to be given permission to leave, because the departing gangbanger can not snitch without also being charged with the murder.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 조직을 위해 살인을 저지르고 범죄 조직을 떠나다
meanings.example: He decided to blood out after years of loyalty.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 수년간의 충성 끝에 조직을 떠나기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sniff (something) in; to snort (something); to smell (something).
meanings.example: He sniffed up the aroma of freshly baked bread.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 갓 구운 빵의 향기를 코로 들이마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy, make or drink a coffee.
meanings.example: Let's coffee up before the meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회의 전에 커피나 마시자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ingest caffeine, such as by drinking coffee or tea.
meanings.example: I need to caffeine up to stay awake during the long drive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 장거리 운전 중 깨어 있기 위해 카페인을 섭취해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sit down.
meanings.example: Please seat down, the show is about to begin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자리에 앉으세요, 공연이 곧 시작됩니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To search for something; to look around for something; to hunt for something.
meanings.example: He's been beating about for that supposed treasure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 보물이라는 것을 찾고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To run around or scurry around.
meanings.example: The children were buzzing about the playground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 놀이터를 바쁘게 뛰어다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be tossed around; to experience turbulence; to shake violently.
meanings.example: The small boat was bucketing about in the rough seas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작은 보트가 거친 바다에서 심하게 흔들렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: kick around.
meanings.example: He likes to kick about the park on weekends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 주말에 공원을 돌아다니는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time on foolish endeavors.
meanings.example: Stop craping around and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: To joke around and be silly or frivolous; to be silly to make people laugh.
meanings.example: They spent the afternoon larking around at the beach.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 오후 내내 해변에서 장난치며 시간을 보냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: lark around.
meanings.example: The students were larking about in the classroom.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들이 교실에서 장난치며 놀고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time by relaxing, sitting, reclining and being lazy.
meanings.example: He likes to loll around on the sofa all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 소파에서 빈둥거리는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: loll around.
meanings.example: They were just loll about, doing nothing productive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 아무 생산적인 일도 하지 않고 그냥 빈둥거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To distribute something by having one person hand it to another so it spreads through the group.
meanings.example: The teacher handed around the worksheets to the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들에게 워크시트를 돌려주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To thoroughly search every part of an area or a container in hopes of finding something.
meanings.example: I had to dig around in my bag for my keys.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 열쇠를 찾기 위해 가방을 샅샅이 뒤져야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To live a life of wild behavior; to stir up trouble or cause a commotion.
meanings.example: He was always helling around with his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 친구들과 어울려 난동을 부렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be idle; to waste time; to socialize.
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon hacking around with his friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 친구들과 오후 내내 빈둥거렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in useless, annoying or unhelpful activities.
meanings.example: Stop fussing around and get your work done.
meanings.exampleKorean: 쓸데없는 짓 그만하고 일을 끝내.

meanings.englishDefinition: fool around.
meanings.example: He was frigging around instead of studying for the exam.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 시험공부 대신 빈둥거리고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time; to spend time on trivial tasks.
meanings.example: Stop buggering about and get on with it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간 낭비 그만하고 어서 시작해.

meanings.englishDefinition: bugger about.
meanings.example: He was just buggering around all day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 하루 종일 시간을 낭비하고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To treat crudely, meanly or harshly.
meanings.example: Don't pull about the new employee like that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신입사원을 그렇게 거칠게 대하지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come onto a new tack.
meanings.example: The ship had to fetch about to avoid the rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 바위를 피하기 위해 방향을 바꿔야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean areas and things that (somebody) has touched or dirtied.
meanings.example: She always has to clean up after her messy kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 항상 어질러놓는 아이들의 뒤처리를 해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: push on.
meanings.example: We must push along if we want to finish the race.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경주를 끝내고 싶다면 계속 나아가야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sail on a tack that as all sails filled.
meanings.example: The ship ramped along with the wind filling its sails.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 바람이 돛을 채우며 항해했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use great effort to progress; to persevere despite difficulties.
meanings.example: She worried along through her studies, facing many challenges.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 많은 어려움에 직면했지만 학업을 계속했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proceed slowly and leisurely.
meanings.example: They decided to fool along the coast, enjoying the scenery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 경치를 즐기며 해안을 따라 느긋하게 가기로 결정했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To idle through life; to be generally aimless.
meanings.example: He felt like he was just drifting along without any real purpose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 뚜렷한 목적 없이 그저 흘러가는 듯한 느낌을 받았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To falsely reassure (someone).
meanings.example: He tried to cosy her along, but she knew he was lying.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그녀를 안심시키려 했지만, 그녀는 그가 거짓말을 하고 있다는 것을 알았습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continue functioning efficiently and smoothly without needing correction or supervision.
meanings.example: The old machine hums along as if it were new.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 기계가 새것처럼 순조롭게 작동합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make food before a meal; to prep meal ingredients so as to make it easy to cook a meal at the correct time.
meanings.example: It's a good idea to prepare ahead for the week's meals.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주간 식사를 미리 준비하는 것이 좋습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To greatly increase in speed or intensity.
meanings.example: The economy is roaring ahead this quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이번 분기에 경제가 빠르게 성장하고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make make contact by radio at a destination before arriving there to let people there know one is coming and to make suitable arrangements.
meanings.example: We should radio ahead to confirm our arrival time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 도착 시간을 확인하기 위해 미리 무선 연락을 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a phone call at a destination before arriving there to let people there know one is coming and to make suitable arrangements.
meanings.example: Please phone ahead to let them know we're on our way.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가는 중임을 알리기 위해 미리 전화해 주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: phone ahead.
meanings.example: Call ahead to make sure the restaurant is not too busy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 식당이 너무 붐비지 않는지 확인하기 위해 미리 전화하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To schedule or plan extensively for problems before it is needed; to come up with solutions before problems occur; to prepare for future situations.
meanings.example: It's important to plan ahead for your retirement.
meanings.exampleKorean: 은퇴를 위해 미리 계획하는 것이 중요합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To pass somebody or something going in the same direction; to move into a winning or advantageous position.
meanings.example: The runner drew ahead in the final lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선수는 마지막 바퀴에서 앞서나갔습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be likely to occur and to be disastrous; to be foreboding.
meanings.example: A major crisis looms ahead for the company.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사에 큰 위기가 다가오고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather momentum; to increase quickly; to increase concerningly quickly.
meanings.example: The stock price has been bounding ahead all year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주가는 연중 계속 빠르게 상승했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become stronger; to gather momentum; to increase in energy; to become more successful.
meanings.example: Despite the setbacks, the team powered ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차질에도 불구하고 팀은 힘차게 나아갔습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make great strides; to advance far in advance of others; to innovate unexpectedly or in an unexpected manner.
meanings.example: The company has leaped ahead of its competitors.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 경쟁사들을 크게 앞질렀습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase slightly; to experience a token increase.
meanings.example: The sales figures nudged ahead in the last quarter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지난 분기에 판매 수치가 조금씩 상승했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be going to happen in the future; to be in store.
meanings.example: Our best days lie ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리의 가장 좋은 날들은 앞으로 있을 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bully (someone); to be bossy toward (someone); to dominate (someone).
meanings.example: He likes to order everyone about in the office.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 사무실에서 모든 사람에게 지시하는 것을 좋아합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To face (someone, something) in a match, problem, competition or fight.
meanings.example: We came up against strong opposition in the final.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 결승에서 강력한 상대와 맞붙었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To encumber an agreement with rules and conditions.
meanings.example: They hedged about the terms of the contract.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 계약 조건에 대해 이런저런 조건을 붙여 제한했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: hedge about.
meanings.example: The politician hedged around the issue, refusing to give a direct answer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치인은 직접적인 답변을 거부하며 문제에 대해 이런저런 조건을 붙여 제한했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To break into many pieces by exploding, literally or figuratively.
meanings.example: The old vase flew apart when it hit the floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 꽃병이 바닥에 떨어지자 산산조각 났습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismantle something into its base components.
meanings.example: The old ship began to break apart as it hit the sharp rocks.
meanings.exampleKorean: 낡은 배가 날카로운 바위에 부딪히자 부서지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be destroyed due to the malevolent actions of others.
meanings.example: Your decisions are ripping this family apart.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당신의 결정이 이 가족을 파괴하고 있습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To destroy (something) by explosion or figuratively similar disasters (e.g. a scandal for a company).
meanings.example: The bomb blew apart the building.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭탄이 건물을 폭파했습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To carefully separate two interlaced or interlocking items; to figuratively separate two opposing or overlapping concepts.
meanings.example: She carefully teased apart the tangled threads.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 엉킨 실을 조심스럽게 풀었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To not talk about a topic so as to allow full consideration to something else.
meanings.example: Let's leave that aside for the time being and talk about what matters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 지금은 그것을 제쳐두고 중요한 것에 대해 이야기합시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore, reject or subdue (something).
meanings.example: He pushed his concerns aside and focused on the task.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자신의 걱정을 제쳐두고 과제에 집중했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To boil a liquid until it has turned entirely to steam/vapor.
meanings.example: Boil the sauce away until it thickens.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소스가 걸쭉해질 때까지 끓여 증발시키세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ignore something; to pretend not to have seen or noticed something; to avoid dealing with something.
meanings.example: It's important not to look away from difficult situations like this.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이런 어려운 상황에서 외면하지 않는 것이 중요하다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (food) very quickly.
meanings.example: He bolted down his breakfast and ran out the door.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 아침을 서둘러 먹고 문밖으로 달려나갔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce a mortgage interest rate by means of a buydown.
meanings.example: The seller agreed to buy down the interest rate for the buyer.
meanings.exampleKorean: 판매자는 구매자의 이자율을 낮추기 위해 비용을 지불하는 데 동의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure an unfurled sail.
meanings.example: The sailors had to clew down the sails as the storm approached.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 다가오자 선원들은 돛을 말아 고정해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: book in. To make a reservation or register.
meanings.example: We booked into the hotel for three nights.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 호텔에 3박을 예약했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To involve a reluctant person with a difficult or dangerous situation.
meanings.example: He didn't want to get involved, but his friends dragged him into the argument.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 관여하고 싶지 않았지만, 친구들이 그를 논쟁에 억지로 끌어들였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repeat something to a (person) until they accept, remember or understand it.
meanings.example: The teacher drummed the rules into the students.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님은 학생들에게 규칙을 귀에 못이 박히도록 반복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: barge in. To enter a place or situation forcefully or without invitation.
meanings.example: He barged into the room without knocking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 노크도 없이 방으로 불쑥 들어가 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: move in (To start living or working in a new place)
meanings.example: They are planning to move into their new house next month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 다음 달에 새 집으로 이사할 계획이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move a potted plant to a larger, more suitable flowerpot.
meanings.example: It's time to pot on the seedlings into bigger containers.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모종을 더 큰 화분으로 옮겨 심을 때이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To touch the rim of a hole or hoop but not go in.
meanings.example: The golf ball lipped out on the final hole.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마지막 홀에서 골프공이 림에 맞고 나왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To complete the upper deck of a vessel.
meanings.example: They decided to deck over the open area of the boat.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 배의 열린 공간을 갑판으로 덮기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig thoroughly through an area so as to loosen the soil and make it suitable for planting.
meanings.example: We need to dig over the garden before planting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 심기 전에 정원 땅을 파 뒤집어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a long discussion of the details of a (topic).
meanings.example: We spent the evening hashing over the details of the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 저녁 내내 프로젝트 세부 사항을 자세히 논의했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To win over (someone); to convince (someone) into joining one's own side in a conflict.
meanings.example: The politician tried to gain over the undecided voters.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 정치인은 마음을 정하지 못한 유권자들을 설득하여 자기편으로 만들려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: turn over (to start an engine)
meanings.example: She's going to kick over the project and do something else.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그 프로젝트를 발로 차버리고 다른 일을 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To crash by landing such that the nose of the plane hits the ground first, causing the plane to topple forward.
meanings.example: The inexperienced pilot caused the plane to nose over on landing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미숙한 조종사 때문에 비행기가 착륙 시 기수가 땅에 부딪혀 넘어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To develop holes caused by rust.
meanings.example: The old car's exhaust pipe had rusted through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오래된 차의 배기 파이프는 녹슬어 구멍이 났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use one's thumb to quickly flip through the pages of a book, magazine, etc., especially without reading them thoroughly or at all.
meanings.example: He thumbed through the magazine while waiting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 기다리는 동안 잡지를 훑어보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fasten a garment with buttons.
meanings.example: Please button through your shirt before you leave.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나가기 전에 셔츠 단추를 채우세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To slap something together; to create or make (something) hastily and haphazardly.
meanings.example: He clapped together a quick meal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 급히 식사거리를 뚝딱 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To put things together hastily as though to combine them.
meanings.example: You can't just cram those two pieces together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 두 조각을 그냥 억지로 합칠 수는 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To form a tight group or close huddle.
meanings.example: The students bunched together for warmth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 학생들은 따뜻함을 위해 옹기종기 모였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bundle up (to form into a bundle)
meanings.example: She bundled together the old newspapers for recycling.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 재활용을 위해 오래된 신문들을 꾸러미로 묶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply or install ink in preparation for printing.
meanings.example: The printer needs to be inked up before we can use it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프린터를 사용하기 전에 잉크를 채워야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expend or waste rapidly.
meanings.example: The company has already blown through $250,000 on legal fees alone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 회사는 이미 법률 비용만으로 25만 달러를 써버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To anticipate a play by watching and analyzing the movements of one's opponent(s).
meanings.example: She keyed me on to the plan.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 계획에 대해 나에게 힌트를 주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To identify or demonstrate connections between two things (e.g. data sets); to display (data) onto or connected to something else.
meanings.example: We need to map the new data onto the existing database.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기존 데이터베이스에 새 데이터를 연결해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To direct or focus one's attention on (a goal); to decide on (a goal); to fixate on (something).
meanings.example: She fixed on a career in medicine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 의학 분야의 경력에 집중했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become a part of (a family or its property, heritage, memberships, etc.), by marrying a member of it.
meanings.example: I married into money, since my wife was the heir of a large estate.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아내가 거대한 재산의 상속녀였기 때문에 나는 돈 많은 집안과 결혼했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To viciously attack.
meanings.example: The dog tied into the intruder with a ferocity that surprised everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 개는 모두를 놀라게 할 정도로 맹렬하게 침입자를 공격했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bid a higher price on one's own property than anyone else at an auction, in order to either secure the item or to raise the final price.
meanings.example: He had to bid in his own painting when no one else would meet the reserve price.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아무도 최소 가격에 도달하지 못하자 그는 자신의 그림에 최고가로 입찰해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a flying switch to switch train cars.
meanings.example: by is the RAF museum at Duxford Airfield, open at weekends, which has a large display of aircraft. Trapped there is the prototype Concorde, which flew in but can never fly out, because the runway was shortened to make way for a motorway immediately after its arrival.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주말에 문을 여는 둑스포드 비행장의 RAF 박물관에는 많은 항공기들이 전시되어 있습니다. 그곳에는 프로토타입 콩코드가 갇혀 있는데, 도착 직후 고속도로를 만들기 위해 활주로가 단축되었기 때문에 날아왔지만 다시는 날아갈 수 없습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat a large volume of food; to begin eating with gusto.
meanings.example: After a long day of work, he really hoed into the pizza.
meanings.exampleKorean: 오랜 작업 끝에 그는 정말 피자를 게걸스럽게 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin (a task or project).
meanings.example: Let's hop into the next task.
meanings.exampleKorean: 다음 과제를 시작해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To erupt in an emotional outburst.
meanings.example: He flew into a rage when he saw that.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그것을 보고 격분했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gradually have less in common with (someone); to mature out of (activities, interests and/or associates).
meanings.example: As they grew older, they grew away from their childhood friends.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나이가 들면서 그들은 어린 시절 친구들과 멀어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To think about or imagine to a time in the past; to search or refer to one's memories of the past.
meanings.example: She cast back to her childhood days.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 어린 시절을 회상했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to change one's position on a topic; to subtly distance oneself from previous associates, ideas, etc.
meanings.example: They're trying to inch away from their policies.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 자신들의 정책에서 조금씩 벗어나려고 노력하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To omit the consideration of details regarding (something); to dismiss the details of (something) so as to concern oneself with higher features and more complex/interesting elements.
meanings.example: The model abstracts away from the complexities of real-world physics.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 모델은 실제 세계 물리학의 복잡성을 추상화한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To accidentally collide with (something).
meanings.example: He banged into the doorframe and hurt his shoulder.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 문틀에 부딪혀 어깨를 다쳤다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To continuously pay money or supplies or provide work to an organization, project, movement or effort.
meanings.example: They are pouring money into the new startup.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새 스타트업에 돈을 쏟아붓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gather (something) together; to collect (something).
meanings.example: He sharked up enough money to buy the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 살 만큼의 돈을 모았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transport across.
meanings.example: The scent of jasmine wafted over from the garden.
meanings.exampleKorean: 정원에서 재스민 향이 건너왔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: close in on.
meanings.example: The police were closing in upon the suspects.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰이 용의자들에게 다가가고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To capture (someone) or arrest (someone); to close in upon (someone).
meanings.example: The police managed to fetch in the escaped prisoner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 탈출한 죄수를 붙잡는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill or murder (someone).
meanings.example: The assassin was hired to fetch off the king.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암살자는 왕을 제거하기 위해 고용되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be gone; to go away.
meanings.example: All my hopes have fie away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나의 모든 희망이 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To provide or supply essential materials (e.g. food) for (someone).
meanings.example: The host furnished out the guests with plenty of food and drink.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주최자는 손님들에게 음식과 음료를 넉넉히 제공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To raise up (something); to foster, nurse, increase or strengthen (something).
meanings.example: He fostered up their anger at the king's actions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 왕의 행동에 대한 그들의 분노를 조장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To space out a work using quads.
meanings.example: The typesetter will quad out the text for better readability.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조판공은 가독성을 높이기 위해 텍스트의 간격을 쿼드로 둘 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean a form using lye or turpentine.
meanings.example: The first step in brushing out a dog’s coat is…
meanings.exampleKorean: 개의 털을 빗질하는 첫 번째 단계는...

meanings.englishDefinition: To form one or more corners.
meanings.example: Those two properties cornered up in the vicinity of Main Street.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 두 부동산은 메인 스트리트 근처에서 모서리를 형성했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold back; to resist moving forward with something despite pressure or intention to do so; to delay, wait or stay behind.
meanings.example: I think we should hang off on that section until we have more data.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 많은 데이터가 나올 때까지 그 부분은 보류해야 한다고 생각합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To set quoins in place prior to fastening them (locking up).
meanings.example: The stonemason carefully quoined up the corners of the wall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 석공은 벽의 모서리에 조심스럽게 퀸을 놓았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To harsh, especially viciously or repeatedly; to criticize or ruin the mood of someone or something.
meanings.example: Don't harsh down the mood with your negative comments.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부정적인 말로 분위기를 망치지 마세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To throw a brushback pitch at (a batter).
meanings.example: The pitcher brushed back the batter with a high fastball.
meanings.exampleKorean: 투수는 빠른 공으로 타자에게 위협구를 던졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a match, fight, battle, argument or competition.
meanings.example: They slugged out two wins last month.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 지난달 두 번의 승리를 위해 격투했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To charm (someone) (to seduce, persuade or fascinate).
meanings.example: Her smile charmed him up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 미소는 그를 매료시켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To grain (something) (To texture a surface).
meanings.example: The artist grained up the canvas.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 화가는 캔버스에 결을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spaghetti make or become tangled.
meanings.example: The wires got all spaghettied up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 전선들이 스파게티처럼 엉켜버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To incinerate (something) by charring.
meanings.example: The fire charred up the wooden beams.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불이 나무 기둥을 태워 검게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To proffer (something); to offer or make available (something).
meanings.example: He's proffering up excuses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 변명거리를 늘어놓고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To steel oneself (to harden or strengthen).
meanings.example: He steeled up before leaving the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 집을 나서기 전에 마음을 굳게 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To respond to greater space availability by growing larger kernels (as opposed to growing at a consistent size no matter what, i.e. fixed-ear hybrids).
meanings.example: These corn varieties flex out well in good soil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 옥수수 품종들은 좋은 토양에서 잘 커진다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To increase a worker's work hours during on-call availability periods, as opposed to scheduled shifts.
meanings.example: The company can flex up hours if demand is high.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수요가 높으면 회사는 근무 시간을 늘릴 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gain the qualities of an apple (as in size or color, usually cheeks blushing red).
meanings.example: Her cheeks started to apple up when she was embarrassed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 당황했을 때 뺨이 사과처럼 붉어지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to eat.
meanings.example: I'm so hungry, I want to start into that pizza right now!
meanings.exampleKorean: 너무 배고파서 지금 당장 저 피자를 먹기 시작하고 싶어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To form (something) or for (something) to be formed into cakes (a block of any of various dense materials).
meanings.example: He caked up the dirt.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 흙을 덩어리로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: base on.
meanings.example: This movie is based off a true story.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영화는 실화를 바탕으로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make someone or someplace crazy (of unsound mind; insane; demented, out of control, very excited or enthusiastic, very unexpected; wildly surprising).
meanings.example: The loud music was driving him crazy up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시끄러운 음악이 그를 미치게 만들고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shrink (to cower or flinch).
meanings.example: As soon as he entered the haunted house, he turtled down into his jacket.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 유령의 집에 들어서자마자 재킷 속으로 몸을 움츠렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To command (someone) repeatedly and unpleasantly, pointlessly or without proper authority.
meanings.example: I can't stand that guy, he's always ordering me around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 남자는 항상 나를 부려 먹어서 참을 수가 없어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrunch one's face or a part of one's face.
meanings.example: He scrunched up his nose.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 코를 찡그렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scrunch up.
meanings.example: She scrumpled up the paper and threw it away.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 종이를 구겨서 버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To abandon the right to pursue a legal issue by not spotting it in time or not raising an objection in court.
meanings.example: He pleaded over his right to appeal.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항소할 권리를 포기했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plead guilty in a plea bargain, usually in exchange for lighter charges.
meanings.example: He decided to plead down to a lesser charge.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 더 가벼운 혐의에 대해 유죄를 인정하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plead guilty in a plea bargain, usually in exchange for a lighter sentence.
meanings.example: The defendant chose to plead out to avoid a lengthy trial.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피고는 긴 재판을 피하기 위해 유죄를 인정하기로 선택했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To plead guilty, especially to elevated charges in a plea bargain, such as to avoid a trial or to avoid immigration proceedings.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 더 높은 혐의에 대해 유죄 인정
meanings.example: He decided to plead up to avoid deportation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 추방을 피하기 위해 더 높은 혐의에 대해 유죄를 인정하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in sexual intercourse, especially intense and sweaty sex.
meanings.example: They were balling down all night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 밤새 격렬하게 성교했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: loosen up.
meanings.example: You need to loose up the knot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 매듭을 느슨하게 풀어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or alter (something) to make it heightened, higher, more powerful or more intense.
meanings.example: The music helped to heighten up the atmosphere.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악은 분위기를 고조시키는 데 도움이 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) darker.
meanings.example: The sky started to darken up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 하늘이 어두워지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) darker.
meanings.example: The room began to darken down as the sun set.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해가 지면서 방이 어두워지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become darker.
meanings.example: The room will dark up quickly in the evening.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁에는 방이 금방 어두워질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) skinny.
meanings.example: He's been skinnying up this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 올해 더 말라지고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) skinny or more lightweight.
meanings.example: I'm hoping to skinny down this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 올해 더 말라지기를 바라고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make something shorter in height, briefer in duration or more close together.
meanings.example: You have to shorten this movie up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 영화를 더 짧게 만들어야 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: shorten up.
meanings.example: Please shorten down the report to one page.
meanings.exampleKorean: 보고서를 한 페이지로 줄여주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reduce the price of a stock by selling short.
meanings.example: He decided to short down the company's stock.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 회사의 주식을 공매도하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) larger.
meanings.example: The photographer will enlarge up the picture for the exhibition.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사진작가는 전시회를 위해 사진을 확대할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shrink up.
meanings.example: The fabric will shrink down after washing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 천은 세탁 후에 줄어들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) less lengthy or shorter in duration.
meanings.example: The project timeline was lengthened down due to unforeseen issues.
meanings.exampleKorean: 예상치 못한 문제들로 인해 프로젝트 일정이 단축되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make longer (in distance or in duration).
meanings.example: We need to lengthen up the meeting to discuss the details.
meanings.exampleKorean: 세부 사항을 논의하기 위해 회의 시간을 연장해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: slow down.
meanings.example: The car began to slow off as it approached the stop sign.
meanings.exampleKorean: 자동차가 정지 표지판에 가까워지면서 속도를 늦추기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) lengthier, longer in duration or otherwise larger.
meanings.example: The road will lengthen out for another mile.
meanings.exampleKorean: 길은 1마일 더 길어질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove clothes.
meanings.example: She skinnied out of her top.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 상의를 벗었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) narrow.
meanings.example: The path begins to narrow up as it goes into the forest.
meanings.exampleKorean: 숲으로 들어갈수록 길이 좁아지기 시작한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) quicker or briefer.
meanings.example: The runner began to quicken up his pace in the final lap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주자는 마지막 랩에서 속도를 높이기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) stronger, tougher or more durable.
meanings.example: He needs to strengthen up his defense before the next match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다음 경기를 앞두고 수비를 강화해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) wider.
meanings.example: The road will widen up ahead.
meanings.exampleKorean: 앞으로 도로가 넓어질 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) weaker.
meanings.example: The storm began to weaken up as it moved inland.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 내륙으로 이동하면서 약해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) tighter, most often screws, knots, straps, etc.
meanings.example: Please tighten down the bolts on the machine.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기계의 볼트를 더 조여주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig through and loosen soil using a plough.
meanings.example: The farmer will plough over the field before planting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 심기 전에 밭을 쟁기로 갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plough over.
meanings.example: The farmer will plow over the field before planting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 심기 전에 밭을 쟁기로 갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dig up and incorporate a legume crop into the soil by ploughing it over when it is fully grown.
meanings.example: They decided to plough down the clover crop to enrich the soil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 토양을 비옥하게 하기 위해 클로버 작물을 쟁기로 갈아넣기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plough down.
meanings.example: They decided to plow down the clover crop to enrich the soil.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 토양을 비옥하게 하기 위해 클로버 작물을 쟁기로 갈아넣기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: sign on.
meanings.example: You need to sign onto the website to access your account.
meanings.exampleKorean: 계정에 접속하려면 웹사이트에 가입해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To move down the trophic chain of seafood, i.e. moving from fishing top-level predators to fishes lower in the food chain, as a result of overfishing affecting top-level predators first.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 먹이 사슬 하위 단계 어종을 낚다
meanings.example: Due to overfishing, we had to fish down the food chain.
meanings.exampleKorean: 남획 때문에 우리는 먹이 사슬의 하위 단계 어종을 낚아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or find by fishing.
meanings.example: All we could fish up in that lake was one old boot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 호수에서 우리가 낚아서 얻을 수 있었던 것은 낡은 부츠 한 짝뿐이었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: plow in.
meanings.example: The farmer decided to plough in the remaining crop.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 남은 작물을 쟁기로 갈아넣기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be a result of.
meanings.example: His hatred falls out upon your actions.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 증오는 당신의 행동에서 비롯된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To engage in a program of stretching, especially as part of a workout regimen.
meanings.example: Before we start today's session, let's stretch it out!
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 세션을 시작하기 전에 스트레칭을 합시다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To use an office.
meanings.example: You'll be officing up on the ninth floor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 9층에서 사무실을 사용하게 될 것입니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce or find (something) by hustling (work hard, con, swindle, or deceive or obtain by illicit or forceful action).
meanings.example: Can you hustle up some snacks for the guests?
meanings.exampleKorean: 손님들을 위해 간식을 좀 급히 마련해 줄 수 있나요?

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (something) schmaltzy.
meanings.example: The director decided to schmaltz up the ending of the movie.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 영화의 결말을 지나치게 감상적으로 만들기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To come up with a total for.
meanings.example: Please total up the expenses from the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 경비의 총계를 내주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate (something).
meanings.example: We need to calculate out the total cost of the project.
meanings.exampleKorean: 프로젝트의 총 비용을 계산해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: pore over.
meanings.example: She poured over the documents for hours.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 몇 시간 동안 서류를 자세히 살폈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrounge (something of minimal value); to barely survive or succeed.
meanings.example: Despite our hangover and lack of supplies, we scrounged out a breakfast.
meanings.exampleKorean: 숙취와 보급품 부족에도 불구하고, 우리는 아침 식사를 간신히 마련했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (something) heartily; to eat (all of something).
meanings.example: He scrounged down the entire pizza in minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 분 만에 피자 한 판을 게걸스럽게 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To simplify or automate a process using scripts (a file containing a list of user commands, allowing them to be invoked once to execute in sequence).
meanings.example: You could finish this quicker by scripting it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스크립트로 만들면 더 빨리 끝낼 수 있을 거예요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrawl (something); to write out in a scrawl.
meanings.example: He scrawled up a quick note and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 급히 메모를 휘갈겨 쓰고는 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make the ground flat with tools like a hoe or shovel.
meanings.example: The workers scraped down the uneven ground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 일꾼들이 울퉁불퉁한 땅을 평평하게 고르고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrawl (something); to dash off in a scrawl.
meanings.example: She scrawled off a message to her friend.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 친구에게 메시지를 급히 휘갈겨 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrawl (something); to write down in a scrawl.
meanings.example: He scrawled down the address on a napkin.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 냅킨에 주소를 휘갈겨 적었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drive so suddenly and so quickly as to make the tires screech.
meanings.example: The cop car screeched out of the parking lot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰차가 주차장에서 끼익 소리를 내며 급히 출발했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) flat.
meanings.example: He flattened down the dough before baking.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 굽기 전에 반죽을 납작하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) thick (either in width or in viscosity).
meanings.example: The sauce began to thicken up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소스가 걸쭉해지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) thin (in either width or viscosity).
meanings.example: Add water to thin up the paint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트를 묽게 하려면 물을 더 넣으세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: flatten down.
meanings.example: Please flat down the papers on the desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 책상 위의 서류들을 납작하게 눌러주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To spend money or give money to friends and associates freely; to spend money in a showy way or on something high-profile.
meanings.example: He's popular there because he's always splashing money around.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 돈을 마구 쓰기 때문에 거기서 인기가 많다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scour (something) thoroughly.
meanings.example: We scoured up all the information we could find.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 찾을 수 있는 모든 정보를 샅샅이 뒤졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scour up.
meanings.example: She scoured down the dirty pots and pans.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 더러운 냄비와 프라이팬을 박박 문질렀다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To frighten (someone) to get them to do (something).
meanings.example: John's warning scared me into getting to work on time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 존의 경고는 나를 겁먹게 해서 제시간에 출근하게 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tan turn into leather.
meanings.example: They tan out animal hides for clothing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 옷을 만들기 위해 동물 가죽을 무두질한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To beat down on the surface.
meanings.example: Rain belted down throughout the city.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비가 도시 전체에 세차게 퍼부었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack, knock down or shoot (someone) many times; to attack away at someone.
meanings.example: The soldiers blazed on the enemy position.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적의 진지를 맹렬히 공격했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: gobble down To eat or drink (something) quickly.
meanings.example: He slammed down his drink and left.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술을 게걸스럽게 마시고 떠났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shine intensely on someone or something.
meanings.example: Sunlight blazed down on our heads.
meanings.exampleKorean: 햇빛이 우리 머리 위로 맹렬하게 내리쬐었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: chomp down on (to eat).
meanings.example: He champed down on the steak with gusto.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 스테이크를 맛있게 우적우적 씹어 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a bed or sleeping area to sleep; to lie down.
meanings.example: We can bunk down in that room.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 그 방에서 잠을 잘 수 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make oneself numb, especially emotionally.
meanings.example: He tried to numb out the pain with alcohol.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술로 고통을 무감각하게 만들려고 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to be hidden waiting to ambush or attack someone
meanings.example: The soldiers lay in ambush for the enemy.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적을 위해 매복했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To laugh at (someone) until they abandon a performance; to shout down (a performer) with laughter.
meanings.example: The audience hooted down the comedian.
meanings.exampleKorean: 관객들은 웃음으로 코미디언을 야유했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To condescend to (someone) loquaciously; to speak to someone in a forceful, insulting, bossy or aggressive way; to wear down by talking.
meanings.example: He likes to jaw down his subordinates.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 부하들에게 거들먹거리며 말하는 것을 좋아한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hunt down; to search for (someone or something).
meanings.example: The police hounded down the fugitive.
meanings.exampleKorean: 경찰은 도망자를 집요하게 추적했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply ice to (something).
meanings.example: You have ice down your injured wrist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 부상당한 손목에 얼음을 대고 있군요.

meanings.englishDefinition: talk down to.
meanings.example: He always speaks down to his employees.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 항상 직원들을 무시하며 말한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To secure a ship or equipment on a ship, such as in preparation for a storm.
meanings.example: Quickly snug everything down before the storm gets here.
meanings.exampleKorean: 폭풍이 오기 전에 모든 것을 재빨리 단단히 고정하세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To write down in a scribble.
meanings.example: I scribbled down the important points during the lecture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 강의 중에 중요한 내용들을 휘갈겨 썼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To supervise or act as manager toward (someone) below oneself in a hierarchy.
meanings.example: The new manager tends to manage down rather than inspire.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 관리자는 격려하기보다는 아랫사람들을 통제하려는 경향이 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate one's interactions with someone above oneself in a hierarchy (often one's own manager) so as to try to guide them into choosing a certain option (narrow scope) or to guide them into doing a better job at their leadership of their subordinates (broader scope).
meanings.example: Effective employees learn to manage up as well as manage down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 유능한 직원들은 아랫사람을 관리하는 것뿐만 아니라 상사에게 잘 보이도록 처신하는 법도 배운다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assemble a final recording from various audio sources.
meanings.example: The sound engineer will mix down the tracks tomorrow.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음향 엔지니어가 내일 트랙들을 최종 편집할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mop (something) thoroughly.
meanings.example: Please mop down the kitchen floor after dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 저녁 식사 후에 부엌 바닥을 꼼꼼히 닦아주세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To party hard; to celebrate or revel enthusiastically.
meanings.example: We partied down all night long to celebrate our success.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 성공을 축하하기 위해 밤새도록 신나게 파티했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove the fat from (a piece of meat) by cooking.
meanings.example: Render down the bacon fat before adding the vegetables.
meanings.exampleKorean: 채소를 넣기 전에 베이컨 기름을 빼내세요.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dismantle or demolish (something).
meanings.example: They had to rip down the old fence.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 낡은 울타리를 철거해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To order (something) through room service in a hotel.
meanings.example: I'm going to send down for some coffee.
meanings.exampleKorean: 커피 좀 룸서비스로 주문해야겠어.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat or drink (something) quickly.
meanings.example: He snorted down the entire glass of water.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 물 한 잔을 단숨에 들이켰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean (something) with soap; to cover (something) with soap.
meanings.example: He soaped down the car before rinsing it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 차를 헹구기 전에 비누칠을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean (something, especially the deck of a ship) with a sponge or similarly absorbent material.
meanings.example: The sailors swabbed down the deck every morning.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선원들은 매일 아침 갑판을 대걸레로 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stabilize a corbel by laying back bricks first to stabilize the structure.
meanings.example: The mason will tail down the corbel to ensure it's stable.
meanings.exampleKorean: 석공은 코벨을 안정시키기 위해 아래쪽을 벽돌로 고정할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a surface) smooth by buffing.
meanings.example: He buffed down the table until it shone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 테이블이 빛날 때까지 광택을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to bed; to go to sleep.
meanings.example: It's late, I think it's time to flake down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦었으니 이제 잠자리에 들 시간인 것 같다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink all of (something).
meanings.example: I can't drink that whole thing down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 저것을 전부 다 마실 수 없다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To scrounge for money, especially large amounts of it.
meanings.example: We had to dig down deep to afford the new car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새 차를 사기 위해 돈을 긁어모아야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat (something).
meanings.example: He chucked down a sandwich in two minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 샌드위치를 2분 만에 게걸스럽게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To drink (something) in large gulps.
meanings.example: He swilled down the beer quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 맥주를 빨리 꿀꺽꿀꺽 마셨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To believe (something) unpleasant or undesirable.
meanings.example: He had to swallow down his pride and apologize.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자존심을 누르고 사과해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) thinner or smaller (in overall size, width or viscosity).
meanings.example: You need to thin down the paint before applying it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 페인트를 칠하기 전에 묽게 해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose weight.
meanings.example: I'm hoping to trim down this year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 올해 체중을 줄이고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have mastered a skill.
meanings.example: I have that song down to the last note.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그 노래를 마지막 음까지 완벽하게 익혔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: Don't pull on that, or you'll bring the top down on us.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그것을 당기지 마, 그렇지 않으면 위에 있는 것이 우리에게 떨어질 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To extract oneself from a dangerous or unfortunate situation.
meanings.example: He had to bring himself away from the battleground.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전장에서 벗어나야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assist (someone) in succeeding or surviving a period of difficulty, especially illness; to nurse or aid someone through an illness.
meanings.example: She hoped her training would allow her to bring him through.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 자신의 훈련이 그가 어려움을 극복하도록 도울 수 있기를 바랐다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ensure someone feels confident and outgoing.
meanings.example: The teacher's encouragement helped bring the shy student out of himself.
meanings.exampleKorean: 선생님의 격려는 수줍은 학생이 자신감을 갖도록 도와주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become freckled.
meanings.example: Her nose and cheeks began to freckle up in the sun.
meanings.exampleKorean: 햇볕에 그녀의 코와 뺨에 주근깨가 생기기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To criticize or scold (someone).
meanings.example: I prefer to jacket up workers in private.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 직원들을 사석에서 질책하는 것을 선호한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To coat (something) (to cover with a coating of some material).
meanings.example: We coated up the anodes before the experiment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 실험 전에 양극에 코팅을 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To gnash (something) until it is mashed up; to mash something up as if by gnashing it.
meanings.example: He gnashed up the herbs into a paste.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 허브를 갈아서 페이스트로 만들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To delay (someone) by talking to them.
meanings.example: I'm late because Debbie bailed me up on the phone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 데비가 전화로 나를 붙잡고 있어서 늦었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To expect (something).
meanings.example: I hadn't bargained on her bad reaction to the prank.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 그녀가 장난에 나쁘게 반응할 줄은 예상하지 못했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To chair (something) (act as chairman/chairwoman for).
meanings.example: She will chair up the next meeting.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀가 다음 회의의 의장을 맡을 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hold up one's side in a relationship.
meanings.example: She'll never dump him, he knows how to hold it down.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 절대 그를 차버리지 않을 것이다. 그는 관계를 잘 유지하는 법을 안다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To use a process of sequential drilling and blasting in building tunnels for mining, subways, etc.
meanings.example: The engineers decided to breast down the tunnel section.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기술자들은 터널 구간에 굴착 및 폭파 공법을 사용하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To blab (something) (to speak carelessly or excessively).
meanings.example: He blabbed out the secret to everyone.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 비밀을 모두에게 누설했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: prep up.
meanings.example: We need to prep out the presentation thoroughly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 발표를 철저히 준비해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be removed from a competition by not qualifying.
meanings.example: He qualified out before the Olympics even started.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 올림픽이 시작되기도 전에 자격 미달로 탈락했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To eat with gusto.
meanings.example: The hungry children bog into their dinner.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배고픈 아이들이 저녁을 게걸스럽게 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To bolster (something); to provide support to (something).
meanings.example: This ought to bolster up the case for intervention in the conflict.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것이 분쟁 개입의 근거를 강화해야 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To officially leave an agreement.
meanings.example: The department contracted out of that deal ages ago.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 부서는 오래전에 그 거래에서 탈퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To modify a horseshoe to relieve pressure from the sole.
meanings.example: The farrier will seat out the horseshoe for the lame horse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마구간지기가 절름거리는 말을 위해 편안굽을 조정할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To earn a badge (such as by becoming a police officer).
meanings.example: He finally badged up after years on the force.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 마침내 경찰로서 수년간 복무한 후 배지를 달았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a building by swiping an identification badge; to give up or cause to give up an identification badge when it is no longer needed.
meanings.example: He badges out at five every day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 매일 다섯 시에 출입증을 찍고 나간다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fatten up (to cause to gain weight by means of feeding)
meanings.example: The farmer is trying to fat up the pigs for market.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 시장에 내다 팔 돼지들을 살찌우려 하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fuel up a ship, or sometimes other modes of conveyance.
meanings.example: The ship will bunker up before it sails.
meanings.exampleKorean: 배는 출항 전에 연료를 채울 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To suit up in firefighting gear.
meanings.example: The firefighters quickly bunkered out and headed to the scene.
meanings.exampleKorean: 소방관들은 재빨리 소방 장비를 착용하고 현장으로 향했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall with the palms out to break the fall.
meanings.example: When he punched me, I palmed out and broke my wrist.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그가 나를 쳤을 때, 나는 손바닥을 짚고 넘어지면서 손목이 부러졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play around; to waste time frivolously.
meanings.example: Stop titting about and get back to work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빈둥거리지 말고 일로 돌아가.

meanings.englishDefinition: To clean (something) until it looks as good as new.
meanings.example: She toshed up the old furniture before selling it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오래된 가구를 팔기 전에 깨끗하게 닦았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit doing something.
meanings.example: I've been at this long enough, so I'm ready to toss it in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나는 충분히 오래 해왔으니, 이제 그만둘 준비가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go into a trance.
meanings.example: The music made him trance out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 음악은 그를 황홀경에 빠지게 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To decorate (something) or be decorated with (something).
meanings.example: They were tricked off in their best clothes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 가장 좋은 옷으로 치장했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add tricks to a course or hole, including hazards in full golf and barriers in minigolf.
meanings.example: The course designer decided to trick up the final hole with a water hazard.
meanings.exampleKorean: 코스 디자이너는 마지막 홀에 워터 해저드를 추가하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave, especially when it requires great effort.
meanings.example: After the long hike, we had to trog off back to the car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 하이킹 후, 우리는 차까지 힘겹게 걸어 돌아가야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go away.
meanings.example: The bus came and all the tourists trolleyed off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 버스가 왔고 모든 관광객들이 사라졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To roll up a joint or other cigarette.
meanings.example: He sat on the porch and twisted up a cigarette.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 현관에 앉아 담배를 말아 피웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To share a cell with someone in prison, especially in a one-person cell.
meanings.example: Jerry is twoing up with Harrison.
meanings.exampleKorean: 제리는 해리슨과 감옥 방을 같이 쓰고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To produce (something) carelessly or thoughtlessly.
meanings.example: He whacked out a quick report.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 되는대로 보고서를 급하게 작성했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To masturbate.
meanings.example: He decided to whack one out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 자위행위를 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To change television channels, especially rapidly.
meanings.example: He zapped over to another channel.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 다른 채널로 빨리 돌렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To improve (something) by making it more exciting.
meanings.example: Let's zap up this presentation.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 발표를 좀 더 흥미롭게 만들어 보자.

meanings.englishDefinition: To halter (something).
meanings.example: The farmer haltered up the horse.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 말에 굴레를 씌웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To boil down maple sap into maple syrup.
meanings.example: They are sugaring down the sap.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 단풍나무 수액을 졸이고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To begin to affect.
meanings.example: The more you work at meditation, the more you'll find serenity creeping in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 명상을 더 많이 할수록, 평온함이 서서히 스며드는 것을 더 많이 발견하게 될 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak loudly.
meanings.example: Stop airing off and speak normally.
meanings.exampleKorean: 큰 소리로 말하는 것을 멈추고 정상적으로 말해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get around censorship, bureaucratic barriers or authorities.
meanings.example: This program will sneak around state laws.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 프로그램은 주 법을 피해갈 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reach the rank of Eagle Scout in Scouts BSA.
meanings.example: He eagled up at 17.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 17세에 이글 스카우트가 되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To leave a scouting troop because one has achieved the rank of Eagle Scout.
meanings.example: She eagled out of her troop last year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 작년에 이글 스카우트가 되어 부대를 탈퇴했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) yellow, such as a material aging, a plant leaf or fruit ripening or developing a rust disease.
meanings.example: The leaves yellowed up in the fall.
meanings.exampleKorean: 가을에 잎이 노랗게 물들었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become or make (something) brown in color, such as by cooking.
meanings.example: The bread browned up nicely in the oven.
meanings.exampleKorean: 빵이 오븐에서 보기 좋게 갈색으로 구워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stop maturing prematurely and also usually die off.
meanings.example: The fruit yellowed off before it was ripe.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과일이 익기 전에 조숙하게 노랗게 변해버렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become thoroughly yellow, such as drying tobacco leaves, ripening fruit or aging newspaper.
meanings.example: The newspaper has yellowed out over time.
meanings.exampleKorean: 신문이 시간이 지나면서 완전히 노랗게 변했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To apply a tattoo.
meanings.example: He's tatting up that client.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 그 고객에게 문신을 새기고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To brazen out something.
meanings.example: He decided to brass it out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 뻔뻔하게 대처하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To play music with brass instruments.
meanings.example: The band will brass out a tune.
meanings.exampleKorean: 밴드가 금관악기로 곡을 연주할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ponder or evaluate; to assess the merits and demerits of.
meanings.example: All these arguments must be weighed in the balance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 모든 주장들을 숙고해야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove by chopping.
meanings.example: You have to chop out all the weeds.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 잡초를 쳐내야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To be funny, make jokes and carry on.

meanings.englishDefinition: To join up or ally with (someone).

meanings.englishDefinition: To trim the edges on a boot or shoe of bits of leather, etc. that may be attached to the sides.

meanings.englishDefinition: To attack.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's cornflakes.

I knew what I was doing but basically I got too big for my boots but please take into consideration, the fact that you and every other person involved in this operation, goes home at night, eats a decent meal, relaxes in a nice warm bath, talks to the wife, plays with the kids, watches the box, puts on a favourite record, gets pissed if he feels like it.

meanings.englishDefinition: glom on.

meanings.englishDefinition: fuck someone's brains out.

meanings.englishDefinition: to fail

meanings.englishDefinition: To count steps.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss on someone's chips.
meanings.example: And I’m hopeless with ladies, as you know, lacking both that easygoing contempt which they find irresistible and also lacking a real instinctive trust in them. I present them I suppose with the worst of both worlds – a limited respect. No wonder they always end by pissing in my chips.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아시다시피 저는 여자들에게 젬병입니다. 그들이 참을 수 없어하는 여유로운 경멸감도 없고, 여자들에 대한 진정한 본능적인 신뢰도 없습니다. 아마도 저는 최악의 두 가지를 모두 보여주는 것 같습니다. 제한된 존중심 말이죠. 그들이 항상 제 기분을 망치는 것도 이상한 일이 아닙니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's Cheerios.
meanings.example: Miriam Barton, the army sergeant from Oregon, described it more colorfully: “After I beaned this broad with a can of peas and tried to run somebody off the road, I was diagnosed with severe PTSD. It feels like God is pissing on your Cheerios.”

meanings.englishDefinition: to act or behave in the manner of a dork.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's Cheerios.

Elizabeth is brand-new but totally outswam Brianna during tryouts. She’s super jealous!” I explain, resting my chin in my hands. / “I hear you,” Frank says, setting his knitting needles aside. “That little whippersnapper’s a piece of work. Who peed in her Cheerios anyway?”

meanings.englishDefinition: piss in someone's Cheerios.

meanings.englishDefinition: piss on someone's chips.

meanings.englishDefinition: sleep around; have sex with various partners.
