meanings.englishDefinition: To make into a pie, or otherwise get rid of.
meanings.example: 'Pie him off,' advises her sister. 'I will,' she promises.
meanings.exampleKorean: '그를 내쫓아.' 그녀의 여동생이 조언했다. '그럴게.' 그녀가 약속했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To get rid of; to eliminate.
meanings.example: The dictator ordered his enemies to be taken out back.
meanings.exampleKorean: 독재자는 그의 적들을 제거하라고 명령했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make a good impression.
meanings.example: The team covered themselves in glory with their championship win.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 팀은 우승하며 훌륭한 인상을 남겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fall off by rotting.
meanings.example: The branches eventually rotted off once the tree was uprooted.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나무가 뽑힌 후 가지들은 결국 썩어서 떨어졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Behave in an irrational, aggressive, or eccentric manner.
meanings.example: He was on one after drinking too much.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 술을 너무 많이 마신 후 이상하게 행동했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: smack around
meanings.example: The bully would often slap around the smaller kids.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 불량배는 종종 더 작은 아이들을 때리곤 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: }} To run away from a battle when it becomes difficult.
meanings.example: When the fight got serious, he decided to roach out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 싸움이 심각해지자 그는 도망치기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To date; to go out with.
meanings.example: She's been walking out with him for six months.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그와 6개월째 사귀고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make or become confused, perplexed, twisted or entangled.
meanings.example: The complex instructions made my brain pretzel up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복잡한 지시 때문에 내 머리가 혼란스러워졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to leave; to scram.
meanings.example: It's getting late, let's morris off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 늦었으니, 떠나자.

meanings.englishDefinition: enter.
meanings.example: Come on in, the door is open!
meanings.exampleKorean: 들어와, 문 열려 있어!

meanings.englishDefinition: To start performing (an activity).
meanings.example: They decided to engage in a new business venture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 새로운 사업을 시작하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: fujo out
meanings.example: The company decided to fujoshi out the remaining profits.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 남은 이익을 페이아웃하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To relax; to be lazy or sluggish.
meanings.example: After a long week, I just want to moss out on the couch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 한 주를 보낸 후, 나는 그냥 소파에서 쉬고 싶다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with; to confront; to tackle head on.
meanings.example: We need to tackle on this problem immediately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우리는 이 문제를 즉시 다루어야 한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To assume bold body language.
meanings.example: He stood up and took a confident stance.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 일어서서 자신감 있는 자세를 취했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To polish so as to make look newer
meanings.example: She decided to furbish up the old furniture.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 낡은 가구를 새것처럼 닦기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To add or set the riggings
meanings.example: They had to rig up a temporary shelter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 임시 거처를 마련해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hoard or stack up
meanings.example: He began to hoard up supplies for the winter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 겨울을 대비해 물품을 축적하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To repair by tinkering
meanings.example: He spent the afternoon tinkering up his old car.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오후 내내 오래된 차를 만지작거리며 수리했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To score provide a musical score
meanings.example: The composer will score up the music for the film.
meanings.exampleKorean: 작곡가는 영화를 위해 음악의 악보를 만들 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To ascend rapidly
meanings.example: The balloon began to fly up into the sky.
meanings.exampleKorean: 풍선이 하늘로 급상승하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quickly stand up
meanings.example: He leaped up from his seat when he heard the news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 소식을 듣고 자리에서 벌떡 일어났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lease; to let (property, as a landlord)
meanings.example: The landlord decided to lease out the apartment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 집주인은 아파트를 임대하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become bristly; to stand on end
meanings.example: Her hairs bristled up in fear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀의 머리카락이 두려움에 곤두섰다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become sour or curdled
meanings.example: The milk started to clabber up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 우유가 시어지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To stoke, to kindle (a fire)
meanings.example: He stoked up the fire to keep warm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 따뜻하게 지내기 위해 불을 지폈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scarf down.
meanings.example: He scarfed up the entire pizza in minutes.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 몇 분 만에 피자 한 판을 게걸스럽게 다 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: scarf down.
meanings.example: She scoffed up her breakfast before leaving for school.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 학교에 가기 전에 아침을 게걸스럽게 먹었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: gobble up.
meanings.example: The first of these would be a big Canadian breakfast of pancakes with smoked bacon and maple syrup, which we’d gobble down before piling out of the house.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이것들 중 첫 번째는 훈제 베이컨과 메이플 시럽을 곁들인 팬케이크로 된 푸짐한 캐나다식 아침 식사였는데, 우리는 집을 나서기 전에 그것을 게걸스럽게 먹어치웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: knife up.
meanings.example: And it was still snowing, but here and there jonquils knived up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아직 눈이 내리고 있었지만, 여기저기서 수선화가 칼날처럼 솟아났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To lose by gambling.
meanings.example: He sported away the entire fortune!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 전 재산을 도박으로 날려버렸다!

meanings.englishDefinition: To take a portion of something; to experience something jointly with others.
meanings.example: He's sharing in our dinner for tonight.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 오늘 저녁 우리와 함께 저녁 식사를 할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to come to an end; to stop
meanings.example: The light winked out, plunging the room into darkness.
meanings.exampleKorean: 불빛이 깜빡이며 꺼졌고, 방은 어둠에 잠겼다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To fortify (a place); to convert into a fort.
meanings.example: They decided to fort up the town against the approaching army.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 다가오는 군대에 맞서 마을을 요새화하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To keep talking in a friendly and informal way to each other.
meanings.example: Opening the carriage door, I was confronted with a carriageful of people dressed to the nines, all chatting away, some holding empty champagne glasses.
meanings.exampleKorean: 마차 문을 열자, 최고로 차려입은 사람들이 가득 차 있었고, 모두 담소를 나누고 있었으며, 몇몇은 빈 샴페인 잔을 들고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To serve from a container with a spoon.
meanings.example: She spooned out some soup for him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 그를 위해 수프를 숟가락으로 떠주었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To dim (lights) until they are totally off
meanings.example: The lights began to dim out as the sun set.
meanings.exampleKorean: 해가 지면서 불빛이 점차 어두워지기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To make (a path) by trampling on vegetation or the like.
meanings.example: The children trampled out a path through the tall grass.
meanings.exampleKorean: 아이들이 키 큰 풀밭을 짓밟아 길을 냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To swell or bulge
meanings.example: His injured ankle began to swell out.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 부상당한 발목이 붓기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To sell stakes in a project to third-party investors.
meanings.example: The startup decided to farm down some of its equity to secure funding.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 스타트업은 자금 확보를 위해 일부 지분을 투자자들에게 팔기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mouse, to move cautiously or furtively, in the manner of the rodent.
meanings.example: The cat was mouse around the kitchen, looking for scraps.
meanings.exampleKorean: 고양이가 먹을 것을 찾으며 부엌을 살금살금 돌아다녔다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to recover quickly To recover quickly.
meanings.example: She's young and should snap back from the illness quickly.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 젊기 때문에 병에서 빨리 회복할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: obsolete ed-form
meanings.example: He snapt back a quick reply.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 재빨리 대답했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enter a vehicle, especially a car.
meanings.example: Hop in, I'll give you a ride.
meanings.exampleKorean: 차에 타, 태워줄게.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tally up; to calculate a total.
meanings.example: Let's tote up the expenses for the trip.
meanings.exampleKorean: 여행 경비를 합산해 봅시다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To calculate the cost of a future enterprise (project, plan, etc.)
meanings.example: We need to cost out the new marketing campaign before we launch it.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새로운 마케팅 캠페인을 시작하기 전에 비용을 산출해야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To perform or play very poorly.
meanings.example: The band really stunk the joint out last night.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 밴드는 어젯밤 공연을 정말 엉망으로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To waste time.
meanings.example: Just shut up and get in the truck! You putz around worse than a ninety-year-old woman!
meanings.exampleKorean: 닥치고 트럭에 타! 90세 노인보다 더 빈둥거리는군!

meanings.englishDefinition: To cause trouble; cause a dust-up; cause a hubbub.
meanings.example: Don't kick up a stew over such a small matter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그렇게 사소한 일로 소동을 일으키지 마.

meanings.englishDefinition: To exploit or mock by means of memes.
meanings.example: Chad had always memed on Jason, but something about the way his followers responded made him wonder if they were lonely, too, if everyone online was searching for some lost warmth, some campfire glow inside a cold, infinite scroll.
meanings.exampleKorean: 채드는 항상 제이슨을 밈으로 놀렸지만, 그의 팔로워들이 반응하는 방식 때문에 그들 역시 외로운 것은 아닌지, 온라인의 모든 사람들이 차갑고 무한한 스크롤 속에서 잃어버린 따뜻함, 캠프파이어의 온기를 찾고 있는 것은 아닌지 궁금해졌다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To remove (a person) from the gene pool when they engage in foolish, thoughtless or reckless behavior.
meanings.example: Let's face it, it's just not possible. A guy like that in The Bronx could never make it to the age where he could get a license. He'd be Darwinned-out before he was twelve.
meanings.exampleKorean: 솔직히 말해서, 그건 불가능해. 그런 놈은 면허를 딸 나이가 되기도 전에 도태될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To have sex.
meanings.example: They decided to get it in after the party.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티 후에 성관계를 갖기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: getting it in.
meanings.example: I did in class was daydream about seein' her wit' out clothes on, then go home and fantasize about gettin' it in wit' her. Then, finally, I got my wish. At first, it was just her bendin' over and lettin' me get sneak peeks of her kitty anytime she thought no one else was lookin'.
meanings.exampleKorean: 수업 시간에 했던 일은 그녀가 옷을 벗은 모습을 상상하는 것이었고, 집에 가서 그녀와 성관계하는 것을 상상하는 것이었다. 그러다 마침내 소원을 이루었다. 처음에는 그녀가 몸을 숙이고 아무도 보지 않는다고 생각할 때마다 내게 은밀한 모습을 보여주는 것이 전부였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place a roof over an outdoor area.
meanings.example: the extension to Crewe carried the line much nearer to the town, it was decided to retain this station, and a large enclosure there was roofed over to accommodate third class passengers (the M.B.R. was more generous than most railways in its treatment of what were then known as the "lower orders").
meanings.exampleKorean: 크루로의 연장은 기차 노선을 마을에 훨씬 더 가깝게 가져왔고, 이 역을 유지하기로 결정했으며, 그곳의 넓은 구역은 3등석 승객을 수용하기 위해 지붕을 덮었다 (당시 "하층민"으로 알려진 사람들에 대한 대우에 있어 MBR은 대부분의 철도보다 더 관대했다).

meanings.englishDefinition: To return (something) to where it came from.
meanings.example: Midlands Railway has confirmed that it will begin handing back its fleet of Class 222s to Eversholt Group, with three sets due to move between now and December.
meanings.exampleKorean: 미들랜드 철도는 클래스 222 열차 편성을 에버스홀트 그룹에 반납하기 시작할 것이며, 3편성이 지금부터 12월 사이에 이동될 예정이라고 확인했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To prevent a person from going to bed at their usual time.
meanings.example: political careers start when people are in their 70s, but Hendy is actually relishing it, although he is incensed sometimes by the pointlessness of the posturing by the opposition, which keeps him up late. "The bit I don't like is getting home at 0100 or 0200 in the morning because the opposition have been fruitlessly arguing for a long time."
meanings.exampleKorean: 정치 경력은 70대에 시작되지만, 헨디는 실제로 이를 즐기고 있다. 비록 야당의 무의미한 자세 때문에 가끔 격분하지만, 그것이 그를 늦게까지 깨어있게 한다. "내가 싫어하는 부분은 야당이 오랫동안 헛되이 논쟁했기 때문에 아침 1시나 2시에 집에 도착하는 것이다."

meanings.englishDefinition: To be well-off
meanings.example: Stick with me, kid, and you'll be farting through silk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 나만 따라와, 애송이. 그러면 부자가 될 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: To botch or ruin.
meanings.example: When a pilot no longer heeds the demands of vanity or misplaced pride; when he attempts not to salvage a plumbered-up approach with a desperate bit of squirming and jockeying, but rather shrugs it off as a well-deserved object lesson in complacency and tools around the circuit again—then he is a real pro.
meanings.exampleKorean: 조종사가 더 이상 허영심이나 잘못된 자존심의 요구를 따르지 않을 때; 필사적인 몸부림과 기교로 망쳐버린 접근 방식을 만회하려고 하지 않고, 오히려 그것을 당연한 교훈으로 여기고 다시 서킷을 돌 때—그때 그는 진정한 프로이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: While the Scots weren't shooting the lights out in attack, they also allowed Belarus to be comfortable for large parts of the game. The fact they had 10 shots more than Scotland is a huge cause for concern.
meanings.exampleKorean: 스코틀랜드는 공격에서 압도적인 성과를 내지는 못했지만, 경기 대부분 벨라루스를 편안하게 내버려 두었다. 스코틀랜드보다 10개의 더 많은 슈팅을 기록했다는 사실은 큰 우려의 원인이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To return to a previous position.
meanings.example: imminent creation of Great British Railways (GBR) shows that the pendulum is swinging back to a government-managed network, bringing fears of political interference and concerns about long-term investment.
meanings.exampleKorean: 영국 철도청(GBR)의 임박한 설립은 시계추가 정부 관리 네트워크로 다시 흔들리고 있음을 보여주며, 이는 정치적 간섭에 대한 두려움과 장기 투자에 대한 우려를 불러일으킨다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To buy recklessly large amounts of an untried cryptocurrency.
meanings.example: He decided to ape in on the new meme coin, hoping to get rich quick.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빨리 부자가 되기를 바라며 새로운 밈 코인에 묻지마 투자를 하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: knock someone's block off
meanings.example: If you don't stop making that noise, I'm going to knock your head off!
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 소리 내기 그만두지 않으면 네 머리를 날려버릴 거야!

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: The park rangers warned off the hikers from entering the dangerous area.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공원 관리인들은 등산객들에게 위험한 지역에 들어가지 말라고 경고했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: en
meanings.example: what little remained of his self-confidence was slowly whittling away
meanings.exampleKorean: 그의 얼마 남지 않은 자신감은 서서히 줄어들고 있었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: complain violently.
meanings.example: This mornin’ she rails out at me fer dunkin’ my cooky and she says COFFEE IS FER SIPPIN’ AND NOT FER SAUCERIN’. . .
meanings.exampleKorean: 오늘 아침 그녀는 내가 쿠키를 커피에 찍어 먹는다고 나에게 맹렬히 비난하며 커피는 홀짝 마시는 것이지 소서링하는 것이 아니라고 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: take turns.
meanings.example: wood burners have African drivers with two African firemen who take in turn the two main duties of putting wood into the firebox and moving it forward on the tender.
meanings.exampleKorean: 목재 연소기에는 두 명의 아프리카 기관사와 두 명의 아프리카 화부가 있으며, 이들은 화실에 나무를 넣고 텐더로 앞으로 옮기는 두 가지 주요 임무를 차례대로 수행한다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bling out
meanings.example: She decided to bling up her old jacket with some rhinestones and glitter.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 오래된 재킷을 모조석과 반짝이로 화려하게 장식하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To place livestock in a pen in a stockyard.
meanings.example: The farmer had to yard out the sheep before the storm.
meanings.exampleKorean: 농부는 폭풍이 오기 전에 양들을 축사의 우리에 넣어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To deal with a difficult situation by sudden difficult action.
meanings.example: Sometimes you just have to rip off the bandage and tell the truth, no matter how much it hurts.
meanings.exampleKorean: 때로는 아무리 아프더라도 단칼에 사실을 말하고 끝내야 할 때가 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enjoy and/or like something in a sexual way or as a fetish.
meanings.example: He confessed that he was really into collecting vintage action figures.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 빈티지 액션 피규어 수집에 정말 빠져있다고 고백했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hide or disappear due to feelings of extreme embarrassment, shame, or helplessness; to withdraw from reality.
meanings.example: After his embarrassing public mistake, he just wanted to crawl into a hole and disappear.
meanings.exampleKorean: 공개적인 실수로 창피해진 후, 그는 구멍 속으로 기어 들어가 사라지고 싶었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become unnoticed and seem to disappear.
meanings.example: come out of the woodwork
meanings.exampleKorean: 갑자기 나타나다

meanings.englishDefinition: To add before publication or broadcasting (as an editorial decision).
meanings.example: The director decided to edit in a new scene to clarify the plot.
meanings.exampleKorean: 감독은 줄거리를 명확히 하기 위해 새로운 장면을 편집하여 추가하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To hire and bring a contractor into a project locally.
meanings.example: The company decided to contract in a local firm for the construction work.
meanings.exampleKorean: 회사는 건설 작업을 위해 지역 업체를 계약하여 참여시키기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To excel, particularly in regards to one's physical appearance; to serve.
meanings.example: She really turned 19 in Poland with her stunning dress and confident demeanor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 멋진 드레스와 자신감 있는 태도로 폴란드에서 정말 19살처럼 빛났다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To speak Antiguan and Barbudan Creole
meanings.example: He started to raw back when he got excited talking about his hometown.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 고향에 대해 이야기하며 신이 났을 때 앤티가 크리올어를 말하기 시작했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To become fat or large, especially from overeating or overfeeding.
meanings.example: {{quote-web|en| get chonkier after a while. I've seen bshs British Shorthairs who chonk up after they hit 1y, and some that need to hit 4y to reach the ultimate chonkiness level.
meanings.exampleKorean: 시간이 지나면 더 통통해진다. 1살이 넘으면 통통해지는 브리티시 쇼트헤어도 있고, 궁극적인 통통함 수준에 도달하려면 4살이 되어야 하는 고양이들도 보았다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To go to sleep as a group for the night; by extension to go to bed.
meanings.example: I was so exhausted after the long shift that I decided to rack out for the rest of the day.
meanings.exampleKorean: 긴 근무 끝에 너무 피곤해서 남은 하루 동안 푹 자기로 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To defeat or dominate; to outdo.
meanings.example: The star player really beast on the opposing team in the final match.
meanings.exampleKorean: 주전 선수는 마지막 경기에서 상대 팀을 정말 압도했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To tough it out; to persevere despite pain, fear or setbacks.
meanings.example: She had to gut through the difficult exam despite feeling unwell.
meanings.exampleKorean: 몸이 좋지 않았음에도 불구하고 그녀는 어려운 시험을 견뎌내야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: roll in wealth.
meanings.example: From what I hear, he's a regular dog; he's rolling in money; he's taking three mistresses and a cook along with him.
meanings.exampleKorean: 내가 듣기로 그는 정말 대단한 사람이야; 돈이 넘쳐나; 세 명의 정부와 요리사를 데리고 다녀.

meanings.englishDefinition: To force someone or something to retreat or move away; repel.
meanings.example: The soldiers managed to drive back the enemy forces.
meanings.exampleKorean: 병사들은 적군을 격퇴하는 데 성공했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: come down on
meanings.example: The authorities will come down upon anyone caught littering.
meanings.exampleKorean: 당국은 쓰레기를 버리는 사람을 발견하면 엄하게 처벌할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To transfer (recorded footage) from a wider to a narrower tape format.
meanings.example: We need to bump down the footage to a smaller file size for easier sharing.
meanings.exampleKorean: 더 쉬운 공유를 위해 녹화본을 더 작은 파일 크기로 옮겨야 합니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To quit a PhD program and earn a master's degree.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 박사 과정을 중단하고 석사 학위를 받다
meanings.example: who do not pass all their standardized requirements or are otherwise lacking are mastered-out in their first year.
meanings.exampleKorean: 모든 표준화된 요건을 통과하지 못하거나 달리 부족한 사람들은 첫 해에 석사 학위로 졸업하게 된다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To mistakenly believe one has reached an important insight, despite not having done so.
meanings.example: Lil Bro is onto NOTHING ‼️‼️💀😭😭😭
meanings.exampleKorean: 릴 브로는 아무것도 알아내지 못했어!!

meanings.englishDefinition: To think intensely and deeply about a confusing problem, situation, or question in an effort to understand it or to find a solution.
meanings.example: Scientists continue to puzzle over the mysterious patterns found in the deep sea.
meanings.exampleKorean: 과학자들은 심해에서 발견된 신비로운 패턴들에 대해 계속해서 골똘히 생각하고 연구하고 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To work methodically to solve or understand something complex.
meanings.example: It took me all night to puzzle through the complex instructions and assemble the desk.
meanings.exampleKorean: 복잡한 설명서를 이해하고 책상을 조립하는 데 밤을 꼬박 새웠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To train an insectivorous bird to eat non-live food.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 벌레 안 먹는 새에게 살아있지 않은 먹이 먹이기
meanings.example: The birds, when secured, are placed in a store cage, and quickly conveyed to the bird dealers in Seven Dials. These worthies then proceed at once to "meat them off" This is accomplished thus :— Some fresh raw beef is scraped; and being divested of all fibrous substance, it is mixed into a soft paste, with cold water and hardboiled yolk of egg. This is put into a large bird pan. In the middle of this food is placed a very small inverted liqueur glass, with the stem broken off Under this glass are introduced three or four lively meal worms, whose oft-repeated endeavours to break out of prison attract the attention of the Nightingale. Not understanding how these worms are placed beyond his reach, he continues to peck at them, until by degrees he tastes the beef and egg, which is artfully rubbed over the sides of the glass. This being palateable, he satiates his appetite with it, and soon feels a zest for it — particularly as his attempts to get at the meal worms always prove abortive. He now eats regularly — he is meated off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 새들은 새장 안에 넣어 즉시 런던의 새 판매상에게 운반됩니다. 이 판매상들은 즉시 "살아있는 먹이를 먹도록 훈련"시키기 시작합니다. 이것은 다음과 같이 달성됩니다: 신선한 날 소고기를 긁어내고 섬유질을 제거한 후, 차가운 물과 완숙 달걀 노른자와 섞어 부드러운 반죽을 만듭니다. 이것을 큰 새 모이통에 넣습니다. 이 먹이 중앙에는 줄기가 부러진 아주 작은 리큐르 잔을 거꾸로 놓습니다. 이 잔 아래에는 활발하게 움직이는 밀웜 세네 마리를 넣습니다. 이 밀웜들이 감옥에서 탈출하려는 반복적인 노력은 나이팅게일의 주의를 끕니다. 이 벌레들이 자신의 손이 닿지 않는 곳에 놓여 있다는 것을 이해하지 못한 채, 그는 계속해서 벌레를 쪼다가 점차 유리잔 옆면에 교묘하게 발라진 소고기와 달걀 맛을 보게 됩니다. 이것이 맛있다고 느끼자, 그는 식욕을 채우고 곧 이에 대한 열망을 느끼게 됩니다. 특히 밀웜을 잡으려는 그의 시도가 항상 실패로 돌아가기 때문입니다. 그는 이제 규칙적으로 먹이를 먹습니다. 그는 훈련되었습니다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To kill
meanings.example: The assassin was hired to slime out the king.
meanings.exampleKorean: 암살자는 왕을 제거하기 위해 고용되었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To demonstrate low reading comprehension.
meanings.example: He clearly didn't understand the instructions; he really pissed on the poor.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 지시사항을 분명히 이해하지 못했다. 그는 정말로 이해력이 부족했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To start a romantic relationship with someone.
meanings.example: They met at a party and decided to boo up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그들은 파티에서 만나 연애를 시작하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: shield from paint etc mask.
meanings.example: The labour cost of this type of paint job comes more from the time it takes to painstakingly mask things off than from the time it takes to spray the paint.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이런 종류의 페인트 작업의 인건비는 페인트를 뿌리는 시간보다 꼼꼼하게 마스킹하는 데 걸리는 시간에서 더 많이 나온다.

meanings.englishDefinition: rip off the band-aid.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 반창고를 떼다, 고통스러운 일을 빨리 끝내다
meanings.example: It's time to rip the band-aid off and tell him the bad news.
meanings.exampleKorean: 반창고를 떼고 그에게 나쁜 소식을 전할 때다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To enthusiastically endorse the implementation of a liberal political agenda.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 진보적 정치 의제 실행을 열렬히 지지하다
meanings.example: The senator is known for libbing out whenever progressive policies are discussed.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 상원의원은 진보적 정책이 논의될 때마다 열렬히 지지하는 것으로 알려져 있다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To forcefully expel air from the lungs onto an object.
meanings.example: He blew on his cold hands to warm them up.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 추운 손을 녹이기 위해 입김을 불었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: give away the store
meanings.example: Don't give away the shop; keep the surprise party a secret.
meanings.exampleKorean: 비밀을 누설하지 마. 깜짝 파티를 비밀로 해.

meanings.englishDefinition: To incrementally enter a position on an oversold market in view of indications of a rebound trend.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 과매도 시장에서 반등 조짐을 보고 점진적으로 포지션에 진입하다
meanings.example: The trader decided to scale in his position as the stock showed signs of recovery.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 거래자는 주식이 회복 조짐을 보이자 포지션에 점진적으로 진입하기로 결정했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To reveal an underlying truth.
meanings.example: The investigation pulled back the curtain on the company's corrupt practices.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 조사는 회사의 부패 행위에 숨겨진 진실을 드러냈다.

meanings.englishDefinition: go nap
meanings.example: I'm tired, I think I'll go nap on the couch.
meanings.exampleKorean: 피곤하다. 소파에 가서 낮잠을 자야겠다.

meanings.englishDefinition: have one's act together
meanings.example: Despite the chaos, she seemed to have her shit together.
meanings.exampleKorean: 혼란에도 불구하고 그녀는 침착해 보였다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To shoot someone in the head.
meanings.example: The movie depicted a scene where a character's head was blown off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 영화는 등장인물의 머리가 날아가는 장면을 묘사했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: to betray (someone) by informing on them.
meanings.example: I’ll dob on you if you break in.
meanings.exampleKorean: 만약 네가 침입하면 내가 너를 밀고할 거야.

meanings.englishDefinition: wrap one's head around.
meanings.example: It took me a while to wrap my brain around the complex theory.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 복잡한 이론을 이해하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: Of a horse or other animal, to rear up on its hind legs in order to throw off or dislodge its rider.
meanings.koreanDefinition: 말 등이 뒷발로 서서 타는 사람을 떨어뜨리다
meanings.example: The horse bucked off the inexperienced rider.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 말은 미숙한 기수를 떨어뜨렸다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To align or sight an instrument (such as a theodolite or level) precisely between two marks or points for accurate measurement.
meanings.example: The surveyor had to buck in the transit to get an accurate reading.
meanings.exampleKorean: 측량사는 정확한 측정을 위해 경위의를 정렬해야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To castigate someone very angrily.
meanings.example: If you fail this test, your parents will rip your face off.
meanings.exampleKorean: 이 시험에 떨어지면 부모님이 너를 몹시 비난할 것이다.

meanings.englishDefinition: bust down.
meanings.example: They had to bust it down to remove the old structure.
meanings.exampleKorean: 기존 구조물을 제거하기 위해 그것을 부수어야 했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: buss down.
meanings.example: The couple bussed it down passionately.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그 커플은 열정적으로 키스했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To experience no hindrances or obstacles; to do something without hesitation.
meanings.example: She found no bones in completing the marathon.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그녀는 마라톤 완주에 어려움이 없었다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To do, indicate, or say something vaguely and with hesitation.
meanings.example: He didn't make bones about telling her the truth.
meanings.exampleKorean: 그는 망설임 없이 그녀에게 진실을 말했다.

meanings.englishDefinition: To control access to (an area, road, etc.) by installing a gate.
meanings.example: The entrance to the private road was gated off from the public.
meanings.exampleKorean: 사유지로 들어가는 입구는 일반인들이 들어오지 못하게 문으로 막혀 있었다.
